summary: Title page: 'Provincial State Papers, Tom. I, 1767–1777. Savage 15'

author: Pedro Toral Valdes (Contador Gen.)
summary: Pay and wine rations for Compañía franca de Voluntarios de Cataluña, destined for Expedición de Sonora. Embarked from Cádiz in Fragata *Juno* and Urca *San Juan*. Signed Francisco de Guerrero y Tagle, Joaquín Treviño, Manuel del Campo Marín
Instructions for receiving Nao de Filipinas at San José del Cabo. No one ashore unless sick. Vigilance, searching

*San Carlos* supply manifest — 'Resúmen de lo embarcado': carne seca 187@, galleta 267@, maíz 160½ fanegas, arroz 37@, etc. 'Mil pesos en reales para una urgencia inopinada.' 32@ panocha for SD and Monterey missions; 12 remaining 'para gratificar á los Indios y rescatar con ellos.' Puerto de la Paz

author: Gálvez (instructions)
summary: Gálvez's Sacred Expedition instructions. Engineer Miguel Costanzó to direct works and reconnaissances. Correct charts, especially 'Piloto Cabrera Bueno que se tiene por el mejor en el concepto comun.' Guards against Indians at Monterey — 'nunca aunque estén de paz... se deberá fiar en ellos por la inconstancia que les es natural.' Build provisional presidio first
Francisco Palou, Presidente de las Misiones, at Loreto. Mission organization, settler families, rations of maize (almud y medio per family)
Transport difficulties — 300 leagues through gentile territory. Soldier discipline, supply loads

author: Bucareli
summary: FAGES RELIEVED. Junta de Guerra y Hacienda confers command to Capt. Fernando de Rivera y Moncada. Fages to hand over, embark from SD to San Blas, march Catalan detachment to Real de Minas de Pachuca. pp.329–30

author: Bucareli
summary: Recruiting troops for California. Open passport for volunteers from provinces of SD and Monterey. Ration distribution

author: Rivera (diary/letter)
summary: Instructions about 300 pesos salary. Use mine mules. Good relations with Padres. Baja California: 'naturales en estado de revolverse' (natives in revolt). 50+ mules expected
San Borja (BC) — no mercury available. Dispute over Rivera's correspondence at Loreto — Governor asked to verify Rivera's handwriting. Threat to arrest someone

author: Ortega
summary: Acknowledges receipt of despacho de Teniente (promotion to lieutenant). Will march with family. pp.355–6

author: Ortega
summary: TROOP DISTRIBUTION, San Diego. Between Presidio and Mission: Ten.te José Fran.co de Ortega, Sarg.to Mariano Carrillo, Cabo Mariano Verdugo. Soldiers: Juan Estevan Rocha, Joseph Raymundo Carrillo, Joseph Camacho + others. 23 men continued to Monterey (names include Ign.o Vallejo, Luis Lugo, Joseph Dominguez, Ign.o Alvarado, Nicolas Beltran, etc.). Sarg.to Joseph M.a Góngora, Cabo Guillo Carrillo with 19 men. Escolta de San Gabriel: Cabo Juan Joseph Robles with 6 men. Total: 48 hombres. pp.357–8

author: Ortega
summary: SAN JUAN CAPISTRANO FOUNDING (Nov 1, 1775, Día de Todos los Santos). Padre Amurrío with 11 soldiers. SD mission reestablished Oct 17 with 12-soldier escort. Soldiers without shirts, no shoes — 'no tienen camisas ni con que cubrirse de noche... no tienen ni zapatos — aquí no los hay ni con que hacerlos.' Ramirez sold cuirass to Monterey soldiers; Ríos sold his; Vallejo sold mochilas. 'Varios soldados intentan desertar.' Padre Calixto promises to receive colonists

author: Ortega
summary: Families arrived at SD presidio Sep 26. Anastasio Camacho arrested, irons, rations, no pay. Shortages. Translation of mission. 'Hombres militares'

author: D.n Fernando de Ullicati
summary: Detained travelers' effects. Alférez Velazquez. Padre Miguel Sanchez. Viceroy's decree from Junta de Guerra, May 6. Governor Barry

author: Ortega
summary: Need for muleteers, warehouse, church, arsenal at SD. Arrieros Manuel Virján and Fran.co Bernel ordered from mission to construction. Bernel detained — must tend King's livestock

author: Bucareli
summary: Acknowledges Rivera's takeover of Comandancia, May 25 [1774]. Fragata *Santiago* at Monterey. Report on presidios and missions pending. p.408

author: Bucareli
summary: ORDERS RECONNAISSANCE AND OCCUPATION OF PORT OF SAN FRANCISCO. Approves keeping 10 Catalan soldiers. Authorizes construction of warehouses

author: Bucareli
summary: SF FOUNDING ORDER: 'la expedición que ha de salir por tierra... el Teniente Coronel D.n Juan B.ta de Anza tiene por principal objeto la ocupación del Puerto de San Francisco, establecimiento de dos Doctrinas en las cercanías y el del Fuerte á cuyo abrigo deben fundarse.' Channel missions also requested (Buenaventura, Santa Clara). 20 more troops needed

LAND GRANT to Manuel Butrón at Mission Carmel — 1,140 varas measured with Junípero Serra present, witnessed by Cabo Hermenegildo Sal and Ign.o Patrón. Authority from Bucareli's order of Aug 17, 1773. Grants extended 'no solo á esta calidad de gente' but also 'á los naturales del País'
10 volunteers under speaker's charge. Horses and mules — King's property, cannot be separated. Sarg.to Mariano Carrillo to escort Piloto-Almacén D.n Juan Soler

author: Bucareli
summary: Instructions: Fr. Vicente de Mora (Presid.te). Discord in the Province. Articles 8–13 on Baja California establishments

author: Bucareli
summary: Cabo and 9 soldiers sent to Lt. Juan Manuel de Ayala at SF. Gov. Neve ordered to report on events. pp.463–4

author: Bucareli
summary: SF reconnaissance, missions, and PRESIDIO WITH ARTILLERY upon Anza's arrival. Rivera decided to wait for Anza. Bucareli approves

author: Bucareli
summary: Presidio as 'custodia y resguardo.' Mules/horses to colonists for breeding. Serra's mission servant wages. Settler families ('familias pobladoras'). Junta decision confirmed

author: Bucareli
summary: Distribution of 84 mulas from California [ship]. 44 to SD and Monterey presidios, rest to missions

author: Bucareli
summary: SUPPLY SHIPS FOR SF FOUNDING: Paquebots *Príncipe* 'debe pasar al Puerto de San Francisco con literas sobrantes... para auxiliar las familias que haya conducido por tierra el Teniente Coronel D.n Juan B.ta de Anza.' *San Carlos* to return to SD with provisions

author: Bucareli
summary: 3 boxes of avalorios (glass beads) from Comisario of San Blas. D.n Bruno Ezeta (Heceta) mentioned. Agriculture for self-sufficiency

author: Bucareli
summary: Settlers must be volunteers. Sailor Lorenzo Rosa denied embarkation (married). Animals distributed 'en calidad de reintegro' (loans)

author: Bucareli
summary: Good treatment of artisans coming to California

author: Bucareli
summary: Rivera to report everything. Growth of Alta California establishments. Baja California to supply Alta California

author: Bucareli
summary: Real Orden from José de Gálvez (Secretario de Indias): Presidio of Monterey NOT to be removed from 1769 site. Neve to reside at presidio. Rivera assigned separately

author: Bucareli
summary: Real de Santa Ana church funding. Miners and vecinos obligated to contribute to divine worship and construction

author: Bucareli
summary: Bando prohibiting discussion/dispute on certain topics. 2 copies

author: Bucareli
summary: COOK WARNING. RESERVADO. Royal Order from Gálvez: 'el Capitán Inglés Cook con destino á hacer nuevos descubrimientos' — 2 corvettes left London, likely heading for Pacific/California. 'Si recalan á alguno de aquellos parajes... no las admitan en nuestros puertos.' Maritime governors and especially California governor to be vigilant

author: Investigation testimony
summary: SAN DIEGO ATTACK INVESTIGATION. Indigenous testimony via interpreter. Prisoner named Guapar (baptized, Christian name unknown) from ranchería Moctatí. Named attack leaders: Oroches (organizer, 'culpa de la muerte del Padre'), Miguel (capitanejo of Matí), Matancio Bernardino, Pablo (viejo de San Luis), Francisco. Gathered at ranchería Matí. Planned second attack. Conspirators from 'los del Sur, los de la Sierra'

author: Neophyte testimony
summary: Pre-attack warning: Gentile came at night — 'yo te vengo á avisar que los gentiles de la Punta están enojados... han ido á convidar todos los gentiles de abajo, y toda la Sierra, para venir á pelear, y todos están trabajando flechas.' Rivera investigated, sent Sargento with 9 men to the Point

author: Hijosa (San Blas)
summary: SF founding supply invoices. Paquebot *Príncipe*, Teniente de fragata Diego Choquet — $3,900 for SD. 12 cajones panocha for SF via *San Carlos*. Paquebot *San Carlos*, Teniente de navío Fernando Quirós — supplies for Monterey. pp.543–5

author: Raphael de Pedro y Gil (Monterey Cmdte.)
summary: RATION SCHEDULE. Weekly: single man 1 almud, married without children 2, with children 2½. Frijol: ½ almud per person. Meat: single 3½ lbs, married 4 lbs. Sargento signs weekly. Jamones counted. 'De puño y letra del Cap.n Rivera y Moncada.' p.565

author: Pedro y Gil
summary: José M.a Góngora partitioned King's storehouse (21 x 23 pies) — not enough room. Orders to vacate

author: Pedro y Gil
summary: Girl given 'en calidad de esclava' — María Yguera, mother of Teresa Iguera, delivered to Pedro y Gil. Padres including Fray Fran.co Dumence taking Teresa to Mission Carmel to be married
Monte Pío Militar regulations — widows' pensions, proper surnames, articles 7–9

author: Rivera y Moncada
summary: Baja California troop reports. Livestock at El Rosario. Alférez José Velasquez and Cabo López with 6 men moving livestock to SD. Servant José Duarte (tonsured) abandoned assignment

author: Rivera y Moncada
summary: Troop distribution: 6 at Santo Domingo, Alférez with 3 at El Rosario, 3 at San Fernando. 'Cada una es frontera' — each mission is a frontier. Need more escorts for new establishments

author: Indigenous testimony
summary: INDIGENOUS RESISTANCE: 'PP y soldados son demonios que han venido á perder su tierra; que desde que vinieron no llueve ni cojen semillas.' Also: they are 'diablos que han venido á perderles las familias en sus tierras.' Violence at arroyo de Santa Mateo. R.P. Fr. Pablo de Mugártegui confirms. Orders to Sarg.to Mariano Carrillo

author: Investigation
summary: End of indigenous rebellion investigation. Sarg.to Francisco Aguiar, prisoner soldier Juan de Duna. Ramón el Borjino knew of plot to kill padres. Pedro Puy and others counseled silence. Proceedings sent to Juzgado de Loreto under Capt. Rivera. 19 absolved; rest guilty of 'crimen de rebelión y tentativa de asesinato.' pp.419–57

author: Caballero de Croix (Chihuahua)
summary: Bando prohibiting troops from selling arms, munitions to vecinos. p.58

author: Croix (Arispe)
summary: Instructions on California frontiers. Grievances are 'imaginarios perjuicios.' Then: instructions for a captain

author: Croix (Arispe)
summary: Deserter pardons: soldiers José Beltrán, Antonio Alegre, Francisco Villagomez, Franc.o M.a Ruiz, servants José Guerrero, Mariano Yepes. Deserted Aug 13, 1779. pp.499–80

author: Croix (Arispe)
summary: ADMIRAL HUGHES INTELLIGENCE: Real Orden re British Admiral Hughes departing England for East Indies, March 1779. Anglo-Spanish War intelligence passed to California
Survivor deposition — José Reyes Pacheco (Soldier, Presidio del Altar), from San Pedro y San Pablo, age 22
Survivor deposition — Pedro Solares (Soldier), from Purísima Concepción
Survivor deposition — Miguel Antonio Romero (Soldier, Presidio de San Carlos de Buenavista), from San Pedro y San Pablo
Survivor deposition — Mathias de Cuello (Settler (poblador)), from Purísima Concepción, age 46
Survivor deposition — Juan José Mirandes (Carpenter, settler), from San Pedro y San Pablo, age 33
Survivor deposition — José Ygnacio de Bengochea (Jalchedun Indian, raised at Presidio del Altar), from San Pedro y San Pablo, age 20
Survivor deposition — José Mora (Indian interpreter, salaried by the King; raised by Bernardo de Urrea), from San Pedro y San Pablo

author: Ramón Antonio Saavedra y Guyralda
summary: 'Pedido de cebo y saleas': for provisional relief, send 12 @ tallow + 32 saleas on the Real Hacienda account until the San Blas commandant fulfills the earlier full order

author: Saavedra
summary: Asks to keep mason Salvador Rivera off the *Aránzazu* (which continues its voyage once a replacement comes); embark the Nootka personnel + the convalescent sick carried by the *San Carlos* & *Princesa Real*; send the requested medicines/utensils via the *Aránzazu*

author: Saavedra
summary: 'Pedido de barro y recomendaciones': from aboard the *Concepción* — send Alférez de Navío Juan Matute 4 loads of clay for the forge; supply Nootka commandant Francisco Eliza the aid he requested, with urgency

author: (sin firma)
place: Presidio de San Diego / San Francisco
summary: 'Índice de los documentos que se recibieron'; note on the cattle remaining for the troop's subsistence (incl. the dispersed herd from the Monterey party); extractos de revista y estado de fuerza (May/Jun/Jul)

author: (Goycoechea)
summary: Verification that the Bulas de la Santa Cruzada sumarios for the prior biennium were burned per S.E.'s order; forwards 2 soldiers' marriage-license requests (not endorsed by the comandante but recommended)

author: Felipe de Goycoechea
summary: Tobacco/cigar consumption (10,710 cigar-papers) + apology for the late report (tobacco ran short); compliance re dead soldiers + a Guevara woman; names Sgt. José María Ortega to manage the interests, Tte. Francisco Ortega for orders; penalties applied to a soldier of Luis Romero's company per ordenanza

author: Sgt. Francisco de Ortega
summary: Request for laborers (the missionaries refused, citing the Presidente's permission) + a site survey: eastward view + ganado mayor & horses; southward 6,000 head + a mare-herd, 'campo muy fértil'; varas-measurements from old mission to new + the río [San Gabriel] crossings

author: Goycoechea
summary: Forwards Soldado Nieto's instancia (delivered by Tte. José de Zúñiga)

author: Goycoechea
summary: Soldado Pedro Regalado Peralta + paisano Pedro Romero pass to that presidio w/ their wives; marriage permit asked for Soldado Felipe González w/ the widow Felipa de la Cruz; cigar stock (1 of 3 boxes left, tobacco scarce)

author: Goycoechea
summary: Orders received; only ½ box cigars + same tobacco + 10 boxes powder left; will pass the permission for Soldado Guevara's wife to go to the pueblo; Soldado Pedro Romero at San Diego sold a milpa cheaply to Soldado Zúñiga

author: Goycoechea
themes: criminal; homicide; indigenous; missions; san-buenaventura → indian-raiding/mission-justice
summary: Indigenous homicide / mission-justice case: diligencias vs the indio Manuel (neophyte of Mission San Buenaventura) for killing the indio Francisco with a stone — they had made a bet over a measure of abalones while working, quarreled, fought 'á pedradas'; Francisco struck in the leg, sick 23 days, then died; 6 witnesses; the accused's answers conform; Manuel sent to the calabozo pending superior order

author: Goycoechea
summary: On whether to give Juan Matute's surgeon an escort to his ship

author: Goycoechea
summary: Being sick, did not send the extractos & usual papers

author: Goycoechea
summary: Arrival of the frigate *Aránzazu* (20th) + departure of chaplain Alejandro Jordan (frigate *Princesa*) for San Diego

author: Goycoechea
summary: Notice of the imprisonment of the indio neophyte [Manuel] in the calabozo for killing another indio with a stone (cf. Doc 11)

author: Goycoechea
summary: Nothing extraordinary at the presidio

author: Goycoechea
summary: Of 3 reses en route to Monterey, only 2 arrived (one guided sick on the road)

author: Goycoechea
summary: 'Aviso de cumplimiento de órdenes' (continues n11)

author: Goycoechea
summary: Having received the order, will draw up the account of the leftover Bulas de la Santa Cruzada to burn them per regulation, before the chaplain & officers

author: Romeu (copy, cert. 29 Dec); orders signed Romeu, José Argüello, Pablo Cota, José de Zúñiga
summary: Copy of a Royal letter announcing the birth of the King's children, permitting customary celebrations; the 7 Jan 1792 orders for Misa solemne, triple artillery salute, illuminations; the R. Cédula received 7 Jan & published at the presidios (the four commanders each certifying)

author: (sin firma)
summary: Consent to various measures taken on *reos* + request for several data for reports

author: Hermenegildo Sal
summary: Sends the accounts he formed in triplicate (p.40); the cuentas drawn in view of the muster Alférez Ramón Lasso [de la Vega] takes as discharging, cert. by Ramón Luis de la Vega (pp.46–49); aviso that the R. Cédula copies + Comandante-General oficios are now archived at the presidio (p.50)

author: Sal
summary: 'Aviso sobre limosnas': a money-*colecta* authorized for the Religiosas Capuchinas; 32 pesos collected at the SF presidio

author: Sal
summary: Reviewing the habilitación's existencias, found boxes for which he paid a 13 p 6 r factura, asks how to cover it (p.53); + an extracto de factura (7 cajones, 2 luncales[?], 19 cabeceras = 19 p 6 r, pp.54–55)

author: (sin firma)
summary: A presidio soldier's *demanda* for permission to keep a chinchorrito (small fishing net) by the caballada; Sal forwards it to the Governor

author: Sal
summary: Having no cash, cannot pay 2 libranzas totaling $1,400 — asks the Gov. to approve them paid by factor José Arbide; + a $32 libranza to the principal Correos office in Mexico; + a carta de cuentas (continues)

author: Sal
summary: Sends the account of the armament he holds + its state + the horses & mules (pp.63–64) → presidio-decay/artillery; + asks measures to count the cattle for year-end accounts (pp.65–67); + order to Juan Bernal re 40 head of ganado mayor (pp.68–69)

author: Sal
summary: Alta in the extracto to recruit José Antonio Sánchez (p.70); sends extractos de revista y estado de fuerza (p.71); one of the 2 presidio missionaries, Fr. Antonio Danti, leaves for Santa Clara (p.72)

author: Sal
summary: Alta to 2 recruits (Tapia & Juan García); marriage permits sought for 2 soldiers; 'aviso de pedidos' — asks Tte José Argüello for cloth, thread, blankets, hats for the troop

author: Sal
summary: 8 mulas sent to Monterey for 8 pieces of the *memorias* (p.78); communication of the Bulas de la Santa Cruzada burned for the 1788–89 biennium (p.79); errors in José de Arbide's facturas (pp.80–81)

author: Sal
summary: 'Pedido de dinero': having sold 97 nutria (otter) skins in Mexico for 833 pesos, asks that the money be sent [SF-presidio otter trade → otter-economy]; + Fr. Martín Landaeta stays at Mission Santa Clara (p.84)

author: Sal
summary: Arrival of the frigate *Aránzazu* + the *memorias* of Mexico (pp.86–87); leaving Monterey, left the company sergeant in charge (p.88); a horse + a soldier's saddle & cape lost at Monterey (p.89)

author: Sal
summary: Sal: arrival at SF, no novelty (p.90); extractos (p.91). Goycoechea (22 Dec): sent Alférez Pablo Cota to San Buenaventura to encuartelar (quarter) the troop (pp.92–93)

author: Goycoechea
summary: Goycoechea: horses lost (searched, not found) + a fire (principio de incendio) fought with the soldiers' help (pp.94–97); received documents (p.98). Then a money matter (a la vuelta) signed H. Sal, Pedro Amador, Luis Peralta, José Manuel Boronda

author: Sal/Arbide; Felipe de Jesús Álvarez; Arrillaga
summary: Certificate re 972 p 2 r of soap in Arbide's factura (pp.99–100); Álvarez's *queja* — bought 7 fanegas of maize from Juan Manuel Rosas, only 3 delivered (pp.101–102); ➡ Arrillaga (30 Oct 1791, Loreto) sends documents from Mexico (p.103) — START of the ARRILLAGA / LORETO section

author: Prudencio Ruiz de Esquino/Arrillaga; Mendívola[?]
summary: Correo account to the capital (528 p 48 c in his favor, pp.104–106); a Mexico copy on paying correo accounts at the per-league round-trip rate (pp.107–108); + Arrillaga (24 Oct) on missionaries leaving (cont.)

author: Arrillaga
summary: Missionaries depart for Mexico to be replaced (pp.109–110); the frigate *Princesa* leaves for San Blas w/ missionary José Armesto[?] (p.111); sends Aug & Sept extractos de revista (p.112)

author: Arrillaga
summary: Reports the *Princesa* arrived with missionaries + 'un naturalista y un botánico enviados por S.M. para hacer colecciones para el gabinete de historia natural' (a royal naturalist + botanist — the Sessé/Longinos-era royal botanical expedition; cf. the 'Dr. José Lon[ginos]' at ~n200); + memorias & recruits' leave for San Diego (pp.114–115)

author: Arrillaga (copia)
summary: Soldier Antonio Nazario to be separated (10-yr voluntary enganche completed, pp.116–117); leave for Soldado Antonio Vray[?] if he presents a replacement (p.118), then voided for the distance between presidios (p.119)

author: Arrillaga
summary: Death of Alférez José María Estrada (his estate to his wife + 3 masses, pp.120–121); Caravantes asks leave to go work at Santa Bárbara (p.122); + a correos notice (cont.)

author: Esquino
summary: Correo-cost charges (pp.123–124); Estrada: the frigate *Princesa* arrived with the presidios'/missions' memorias worth … (cont. n33)

author: Arrillaga
summary: …7,663 p 5¼ r; + return of the correo Zaguí[?] who carried the pliegos de Nuca (Nootka) (pp.125–126); wrote to the correos admin + presidio commandant w/ requested docs (p.127); the correo José Marqués[?] arrived w/ pliegos for the Viceroy + Comandante de San Blas directed by the Comandante de Nuca (Nootka) (p.128)

author: Arrillaga
summary: The *Princesa* arrived w/ 4 Dominican friars (p.129); transmits a Viceroy document (p.130); of 11 potros for the frontier soldiers, one stayed in the Canal de Santa Bárbara — distribution (cont.)

author: Arrillaga
summary: Horse-loss reclaim (pp.131–132); list + estado de fuerza of the company (p.133); a soldier come from Mission San Fernando w/o orders (pretext of illness) arrested, but his illness ordered attended (pp.134–135)

author: Goycoechea
summary: Received the communication re the Viceroy's order on foreign ships (p.136); the accounts/gratificaciones/armament/vestuario documents will be ready + the troop is being instructed in arms-handling (p.137)

author: Goycoechea; Arrillaga
summary: Soldiers leave + one sick; Indians not sent (bad weather); year-end docs to follow (p.138); Arrillaga sends extractos + agrees re temp-leave orders (p.139, cont.)

author: Arrillaga
summary: Will publish the Royal Order that frees the *Comercio de pieles de Nutria* (sea-otter pelts) to private individuals (p.141) — a 1791 liberalization of the otter trade (cf. the 1787 Vasadre monopoly *bando*); + received the order on the uniform for retired officers (p.143)

author: Arrillaga; José Argüello; J. B. Matute
summary: Governor-elect Romeu + family arrived (p.144); Argüello forwards *Aránzazu* correspondence + a Fages-oficio determination (p.145); ⭐ Matute's *Reservado* (12 Jun, aboard the *Aránzazu* at Monterey): the King [via Madrid] orders the Nootka port NOT be abandoned — without this ship's supply 'queda ese establecimiento expuesto' (pp.149–150)

author: Romeu
summary: Limits on what may be charged to the troop (título 13, pp.154–156); copy of the R. Cédula (17 Feb) permitting the Capuchin nuns of Granada to collect alms for 2 yrs via the viceroys/governors (pp.157–159; cf. Sal's 32-p colecta, Doc 23)

author: Romeu (copy)
themes: skilled-labor; missions; indigenous; governorship; san-francisco → presidio-decay/mission-economy
summary: The 1791 artisan-instruction program: by govt order, workers go to San Francisco to teach the *naturales* their trades — 1 albañil, 1 cantero + 2 oficiales de cantero, 1 maestro sastre + 2 oficiales de sastre, 3 herreros (the last a blacksmith condemned to 10 yrs presidio, unpaid); their wages + portions to wives stated (pp.160–162). + Romeu takes possession of the governorship, relieving Coronel Pedro Fages; Pedro de Nava takes the Provincias Internas (p.153)

author: Romeu
summary: Received 16 oficios + estado de fuerzas; instructions re the Capuchin alms (pp.164–172); + ⭐ Construction instructions — how to build a church, walls, bridges, corrals, patios, forge; the materials + the *enmaderamiento*; the church (most important) left last until full completion (pp.173–179)

author: Romeu
recipient: SF comandante; Fages → José F. Ortega
summary: Governor's forensic-verification order on the Manuel/Francisco stone-homicide (Doc 11): witnesses + a competent man to verify whether the victim was truly dead and the stone truly the cause (pp.180–181); + Fages orders Ortega to hand the habilitación to José Argüello (>3 yrs served) w/ the correspondence, tobacco-factor accounts, Correos & Real Hacienda accounts (p.184)

author: Pedro Fages
summary: Departure instructions: the church & walls work continues in his absence, the troop hauls materials, the surgeon attends the sick (pp.183–186); + suspend the prior-year revista in the book, use Sal's Resumen General + 3 pliegos (p.187)

author: Francisco Eliza
summary: From the *Concepción*: unload the ship asap + send a blacksmith and a mason (p.188); list of effects Matute should carry — lime, bricks, tejas/adobes, clay, a herrero & an albañil (p.189); list of cattle & grains for Nootka (p.190)

author: Eliza
summary: Eliza: get the pliegos to the King + Comandante de Loreto w/ diligence (p.191); ⭐ Argüello's ship-sailing instructions: leave Nootka asap, embark the volunteers, carry the effects; replacements can't be given; the mason already assigned cannot go — he is found working on presidio works (pp.192–195) [labor-shortage competition Nootka vs. presidio]

author: Pedro de Nava
summary: The *ayudante* post requested by his predecessor cannot be conferred (suppressed by S.M.); company state to stand until new order (pp.194–195); Vicente Feliz[?] denied sergeant-of-invalids (only 18 yrs service) but named cabo for good conduct (pp.196–197)

author: Nava
summary: Send the revistas in quadruplicate (omit the mass of relaciones, pp.198–199); on invalids — gave course to Rafael Villavicencio's short-sight licencia, but licenses no one without prior proposal (pp.200–201)

author: Nava
summary: The Juez Gral. de Bienes de Difuntos (Audiencia de Guadalajara) grants the territorial judges faculties to collect small debts owed (pp.202–203); + forwards Arrillaga's representation on relieving the troop at Mission San Miguel (p.204)

author: José Mª Salgado, Manuel de Villa, Ignacio Serrano; Arrillaga
recipient: Cmdte. Gral. Jacobo Ugarte y Loyola
themes: labor-coercion; missions; soldiers; founding; baja-california; san-miguel
summary: Labor-coercion dossier (Baja): 3 workers report (via Tte. Diego González) that, transferred to work in the houses of the families assisting the troop, they were imprisoned — they argue working for the soldiers 'no presta al Rey ningún servicio' (p.205); + Arrillaga on the custom that soldiers build new missions (defense works, guard, stores, church, friar's house), and the founding of Mission San Miguel: begun 1787 at the paraje del Encino, moved ~3 leagues to the Arroyo de San Juan Bautista where it stands; asks what to do re the soldiers imprisoned by the Comte. de San Diego for refusing to work in the escort-families' private houses (pp.206–209)

author: Pedro de Nava
summary: 'Aviso de restitución de dinero' — his predecessor (oficio 1 Jun 1790 Nº642) reported having made … (cont. n53)

author: Pedro de Nava
summary: Satisfy the Correos admin of Álamos the 250 pesos cost of the extraordinary courier carrying Real-Servicio pliegos (pp.210–211); gave course to Tte. Diego González's licencia (oficio Nº586, 8 Jul 1789) to pass to the gentilidad or a presidio (p.212); + a circular on carrying sword & insignia of grade at all times (cont.)

author: Nava
summary: Forwards + transcribes the verbatim Royal Decree regulating the *gracia de inválidos* (disability/retirement for sgts & soldiers): the King, noting excessive invalid proposals, fixes 5 points — (I) 18 yrs good service + real ailment; (II) 18 yrs even if only 'tired' (to the Jefes' discretion); (III) under 18 yrs but disabled in action of war / march / garrison / escort / aid to justice / pursuit of contrabandists & malefactors, certified by the immediate Jefe + regiment surgeon; (IV) involuntary illness under 18 yrs; (V) bad-conduct/own-fault → absolute discharge per ordenanza

author: Nava
summary: Gave course to the retirement request of Tte. José Francisco Ortega (Loreto presidio; oficio Nº610, 7 Sept 1789)

author: Nava
summary: Sends the R. Cédula giving thanks for the birth of the Infanta María Teresa (16 Feb 1791, 10:30 a.m.), permitting customary demonstrations (cf. Doc 20's royal-birth cluster)

author: Nava
summary: 'Decreto sobre porte de uniforme': via Revillagigedo (7 Feb) ← Conde de Campo Alange (14 Oct) ← R.O. to the Capitán Gral. de Cataluña (Conde del Asalto) — grants governors & sergeant-majors the chupa with the wide galón, the casaca cuff-fold omitted, aides limited button-holes, kept for gala days

author: Pedro de Nava
summary: 'Carta de aviso' (cont. n61)

author: Pedro de Nava
summary: Officers' 1789 hojas de servicios remain in the Comandancia/Inspección (quad., oficio Nº630, p.226); last revista-de-inspección extractos remain (p.227); + an Antonio-Valdés order (cont.)

author: Nava
summary: R.O.: invalids (retired, non-disperso) ARE subject to Army penalties for theft, insubordination & wounds (the prior 'concepto' that they were exempt corrected)

author: Nava
summary: R.O.: no more recursos on relief of the *Montado y Galeras* service or the 200-ducado license to marry in the Indies; none of these to holders of civil employments

author: Nava
summary: R.O. on an arrest-of-subordinate case (a Guadalajara-infantry subaltern appealed his arrest to the King; the reprimand judged just, his resistance punishable): superiors to act with prudence; proceso only in grave cases

author: Nava
summary: Acknowledges the Santa Bárbara & San Diego revista-de-inspección documents (oficios Nº522/523, Aug 1788); company state pending, gave course

date_text: 1791-08-22 (copy cert. J. A. Argüello, 28 Jul 1792, Monterey)
author: Nava
summary: R.O. 'sobre abonos': on Juan Caballero's petition (Regto. Caballería del Infante; merit at the Sitio de Gibraltar) — soldiers w/ *licencia absoluta* who re-present voluntarily in their own corps get previous service credited toward premios/inválidos after 6 revistas (vs. 4 for quintos, 3 for volunteers)

author: Nava
summary: R.O.: those with fuero militar giving declarations the Tribunal del Santo Oficio (Inquisition) requests — the Comisario applies via the individual's immediate Jefe

author: Nava
summary: R.O.: quinto soldiers w/ *licencia absoluta* returning to their regiment (after 4 revistas de ausencia) credited prior service toward premios (R. decreto 14 Oct 1766 / 20 Mar 1767); non-quintos likewise but not having missed 3 revistas

author: Nava
summary: R.O. 'sobre desertores': the oath to the flag is NOT required to punish a deserter by ordenanza; soldiers who never swore it are to do so at the next revista de Comisario (tít. 8, trat. 3)

author: Nava
summary: R.O. on the hoja-de-servicios format: services in *extract* (companies/actions/advantages, not naming destacamento commanders); captains sign the libretas; separate pliego of merit-notes; a *libro de servicios* only for the most senior sergeants

author: Nava
summary: R.O. 'sobre el sueldo de oficiales': no officer with an employment/destiny assigned by reglamento may claim the *sueldo de grado* (pp.351–353); + an order 'sobre encarcelamiento de inocentes' begun (cont. n71)

author: Nava
summary: R.O. via the innocent-fisherman case: at Alicante, José Porras (*pescador*) + his son were jailed for having 'captured' 6 deserting Flandes-infantry soldiers (1770) — the soldiers had seized his boat by force; the Consejo de Guerra & King order the boat returned and both freed *'en libertad libremente y sin costos.'* ⭐ Copyist's note: the innocents spent 5–6 months in jail, and the 1770 order reached the Gov. of California in 1791 — '21 años después.'

author: Nava
summary: Duplicate of the *abono* R.O. (= Doc 72, 'página 64 de estos extractos,' pp.257–259); + an *advertencia* that Fages-forwarded instancias lacked the required margin-note of circumstances (p.260); + a 'deudas de provistos' order (cont.)

author: Nava
summary: R.O.: creditors of newly-provided officeholders in the Indies to seek the Tribunales, not the Ministerio de Indias (pp.261–263); + ⭐⭐ ([1793] Loreto arsenal) — Capt. Arrillaga had *suspended the repair of the Loreto arsenal* over a misunderstood Fages permission-order of 3 Jun 1786 (cont.)

author: Nava
summary: Clarifies the Loreto arsenal *almacén* repair may proceed w/ the 400-peso allocation (Art.7 tít.2 of the reglamento), justified per Art.21 tít.13 — transmitted to Arrillaga (pp.264–265); + ⭐ the Luzón-regiment deserter José Antonio Pedraza (took indulto, left Manila 1785 on the frigate *San José de Filipinas*) found in CA — if his conduct is bad, send him to San Blas (p.266)

author: Nava
summary: Santa Bárbara presidio state (supposed already concluded; the San Francisco, San Diego & Monterey troops who worked on it + their gratificaciones, pp.267–268); + an inválido proposal for SF soldier Juan [Sta?] Altamirano — query whether still alive (cont.)

author: Nava
summary: Altamirano query (p.269); + ⭐ approves the translation of Real-Hacienda cattle from Mission San Francisco to the Llano de Monterey (Fages oficio 27 May, p.370); + acknowledges the state of 'those new establishments' (p.371)

author: Nava
summary: R.O. on habilitado insolvency (*quiebra*): arrest the defaulting habilitado (2 r/day), purify the shortfall and charge it *prorrata* among officers/chaplain/troop, embargo his goods → Caja del fondo de gratificación; publish before the companies every 3 months

author: Pedro de Nava & Manuel Merino
summary: Carta de aviso re a bando against disorders in the sale/barter of bestias (pp.275–276); + the bando itself — re-publishes the Caballero de Croix bando of 22 Mar 1778 barring anyone from buying/selling/pawning troop animals, arms, monturas, vestuario; animals already bought to be presented & branded before the inspector de presidios Tte. Cnl. *Diego de Borica* (Sonora: Roque de Medina); term 4 mo. (>100 leagues) / 2 mo. (less) [⭐ Borica before he was CA governor] (cont. n80)

author: Nava/Merino
summary: End of the livestock bando: round-up procedure; cepo 8 days for soldiers in default (cites the Caballero de Croix order of 23 Jun 1779); Indians who violate → 15 days' jail; animals found in private hands seized + 10-peso fine each (16 for Spaniards); published in all provinces

author: Conde de Revilla Gigedo + Pedro de Nava
summary: NOOTKA / OPEN DOOR (heading reads *'Sobre prisiones'* — ⚠ mismatch; content is the fishing-vessel order): transmits Floridablanca's R.O. of 25 Sept [1790] *'sobre que no se hagan hostilidades á embarcaciones de pesca inglesas ú otras'* (no hostilities to English or other fishing vessels — the post-Nootka-Convention free-navigation rule), with the added caveat that this does NOT extend to those 'haciendo contrabando, ni introducir á los establecimientos que se intenten formar en el mar del Norte ó en el del Sur ó Pacífico á la parte interior de las costas.' ✅ verified scale 2. → THESIS-CRITICAL #3

author: Nava
recipient: Gov.; Romeu (copy); "Yo el Rey" / Antonio Ventura de Taranco
summary: Forwards + transcribes the R. Cédula of 21 Dec 1787 on jurisdiction over *causas de concubinato* (mixed fuero church vs. royal courts; cites the 1771/1777 cédulas + Obispo de Plasencia). ⭐ Circulation-compliance certs name the four presidio commanders, 1792: Hermenegildo Sal (SF, 26 Feb), José Argüello (Monterey, 28 Jul), Felipe Goycoechea (Sta Bárbara, 8 Aug), Juan Pablo Grijalva (San Diego, 14 Aug); + the printed cédula (pp.292–294)

author: Pedro Amador; Felipe Barry; cert. Sal
themes: registry; biography; service-record; pedro-amador; san-francisco; loreto → military-history
summary: Pedro Amador — Expediente de Servicios: his petition to credit the 8 yrs 4 mos he served at Loreto (soldado/cabo/sargento; separated 11 Dec 1773 for having married); Fages's margin-deferral (SF 13 May 1791, 'vaya á donde le convenga'); the 1773 Loreto passport from Felipe Barry (sargento mayor de Caballería, Intendente RH y Comandante de la Península de California); and his ⭐⭐ 1791 filiación (cert. Sal): native of *Tecuala*, son of Juan Amador & María Josefa de Carpio, enlisted Apr 1764, separated from Loreto 1784 (order of Ayudante Inspector Nicolás Soler), ascended sargento in the SF company 20 Jul 1787

author: Nava
summary: R. Cédula: Ministerio de Marina subalterns (Comisario de Provincia rank, real nombramiento) to declare under word of honor in military & political tribunals

author: Nava
summary: R. Cédula: in the Indies/Filipinas/Barlovento, cofradías/hermandades/congregaciones may hold no junta without being presided by a Real Minister

author: Nava
summary: R. Cédula on the Pragmática de Matrimonios: denies extending José Lebrun's *Anotaciones* to the Indies; observe the Pragmática + Recopilación laws literally

author: Nava
summary: R. Cédula on ministers fulfilling their obligations (R. decreto 23 Dec; extended to America/Filipinas)

author: Nava
summary: Approves naming a mayordomo + 5 vaqueros for the Real-Hacienda cattle moved from San Francisco to Monterey (Romeu oficio Nº582, 7 Jul 1789; cf. Doc 83)

author: Nava
summary: Invalids: credit only what the 13 Jun 1773 reglamento marks (Campo de Alange, pp.318–319); + R. Cédula 19 Aug 1789 on junta presidency (Antequera de Oaxaca diezmos — Vireyes/Gobernadores preside even w/ prelates as vocales, pp.321–323)

author: Pedro de Nava
summary: Sends 11 conceded invalid-cédulas + asks the afiliaciones of soldiers Antonio Peña, Antonio de Acosta, Miguel Rubio (San Diego co.) & Luis Lugo (Sta Bárbara) (p.324); + the despacho for Tte. Diego González's retirement (Monterey presidio) (p.325)

author: Nava
summary: Order 'sobre Correos y Conversaciones políticas entre soldados': soldiers employed as couriers who leak the contents of letters entrusted to them, or discuss 'cosas de paz y guerra,' to be punished (cont. n96) [security/discipline]

author: Pedro de Nava
summary: ⚠ BOUNDED ROUTINE RUN (per efficiency rule 6): a long stretch of printed general-empire royal cédulas transmitted by de Nava — zero California specificity, so noted as a block rather than leaf-by-leaf. Sampled contents: ministers/Real-patronato provision + a Durango/Sonora *doctrinas* dictamen (n100); ⭐ indulto general del delito de contrabando (a Viceroy's bando pardoning the contraband crime for non-homicides, n105); requirements for títulos de Castilla / hidalguía (n110); ⭐ the Bourbon cemetery-reform cédula (burials out of churches for public health, from Gov. Ezpeleta of Cuba's 1787 report, n115). ⏳ If item-level completeness is ever needed, read n96–n119 leaf-by-leaf; for now characterized + bounded.

author: Goycoechea
summary: Sgt. José Olivera to the Purísima Concepción escolta; Soldado Miguel Flores carries Carabantes/Montalbán avíos (p.424); + resumen de fondos de Gratificación/retención + cuenta de municiones (p.425)

author: Goycoechea
summary: Informed of the R.O. on licencias temporales for officers (p.426); + ⭐⭐ informed that S.M. ordered the *acopio de pieles de Nutria* suspended 'por quedar libre este comercio' (p.427) — the Santa Bárbara end of the 1791 otter-trade liberalization (corroborates Doc 47)

author: Goycoechea
summary: Cabo Ignacio Vallejo delivered the San Diego/frontera *potros* (1 died); Sgt. José Ortega conducts 20 to the frontera; ⭐ Cabo Vallejo & Mariano Castro to return after their marriages (both about to wed, p.428); + a prisoner remitted via Juan José Tapia + 4 men to Grijalva at San Diego (p.429)

author: Goycoechea
summary: Correo movements (Miguel Flores → San Diego; soldiers Juan Mª Pinto, Dionisio Bernal, Dolores Mesa return, p.430); + sends the year's cuentas generales + ⭐ an estado del pueblo (goods of the SB pobladores & vecinos, p.431)

author: Goycoechea
summary: Forwards the 1791 election of the alcalde of the pueblo de la Reyna de los Ángeles (Los Angeles), 'á pluralidad de votos,' for approval (p.432); + Verdugo's voter-list (who voted & for whom, for alcalde + 1st/2nd regidor; formed by order of Goycoechea) (p.433)

author: Arrillaga
summary: Romeu formally put in possession as Gobernador político y militar de ambas Californias + inspector general de presidios (per the 26 Feb order); certification to follow to the Virrey

author: Fr. Juan Crisóstomo Gómez
summary: Certificate of the founding of Mission Santo Tomás de Aquino (Dominican, Baja Calif., 26 Apr 1791, paraje 'antes de San Solano[?]'): baptisms of gentile children + 20 adult gentiles; Fr. José Loriente named director

author: Goycoechea
summary: No libranzas against the gastador (p.437); + ⭐ punished (per the 4 Apr order) cabo Francisco María Ruiz [future SD comandante] & soldier Francisco Lugo (p.438)

author: Revilla Gigedo
summary: NOOTKA/OPEN DOOR (corroborates THESIS-CRITICAL #3): by his 25 May letter, Floridablanca acknowledged the Gov.'s compliance with the order that 'no se hiciesen hostilidades á las embarcaciones de pesca inglesas ú otras, ni á establecimientos que se hallasen en costas desamparadas' — the post-Nootka fishing-vessel rule applied & confirmed at the CA level (pp.439–440); + Goycoechea's April/May extractos de revista (p.441)

author: Goycoechea; José Zúñiga
summary: SB Gratificación/retención cuentas (p.441½); SD libranzas order (p.442); + a 'caldo y fruta' matter (cont.)

author: Goycoechea
summary: Cornelio Avilés's wine/fruit transport in New California (p.443); + acknowledges Romeu's possession of the Gobierno (16 Apr, Loreto, p.445); + Pedro Nava's possession of the Comandancia Gral. (cont.)

author: Goycoechea
summary: Acknowledges Nava's Comandancia + Romeu's Gobierno (Loreto, pp.446–447); + ⭐ a 'Notas de documento' meta-index (Nº33–39, 62): tropa-ajustes; ajustes/ayudante-inspector Soler; memorias via Comtes. de Marina; Nº36 = the corvettes *Descubierta y Atrevida* arrived at Monterey; Nº37 = Mission Santo Tomás founding; Nº38 = the goleta *Saturnina* passing to San Blas; Nº62 = cemeteries cédula; Nº39 = the artisans (p.448)

author: Revilla Gigedo
summary: R.O. (Campo de Alange): the King approves the long-pending expediente on the *ajustes* (pay-settlements) of the California presidio troops

author: Revilla Gigedo
summary: San Diego *ajustes*: 4,700 pesos alcance to the Real Hacienda; that sum tied up in credits w/ other presidios + ayudante-inspector Nicolás Soler, and a full year's situado advanced to San Diego (Loreto & SD a year in debt) — wants the practice explained

author: Revilla Gigedo
summary: Artisan-instruction program (cf. Doc 50): the *pliego de asiento de los artesanos destinados á la enseñanza de indios* to be registered; their families supported via the Guadalajara treasury; the convict José Antonio Ocampo sent to replace the deserter blacksmith of that presidio; the Audiencia de Guadalajara to send artisans guilty of lesser crimes to the peninsula

author: Revilla Gigedo
summary: Socorros to the presidios *with a stop at Nootka* — deliver the memorias 'sin hacer el tabaco' (p.454); + ⭐⭐⭐ acknowledges the arrival at MONTEREY of the corvettes *Descubierta y Atrevida* (the Malaspina Expedition, Sept 1791) + the aid given them to continue the voyage (p.455; cf. C-A 15 Malaspina@Monterey)

author: Revilla Gigedo
summary: Acks the founding of Mission Santo Tomás de Aquino — ⚠️ here dated *24 April* (vs Doc 117's 26 April; flag) (p.456); + ⭐⭐⭐ acks that the Gov., arriving the 13th, found the goleta *Saturnina* 'procedente de Nuca [Nootka]' heading to San Blas (p.457; the *Saturnina* = the Límites-expedition schooner, later the 1792 SF Cantil-Blanco friendly-fire ship — Bodega/SF-defense thread); + a printed cédula on the *indulto del delito de bebidas prohibidas* (dup of p.111, pp.458–459)

author: Santa María y Acevedo (Real Tribunal de la Acordada); Revilla Gigedo
summary: Acordada: form the list of *tenientes/cabos/comisarios/cuadrilleros* (the rural-police roster; Tte. Juan A. de Cuevas never answered, pp.460–461); + repair the Loreto presidio church (p.462)

author: Revilla Gigedo
summary: 5,200 pesos to habilitado-general Manuel Cárcaba to gratify the troops who built the presidios (memoria: Monterey 1,600 + the others incl. Sta Bárbara 1,200 each)

author: Revilla Gigedo
summary: S.M. approves Manuel Cárcaba as *habilitado general* (Capt. Inf. Puebla → graded Capt. Cav., 1,200 p/yr); future habilitados to be elected by the presidio officers w/ a 5,000-peso bond (pp.465–466); + ⭐⭐ a carpenter contracted at 10 r/day 'para enseñar á lo menos á 12 indios' of the razón/missions — same terms as the artisans dispatched March 1791 via the Guadalajara intendant (pp.467–468)

author: Revilla Gigedo
summary: Matute authorized to go to Monterey (Nootka supply; leave a substitute, pp.469–470); + ⭐⭐ armament & sacred vessels for the two new Alta-California missions to be founded by the San Francisco friars — *Soledad* & *Santa Cruz* (other missions to lend meanwhile, pp.471–472)

author: Campo de Alange / Revilla Gigedo
summary: The King accepts Coronel Pedro Fages's resignation as Gov. of California and confirms Tte. Cnl. José Antonio Romeu (sargento mayor, Dragones de España) as Governor in propriety. ⚠ Note: this copy was made at the request of Doña Josefa Sandoval, *widow* of the late Gov. Romeu — i.e. Romeu had since died (1792)

author: Revilla Gigedo
summary: Nootka supply: facilitate the auxilios requested by Francisco Wisa[?] & Ramón Saavedra for 'el nuevo establecimiento de Nuca' (charged at San Blas); Saavedra to keep the albañil at Nootka until San Blas sends another; the sick soldiers/sailors + medicine left by the paquebot *Aránzazu* (pp.474–475); + the Loreto-presidio *ajuste* (pp.476–478)

author: Pedro Fages
summary: Proposes a drummer (tambor) for each presidio (brought 1 each + had a boy given 10 lessons); inválido Francisco Vaqueros[?] + wife to San Luis; the Diegueño Indians Gabriel & Constantino employed at the presidio for 3–4 years; sends the Sta Bárbara company year-end docs

author: Fages
summary: Sargento José Pérez Fernández to a vacant plaza; Fages, unable to travel, sends Capt. José Arrillaga to receive 'el mando de la península'; handover docs for the SF-company revista (pp.481–482); + sends 8 Viceroy orders post-dating Romeu's accession (p.483)

author: Pedro Fages
summary: Fages's foot ailment prevents him passing the Sta Bárbara revista (the 2 tenientes doing it, pp.484–485); informed of Nava's Mando General (p.486); received the toma-de-posesión docs from Capt. José Arrillaga to send to the Virrey (p.487)

author: Fages
summary: Fages's handover instructions (Romeu enters via Loreto; leave a state-of-the-province instruction; recap pending points + mission reports, pp.487–488); + ⭐⭐ Fages's 'Índice de documentos' (2 Mar 1791) — a finding-aid to the 1791 California archives he transferred: Archivo de Gobierno Nº1–926, Inspección 1–93, the SF / Sta Bárbara / San Diego presidio archives, 2 mission-state reports, a report 'sobre no convenir que estos presidios se surtan de la Nao de China,' the 1783 Soler Loreto revista, officers'/sgts' hojas de servicios to end-1790 (p.491); + the burned-Bulas cert. for 1788–89 (p.494)

author: Fages
summary: Mission Santo Tomás certification (Fages dates the founding 24 Apr — agrees w/ Doc 127 vs Doc 117's 26 Apr; 24 Apr now the better-attested date, p.496); + Revillagigedo permits Fages 5–6 mules + horses to carry his effects out (p.495)

author: (unsigned; Fages by the hand)
themes: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ governorship; officer-assessment; sal-papers; missions; church-vs-state; indian-raiding; horses; monterey
summary: 'INFORMES PARTICULARES' — Fages's confidential exit assessment to his successor. OFFICERS: José Joaquín Arrillaga 'un buen oficial'; José Zúñiga apt w/ the Diegueños; ⭐ Goycoechea's pretensions — his order to register a few cattle 'fue causa que se mataron dos soldados'; Diego González incompetent ('abuso del mando, falta de inteligencia… no tiene narices ni adiento'); José Argüello good; ⭐ 'para la habilitación solo queda el alférez Sal en quien echar mano, porque los alféreces Grijalva y Cota son casi legos y han dado pruebas de abandono' (Sal the only one fit; Grijalva & Cota nearly illiterate + negligent); only sgt. Vargas useful (his amanuensis). MISSIONARIES: Dominicans OK (pres. Fr. Juan Antonio Gómez); ⭐⭐⭐ the Fernandino (Franciscan) friars 'opuestísimos á las máximas del reglamento y Gobierno… quieren ser independientes y absolutos cada uno en su misión' (fights w/ Padre Sta María at San Buenaventura); ⭐⭐⭐ the San Francisco mission friars 'han hecho nuevo y formal establecimiento á 7 leguas de distancia,' keeping most of the neófitos — and Santa Clara 'lo mismo.' ⭐⭐⭐ The fugitive-Indian-pursuit problem (separate paper 'Confesiones'). ⭐⭐⭐⭐ 'el ganado de las misiones se ha aumentado mucho… que los indios empiecen á encarnizarse… van aprendiéndose y adiestrándole… á manejar el caballo y adquiriendo la insolencia de los apaches' — a 1791 anticipation of the horse-raiding crisis → indian-raiding-article. Closes leaving Romeu the garden/furniture/cattle

author: Pedro Fages
themes: ⭐⭐⭐ missions; friar-abuse; indigenous; santa-clara; de-la-peña; agriculture
summary: Papel de puntos, pts 34–35 (END, signed 'Monterey 26 de Febrero de 1791. Pedro Fages'): (34) ⭐⭐⭐ three Indians (Ortiz, Anacleto Valdés, Antonio Moraga) complained of Fr. Tomás de la Peña (minister of Mission Santa Clara) for the 'escasos violentos' with which he punishes the Indians 'por su duro genio' — Fages himself 'vi haberle sangre á un muchacho á un tiro de oreja' (saw him draw blood from a boy); Fages formed a sumaria and has kept the 3 complainants at Monterey since 1785 w/ no resolution [direct friar-abuse evidence; cf. the 1785 Sta Clara mortality investigation] → missions/indigenous; (35) Fages's orchard (½ legua off, since 1783, ~600 fruit trees).

author: Pedro Fages
themes: ⭐⭐⭐ presidio-construction; presidio-decay; monterey-fire-1789; sal-papers; settlers; los-angeles; criminal; manila-galleon; moraga
summary: Papel de puntos, pts 19–33+: (19–20) ⭐ foreign-ship-trade control — the memorias kept closed while ships are in port (to stop the troops/settlers bartering their clothing in the *cambalache*), commanders to watch the sailors to prevent scandal with the Indians; (21–22) ⭐⭐ the incorrigible LA/San-José settlers — Sebastián Albitre & Francisco Ávila (jailed ~6 yrs for 'excesos con las indias,' then made pobladores; Albitre fled with a Sta Clara neophyte 3 months among the Gentiles; Ávila accused of 'crimen bestial'), José Antonio Navarro (amancebamiento) — prominent early-Angeleno family names; (23) ⭐ José Antonio Pedraza, the Luzón-regiment (Manila) deserter off the 1786 Nao de Filipinas, left on the beach, kept in Fages's garden, later jailed for a crime w/ a Carmelo neophyte (fuller version of Doc 81); (24–27) the officer/habilitación reshuffle after Tte. José Joaquín Moraga's death (SF founder) — ⭐ Sal's 'quiebra' (default) imputed by the ayudante but then cleared, Ramón Lasso's 2nd default (docked to 2 r/day), Ayudante-Inspector Nicolás Soler retired & owing 7,000+ p, then died; (28–29) the Loreto/company revistas; (30) ⭐ 4,000 pesos to each of Monterey/SD/SF to gratify the troops who built the presidios; (31) ⭐⭐ THIS (Monterey) presidio concluded but its *tule-thatch (Jacal de tule)* roofs BURNED 11 Aug 1789 — rebuilding improved, Indians + San José Gentiles volunteering, a church-with-tower replacing the one on the plaza; (32) ⭐⭐⭐ construction state of all 4 presidios: San Francisco 'poco ha adelantado… por la escasez de madera y mala dirección'; Santa Bárbara concluded but a short stretch; San Diego nearly done; (33) the 1789 fire burned Real-Hacienda goods. ⏳ continues past n167

author: Pedro Fages
themes: ⭐⭐⭐ governance; pueblos; land-grants; missions; demography; syphilis; neophyte-flight; church-vs-state; economy; situado; manila-galleon; cattle; horses
summary: 'CARTAPACIO Nº4 — Papel de varios puntos concernientes al Gobierno de la península de California é Inspección de las Tropas' — Fages's comprehensive numbered state-of-the-province memorandum (the formal companion to Doc 139). Points read so far: (1–6) the two pueblos — fomenting San José & Los Ángeles with retired soldiers, the 1784 grain-purchase order, the suertes/solares/ejidos land distribution (San José de Guadalupe relocated off a barranca for fire risk), escoltas kept past the 2-yr limit, Vicente Feliz commissioned at LA from its founding, the annual alcalde/regidor elections under comisionado oversight (orders 1785–86); (7) ⭐⭐ the old missions' demographic decline from the 'Gálico' (syphilis) vs the flourishing new ones; (8) ⭐⭐⭐ neophyte flight + the troop expeditions to recover them ('por la mala dirección de los Curas'), and Fages's preferred method — trusted Indians + gifts to the ranchería captains rather than force; (9–10) ⭐⭐ refusing the friars' escort demands (approved 13 Jan 1790 Nº886) + humane-march instructions; (11) the archive indices; (12) Neve's price-tariffs (aranceles), adjustable by abundance/scarcity; (13) ⭐ the excessive Real-Hacienda cattle → annual *matanza* (cf. the Apache/horse warning, Doc 139); (14) horse/mule breeding off the Sonora line at presidios/pueblos/missions; (15) refusing the missions' attempts to buy military goods from the troops; (16) ⭐ the prohibition on trading with the Manila galleon (Nao de Filipinas) at Cabo San Lucas (+ Bucareli's ban on friars boarding it); (17) the San Blas–California trade duty-free 5 yrs (from 11 Oct 1786); (18) ⭐ this 'comercio pasivo' harmful — the only cash entering is the limited situado for troop pay (cash-scarcity). ⏳ Continues past n159 (points 19–20+ on the *cambalache*/foreign-ship commerce control, per the earlier gap-sample at ~n160).

author: Fages
summary: On remitting artisans (resolve via arts. 32 & 34 of his 7 Jun 1787 report on the new missions); the Virrey's order on the two masons + two tailors to San Blas (pp.532–533); + an officer's leave begun (cont.)

author: Fages
summary: Officer leave: María Bárbara Martínez's son needs to go to México for his patrimony — but his presence is 'utilísima'; Grijalba can sub 1 yr w/ Sgt. Manuel Vargas (pp.534–535); + 'Informes' (26 Apr): forwards Soler's report; the diezmos to the Rosario cajas; ⭐ 18 mare-herds, 1,582 head extracted, 200 broken *potros* distributed to the 5 presidios (cont.)

author: Diego González
summary: 'Muerto de un indio': the Gentile Indians of the Sierra killed a fugitive Christian Indian (of Mission San Gabriel) — asks what to do; + dispatched the cargas left on the beach

author: Diego González
summary: Two *cartas de disculpa* answering the mission minister's complaints against him (didn't send 'el tercero,' didn't order the prisoner removed — that was the presidente; didn't turn the troop against the padre) [friar-vs-military; cf. Fages's dim view of González, Doc 139] (cont.)

author: Diego González
summary: Continues the disculpa (didn't go to the missionary's house, didn't impede the troop working on the padre's works, pp.556–563); + 'cuentas particulares' — disputes several charges in his 1785–90 accounts (pp.565–568)

author: Diego González
summary: Acknowledges orders re the missionaries' representation (pp.559–560); + sent a squad for a presidiario (convict) found at the paraje de la Agua Dulce, to dispatch him to his mission (pp.561–563)

author: Diego González
summary: The Christian Indian's death (cf. Doc 143): he left his ranchería w/ 3 Gentiles, met 6 more, they fought (cause unknown), 1 of the 3 left for dead; + ⭐⭐ 'corre el ruido que los gentiles quieren ir á matar á la gente y llevarse la caballada' (the Gentiles rumored to plan attacking the people & stealing the horses) — echoes Fages's Apache/horse warning (Doc 139); + a welcome to Romeu (pp.566–567)

author: Arrillaga (cert.); the 5 commanders
summary: ⚠ BOUNDED DUPLICATE CLUSTER: a run of 1792 re-certified copies of de Nava/Revillagigedo orders already extracted earlier in Tomo X (at orig. pp.40/49/60–61/62/64/65/76/77/87/94) — re-copied & certified by Arrillaga, each carrying publication certs from the five presidio commanders in 1792: Sal (SF, 26 Feb & 28 Jul), Argüello (Monterey, 22–23 Jul), Goycoechea (Sta Bárbara, 14 Jul & 8 Aug), Grijalva (San Diego, 14 Aug & 26 Nov), Arrillaga (Loreto, 31 May & 14 Oct). Content duplicated; the new info is the province-wide 1792 publication record. Includes the alcabala order (suppressing the 2% war-surtax back to 6%, pp.587–590).

author: Pedro de Nava
summary: Sends the R. Cédula (17 Feb) granting the Capuchin nuns of Granada a 2-yr alms collection (cf. Doc 49)

author: El Rey/Taranco; Revilla Gigedo
summary: A cluster of 1792 cédulas/reglamentos: student/scholar marriages require seminary/bishop consent (Aranjuez 11 Jun 1792); the confraternity-junta cédula (re-pub., cf. Doc 91); the Acordada militia-list request (cf. Doc 128); a 24-article reglamento for the 2 fixed Light-Infantry companies of New Spain (Revillagigedo, 8 May 1792)

author: Revilla Gigedo
themes: royal-cedula; palafox; invalids; economy; missions; arrillaga
summary: The student-marriage bando (p.20); a Zúñiga correo (San Diego, p.21); ⭐ the beatification of Bishop Juan de Palafox y Mendoza — alms-permission extended 4 yrs, published by the 5 commanders 1793 (pp.22–25); the inválidos/Montepío discount cédula (dup, pp.26–30); ⭐ an economy order — cover 'la deuda contraída en la guerra con los ingleses' (pp.31–32); ⭐ the retirement of missionary Fr. Cristóbal Oramas to the Colegio de San Fernando (p.33)

author: El Rey; Revilla Gigedo
summary: ⚠ BOUNDED cédula-run tail: the alcabala war-surtax suppression (re-pub. via Fages/de Nava); ⭐ Gardoqui/Conde de Lerena order permitting Indians to go to Spain to sell *grana* (cochineal) — prompted by a Tributary Indian of Mitla, Oaxaca (an indigenous-agency/trade item, but not CA); the uniform/sword cédula. General-empire; gap-sampled.

author: Arrillaga (via de Nava ← Revilla Gigedo)
themes: exploration; france; dentrecasteaux; laperouse; foreign-ships; family-compact; baja-california
summary: Orders to give 'cuantos auxilios necesiten' to the French Royal Navy ships searching for the lost *Lapérouse* expedition: S.M., on the petition of D. Augusto de Urtubise[?], directs the governors of America's colonies to assist the French Navy vessels commanded by *M. de [d']Entrecasteaux* (+ a merchant of the Puerto de San [...], 'Carmón' & 'Dussant'[?]) 'que van en busca de la *Peyrouse* [Lapérouse], cuyo paradero se ignora.' The famous 1791–93 d'Entrecasteaux search for the vanished Lapérouse; Spain (Family Compact) aiding French ships in American waters. ✅ scale 2

author: El Rey; cert. Arrillaga/Merino
summary: Two cédula duplicates: a reglamento on captains' duties as political judges (art. 50 Reglamento de Presidios; copy by Arrillaga, Loreto 16 Sep 1792); the gracia-de-inválidos decree (DUP of Doc 62)

author: Arrillaga
summary: The $250 extraordinary-correo reimbursement via the situado (pp.61–62); the habilitación handover to José Fco. Cota; the death of Habilitado José Mª Estrada + account arrangements; the botanist's *viaje* (Longinos)

author: Arrillaga
summary: [José Longinos] Martínez, of the Real Expedición Botánica de Nueva España, went to the new [Baja] establishments (18 Apr); the Frontera commander to give him aid; 'se conserva la tranquilidad inalterada' (pp.67–68); + Loreto arsenal repuestos query (p.69)

author: Revilla Gigedo
themes: fiscal; situado; presidio-decay; loreto; pearls; baja-california
summary: The detailed Loreto-presidio SITUADO report (Tribunal de Cuentas): the annual situado of Loreto + its Marina dept. = $14,818 4 3/8 + $4,130 6r augment; the double-accounting confusion (Caja de San Blas vs. habilitación) harming the Real Hacienda; order to hand the $14,632 integrally to habilitado Manuel Cárcaba (¼ reserved for víveres/memorias); the regulation of the troop/marina pay, pearls & cattle as Real-Hacienda income; approved by Fiscal Campo Marín (20 Dec 1792)

author: Arrillaga
summary: Routine Baja admin: Jan–Mar musters (p.77); ⭐ a monthly maritime correo from Loreto Bay to the Sonora coast (pp.78–79); the petition-informe procedure (pp.80–90); the arrested Oaxaca-militia ayudante José Tommcha[?] (deserter watch, pp.91–92); the Capuchin-nuns alms commission (p.93); the arsenal repartimiento (p.94); 3 superior resolutions + a bando (p.95)

author: Hermenegildo Sal; José Argüello
summary: Sal went to Monterey to rectify the 1790 tobacco accounts associated with 'Dn José Fran. de Ortega'; my first reading took 'cuya muerte tuvo la desgracia de presenciar' as Ortega's death — ⚠️ NOW KNOWN WRONG (Doc 180): Ortega was alive & commanding Loreto in 1793. The death Sal witnessed was someone else / a misread antecedent; do not cite an Ortega death here (p.96); + Argüello forwards San Blas-Comisario oficios opened by José Sandoval (p.97)

author: Goycoechea; Arrillaga
summary: Goycoechea acknowledges the DEATH of Governor José Antonio Romeu (notice 9 May 1792) and that Arrillaga is now interim governor (p.98); + Arrillaga sends superior orders Nº1–11 + 5 copies (p.99)

author: Arrillaga
summary: DUP cédulas re-published at Monterey 1792: the inválidos sueldo order (pp.100–101); the general indulto for the contraband crime (non-homicides; reincidence → 10 yrs presidio) — DUP of the bounded Tomo-X version (pp.102–109)

author: Arrillaga
summary: LAND-GRANT POLICY: transmits the order (Cmdte. Gral. → Romeu) that Presidio Captains may henceforth grant *solares y tierras* to soldiers & vecinos who ask to settle (despite Art. 81 of the Ordenanza de Intendentes) — within 4 *leguas comunes* measured from the center of the presidio plaza, and no more; for the new poblaciones to be founded. [Californio land-tenure origins] + a Castilla-titles cédula begun (DUP, cont.)

author: El Rey; cert. Romeu/Argüello
summary: ⚠ BOUNDED 1792 cédula-duplicate run (re-published by Romeu Jan 1792 + Argüello/the 5 commanders Jul–Oct 1792): Castilla titles, concubinato, the matrimonios pragmática (Lebrun), Marina-officer declarations, the slave-*alcabala*/manumission cédula, confraternity juntas, the papal *meat-on-Saturdays* indult (Pius VI), ministers' obligations — all DUPs of the Tomo-X de Nava cédulas (the slave-manumission + meat-indult are the only new ones). + a minor Sal soldier-return note (n213).

author: Arrillaga
recipient: the comandantes of San Diego, Monterey & San Francisco
themes: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ nootka; open-door; limites; boundary; vancouver; bodega-quadra; presidios
summary: NOOTKA BOUNDARY COMMISSION / OPEN DOOR: 'sobre la actitud que deben observar para con los Ingleses' — warns that in 1792 the English & Spanish ships that must unite at *Nutka [Nootka]* may arrive at those CA ports, 'cuyos buques… deben tratar de la cuestión de límites entre las dos naciones' (the *boundary question* between the two nations), and charges the commanders to 'conservar el carácter de verdaderos españoles… que en todo tiempo han sabido sostener el lustre de su nación.' The 1792 Bodega y Quadra–Vancouver *límites* negotiation, with Arrillaga prepping the presidios. ✅ scale 2. → THESIS-CRITICAL #4

author: Arrillaga
summary: Papers found in the late Gov. Romeu's bag (confirms his death again, p.151); + the $793 7½ extraordinary-correo reimbursement to be paid at the México treasury for the now-expired Loreto situado (p.152)

author: Romeu; Arrillaga
summary: The Nootka-correspondence correo expenses ($200 charged to Loreto for the *pliegos de Nuca*, abonado to the situado per de Nava, pp.153–4); + a matanza-de-ganado order begun (cont.)

author: Arrillaga
summary: Annual matanza of 200 Real-Hacienda cattle (Sta Bárbara/Canal's share; authorized despite lack of people/vacancies, pp.155–7); + pliegos useful to the late Romeu's successor (p.158)

author: Revilla Gigedo
summary: Nootka-establishment supply authorized via Real Hacienda for Francisco Eliza & Ramón Saavedra ('el nuevo Establecimiento de Nuca'), the mason kept at Nootka until San Blas sends another (p.159; cf. Doc 133)

author: Revilla Gigedo
themes: ⭐⭐ nootka; open-door; floridablanca; nootka-convention; mexicana; mourelle; exploration
summary: Floridablanca no-hostility order, THIRD copy (reinforces THESIS-CRITICAL #3): no hostilities to English fishing/other vessels; but if establishments are found, recognize them and 'sujetarlos á la última convención' [the Nootka Convention], maintaining precautions on the sites & establishments (pp.159–160); + ⭐⭐ Tte. de Fragata Francisco Antonio Mourelle, commanding the goleta *Mexicana*, on 'una comisión interesante del servicio en la Costa de Californias' — give him aid (the 1792 Sutil y Mexicana expedition, p.160)

author: Arrillaga
summary: Iron supply (5 quintales) requested from the Virrey (p.161); + ⭐ Romeu's death — Arrillaga received notice 8 April 1792, will form the *expediente* of his last will (refines the death to ~late Mar/early Apr 1792, p.162)

author: Romeu
summary: Rest of Romeu's admonition on the friar-vs-military disputes (precaution, justice to each side, manifest disobedience to be proceeded against) — governance/church-vs-state

author: Romeu
summary: (1791 letter inserted) Romeu's cautious response on the Gentiles killing a fugitive Christian Indian of the [Baja] mission (distrust the gentiles' veracity; gather facts before acting); the friar Miguel Abad cargo-bestias matter; ⭐⭐ the bitter complaints against González ('escándalo' w/ the mission's lands/cattle, reserving the troop's caballada); Romeu's commentary 'cuán perjudicial es la demasiada adherencia á los dictámenes [de los frailes]' [church-vs-state] (cont.)

author: Diego González
summary: The indio killed by the sierra Gentiles (+ motives) of the [Baja] mission; the friar minister's cargos; the presidiario Rafael Pacheco (ill, at Mission San Fernando); 3 Christian Indians imprisoned at Mission del Rosario (Fr. Ricardo Texeira); the padrón-formation at the missions; Mission Santo Tomás 'se adelanta'

author: Hermenegildo Sal
themes: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ presidio-decay; san-francisco; fortification; architecture; plan-drawing; garrison; fire-risk; bodega-open-door
summary: THE SAN FRANCISCO PRESIDIO CONDITION REPORT + a hand-drawn MEASURED FLOOR PLAN (Savage copied Sal's drawing, n234): room-by-room w/ dimensions — sala de diversión, cuartel, 2 calabozos, ropa & víveres almacenes, the Comandante's houses, iglesia (19½ varas), casa mata (powder), sargento's house (the lienzos: 114 varas N-facing, 134 varas E-facing). Verbatim condition report (n235): *'Los almacenes son de piedra y lodo, sin amarre alguno, y expuestos á una ruina'*; the guard/barracks walls *'muy desplomadas'* propped with stone buttresses 'por sostenerlas'; the sergeant's house *'viniendo abajo'*; the church walls *'desplomadas y deterioradas'*; ALL roofs of *zacate ó tule* 'tan sumamente expuestos á un incendio'; the winds (NE summer, S/SE winter) *'como fuertes huracanes'* wrecking the roofs in *'trabajo inacabable'*; *'las fuerzas son pocas… lo más del año se halla este sin más fuerza que el sargento ó un cabo y la guardia de plantón'*; exposed to fire, short of arms

author: Sal
summary: The SF presidio CONSTRUCTION HISTORY (1776–92): begun 27 Jul 1776; by end-1778 the commandant's house was adobe + partial walls + a stone *casa mata* + palisade-and-earth warehouses/church/quarters; the Jan 1779 rains collapsed 2 warehouses, the casa mata, church, commandant's house, 6 troop houses & most of the 4 wall-curtains; by 1780 nothing of the 1778 work remained; the adobe is bad ('con solo la humedad se deshace'); timber 10+ leagues off; the presidio built three times; in 1787 & 1791 the troops reverted to palisade houses to shelter their families. Signed Sal

author: Sal
themes: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ presidio-decay; san-francisco; fiscal; fortification; fire-risk; bodega-open-door
summary: Sal's SECOND SF-presidio report: the $1,200 construction gratification never arrived (vs $4,000 to each Provincias-Internas presidio — Sal's pointed complaint); no worker list; the construction history again; the lienzos building-by-building — almacenes/guardia/cuartel 'viniendo abajo,' commandant's & troop houses needing repair, the church to be rebuilt for lack of foundations, the zacate/tule roofs fire-exposed, the 4th lienzo mere palisade; only ~6 usable houses; asks for 8–10 servants/San Blas sailors + a master mason for 4 yrs OR 3,000 pesos to keep building. ⚠️ n240 (the roof-repair/wind/*tule-3-leguas*/few-forces/supply-ship passage) is PART OF THIS SF REPORT — see the audit re-correction note below

author: Sal
summary: Sal forms & sends the SF company's 1791 accounts to the Real Tribunal de Cuentas (originals went to the late Romeu); notes he received the SF presidio & its interests on 16 Apr 1791

author: Pedro de Nava
summary: Confirms Gov. José Antonio Romeu DIED 9 April 1792; Arrillaga assumes interim command as Teniente Gobernador (most senior officer) pending a Viceregal appointment (p.186); + a Castilla-titles cédula dup (cont.)

author: El Rey; cert. commanders
summary: DUP cédulas re-published 1792–93: Castilla titles (re the death of the Marqués de Sigüeros, heir María Mercedes, pp.187–190); the student/scholar-marriage cédula (Aranjuez 11 Jun 1792; pub. Arrillaga SD 31 May 1793, Sal SF 6 Jun, Argüello Monterey 19 Jun, pp.191–196)

author: Pedro de Nava
summary: Approves crediting an SF-presidio sergeant the service time omitted by Neve & Fages (p.199); receives the propuesta of José Pérez Fernández for Alférez (p.200)

author: José Fran. de Ortega
summary: A property-inheritance dispute: Francisca Velásquez bought a house from Ayudante Mayor Juan Gutiérrez de la Cueva while her husband was absent; her father later withheld the escritura; after his death her younger sister claims the house — Francisca alleges malice/usurpation; witnesses Miguel Alvarado, Camacho, Javier Aguilar, Diego Sainz

author: Fran. de Ortega
summary: Ortega is ALIVE & commanding Loreto in 1793 (no novelty, p.205; Alférez Ildefonso Bernal's sueldo, p.206); ⭐ the Indian Juan Tarpio asks exemption from pueblo *recogimiento* for 15+ yrs' service in the marina (Ortega consults, pp.207–208); inventory via Sgt. Carrillo (pp.209–210). ⚠️ RE-CORRECTION: proves my Doc 159 reading of 'Ortega's death' (n208) was WRONG — Ortega lived (the famous J. F. de Ortega d. 1798); the death Sal witnessed at Monterey was someone else / a misread. *Do not cite Doc 159's Ortega-death.*

author: Ortega
summary: The goleta *Santa Saturnina* (alias 'La Orcasitas') at Loreto, May 1793 — to stay in the port warehouses; crewed by the *práctico* Joaquín Robles + 2 artillerymen + 5 sailors; came empty (no timber); carried 12 cases of *azogue* (mercury) from Álamos; arrived 29 May (the same Límites-expedition schooner of the 1792 SF friendly-fire & Docs 122/127)

author: Ortega
summary: The death of soldier José de Castro (8 May 1793, intestate; his books seized over suspected dealings in metals/silver/pearls + a possible robbery; the Real-Caja-de-Álamos cajero to investigate, pp.214–216); + iron/steel accounting (p.217)

author: Ortega
summary: The death of Padre José Vida Urreta (minister of San José del Cabo, 22 Mar 1793); + the México/San Blas factura accounts for 90–92 (p.219)

author: Ortega
summary: Routine: hoja-de-servicios extract format (p.220); the Tescalama-Hacienda administrator's salary suspension (p.221); no novelty (p.222); musters (p.223); the royal-birth thanksgiving cédula published (p.224)

author: Ortega
summary: Floods (avenidas) causing crop damage & 'muchas necesidades' + a food shipment (30 fanegas frijol from P. Valdez, p.225); + the Loreto arsenal roof renovation begun 14 Feb (p.226)

author: Ortega
summary: Approves (on Fr. Andrés Sánchez's proposal) the election of an indigenous Gobernador — Juan Xavier Nájera — by the people of Mission La Purísima (Baja)

author: Ortega
themes: shipwreck; naufragio; ships; marina; inquiry; baja-california
summary: Inspection of a royal goleta (patrón Diego Pérez, calafate José Vélez, carpintero Jaime Mineta, p.228); Tabacos factor (p.229); + ⭐⭐⭐ the *información* (sworn inquiry) into the NAUFRAGIO (shipwreck) of a Goleta de S.M. — patrón Diego Sáiz, calafate José de la Cruz, carpintero Jaime Morillo depose, sent by Capt. Ortega to the spot where the lost royal goleta lies beached (cont.)

author: Grajera
summary: Monthly inspection rounds of mission escorts (N/S alternating, diary to Gov); each escort issued 2 cartridge-sets, 40 flints, 60 cartridges/man

author: Grajera
summary: Correspondence relay: monthly courier (cabo+soldier) to mid-frontier → comandante de la Frontera → mission-to-mission

author: Grajera
summary: Ack. R.O. on the *intimación* (summons) to the French in their dominions; will comply. Savage: 'no dice cuál fue la intimación'

author: Grajera
summary: Ack. 3 cédula-certificates for *inválidos* (royal grace), per Gov 11 Dec

author: Grajera
summary: Ack. R.O.: 10-yr extension of the San Blas–California commerce duty/alcabala exemption (incl. salted hides/meat per R.O. 10 Apr 1793)

author: Grajera
summary: R.O.: Madrid *gremios mayores* projected trade expedition Cádiz→Callao→San Blas→Cal. ports aboard ship *Levante*; report if it arrives. R.O. not attached

author: Grajera
summary: Ack. accession of the Marqués de Branciforte as Viceroy/Gov/Capt-Gen; notified troops & public by bando

author: Grajera
summary: Per Gov 12 Nov: extracted 9 English-addressed letters/packets from the mail bag; forwarded to Viceroy 1 Dec

author: Grajera
summary: FOREIGN-SHIP EXPULSION ORDER (recd.): intimate any foreign ship to leave w/o excuse; no dealings/no supplies except urgent humanitarian (*derecho de gentes*); prevent them learning the country; specific orders re Vancouver & Peter Puget's ships at Monterey; warnings to all escort comandantes → E1

author: Grajera
summary: Return of war-*donativos* to civilians/military; amount credited to each account

author: Grajera
summary: Indian *jornaleros* at SD presidio paid 1 real/day + ration from 10 Oct

author: Grajera
summary: SD company muster extracts, last quarter; 1 discharge + 1 enlistment

author: Grajera
summary: Forwards a cabo's petition + recommends 2 soldiers for promotion to *cabo*

author: Grajera
summary: Rotating mission escorts w/ presidio soldiers per verbal order; notes hardship on soldiers' families in transit

author: Grajera
summary: LA vecinos to sell maize/frijol to the Habilitación (4 rl cheaper); weak presidio mules; transport-pay rates (15%)

author: Grajera
summary: San Miguel escort: mission-supplied Indian *cocinero* (from Fages's day) now dispensable since soldiers married — proposes saving the cost

author: Grajera
summary: Soldier Vicente Félix continues commissioned at LA pueblo (interim for the cabo); correspondence relay via comandante de la Frontera

author: Grajera
summary: Discharge & final pay of soldier Juan Padilla (to 14 Nov); itemized account, liquid balance $402.2.4¾

author: Grajera
summary: Wrote Capt. José de Zúñiga (Tucson) for the missing account-books for presidio/mission settlements + SD Co. adjustments; encloses soldiers' *memoriales*

author: Blas García (pilot, frig. *Concepción*)
summary: Complaint vs Grajera: insults & threats, false accusation of illicit dealing w/ a soldier's wife; accuses Grajera of habitual drunkenness; asks justice (witness Capt. Sebastián Menéndez[?])

author: Grajera
summary: Departure of Capt. José Joaquín de Arrillaga from SD (11 Oct) + frig. *Concepción* (aids given); Gov's letters arrived after sailing

author: Grajera
summary: Nootka: imminent departure of Brig. José Manuel de Álava (succeeding Bodega y Cuadra) for an important commission at Nootka; promises aid if he calls at SD. *(The p.34 English-ships fragment on this leaf's top belongs to Doc 38, cont'd here.)*

author: Grajera
summary: Ship in sight — believes it carries the *memorias* (supply shipment)

author: Grajera
summary: Otter trade: published Viceroy's bando (5 Jul; Junta Sup. R.H.) — otter pelts exported via Acapulco now duty-free

author: Grajera
summary: Armament: arms to be delivered by frig. *Concepción*; value discounted over 3 *situados*; will instruct troops on upkeep

author: Grajera
summary: Libranza: $2000 draft in favor of San Juan Capistrano mission (via Fr. Vicente Fuster); if uncashable in Mexico, funds obtainable from nearby missions

author: Grajera
summary: Gentile Indian 'el Salvo'[?] brought by frig. *Concepción* put to presidio labor under secure custody

author: Grajera
summary: Received *avalúos* from Tte. José Argüello (held for Gov's arrival); forwards letters; sent animals per Arrillaga's order

author: Grajera
summary: Pack-animal supply: cabo waited at Sto. Tomás (no pasture at Sto. Domingo) to spare the animals; route Sto. Tomás–San Vicente–San Fernando–Sto. Domingo

author: Grajera
summary: Logistics of Borica's overland journey to the governorship: dispatched Cabo Juan de Olivera + 8 soldiers w/ 29 cargo-mules & 35 saddle-horses for the Gov & the *señoras* accompanying him; Arrillaga not at SD

author: Grajera
summary: Few of Félix's horses came; 2 soldiers leave with them to join Cabo Olivera

author: Grajera
summary: Recognition of Borica as Político-Military Gov (per S.O. 14 May) proclaimed at SD before the troop; orders to the 4 missions of the SD jurisdiction — San Miguel, San Diego, San Juan Capistrano, San Gabriel — & their ministers to have all Indians attend

author: Grajera
summary: License to Ramón Márquez (comercio de Indias): trial trade expedition Cádiz→San Blas→California ports; provide all aid if his ship reaches SD, report circumstantially

author: Grajera
summary: Mule-rental: credited *fondo de gratificación* / charged Monterey habilitación for the pack-mules that carried the Gov's luggage; no novelty

author: Grajera
summary: Troop supply: Habilitado may buy consumption articles only with Grajera's agreement, at merchant prices & quality equal to Mexico/San Blas goods

author: Grajera
summary: Practice not to open the *tercios* (bales) of the *memoria* until the ship is unloaded; no novelty

author: Grajera
summary: Arrival of correspondence + frig. *Concepción* (pilot Menéndez) with the year's *memorias*; no novelty

author: Grajera
summary: 'Dar abrigo á buques ingleses': ack. R.O. 20 Aug — English merchant & war ships to be sheltered in Spanish ports (the 1793–95 Anglo-Spanish alliance vs France); will comply as occasion arises

author: Grajera
summary: Chain of command: deal with Capt. Arrillaga until Borica reaches Monterey

author: Grajera
summary: Márquez trade license + free-trade extension: King's grant to Ramón/Román Márquez (comercio de Indias) for a Cádiz→San Blas→California expedition (South-Sea stops), all aid w/o burden to the Treasury; the franchises extended generally — absolute freedom from Spanish export duties, *alcabala* on first sale & municipal duties on national fruits/manufactures bound to Cal. ports, foreign goods pay 7% (as at Veracruz)

author: José Pérez Fernández (SF cmdt)
summary: Libranza in favor of Mission San Francisco; recorded by habilitación pending Vº Bº, remitted to the mission's Padres

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Will follow prescribed form for force-states & monthly *novedades*; mail & musters on the 1st of each month

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Escort vigilance: patrol & report troop conduct, armament, uniform; guard often short 2–3 men used as couriers; some being incapacitated

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Supply/manufacture: SF mission supplied blankets & *gerga* this year; but no local maker of soap, saddles, cutlery, steels, boots

author: Pérez Fernández
