summary: Will follow prescribed form for invalid-grace relations

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: SF mission escort: in Dec 1783 found it reduced to a cabo + 3 soldiers; augmented by 1 (Gov approved 11 Jan); 4 men rotate for *correos*/fatigue

author: Cabo Nicolás Galindo
summary: Veteran SF *compañía de cuera* cabo, aged & infirm, requests retirement; held until a recruit replaces him

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Antonio Santos (crewman, frig. *Aránzazu*) stayed at SF presidio 14 Aug 1793 to make tile; made all the tile & brick for the Castillo construction; embark on *Princesa* or stay till supply ship?

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Benediction of the Castillo de San Joaquín (SF fort) 'yesterday' [= 8 Dec 1794], formalities of style; asks which account to charge candles & guard expenses

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Uniform discipline: permits no non-uniform garment; no *paño* here to make coats; 'better without any than out of regulation'

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Paper shortage so severe he must combine subjects in one letter

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Sends tools + 12 @ sugar[?] in 2 *tercios* to the Monterey almacén

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Four communications: ack. order 28 Nov; banned soldiers wearing *arracadas* (earrings); promises timely service docs; artisans + tools sent to Monterey tomorrow

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Friars sought 2–3 more escort soldiers to push a missionary 'conquista de gentiles' across the bay north of the Port (toward Marin); Pérez Fernández declined (3 reasons: unknown/hostile country; a friar + 2–3 soldiers too weak; he lacks authority), asks instructions. + tule-balsa shipwreck at the Farallones — 2 of 4 SF mission Indians drowned (4th of month) [= prior mil-hist #45]

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Receipt of dispatches (his + SF mission Padres'); orders will be executed

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Requests 40 vaquillas (1–2 yr) + 15 novillas for SF presidio supply, brought by the cabo + 5 SF soldiers at Monterey (margin: remitted 75 head)

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Castillo work concluded this year; workers finishing *remates*; expects to announce it terminated next month

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Castillo de San Joaquín completion report: remaining = planking, cuartel armory, *garita*, ranch bridge (finish this week); sending to Monterey the artillery *condestable*, carpenter, 2 *herradores*, warehouse mason + servant; mules spoiled, need rest

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: English launches off Santa Cruz (night of 24th): cabo warned per Reglamento; sent 5-man reinforcement; sent sargento to investigate; applied Borica's doctrine — no dealings, prevent them learning the country, make them retire if treacherous → E2

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: English launches withdrew 28th (no excess); Sgt. Pedro Amador returned; soldier Mesa punished (cepo) for false report; unrest among San José gentiles continues → E2

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Invalid soldier Justo Altamirano seeks transfer to Pueblo de San José; appraisal of his military gear (cuera $20, musket $5, 5 horses @ $9…)

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Suspended supply-advances (*avíos*) to troop & families per order, until further notice

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Transmitted King's thanks (Revillagigedo decree 26 Jun) to those who made donativos for the war with France; will credit each

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Circumstances of the Port & foreign attention to it; great distance between presidio & Castillo; proposes relocating the Presidio to a site near the church/fort — stone, lime kiln, adobe works at hand, more fertile land, purer winds, proximity to the castillo; once the fort is done, can gather materials for the new presidio

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Asks what to do if Vancouver / another English war-or-merchant ship appears at SF — the alliance R.O. doesn't mention Cal. ports; invokes what was observed with Vancouver's expedition → E3

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: San Blas naval men commissioned to build the Castillo: supplies/wages/accounts, short *situado*, debts; charges to pass to San Blas via Comisario de Marina José María Híjar[?]

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Will aid the *Levante* (Madrid *gremios mayores*) if it reaches SF [cf. Doc 6]

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Per reserved order 12th: vigilance of correspondence — will extract any English/foreign-language letters & forward to the Viceroy [SF parallel of Doc 8]

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Published Branciforte's accession as Viceroy [cf. Doc 7]

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Published R.O. extending 10-yr San Blas–California duty/alcabala exemption (incl. tallow & salted meat) [cf. Doc 5]

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Ack. R.O. (9 Dec 1793, via Viceroy 21 Jun) on the *intimación* to French prisoners — Savage: 'no dice cuál fuera' [cf. Doc 3]

author: Pérez Fernández
themes: missions; friar-violence; indigenous; labor; santa-cruz; santa-clara
summary: Arrillaga's command ends (→ deal w/ Borica); discontent of San Blas sailors at Mission Santa Cruz w/ manual labor (adobes etc.) — proposes paying them to work at presidio; + cabo Miguel Pacheco's report: a Padre badly beat (*aporreó malamente*) a gentile who refused baptism; the soldier-escort reprimanded for allowing it; Pérez laments actions 'diametralmente opuestas al servicio de Dios y del Rey' undoing his work to curb Indian 'altanería'

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Shortage of a literate cabo at SF; requests an apt soldier from Santa Bárbara

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Troop shortage: barely 5 men available for SF presidio's needs; begs his Monterey-bound men not be detained; asks baja of soldier Salvador Ignacio[?] (completed, bad conduct) once replaced

author: Sgt. Pedro Amador (Santa Cruz)
summary: Amador's own report: withdrew with the SF reinforcement once the English launches departed; no novelty during their stay near the mission → E2

author: Cabo José Sánchez (Sta Cruz)
summary: 3rd report on the Santa Cruz launches: the English commander kept his sailors from the mission; no disorder; Indians kept working; 'los ingleses se marcharon en paz' → E2

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Will aid Brig. Álava if he reaches SF (Viceroy order 10 May) [SF parallel of Doc 22]

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Deal with Capt. Arrillaga in service matters until Borica arrives [cf. Doc 39]

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Recognized Borica as Gob. Político-Militar & Inspector [cf. Doc 32]

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Ack. King's concession to Román Márquez (Cádiz→San Blas→Cal. expedition); will aid if it reaches SF [cf. Docs 33/40]

author: Viceroy Branciforte
summary: Separation of California from the Comandancia Gen. de Provincias Internas; repartimiento de tierras ordered by Jacobo de Ugarte y Loyola but never executed; encloses Arrillaga's 26 Sep 1793 letter to Revillagigedo on granting *sitios* to discharged soldiers/vecinos vs. undefined mission limits, & Indians' natural right to the best sites → E4

author: Pérez Fernández (alf.)
summary: Castillo timber: cut timber in the sierra for the fort's works; conduction underway but oxen & packsaddles lacking [cf. prior-pass Sal 'felling timber' find]

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Artillery conservation: per the *condestable*, pull the castillo's cannons (except one) under roof to protect the *cureñas*; those on the esplanade endure for now [cf. prior-pass 'guns on esplanade']

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Horse-herd: remount of colts arrived, joined to the tame herd (2 guards); held until Hermenegildo Sal's arrival to distribute [= prior-pass xii.126 ✓]

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Santa Clara cattle-raiding: Fr. Diego de Noboa reports gentiles beyond Arroyo San José Cupertino took *reses*; cabo ordered to recover/capture; 23 ox-yokes under Bernal to the sierra for timber

author: Pérez Fernández
themes: indigenous; military; forced-labor; santa-clara; peralta; amador; noboa
summary: 'Crítica situación, débiles escoltas' (→ E5): Santa Clara cattle-theft pursuit (Noboa's denunciation); an Indian kept in the *cepo* 10 days though declared innocent; Christian Indians steal horses/cattle; Sgt. Pedro Amador's round-up (15 men + pueblo vecinos, gentiles captured) & recruitment of gentiles for Castillo labor; captives applied to the fort works in irons by day; encloses Fr. Diego de Noboa's 14 Mar letter warning of 'un rompimiento muy semejante al de Sonora' [= prior mil-hist #43, at Sav p.53]; only '2 useful men' to resist invasion

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Of the ~22 Indians imprisoned & set to work on the Castillo, several fled at night (left cotones/frazadas in the cañada); search party couldn't catch them → E5

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Capture at Santa Cruz of gentile Hímelente[?] with a Christian woman stolen from Carmelo (he in irons, she in a *corma* at the mission); 9 Indians under Alférez Pablo Cota still out after gentile Ochole[?] — + Savage's ⭐⭐⭐ annotated drawing & description of the *corma* → E6

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: On Sal's charge, examined the SF powder-magazine w/ the first artilleryman — roof full of leaks, no powder would stay dry if it rained

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Building upkeep vs the rains; completed tile for the leaky casa de la pólvora; will keep visiting it

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: SF tile-kiln (*horno de teja*) damaged; tiles imperfect (sandy adobe, poor earth); better clay for tile/brick found near the Castillo

author: Felipe de Goycoechea (SB cmdt)
summary: $1,200 royal gratificación for workers on the Santa Bárbara presidio (esp. tile-makers); + cost estimate & timeline to finish the works (list/budget enclosed later)

author: Gov. Borica (Monterey)
summary: Passing through SB: troop to make small corral repairs + church enlargement (charged to *fondo de gratificación*); distribution of the $1,200 via Habilitado Manuel de Cárcaba — 7 men @ 21 p, ~58 @ 14 p, 8 @ 10 p 1 r (living/invalid/licensed/heirs), duplicate vouchers

author: Goycoechea
summary: Ground plan(s) of the Real Presidio del Canal de Santa Bárbara, 1794 construction state (quadrangle, numbered *lienzos*/rooms + legend) — ⚠ washed-out scan; scale-1 (full-res) re-read advised if the plan is wanted

author: Goycoechea
summary: SB company labor-roster/account table (1786–92): individuals by class, names, amounts; Nota: ALL the garrison worked on building the presidio (alternating with escort duty) except Cabo Francisco María Ruiz & Sold. Josef Thadeo Sánchez; signed Goycoechea 30 Dec 1792

author: Goycoechea (cert.)
summary: Certified estimate to finish the SB presidio works: 15 jornaleros × 6 mo @ 1½ r/day + 34 fanegas maize @ 3 r = $561.4 (per Revillagigedo R.O. 22 Jan)

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Departure of Alférez Hermenegildo Sal (w/ escort) to Monterey; pack-animals loaded w/ salt (none at presidio); SF bastion (*baluarte*) works active since Dec, aiming to finish this year [= prior-pass xii.152]

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Can't sign Sal's accounts (knows nothing of the supplies under Sal's charge); troops escorted Sal to Monterey with his family; adobe/tile works progressing [= prior-pass xii.153–4 ✓]

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Accounting error: Habilitado Gen. Manuel Cárcaba double-charged $1719.3 from 1792

author: Tte. Francisco Ortega (Loreto)
summary: Baja: the King's launch (*lancha del Rey*) delivered to Mission Mulegé; mission to bear the costs in its current state

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: SF tile/brick poor quality (sandy, defective clay, premature firing, defective kiln); Indians given a quota of 125 tiles/day — hard even for skilled tilemakers; still usable for the Castillo

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Castillo & batería labor shortage — needs more *brazos* before the season; tension with missionaries who need the Indians

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: SF housing shortage: 2nd-officer's house unsalvageable, must rebuild; presidio 'muy escaso de casas,' population rising with marriages

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Artillery mounted at the Castillo ('en batería,' *troneras*, esplanade, fort 'en punto de combate'); done mostly by troop + presidio ox-teams, Indians only briefly; needs nails & 4 more cannons each for Monterey & SD

author: Tte. Francisco Ortega (Loreto)
summary: Complaint vs blacksmith Secundino — excesses, threatened to kill the P. Ministro, stirred the gentiles; wants him removed

author: Ortega
summary: Accounts of *bulas* (Santa Cruzada) from the deceased official's money; 1790 mail accounts

author: Ortega
summary: Omnibus report: 1790 Memoria factura; guard upkeep; Cabo Sandoval ~50 p; Dec–Jan musters; frigate costs to R.H.; + Indian desertions — admonitions failing, orders the *cepo* at first re-offense

author: Ortega
summary: Detailed account of a sick alférez, badly needed — asks replacement

author: Ortega
summary: Loreto supply scarcity easing by Dec; *agregados'* salaries (2½ r); recruits without mounts; a gravely ill soldier

author: Ortega
summary: Sends padrones de almas (censuses, by estado & clase); Memoria delay held up accounts to end-1793

author: Ortega
summary: Ortega hands Loreto command to successor Tte. Francisco Rea (12 Apr); papers/fondo handed over; Borica due at Loreto by the 2nd (en route to govern)

author: Ortega
summary: Soldier-wounding case: Sáiz wounded Cisneros (violent provocateur) in self-defense; Cisneros recovered

author: Ortega
summary: Notified San Blas Comisaría of the timber the presidio needs

author: Ortega
summary: War-donativo (Baja): four lists — troop, vecinos & miners voluntarily gave H.M. $3,194.4 for the war; remits libranza

author: Hermenegildo Sal
summary: Sal's own report on the Castillo/baluarte labor force: 33 gentiles from San José ('muy contentos, se les trata bien'); 78 men total on the works = 30 from the SF mission + punished *cimarrones* + 8 from Sta Clara + 37 gentiles, under 1 sgt/1 cabo/2 soldiers; mission Padres demand a blanket/trousers monthly on top of pay (refused); 1,500 adobes/day; cut 52 *cuartones* for the esplanade; gentiles fled leaving their *prendas* — wages/belongings left for them at Sta Clara; the 4 Christian captives (from E5) to go to Mission Santa Clara → folds into E5

author: F. Rea (Loreto)
summary: Borica & family embarked from Loreto on the schooner *Santa Saturnina* (20th) for his destination

author: Rea
summary: Ack. circular on military judges' jurisdiction over troop causes; archived

author: Rea
summary: Sends company lists/force-states Apr–Jun (margin: return Apr & May, erroneous)

author: Rea
summary: Maritime mail departed dawn 10 May on arrival of 9th overland mail

author: Rea
summary: Borica reached Loreto 12 May & took possession of the Político-Military Gov of the Peninsula 14 May 1794; held mail till maritime mail arrived

author: José Fco. de Ortega
summary: Account re costs of the frigate *Santiago* (sailed 20 Nov 1793)

author: Ortega
summary: Handed over the Loreto Comandancia archive (Caja del Fondo / vault room) per Borica's orders

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: SF/Santa Cruz mission-escort understaffing (reduced to 3, one invalid); Padres demand completion; added one man

author: Ortega
summary: Praises successor Tte. Francisco Rea ('hombre muy puro'); their small difference settled

author: Ortega
summary: Handed Borica the dispatch Arrillaga sent 31 May

author: Ildefonso Bernal
summary: Per order 31 May, pay Bartolomé Salida $128.5 owed to the estate of the late Alejo González; deductions incl. Monte Pío militar

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: SF construction timber/oxen logistics: hides for salt-hauling, 37 *novillos* to break, cutting *cuartones* (6 varas×14 in) & *vigas* (10 varas), 25 yokes; San Mateo timber; 5 cart-wheels + 4 axles; references Sal's reports

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: SF condestable & carpenter can make nails/ironwork for SF, SD & Monterey presidios

author: Ortega
summary: Baja population censuses end-1793 by mission (Guadalupe, Sta Rosalía, San Ignacio, Sta Gertrudis, San Borja…); now in Gov's possession

author: Ortega
summary: Southern *padrones* delayed; sends *resumen de almas*; 1793 accounts delayed by memoria arrivals

author: Ortega
summary: On notifying Borica's accession; successor Rea deferred the courier till the maritime mail

author: Ortega
summary: The sick Alférez (Ildefonso) improving on a milk regimen; still doing duty

author: Ortega
summary: Sends Jan–Mar muster extracts

author: Ortega
summary: Forwards letters (Castel, interventor) re silver & mistreatment of mine *operarios* — 13 workers owe >$800 yet 'violentos para salirse' over the Administrator's *mal trato*; P. Fray Mariano consulted

author: Ortega
summary: Handover confusion re Rea / Comand. Gonzalo & suspensions ordered

author: Ortega
summary: Plan to dispatch schooner *Santa Saturnina* (9th–10th) to convey Borica & family

author: Ortega
summary: Will vacate his house for the Gov (Borica)

author: Ortega
summary: King's launch returned by Mission Mulegé after overhaul; shipworm (*broma*) damage; received 'in equal terms' [cf. Doc 100]

author: Ortega
summary: Encloses an alférez's *hoja de servicios*

author: Ortega
summary: Loreto house-building for the *marinería* + materials (adobes/tiles) + the *casa de habilitación*

author: Goycoechea (SB)
summary: Vancouver's gifts & the SB supply to him: Vancouver's gifts (8–12 mirrors, powder bags) split SB/San Buenaventura/presidio + offer of a London *libranza* (Goycoechea declined as too small); + itemized list of what SB supplied Capt. George Vancouver — bullocks, Puebla beans, blue royal-factory cloth, fine Puebla crockery, maize, a red *cuera* → E7

author: Goycoechea
summary: Settlement (*ajuste*) of soldier José Miguel de Leyba + arms/horse

author: Goycoechea
summary: Asks whether to complete the 14-man escorts at San Buenaventura & La Purísima; no novelty among Indians

author: Goycoechea
summary: Escort-inspection order: cabo to inspect N/S mission escorts w/ diary; 2 spare muskets, 40 flints, 60 cartridges/man; 'tratar bien á los indios, pero sin familiarizarse con ellos' — violations severely punished (Borica Indian-treatment instruction)

author: Goycoechea
summary: Bad weather delayed the *novillos*; must send for them

author: Goycoechea
summary: Ack. year-end receipt of monthly musters/force-states

author: Goycoechea
summary: Ack. *formulario* for monthly revista extracts

author: Goycoechea
summary: Re annual Memoria de efectos & pay calc.; request only surplus common goods (no costly luxury); buy in-country

author: Goycoechea
summary: Dispatched 9 armed men to the LA alcalde per order (1 married; others bachelors w/ broken-down mounts)

author: Goycoechea
summary: Credited *fondo de gratif.* the mule-rent for conveying Borica's luggage (½ real/legua) — Borica passed through SB

author: Goycoechea
summary: Year-end docs: military → Gov, hacienda → José Argüello

author: Goycoechea
summary: Forwards the Real Audiencia de Guadalajara sentence vs prisoners (a man 'Rechin'[?] & M.ª Bernarda de Silvas); Goycoechea presumed *fiscal*

author: Goycoechea
summary: Viceroy declined the $740 war-donativo from the vecinos of the 4 Cal. presidios (thanked their 'patriótico desprendimiento')

author: Goycoechea
summary: Monthly musters; jurisdiction calm, 'gentilidad muy sosegada'

author: Goycoechea
summary: Will aid any Gremios Mayores de Madrid ship reaching his ports [cf. Docs 6/67]

author: Goycoechea
summary: Will comply re French prisoners [cf. Docs 3/71]

author: Goycoechea
summary: Proclaimed Branciforte's accession as Viceroy [cf. Docs 7/69]

author: Goycoechea
summary: Filiación distinguishing soldier Fco Mª de Ortega; reports death of vecino Fco Tinoco (accused á la Habilitada) who, having secured his estate, could not make a will — leaves 4 daughters, one married 3 days prior

author: Goycoechea
summary: 10 mares sent to La Quemada (site conceded to him & José de Ortega for the Govt); Ávila's stock also went to the vecino; aranceles posted in guard corps; weak mules to be distributed once recovered

author: Goycoechea
summary: Baja of soldier José Migl de Leyba; encloses filiación (made the 1st) of recruit Pedro Migl Álvarez

author: Goycoechea
summary: Forwards a personal letter from George Vancouver (read it before the Pe Presidente; contents merely greetings, asks after Goycoechea's health & requests *Gazetas nuevas*); Vancouver writes he is bedridden with head/body pain — 'no sé si habrá algún empacho, ó será resfrío del estómago, porq[ue] éste no resiste arriba de 3 ó 4 bocados, y aventado todo el lado del hígado'

author: Goycoechea
summary: Asks the partida going with the Govr to bring back 240 *novillos ó toros* and no cows for the troop's maintenance — cows useless, 'aquí vienen á parir y solo sirven de estorbo'

author: (Borrador sin firma)
summary: Reglamento on troop accounts & supply (7 articles): no reales but every 2 yrs; no payment until alcances received; bans direct/indirect trade with troops & families — 'es esto escandaloso abuso'; habilitado signs/posts tariffs; book to specify all *especies*; no officer to sell horses to troops; presidio cattle limited (4 cows/plaza de prest, 8/officer); halt *gastos de fábrica* except to finish the church

author: Goycoechea
summary: Per order of 16 Oct, will procure for the troop in the Peninsula (jabón, cera, frazadas, jerga) via the habilitado, equal in quality/price to San Blas or the King's supply; encloses revista & fuerza states (Oct–Nov, 'pero no se hallan')

author: Goycoechea
summary: Alférez Pablo Cota with 9 men & arriero takes 30 horses & 24 *mulas de recua* (worn, viejas)

author: Goycoechea
summary: Sedición de Indios at San Luis: gentiles & Christians invited to revolt by 27 *serranos*; declared it; the *convite* came to nothing; 4 gentiles of the rancho de Vte Nomris[?] imprisoned for inciting & threatening; 2 lesser captains sent to Monterey (margin: 'Nov 11-94 se encargó este asunto pr Arrillaga')

author: Goycoechea
summary: Per his order of the 29th, sent to San Diego 9 men under a cabo with 29 mules & 30 horses; will transmit also to the Comandte of Monterey

author: Goycoechea
summary: Acks order of 20 Aug enclosing R.O. of 23 May 1793 'para que los Buques Españoles é Ingleses como auxiliares mutuamente en las actuales circunstancias de la guerra que los Franceses han declarado á ámbas naciones' [the Anglo-Spanish mutual-aid order — source of the E3 contradiction]

author: Goycoechea
summary: Acks order of 20 Aug w/ dictamen of 10 May: Brig. José Manuel de Álava sailing to Nootka on the frigate *Princesa* from San Blas 'á una comisión importante' (Nootka final settlement); Goycoechea to supply him carnes &c. if he touches Sta Bárbara

author: Goycoechea
summary: Florid courtesy letter offering every aid to Brig. Álava on arrival; wishes Borica & 'mi Señora Doña C.B. (cuyos pies beso)' a happy voyage

author: Goycoechea
summary: San Luis sedition (cont.): new declarations from the imprisoned La Purísima gentiles — they were *convidados* by the Indian Miguel of S. Luis, urged against the Christians, paid in *avalorios* (beads); only the gentiles seem culpable (the two factions of S. Luis Christians were to fight); meanwhile he keeps the prisoners 'divertidos en la necesidad de esta obra' (set to forced labor on the works)

author: Goycoechea
summary: Acks (10 Jul) the relation charging this presidio with armament in its various arms, per R. Hda / Viceroy's order

author: Goycoechea
summary: Holds the Indian Chuarquin[?] in *grillos* — apprehended as the Govt's by Salvador Menéndez, cmdr of the frigate *Concepción*; to be sent to San Diego

author: Goycoechea
summary: San Luis sedition: Alf. Pablo Cota returned from La Purísima (15 Aug) with 9 Indians + 17 gentiles, accomplices in the S. Luis *alzamiento*; said incited by Miguel & Marito, Indians of that mission; new declarations to be taken when the *Concepción* sails

author: Goycoechea
summary: Asks instruction on the 5-reales fee to the escribanía de Cámara (order of Cmdt-Gen. Jacobo de Ugarte y Loyola from S. Pedro nr Guadalajara; R. Audiencia accord 24 Nov 1792, royal approval 6 Jul 1793)

author: Goycoechea
summary: 3 oficios: recognizes Borica as Gov. & ordered mission/LA escort cabos to do likewise; will deal w/ Arrillaga until Borica reaches Monterey; ⭐ notes R.O. of 1 Oct 1793 granting Hernán Márquez a commercial expedition Cádiz→San Blas & the Californias — will aid it if it reaches his ports

author: Goycoechea
summary: Announces arrival (15th) of the frigate *Concepción* with this presidio's *Memorias*

author: Frs. Cayetano Pallás & co.
summary: Founding of Mission San Pedro Mártir de Verona (Dominican, Baja) on 27 Apr 1794, in the sierra betw. Rosario & Sto Domingo (native name *Casilepi[?]*), ~12–14 leagues E of Sto Domingo; cross set & first mass said

author: José Manuel Ruíz (San Vicente)
summary: Will report the mules & road-leagues used on his journey (mule-freight)

author: Ildefonso Bernal (Loreto)
summary: Wishes safe arrival at Monterey; mail charges ($21.2 rl) charged to the presidio habilitación; same to be done w/ P. Franco Iagn Valdés

author: Fr. Rea (Loreto)
summary: Encloses *pies de lista* & revista extracts (troop & marinería, Oct–Dec) + list of contented prisoners; returns May extracts the Habilitado Arrillaga rejected (24 Oct) for missing cert/signature

author: Rea (Loreto)
summary: Arrival of the goleta *Santa Saturnina* w/ 2 soldiers & families, 4 prisoners (by S. Señoría's order), & frontier-Sgt. José Manl Ruíz

author: Rea (Loreto)
summary: Cap on next year's Mexico orders at $8,000 (less if possible); surplus of the Monterey habilitación to the *caja de tres llaves* w/ the troop-retention & 10% gratification funds

author: Rea (Loreto)
summary: Acks R.O. transmitted 20 Aug 'sobre el modo de tratar á los buques ingleses' [cf. Doc 168]

author: (Borrador, supposed Borica)
summary: Baja→Alta fruit-trade order: continuing Fages's policy, mission produce (incl. wine & grape *aguardiente*) may enter Alta Cal. with the friars' papers; mezcal prohibited, and itinerant *mercaderes/traficantes* barred from peddling/*cambalachear* at the missions 'por los muchos perjuicios'

author: Sgt. José Manl Ruíz (S. Fernando)
summary: His steps to obtain mules & horses from the missions for the Governor's use (~50 head); leaving for Loreto

author: Borica (Sta Gertrudis)
summary: Borica's overland-journey logistics N through Baja: 15 pack-mules + saddle stock to come w/ 4 soldiers & arriero via S. Borja to S. Fernando; not to rush the beasts; report water-holes for clearing; approves leaving frontier command to Alf. Juan Pablo Grijalva

author: Rea (Loreto)
summary: Death of a soldier; need to replace him

author: Rea (Loreto)
summary: The soldiers named for re-enganche refused to sign the 8 memoriales — plead 'no hallarse aptos pª continuar en el servicio' & request retirement; 'Otra': mail delayed by the poor state of the beasts

author: Rea (Loreto)
summary: Arrival of the king's schooners *Saturnina* (8th, piloto Luis Tejada) & *Mexicana* (7th, Sgt. Robles): 626 fanegas maize, blacksmith tools; maritime mail storm-driven to Mulegé

author: Sgt. José Manl Ruíz (S. Vicente)
summary: Acks the 23 Sept fruit-trade order [Doc 185], will comply

author: Rea (Loreto)
summary: The bando on free trade in sea-otter pelts (extraction to Acapulco) filed in the Comandancia archive

author: Rea (Loreto)
summary: Pearl-fishery revenue evasion at Mulegé: the King's canoe (*arráez* Ventura Mabu, *armador* Manuel Salazar) dove the Mulegé *placeres* & crossed back to the other shore without paying the royal *quinto* or showing a concealed pearl; the diver pleads 'las cosas del soberano son delicadas' & gives his word; on Rea's arrival (23 Aug) he confronts him, the pearl & its fifth are surrendered, Buenavista's alférez notified; encloses the diver's reply of 30 Oct (Dupdo)

author: Rea (Loreto)
summary: Death of Sgt. Luis López (afternoon of 17 Oct)

author: Sgt. José Manl Ruíz (S. Vicente)
summary: Reconnaissance of water sources at Santa Catalina & San Pedro Mártir (w/ Fr. Tomás Belderrain, standing in for Pe Pres. Pallás) for the new mission/*resguardo* siting; doubts the gentiles' water lore; gentiles have damaged the *ganado mayor*, culprits unknown

author: Rea (Loreto)
summary: Departure of the king's schooners *Saturnina* & *Mexicana* from Puerto Escondido for San Blas (6th) (Dupdo)

author: Rea (Loreto)
summary: Next year's *memorias* not yet arrived; Ildefonso Bernal's alférez *despacho* delivered (incl. 23 Sept)

author: Arrillaga (San Diego)
summary: Encloses a state-report on both Californias at the close of 1793 for the Viceroy; formation delayed for want of the southern missions' inventories — ⚠ the *estado* itself is not present in the volume ('No se encuentra aquí el estado')

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Index of his official correspondence to the Viceroy (oficios of 26 Oct & 9 Sept): Borica's possession at Loreto 14 May; Fernandino missionaries (incl. Fr. Thomas de la Peña) retiring w/ licenses; ⭐ arrival/departure of the goleta *Valdés* from Manila under Lt. Cosme Bertodano (aid given); a $445 6r 9g claim for the SB presidio from errors in the 1781 *ajustes*; ⭐ the SF baluarte will be finished by year-end 1794 + query on which fund repays the habilitación's construction advances; Loreto presidio accounts 1781–89

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Frigate *Concepción* (1st piloto Salvador Menéndez) arrived Monterey 5 Jul, sailed for Sta Bárbara & San Diego; aboard, Frs. Diego Noboa & Thomas de la Peña + chaplain Fr. Bartolomé Gili embarked

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Commandants to keep observing orders until Borica reaches Monterey; meanwhile *partes* sent directly to the Viceroy

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Retirement (w/ Viceroy's license) of Fernandino missionaries Miguel Pieras, Bartolomé [Gili], Diego Noboa (embarked on the *Concepción*; Pieras to embark at San Diego) & Thomas de la Peña (declared 'inepto pª continuar en sus trabajos,' per medical cert)

author: José Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Will furnish in time a circumstantial muster of his presidio company per the formulary sent the 22nd

author: José Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Return of the war-donativo to the 4 Alta Cal. presidios — will at once repay the individuals of his company & restore to the SF/SB/SD habilitados the sums each contributed [cf. Doc 153, the Viceroy's refusal]

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Delivered the royally-approved invalid *cédulas* to Sgt. Manuel de Vargas & soldier [Mathl] Higuera

author: Hermenegildo Sal (Mission San Antonio)
summary: Sal reports himself back at S. Antonio from a commission (no novelty beyond what his *diario* records, to be told verbally); forwards the down-coast mail

author: Juan de Dios Ballén[?] (S. Luis)
summary: Will care for the 3 artisans at the mission as ordered [surname vowel unclear at scale 1 — Ballén/Ballón]

author: José Argüello (Monterey)
summary: The San José / Santa Clara affair (see E10): forwards Cabo Gabriel Moraga's report of a rumored gentile uprising round Pueblo S. José caused by Fr. Manuel Fernández's violent forced-conversion sweeps; Argüello sent Hermenegildo Sal to reconnoiter (Sal found the Indians quieted, the plot rumor untrue, but confirmed the friar punished 'el indio Macho,' which caused the village abandonment; Sal withheld the Santa Clara ministers' letter ('la carta del Rdo P. Sánchez')); Borica's marginal note (1 Dec) records he agreed w/ Pe Pres. Lasuén to 'moderar el celo' of Fr. Fernández. Enclosures: Nº1 Moraga→Argüello (S. José, 30 Oct), Nº2 Argüello→Sal (31 Oct, w/ PS to deny the friar an escort if he won't leave), Nº3 Sal→Argüello (S. José, 2 Nov)

author: José Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Will enforce the foreign-ship exclusion R.O.s (no foreign vessel admitted; if one comes, handle per Borica's order of 12 Nov) [cf. E1/E3]

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Artisan-family advances: $423.6r paid by the Guadalajara treasury to the families of artisans Manuel Ruíz, Salvador Pierres[?], Joaquín Rivera, Pedro González; will charge each his share

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: 'Cinco oficios': (1) French-prisoner internment method; (2) recognizes Branciforte as Viceroy; (3) soldiers' *cédulas* (cabo Pablo w/ Andrés Ramírez; one suspended pending a replacement for Cantúa, needed on the R. Hacda ranch); (4) a Gremios Mayores de Madrid ship at SF & the Gibraltar→San Blas/Californias commercial expedition (effects shipped at Cádiz); (5) ⭐ intercept all foreign-addressed mail & forward to the Viceroy [cf. Docs 8/68]

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Acks R.O. of 1793 granting California absolute exemption from duties for 10 more years; will collect half the *alcabala* this quinquennium; asks whether tithes (*diezmos*) are included (now collected by the Tte de Sal[?] & paid to the Rl Casa del Rosario)

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Account of blacksmith Pedro González ($3.5r)

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Annual accounts to the Gobierno Superior (per order of 3 Dec); needs the R. Hacda accounts of SF/SB/SD first; per Revillagigedo's disposition, the tobacco-branch surplus is to cover the *fábrica* material costs of the other presidios (except this church); must keep separate annual accounts with the missions S. Luis, S. Antonio, La Soledad, S. Carlos, Sta Cruz & Sta Clara

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Invalid-pension *formulario*: relations to be sent quadruplicate w/ surgeon's certificate & cause, omitting the men's own petitions

author: (Unsigned, supposed Borica)
summary: Instruction for the inspection of the mission escorts (Carmelo→Soledad→S. Antonio→S. Luis), to be run by Tte. Parrilla w/ Alf. Hermenegildo Sal & relief men (see E11): inspect by night the guard, sentinel, arms/munition, dress & mounts; art. 7 — treat the Indians 'sostenido, sin [ofenderlos?] en lo más mínimo, bien sean cristianos ó gentiles'; keep a circumstantial *diario*; if the Teniente is incapacitated, Sal takes over (art. 9); the Comandante to act 'con messura' so neither Padres, troops, nor Indians are aggrieved (art. 10)

author: Hermenegildo Sal (Mission San Carlos)
summary: Tte. Parrilla disrupted the inspection's order & confessed himself incapable of keeping the prescribed *diario* (Sal had to show him a copy of the Governor's Instruction); Sal formed the padrón of Mission San Carlos: 887 souls; the commission departs for La Soledad after first mass

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Acks order on signing the monthly *estados de fuerza* of the company

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Will regulate the *Memorias* of goods to order from Mexico after year-end inventory of troop clothing/arms/mounts — acquiring *del país* where possible to omit San Blas shipment, excluding luxury goods

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Acks the bimonthly escort-inspection order (will commission the alférez alternately per the Instruction copied at Sav. pp.138–9 [Doc 216]); will send each escort its spare cartridges & flints

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Acks the order not to dismember the mission escorts on any pretext; but notes escorts *are* thinned when soldiers must escort traveling Padres — asks Borica's guidance

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Will uniform the presidio troops per the royal *reglamento*: order classes for cabo & soldiers 'con sola vuelta, y collarín, encarnado… se omitan las solapas' (red cuff & collar, no lapels)

author: José Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Vancouver's frigate *Discovery* anchored at Monterey 5 Nov (see E12): came only to collect deserters & to forward Court *pliegos* he expected in Cal.; finding none, begged a courier toward San Diego (had charged Tte. Grajera) & that a recommended letter be forwarded to the first English ship there. Vancouver asked if there'd be 'the same retribución as last year' — Argüello answered NO, he now had orders to give *abrigo* to ships of Vancouver's nation, warships & merchantmen, under the Alliance — which Vancouver & the Viceroy's letter 'celebró infinito'

author: Argüello (Monterey)
themes: chatham; puget; vancouver; deserters; nootka; monterey
summary: The brig *Chatham*, Capt. Peter Puget, of Vancouver's expedition, arrived 2 Nov (sailed in company w/ Vancouver from Nootka 16 Oct); claimed the deserters — of the 6, only 2 his, handed over today; the other 4 when Vancouver arrives, w/ the cost account

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Suspended issuing *vestuarios* to the troop & *avíos* to families (per order of 28 Sept)

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Measure to prevent the English deserters' flight: two tried to flee the presidio, so he put them (and two others) in *grillos*, with a *centinela de vista* on the rest

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Muster & force-state; dispatched to Mission S. Luis 25 pack-mules & 55 tame horses for Borica's use (per order of 8 Oct)

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Sends 25 mules, 55 horses & an escort (sgt, cabo, 9 soldiers, arriero) for Borica's journey

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Holds the Viceroy's letter for Vancouver (per oficio of 20 Aug); will deliver it on his arrival or, failing that, send it to London via the first English ship

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: The 6 English deserters (brought by the *Concepción*): three superior orders (20 Aug) to deliver them to Vancouver or the first British ship, recovering costs amicably; the resolution kept reserved to stop them making 'esfuerzos extraordinarios pª huirse'; the men, idle on rations, expected repatriation (some sought baptism, others enlistment — denied); if no English ship comes, to be sent to Nootka w/ the charge-document

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Will give the aid requested by Brig. José Manl de Álava (expected on the frigate *Princesa*) per the Viceroy's order [cf. Doc 169]

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Frigate *Aranzazu* (Capt. José Tobar) arrived 22nd, could not carry the lumber to San Diego, left 28th — the sick embarked on it

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Per oficio of 20 Aug: henceforth English ships, war or merchant, to be permitted in port & sheltered 'en cualquier evento' [the explicit alliance order behind E12]

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Sent the *abalorios* (trade beads) to the San Diego commandant

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Receipt of 8 cargo pieces from habilitado-gen. Manuel Cárcaba on the *Concepción* (Menéndez); a wine barrel found leaking (shipped thus at San Blas); encloses the Memorias/accounts

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Had Borica recognized as Governor at the Monterey presidio (2 Aug) & circulated the order to the posts of his jurisdiction

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Acks R.O. of 1 Oct 1793 (rec. 10 Jun); will give the expedition the needed aid & report personnel [cf. Docs 176/211, Hernán Márquez]

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Will keep executing Arrillaga's orders until Borica reaches Monterey

author: (Arrillaga, unsigned) (Loreto)
summary: Grants him a cadet's place in the Monterey cavalry company (per his 23 Apr petition); to transfer by the first ship from San Blas

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Order to load lumber on the *Aranzazu* (expected under Dn Juan Kendrick[?] — see E13) for the explanadas (gun platforms) & habilitación house of the *baluarte* to be built at the San Diego presidio

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Since Kendrick did not come & Tobar came in his place on the *Aranzazu*, asks Tobar to take charge of carrying the urgently-needed lumber to San Diego

author: ⭐⭐⭐ George Vancouver (aboard the *Discovery*, Monterey)
summary: Verbatim letter on the deserters: of the 6, only three are British subjects — Diego Acheson & Diego Lanson (Landon) (deserted the *Chatham* at Monterey 6 Jan 1793) and Tomás Smith (deserted the urca *Dédalo/Daedalus* at San Diego 7 Dec 1793; my earlier '[Owen?]' → corrected to Smith per Doc 264); he does not claim the other three (deserters of the *Dédalo*, Dec 1792); cannot pay the costs himself but will lay an itemized account before the Lords Commissioners of the Admiralty in London. (Spanish translation tied to Brig. Álava.)

author: ⭐⭐ Juan Kendrick (Monterey)
summary: Demands the return of the chaplain he brought (now at Mission Carmelo) for his Nootka-bound ships (sailing in 2–3 days); the Pe Presidente claims no authority to appoint one; threatens to hold the friars responsible 'delante de Dios y del Rey' for accidents to 'el pasto espiritual' on his voyage

author: Juan Kendrick (Monterey)
summary: Sends a list of the sick aboard; since the *Concepción* cannot carry them, asks to land them ashore, offering to take them back on his return from Nootka

author: Juan Kendrick (Monterey)
summary: The chaplain tangle: per Nootka cmdt Ramón Saavedra's instructions he must transfer a chaplain to his ship; the *Concepción* (Menéndez) is ordered to Acapulco w/ the sick friars; he needs a chaplain + *colegial* lest a man die at sea 'sin los sacramentos'

author: Juan Kendrick (Monterey)
summary: Asks that the troops Lt. Juan Matute left at Monterey be embarked on his ship for transport to Nootka (per Saavedra), to spare the *marinería*

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Re chaplain & Nootka supplies: can furnish most of the Nootka cmdt's requested list except pigs & pharmacy goods (lacking here); refers him to the Monterey habilitado

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Authorizes landing the sick, but warns 'aquí no hay proporción de que se cuiden como deben, ni tal vez habrá medicinas, solo sí carne y maíz'; replacements scarce, may spare 2–3 healthy men

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Re chaplain: returns the *Concepción* cmdr's letter; encloses Fr. Magín Catalá's reply to the request to serve as chaplain on Kendrick's ship; if the *Concepción*'s chaplain transfers, the Pe Presidente will fill the vacancy

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Orders a Nootka mason (*albañil*) — left at San Francisco by the former Nootka cmdt Juan Matute *pª el establecimto de Bodega* — to embark on the *Aranzazu*, reclaimed by the Nootka commandant

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: The troops Matute left at SF were already handed (by SF order) to the *Concepción* by Sal; Kendrick must apply to the *Concepción*'s cmdr — no longer in the Cal. govt's power

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Asks a chaplain for the *Aranzazu* (Nootka→S. Blas via Monterey); Fr. Magín Catalá served thus far but refuses to continue — requests a friar with leave to return to his college, or a diocesan supernumerary

author: Juan Kendrick (Monterey)
summary: Asks Arrillaga to expedite the Nootka supplies (per Saavedra's list), particularly the means to maintain the cattle

author: [Hervosa?] (San Blas)
summary: Accounts of surgeon Dn Pablo Soler (for the Monterey habilitado's advances): salary from his Veracruz landing 27 Dec 1789, daily ration, deductions incl. a Gremios de Madrid sum withheld at Guadalajara. Copy made at Monterey 17 Feb 1794, w/ Gov. Romeu's rubric

author: ⭐⭐⭐ Juan Fco de la Bodega y Quadra (aboard the goleta *Activa*, Monterey)
summary: The foreign-ship policy in his own hand (see E14): the London convention bars foreign ships from Spanish establishments, but one that arrives by accident 'sin órden de S.E., ó permiso de S.E.' — 'como se ha verificado por esta vez, con el Comandante Vancouver' — is to get 'los precisos auxilios que manda la Hospitalidad, sin permitirles se demoren'

author: Fr. Magín Catalá (Monterey)
summary: Refuses to continue as chaplain on the *Aranzazu* to Nootka (he has already 'residido 13 meses' there; a New-California doctrinero subject to the Pe Presidente); proposes instead that the *Concepción*'s chaplain shift to the *Aranzazu* and Fr. Bartolomé Gili ship on the *Concepción*

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Accepts Gili's offer to ship as chaplain on the *Concepción* (encloses the *Concepción* cmdr's letter)

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Encloses the two frigate-commanders' letters; asks Catalá to cover the *Aranzazu* chaplaincy for the Nootka commandant's need

author: Fr. Fermín Fco de Lasuén (San Francisco)
summary: Declares himself without authority to compel any missionary to go as chaplain on the *Aranzazu*; notes that ailing friars aboard the *Concepción* might agree to return Acapulco→San Blas if asked

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
themes: vancouver; deserters; bodega-quadra; anglo-spanish; fiscal; monterey
summary: Vancouver-deserter cost dossier: encloses Viceroy Revillagigedo's order (Mexico, 22 Jan 1794) to return the 5 English deserters (carried on the *Concepción* to Nootka) to Vancouver & recover their keep; when handing them over, also reclaim the *three Spanish sailors* Marine Cmdt Juan de la Bodega [y Quadra] gave Vancouver in their place; cost $298.3½ rl; w/ Francisco Hijosa's San Blas account (26 Apr 1794) & Menéndez's Nº3. ⚠ numbers vary (6 held / 5 ordered returned / 3 claimed British)

author: (Borrador, Argüello)
summary: Handed over 2 of the 6 deserters (those Puget claimed as his ship's): soldier Diego (James) Landon & smith Diego (James) Acheson; asks a receipt; cost of the two = $86.5 rl

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Handing over the deserters: he holds 4 (the other 2 went to Puget); encloses the full cost dossier — $747.1 rl 1¾ gr for the 6 (San Blas + Monterey keep)

author: (Borrador, Argüello) (Monterey)
summary: Orders him to give *plazas* on his frigate to the 3 foreign deserters Vancouver refused — Robert Spring (Dane) and Antonio Juan & Manuel José (Portuguese) — on condition they repay their *armada* rations

author: Argüello (Monterey)
summary: Reply to V's 16 Nov letter: V claims only the 3 British — Diego Acheson, Diego Lanson, Tomás Smith — leaving the Dane Roberto Espring & Portuguese Antº Juan & Manl José; itemizes the 3 Britons' keep ($325.5 rl 7½ gr) for the Admiralty; asks Vancouver's 'gracia' (clemency) for the three

author: José Argüello (Habilitado, Monterey)
summary: Resumen of the 3 non-British deserters' costs: Robert Spring $133.5.2, Antº Juan $135.5.2, Manl José $152.1.6 — total $421.3.6

author: [Salvador] Hidalgo (Monterey)
summary: Accepts the 3 non-British deserters onto his frigate — will give them *plazas* & pay the R. Hacienda the $421.3.6 they owe

author: Pedro de Nava (Cmdt-Gen. Prov. Internas, Chihuahua)
summary: Acks Borica's accession (took possession 14 May); orders the Chihuahua treasury to settle Borica's prior salary as Ayudante Inspector (to his apoderado Sabino de la Romana)

author: Borica (Monterey)
summary: Uniform order: no insignia beyond the *reglamento de Presidios* (tít. 3), no 'adorno ridículo'; only addition is red *chacos*; Mexico orders to specify 'vuelta y collarín encarnado, y no solapa' [cf. Doc 222]

author: Borica (Monterey)
summary: Asks what gifts Vancouver gave the Monterey presidio in 1792–93, which survive, & where the missing went [Borica auditing Vancouver's gifts province-wide; cf. E7 at Sta Bárbara]

author: Borica (Monterey)
summary: Orders the establishment of primary schools (*escuelas de primeras letras*) at the presidios & the pueblos of LA & San José (see E15): asks for fit schoolmasters, a salary, & a funding *arbitrio* — citing Nueva Vizcaya presidios where soldiers give $2/yr

author: Borica (Monterey)
summary: Encloses a $423.6 rl account vs various artisans (supplies to their families by the Guadalajara treasury) to charge each his share [cf. Doc 210]

author: Borica (Monterey)
summary: Year-end presidio documents to come 'con la debida puntualidad'

author: Borica (Monterey)
summary: The 10-year exemption from duties incl. *alcabala* for the San Blas & California commerce runs from the R.O.'s date (18 Dec); the half-*alcabala* of the quinquennium still owed to R. Hacienda; none of these provisions repeals tithe collection [cf. Doc 212]

author: Borica (Monterey)
summary: Baja of a soldier incurably ill (per surgeon Pablo Soler's cert); alta of Don Juan de Ortega, son of the retired Capt. José Francisco de Ortega, as a *Distinguido*

author: Borica (Monterey)
summary: Will not acknowledge the *estados* of revista & monthly states until each year's end

author: Borica (Monterey)
summary: Requests a full register of all presidio prisoners — class, names, crimes, whether under *sumaria*, where held, & other particulars of each case

author: Borica (Monterey)
summary: Sends the *formularios* for the company's monthly documents

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Per Viceroy's order (22 Jan): list the Provisional Company members owed the gratification for *effectos* in deposit, & those who worked on the presidio works through completion — but the church *fábrica* kept separate

author: Virrey Revillagigedo (México)
summary: Order on the presidio works (per Junta Superior, 28 Dec 1793): carry them to completion; the sums advanced by the companies to be reimbursed by the Real Hacienda (paid from Tobacco revenue, as *reintegro*); remaining work to be separately estimated & finished 'con solidez y economía'; the presidial soldiers who worked & still work on the fortifications *sin salario* to be recompensed somehow. Copy authorized Monterey 23 Jul 1794 (Arrillaga)

author: Borica (Monterey)
summary: The mission escorts never to be left incomplete on any pretext; commandant held responsible [cf. Doc 221]

author: Borica (Loreto)
summary: Until the new Gov. reaches Monterey, the commandant deals with Arrillaga (ex-Gob. intº), obeying his orders — issued the day Borica took possession at Loreto

author: Borica (Monterey)
summary: Detailed annual supply-*memoria* instruction: weigh the state of troop & family clothing, arms, mounts & warehouse stocks; exclude luxury goods from the troop *memorias* (velvet skirts, fine lace, silk taffeta, etc. — officers order those in their own *memorias particulares*); buy soap, blankets, *jerga* in the Peninsula where cheaper than San Blas; request the balance in coin; the Márquez / Gremios Mayores trade

author: Borica (Loreto)
summary: Per R.O. of 1 Oct 1793 (Viceroy 7 Apr): give Don Román Márquez's ship 'todos los auxilios posibles' if it reaches port, & report exactly its burden, force, captain, crew & trade. ⚠ Name variant — Docs 176/211/237 read 'Hernán Márquez'; this clearer scale-1 scan reads Román (same person; reconcile, lean *Román*)

author: Borica (Monterey)
summary: General foreign-ship EXCLUSION order, synthesizing the whole policy (verified scale 1; see E16): no foreign ships in American ports — expel any arrival 'sin pretexto ni excusa,' no one 'por privilegiada q[ue] sea' to trade 'directa ó indirectamente,' nor even supply victuals; only in the remote case of urgent necessity give 'los precisos auxilios que exige la Hospitalidad, y pida el Derecho de Gentes,' with precautions 'á q[ue] no tomen conocimto del país'; and names Vancouver & Puget (in port w/ the *Descubierta* & *Chatham*) — already given full aid at Monterey, so any request at other ports 'deberá ser destituida,' made to understand the orders so they retire

author: Manuel Rodríguez (México)
summary: Petitions a cadet's place (Monterey or Loreto company); margin: Borica's decree granting it (Loreto, 15 Jun); encloses his memorial to the Viceroy (16 Nov 1793, age 24) & Manuel Cárcaba's pledge (his relative, the habilitado-gen.) to support him $20–25/mo

author: Rodríguez (México)
summary: Thanks for the cadet place; will report to his post promptly

author: Arrillaga (Gob. intº)
themes: both-californias; state-report; castillo-san-joaquin; bodega-failure; pueblo-papers; indigenous-resistance; mission-mortality; presidio-decay
summary: 'Estado de las Californias' / Papel de Puntos — his 32-point handover state-of-the-province report (⚠ *trunco*; see E17). Highlights: Indian-fire *bandos* (pt.2); 1792 pueblo unrest + San José land-*suerte* distribution in property, inalienable (pts.4–5); Monterey-river settlers (pt.6); soldier cattle-limits 3–4 cows SD/SB/Monterey (pt.9); ⭐⭐ SF baluarte 'ya puede hallarse al concluir' while SD & Monterey defenses suspended (pt.13); San Blas artisans + the mason for the *establecimto de Bodega 'que no se verificó'* → the Castillo (pt.14, = Doc 250); SF presidio & troop quarters 'en infeliz estado', the SF troops did most of the fort work (pt.16); artisan-instruction program placing craftsmen to teach Indians at Sta Cruz/S. Carlos/Monterey/S. Luis (pts.17–21); unbuilt gristmill (pt.19); ⭐⭐ indigenous-resistance catalog (pts.22–25, see E17): San Luis conspiracy ringleaders Miguel & Morillo → SF fort forced labor w/ Ildefonso/Constantino/Pablo; Charquín (SF mission) jailed for harboring runaways; a plot to burn Mission San Miguel & kill the escort; ⭐⭐ high child/adult mortality at Missions SF & Sta Clara (pt.29); muster-readiness gaps — weekly musket drill, night sentinels asleep (pts.26–28); Tte León Parrilla deemed 'insuficiente' (pt.31, cf. E11)

author: Vallejo
summary: Vecinos refuse faenas — say they're soldiers without stipendio; must pay 2 pesos for mail courier. Indians near presa; Ignacio Castro fired and killed a gentile with horse

author: Vallejo
summary: Capitanejo Pala offers gentile workers for hemp; Indians willing if work near houses — worried about attacks on families. Sends muestras del cáñamo

author: Vallejo
summary: Hemp floreciendo; vecino Castro insolent/refused orders; dispatched 30 men for Mission SJ and presa

author: Vallejo
summary: Sent 20 hombres armados with Sargento Amador; returned sin novedad. Monthly review; target practice: 44 bombas, 88 cartuchos; drum calls replace bells (14 falta de campanas); guardia repairs; yegua-eating gentiles reported by capitanejo Pala

author: Vallejo
summary: 4 men to support Amador; carrastras for grinding wheat (8–10 needed); order to give land to vecinos without it; alcalde-comandante interview on moving pueblo to other side of river for flood protection

author: Vallejo
summary: Cabo Mateo arrived; beneficio del cáñamo begins; indios voluntarios ready and 'very content for being freed from other faenas y tareas that oppressed them'

author: Vallejo
summary: People busy with faenas agrícolas; asks 100–200 pesos to buy harina; hemp ready, Indians content; asks about Sta Clara lands

author: Vallejo
summary: Nothing important (Dec 9); move pueblo houses to other side for floods; bridge construction asked; hemp to Sta Clara padres for spinning; 15 gentile workers distributed among vecinos, paid punctually

author: (unsigned), SJ
summary: MILITARY CONSCRIPTION CENSUS: 14 named young men apt for service — Julián Zaenz, José Archuleta, José Amezquita, Fco. Ma. Amezquita, José Ma. Aceves, Juan Ma. Hernández, Romualdo González, Isidoro Soto, Fco. Ma. Soto, Joaquín Buelna, Ignacio Dolores Mesa, Marcos Altamirano, Sebastián Butrón, Pascual Villavicencio. Notes on each family situation

author: Vallejo
summary: Hemp in pila awaiting Cabo Mateo; hemp reaped; carreta de harina to presidio; aguardiente de caña operational in Oct

author: Vallejo, SJ
summary: Wheat harvest report: 23 vecinos + 15 inválidos = 3,710 fanegas; also sends lists of escort-able men and landless vecinos

author: Vallejo
summary: English ship at Santa Cruz: agreed with Alcalde to march to provide help if necessary. Fr. Barcanilla complains about vecinos' livestock trespassing on Mission SJ lands — 'son propiedad de estos Indios'

author: Vallejo
summary: Ordered vecinos off Mission SJ pastures — 'no es muy fácil el hacerles olvidar aquel terreno sino es con pólvora y balas.' Hemp seed: 8 fanegas; 1 to San Carlos, 1 to Sta Clara

author: Vallejo
summary: Hemp beneficio done by 29th; caballada moved from Mission SJ lands to Laguna Seca; debts: González and Altamirano owe 1–6 years

author: Vallejo
summary: Hemp processing despite fog/sun problems; Cabo Mateo reluctant — delivered about 1 fanega with 'repugnancia'; 8 tercios of sebo sent; presos conducted to SF

author: Vallejo
summary: Bridge construction (laboring maderas); maize harvest 1,903 fanegas, beans 160 fanegas; asks about cabo Pacheco taking turns at guardia; requests calzadas de fierro y acero for hemp tools; letter from Teniente Coronel Alberni

author: Vallejo
summary: Road bad — can't deliver provisions to Sta Cruz escolta; hemp planting concluded; 3 almudes seed to Sta Clara

author: Moraga
summary: Military readiness: arms from SF — 10 frazadas de escopeta + distributions from Argüello/Sal. Carpenter Pablo Véjar at mission. 'Negro named Pablo Cibrián' settled in pueblo (from Paquebot San Carlos). Soldados Juan Mejía y Joaquín Mesa

author: Moraga, SJ
summary: MILITARY FITNESS CENSUS: Armados y aptos 20; Soldados inválidos 8; Aptos sin armas 12 (8 inválidos); Inaptos 9 (4 inválidos); Muchachos 13. Total 61, of which 30 hábiles for military service

author: Moraga
summary: Transfer of command: Moraga hands over documents + archive + 346 cabezas de borregas to Vallejo. Inventario de efectos y documentos 1785–1797; inventario de armamentos y municiones — formal transfer of comisionado

author: Vallejo
summary: Hemp first escarda y riego done; Moraga left for Sta Cruz; hemp planting underway, Cabo Mateo returning

author: Amador
summary: CONSTRUCTION DIARY at Mission SJ: casas de padres (8 pieces, 5 varas each), Guardia (8×5 varas), Almacén (5 varas sq), 20 casas de soldados, corral (30+ varas). Bueyes exhausted from hauling. Day-by-day diary: punished 2 soldiers (Sánchez struck Indian, Duarte drunk); 3 gentiles came wanting Christianity but feared Sacelanes retaliation; Fr. Muñoz left for SF; Padre Barcanilla to Sta Clara

author: Amador
summary: Sacelanes resistance intelligence: Capitán Gentil near mission reports gentile leaders planning to kill padres + soldiers. Amador proposes punitive expedition: 'cortar el hilo de este maligno cáncer' — requests 15–20 men. Sacelanes vecinos of Valle de SJ 'de la parte de la playa en frente de San Francisco'

author: Ruiz
summary: Sta Catalina founding: order from Arrillaga; escoltas of San Pedro Mártir + San Vicente assembled; 14 hombres; Fr. José Loriente to verify

author: Ruiz
summary: Frontier vigilance order from Marqués de Branciforte published

author: Ruiz
summary: War declaration against Britain acknowledged: will patrol coasts, exercise troops in arms, provide cartucheras; vecinos and mission Indians armed and weapons kept in guardia

author: Ruiz/Pons/Saavedra
summary: Arms + munitions delivered by Arrillaga; auxilio to Goycoechea for Monterey; extraordinary auxilio to Córdoba; compostura of paquebot

author: Saavedra
summary: San Carlos wreck at SF — total loss of royal tabaco; documents of averías enclosed. Also: Aranzazu arrives SF after 29 days

author: Lizalde, SJC
summary: MISSION SAN LUIS REY FOUNDING DIARY: Soldado Lizalde escorts R.P. Presidente Lasuén + Fr. Juan Santiago + 7 soldiers + 5 Indians from SJC. Days: Ánimas → Lagunitas → Temecula → Pauma → Pullala → SJC. Lasuén personally scouted the site

author: García
summary: Soldier's wife Antonia Marcelina Leal paid her husband's salary at San Blas

author: Argüello + Sal
summary: Financial documents: glosa of Real Hacienda accounts Monterey 1782–97; Sal presents 6 piezas originales; fábrica accounts 1781–92; signed by Ortega, Sal. Sal presents SF Real Hacienda accounts

author: Peralta
summary: Mission of Sta Cruz no novedad; asked missionaries about feeding Branciforte pobladores — 'tan maíz no pueden porque no tienen mas de la pura ración para la escolta'

author: Alvarado, LA
summary: LA MONTHLY DIARY Feb 1797: election of alcaldes/regidores; named recuas (Cornelio Avila 450 fan. maíz, Quinto Zuniga 24, Bartolo Tapia 27 to SD); Casimiro Borcles 30 mulas maíz to SB; cabo Juan Ramos 14 soldiers + recua; 32 vecinos + 72 mujeres to misa at San Gabriel; ganado menor increased to 300 borregas (50 per 6 vecinos)

author: Ruiz, SBV; Olivera, Purísima
summary: SBV diary Jan: soldiers, vecinos, recuas of maíz passing through; target practice — 4 men, 16 cartuchos, 13 balas recovered. Purísima diary Jan/Feb: nada notable

author: Alvarado, LA; Cota, SB; Ruiz, San Vicente
summary: LA diary Feb: nada notable. Cota diary: recorrer escoltas Norte, nada notable. Ruiz diary San Vicente: nothing except Innocencio Mesa + wife murdered by gentiles Jul 11; retaliatory killing because 'un padre antes había matado algunos gentiles de este Valle'

author: Ruiz
summary: Mysterious cannon-like noises heard at sea Jul 11, 5am, in Sto Domingo + all frontier escoltas; 3 more on Jul 13. Guardia/capilla/casa de R.P. finished. Preso gentil (Mesa's killer) held in goaleta; several salidas to capture accomplices

author: Ruiz
summary: Sta Catalina founding acknowledged; cañoncito from SD in case of attack; gentiles of Mission Trinidad + Mesa's widow identified the killer; killer given 50 azotes in presence of mission Indians; accomplices still at large

author: Ruiz
summary: At Sta Catalina: tropa + neófitos must treat gentiles with 'amor y caridad'; 3 cañoncitos with pertrechos for defense; buque from China expected, convoyed by war frigate; gentile preso escapes at Mission San Agustín

author: Ruiz
summary: Gentiles of new foundation peaceful; cimarrones tried stealing bestias; captured gentiles punished with 50 azotes + sent to SD. Salgado diary: escoltas of San Fernando, Rosario, San Pedro Mártir, Sto Domingo — nada notable

author: Ruiz
summary: Sta Catalina fortified: baluarte with troneras for cañones + fusiles; gentiles peaceful; 2 soldiers imprisoned for letting prisoner escape; gentiles peaceful, gone to Río Colorado

author: Borica/Bernal
summary: Deserter Francisco Ampurano: traced to sierra of Sta Catalina, then crossed to Río Colorado — Indians warned him not to go but he crossed to other side

author: Bernal
summary: Released soldado Manuel Villa (let Indian presos go). BC FRONTIER GARRISON TABLE: San Vicente 12, Sto Thomas 5, Sto Domingo 2, San Pedro 6, Rosario 2, San Fernando 1, San Borja 2, Sta Gertrudis 1 = 31 soldiers total

author: Bernal
summary: Arrillaga left for Loreto Jan 11; Bernal diary of visita to frontier missions — nada notable; Olivera diary Purísima Jan — nada notable

author: Bernal/Ruiz
summary: BC garrison table (duplicate): same 31 soldiers. Viceroy sending 2 frigates de guerra to patrol California coasts — Ruiz acknowledges, will prepare defense

author: Ruiz
summary: Diarios of inspection visits to all frontier escoltas (San Fernando, Rosario, San Pedro Mártir, Sto Domingo, Sto Thomas, San Vicente). Arms repair satisfactory; Sta Catalina construction with 12 soldiers, 1 cabo, sargento

author: Ruiz
summary: Auxilio to José Pons (mulas + escort to Loreto); Mesa's killers near Sta Catalina — left with 6 men but they retreat to secure lomas. Indian interpreter killed by gentiles; 2 boys + 1 man killed near Sto Domingo. Orders from Arrillaga to apprehend

author: Ruiz
summary: Road to Sta Catalina; arms to San Miguel mission (cañoncito + 7 fusiles); freed Indian presos José Manuel y Jacinto to San Pedro Mártir with warning; coastal vigilance — watch for enemy buques; 16–17 sails spotted off southern coasts from San Miguel mission

author: Ruiz/Bernal
summary: Cabo reports sails (10 + 7) off southern coasts — unconfirmed. Soldier at Sto Domingo: entered cuarto de solteras, committed excesos y escándalos; resisted punishment; fought the cabo with espada; subdued and given cueros + grillos

author: Bernal/Ruiz
summary: 5 Indian presos delivered — 1 died en route to Loreto. Ruiz diary: visita from San Fernando to Sto Domingo, noting defects in tropa and naturales

author: Ruiz/Branciforte
summary: Recaptured escaped Indian put in grillos. Branciforte bando: prohibits sale of aguardiente de caña fabricated before the prohibition was lifted

author: Branciforte
summary: Royal indulto: general pardon for all prisoners jailed for making chinguirito (illegal cane liquor). Guatemala intelligence: noticias of possible threats to the peninsula — asks if socorro from Sonora needed; coordinate with Arrillaga

author: Guatemala intelligence
summary: English sails in Pacific: Dr. Juan de Dios Juarros reports 16–17 English sails near coast of San Miguel y Zacatecoluca; 4 fired at Sonsonate; milicias mobilized; Dr. Gaspar Juarros: 'sustos de guerra en el mar del Sur.' Señora Manrique: 'por Trujillo y el Castillo de San Juan…no hay resistencia'

author: Branciforte
summary: Fragatas Concepción + Princesa ordered to patrol all California coasts — defense + reconnaissance of puertos, calas, surgideros, playas. 200 fusiles + 17,000 piedras de chispa shipped from Mexico to San Blas. 17 pobladores (3 families) left Guadalajara for Villa de Branciforte
VILLA DE BRANCIFORTE SETTLER ROSTER from Guadalajara: names, oficios (trades), edad (age), calidad (race — español, mestizo), estado civil. ~8–10 entries in tabular format

author: Branciforte
summary: Dominican PP Fray Antonio + Fray Rafael Cavallero granted license to go to Mexico for medical cure; Real Orden on señalamientos for families in Spain

author: Branciforte
summary: SMALLPOX PREVENTION BANDO (13 articles): quarantine houses, mandatory reporting, city manzana system, cordón sanitario, quarantine for travelers, fumigation bonfires, mail fumigation with azufre, voluntary inoculation (tested in Oaxaca/Tehuantepec), provisional hospitals, cementerios outside churches, rogativas, sociedades de caridad. Epidemic traced from Guatemala → Oaxaca since 1795

author: Branciforte
themes: Viceroy; BC; defense; Yuma; Río Colorado
summary: Fortify Sta Catalina with cañones (2–3 libras) from San Miguel + Loreto — in case Yumas del río Colorado attack the new mission

author: Branciforte
summary: Branciforte recalled as Viceroy — replaced by Miguel José de Azanza (Secretario de Estado y del Despacho de la Guerra). King orders Branciforte return to Spain on same navío

author: Branciforte
summary: Service records every 2 years; inválido certificate Sgt. Ignacio Alvarado; Real Orden on missionary licencias — travel costs paid; licencias from Gov. Monterey + prelados suffice for return from California

author: Branciforte
summary: Missionary return travel rules cont. San Ildefonso Oct 16, 1794, Eugenio de Llaguno. Superiores of Sto Domingo + San Francisco notified. Promoted José Pérez Fernández to Teniente (Loreto) replacing Roa; cadete Manuel Rodríguez to Alférez

author: Branciforte
summary: Hemp progress approved: satisfied with missions' siembra y cultivo; promises to send harvest to San Blas

author: Branciforte
summary: Acknowledges receipt of oficios #318–365; San Carlos departed Loreto, Aranzazu departed SD, both returned to San Blas safely

author: Branciforte
summary: Fragata Princesa to SD + canal; Concepción to SF Mar 15; Aranzazu sent to Cavite (Philippines) by royal order — constructor + maestranza for Arsenal. Only 2 fragatas left for California defense

author: Branciforte
summary: Bulas de defuntos correction; 480 Bulas de Composición to be burned. Arrillaga's Río Colorado reconnaissance — Fiscal reviews his report on Indians of inmediaciones del Colorado, reconnaissance of terrain between this and frontier missions
Title page — Provincial State Papers, Tomo XVI, 1797 (C-A 9)

author: Fernando de Herrera
summary: Encloses the originals of the *juicios*/accounts of the Loreto presidio (1787–89–91–92) + troop-adjustment accounts (1788–92), to comply with the Tribunal's orders; return when diligenced

author: Herrera
summary: On the loss of documents — given accounts-judgments lost in transit, orders a receipt by return mail for all packets sent, noting Treasury/Administration/branch/year

author: Herrera
summary: Liquidated the account of what was spent on the artisans destined for the Peninsula [Baja], 1796; 95 p 4 r 9 g deducted in favor of the Real Hacienda; warns the Monterey presidio habilitado to keep proper accounts

author: Juan de la Fuente
summary: Sends the certified copy of the Tribunal's resolution in the account of the artisans destined to teach the Mission Indians, formed by the Monterey habilitado for 1795

author: Beltrán
summary: *Informe* reconciling the 1795 artisans' families' accounts (Guadalajara Treasury) + the Loreto/Monterey presidio account discrepancies (1791–95): artisan Miguel Ruiz, Manuel Cárcaba (911 p owed by Monterey), the Rivera brothers. Closes with the Tribunal's *decreto* (21 Nov 1797) ordering the habilitado-general to reintegrate 911 p 4 r

author: (auditor)
summary: 'Satisfacción' — point-by-point reply to the audit objections (*reparos*) against the liquidated judgment of the Loreto presidio Real Hacienda accounts 1787–92 (objections numbered through ~18, then a 7-point set): *plomo de tiradura*, weights, the habilitado being 'recién venido,' etc. ⭐ Objection 7 concerns the San Blas Comisaría's 1792–93 supplies to the ships *San Carlos*, *Concepción* & the goleta *Saturnina* (the Bodega/Vancouver-era vessels) — still unsettled. Ends 'Omito el responder'

author: Juan de la Fuente
summary: *Recibo de cuentas* — acknowledges receipt of the judgment of the Santa Bárbara *fábrica* account, 1784–1794

author: De la Fuente
summary: *Recibo* — acknowledges the San Francisco presidio account, 1 Aug 1793 – 30 Nov 1794

author: De la Fuente
summary: *Recibo* — acknowledges the four judgments of the San Diego presidio accounts (1787–89, 1790–92), the troop-adjustments (1781–92), and the Bulas branch (1788–89)

author: De la Fuente
summary: *Recibo* — received the *juicio* of the San Diego presidio *fábrica* (church-works) account, 1781–95

author: De la Fuente
summary: *Recibo* — received the three judgments of the Monterey church account (1 Apr 1791–31 Dec 1795), the Real Hacienda account of the same presidio (1795), and what was spent on the *Esplanada*, *Garita* & *Casa Mata* (1 Jan–26 Sep 1795) — i.e. Monterey fortification works

author: Fernando Herrera
summary: *Recibo de Cuentas / Prevención* — Lt.-Col. Manuel de García, habilitado-general of the five presidios, has presented the Loreto Real Hacienda accounts for 1793 & 1794; Tribunal orders the Gov. again to press him for the 1795 account, still not received

author: Herrera
summary: *Demora de documentos* — 38 of 42 judgments received; the 4 outstanding still not answered by Capt. José Zúñiga: two on the Monterey Real Hacienda accounts (1787–92), one on the troop-adjustment (1781–92), one on the bullets (*Balas*) account (1788–89)

author: Herrera
summary: *Demora de documentos* — the Loreto Real Hacienda accounts for 1793, 1794 & 1795 still not remitted; Tribunal orders providences so the responsible official forms & presents them

author: José Laso & José Vildósola
summary: *Envío de documentos* — forward 480 *sumarios de bulas de difuntos* (2-real, current biennium) to supply the five presidios of the Peninsula, 96 each

author: Andrés de Mendívil
summary: *Demora de Cuentas* — the Baja habilitados have rendered their post-office (*estafetas*) accounts for 1796, except Loreto (also missing 1795); orders Loreto to present both years without further delay

author: Cabildo of México
summary: *Sobre cierta limosna* — asks Borica to contribute alms toward the canonization of the Protomártir Felipe de Jesús (patron of the city, cause stalled 'nearly two centuries'; the three Viceroys & Archbishops back it), and to license a public collection in all places under his government

author: engineer Alberto de Córdova
summary: *Sobre cierta Representación* — reports on the settlers' petition to move the pueblo of San José out of flood-reach: found higher ground ¼-league off and told the *vecindario* to stake it, but they replied they'd only asked to place some little houses for refuge when floods threatened, not a total relocation; sees no problem with the water being a little farther. [cf. Doc 20, the underlying petition]

author: Alcalde Marcos Chaboya
summary: *Solicitud sobre traslación del pueblo* — the alcalde, for the *vecindario*, petitions to relocate the pueblo across the river to the higher *mesa*: the surrounding arroyos overflow to within ~a *vara* of the houses, isolating the pueblo, and the road to the Mission is undermined by *pozas* (waterholes). Borica's marginal reply (verbatim): *'Monterey Enero 10 de 1797 = Reservaré la determinación hasta que el ingeniero Alberto de Córdova regrese de San Diego, reconozca el terreno é informe lo que le parezca = Borica'*

author: Mariano Tapia (potter)
summary: *Inconvenientes para una fábrica de loza* — finished his pottery workshop (*obrador de alfarería*) at SF but can't produce fine glazed ware: the wind never stops, lacks very-fine white sand, and the *Reverendos Padres* haven't supplied all he needs; meanwhile makes ordinary ware and will train the apprentices as ordered. *(Artisan-instruction program in action — cf. the 1795 artisan accounts, Docs 4–6)*

author: Prudencio Ruiz de Eguino
summary: *Cuenta de gastos de Correos* — itemized account of the extraordinary couriers' pay: courier Juan José Espinosa carried *pliegos* from Lt.-Col. José Joaquín de Arrillaga (Comdt., Loreto presidio) Álamos→México→pueblo de Raum; leagues & hours computed; *Rebaja* (one-third deducted per the Renta); *Líquido* 56 p 7 r 2 g to the couriers; signed receipt

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre marcha de un alférez* — once Alf. R[amón] Carrillo finishes the transfer documents he'll move to that presidio with escort & mules

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre inutilidad de un soldado* — soldier José Vizcarra maimed in the left hand by a fall from a colt, left half-senseless and unfit to continue service

author: Argüello
summary: Of the presidio's *gente de mar* (sailors), only the cabin-boy Diego Flores wishes to stay on as a soldier

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre un proyecto de ataque* — the gentiles' project to attack Mission San José came to nothing (*salió nulo*), per the *diligencias* run by Sgt. [Pedro] Amador

author: Argüello
summary: *Huida de unos Cristianos* — P. Martín Landaeta reports three Christian Indians fled the mission back to their lands — one Saclan and two Cuchillón

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre cierta licencia* — the corporals of the Sta Clara & Sta Cruz escoltas request leave to come to the presidio on an errand

author: Argüello
summary: *Fuga de un Gentil* — on the night of the 25th a shackled gentile prisoner (condemned to 2 months' presidio labor) escaped to his land still wearing the grillete

author: Argüello
summary: *Enfermedad de dos Indios* — two gentile prisoners (2-month labor sentence) are gravely ill; asks what to do with them given their uselessness

author: Argüello
summary: *Envío de una Relación* — encloses a detailed muster *Relación* of the present individuals of this (SF) company

author: Pedro Amador (sergeant)
summary: Diary of the expedition to found Mission San José — see expanded entry below

author: Pedro Amador
summary: *Prevenciones al Cabo sobre los trabajos y el servicio* — leaves the corporal the stockade plaza marked out (70 varas long, 60 at the narrowest); troop to share the mission works equally; 3 of the Padres' rooms fenced & today began plastering, to be roofed so the Padres can live there; on timber-hauling & felling the cabo is instructed; soldiers never to go without their arms; pass arms-&-cartridge review every 8 days

author: Argüello
summary: *Envío de Documentos* — encloses the certification of when cabin-boy Miguel Salazar enlisted in the company, and the power he granted to be paid the *alcance* owed for his prior service in the San Blas Department

author: Argüello
summary: *Pedido de madera* — encloses three copies of the receipts his predecessor Raymundo Carrillo gave for effects landed by the ships for the *fáginas* & gear of the paquebot *San Carlos*; since they sit in deposit, requests some of the (scarce) timber for the presidio's benefit

author: Raymundo Carrillo (cert. Borica)
summary: *Recibo* — inventory of effects of the paquebot *San Carlos*, wrecked (*naufragados*) in the port of San Francisco, left in deposit, delivered by the ship's *contador* to Alf. de fragata Gonzalo López de Haro. Carrillo notes the heavy timber & plank lie outside, the iron-work (*pernería/clavazón*) is badly rusted by saltwater — *'no soy responsable de mermas'*; signed for the sick/absent contador José de Tobar

author: Raymundo Carrillo (cert. Borica)
summary: *Recibo* — Carrillo, SF habilitado, received from Esteban Martínez, contador of the fragata Concepción, eight *palanquines*, three *enceradores*, and a little light *caballería* for the *fáginas*

author: Raymundo Carrillo (cert. Borica)
summary: *Recibo* — received from the *San Carlos* contador B[ernardo] López de Haro 200 varas plank, a beam, 25 varas, a hawser, 30 cable-pieces & clavazón — spent on *fáginas*, the renovation of the San Joaquín *esplanada*, and the new battery built at Yerba Buena (2nd SF battery). *'Estos dos últimos recibos se duplican en las pp.65 y 66'*

author: Argüello
summary: *Envío de cierto documentos* — forwards the Mission San José minister's repeated complaints against Cabo Miranda, with Miranda's rebuttals & reply; says the friar's *genio* drives him to a continual *desazón* aimed only at upsetting tempers

author: Fr. Isidro Barcenilla
summary: *Rencilla con un Cabo* — the friar's account: tried to engage the cabo's son to mind the cattle for 4 pesos/month; son begged off (couldn't count), then friar asked him to make plows, refused; cabo angrily barred the friar — *and any Indian* — from his house; cabo gave only one soldier (not the three the friar wanted given gentile danger) to retrieve missing mules & cows; asks the cabo be replaced

author: Cabo Alejo Miranda
summary: *(reply to the friar)* — Miranda's side: the Padres are furious he can't do more mission labor given guard & home duties; recounts hunting lost cattle day after day, wearing out his horses; could spare only one escort soldier (others at Sta Clara / sick / on guard); the real cause is he wouldn't let his son mind the mission cattle (no horse lent) & couldn't make the plows; begs to be transferred and to send his family to the presidio

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre la misma rencilla* — Argüello mediates: regrets he can't remedy the friar's troubles, but the cabo is seen angry, scolding, *'echar pestes,'* shouting that it was the Indians who did all the mission's works; at the friar's request removed Higuera from the mission; now the cabo complains bitterly and only the Gov. can remove him; Alejo also asks relief — refer to the Chief

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre formación de un proceso* — received 83 Christians of all ages & sexes and 9 gentiles; separated the 9 gentiles and the Christians accused in the deaths of the Mission San Francisco Indians and the *atropellamiento* of Raymundo & his party, returned the rest to the friars; will form the criminal case once the two interpreters arrive

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre unos almacenes y cierto cargo* — will hand over the *Habilitación* once the case of the imprisoned Indians & Christian fugitives is done; the warehouses are fire-prone (*propensas á un incendio*) & need rebuilding; disputes charging only his company for the late Raymundo Carrillo's deficit (artillery & volunteer detachments also draw rations); the 1791 *descubierto* of Ramón Laso (now retired at 200 p/yr) should be reintegrated by Laso

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre gastos de un cierto pleito* — per Borica's 13 July order, the lawsuit costs that Manuel Cárcaba incurs (suing for the province's officers to recover sums advanced to the late Ayudante Inspector Nicolás Soler) are charged to the *Fondo de Gratificación* pending reintegration

author: Argüello
