summary: *Sobre cierta espedición* — forwards Sgt. Pedro Amador's *parte* and original diary of his [July 1797 punitive] expedition (against the fugitive Indians), and awaits orders on the *presa* (captives) he brought in

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre cierto relevo de servicio* — among the detachment's artillerymen, José Álvarez is fit to run the escuela de primeras letras (primary school); asks the Comandante de armas release him for it, with a 2–3 peso/month gratification

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre inutilización de unas escopetas* — some shotguns broke; took the spare ones from the mission escoltas, replacing them with this year's new muskets, until the armorer Rafael Arriola repairs them — *once he finishes boring the 24-pdr cannon*

author: Argüello
summary: *Consulta sobre cierta compra* — since missions are to be preferred for country-goods supplied to the *Habilitaciones*, asks whether to keep buying blankets from Mission San Francisco or request them from the presidio per the 13th's order

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre ciertas Cuentas* — Lt. José Pérez Fernández charged 267 p 1¾ g (twice drawn against this Habilitación for Monterey's); his account sent to Lt.-Col. J. J. de Arrillaga with a 223 p 7 r 6½ g debit to satisfy that presidio

author: Argüello
summary: *Salida de un soldado* — will discharge soldier Carlos Carrillo from the company on 1 Nov.; his *ajuste* & *filiación* go to Comandante Hermenegildo Sal

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre fuga de Indios* — 4 gentile Cuchillón Indians brought in 11 Christians of the Mission and reported the other fugitives scattered through the Islands and inland; two more Indians fled to the *otra banda* (East Bay) with two gentiles of their land

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre cierta licencia* — the Mission SF minister asks two Christian Indians be allowed to go by sea to the *otra banda* to seek their children, since by land lies *peligro de la Indiada* and a 100+ league detour; if refused they'd likely flee. Asks guidance for such cases

author: Fr. José Espí
summary: *Sobre cierta licencia á dos Neófitos* — two neophytes, natives of the *otra banda del Puerto* (East Bay), ask leave to bring back their children; await resolution (the other Christians held till evening)

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre cierta orden* — Gov.'s order: East-Bay Indians wishing to *pasear* may, with their ministers' leave, but by land; the two neophytes go by land, a soldier escorting them to San José, whence they continue without risk

author: Argüello
summary: *Disposiciones para evitar una epidemia* — received Viceroy Branciforte's measures to keep smallpox out of the Peninsula; will execute all, both inoculation and quarantine

author: Manuel Rodríguez
summary: *Agradecimiento* — thanks Borica for recommending him to the Viceroy for the post of alférez of the San Francisco presidio, whose *despacho* has issued

author: Argüello
summary: *Instancia / Licencia / Causa de disgustos* — the Mission San José Padres demand the troop keep doing all the mission's *faenas* though the principal works (per the *Instrucción*) are done; Fr. Barcenilla complains of soldier Salvador Higuera; the soldier-courier declared the Padres want the troop to work the same as the Indians, that he built doors & yokes unthanked & refused to make plows he didn't understand. Argüello's key remark: these friars 'acaban de llegar de su Colegio sin la menor experiencia ni conocimiento en el gobierno de Misiones' — hence continual friction with the escort, worse if forbidden to make the soldiers 'trabajar… como los Indios'

author: Fr. Barcenilla
summary: *Sobre una acusación* — none of the escort will lift a hand for mission tasks, even common ones 'the poorest Indian wouldn't refuse'; blames Salvador Higuera for turning them against helping the Padres; asks him removed at once; notes the cabo 'enteramente mudado,' now wanting pay for any task — attributes the change to Higuera (the cabo's *compadre*)

author: Cabo Alejo Miranda
summary: *Enojo de unos PP / Enfermedad de un soldado* — the Padres asked for soldiers to fetch cattle; when he said he couldn't decide, they got angry; what the Gov. ordered is all done, but the Padres want soldiers 'trabajen mas y como si fueran gananes' (farm-hands); Salvador Higuera asks relief from the horse-herd (hip ailment) and his discharge

author: Raymundo Carrillo
summary: *Estravío de un Diario* — hasn't sent the Mission Santa Cruz diary, mislaid it (*traspapelado*); will once he finds it; begs the lapse be excused

author: Argüello
summary: *Suspensión de marcha* — suspends the transfer of the soldier-armorer Rafael Arriola to Santa Bárbara until he recovers; SF needs more the artisan Faustino Arriola, destined to make bits & spurs at Santa Bárbara

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre una orden* — per the Contaduría mayor's order, will have the SF habilitado comply with the eight articles regulating the Real Hacienda accounts and the inventory of *Enseres*

author: Argüello
summary: *Nombramiento de Cabo* — reviewing the company's *renta*, found a corporal vacancy from the increase, and named soldier Pedro Regalado Peralta (a *lego*, but otherwise qualified) cabo

author: Raymundo Carrillo
summary: *Sobre unos víveres* — yesterday sent the mule-train to the pueblo [San José] for maize to ration the troop; for the presidio's *gente de mar* there's no lack of common flour — asked the Padres to mill all they can

author: Carrillo
summary: *Sobre abono de una libranza* — forwards an enclosed *libranza* for the gov.'s *V.B.* and payment to this Habilitación

author: Carrillo
summary: *Ramo de Cruzada / Cuentas de Bulas* — notes Viceroy Branciforte's resolution that the Monterey habilitado administer the *Ramo de Cruzada* throughout the Peninsula; pending *Bulas* accounts rendered to treasurer José M. Bucheli

author: Carrillo
summary: *Cédula de inválido* — received the invalid certificate (half-pay) for company member Miguel Pacheco

author: Carrillo
summary: *Nombramiento de vicario* — the Bishop of Sonora has named Fr. Presidente Fermín Francisco de Lasuén his vicario foráneo in Alta California

author: Carrillo
summary: *Real Orden* — all naval officers & dependents at or arriving in the ports must obey the Viceroy's orders, requesting through him what they deem fit for the service

author: Carrillo
summary: *Entrada y salida de Soldados* — enlists three sentenced men (Rafael Arriola, José Escamilla, José Franco) as company soldiers from 1 April per Lt.-Col. Pedro Alberni; discharges cabo Miguel Pacheco & imprisoned soldier Joaquín Mesa

author: Carrillo
summary: *Orden sobre buques* — acknowledges the order to give prompt notice of any ships sighted on the coast

author: Carrillo
summary: *Suministro de pertrechos* — to issue Lt.-Col. Alberni the fine powder, ball cartridges & flints his company needs

author: Carrillo
summary: *Recibo de Factura* — received the invoice of México & San Blas goods for the presidio carried by the fragata *Concepción*, entered this port yesterday the 13th

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre auxilios á un Ingeniero* — per the Viceroy's order, to give engineer Alberto de Córdoba all aid he requests to put the San Joaquín fort battery in a state of defense & offense AND to build another at Yerba Buena *(1797 origin of the 2nd SF battery — cf. Doc 38; antedates the C-A 54 1802 renovation reference)*

author: Argüello
summary: *Distribución de pertrechos* — to receive the muskets, holsters, cartridge-boxes & flints (carried by the *Concepción*) for the jurisdiction and distribute them among the invalids & *vecinos* who lack them; render account to the Monterey habilitado

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre cartuchos* — each company member to keep extra cartridge-packets in saddle-cushions beyond the cartridge-box; weekly review at presidio & escoltas

author: Argüello
summary: *Remisión de un cierto documento* — henceforth send the Government only the principal monthly Diario, omitting the duplicate

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre empleo de un artesano* — will employ José Faustino Arriola in his blacksmith trade as ordered; habilitado supplies him, charge passed annually to Monterey

author: Argüello
summary: *Reasunción de mando* — arrived at the presidio the 18th and resumed command of his company, with the later orders (Borica's) addressed to Raymundo Carrillo *(the interim habilitado; explains the Mar 31 letters are Carrillo's, Apr 19+ Argüello's)*

author: Argüello
summary: *Reprensión* — reprimanded the Santa Clara cabo for giving only one man to escort seven religious bound for Monterey, when he should have given two soldiers

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre reintegro de cierta cantidad* — Lt. de fragata Ramón Saavedra requested a *mesada* (1026 p total) for his crew, paid by the habilitado on receipt; needing cash for the *fáginas* works, asks the Monterey habilitado reintegrate it

author: Ramón Saavedra
summary: *Suministro de una mesada* — encloses the crew-list of the paquebot *San Carlos* for an advance month's pay to re-equip them, most having lost clothing, tobacco & soap in the wreck (*naufragio*) the ship suffered *(confirms Doc 36 — Saavedra commanded the wrecked San Carlos)*

author: Argüello
summary: *Entrega y envío de dinero* — Saavedra deposited 800 p of his own funds with the habilitado to be credited; only 226 p returned by the next courier

author: Carrillo
summary: *Envío de documentos* — forwards the sworn, documented account of the cost of the 9 houses & barracks built last year (1796) at the presidio for the Catalan Volunteer troops & families

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre un matrimonio* — soldier Isidro Flores wishes to marry María Dolores Mojica, daughter of José Vicente (arrived on the *Concepción*); the chaplain began the process but the ship's commander objects (no order to leave any of his people); asks the woman be disembarked so the marriage can proceed

author: Carrillo
summary: *Envío de documento* — encloses the inventory of ordnance/stores delivered by the *San Carlos* contador Gonzalo de Haro, destined for the Fuerte de San Joaquín of this port

author: Carrillo
summary: *Despacho de un Correo* — dispatches an extraordinary courier (the México/San Blas Memorias for next year should reach Borica, and the Comandante de Armas had service business with him)

author: José Roca (artillery sgt)
summary: *Envío de cierta Nota* — passed the habilitado the Nota of effects needed for his Corps' artillery individuals detached in the Peninsula, the *barco de las Memorias* touching here first, then forwarding the shares for Monterey & San Diego

author: Carrillo
summary: *Envío de Documentos* — remits the México & San Blas Memorias in triplicate, including what artillery Sgt. José Roca requests for his corps

author: Carrillo
summary: *Recibo de tabacos* — received from the *San Carlos* contador 16 cases of cigarros & 8 of puros (tobacco)

author: Carrillo
summary: *Envío de un Pliego* — forwards the *pliego* handed him by Ramón Saavedra, comandante of the paquebot *San Carlos*, which entered this port today *(dates the San Carlos's SF arrival; wreck came later — cf. Docs 36, 83)*

author: Argüello
summary: *Surtimiento de plomo* — the SF presidio's lead being consumed making 6,000 shotgun-cartridge balls in 1796, asks Monterey supply enough for that presidio's service needs

author: Carrillo
summary: *Retención de sueldo* — henceforth 100 pesos is the sum retained from this company's cabos & soldiers, continuing the 25/yr discount until complete

author: Carrillo
summary: *Castigo á un soldado* — soldier Joaquín Mesa put in a foot-shackle (no chain), to be discharged after 2 months' presidio labor

author: Carrillo
summary: *Distribución de Avíos* — has issued the *avíos* to the presidio's & Mission SF escolta's troop & families; Sta Clara, Sta Cruz & attached Invalids pending this week/next

author: Carrillo
summary: *Huida de un soldado* — arrested Joaquín Mesa for a lie proven false, sent him to repair the corral, where he insolented the sergeant (who beat him with sticks); Mesa took a horse and fled, still missing

author: Carrillo
summary: *Envío de libranzas* — encloses the Sta Clara & Sta Cruz mission *libranzas* for the gov.'s *V.B.*; advanced Argüello that of Mission San Francisco

author: Carrillo
summary: *Castigo á dos soldados* — soldiers Juan J[osé] Higuera & Isidro Flores foot-shackled; to be freed after their 2-month sentence

author: Carrillo
summary: *Sobre libertad á un Indio* — freed the Indian Remigio of Mission San Francisco, his prison time served

author: Carrillo
summary: *Recibo de libranzas* — received the three *libranzas* (visaed) for Missions San Francisco, Sta Clara & Sta Cruz, forwarded to destination

author: Carrillo
summary: *Reconocimiento de dinero* — per Branciforte's reserved resolution, inspected the coin held for this Habilitación and found no peso fuerte of the nature indicated in that *oficio*; will watch for it henceforth *(a reserved viceregal order on suspect/contraband coin)*

author: Carrillo
summary: *Recibo de documentos* — received all documents through end-Dec. per the enclosed Index; the 72 p 4 r of the 5th *partida* properly charged to the fund

author: Carrillo
summary: *Concesión de ciertas gracias* — notes the Viceroy's *oficio* on the graces S.E. grants those who contributed to the *donativo* for the last War's expenses

author: Carrillo
summary: *Suministro á una tropa* — will issue mid-year only the troop's essentials (so it stays equipped & mounted for useful service), reserving the *avíos* until Jan/Feb after the prior year's *ajustes*

author: Carrillo
summary: *Entrega de un documento* — handed the habilitado the company's *Ajustamiento* for 1795, formalized by the México Tesorería de Ejército y Real Hacienda

author: Carrillo
summary: *Recibo / Solicitud de un reintegro* — decrees charging the *Fondo de Gratificación* with the costs of re-roofing the church, barracks, troop houses & company commander's house; notes the gov. seeks reintegration of 468 p 3 r 4 g spent on the Casa Mata, Baluarte (bastion), Garitas & artillery transport

author: Carrillo
summary: *Real Orden* — acknowledges the R.O. of 24 Feb 1777 on the entry of ships (re-circulated)

author: Argüello
summary: *Envío de documentos* — forwards two detailed *Relaciones* taken against the imprisoned Indians Sgt. Pedro Amador brought in — the names & declarations of each, plus the motives the Christian fugitives gave for abandoning their Mission (see Doc 112 + expanded entry)

author: Pedro Amador
summary: *Indagación* — arrived the 7th to investigate a reported gentile attack: the Sacalan rancherías who killed some SF Christians threatened a local *capitanejo* that if he went to work at the Mission and his ranchería was baptized they'd come kill them; many Indians gathering & making quantities of arrows

author: Borica
summary: *Ataque á una Ranchería* — orders Amador with 20 invalids/vecinos + 2 soldiers to dawn-raid (*albazo*) the Sacalan ranchería, seize the ringleaders & those who took part in the killing of the seven Christians of Mission SF in 1795, take them to San José with any fugitive Christians among them — 'se ha de evitar toda efusión de sangre,' firearms/lance only if attacked and only as much as needed to contain them

author: José Argüello (4 witnesses)
summary: Relación of the depositions — the verbatim testimonies of the Sacalan gentiles who took part in the 1795 killing of seven SF Christians and the Cuchillón Indians who attacked Raymundo's party; named declarants give their own accounts & reasons for flight. See expanded entry

author: José Argüello + Joaquín Tico[?], Claudio Galindo, José Miranda, José González
themes: sf; indigenous; mission-flight; mission-conditions; danti; bay-area-borderlands
summary: Relación of the fugitive Christians' reasons for fleeing Mission San Francisco — ~30+ named *huidos* each state why; the litany is hunger, family dead of hunger, and floggings by P. Antonio Danti for seeking food (hunting/fishing/clams/grass). See expanded entry

author: Borica (sentencing)
summary: *Castigos de criminales indios* — Borica's sentencing decree apportioning punishment (azotes + months of grillete at ration, no pay, then returned to the friars) to the Sacalan/Cuchillón participants per their Aug-9 declarations; the 60 neophytes sent to the *otra banda* to recover fugitives were the party attacked; closes ordering work to begin 1 Sept, rations so they 'no padezcan hambre,' King's-works only the daylight hours health permits, 'tratándolos con mucha humanidad.' See expanded entry

author: Pedro Peralta
summary: *Estravío de unos caballos* — two soldiers pass to recover six horses strayed from this escolta (2 with the company brand, 2 with the King's brand + *venta*, 2 with the King's brand)

author: Juan de Dios Ballesteros
summary: *Apropiación de un Parage* — remits the certification of having taken possession of the site named San Juan Bautista *(the founding of Mission San Juan Bautista, 24 June 1797 — third 1797 founding in this volume, with San José & the SJ pueblo relocation)*

author: Ignacio Olivera[?]
summary: *Salida de un artesano* — artisan Antonio Enríquez leaves for Monterey, accompanied by soldier Manuel Cota

author: Marcos Briones
summary: *Pedimento de una licencia* — asks leave to seek medical treatment *'por vida del niño y Sr. D. Cosmito.'*

author: Viceroy Iturrigaray
summary: *Sobre un nombramiento* — notifies that Felipe de Goycoechea, governor-elect of Antigua (Baja) California, is to be sworn & given possession in the usual form, his salary paid, on condition he posts bond to present his *título de Gobernador Político* within the ordinary overseas term, else lose the salary & repay it *(anomalous 1805 insertion — Goycoechea's Baja governorship)*

author: Argüello
summary: *Cargo á una Compañía* — encloses his company's *Lista de cargo*; the company carries a complete debit of 1,425 p 1 r 7⅜ g; to cover it he must withhold all supply, *'con sumo dolor suyo,'* even from the previous men's families, issuing only service essentials; knows the charge is interim pending retentions on his predecessor

author: Argüello
summary: *Envío de una Relación* — encloses (quadruplicate) the *Relación* of soldier Salvador Higuera for his Invalid-status (*gracia de Inválido*) application

author: Argüello
summary: *Enfermedad de un soldado* — soldier Joaquín Bernal fractured a leg, under treatment

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre cierta solicitud de maíz* — Mission Santa Clara supplied 50 fanegas of maize (4 carts) to the Habilitación; San José vecinos bringing seeds to be paid freight; couldn't haul the maize himself (company too small); asks Borica tell the San José Comisionado that vecinos owed in Monterey will be paid by Hermenegildo Sal

author: Argüello
summary: *Sobre formación de unos Padrones* — commissions Cabo Luis Peralta to form the censuses (*Padrones*) of Santa Clara, Santa Cruz & San José per the enclosed forms, and formalize the two escolta-cabos' accounts; invalid Gabriel Peralta to cover Santa Clara meanwhile

author: Argüello
summary: *Envío de documentos* — encloses the Noticia of the Correos & Tobacco revenues 1792–1796 and the per-biennium yield of the *Bulas* distribution

author: Argüello
summary: *Envío de un documento* — encloses the original *Relación* (cited as doc n°9) accompanying the end-June-1794 inventory by which Lt. Hermenegildo Sal handed over to José Pérez Fernández

author: Argüello
summary: *No recibo de armamento* — disputes a 242 p charge by habilitado-gen. Manuel Cárcaba for 20 carbines & 15 pairs of pistols (sent 1791 by the substitute Factor) his company never received; asks the Superioridad relieve the company of the debt

author: Argüello
summary: *No remisión de documentos* — won't send the annual documents, having fallen ill; will once he recovers

author: Argüello
summary: *Real Decreto* — acknowledges the R.D. of 27 Oct (prior yr) granting half-signature to Juan Manuel Álvarez on papers not bearing the Sovereign's signature

author: Argüello
summary: *Real Orden* — acknowledges the R.O. of 8 Dec (prior yr) that no lawsuits be formed over pecuniary interests under 500 r in Spain / 100 p in the Indies (unless perpetual rights)

author: Argüello
summary: *Recibo de R.O.* — acknowledges the S.M. resolution of 12 Jan on the method by which military men should make their *instancias*

author: Argüello
summary: *Orden sobre Inválidos* — acknowledges the order to settle Invalids (present & future) in the pueblos of San José, Los Ángeles & villa de Branciforte so their children inherit lands; those refusing & staying at the presidios rotate with the company in the *Guardias de Prevención*

author: Argüello
summary: *Entrega de ciertos documentos* — on receiving the vouchers for the 50 p spent conveying the Marinería, gave them to Alf. Raymundo Carrillo (transferring to Monterey today) to add to the San Blas accounts

author: Argüello
summary: *Suspensión / salida de soldados* — did not admit cabin-boy Diego Flores (chest-ill & bad conduct); enlisted Miguel Salazar to fill the vacancy left by José Vizcarra's discharge

author: Argüello
summary: *Estravío de unas reses* — 12 cattle strayed, likely returned to their *querencia* (the presidio's rancho); since penning made the cattle deteriorate, decided to let them out at night with precautions

author: Fr. José Espí & Fr. Martín Landaeta
summary: *Llegada de Cristianos* — the friars' defense: today received the 79 returned Christian fugitives; took offense at Argüello's *oficio*, insisting that over the past two years 'á cuantos han vuelto, lejos de castigarlos, han sido muy bien recibidos y vestidos' — yet this didn't suffice to hold them; any fitting punishment would be Argüello's to apply *(direct counter-claim to the fugitives' flogging testimonies — Docs 112–114; the cruelty question is contested within the dossier)*

author: Argüello
summary: *Rumores de Indios* — encloses the San José escolta-cabo's letter; orders Sgt. Amador to verify the Indian rumors with certainty

author: Cabo Alejo Miranda
summary: *Amenazas de Indios* — on the 26th a captain of the San José valley wishing to be baptized told him the gentiles threatened to kill them (and the soldiers) if they became Christians

author: Argüello
summary: *Precauciones contra un ataque* — on the gentile rumors of attacking San José, sent Sgt. Amador + 3 soldiers; if a sortie were needed to break up the gentile *sediciones*, to ask cabo Vallejo for 6 of the best-armed men and mount the sortie with 10 men, using his experience's precautions

author: Cabo Alejo Miranda
summary: *Informe de un Gentil* — the Padres wanted to dismantle the houses & guard (he refused); Sunday night a gentile reported the San José-valley gentiles preparing to attack the gentiles working at the mission; keeps a mounted, fully-armed sentinel circling the mission

author: Cabo Alejo Miranda
summary: *Ataque meditado / Conclusión de casas* — Indians say the gentiles from where Raymundo had gone want to attack them; the Padres arrived the 28th; on possession-day a pound of powder was spent (the founding salute); hopes to finish the houses & guard this month *(more Mission San José founding detail — cf. Doc 32)*

author: Argüello
summary: *Alta y baja de oficiales* — discharged (31 Aug) José Pérez Fernández, promoted Teniente of Loreto, and enlisted same day Manuel Rodríguez, raised to Alférez of this company *(cf. Doc 57)*

author: Argüello
summary: *Fundación de un Rancho* — on the Real Hacienda herd arriving, built a corral & *toril* at Buriburi (Buri Buri), between San Mateo & San Bruno — good pasture & water — to establish the [SF presidial] rancho; asks 200 more *hembrío* to make a sufficient breeding base for the presidio & arriving ships; will keep exact accounts of increase, profits & losses

author: soldier José M. Arce
summary: *Pedimento de baja* — having completed his 10-year enlistment, asks free retirement; Arrillaga's marginal order (17 Jul, Loreto): the request is just & his separation fitting *(section break → Baja/Loreto Arrillaga correspondence begins)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre una Nueva Fundación* — on the new founding of Mission Santa Catalina Mártir (Dominican Baja frontier): comisionado José Loriente, Sgt. José Manuel Ruiz; founding stalled by inability to convey seeds for the people to be moved (scarce pasture); per S.E., sacred ornaments to be drawn from those given Mulegé at the extinction of Mission Guadalupe

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Remisión de documentos* — remits his company's & marine crew's Aug–Sept documents

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Lista* — a Nota of the sacred vessels, ornaments & jewels of the extinct Mission Guadalupe, drawn for the new Santa Catalina Mártir founding by Viceroy Branciforte's order from the missions of La Purísima de Cadegomó & Sta Rosalía de Mulegé

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Envío de ornamentos de iglesia* — remits by the goleta *Saturnina* to the port of San Luis (for Santa Catalina) the ornaments & sacred vessels drawn from La Purísima & Mulegé *(the *Saturnina* — the Bodega/Vancouver-era vessel — still in Baja service 1797; cf. Doc 7)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *(brief)* — remits a *Nota* mistakenly addressed to him

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Permisión de embarque* — will permit (as ordered) the embarkation of the missionaries Fr. Ricardo Tejeiro[?] & Fr. Juan Salgado

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Recibo de una Cotejación* — received the Viceroy's *cotejación* of his report on his operations on the banks of the Colorado River; will reply next courier *(Arrillaga's 1796 Colorado frontier reconnaissance)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Orden sobre las viruelas* — received the Viceroy's order on measures to prevent smallpox contagion

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Recibo de una licencia* — received soldier José M. Arce's absolute discharge, to deliver once there's a replacement *(cf. Doc 144)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Alta y baja de oficiales* — received the interim *despacho* of Teniente for José Pérez; from 1 Sept the propietario Francisco Roa retired, Pérez filling the vacancy that day

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Orden de aumento á una Compañía* — forwards a copy of the Viceroy's order to add five men & a cabo to his company

author: Viceroy Branciforte
summary: *Aumento de una Escolta* — per his request, the R.Hac. fiscal's *dictamen* & the Junta Superior, resolves the augmentation of 5 soldiers + 1 cabo for the new Mission Santa Catalina Mártir

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Parecer sobre cierta deuda* — by the enclosed copy the 2,000 p the late Ayudante Inspector Nicolás Soler left for this presidio appear not to have entered the *concurso*; asks orders on what to collect

author: Manuel Cárcaba
summary: *Pérdida de un Pleito* — lost the appeal on reimbursing the presidial Habilitaciones for what they advanced the late Insp. Nicolás Soler; the four newer presidios' condemnation omitted 2,000 p held by the late Factor José Arvide for the Loreto supplements; pursuing collection (1,600 p) with the Arizpe Pagador, leaving 400 p toward this aged suit's costs

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre un proceso por asesinato* — on 10 Aug learned the Indian Blas Calderón of Mission Comondú murdered an India; *diligencias* prove him a confessed *reo alevoso*; secured; case sent for sentence to the Audiencia de Guadalajara

author: Ildefonso Bernal (receipt)
summary: *Recibo* — received from Gerónimo Amador, condestable of the goleta *Valdez*, an artillery inventory: 2 pedreros (3-cal.), 4 chambers, 2 *bozas*, iron wedges, 50 balls, 25 grapeshot bags, etc.; note: 10 balls + 35 grapeshot bags added from the presidio's reserve (signed Arrillaga)

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre composición de una Capilla* — for repairing the Santa Ana partido chapel the vecinos' offerings will barely suffice, counting on the cattle of the rancho Las Calabazas (belonging to Señora Santa Ana) to feed the *peones*

author: Arrillaga (copy)
summary: *Lista de individuos* — list of 30 vecinos volunteering for the Santa Ana chapel repair

author: Francisco Roa
summary: *Oficio sobre moneda falsa* — received Branciforte's *oficio* on counterfeit coin; will spare no diligence to detect any introduced here *(the Baja counterpart of the SF Doc 102)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre rendición de unas Cuentas* — per the Viceroy & the Tribunal de Cuentas dean, on 25 Apr remitted the 1793 & 1794 Loreto accounts; the 1795–96 (handled by the ex-habilitado Francisco Roa) still pending

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Envío de un documento* — encloses a copy of his 6 Feb *oficio* to the Viceroy

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Solicitud para aumentar una Escolta* — long memo on the troop shortage for Santa Catalina & the Baja frontier: sgt + cabo + 12 rarely subsists (caballada needs 4 men day/night; bastimentos hauled 30–50 leagues from Sto Domingo/El Rosario); Mission Santo Tomás (1791) got no augmentation; asks +1 cabo & 5 soldiers; needs men for sorties after *Cimarrones* and to repel the Colorado-river Indians if they attack the founding — 55 plazas cover 400 leagues from southern Baja to San Miguel, and the Santa Catalina rancherías have intimate communication with the numerous Colorado ones

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Recibo de un bando* — received the *bando* that military men are indistinctly included in the Royal Pragmatic of 23 Mar 1776 (on marriages)

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Congratulación* — received the Viceroy's *oficio* congratulating the jurisdiction's individuals for contributing to the donativo gracioso *(cf. Doc 104)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Entrega de Ajustamiento* — received his company's & crew's *Ajustamiento* (Tesorería de Ejército) and gave it to the habilitado

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Orden sobre sueldos* — holds the R.O. on the future pay of sergeants, cabos, soldiers & retirees

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Ajuste de un arriero* — encloses the account of the fugitive muleteer José Ampárano (owes 62 p 3 r 9 g, chiefly for *avíos* when sent to the Frontier, served little)

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Recibo de una R.O.* — to observe the customary questions & answers when any ship enters the Peninsula's ports

author: Arrillaga
summary: *(brief)* — remits the monthly documents for Jan & Feb

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Envío de un Padron* — remits the Estado of inhabitants & castes of the jurisdiction at end-1796 (Baja census)

author: Viceroy Branciforte
summary: *Cambio de cierta administración* — per the 18 Aug Junta Superior, from 1 Jan 1796 the administration of the *Diezmos* & the mercury (*Azogues*) provision of the Californias passes from the Rosario treasury to Guadalajara

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Remisión de una libranza* — remits to the Guadalajara treasury the *libranza* for the 1795 tithe-product of the jurisdiction

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Recibo / Entrega de un dinero* — received the obligation given by the retired Lt. José Francisco Ortega & his three sons (added to the *descubierto* expediente); one son paid 60 p to the Santa Bárbara habilitado

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Aprehensión de unos Indios* — the San Borja-detached soldier had five Indians imprisoned (named, with 'crimes': Ignacio — sent to catch *Cimarrones*, instead wandered 7 months bringing none; Benito — lifelong runaway 'but never did harm'; Cristóbal, Vicente, Alonso — cattle-destroyers / *Cimarrón*); Arrillaga sent them to the frontier as more useful for the new [Santa Catalina] founding *(coerced-labor transfer)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre cierta Causa criminal* — the Audiencia de Guadalajara returned the criminal case against the Indian Solís (for killing another Indian, both of Mission San José del Cabo) to fix defects; lacking time refers it to Borica; notes a Royal Indulto covers the crime, urging relief for the *infeliz* imprisoned 2+ years

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre una Propuesta* — forwards Diego Pérez's retirement *instancia*; proposes keeping him (at his current pay) under the habilitado to run the Loreto arsenal/shipyard (collecting ship's gear after voyages, overseeing convict labor there) since no officer knows the daily ship mechanics

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Remisión de un documento* — encloses (quadruplicate) the *instancia* of the presidio's launch-patron Diego Pérez for the gov.'s mediation with the Viceroy toward an advantageous retirement

author: Diego Pérez
summary: *Solicitud de retiro* — ~29 years' service as caulker, carpenter & patron of the Loreto department's ships; broken health & failing sight; asks retirement. Marginal recommendations by Arrillaga & Borica (a first-Guardián's retirement / one better than his post)

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre creación de una Escolta* — establishes an escolta at Mission San José del Cabo (4 men + cabo + 2 vecinos) for night-and-day coastal watch against enemies (the cape near San Lucas, 30 leagues from the Sgt. at Real de Santa Ana): to drive off livestock from raiders & keep anyone from approaching the coast to be seized; only 9 shotguns for 120+ able men in the South, so sent 40 shotguns + 2,000 cartridges; vecinos rotate guard every 15 days, fed cimarrón-cattle meat

author: Francisco Santos Quevedo
summary: *Recibo de documentos* — received the 25 Apr *oficio* & three *pliegos*, to forward at the Chihuahua courier's departure

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Remisión de correspondencia* — per the 2 Apr order, sent the correspondence to Real de Álamos; the postmaster's enclosure shows it will go on at the Chihuahua courier's departure

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre embarque de dos Religiosos* — on the Viceroy's 17 Feb order: Fr. Andrés Caballero has a habitual illness yet keeps advancing his mission (Naturales' instruction & temporal goods), and his brother Fr. Rafael Caballero (delicate, lively temper) is occasionally ailing; both have worked as hard as any; leaves their embarkation to the gov.

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre cambio de Habilitado* — Alf. José Pérez Fernández will leave (19 Apr) for the Loreto Habilitación (company lacks subalterns), though still mustering in his own San Francisco company; asks when to discharge Teniente Francisco Roa *(cf. Docs 142, 154)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Reglamento — administración de aguardiente* — holds the R.O. of 20 Dec lifting the prohibition on cane brandy (*aguardiente de caña*) + its administration *Reglamento*; thinks the 2–3 southern Baja cane-growers (and one wanting a cane-mill/still) fall outside it, Baja being 'realmente un país libre' (no *alcabala*, exempt for 10 years)

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre cartuchos* — his troop keeps a flap-covered *vaqueta* packet of 16–18 cartridges beyond the full cartridge-box; will make the ordered *cojinillos*, or rather a second packet (same purpose, cartridges less degraded) *(Baja counterpart of SF Doc 77)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Remisión de un documento* — encloses the Relación of the jurisdiction's state for the first six months of 1797

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre envío de Cuentas* — will send the 1795 & 1796 Real Hacienda accounts (nearly finished)

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Participación de una orden* — told Alf. Ildefonso Bernal to route his transfer request to the Provincias Internas through the Comandante General

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Publicación de un Bando* — will publish the *bando* noting the Viceroy's word that cane brandy made before the lifting of its ban is still being sold *(cf. Doc 188)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre cargo de cierta deuda* — muleteer José Ampárano's debt charged to the *Fondo de Gratificación*, to be recovered if he ever appears *(cf. Doc 171)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Pase de un soldado* — once Teniente Felipe Goycoechea confirms enlisting soldier Salvador Caravantes in his company, will discharge him here

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre defensa de Costas y auxilio á dos fragatas* — notes the Viceroy's order that the frigates *Concepción* & *Princesa* remain in those seas to defend the coasts; to supply them, as occasion offers, from the Baja repuestos, missions & pueblos

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Modo de remisión* — will henceforth send the principal monthly diario

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Prevenciones con motivo de la Guerra* — acknowledges the charge given on S.M.'s declaration of war against the English: already ordered the Southern Sgt. full vigilance, sent 500 ball cartridges to arm the able vecinos, had a list made of able-bodied men & those with serviceable arms (unserviceable ones repaired at once), with further precautions

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre que se disponga de un pedrero* — per the Viceroy, to take the small cannon (*pedrero*) at Mission San Miguel plus 7 long muskets there and send them to the new [Santa Catalina] founding

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Remisión de documentos* — remits his service records (quadruplicate); henceforth octuplicate every two years

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre carencia de Situados* — they may lack the *situados* next year (ships may fail at San Blas); will economize whatever is in the Almacenes & arrives this year

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Declaración de Guerra* — published the *bando* in which the Sovereign declares war on the British Nation; has taken all fitting providences and will execute what's ordered as far as in his power

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre medios de defensa* — acknowledges the defense means for the jurisdiction (per Borica's 27 Mar oficio): munitions provision, the manner of relaying warnings, and the retreat of cattle & seeds *(the scorched-earth/withdraw-from-coast element)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Decreto de libertad* — received, with the freedom-decree, the *diligencias* against the neophyte Marcos Solís *(the San José del Cabo homicide accused, freed under the Indulto — cf. Doc 179)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *(three oficios)* — (a) if the four surviving San Borja Indians (one died) show signs of fleeing to the *Gentilidad*, will send them to San Diego; (b) will observe the orders on prompt coastal warnings; (c) will publish the *bando* of S.M.'s General Indulto on the peace concluded with France [Basel 1795]

author: Arrillaga
summary: *(three oficios)* — (a) all royal servants incl. naval officers arriving at NE ports must obey the Viceroy; (b) received a certified copy of Teniente José Argüello's report on the terrain he reconnoitered on the banks of the Colorado & Gila and the gentiles inhabiting them; (c) the pearls (*perla*) in the warehouse + last year's collection weren't sent to San Blas (the ship's commander left)

author: Arrillaga
summary: *(five oficios)* — (a) soldier Ignacio M. Frasviña's absolute discharge (on replacement); (b) the mail was at sea for San Antonio de la Huerta within 24 h; (c) received the Sargento appointment for cabo José Joaquín Amador; (d) ⭐ the R.O. to detain by reprisal all English ships in our ports, later adjusted to the formal war declaration; (e) will list the attached Invalids' pay in the muster extracts

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Envío de documentos* — sends the monthly documents for Mar, Apr & this May

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Remisión de un Diario* — returned the 18th, his reconnaissance operations concluded; encloses a copy of the Diario, kept in a folder titled 'Sobre Aventuras del Camino para Sonora y Nuevo México' *(Arrillaga's 1796 overland Sonora-route / Colorado reconnaissance)*

author: Ignacio M. Frasviña
summary: *Solicitud de retiro* — asks retirement, enlistment served; Arrillaga's margin: just; entered service 16 Nov 1786

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre unos Escolantes* — encloses a list of the current Loreto schoolchildren (*Escolantes*): 2 in *primeras letras*, 8 spelling, 6 *decorando*, 1 writing (writing sample remitted)

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Envío de una Nota* — pearl-diving (*buceo de perla*) report, last season: 4 canoes (Mission San José del Cabo 1 oz 6; Mulegé 0 oz 10; José Nicolás Mesa, Río Yaqui 2 oz 2; Antonio García Herreros, Presidio Buenavista, Sonora 2 oz 11; total 6 oz 13 adarmes), ordinary pearl, no special grain (signed Francisco Roa)

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre el Jabeque-correo* — the xebec (*jabeque*) for the overseas mail is unfit to sail; the 'Guairo' substitutes; can't repair the xebec for lack of timber (*vigas/tosas*)

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Remisión de un nombramiento* — remits a sergeant's appointment for approval; the +1 sgt, 1 cabo & 8 soldiers begins the day it's effected

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Envío de un documento* — remits the Relación of the jurisdiction's state in 1796

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Conocimiento de ciertas causas* — the Viceroy declared that jurisdiction over the cases of gentile & neophyte Indians of the Peninsula belongs to the Capitanía General *(bears on the Solís/Calderón cases)*

author: Francisco Roa
summary: *Sobre demora de documentos* — ill ~8 months; hasn't touched the Habilitación documents; will rush them once Arrillaga arrives

author: Roa
summary: *Recibo de dos cédulas* — received the Invalid certificates of the late Juan Miguel Camacho & Juan María Miranda, former soldiers of this company

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Envío de documentos* — encloses the Relación & *libramiento* of the rest of the Peninsula's individuals' contributions to the last War's expenses (copies in the 1796 *Donativos* folder)

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Envío de una certificación* — encloses for authorization the certification of Teniente Roa's honorable conduct

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Avistamiento de 16 Buques* — the express by *cordillera* with news of 16 ships sighted off the coast of Mission San Miguel reached him 11 PM on the 2nd; by 2 AM he'd dictated providences to the Southern Sgt.; couldn't relay the original *cordillera* up the opposite coast (the 'Guairo' stove-in, the 'Loretana' kept out of port by NW winds), so sped it to Real de los Álamos to dispatch an extraordinary to S.E. via Rosario & Guadalajara for advance notice along the NE coast

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Remisión de cierta perla* — the *armadores* paid the royal Quinto on this year's diving; with it sent the Viceroy all pearl collected since 1788, net 3 lb 9 oz 5 adarmes

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Avistamiento de un buque* — the Southern Sgt. reports the Mission San José cabo sighted a large ship on 12 Oct standing in for land on a south wind, then veering SE out of sight

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre auxilio á ciertos buques* — warned the Southern Sgt. that the Manila Galleon (*Nao de Manila*), convoyed by a war frigate, may make landfall there; to supply it as he can

author: Arrillaga
summary: *(brief)* — sends the monthly documents for Oct, Nov & this Dec

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Avistamiento de embarcaciones* — the Southern Sgt. relays the Mission San José cabo's report of two vessels sighted 16 Nov ~10 AM, standing prow toward San Blas

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre Indios homicidas* — ordered the Frontier Sgt. to send homicidal/troublesome Indians to Loreto; one dispatched, but escaped

author: Andrés Mendívil
summary: *Saldo de Cuentas* — received the 1795 & 1796 post-office accounts & a 277 p libranza from habilitado-gen. Cárcaba; accounts settled but for 4 r in the 1795 one; henceforth send triplicate

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre Cuentas de estafeta* — encloses the copy showing Correos Administrator Mendívil satisfied with the 1795–96 post-office accounts

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre aumento á una Compañía* — on the 1st began the +1 cabo & 5 soldiers granted his company

author: Arrillaga
themes: baja; frontier; colorado-river; yuma-revolt; sonora-road; presidio-strategy
summary: *Sobre comunicación con la Sonora* — major strategic memo (answering the R.Hac. fiscal's 1 May request) on opening free overland communication with Sonora across the Colorado: the numerous Colorado-river gentiles begin ~18–20 leagues from Santa Catalina; recalls 'el sacrificio que se verificó en 81' (the 1781 Yuma Revolt); proposes two options — (1) revive the Santa Olalla presidio at the Colorado crossing (planned 1778–83, ~100 men split among Sonóitac, San Felipe, the presidio) or (2) found a new presidio near the river mouth on the Antigua-California side, 20–25 leagues from Santa Catalina (preferred); closes urging officers experienced in handling Indians

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre municiones* — encloses the Nota of pedreros & munitions sent to the Frontier Comandante to place at Mission San Pedro Mártir

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Envío de un documento* — encloses the Nómina of the Dominican missionaries of Antigua California (the roster itself = Doc 234)

author: Arrillaga
summary: Nómina of the Dominican missionaries of Antigua California (by mission, late 1797): Loreto — Pres. Fr. Cayetano Pallás, Fr. Plácido Sanz; San José del Cabo — Pablo Zárate, Eudaldo Surroca; Todos Santos — Francisco Hontiyuelo, Mariano Fernández; San Javier — Gerónimo Soldevilla, Tomás Marín; Comondú — Jorge Coello, Antonio Sánchez; Mulegé — Miguel Gallego; San Ignacio — Domingo Timor; Sta Gertrudis — José Espín; San Borja — Melchor Tonz[?], Antonio Lázaro; San Fernando — Rafael Arviña, José Caulas; Smo Rosario — Vicente Belda; San Pedro Mártir — Mariano Apolinario, Juan Rivas; Sto Domingo — Miguel Abad, Jaime Codina; San Vicente — Ramón López, Sigismundo Fonteuverta; Sto Tomás — José Loriente, Miguel López; San Miguel — Mariano Yoldi, Raimundo Escala; Sta Catalina Mártir — Tomás Valdellou

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre correspondencia* — had the correspondence at Buenavista collected (none left in that *estafeta*); guides now routed via San Antonio & Álamos

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Envío de un oficio* — encloses the *oficio* from the Real de los Álamos postmaster

author: Prudencio Ruiz de Eguino
summary: *Despacho de pliegos* — received the 3 pliegos & *cordillera* letter the 15th at 11 AM, handed to the subdelegado who continued the *cordillera* to Rosario; dispatched by express the 3 pliegos for the Viceroy, the Nueva Galicia Comandante Gen. & the San Blas naval Comandante *(the relay of the 16-ship war alarm — cf. Doc 221)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Envío de correspondencia* — sent the correspondence to San Antonio; encloses that administrator's letter

author: Francisco Santos Quevedo
summary: *Sobre correspondencia* — mail-routing details (Savage's margin: *'detalles sin importancia'*); the gov.'s correspondence to be routed via Chihuahua / Álamos / Tesopaco / Buenavista

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre remisión de ciertos Indios* — the goleta having gone up to San Luis, ordered the Sgt. to send the homicidal/harmful Frontier Indians on it *(cf. Docs 178, 227)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *(brief)* — remits his company's Estado per the model sent 23 Sep

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Envío de pertrechos, ornamentos y puertas* — on the 3rd the goleta *Saturnina* sailed for San Luis with clothing + war-supplies for the new Santa Catalina founding: a box of powder, small cannon, 60 balls, 60 grapeshot bags, silver sacred vessels & 6 ornaments from Cadegomó & Mulegé, doors for San Vicente & the founding

author: Arrillaga
summary: *(three oficios)* — (a) the merchant *balandra* 'La Merced' sailed 4 Aug for the Río Yaqui; the free-commerce goleta 'Nuestra Señora del Carmen' anchored here the 2nd from San Blas, left for the Yaqui the 5th; (b) ⭐ S.M. relieved Branciforte of the viceroyalty, naming in his place Miguel José de Azanza (Secretary of State & of the War Despacho); (c) the habilitado-gen.'s costs in the Soler suit charged to the *Fondo de Gratificación*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre Indios homicidas* — had ordered the Frontier Sgt. to send him the gentiles imprisoned for killing Christian Indians, but Borica's 10 Aug order was to seize all accomplices & send them to San Diego; unaware of that, he'd urged Loreto instead (if they fled from San Diego they'd reach their nearby rancherías & do more harm)

author: Arrillaga
summary: *(brief)* — remits the monthly documents for June & July

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Recibo de cierta copia* — holds the copy beginning *'A consecuencia de una instancia que ha hecho Josefa Matamoros, mujer de Domingo Camuñez'*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre correspondencia* — will certify the state of any *pliegos* arriving worn/torn; Borica's correspondence travels double-wrapped (cotense + the general bundle) from San Diego up, never opened, other mail kept separate to the Frontier for the soldier-courier to deliver

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Llegada de Religiosos* — names the 8 missionaries arrived on the goleta *Valdez*: Jaime Codina, Antonio Lázaro, Juan Rivas, Raimundo Escala, Sigismundo Fonteuverta, José Caulas, Eudaldo Surroca & Plácido Sanz *(cf. roster Doc 234)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre solicitud de armamento* — will arm the southern vecindario while requesting from S.E. the cut-down muskets + cartridge-boxes, holsters, powder & lead; if it comes, to be treated as a R.Hac. reserve

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Entrega de una Instancia / Envío de libranza* — Alf. José Pérez arrived; gave him Hermenegildo Sal's *instancia*; remits the libranza for the charges against him

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre correspondencia* — for the war's duration keep a ship ready for the monthly mail (a trusted man carrying it to San Antonio, costs from the Gratificación fund); couriers slow lately for the bad road & scarce pasture north of San Ignacio

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre reemplazo de Misioneros* — received & returns the San Pedro Mártir friars Fr. Antonio & Fr. Rafael Caballero's request to retire for ill-health; awaits the Fr.-President (expected hourly) to process their replacement & departure *(cf. Doc 186)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Sobre formación de un Documento* — will form the annual *Estado* from the jurisdiction's *Padrones* per the model sent

author: Arrillaga
summary: *(two oficios)* — (a) to examine with the Fr.-President whether divine service is better fixed at San Antonio or alternating with Santa Ana (& whether to repair the Santa Ana chapel); (b) ⭐ soldiers' uniform: may wear a dyed buckskin *chupa* with red collar & cuffs & gilt buttons, dyed buckskin breeches & red vest, but the rigorous *Reglamento* dress on feast & guard days

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Indulto á fabricantes de Chinguirito* — with the Royal Indulto, the R.O. granting general pardon to all men & women in jails/presidios/exile/correction-houses for having made *Chinguirito* (illicit cane liquor) while it was banned *(cf. Doc 188)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Continuación de servicio / Adelanto* — José Pérez continues serving here; advanced 200 p this year

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Llegada de una Goleta* — the goleta *Valdez* arrived with the presidio's & missions' Memoria, 300 fanegas maize & 8 Dominican missionaries *(cf. Doc 248)*

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Llegada de una balandra* — the balandra 'N.S. de la Merced' arrived the 22nd in transit to Sonora, free-commerce navigation per R.O.

author: Arrillaga
summary: *Llegada de una balandra* — the royal balandra 'Orcasitas' arrived from San Blas with 500 fanegas maize

author: Antonio Grajera
summary: *Remisión de una Sumaria* — remits the sumaria against Cristóbal Rey, soldier of the Volunteers, concluded with his freedom (prison served, the wounded man fully recovered & on duty, the Royal Indulto); the *reo* stays held pending superior decision *(section break → San Diego/Grajera section)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Pedido de dinero* — Pedro Alberni sent a libranza (800+ p) for the 1796 supplies to his company's *piquete*; needing cash, asks it be remitted on the Memorias ship, with the artillery supplies (Sgt. José Roca's receipt)

author: Grajera
summary: *Homicidio* — cabo Pedro Poyorena of the San Juan Capistrano escolta reports an Indian named Olerio killed his wife out of jealousy of another named Juan José; both summoned; asks whether the Indulto favors them or the case proceeds

author: Grajera
summary: *Solicitud de la gracia de inválido* — certifies Sgt. Antonio Yorba (1st free company, Catalan Volunteers) deserves Invalid status (18+ years' service, tired & ailing); *filiación* certified by Comandante Pedro Alberni *(Yorba — founder of the prominent Californio family / Rancho Santiago de Santa Ana)*

author: Grajera
summary: *(two oficios)* — continue the 100-peso retention per individual; express retirees' (*cédula de retiro*) pay in the muster extracts; asks if monthly

author: Grajera
summary: *Giro de una libranza* — at the Mission San Juan [Capistrano] Padres' request, issued the enclosed 3,000-peso libranza for the gov.'s *visa*

author: Grajera
summary: *Envío de documentos* — returns the account of the costs of building the Fuerte 'Punta de Guijarros' (the SD harbor fort), with the receipt of payment to Mission San Diego for the Indians' wages (*jornales*)

author: Grajera
summary: *Envío de Sumaria informal* — remits the Sumaria Información against Cristóbal Rey, who wounded the *soldado de cuera* Albino Ibarra *(cf. Docs 260, 273)*

author: Grajera
summary: *(two oficios)* — remits the Retention-Fund account & the Inventory of stores (both as of 31 Dec)

author: Grajera
summary: *Envío de Padron* — encloses the Estado of the jurisdiction's censuses (Dec)

author: Grajera
summary: *Envío de documentos* — remits the credits-&-debits list of his company (Dec)

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre pago á un carpintero y galafate* — sent two receipts (5 p each) for the carpenter & caulker of the frigate *Aranzazú* repairing the boat & a cart-wheel; notes he's never charged for the troop's labor in such cases, finds the unequal procedure strange

author: Grajera
summary: *Inclusión de documentos* — encloses (duplicate) the Situado regulation for this presidio in 1798 and the needed México/San Blas Memorias

author: José de Font
summary: *Herida á un soldado* — while on guard 31 Jan, his company's soldier Cristóbal Rey gravely wounded José Albino Ibarra, apparently with a knife; the *reo* shackled *(origin of the Rey case — Docs 260, 267)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre sueldos de Inválidos* — holds the R.O. of 23 Mar on the Invalids' assigned pay

author: Grajera
summary: *Recibo de un bando* — acknowledges the R.O. of 8 Nov 1795 that military men are indistinctly included in the 1776 Marriage Pragmatic

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre preguntas y respuestas en los Puertos* — the R.O. of 9 June: as the port challenge-questions are observed when ships enter, the forts answer with their flags while the ships fly theirs

author: Grajera
summary: *Agradecimiento* — notes Branciforte's 26 Sep *oficio* thanking for the donativos for the last war

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre economía de suministración* — per the 16 Jan order, will economize mid-year supply (company well-equipped) so the memorias suffice, withholding *avíos* until the *Ajustes*

author: Grajera
summary: *No remisión de documentos* — won't send last year's documents this courier (some signatures missing)

author: Grajera
summary: *Casamiento* — soldier Albino Cañedo married Felipa, daughter of the *poblador* Pablo Rodríguez

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre un cargo* — charges 24 p to that presidio (paid half to Ildefonso Bernal) for 2 pairs of boots & 12 shepherd-blankets paying for hides sent to Cárcaba

author: Grajera
summary: *Salida de un Ingeniero* — engineer Alberto Córdoba left San Diego on the 30th heading north (to the SF/Monterey fortifications), given all aid requested *(the Córdova fortification circuit — cf. Docs 26, 75)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre un descuento de bajas* — a 1 p 4 r 5½ g gratification-discount question in the 1795 *Ajustamiento* re soldier Vicente Ortega's 57 unaccrued days

author: Grajera
summary: *Solicitud de un reintegro* — encloses the account of 1796 supplies to the Volunteer company's *piquete* for reintegration *(cf. Doc 261)*

author: Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — remits the munitions Relación (Dec)

author: Grajera
summary: *Solicitud de gracia de Inválidos* — (quintuplicate) Relación of four company members deserving Invalid status: cabo José Verdugo & soldiers Manuel Nieto, Francisco López & Francisco Bruno (with certified filiaciones) *(Nieto & Verdugo — founders of the great SoCal ranchos / Californio families)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Envío de balas* — Felipe Goycoechea, Sta Bárbara commandant, sent 5,000 shotgun balls (cal. 14) to make as many cartridges

author: Alf. José Lujan
summary: *(medical)* — can't comply (grave continual illness); asks examination to return; Borica's margin orders it; surgeon Pablo Soler certifies Lujan has a curable venereal disease *(section shift → Monterey docs interleaved)*

author: Lujan
summary: *(copy) Sobre su inutilidad* — pleads grave ailments suffered since childhood make him unfit; asks to be placed at S.E.'s disposal

author: Grajera
summary: *Relación de una Escuela* — the San Diego *escuela de primeras letras* roster (teacher: retired Sgt. Manuel Vargas): 25 children with name, age, months attending, class & progress — incl. José Antonio Yorba (12), Tomás Yorba (9), Juan Peralta (10), Gil/Desiderio/Andrés Ibarra, Matías/Desiderio Olivera, Manuel & José Verdugo, Patricio/Enrique Sepúlveda, José Antonio Romero, Máximo/Miguel Valenzuela, Diego Lizalde, Isidro/Nicolás Alaniz, Pedro/Cecilio Lobo, José López, Doroteo Alvarez, Estévan López, León Rosas, José María Silva. *Resumen*: writing 5, *en carta* 4, *en libro* 4, *en catón* 4, *en cartilla* 8 = 25; writing samples follow

author: Manuel Vargas
summary: *Solicitud de remoción / Pedido de dinero* — the schoolteacher asks for his removal and 50 p for sundry needs; pins all hope on the gov. to get him out of here

author: Grajera
summary: *Remisión de una Causa criminal* — remits the case against Aurelio, Indian of Mission San Juan Capistrano, for killing his wife; thought no formal process needed given the *reo*'s sincere confession that he only meant to punish her with blows and she likely took a fatal head-blow falling *(possibly = the 'Olerio' case, Doc 262)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre un cargo* — the habilitado-gen.'s factura charges 62 p 2 r as part of the late Insp. Soler's *concurso* costs; asks how to cover it

author: Grajera
summary: *(Estado)* — a mid-year Estado of the San Diego primary school *(cf. Doc 290)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Reconocimiento de balas* — Goycoechea's balls are all cal. 9; having enough cal. 14 + a mold to make more, no need to request them from Goycoechea *(cf. Doc 287)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre su trato á Indios y sobre evitar tratos ilícitos* — answering reflections on the death in a waterhole of the Indian Juan Nepomuceno: he treats Indians with piety but corrects them — moderate work, sufficient food, regular rest, care when sick; keeps the local natives from mixing much with the mission Indians to avoid the *ilícitos comercios* with the women *(a presidio counterpart to the SF Indian-treatment debate)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Castigo por dejar fugar á Indios* — the two soldiers who let six imprisoned Indians escape en route to San Miguel finished their 8-day punishment (2 hrs sentry daily wearing three *cueras*)

author: Grajera
summary: *Recibo de un formulario* — received the form for the year-end census Estado

author: José Lujan
summary: *Solicitud de renuncia* — resigns the SD alférez post (feeble constitution), asking uniform-use & transfer to a regiment after cure; Borica's unsigned margin is damning: Lujan reached Monterey 10 May, wife died, refused to serve citing ailments, no merits — 'jamás podrá desempeñar las funciones de su empleo'

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre correspondencia* — will condition the mail both directions, re-wrap worn covers; suggests one or two *maletas* made at the Renta's cost

author: Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — the frigate *Princesa* anchored here on the 15th *(the war-defense frigate — cf. Doc 196)*

author: Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — sends the Relación of the artillery & its ordnance [SD presidio]

author: Grajera
summary: *Indio ahogado* — the convict Indian (*presidiario*) Juan Nepomuceno, a quiet man, found drowned in a waterhole across the arroyo opposite the presidio; investigated for an aggressor, found nothing *(the death behind Doc 296)*

author: Grajera
summary: *(two oficios)* — remits the sworn Relación of royal-service *pliegos* & letters he's franked (asks reintegration) & the Cruzada *Bulas* account for the closing biennium

author: Grajera
summary: *Envío de Cuenta de retención* — the Retention-Fund account (discounts to complete 100 p per man)

author: Grajera
summary: *Definitiva de un Pleito* — will inform the interested party, Capt. José Zúñiga, of Cárcaba's news of the unexpected outcome of the Soler-debts expediente

author: Grajera
summary: *Recibo de orden* — instructed on the R. resolution of 8 Dec re suits under 100 p & minor criminal complaints

author: Grajera
summary: *Cuenta de Diezmos* — sworn San Diego tithe account 1797: 43 cattle & 30 horses branded, 5 cows slaughtered, 194 head of R.Hac. cattle born = total 37 p 6 r 9½ g; less Grajera's 10% honorarium, the rest by libranza to the Real Caja de Guadalajara

author: Grajera
summary: *Cuenta de Fragua y Carpintería* — San Diego forge & carpentry account 1797: products 93 p 4 r (incl. work for the SD troop, Volunteers/Artillery, Presidio Loreto, & Missions San Juan, Sta Catarina, Sto Tomás, San Miguel — the SD armory served Baja); discounts 163 p 7 r 6 g (Master Armorer's gratification, 2 apprentices, forge & carpentry costs); net deficit 70 p 3 r 6 g

author: Grajera
summary: *(two oficios) Media firma / Vinos* — (a) Juan Manuel Álvarez may sign with surname only on non-royal papers; (b) ⭐ will permit the free wholesale & retail introduction & sale of California-made wines & grape-brandy (*vinos y aguardiente de uva*) at agreed prices, merchants barred from suing troops/Loreto sailors/artisans/pobladores for supplies (who'd lose pawned uniform/arms/saddle)

author: Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — understands the R.O. of 12 Jan on forwarding the *ocursos* made to the King

author: Grajera
summary: *Pase á Inválidos* — tomorrow cabo Pedro Poyorena leaves for San Gabriel replacing cabo José María Verdugo, passing to Invalids

author: Grajera
summary: *Señales de reconocimiento* — received the recognition signals the San Blas ships must observe on reaching these establishments

author: Grajera
summary: *Ascenso* — received three royal despachos: alférez of this presidio for José Lujan, and Teniente + retirement-of-alférez for Pablo Grijalva *(Grijalva — Anza-expedition veteran, ancestor of the Yorba/Peralta lines)*

author: Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — will remit the documents due that Government as soon as possible

author: Grajera
summary: *(two oficios) Indulto á desertores / Noticia* — received the *bando* of the Royal Indulto to naval-corps deserters; encloses the Noticia of the last 5 years' yield of SD's Real Hacienda branches

author: Grajera
summary: *(three oficios) Hojas / Cargo / Indios* — remits his service records; charges 66 p 7 r; cabo José Pico to San Miguel with 4 men, and cabo Francisco Acevedo (instructed to manage it *sin efusión de sangre*) to arrest the Indians Fr. Mariano Yoldi names for their frequent excesses *(restraint order echoing Borica's SF instruction)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Aprehensión de unos Indios* — cabo Acevedo reports the valley gentiles brought in the Indian Luis (who wounded soldier Ignacio Acevedo) and 3 others; sends for the 3 co-aggressors to punish them; asks whether Luis recovers

author: Grajera
summary: *Solicitud de reclutas* — acknowledges the providence to seek recruits in the Pueblo de Los Ángeles

author: Grajera
summary: *(two oficios)* — Invalids/absolute-discharge men must settle in the two pueblos or villa de Branciforte, only staying at the presidio if they rotate guard with the company; received the invalid certificate for Sgt. Antonio Yorba, who asks to wait on the gov.'s decision re his destination *(cf. Doc 263)*

author: Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — won't send this month's Revista (signatures missing)

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre una fragata* — Goycoechea reports the Manila frigate 'Magallanes' put in at Santa Bárbara seeking the San Blas ships to convoy with *(cf. Doc 224)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Herida* — Cristóbal Rey wounded the recruit Albino Ibarra, who says the quarrel began over a *mascada* (tobacco) of soldier Juan Alipas; the *reo* held *(cf. Docs 260, 267, 273)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Pelea* — Fr. Mariano Yoldi reports that at the Mission San Miguel rancho two vaqueros & soldier Ignacio Acevedo were wounded; sent the alférez + cabo + 4 soldiers to seize the aggressors, chief being the indio Luis, one of the two 'most perverse' of that mission, who'd been kept here shackled (*mancornado*) at labor

author: Francisco Acevedo
summary: *Sobre la misma agresión* — sent his brother + 2 vaqueros for a steer on 30 Nov; at the *rodeo* found indio Luis & another about to kill the cattle; on being seen they took up arrows and fought — vaqueros & the soldier wounded, the soldier taking six head-wounds and a broken arm (from pieces of the shotgun), now much recovered

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre víveres* — copies the order for Sta Cruz & villa Branciforte so that, if a post is invaded, the enemy gets no fresh provisions or cattle *(scorched-earth)*

author: Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — Fr. Hilario Torrent embarked on the frigate *Princesa*

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre permiso de embarque* — awaits leave for the 'moro' Manuel Gustinzar[?] (Filipino/Asian sailor) to cross to the *otra banda*

author: Grajera
summary: *Entrega de una Carta* — delivered in person to Tte de Navío García Zerpa Manrique, commander of the frigate *Princesa*, Borica's letter (2nd inst.); encloses his reply

author: José Lujan
summary: *Consulta de opinión* — his ailments worsening, fears the worst without medicines or a *facultativo*; asks guidance on restoring his health

author: Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — remits the detailed company list

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre embarque de dos Domínicos* — will let Fr. Antonio & Fr. Rafael Caballero embark with their Prelate's license *(cf. Docs 186, 252)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre socorros á una Nao* — will supply the Nao de China (Manila Galleon) if it puts in at any of this coast's anchorages

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre permiso de embarque* — will let Fr. Hilario Torrent embark on presenting his Prelate's license

author: Francisco Acevedo
summary: *Avistamiento de barcos* — an Indian sent by the San Fernando mayordomo brought word of 16 vessels heading for the presidio; Acevedo climbed the mission hill and saw 5 ships near the outer Coronado [Island] *(the on-the-ground origin of the 16-ship war alarm — cf. Doc 221, the Loreto relay)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Providencias sobre lo mismo* — at 8:30 PM a soldier from San Miguel arrived with the report; notified the *Princesa* commander and is reinforcing the castillo [Punta de Guijarros], doubling lookouts, withdrawing cattle, summoning the invalids & vecinos

author: Grajera
summary: *Investigación de lo mismo* — no ship seen elsewhere within 30 leagues; re-examined the two original spotters — an 'Indio Castellano' saw 17 sails, the San Fernando mayordomo 14, seemingly bound for Ensenada de Todos Santos — but the Todos Santos cabo saw nothing *(the alarm largely deflated — likely a false alarm)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Víveres á una fragata / Reparo de barca y aguada* — the frigate *Princesa* lent its cooper to fix the Fort's water-supply (*aguada*) & caulkers to careen the boat (half-pay); provisions to the frigate charged to San Blas

author: Grajera
summary: *(two oficios)* — no time to answer that quarter's mail; no novelty on the jurisdiction's coast

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre aplicación de un alférez* — hasn't formed a full view of Alf. José Lujan's application/inapplication given his pretexted ailments; will employ him once recovered

author: Grajera
summary: *Salida de una fragata* — the frigate *Concepción* was sighted and the *Princesa* sailed; neither seen again today

author: Grajera
summary: *Remisión de llaves de correo* — sends this courier in one of the two new mail-bags; remits six identical mail-keys via Goycoechea (4 for Borica, 2 kept, 2 to Loreto)

author: Pedro Poyorena
summary: *Solicitud de permuta* — asks transfer to Monterey or Sta Bárbara, or absolute discharge, because his commandant Grajera is the cause that he now lives 'como divorciado de su mujer, lleno de los mayores recelos y muy sonrojado' (implying Grajera's impropriety with his wife); margin: *providencia* taken

author: Grajera
summary: *(two oficios)* — urged Alf. Lujan's servant Manuel Gustinzar (the 'moro') to enlist; he refused & embarked on the *Princesa*; received the four invalid certificates (Verdugo, Nieto, López, Bruno) *(cf. Doc 286)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Avisos sobre novedades de embarcaciones* — per Borica, not to report ship-sightings by express without some certainty (after the false 16-ship alarm); will keep the caution discreet so others still report real news

author: Grajera
summary: *Ajuste de un soldado* — the discharge account (*borderol*) of soldier José Herrera (absolute license): abonos 373 p 0 r 2 g, charges 215 p 5 r 11½ g, received 157 p 2 r 2½ g *(sample presidio-pay accounting)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre no obedecimiento* — order that if captured by the English, he is to obey no order he gives while prisoner (even one strongly revoking this); transmitted to the escolta commanders, who likewise won't obey him in that case

author: Grajera
summary: *Solicitud de reclutas* — tried to recruit from the *Princesa* crew; only one was willing & he rejected him as unfit

author: Grajera
summary: *Envío de un proceso* — sends the (he believes complete) process against the Indian Aurelio of San Juan Capistrano for killing his wife *(cf. Docs 262, 292)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre expresión de peso de efectos* — the San Blas effects Relación omits the weight of the *panocha* (which arrives spoiled in ill-conditioned *cuarterolas*); can't justify the *merma*; asks weights be stated henceforth

author: Grajera
summary: *Provisiones á una fragata* — provisioning the frigate *Princesa*: asked Mission San Diego for 500 arrobas flour (Padres sending plenty since), bought 50 cattle for fresh meat, supplied oxen & carts for water & oven-wood

author: Grajera
summary: *Orden sobre cierto alférez* — Alf. José Lujan arrived today; to be exercised touring the missions with a trusted cabo/sgt, on foot & horse, joining all works to 'limber up' & meet his obligation

author: José Pérez Fernández
summary: *Entrega de Habilitación* — presents the documents (per the index) that were his as Monterey habilitado, and reports handing all the Existencias over to Hermenegildo Sal *(section shift → Monterey habilitación accounts)*

author: José Pérez Fernández
summary: Índice of the Monterey Habilitación accounts/documents (end-1796), handed to Sal: 14 items — company general account, credits/debits list, Gratificación & Retención funds, Correos, Diezmos, Tabacos, Real Hacienda, Municiones, Artisans' Salaries, inventories of Existencias & papers, the 10-inventory carpeta, & a separate post-handover cargos/abonos account

author: José Pérez Fernández (habilitado)
summary: 1796 Monterey company General Account (Cargo/Data), balanced at 81,857 p (2 r 4⅛ g in the habilitado's favor); notable: artisan salaries 5,278 p; botica effects to surgeon Pablo Soler; the named artisans Santiago Ruiz, Pedro González, Joaquín Botello, Manuel Muñoz, Mariano Mendoza; and ⭐ a debt-network snapshot — sums owed: Loreto 743, San Diego 530, Sta Bárbara 1154, San Francisco 1191, Misión San Carlos 1333, Sta Cruz 582, Sta Clara 2147, San Antonio 482, San Luis 1131, La Soledad 417 … total 9,714 p (inter-presidio/mission credit web; a parallel 1795 debt-list = 9,713 p)

author: Pérez Fernández
summary: Handover inventories to Sal: the 16-carpeta archive of books & documents 1781–1796; church utensils; habilitación effects; carpentry/forge/factory tools; R.Hac. effects; and the ⭐ mule-train (*Recua*) inventory — 25 good mules, 3 fair, 4 useless, 12 pack-horses, 36 aparejos, 15 *mantas de jatear*, 50½ loads of *vaqueta* sacks, etc.

author: Pérez Fernández (inventory)
summary: Monterey presidio Artillery & Munitions inventory (end-1796): flagstaff + 2 flags; 5 eight-pdr cannon, 3 six-pdrs, 2 three-pdrs (10 guns); 1,310 iron 8-balls, 70 six, 190 four, 373 bronze 3-balls, 85 two, 63 one; 400+ grapeshot sacks; tools; 700 cal-8 cartridges, 100 six, 200 three; powder 3 + 67 quintales (mecha-cord/fine), 29 medium, 43 ordinary + 369 arrobas ordinary & 32 fine; 4,096 shotgun cartridges, 7,940 flints, 409 arrobas plank-lead; ⭐ 3 *pedreros* (cal-2): one at the San Luis escolta, one at the San José pueblo, one 'hecho pedazos' in the presidio guard

author: Pérez Fernández / Sal (interv. Argüello)
summary: *(reconciliation)* — the account cutting all loose cargos/abonos between Pérez Fernández & his successor Sal: cargo 231 p, data 64 p, delivered to Sal 167 p (notes reduce it to ~110 p, then ~157 p in cash; 18½ fanegas wheat consumed Jan in presidio & married-Volunteer rations)

author: José Argüello
summary: *Sobre entrega de una Habilitación* — confirms Alf. Pérez Fernández has completed the handover of the Monterey Habilitación & its branches to Teniente Sal (with his intervention), both parties fully satisfied

author: Hermenegildo Sal
summary: *Recibo de Habilitación* — received the Monterey Habilitación with all its Existencias from Alf. José Pérez

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre establecimiento de fábricas de aguardiente* — received Branciforte's *bando*: given cane-brandy sold without paying duties, to cut the abuse S.E. proposes to S.M. establishing distilleries for it *(cf. Doc 188)*

author: Sal
summary: *Contrata de dos artesanos* — the blacksmiths José Faustino [Arriola] & José Arriola are contracted 2 years at 360 p/yr; to supply their salaries *(cf. Docs 62, 79)*

author: Sal
summary: *Remisión de armamento* — the Viceroy ordered the armament shipped on the *Concepción* distributed among the Invalids & pueblo vecinos; sent the Pueblo 9 shotguns, 16 cartridge-boxes, 2 lead plates, a mold, paper for a cartridge reserve & 300 flints, ordered weekly arms review + one target round at presidio & mission escoltas

author: Marcos Briones
summary: *Aprehensión de un Indio* — on the 18th seized the gentiles who killed the Christian Indian Toribio of this mission (and burned his body); fell on a ranchería at the edge of the Tulares valley, caught the chief malefactor + 2 others; the *reo* explains he killed Toribio because a Christian in Toribio's party had come near his house — *(the Tulares-expedition / San Luis killing of the gap-sample)*

author: Sal
themes: monterey; indigenous; death-in-custody; forensic; military-history; soler
summary: *Declaraciones sobre muerte de un Gentil* — the death-in-custody of the gentile Choychi (from near San Luis) in the Monterey guardhouse: held 22 days, he seized a bayoneted musket and wounded soldier Marín Quesadas in the chest; soldier Marín clubbed him on the head & he died ~1½ hrs later, foaming blood. Witness depositions (cabos Antonio Yorba & Aniceto Soler, soldiers Narciso Forés[?], Juan Pinto, Juan Jiménez, Quesadas). ⭐ Surgeon Pablo Soler's testimony: Choychi had two ribs fractured when brought from San Luis (i.e. injured at capture) and died more from the chest contusion than the head blow

author: Sal
summary: *Equipo de cartuchos y piedras* — equipped the troop with 60 cartridges each (20 in the box, 2 packets in the saddle-cushions) + 6 flints; the artillery & volunteer detachment with 20 cartridges & 3 flints *(cf. SF Doc 77)*

author: José Antonio Rodríguez
summary: *Sobre unos cañonazos* — an Indian heard three cannon-shots up-coast the night of the 12th; margin: relayed to the Monterey lookouts

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre remisión de documentos* — will send the principal monthly Estado + Diario, duplicated with the muster extracts

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre un Cargo* — charged the 50 p relieved to the blacksmiths José Faustino Arriola & José Arroyo, destined to this Province on the *Concepción* *(cf. Doc 362)*

author: Sal
summary: *Medidas contra una invasión* — Monterey's defense plan against invasion: troops supplied with shotguns, swords, lances & munitions; the nearby Mission, Real de Caballada & R.Hac. Rancho ordered that at the first cannon-shot the rally point — at full gallop — is the Presidio; bastimentos to be disabled so they can't shelter the enemy

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre detención de buques ingleses* — the R.O. to detain by reprisal all English ships in our ports; will proceed cautiously to seize any reaching this port or jurisdiction anchorage

author: Sal
summary: *Nombramiento en el Ramo de Bulas* — the Monterey habilitado to administer the Cruzada *Bulas* for all four Alta California presidios *(cf. Doc 67)*

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre declaración de Guerra* — received the *bando* of S.M.'s declaration of war on the Court of London *(cf. Doc 202)*

author: Sal
summary: *(brief)* — R.O. that naval officers reaching the Viceroyalty's ports obey the Viceroy

author: Sal
summary: *(brief)* — received from surgeon Pablo Soler 3,000 p at Lt.-Col. Pedro Alberni's disposal

author: Sal
summary: *(brief)* — received the General Indulto for the peace with France & the Infantas' weddings

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre impedir desembarque* — will fulfil the 6 decrees on prompt warnings of any ship sighted & preventing its landing, using the troop, invalids, vecinos & mission Indians

author: Sal
summary: *Nombramiento de Vicario* — the Bishop of Sonora named the Fr.-President [Lasuén] vicario foráneo *(cf. Doc 69)*

author: Sal
summary: *(brief)* — received 8 Invalid certificates for the 2 cabos & 6 soldiers of this company

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre el fondo de Alcance* — the retention for the *Alcance* fund raised from 50 p to 100 p per cabo/soldier

author: Sal
summary: *(brief)* — will express each Invalid's pay in the monthly extracts

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre un cierto pago* — the Viceroy ordered the local R.Hac. cajas pay Cárcaba 444 p 2 r 9½ g spent on the Monterey Esplanada (fortification works)

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre remisión de documentos* — each March will send (triplicate) the Situado regulations for his company (excluding volunteers/artillery); the México & San Blas Memorias triplicate & separate, no luxury goods in the first

author: Sal
summary: *Entrega de plomo* — will deliver 30 arrobas of lead to the San Francisco presidio at first chance

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre un pago* — the Viceroy ordered Cárcaba paid 308 p 4 r for the refreshments supplied to the English frigate of Don Guillermo (William) Broughton *(HMS Providence at Monterey 1796 — cross-ref C-A 55 / Macario de Castro diary)*

author: Marcos Briones
summary: *Sobre avistamiento de un barco* — Mission San Luis Indians sighted a ship today; went at once to verify & saw nothing; learned at a ranchería it was heading for the Channel [Sta Bárbara]; left a soldier to keep watch with Christian Indians

author: Sal
summary: *(brief)* — received R.Ord. on the questions & answers to follow at the arrival of vessels

author: Sal
