summary: *Sobre suministraciones* — is instructed in the method of supplying the troop & their families, advancing the *avíos* through the 1st or 2nd month of the coming year, per B.'s ord. of the 16th

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre un pago* — Viceroy orders 1,500 p 6 r 10 g that this Habilitación had advanced from its church's building fund be repaid to Manuel Cárcaba

author: Sal
summary: *Bando sobre aguardiente / Baja de revista / Economía en las distribuciones* (3 ofs) — received Viceroy Branciforte's bando re cane-*aguardiente* made before the lifting of its ban being sold; will strike carpenter Salvador Béjar from the muster once he enlists as soldier in the S. Diego company; ordered to economize stores in the Almacén & the year's Memorias in case no ships bring the '98 Memorias, given the war with England

author: Sal
summary: *Envío de un juicio* — forwards the *juicio* of balances & repairs produced by the account of the Fort of San Francisco

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre fábrica de aguardiente* — received copies of the R.Ord. lifting the cane-*aguardiente* ban & permitting its manufacture & sale, and of the Reglamento for administering this new branch of the R. Erario

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre asistencia á Pobladores* — received the roll of families/individuals who volunteered to the Intendente of Guadalajara to begin the important settlement of this Peninsula [Baja Calif.], & list nº 1; will begin assisting the 9 new pobladores in Relación nº 2

author: Sal
summary: *Recibo de Instrucciones pa Cabos de nuevas Misiones* — received the Instructions for the corporals commissioned to the foundings of Missions San Juan Bautista & San Miguel; will advance the listed items so there is no delay when the founding friars ask to set out

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre uso de chupa* — made known to his company B.'s order that soldiers who wish may wear *chupa* & *calzón* of dyed buckskin (*gamuza teñida*)

author: Sal
summary: *Instancia sobre asistencias / Indulto* (2 ofs) — noted R.Ord. granting indulto to all who broke the *chinguirito* (cane-liquor) ban; & R.Ord. on the petition of Josefa Matamoros that the treasury of Ceuta, where she resides, continue the assistances her husband Domingo Camuñez assigned her

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre pliegos de correspª* — per B.'s ord. of the 12th, when worn R. service covers (*pliegos luidos*) reach his administration he will re-cover them & forward letters in separate packets

author: Sal
summary: *(brief)* — noted that [Miguel José de] Azanza is the new Viceroy

author: Sal
summary: *Concesión de un Obispo* — noted that the Bishop of Sonora, Francisco Rouset de Jesús, as Teniente de Vicario General de los Ejércitos, grants to Padre Presidente Fermín Francisco Lasuén & all his subject friars who administer the Sacraments to the military the use of the privileges the pontifical Breves grant the Vicario General

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre cobro de un cierto trabajo* — if Lasuén decides the friars of San Juan Capistrano should pay a just sum for the work the weaver Mariano Mendoza spent on behalf of that mission, Sal is to collect it & credit the R. Hacienda

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre cobro de un trabajo á favor de una Misión* — re the office sent to the friars of La Purísima on the destination of the weaver Antonio Enrique: apply ⅞ of his work to the R. Hacienda & ⅛ to himself, charging the mission what corresponds

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre entrada de una cantidad* — will enter the 165 p in the R. Hac. account if it assumes the sum advanced at Guadalajara to the individuals who presented as pobladores of these Establishments (for their journey to Tepic)

author: Sal
summary: *Establecimiento de un telar de frazadas / Gastos de cierto Pleito* (2 ofs) — ordered not to request blankets from México since a blanket loom (telar de frazadas) has been set up at this presidio; will charge the *fondo de Gratificación* the costs of the Cárcaba lawsuit to recover what was advanced to Soler, pending resolution of repayment

author: Sal
summary: *(brief)* — received the model for the annual Padrón (census)

author: Sal
summary: *(brief)* — received the office of the measures to prevent smallpox (viruela)

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre envío de ganado* — will buy 150 range cows & 6 bulls from Mission San Luis Obispo to dispatch to the San Francisco presidio, together with the 37 heifers & 13 young bulls to be drawn from the Rancho del Rey

author: Sal
summary: *Baja en revista* — on the 1st of next month will strike soldier Mariano Duarte from the muster; he will continue in service at San Francisco

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre pobladores de tres villas* — per B.'s ord. of the 15th, will require every sergeant, corporal & soldier who is discharged or retires as Invalid to go settle in one of the pueblos of San José, Los Ángeles, or the villa de Branciforte

author: Sal
summary: *Permuta de un soldado* — on 1 Nov. will admit into this company the San Francisco soldier Carlos Carrillo

author: Sal
summary: *(brief)* — will forward the *Extracto* of his company as soon as possible

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre ausilios á una Nao* — should the Nao de China (Manila galleon) put in along these coasts, will furnish the aid it requests

author: Sal
summary: *Prevenciones contra un ataque* — should the Enemy take this post, will draw on the Indians of Mission San Carlos, drive all cattle & horse-herds inland, and destroy all stores that cannot be carried off (scorched-earth), per B.'s ord. of the 19th

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre curso de Instancias* — noted the R. resolution holding chiefs (*Gefes*) responsible who, through sloth or personal resentment, delay the course of *Instancias* of any class, even those seeming unfounded

author: Cayetano Espinosa
summary: *Sobre dos cañonazos* — the Indians heard two cannon-shots & shouts near the beach this morning; tomorrow he will go to the *Vigía* (lookout) to see what is happening

author: Marcos Briones
summary: *Pesquisa de malhechores* — set out on the 8th in search of the malefactors per the governor's order & could not find them; at a ranchería an old India told him the Gentiles of those parts had gathered near the headwaters (nacimiento) as if heading for the Tulares; he turned back for lack of provisions & intends to return on the 19th to quiet that gathering of Gentiles

author: Marcos Briones
summary: *Muerte á un Indio cristiano* — the Padre sent Christian Indians to fetch a Gentile woman to marry a Christian who had been her husband in heathendom; returning with her, they passed a ranchería where an old Gentile with two sons killed Toribio (one of the party) on suspicion he had bewitched/poisoned (enyerbado) his Christian daughter who died at this mission; Briones set out today with 3 soldiers & Christian Indians to seize the malefactors

author: Marcos Briones
summary: *Sobre alboroto de Indios* — the Gentiles are much agitated per the Christians' reports; Corporal [José Francisco de] Ortega has reached this mission & may be detained because of the uncertainty whether the Gentile Indians will 'fall' [submit] or not

author: Sal
summary: *Abono á herreros / Rl resolución / Indulto á desertores / Media firma* (4 ofs) — to credit blacksmiths Faustino Arriola & José Arroyo their pay from 16 March; will instruct subordinates on the R. resolution re the *expediente* of María Rosique, food-vendor; noted S.M. grants indulto to deserters of the naval guild (gremio de la Marina); & that Don Juan Manuel Álvarez may sign with surname only except on documents bearing the royal signature

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre señales de reconocimiento* — has the certified copy of the recognition signals that San Blas vessels are henceforth to observe when they present at these ports

author: Sal
summary: *Remisión de armamento* — received the invoices for the 200 short muskets, 200 covers, 200 cartridge-boxes & 16,000 gunflints the Viceroy sent on the R. Hac. account to arm the citizenry (vecindario) of New California

author: Sal
summary: *(brief)* — forwards a *Noticia* of the R. Hacienda product, 1792 through 1796 inclusive

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre venta de vinos* — will permit in his jurisdiction the introduction & sale of wines & grape-brandy grown in the Californias at agreed prices; sellers have no recourse to claim what they advance to individuals the King maintains [soldiers]

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre casamiento sin licencia* — noted the R. resolution ordering the punishment of Lt. José Sartorio of the Naples regiment for marrying without royal license

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre esponsales* — received the Viceroy's bando & 2nd ord. of 22 Jan. on declarations by Correos (postal) dependents in betrothal (*esponsales*) matters

author: Sal
summary: *Entrega de dineros* — ordered 1,500 p delivered to Goycoechea on this Habilitación's account: 1,309 p 6 r at Grajera's disposal, 824 p 3 r 7 g for Alberni, 285 p 2 r 11 g for the artillery detachment

author: Sal
summary: *(brief)* — on 1 Jan. next will enroll Joaquín Castro in the Invalid class

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre defensa de la Costa* — noted the Viceroy ordered the war-armed frigate *Concepción* with another of greater or equal force to remain in these seas to defend the coasts from enemy insults; Sal stands ready to furnish whatever aid the frigates' commanders may need

author: Sal
summary: *Sobre cuentas de mulada, manteca y cueros / eliminación de fracciones de grano* — in reply to B.'s office of the 4th: this year all R. Hac. mule-stock (mulada) of *herradero* age & up inventoried on a separate account; individual accounts of lard & tallow; same for usable hides of dead cattle (those slaughtered for the troop uncollected for years); will pay the tithe (diezmo) to the Guadalajara cajas — 2 r per female slaughtered + 25-per-thousand on sheep; sworn inventory of all R. Hac. stock; will drop grain fractions from accounts

author: Sal
summary: *(brief)* — received the Reglamento for administering cane-*aguardiente*

author: [Felipe de] Goycoechea
summary: *Sobre moneda falsa* — received Branciforte's order to investigate circulation of counterfeit pesos fuertes; found 131 p whose double 'F' marks mean *fábrica falsa* & 6 more with reversed F & M of the worst quality; says ~5 million of this coin was struck at Puebla, & that when the maker was found the King spared his life but exiled him to a presidio for life for having stamped the truth of its falseness on the coins

author: Goycoechea
summary: *(brief)* — forwards the list of present individuals of his company

author: Goycoechea
summary: *(brief)* — forwards 4 escolta diaries for last September

author: Goycoechea
summary: *Visita á unas Escoltas* — transmits the diary kept by Sgt. José María Ortega on his tour of the escoltas dependent on this company

author: Goycoechea
summary: *Remisión de documentos / Fundación de una Misión* (2 ofs) — forwards Extracts & company State; encloses Sgt. Ignacio Olivera's document showing that on 8 Sept. last possession was taken of the new mission, named San Fernando Rey de España, where 13 persons of both sexes have so far been baptized; the Guardia-Almacén is finished, 2 houses begun, & the missionaries hold the former house of Reyes & other dwellings

author: Goycoechea
summary: *(brief)* — relayed to his subordinates the order to aid the Nao de China (Manila galleon)

author: Goycoechea
summary: *Petición sobre un herrero* — master smith José Faustino Arriola is being sent to the capital [Monterey]; asks he be left at this presidio. *Marginal note: this point was already answered = Borica*

author: [Antonio] Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — today's Correo leaves with orders to be at San Juan [Capistrano] by daybreak

author: Grajera
summary: *Proposición pa asalariar lancheros* — if the Indians are dismissed there will be no one to row, yet the boat is needed for the Fort's garrison & there may be no prisoners to do it; proposes recruiting 4 young men (mocetones) to serve in the launch permanently for a wage of 1½ r/day & ration

author: Grajera
summary: *Detención de un albañil / Daño en los ganados / Falta de tacos y barriles / aumento á una escolta* (3 ofs) — detains mason Toribio Ruiz with soldier José Lobo (running the brick-works) to advance 2 houses & a small warehouse this month; Gentile Indians with some cimarrones are doing notable damage to the San Miguel cattle — will detach the sergeant & 8 men to seize them; short of cannon-wadding material, has resorted to burlap sacks (*guangochas*); needs ≥24 good barrels & wood for oars; adds one man to the San Juan escolta to keep a daily lookout on the beach

author: Grajera
themes: san-diego; militia; arming-settlers; los-angeles; san-gabriel; anglo-spanish-war
summary: *Sobre armar vecinos* — on B.'s order to arm & equip the Invalids & citizens of this jurisdiction, had already directed Los Ángeles, San Gabriel & the rancho de Nieto to stand ready with their arms to act promptly on first notice, ordering the San Juan corporal to summon them the instant any ship is sighted

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre compostura de carretas* — has no means to repair the carts; haulage of water & other necessaries to the Fort of Punta de Guijarros is becoming difficult, & the want of a carpenter is keenly felt

author: Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — encloses the proposal of Antonio Ibarra (Yrba) for his Invalids

author: Antonio Grajera (juez fiscal)
summary: *Sumaria information* against Cristóbal Rey, soldier of the 1st Volunteer Company, for wounding a *soldado de cuera* [named José Albino in the heading; the testimony calls the wounded combatant Ibarra — Savage's text is inconsistent]; declarations taken 31 Jan–22 Feb 1797 (escribano Juan [Aguirre?]). Witness Juan Nepomuceno Alipas: a stolen *mascada* (headcloth) sparked it; the two fought with sword & knife by the arroyo below the smith's orchard at 7:30 p.m.; the surgeon found 2 wounds (one a *geme*+ long from the left shoulder toward the lungs). 22 Feb. the wounded man declared fully healed; 8 Apr. Grajera decreed Rey's release for time already in prison; Borica confirmed, applying the recent *indulto* (Monterey, 25 Apr.), Rey to continue in service

author: Grajera
summary: *Avistamiento de un buque* — Goycoechea reports the San Luis Indians sighted a large ship on the 18th, which the La Purísima lookout watched until past midday when fog hid it

author: Grajera
themes: san-diego; defense; scorched-earth; punta-de-guijarros; san-juan-capistrano; anglo-spanish-war
summary: *Sobre preguntas para el caso de ataque* — defense questionnaire: should powder-boxes be kept loose & ready in the Casa Mata of Punta de Guijarros; what to do with artillery & powder if the Enemy enters at anchor; whether to burn the presidio if evacuated; who defends each post if invaded; notes the English surely know the San Juan Capistrano anchorage, where a 4-cal pedrero with grapeshot + two more of like calibre would be well placed

author: Javier Alvarado
summary: *Vista de barcos* — a Gentile Indian reports having seen, the day before, three ships on three occasions behind Punta de la Amarga; sent a courier to the presidio 'just in case'

author: Grajera
summary: *Envío de Indios y artesanos* — sends to Santa Bárbara the three San Francisco Indians & the one from Carmel, & mason Toribio Ruiz; encloses the Pueblo commissioner's report; since nothing stirs on the coast, dispatches the weaver, all to continue together up to the Channel

author: Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — dispatched an office to the San Blas Comisario, who began reading it without noting the title

author: Grajera
summary: *Envío de un documento* — forwards to San Francisco, to join the *Expediente*, the reply to the objection the Contaduría mayor made to his 1795 R. Hac. account *(note: 1798 date)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Remisión de documentos* — returns the corrected Fábrica account, with Fr. Juan Mariner authorizing payment of the wages earned by the Indians of this [San Diego] Mission

author: Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — today Alférez José Pérez continues his march to Loreto

author: Grajera
summary: *Envío de una Sumaria* — forwards the sumaria against Aurelio, a San Juan Capistrano neophyte who confessed to killing his wife; the Indian accused of cohabitation (*amancebamiento*) with said wife was returned free to his mission

author: Francisco Acevedo
summary: *Sobre devolución de solds* — Sgt. Ruiz asks the return of the soldiers escorting the mail-bag, since the sighting of 17 sails off Cabo San Lucas heading north has increased the coast-guards *(Baja end of the 1797 war scare — cross-ref Docs 221, 335–337, 444)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre avistamiento de velas* — encloses the letter of the San Miguel corporal in which the Comandante de la Frontera reports sails seen off Cabo San Lucas

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre traje de soldº en ciertas faenas* — told his company that for fatigue duties (guarding herds, droving the *recua*, courier, hauling, cutting & dressing timber) soldiers may wear buckskin *chupa* & breeches with navy collar, red cuff & yellow button

author: Grajera
summary: *Indulto* — noted S.M. grants a General Indulto to persons imprisoned or exiled for the *chinguirito* (cane-liquor) contraband

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre ciertas retenciones* — noted R.O. of 25 Sept. that the Kingdom's treasuries must withhold the portions of pay that officers with families in Spain have assigned them

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre una fundación* — received the Instructions for the officer commissioned to erect a new Mission on terrain the Padre Presidente chooses, & a list of provisions & goods for the founding *(precursor to Mission San Luis Rey, founded 1798)*

author: Grajera
summary: *Retención á un Alfz* — that presidio [Monterey] will assist Alférez José Luján with ⅔ of his pay & this presidio will do likewise until what was advanced to him in México is covered

author: Grajera
summary: *Cuenta de un Soldado* — account of soldier Francisco García, this day passing to Invalid at Santa Bárbara; received 10 p 7 r 8⅝ g in hand

author: Grajera
summary: *Exclusión de un soldº* — today struck soldier Francisco García for his chronic illness; forwards his *Ajuste*

author: Grajera
summary: *Prevenciones para caso de ataque* — noted the frigate Concepción & another will guard these coasts; the instant he learns of enemy ships or any post being invaded he is to report in detail, preparing for defense & offense as far as possible, keeping the standing orders in view

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre cuentas de ganados* — noted the requisites the R. Tribunal de Cuentas demands for the accounts of the R. Hacienda livestock

author: José Luján
summary: *Sobre un despacho* — did not report his arrival owing to his wife's illness; asks the *cúmplase* be placed on his despacho [commission]

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre tejeduría* — asked Cpl Pizorena to report what weaver Mariano Mendoza has taught the San Juan [Capistrano] Indians — whether they can work alone on full-size blankets, half-shirts & cotton processing; the matter is building a loom; only a carpenter Gutiérrez is at hand, & the retired carpenter Lorenzo Esparza is no use (his trade is carving)

author: Pedro Pizorena
summary: *Sobre Indios tejedores* — gathered discreetly from master-weaver Mariano Mendoza that he made a plain loom & combs for wide weaving & wove blankets, half-size *cameras*, vaquero sleeves, 30 varas of fine wide cloth & 30 varas of baize in 4 days with 5–6 Indians; the Indians are apt & weave alone shepherd's blankets, *cordoncillo* & *jerga* of better quality than before; the Indian women spin & card wool & cotton well & make dyes (imperfect for want of materials but serviceable)

author: Grajera
summary: *Espendio ilícito de aguardiente / Libertad pa el aguardiente* (2 ofs) — received Branciforte's bando on the fraudulent introduction & sale of cane-*aguardiente* made before the ban ended; & the bandos lifting its manufacture & sale plus the Reglamento for this branch

author: Grajera
themes: san-diego; muster; armament; militia; lujan; carpentry
summary: *Alta de un Alfz / descuento / retención / revista de armas / economía / abono al soldº carpintero / remisión de balas* (7 ofs) — enrolls Alférez José Luján from the 17th, with pay-deductions for the halberdier-vs-current-grade difference & to recover the 115 p advanced him; weekly inspection of arms & munitions; will economize stores if the '98 Memorias fail; pays carpenter Salvador Vejar 28 p/month to keep the launch & carts in repair (+2 apprentices); Santa Bárbara to send 6,000 balls for the Ripoll muskets & others for invalids & citizens

author: Grajera
summary: *Alta de un soldº* — at next muster will enroll Salvador Vejar in the soldier class

author: Grajera
summary: *Deudas de un Presidio* — encloses a statement of this Habilitación's debts so B. may endorse the *libranzas* to the creditor missions. Accounts at end of Dec. 1796 — *Active (owed to SD):* Monterey 929 p 7 r 1¾, Loreto 998 p 5 r 11½, Sta Bárbara 2,761 p 5 r 1⅞; *Passive (SD owes):* Mission San Diego 3,534 p 4 r, San Juan Capistrano 4,785 p 0 r 9½, San Gabriel 2,881 p 0 r 1¾

author: Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — forwards triplicate of the Memorias & Regalado[?] of the 1798 Situado

author: Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — received the bando of the General Indulto on occasion of the peace with France [Peace of Basel, 1795]

author: Grajera
themes: san-diego; bulas; fiscal; foreign-ships; lasuen; navy
summary: *Permuta / Bulas & Cuentas de R. Hac. / Avisos de buques / Nombramiento de Vicario / dependientes de Marina* (5 ofs) — Invalid cédula for Francisco García (to enter at Sta Bárbara); per Branciforte the Monterey Habilitado administers the Ramo de Bulas & R. Hac. accounts for all presidios, Grajera rendering his to treasurer José María Bucheli; prompt warnings of ships to prevent landings; the Bishop of Sonora named Fr. Fermín Francisco de Lasuén his vicario foráneo; naval dependents arriving at these ports to be under the Viceroy's orders

author: Grajera
summary: *Demora de documentos* — when he received the Volunteers' lodging account Alférez Pablo Grijalva had already left, & lacking his signature he has not closed it

author: Grajera
summary: *Declaración de Guerra / Precauciones contra Hostilidades* (2 ofs) — noted the Declaration of War against the English; should they attack he will keep the battery in best state, reinforce the garrison, maintain continuous coastal patrols (descubiertas), & train the volunteer & leather-jacket artillerymen in the cannon & their arms, per B.'s order of the 13th

author: Grajera
summary: *Detención de buques* — will comply with the order to detain & seize English ships

author: Grajera
summary: *Prevenciones contra hostilidades* — explains to the 1st artillery corporal the standing orders for the Punta de Guijarros battery: the corporal & 4 of his piquete (5th as cook) alternate the guard with the Volunteers every 24 h, keeping a live sentinel at the battery day & night watching the harbour-mouth (bocana del Estrecho); on sighting a sail, leather-jacket soldier rides with the report; if a ship nears at dawn without answering the signal, raise the flag & fire a cannon-shot; weekly drills; had 4 leather cases made to keep cannon-cartridges dry in the battery

author: Grajera
summary: *Entrega de armas* — ordered the San Miguel corporal to hand Sgt Ruiz the small cannon (cañoncito) there & the muskets belonging to Loreto; a party of Frontera troops came with other shotguns, all was put in order promptly & they returned *(tied to the Cabo San Lucas alarm)*

author: Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — noted the Viceroy's measures on smallpox (viruela)

author: Grajera
summary: *Cta de R. Hac. / Correos / Relación de artillería / Lista de crédito / Cta de ganado* (5 ofs) — year-end accounts: summary of the Habilitación's R. Hac. credits & debits; Correos account; relation of the artillery & ordnance at Punta de Guijarros; list of the company individuals' credits/debits in today's ajustes; & the R. Hac. livestock account for the closing year

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre armar inváls y vecinos* — per his office of 5 Apr., the frigate Concepción was to bring 200 short 16-cal muskets, 200 covers & cartridge-boxes & 16,000 gunflints to arm ¼ of the Invalids & citizens of each presidio's jurisdiction; poor citizens not to receive arms until invasion (collected back after); invalids & citizens to be supplied cartridges with a good reserve at the escoltas; monthly diaries to specify the number & calibre of arms & cartridges held & those spent in target practice

author: Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — the courier arrived yesterday; a San Vicente soldier brought the enclosed, which the San Vicente men had lost, being the last signature from Santo Domingo [Baja missions]

author: Grajera
summary: *(brief)* — encloses the relation of the single artillery piece existing in this jurisdiction

author: Pedro Álvarez
summary: *Petición de licencia* — asks discharge from the SD company for a fistula; *(margin)* Borica asks Grajera to report; Pizorena (4 Oct.) reports the petitioner is unfit for all service & has been ill as stated, wishes discharge partly to tend ~10 pack-mules inherited by his widowed daughter, & his term is complete

author: Juan José Herrera
summary: *Retiro ó licenciamiento* — asks discharge as a *soldado de cuera* of SD for a chest illness contracted in service; Pizorena (margin, 4 Oct.) reports his term complete, one arm somewhat disabled from riding, no surgeon to examine him; no objection to discharge

author: Grajera
summary: *Sobre el comandante de una escolta* — will provide Fr. Fermín Lasuén the corporal or soldier he chooses to command the 5 men who are to escort the founding of Mission San Luis Rey de Francia *(founded June 1798)*

author: Grajera
themes: san-diego; viceroy; azanza; lasuen; foreign-ships; supply
summary: *Nombramiento de Virey / Concesión eclesiástica / Provisiones a una fragata / Mermas de ciertos efectos* (4 ofs) — noted Azanza is the new Viceroy; Bishop Francisco Rouset's concession to Lasuén & friars administering Sacraments to the military; requested 500 arrobas of flour from Mission San Diego for the frigate Princesa; sends (signed by 1st pilot Salvador Menéndez) the document justifying the shortfalls (mermas) of the San Blas goods received in 1793

author: Grajera
summary: *Envío de un correo* — dispatches this express at the request of Don García Zerpa, commander of the frigate Princesa

author: Grajera
summary: *Permuta de solds / Adopción de un niño* (2 ofs) — sent the Santa Bárbara soldier detached here back to his presidio & awaits leave to recall the one of this company at the Frontera; the 1st pilot Salvador Menéndez has contracted with soldier Claudio López & his wife to take their 7–8-year-old son, offering to educate him, relieving the parents; await resolution

author: Grajera
summary: *Envío de un parte* — encloses the San Miguel corporal's report on a thunder-like cannon-shot heard at Santo Tomás

author: Francisco Acevedo
summary: *Sobre un trueno* — a soldier from Santo Tomás brought Sgt Ruiz's letter reporting that on the 11th at 5 a.m. a shot-like noise was heard, which several affirm seemed a cannon-shot, though no vessel has been sighted *(closing war-alarm of the volume)*

author: Various
summary: SJ Pueblo disorders — arson (verbatim fire certification), vagabundos, robberies at San José pueblo

author: Carrillo
summary: Sal death notice — Hermenegildo Sal died Dec 8, 1800 at Monterey

author: Carrillo
summary: Carrillo recalled, found Sal 'expirando' (dying)

author: Arrillaga
summary: Estate settlement ordered — Moraga, Estudillo, Roca named executors

author: Surgeon Morelos
summary: Cause of death: TUBERCULOSIS — certified by surgeon Juan de Dios Morelos with quarantine instructions
Loom shut down days before Sal's death

author: Matute
summary: Matute 1792 artisan passenger list — complete roster of artisans on Concepción
Funeral at Mission Carmel — Lasuén presiding
Spain-Russia war acknowledged at Santa Bárbara

author: Argüello
summary: SF Presidio decay — hurricane damage, rotting buildings, no repair materials
American frigate *La Elisa* provisioned at SF

author: Various
summary: Bear plague at Monterey — 49 bears killed, 1,000 cartuchos requested
SB garrison census — 189 total population
LA wheat census — complete vecino list, ~2,270 fanegas capacity

author: Carrillo
summary: Carrillo education/artisan proposal — children know only 'el caballo y el campo,' requests schoolmasters

author: Alberni
summary: San Miguel Poisoning — see highlight above

author: Pacheco / Amador
summary: Gijigües engagement — see highlight above

author: Alberni
summary: 4 foreign ships sighted near San Antonio
San Vicente inventory (Ruiz)

author: Marquina
summary: Infanticide sentencing — María Antonia, 10 yrs recogidas

author: Marquina
summary: Charges against Carrillo & Argüello (Puente de San Jorge)
Tribunal de Cuentas — libranza payments (damaged)

author: Marquina
summary: Naval supplies from Veracruz, $2,904

author: Marquina
summary: Ship at Monterey + foreign ships vigilance order (N coast, Cabo San Lucas)

author: Marquina
summary: Earthquakes at San Buenaventura — strong tremors Oct 11–31
Goleta *Ntra. Sra. del Carmen* to islands with flour

author: Marquina
summary: Padre Gallegos returns to Convento

author: Marquina
summary: Menorca surrender punishments — Consejo de Guerra

author: Marquina
summary: Wheat supply for San José & Branciforte — fiscal opinion on San Blas contract, 1,000 fanegas at 20r each; contractor Juan Bautista Cirneta

author: Marquina
summary: SF Presidio repair funding — $252.5r for church, comandancia, 3 families

author: Marquina
summary: Volunteer/artillery/pobladores 1-year advance pay

author: Marquina
summary: Anglo-American ships warning — continued caution ordered

author: Ladrón de Guevara
summary: Presidio financial accounting — ships *Concepción*, *Aranzazu*, *Saturnina*, *Activo*, *Horcasitas*, *Sutil* (Capt. Salvador Hidalgo)

author: Ladrón de Guevara
summary: Sal death — official response. Accounts settled. Argüello's bid to succeed Sal DENIED — Raimundo Carrillo already appointed

author: Fiscal de lo Civil
summary: California–New Mexico communication project — fiscal opinion on Colorado River road/bridge; Fages, Borica, Arrillaga positions; 500+ leagues Baja to Monterey; connects to Nava doc (pp. 35–38)

author: Marquina
summary: California hemp industry — 25 arrobas at San Blas, Condestable Sánchez transferred

author: Marquina
summary: 3 replacement padres (Ibáñez, Vargas, Labrada) from Colegio San Fernando

author: Marquina
summary: Royal decree: workers must wear decent clothing (Cédula Dec 13, 1799)

author: Marquina
summary: Mail bergantín lost — balandra *Mercedes* substituted; *Horcasitas* investigated

author: Marquina
summary: Death of Alférez Pablo Antonio Cota (Santa Bárbara), Dec 30 1800

author: Marquina
summary: English warships — 1 navío + 2 frigates rounded Cape Horn. Troops readied to repel aggression

author: Marquina
summary: San Blas ships failing to reach SF — captains make excuses

author: Marquina
summary: Alférez for SB: M. Ruiz promoted; Gabriel Moraga passed over ('menos aptitud, más servicios')

author: Marquina
summary: Monterey Presidio ruins survey ordered — church roof threatening collapse

author: Marquina
summary: Branciforte budget: $22,429 (Engineer Córdova). Families on *Concepción*

author: Marquina
summary: Sargento Pedro Amador (SF) granted retirement

author: Marquina
summary: San Juan Capistrano storehouse burned Mar 8 — grave losses

author: Arrillaga
summary: Ship recognition signal system — 2 cannon shots + 1-min interval, answered by presidio

author: Arrillaga
summary: English warships warning relayed to all comandantes

author: Marquina
summary: Caballero named Secretary of War and Navy (replacing Cornel)

author: Marquina
summary: Ex-Jesuit tracking order — list all members of extinguished Compañía de Jesús

author: Marquina
summary: 3 ships sighted Monterey–SB channel, heading north

author: Marquina
summary: Punta de Guijarros fort expenses sent to Tribunal

author: Marquina
summary: Trinidad Island surrender sentence — communicated by King

author: Marquina
summary: JESUIT ARREST ORDER — 'Muy reservado' — midnight arrest of all Jesuits returned from Italy; transport to Veracruz

author: Marquina
summary: Criminal jurisdiction ruling — Gov + comandantes have jurisdiction over ALL settlers; case mentions Comandante Sal

author: Pérez de Tagle / Crown
summary: Manila colonization proposal — see highlight above

author: Marquina
summary: Archive conservation order — papers could provide enemies intelligence; 1741 Cédula on census

author: Marquina
summary: Fuero de guerra denied to transient foreigners in the Indies

author: Marquina
summary: Loreto presidio credits — $9,000 to San Blas ships at Puerto Escondido

author: Marquina
summary: Suspicious ship Cabo San Lucas + suspicious ships in Alta California — strict vigilance

author: Marquina
summary: Governor delegated missionary retirement authority (fiscal Borbón)
Courier expenses for Nootka news: $880 — Nootka still generating emergency communications

author: Marquina
summary: Interim Habilitado needed (Columna reassigned to murder case)

author: Marquina / Fiscal
summary: Balandra *Horcasitas* — $3,124 value + $1,500 repairs; *Mercedes* alternative

author: Marquina
summary: 23 Dominican missionaries refuse service — deemed 'muy desagradable,' no replacements from Spain due to war

author: Marquina
summary: Military budget 1802 — see highlight above

author: Marquina
summary: Habilitado succession plan — Branciforte's 1797 decree

author: Marquina
summary: Convicted killer sent as poblador — Manuel Ramírez (killed Gregorio Vela), sentence commuted, sent to Monterey with wife/son

author: Marquina / Cañizares
summary: Hemp tools shipped to Monterey on *Concepción* — complete inventory, $386 total

author: Marquina

author: Arrillaga

author: Jacobo Velarde (Alcalde), Regidores, Ignacio Vallejo (Encargado)

author: Joaquín Sánchez (San José)
summary: Hemp harvest failed — fog and winds destroyed most seeds; farmers discontented because previous year's (1804) harvest also burned

author: Joaquín Sánchez
summary: ★ Flax vs. hemp economics — flax harvested same way as hemp; in Spain flax costs 4 rs/lb, hemp 10 rs/lb because used for rope/sacking; hemp processing (washing, drying, combing, spinning) produces large waste (tow/estopa) and raises costs; two-thirds of cost far from profit; nobody will undertake it; hemp farmers can cover costs themselves without Royal Treasury subsidy

author: Raimundo Carrillo
summary: ★ DAILY DIARY — FEBRUARY 1802 EPIDEMIC at Monterey/Carmel/Soledad. Day-by-day log: Day 1 transferred command; Day 3 three soldiers sick with pleurisy; Day 5 Mission Soledad Indians fled — pages, cooks, shepherds abandoned mission out of fear of epidemic; Day 6 son of Moraga died; Day 8 Carmel: 7 sick at dawn, 1 Indian dead; Day 10 wife of Sargento Moraga died + invalid Montaña died + gentile cowboy baptized dying; Day 11 Soledad: 5–6 more sick, padres baptizing dying gentiles; Day 12 Indian died; Day 15 Indian man + woman died; Day 16 wife of [Moraga?] died, buried at Mission; Days 17–18 6 Indians died; Day 20 Indian woman + child died; Day 21 two Indians died; Day 25 Indian woman + small child died; Day 28 news from Soledad: 5 Christians and 3 gentiles murdered + 3 more at nearby ranchería (massacre, not epidemic). 12+ epidemic deaths + 8 murdered in single month

author: Carrillo

Roca; Suárez refuses (too busy with Alberni estate); Carrillo orders compliance without excuses; Suárez says it's the Ayudante de Plaza's job; Carrillo compels again; case forwarded to Argüello who also refuses (claims he can't mount a horse due to injury). Multiple letters, complete institutional dysfunction

author: José Manuel Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: ★ MURDER OF PADRE SURECA AT SANTO TOMÁS — deportation of accused Indians. By royal order, will embark the guilty: Lázaro, Alejandro, Bárcena, and Juan Miguel (husband of the Indian woman whose testimony alone condemns him). Murdered Padre Fr. Eduardo Sureca at Mission Santo Tomás [Baja California]. Ruiz resolved to ship them all together; 'if there was error, it is easy to return them.' [continues]

author: José Manuel Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: [cont.] Murder of Padre Sureca — boy Juan de Dios told Indian Bárbara that Lázaro and Juan Miguel killed the padre; Lázaro entered padre's room at night, took key, opened door, brought Juan Miguel; also brought Mariano; the three killed the padre; Lázaro re-entered and buried body in the field. Ruiz decides to ship Lázaro + Juan Miguel; Mariano stays; notes Bárbara has 'diabolical spirit'; Indian Encarnación knows the truth. pp. 557–8

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Of 4 Indians from previous letter, only shipping Lázaro and Alejandro (guilt proven); will send others in time. pp. 554–5

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: ★ SANTO TOMÁS UPRISING — Indian uprising at Santa Tomás, not serious; promoters punished by cabo; at the sierra people gather reportedly to avenge deaths of the Tomaseños; a fight among them and two died. pp. 570–2

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Padre Rivas finally won't go to San Diego; will stay at Santa Tomás as Minister; Padre López very ill. pp. 573–5

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: No news on the frontiers nor other things of any importance. pp. 576–7

author: Fr. Juan Rivas (Rosario)
summary: Due to illness, his trip to Santa Tomás is delayed. p. 580

author: Fr. Antonio Lázaro (San Fernando)
summary: Without help from the troops he cannot manage the neophytes' affairs. p. 581

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Sends diligencias (investigations) on death of Padre Miguel López. p. 582

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Received the circular sent Nov 8. p. 583

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Has not complied with order about Fr. José Faulas transferring from Mission San Pedro Mártir to Santa Tomás; Fr. Juan Rivas was already serving there. pp. 584–6

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: ★ DEATH OF INDIAN PRISONER — Indian Juan Miguel Camilo died on the 10th in the calabozo; imprisoned for complicity in poisoning the Padres of Santa Tomás. p. 587

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Tranquility on frontiers — arrival of goleta *Saturnina*; delivered the arraez; peace continues. pp. 588–90

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Delivered letter sent for Fr. Ramón López, who is in serious illness; and for other motives. pp. 593–4

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Frontier occurrences — Indian wanted to kill Padre of Santo Domingo; investigations sent; two soldiers imprisoned for fornication. pp. 595–8

author: Ruiz
summary: Regrets not following investigations on Santo Tomás events; pressure/rush was the cause. pp. 599–600

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Remain at Mission Santa Tomás as Minister; for service of God and King. p. 601. (At foot: Rivas's acquiescence, under Ruiz's responsibility)

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: New declarations from prisoners Juan Miguel Rodríguez and Melchor Gutiérrez. pp. 602–3

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Fr. Rivas finally staying as Minister at Santa Tomás, spiritual functions only. p. 604

author: José Font (Monterey)
summary: ★ DEATH OF FR. FERMÍN LASUÉN — Father President died at 3pm, June 26; buried next day with full ceremony, accompanied by 6 religious. pp. 605–6

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: State of frontiers — no news; general flight of natives from Mission Santo Tomás caused no harm; mass not celebrated there; Indian revolutions since May 17; thinks a missionary will never be sent to Santa Tomás again. pp. 607–8

author: José Font (Monterey)
summary: Order to detain the soldier conducting the mail from the North by overland route (cordillera). pp. 609–12

author: Manuel Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: ★ LÓPEZ DE HARO SURVEY — commissioned Alférez Graduado de Fragata Gonzalo López de Haro from San Blas under the command of balandra *Horcasitas* to survey and map the California coast from San Diego to Santa Lucía and the gulf. pp. 613–14

author: Isaac Stoughton, Spanish Consul, Boston
summary: ★ AMERICAN FRIGATE *ALEXANDER* — PASSPORT. Passport for American frigate *Alexander*, Capt. Juan Brown (John Brown), property of Henrique Bassy & Co. and José Taylor of Boston; requests assistance if vessel enters Spanish dominions. (Copy recd. by Arrillaga May 8, 1803). pp. 615–16

author: Manuel Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: ★ FRIGATE *ALEXANDER* AT SAN DIEGO — arrived night of Feb 26, 1803; from Boston Jul 19, 1802; 110 tons, 19 crew + Capt. Brown + 2 pilots; scurvy + lacking provisions; supplied 40 barrels water, beef, salt; sick disembarked far from port; tried to trade furs; Sargento sent with 6 soldiers; despite precautions, large quantity of furs smuggled aboard night of the 3rd; searched vessel and confiscated 491 fur pieces; ordered to set sail immediately without the 8-day stay requested; left Todos Santos on the 7th at night, went ashore with pretext of needing firewood. pp. 617–19

author: Argüello (S. Francisco)
summary: ★ SF BAY LIVESTOCK & AGRICULTURE REPORT — Viceroy's resolution on provincial tariffs; livestock increasing annually, especially at Missions Santa Clara and San Francisco; estimates 8,000 head of cattle (cabezas de vacuno) and similar numbers of other stock; 4 missions harvest wheat, barley, chickpeas, corn, beans, peas, except SF due to water scarcity; abundance of grains and animal products; butter at 2 pesos/arroba, flour at 12 pesos. pp. 620–22

author: Noriega (Monterey)
summary: ★ MONTEREY PRICE TABLE — detailed price list for goods and livestock: wheat, corn, beans, common flour, butter (vaca/cerdo), sheep 2+ years, horses (tame/untrained), mares, colts, mules (tame/young), with prices at Monterey and comparison to other jurisdictions. p. 623

author: Argüello (S. Francisco)
summary: ★ POLICY: NO OPEN PORTS TO AMERICANS — sends correspondence about frigate *Alexander* incident; advises that ports should NOT be opened to American ships unless in evident danger of loss. pp. 624–5

author: Font (Monterey)
summary: Frigate *Alexander* at Monterey — anchored to restock provisions, make topmast + sail; facilitated by Capt. of *Princesa* Braulio de Olavea; ordered to set sail immediately that night; left without paying expenses. pp. 626–7

author: Argüello (S. Francisco)
summary: Received report on frigate *Alexander*; will do everything possible to apprehend Capt. Juan Brown if he appears at San Francisco. p. 628

author: Fr. Giménez (Sta. Clara)
summary: [cont. from deep read pp. 253–277] Mission policy on neophytes — Santa Bárbara Channel experience: gentiles voluntarily became Christians; threat of expulsion for refusers; many from Channel rancherías settled freely at missions, quiet and happy. Signed Sta. Clara. pp. 664–71

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Forwards Royal Order of Jul 21, 1802 — Consulado de Veracruz to be provided all news about agriculture, industry, and territories of the kingdom. p. 673

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: ★ BATTERY OF PUNTA DE GUIJARROS — orders payment of 180 pesos 7¼ reales from San Diego funds for 1801 renovation of the battery. p. 643

author: Royal Order (Aranjuez)
summary: Royal Order — King directs that all agricultural and industrial news from kingdom's territories be provided to the Consulado de Veracruz. pp. 674–5

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Retired sargentos' salaries — resolved that Sargento Macario de Castro and all retired in his case receive salary per Rule 5, Royal Order of Aug 23, 1796 (half of active-duty pay). pp. 676–7

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Habilitado substitution — forwards order that Capt. Felipe de Goicoechea be given the habilitación of his post during his absence in Spain; leave granted. pp. 678–9

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Royal Treasury funds — all funds received by habilitados from Real Hacienda to be administered through presidio situados and company responsibility to prevent loss. p. 680

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Retirement to invalids — Padrón de los Barquitos de Loreto, Pérez, retires with invalid status to his post. p. 681

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: ★ MERCURY (AZOGUE) SUPPLY TO BAJA CALIFORNIA — writes Intendente de Guadalajara; only sure method is appointing revenue administrators; Tribunal de Cuentas proposes buying gold/silver at 10 pesos/onza (gold) and 7/mark (silver); resolved that mercury be sent from capital to California on the supply ship every 1–2 years, consigned to Habilitado as formerly from Tesorería de los Álamos. pp. 682–4

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Acknowledges receipt of estado (report) in triplicate sent in July. p. 685

author: Arrillaga (Loreto)
summary: ★ FOREIGN SHIP EXCLUSION ORDER — forwards Viceroy's 1796 order on preventing foreign ships from approaching coasts; cites Frigate *Alexander* entries as cause; orders: do NOT permit arrival, do NOT provide assistance unless ship in distress. p. 686. (Copy: José de la Guerra, Monterey, Dec 2, 1803)

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: ★ FOUNDING NEW MISSION AT LAJALUPE — resolved to found a mission at Paraje de Calahuas, site called Lajalupe; orders Ministers of Cajas of the capital to give síndico of San Fernando $1,000 per Reglamento; Guardian to send necessary religious. p. 687

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Mission escorts restored — in view of complaints from Guardian of San Fernando about prior Governor's reduction of mission escorts; resolved to equip missions with the force they need, using troops recently sent to the Province. pp. 688–9

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Hemp prices — hemp from California worth only 3 pesos/quintal due to imperfect processing; orders it paid at $3¼ to stimulate farmers; henceforth observe order of Sept 3, 1801; also orders new tool shipment. pp. 690–2

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Grants permission to Padres Antonio Sánchez and Miguel Gallegos to retire to Antigua California as requested. p. 693

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: ★ PRESIDIO REPAIR ORDER — resolved to proceed with execution of construction works per Fiscal de Real Hacienda's directive; harm would follow if Peninsula buildings not repaired. p. 694

author: Fiscal Borbón (Mexico)
summary: Dictamen on repair of works at the Presidios of California. pp. 695–7

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Marriage of Prince of Asturias — Royal Order granting indulto (pardon) to officers who married without consent. pp. 698–700

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Freight charges — warns that freight should not be collected from individuals/goods on supply ships, per superior orders. pp. 701–5

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Sends copies of bando with Royal Cédula on Prince of Asturias's marriage. p. 706

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Soldier rewards — Royal Order granting awards to all with 5+ years service, per petition of Melchor Montes (Regt. Infantry of Guadalajara). pp. 707–9

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Royal pardon (indulto) — bando with royal indulto for prisoners covered by same proclamation; includes deserters. p. 710

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Officers' salaries — Montepío pension deduction to be taken from remainder after food/ration allowance, not full salary. p. 711

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Grants Alférez of Compañía de Caballería del Presidio de Loreto the salary of Abanderado de la Guardia. pp. 713–14

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Alberni's accounts — informed of settling accounts of Coronel Alberni; funds insufficient to cover them all. pp. 715–16

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: ★ MILITARY APPOINTMENTS — José Font promoted to Captain and Simón Suárez to Teniente of the Compañía de Voluntarios de Cataluña. pp. 717–18

author: Real Tribunal de Cuentas / Fiscal Borbón / Iturrigaray
summary: ★ HABILITADO AUDIT — SAN DIEGO & MONTEREY — Real Tribunal de Cuentas + Fiscal report on habilitados' failure to account for supplies delivered by bergantín *Activo* (1798). SD habilitado never reported; excused with 'rare pretext'; supplies from 1798 valued at 215,000 reales ¾ mark of silver. Monterey habilitado's response also unsatisfactory (claims 6 arrobas wax + cedar box never arrived). Fiscal demands formal accounts of ALL supplies received from ships; orders interim Gov. of California to enforce; certified copies required; both habilitados charged; Marina de San Blas notified. Signed Monteverde, Lacunza, Arnaiz (Contaduría Mayor), Apr 4, 1803; Fiscal Borbón Apr 20; Iturrigaray Jun 12. pp. 719–26

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Mission reports overdue — warns to send estados de misiones regularly; not done despite repeated orders. p. 727

author: Ladrón de Guevara (Mexico)
summary: Orders assessment/notes on memorias (supplies) be sent promptly. p. 728

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Acquittal — Real Sala del Crimen declares D. Toribio del Mazo (Teniente, Batallón Voluntarios Blancos de Mérida) free of charge of assassination of D. Lucas Gálvez, Gov. of Yucatán. pp. 729–31

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Orders accounts of Presidios of Monterey and Loreto for 1802 sent; Real Tribunal demands them. p. 732

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Indulto for officers married without royal permission — Royal Order of Oct 5, 1802; occasion of Prince of Asturias's betrothal. pp. 733–34

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Bando with indulto for deserters of the Real Armada. p. 735

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Officers' salary — Royal Order that Army officers paid and take possession upon Gazeta publication of appointment, wartime only. p. 736

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Royal Cédula — fuero de guerra (military jurisdiction) granted to foreigners/transients has no place for Indians, per stipulation in treaties. pp. 737–8

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Orders invoices/certifications for Memorias de Loreto deliveries to resolve expediente on shortages in 1798–99 shipments. p. 739

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Acknowledges arrival at Loreto of goletas 'San Joaquín' and 'Santa Anna'. p. 740

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Re: Frigate *Alexander* — acknowledges report on entries in Peninsula ports/ensenadas; reiterates order against foreign ships approaching coasts. p. 741

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Sobre Reyes de Armas — reminds prohibition that only Reyes de Armas and supernumeraries may perform heraldic functions. p. 742

author: Antonio Porcel (Madrid)
summary: King renews prohibition — only Reyes de Armas may make genealogical certifications and coats of arms. p. 743

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Received notes of supplies requested by Comandantes of the 4 Presidios of California to clothe the troops. p. 744

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Royal Order — Bishop of Popayán failed to visit Governor of Antioquia; directs observance of Law 51, Tit. 3, Book 3 of Municipales. pp. 745–6

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Phlebotomists — Real Tribunal del Proto Medicato orders that public professors of phlebotomy not be imprisoned; examined ones to be received by departmental delegates. pp. 747–8

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Phlebotomist examination fees — media anata of 4 pesos 6½ reales plus 2 pesos regulation; regulated by Arancel de gracias. pp. 749–50

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Sends Soler's representation requesting return of 2% habilitados charge for direction/receipt of memorias. p. 751

author: Soler (S. Blas)
summary: Requests reimbursement of 2% deducted by habilitados Argüello, Coy, and Cal from his salary for freight of memorias. pp. 752–57

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: ★ FRIGATE *LELIA BIRD* AT SAN FRANCISCO — informed of what happened with frigate *Lelia Bird* of Portsmouth upon arrival at port of San Francisco; no punishment resulted; directs sovereign dispositions about not permitting foreign ships on Spanish coasts be kept in mind. pp. 758–9

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Re: Frigate *Alexander* — received letter of Jun 21 about the incident. p. 760

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Mail fraud — resolves to label mail covers 'De oficio y Criminales' to prevent future fraud incidents involving soldier Francisco Antonio (a) Chiembone of Milicias de la Costa del Sur. pp. 761–3

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: ★ MEZCAL PROHIBITION DEBATE — Gov. Diego de Borica (1798) requested ban on aguardiente mezcal imports via San Blas ships due to harm and scandals in new establishments; antecedents of prohibition not found; since then ships permitted to carry goods freely including aguardiente de caña (cane spirit); asks Gov. to report whether cane spirit causes same problems as mezcal. pp. 764–5

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Informed that balandra *Horcasitas* anchored at San Diego on Jun 16. p. 766

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: ★ *ALEXANDER* ESCALATED TO WASHINGTON — letters about frigate *Alexander* leaving San Francisco forwarded to the Ministro Plenipotenciario de España en los Estados Unidos for appropriate consequences. p. 767

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Military service awards — Royal Order on premios de constancia; paid from one year after completing designated time. p. 768

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Invalid status policy — Royal Order: no one proposed for invalid status without 18 years service unless disabled in action of war/service after 8 years. p. 769

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Postage rules — certified copy of decree on when postage must be paid for packages with remunerations/solicitations. pp. 771–2

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Invalid certificates granted to Bartolomé Pacheco, José Claudino Villavicencio, and Clemente Navarro of Companies of Monterey, San Francisco, and Santa Bárbara. pp. 773–4

author: José María Ruiz (S. Francisco)
summary: Received absence lists of soldiers Juan Espinosa and Nepomuceno Osio. pp. 775–6

author: Arrillaga (Loreto)
summary: Forwards Tte. Inválido Macario de Castro's request for better accommodation to the Viceroy. p. 781

author: Arrillaga (S. Javier)
summary: ★ HEMP CULTIVATION SKEPTICISM — didn't request tools, superioridad sent them voluntarily; 'I have my suspicions hemp won't prosper much, because they lack conviction of its utility, the natural laziness (*desidia*) of the residents, and especially because they lack the multitude of gentiles they formerly had in their service.' Orders Comisionado to verify shipment; presses for results as Superioridad watches this branch with interest. pp. 783–4, 789–90

author: Arrillaga (S. Javier)
summary: Hemp cultivation orders — Comandante of Monterey to report on hemp industry progress; if Gov. were in Monterey all would be easier; Sánchez to cooperate with Comandante and press for results. pp. 785–8

author: Arrillaga (Loreto) [borrador]
summary: ★ ARRILLAGA MISSION REPORT (cont. from deep read pp. 337–340) — ~17 pages total. FOOD: proportional to abundance; some neophytes 'dispatched' to forage. CLOTHING: 'el gentil siempre anda desnudo'; cotones/blankets sufficient; looms since *Concepción*. HOUSING: prefers chozas for health; infirmería/Casa de Solteras reformed. LABOR: work 'sumamente flojo'; women more docile; denies forced labor of pregnant women. PUNISHMENT: stocks, shackles, dark rooms, calabozos exist; 'the common punishment is azotes y grillos'; 'in my estimation the Padres lack more indulgence than rigor.' MISSIONARY SALES: few excesses; defends missionaries' right to wine/aguardiente — 'they do well to have it in such unhappy destinations... scolding a rational species of second class.' MISSION FINANCES: libranzas for 18 Alta Cal. missions total 12–18,000 pesos/year. DEFENSE OF SYSTEM: punishment 'has its origin in the very conquest of Antigua California, practiced for more than 100 years'; 'varying it could have fatal consequences.' PRESIDENTIAL AUTHORITY: witnessed 5 consecutive President appointments in Baja Cal.; missionaries shown cuaderno of 10–16 pages of spiritual faculties. Signed: 'I consider I have been somewhat diffuse and tiresome.' pp. 835–41

author: Argüello (S. Francisco)
summary: Complains about raised prices of goods sent from San Blas; asks for providences to stop the harm. pp. 842–3

author: Argüello (S. Francisco)
summary: Includes certification by Contador Pedro Campos — weight of goods from San Blas doesn't conform with nota de efectos and prices. pp. 844–5

author: Argüello (S. Francisco)
summary: ★ PERALTA PURSUES GENTILE ASSASSINS — Sargento Luis Peralta with free troop sent to Sierra de San José to pursue gentile assassins of Christians; managed to catch them despite many days of pursuit. pp. 846–8

author: Argüello (S. Francisco)
summary: Instructions for second pursuit of gentiles — indispensable condition: bring good guides (cf. leaf 334: failed for lack of good guides). pp. 851–2

author: Pedro Campos, Hab. del Bergantín *Activo* (S. Francisco)
summary: Certifies soap from San Blas didn't meet weight specified in nota — claims 8 arrobas/box but only 4½. p. 853

author: Cabo Mariano Fernández (S. Joaquín)
summary: ★ ARTILLERY INVENTORY AT FORT SAN JOAQUÍN — inventory of artillery and war supplies at that fort. pp. 864–86

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: Has prisoner Gil Horra for wounding an invalid with a belduque (knife). pp. 857–8

author: Raymundo Carrillo, Tte. de Caballería, Cmdte. Sta. Bárbara
summary: ★ KNIFE PROHIBITION AT PUEBLO DE LOS ÁNGELES — prohibits carrying belduque in boot/waist within the pueblo; repeated stabbings in quarrels; 'prohibited in all places of the kingdom with much rigor'; persons of *color quebrado* (mixed race) publicly whipped; orders Alcalde of Pueblo de la Reina de Los Ángeles to enforce; knives only permitted going to the field for work. p. 859

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: Sends company review lists (listas de revista). p. 860

author: Argüello (S. Francisco)
summary: ★ SF PRESIDIO IN 'LAMENTABLE STATE OF DETERIORATION' — reports repairs needed in general; works in lamentable estado de deterioro. pp. 861–63

author: Argüello (S. Francisco)
summary: ★ PERALTA'S 2ND EXPEDITION — Sargento Luis Peralta failed to catch gentile murderers but captured 11 Christian fugitives from Missions San José and San Francisco; returned women/children to Padres; arrived at Presidio with 32 gandules (able-bodied non-mission Indians). pp. 864–6

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: Sends Padrón documents for his jurisdiction. p. 867

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: Duplicate inventory of war supplies at Sta. Bárbara Presidio, 1803. p. 868

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: Tobacco account — net liquid $1,888.9 gr. p. 869

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: Sends weather/climate report (relación de temperamentos). p. 870

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: Sends gratification accounts. p. 871

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: Received Royal Order on processing invalid solicitations. p. 873

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: Received Real Tribunal expediente on accounts of Presidio of San Diego. p. 873

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: Couldn't deliver office sent to Francisco Reyes. p. 874

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: Soldier Montalbán permitted to complete 18 years service; assigned to Escolta de San Fernando. p. 875

author: Raymundo Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: ★ FOUNDING OF MISSION SANTA INÉS — formal act of taking possession of paraje called Lajalup (in native language) for founding Mission Santa Inés; cross raised and adored; chapel/ramada formed; first Mass celebrated and sung by Father President Fr. Estevan Tapis and Fr. Marcelino Ciprés; founders Fr. José Antonio Calzada and Fr. Romualdo Gutiérrez; solemn Te Deum sung. Copy certified by Arrillaga, Loreto, Nov 22, 1804. p. 876

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Livestock tithe — received 240 yearlings + 60 heifers from Sta. Bárbara tithe; too skinny to sell; SD rancho of Real Hacienda has 1,800 head but bad pastures; has had to get livestock from Missions San Juan Capistrano and San Gabriel for better pastures. pp. 877–80

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: ★ FRIGATE *HAZARD* REFUSED SUPPLIES — Cabo of San Juan Capistrano reported American frigate *Hazard* (400 tons, Capt. James Rowan, 22 cannons, 60 crew) entered anchorage; sent 4 soldiers with order to refuse supplies; Captain obliged to set sail. pp. 881–4

author: Argüello (S. Francisco)
summary: ★ BATTERY OF YERBA BUENA DESTROYED / USELESS — storm of Nov 26 destroyed palisade of Battery of Yerba Buena (fascine bundles with leather ties rotted, wind blew down); Fort San Joaquín in deplorable state; cannon wheels rotten; must maneuver with great caution. 'Experience has made known the uselessness of the Battery of Yerba Buena in the site where it is, because ships can enter, anchor, and leave without being harassed' — engineer D. Alberto de Córdova should have known; if moved, better on loma adjacent to Fondeadero; moving artillery requires supply ships, by land very difficult and costly. pp. 885–8

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Sends duplicate review extracts. p. 889

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Received Royal Cédula on invalid proposals. pp. 890–1

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Drowning — servant Javier Forba drowned crossing flooded arroyo at Mission San Miguel on horseback. pp. 892–4

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Account of supplies to Capt. Juan Brown (*Alexander*) — requests reimbursement. pp. 895–6

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Informed that Viceroy has communicated about what happened with the *Lelia Bird*. pp. 897–8

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Sends review extracts. p. 899

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Sends two libranzas for collection. p. 900

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Bulas of Santa Cruzada published by Mission padres. pp. 901–2

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Sends quintuplicate invalid reports. p. 903

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Cabo vacancy filled by Pablo de la Osa. p. 904

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: ★ BARGE CONSTRUCTION AT SAN DIEGO — Junta Superior approved $688.6½ for flat barge (barca plana), 26 feet, for Punta de Guijarros garrison; construction charged to Comandancia de San Blas. pp. 905–6

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Grain harvests — Missions San Gabriel, San Juan Capistrano, San Luis Rey regular; San Diego insufficient for its residents. pp. 907–9

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Sends mail service accounts and libranza. pp. 910–11

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Charged supplies provided to deserter of American frigate *Union*, Juan Brown. p. 912

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Discharge of soldier Ignacio Valencia. p. 913

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Rejects Alférez's pay claims as unjust. pp. 914–16

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: ★ ARRILLAGA RECOGNIZED AS GOVERNOR — King named him Gov. Military and Political of Alta California on Mar 26, 1804; Rodríguez has recognized him as such in pueblos of his jurisdiction. p. 917

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Absolute license granted to soldier Antonio Bedillo. p. 918

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: Widow Concepción requests to move to San Blas with family. pp. 919–21

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: Soldier Julián Lima used his license. p. 922

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: ★ LOS ÁNGELES OFFICIALS APPOINTED — approved appointments of Alcaldes and Regidores for Pueblo de Los Ángeles, using faculties granted. pp. 923–4

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: Baptism of gentile Schnichasí. pp. 925–7

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: Invalid certificate for Ignacio Lugo. p. 929

author: Carrillo (Sta. Bárbara)
summary: Tithe goods — ordered Monterey Comandante and Habilitado to receive and account. pp. 930–1

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Salary deductions for Presidio Captain D. José de Zúñiga. pp. 932–3

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: ★ SHIPS SAIL FOR SAN BLAS — Nov 21: fragata *Princesa* (Cmdr. Agustín Bocalán) and bergantín *Activo* (Piloto José Naranjo); embarking Baja California missionaries: Miguel Abad, Mariano Soldí, Apolinario Fernández, Tomás Valdelón, José Manuel Mortiarna, Pedro Adriano Martínez, Isidro Barcenilla. pp. 934–34½

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Requests barrels from Cmdr. Agustín Bocalán — existing pipas at Punta de Guijarros unserviceable. pp. 935–7

author: Argüello (S. Francisco)
summary: Frigate *Hazard* — couldn't comply with order to prevent departure; ship already sailed before order arrived. pp. 938–40

author: Ignacio Castro, Alcalde of Pueblo de San José
summary: ★ FORNICATION SCANDAL AT SAN JOSÉ — Antonio Sandoval accuses Potenciana Ramírez re: her daughter; Potenciana alleged *mala amistad* (sexual relationship) with D. José Estudillo and Da. Gertrudis Orcasitas with Fr. José Vinales (who had 3 children from her); Castro tried to investigate but Potenciana and Gertrudis insulted him; Comisionado Sargento Macario de Castro (Potenciana's husband) refused to cooperate; Castro brings witnesses (Regidor Manuel Butrón, Poblador Tiburcio Vásquez, vecino Antonio Sánchez) but Macario says he's not competent; Castro renounces the case and files civil/criminal complaint against the Comisionado. pp. 943–4

author: Argüello (S. Francisco)
summary: ★ FRIGATE *HAZARD* AT SAN FRANCISCO — anchored Jan 30; facilitated supplies because Captain proved coming damaged (averiada); set sail for Northwest Coast. pp. 945–8

author: Rodríguez (S. Diego)
summary: Corn shortage — caused by transporting maíz by freight to pobladores; mission mules unserviceable. pp. 949–50

author: Joaquín Sánchez (Monterey)
summary: Explains why he and his wife couldn't fulfill Church precepts. pp. 951–2

author: Sargento Luis Peralta + Cabo Alijo Miranda (S. Francisco)
summary: ★ INSPECTION OF FRIGATE *HAZARD* — by order of Argüello: 350 tons, 60 crew, 16 Indians from San Blas, Capt. James Rowan, 22 cannons (16 mounted); storm damage confirmed. pp. 954–5

author: James Rowan (S. Francisco)
summary: Captain's request — ship needs aid for storm damage. p. 956

author: Argüello (S. Francisco)
summary: *Hazard* damage verified true; sends interpreter's and Captain's notes; permitted port stay and repairs with precautions. pp. 957–8

author: James Rowan (S. Francisco)
summary: Rowan letter via interpreter Francisco de Paula Marin — speaks various languages, knows piloting, interprets with NW Coast Indians and 'Islands of San Luis'; ship lost 2 pilots; details storm damage to *Hazard*. pp. 959–60

author: Council of Indies President
summary: Certifies diligencias regarding calumnies against Viceroy Azanza. pp. 961–7

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Army surgeon establishments — regulations for military medical staff. p. 968

author: (Mexico)
summary: Military court declarations to be held at Real Aduana. p. 969

author: Royal Order
summary: Deserters ineligible for awards or decorations. p. 970

author: (Monterey)
summary: Invalid certificates for Cabo Ignacio Pacheco and Soldado Ignacio Mesa, Cía. de Monterey. pp. 971–2

author: (Mexico)
summary: Judicial processes to proceed via Consejo and Ministry of War. pp. 973–4

author: Royal Order
summary: Military marriages — suppress limpieza de sangre investigations for soldiers. p. 975

author: (military tribunal)
summary: Batallón de Santo Domingo captain absolved of charges. pp. 976–8

author: Royal Order
summary: Chaplain funeral rights — capellanes castrenses have no right to collect funeral offerings from parishioners. pp. 977–80

author: Royal Order
summary: Military marriage license requirement reiterated. p. 981

author: (Mexico)
summary: Interpretation of army ordinances — procedural clarification. pp. 982–3

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Transmits Royal Order: no one may contract marriage without established legal requirements. p. 984

author: Fr. Tomás de Pangua (Colegio de S. Fernando)
summary: ★ RANCHO DE LA BREA DISPUTE — about concession of Rancho de la Brea to Macario de Castro; inserts letter from PP. of San Juan Bautista to President Tapis about inability to comply with order to transfer mission cattle; prejudices to mission if Castro keeps rancho. pp. 985–9

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Rancho de la Brea — sends Guardian of San Fernando's representation asking Castro be removed; orders investigation and if true, Castro to relocate. pp. 990–1

author: Iturrigaray (Mexico)
summary: Payment of 90-reales premium per Royal Order of Jan 15, 1783. pp. 992–3

author: José Manuel Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: No novelty in pueblos of his demarcation. pp. 994–6

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Sends diligencias against neófitos Rodaly and Alejandro de la Cruz. p. 997

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Sends church compliance cédulas; other minor matters. pp. 998–1001

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Unknown frigate arrived Jun 17, set sail Jun 22 heading south. pp. 1002–3

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Presidio occurrences — matanzas for troop meat supply; soldier Macedonio unfit for service due to gálico (syphilis). pp. 1004–7

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Received from Sargento of Sta. Catalina facturas of belongings of deceased Juan Camacho and José Peiquera; forwards to Habilitado. pp. 1008–9

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: ★ GENTILE ATTACK AT STA. CATALINA — gentiles wounded a Christian Indian woman; soldier José Romero imprisoned for bad conduct; placed 3 mounted guards at Mission Sta. Catalina to prevent surprise attack by gentiles. pp. 1010–12

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Received 2 Indian prisoners from Comandante of San Diego. pp. 1013–14

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Complied with two circulars sent. p. 1015

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: ★ Indians who killed soldiers are known to roam the sierra; received 4 lanzas from Antonio Costa. pp. 1016–19

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: No novelty; American ship set sail; sending church compliance cédulas to escorts; other minor matters. pp. 1020–22

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: List of 5 prisoners sent by goleta arráez to deliver in Loreto. p. 1023

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: List of effects Ignacio Saña must deliver to goleta arráez for transport to Loreto. p. 1024

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Five missionaries arriving by Goleta *California* for frontier missions under his command. pp. 1025–27

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: ★ BATTLE AT SANTA CATALINA — gentiles who wounded Christian woman were same who killed soldiers; dawn attack on Oct 17 but gentiles struck first; 1 soldier and 1 friendly gentile wounded; gentiles fortified on hilltop throwing stones; hours of combat; Sargento ordered to pursue with 8 men. pp. 1028–30

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Ship arrived; delivered 4 deserters to its Captain; departed without receiving provisions. pp. 1031–33

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: ★ FRAGATA *O'CAIN* — departed San Quintín May 26; Captain sent canoes ahead with fishermen visiting the coast; believes deaths of soldiers carrying gentile prisoners connected to this. pp. 1034–7

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Retirement of Padres — sent 4 pairs of shackles and 16 escopetas; PP. Apolinario Abad, Valdellon, and Yoldi have permission to retire from missions and embark at San Diego. pp. 1038–41

author: Manuel Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: ★ DECLARATION OF DESERTER FROM *O'CAIN* — testimony of 'Negro Anglo-American' Es Brown (sic), deserter from Fragata *O'Cain* at San Quintín. pp. 1042–3

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: Forwards Brown's declaration from American Fragata *O'Cain*. pp. 1044–46

author: Ruiz (S. Vicente)
summary: New Padres arrived and took possession of frontier missions; church compliance cédulas included; 2 soldiers sick. pp. 1046–48

author: Alejo García Conde (Arizpe)
summary: ★ ENGLISH FRIGATE AT GUAYMAS — extraordinary midnight dispatch; English frigate arrived with hostile intent; ordered Capitán José Fernández de Loredo to apprehend; admits lacks cannons and ships to enforce; 'if not aided by warships, the foreigner will mock his measures.' pp. 1049–50

author: García Conde (Arizpe)
summary: English frigate anchored at Guaymas — requests Comandante of Royal Armada protect Loredo and enforce orders to apprehend English ship. pp. 1051–2

author: Raymundo Carrillo (Sta. Barbara)
summary: ★★ INSTRUCCIÓN FOR FOUNDING OF MISSION SANTA INÉS — 18 ARTICLES. Complete military regulations for escort commander at new mission founding. Art. 1: troops serve as if facing hostile Indians. Art. 2: build enramada for guard post, store arms/munitions/provisions. Art. 3: day/night sentinel at commanding point. Art. 4: daytime cavalry custody, caballada corralled at night. Art. 5: soldiers never leave guard post. Art. 6: gentiles never enter compound armed; leave weapons at rancherías. Art. 7: treatment of Indians must be gentle ('suave'); no extortion; return stolen goods. Art. 8: trade (cambalache) only before escort commander; never provide arms, belduques, swords. Art. 9: soldiers never go alone to rancherías; punish violations instantly. Art. 10: rare punishment of gentiles; arms only against Christian Indians for theft or arson; moderate punishments. Art. 11: troops exist to propagate Gospel; must cooperate in construction (church, habitations, almacén, guard post, family houses); never send soldiers alone. Art. 12: treat missionaries with veneration; assist in attracting Indians. Art. 13: graduate escorts for Padres visiting rancherías proportional to Indian temperament and distance. Art. 14: do NOT dispatch parties hunting cimarrones (runaways) — troop too small. Art. 15: observe general orders from old missions. Art. 16: on day of toma de posesión and first Mass, write certification and send to Governor. Art. 17: coordinate provisions and arms with Carrillo. Art. 18: faithful compliance brings glory to God and career advancement. pp. 1053–56

author: Macario de Castro (S. José)
summary: ★★ REPORT ON ILLEGAL COMMERCE FROM AMERICAN FRIGATE — Viceroy's order re: entry/commerce of frigate at Monterey and SF; Castro reports frigate anchored at Puerto de SF and Yerba Buena in May; stayed several days; considerable commerce; Cadete José Ma. Estudillo told him ship carried $500 and returned with cargo; invalid Luis Romero seen carrying goods outside pueblo; Mayordomo of Mission del Carmelo went; beach crowded with people trading; 'everyone got some little thing; to this day foreign goods are still circulating' ('hta. el día corren géneros que parecen extranjeros'). pp. 1057–60

author: Macario de Castro (S. José)
summary: About Fragata *Alexander* — repeats previous report with little difference; confirms the frigate at Monterey and twice at SF was the *Alexander*. pp. 1061–64

author: Arrillaga (Loreto)
recipient: Comandantes of S. Diego, Sta. Barbara, Monterey, S. Francisco
summary: Exportation of effects — Viceroy's nota of May 1; Minister of San Blas ordered to tell ship comandantes not to embark goods that labradores and merchants send; they should name agents to receive consignments at San Blas. pp. 1065–7

author: Arrillaga (Loreto)
recipient: Comandantes of S. Diego, Sta. Barbara, Monterey, S. Francisco
summary: ★★ FOREIGN SHIP DETENTION ORDER REVOKED — Viceroy's nota of Apr 24: the order to detain foreign ships entering California ports has NOT been approved; 'in view of prejudices and complications that could follow'; orders it be revoked immediately. pp. 1068–9
Title leaf: 'Provincial State Papers, Tomo XIX, 1805–1815.' H.H. Bancroft Collection stamp; Savage.

date_text: 1805 [Savage header 'Nbre 19'; decree body dated '19 de Abril de 1805' — discrepancy, flagged]
author: Viceroy Iturrigaray
people: Iturrigaray; Fray Antonio de la Concepción; Arrillaga; M. Rodríguez; L.R. Carrillo; de la Guerra; Argüello
places: México; Nueva California; Loreto; S. Diego; Sta Bárbara; Monterey; SF
summary: Decree declaring 'desvanecidos' (unfounded) the charges Fray Antonio de la Concepción raised against abuses/mismanagement in the Nueva California missions run by the Colegio Apostólico de San Fernando; Fiscal de Real Hacienda found the accusations baseless; praises the missionaries' apostolic zeal; orders presidio commandants to keep 'recíproca buena correspondencia' with the friars. Certified copy by Arrillaga (Loreto, 10 Jun 1805); registered at S. Diego (2 Jul, Manuel Rodríguez), Sta Bárbara (5 Jul, Luis Raymundo Carrillo), Monterey (8/12 Jul, José de la Guerra y Noriega), SF (Argüello).

author: Iturrigaray
summary: On the order of formation/precedence of artillery troops and dismounted dragoons. Transmits royal order (via Min. José Antonio Caballero), 4 articles: dismounted dragoons serving as infantry rank after artillery & before dragoons; militia artillerymen attached to artillery form with veterans; dismounted-dragoon companies per Rl Orden 8 Jul 1799; militia infantry regiments in the Indies.

date_text: 1805 México [header 'Set. 32' — impossible day; Savage copy error, flagged]
author: Iturrigaray
summary: On appointment of sergeants & corporals: 1st sergeants chosen from most senior 2nd sergeants without disqualifying defects; 2nd sergeants from senior 1st corporals; 2nd corporals chosen by company commanders from the most suitable soldiers who request it.

date_text: 1805 México (header 'Set. 23'; Rl orden 14 Feb via Caballero)
author: Iturrigaray
summary: On deserter punishment: abolishes the penalty of *baquetas* (running the gauntlet) throughout the Indies; 2nd-time deserters 'con iglesia' instead get 6 months in shackles (cleaning duty) then 10 years presidio, without formal trial, whether or not they hold sanctuary.

date_text: 1805 México (header 'Set. 22'; Rl orden 9 Dic via Caballero)
author: Iturrigaray
summary: Royal order: only the subaltern officers of presidial companies are exempt from the [property/'contribución'] obligation affecting subalterns of 'cuerpos políticos.' [exact scope of exemption slightly unclear in abstract]

author: Comd.te Gen. José Joaquín Arrillaga
summary: Five oficios of the same date on military routines — Savage skip-notes them 'sin importancia' (pp. 6–10).

author: Viceroy Iturrigaray
summary: Orders compensation to Mission San Juan Bautista + 15 Indians for aid given in 1800 to Monterey when sails believed enemy were sighted (aid = 3 carretas, 9 horses, 15 Indians). Viceroy + Tribunal de Cuentas order payment for carts/teams/horses to the community and personally to the 15 Indians — 'so they won't hold back from giving aid in future, and do so with greater zeal.' Legalized copy by Arrillaga.

author: Arrillaga
summary: de la Guerra's Real despacho (commission) copies arrived incomplete; send the original or a full certified copy.

author: Arrillaga
summary: On the San Juan Bautista reimbursement (ref. C8): set it at the customary stipend paid to Indian workers on presidio works; submit accounts w/ vouchers to Viceroy + Tribunal de Cuentas.

author: Arrillaga
summary: On hemp (cáñamo) cultivation and valuing its cost: refers the query to Sánchez, the official charged with hemp, who alone should decide cost disputes — 'for now no change should be made.'

author: Arrillaga
