summary: Acknowledges an artillery & ordnance inventory (3 Aug); must be in duplicate — one to the Viceroy, one in the archive.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Forwards the Tribunal de Cuentas' objections (reparos) to the Monterey habilitado's accounts for roof/warehouse repair expenses at that presidio in 1802.

author: Arrillaga
people: Arrillaga; de la Guerra; M. Espinosa
summary: Acknowledges the quadruplicate retirement papers of Cpl. Miguel Espinosa; warns they 'should be quintuplicate, it being wartime.'

author: Arrillaga
summary: Circular transmitting Habilitado Gen. Felipe Goycoechea's 19 Jun proposal for a cleaner method of receipting/inspecting supply-shipment 'Memorias' so shortfalls and responsibility can be traced.

author: Arrillaga
people: Arrillaga; Goycoechea; Pablo Vicente de Solá
summary: On the Habilitación General: Goycoechea given a new post (gov. of Baja Cal.) and ceasing as Habilitado Gen.; Pablo Vicente de Solá (capitán de milicias), proposed by Arrillaga in 3rd place, to substitute in case of death/illness (serving without salary until Goycoechea departs) — quotes the Viceroy's order noting Solá has his sureties ready. Ends p.28. ⭐ Early appearance of Solá — future CA governor 1815–22.

author: Conde del Valle de Orizaba + Fiscal de Rl Hacienda *dictamen* & Viceroy decree — expediente promoted by Gov. Felipe de Goycoechea (Baja Cal.)
people: Conde del Valle de Orizaba; Goycoechea; Anza; Rivera y Moncada; J.T. Vázquez
places: Californias; S. José; Los Ángeles; Branciforte; Sta Cruz; Sta Clara; S. Juan Bautista; Pto San Quintín
themes: exploration; trade; fiscal; missions; indigenous-demo; america; defense; royal-order
summary: Development memorandum on the Californias. Recalls the Anza (1775) & Rivera y Moncada (1781) colonizations that founded San José de Guadalupe & Los Ángeles (informant commanded the latter under Sta Bárbara's jurisdiction); Villa de Branciforte's failure — cramped beside Mission Sta Cruz on a creek, 'not capable of sustaining a district,' only 'the sound of its title'; proposes Sta Cruz share neophytes to Sta Clara/SJB so its friars found elsewhere. Economy as the means: gold placers & silver mines on 'el Río,' whale fishery on both coasts, redwood (*Palo Colorado*) for shipyards, weaving-masters & a *maestro de primeras letras* per presidio; each province to get its own export buque separate from the San Blas Dept ships; warns *against* peopling with convicts, favoring families + orphans aged 10–12. 'Movimientos de Anglo-Americanos y peligro que amenazan'; advises shifting the Department of San Blas → Puerto de San Quintín; population increase = 'la primera necesidad de aquella colonia.' Complete: ends n29 with the Viceroy's decree ('Como pide el Sr Fiscal de Rl Hac.a'), authenticated copy by Conde del Valle de Orizaba, 20 Dec 1805. See expanded Entry C17 (verbatim quotes).

author: Cabo Luis Peralta (+ witnesses) — sworn declaration
places: Santa Clara; ranchería de los Asisones
summary: The 'Cuevas attack' (deposition). Sworn deposition on an expedition (*salida*) by Padre Fr. Pedro Cuevas, during which a clash broke out between Christian and gentile Indians of the ranchería of the Asisones, and the padre received arrow wounds. Savage: 'no arroja mas luz sobre el asunto.' [One of 3 docs on this affair — see C24, C25.]

author: [informant]
places: Mission San José; Sta Clara; Pelbones[?]; Seunenes
summary: Plot to burn Mission Santa Clara & kill the padres. A Christian Indian caught scouting (with a gentile from the ranchería) a planned night attack to burn the mission and kill the friars; gentiles massed in great number at the rancherías of Pelbones[?] & Seunenes; the prisoner declared all — 5 Christian Indians implicated & arrested.

author: José Ignacio Argüello
summary: SF garrison increase per Viceroy's order (8 Apr 1805): add 1 sargento 2º, 4 cabos, 29 soldiers (+ '34 plazas sencillas'); proposes Argüello for sgt-2º & 5 soldiers for cabos; thinks it hard to fill.

author: Argüello
summary: New powder *casa-mata* at the Castillo de San Joaquín — powder endangered in the barracks; built a new magazine 300 varas out (beyond the sand circle), at no cost to the Rl Hacienda; also repaired 3 curtain-wall sections + the palisade.

author: José María Bonifaz[?]
people: Bonifaz[?]; de la Guerra; M. Torres
summary: Re missions; recovered from illness thanks to Dr. Manuel Torres's medicines. (minor)

author: Argüello
summary: Rl Hacienda cattle barely countable — only 32 + 30 calves; herd mixed with the mission's; bears, wolves & mountain lions destroy many across vast, partly uninhabitable terrain; protests he cannot be held responsible for the *merma*.

author: Sgt. Luis Peralta — diary
summary: Peralta's punitive expedition (19–27 Jan 1805) vs. the gentiles who attacked Padre Cuevas near Sta Clara. Left SF 19 Jan w/ inválidos, vecinos & 18 troops; tracked into the sierra (rains hid the trail), seized 2 gentile guides; 23 Jan battle — 'fueron haciendo armas y nos comenzaron á tirar… dimos sobre ellos': ~5 gandules killed incl. captains; pursued into the woods; took 25 women + 11 Indians (2 wounded) captive; released 1 on 27 Jan to prove innocence.

author: José Antonio Sánchez
people: J.A. Sánchez; J. Argüello; Fr. Pedro Cuevas; I. Alviso; J. Higuera; I. Higuera
summary: The Cuevas attack itself. Padre Pedro Cuevas requested an escort (soldiers Ignacio Alviso & Joaquín Higuera) to confess the sick at a Christian ranchería; in heavy fog the guide led them to a gentile ranchería claiming it Christian; arrows + great shouting, more came from nearby rancherías — Ignacio Higuera killed (= the *mayordomo*, per C26), Padre Cuevas wounded in the face, Joaquín Higuera in the thigh; saddle & spare horses killed; party besieged midday→dusk, losing all but their *cueras*, *adargas* & muskets.

author: José Argüello
people: José Argüello; Arrillaga; Fr. Cuevas; I. Higuera (mayordomo); J. Higuera; L. Peralta
summary: Cuevas affair — umbrella report (ties together the C24 diary + C25/Sánchez & Alviso declarations). Confirms the mayordomo Ignacio Higuera killed; dispatched Peralta with 34 men (15 vecinos); complains Padre Cuevas, newly arrived from México, recklessly ignored the soldier's warnings; asks the Gov. to order Monterey to send help promptly for future *salidas* vs. hostile Indians; Peralta's caballada returned broken-down.

author: Manuel Rodríguez
summary: Armament: armorer to repair faults in 74 carbines received (Rodríguez doubts he's capable); once fixed, distribute to LA / Sta Bárbara / Monterey / SF; San Diego keeps 14.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Death of soldier Carlos Rosas (pleurisy); married to an India baptized at San Gabriel; left 2 sons & 3 daughters.

author: José Raymundo Carrillo
summary: Encloses a list of artillery ordnance (pertrechos).

author: Carrillo
summary: Arrillaga formally recognized as Gov. político y militar of Alta Cal. (9 Dec 1804) before plaza, vecindario & missions.

author: José Argüello
people: José Argüello; L. Peralta; Fr. Cuevas
places: SF; Sta Clara; San José; el Pescadero
summary: Second Peralta expedition vs. the Cuevas attackers — 'felices resultados': most near-Christians returned to missions Sta Clara/San José, some gentiles sought baptism; a capitanejo of the big ranchería 'el Pescadero' (SF rivers) assured Peralta his people weren't involved; Peralta returned 22nd with 2 Christian accomplices (held + 4 gentiles), recommends exiling the Christians to a distant presidio.

author: Argüello
people: Luis Argüello; Rafaela Sal; Hermenegildo Sal; Arrillaga
summary: Alférez Luis Argüello requests license to marry Doña Rafaela, daughter of Teniente Hermenegildo Sal. [Sal genealogy; the future 1st Mexican gov. of Calif. weds Sal's daughter. Confirms Sal = *Teniente* by 1805.]

author: Argüello
people: Argüello; L. Argüello; Pablo V. de Solá; Goycoechea
summary: Nomination of Habilitado General: Argüello & Luis Argüello agree Pablo V. de Solá take charge (interim) as Goycoechea (propietario) leaves to be governor. (Echoes C16.)

author: Argüello
summary: Missions La Purísima & San José offered to supply the SF company (knapsacks, weapons, etc.) at better quality & lower price; asks San Blas omit the equivalent shipment.

author: Argüello
summary: Pursuit of fugitive Indians of Mission San José who fled back to the ranchería of the Luechas (site of the Cuevas attack); sent all presidio troops + a cabo & 5 soldiers from Monterey, Peralta & Alférez Luis Argüello operating; results pending.

author: José de la Guerra
summary: Monterey company increase (Viceroy 8 Apr): 1 sgt, 5 cabos, 29 soldiers; chose officers, enlisted 19 soldiers.

author: Argüello
summary: Recommends the 4 gentile accomplices in the Cuevas attack be transported to Sta Bárbara or San Diego presidio.

author: José Manuel Ruiz
summary: Frontier calm except a measles (sarampión) epidemic expected to ravage the Indians; urges coastal security since war declared between Spain & England.

author: Ruiz
summary: Long report: frontier military-service routines; nearly identified who killed the Christian Indians (earlier report); measles already at missions San Fernando & Rosario, spreading — fears great mortality.

author: Papal Breve of Pius VII (via Ignacio García[?])
summary: Certified printed copy (reprinted México 1805): at the King of Spain's request, extends for 7 years the castrense Vicariate of the army & navy.

author: Viceroy Iturrigaray
summary: Royal order to CLOSE ALL PORTS (only mail-boats may sail, without funds/goods), after a British squadron in peacetime attacked & seized 4 Spanish frigates en route Montevideo→Cádiz [Cape Santa María, Oct 1804 → the war]; orders reprisals vs. British ships (embargo & impound).

author: Cosme de Mier
summary: Acknowledges the founding of Mission Santa Inés (17 Sept 1804) at the paraje Las Cieneguitas[?], near but distinct from the planned site.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Authorizes Arrillaga (given the distance from México) to take his oath of office before the highest-ranking nearby officer.

author: Manuel Velázquez de León (for the Sec. of the Viceroyalty)
people: M. Velázquez de León; Arrillaga; Vol. de Cataluña
places: SF; Monterey; Sta Bárbara; San Diego; Loreto
summary: Decree (1 Apr 1805) to increase the presidial companies' force — withdrawal of the 1st Catalan Volunteers + new mission foundations + 'frecuentes arribadas de buques Americanos á los puertos' leave too few troops. Quotes Arrillaga's 1803 plan (Nº 435) for exact augmentations: SF +1 capt, 1 sgt, 4 cabos, 29 soldiers; Monterey +1 sgt, 5 cabos, 19 sol.; Sta Bárbara +1 capt, 4 cabos, 3 sol.; San Diego +1 capt, 1 sgt, 3 cabos, 14 sol.; Loreto +1 alférez, 1 sgt, 4 cabos, 10 sol.

author: Viceroy Iturrigaray
summary: Notifies the decreed presidial-force increase; orders appointment of sgts/cabos & recruitment of soldados rasos.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Contraband: acknowledges the report of smuggling by the frigate Concepción, hidden at the Sta Bárbara presidio & mission and the Refugio ranch; asks the investigation's results, and what's known of contraband at Monterey & SF ports by an American ship.

author: Iturrigaray
people: Iturrigaray; Fray Antonio de la Concepción
summary: DUPLICATE of C2 — the decree dismissing Fray Antonio de la Concepción's charges of mission abuses. Savage: 'otra copia en las pp 2-3 de este tomo.' `dup: internal — same volume tomo 1–3`

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Habilitación funds: 23,437 pesos 4 rs 7 grs delivered to Habilitado Gen. Goycoechea for Loreto presidio supplies (troops, marinería, arsenal) shipped from San Blas.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Acknowledges the gentile attack on the San José missionary (= the Cuevas affair, C25/C26) and confirms he has favorably resolved the presidial-company increase, urging haste.

author: Rl Tribunal de Cuentas
summary: On developing flax (lino) & hemp (cáñamo) cultivation in California — pay growers promptly (not after the San Blas appraisal) so they can fund new sowings; the Condestable Sánchez commissioned to teach sowing & processing.

author: Borbón, Fiscal de la Rl Hacienda
summary: On flax/hemp development: the seed was sent to establish the industry for the benefit of vecinos and the missions; backs the Tribunal — growers paid promptly by the presidio Habilitados, not after San Blas appraisal.

author: Viceroy Iturrigaray
summary: Final decision: the Junta Superior de Rl Hacienda approves subsidizing flax & hemp sowing at the missions & other sites; the crop sold to the San Blas ship commanders, paid cash at dockside by the presidio Habilitados per its cost. (Closes the C50–C52 expediente.)

author: Iturrigaray
people: Iturrigaray; J.Mª Rodríguez; P. Solá; Goycoechea
summary: Habilitado General succession: José Mª Rodríguez to substitute in vacancy/illness/absence while Pablo Solá is called to the post (Goycoechea leaving); Rodríguez rewarded with cadete supernumerario rank (no salary) for assisting Goycoechea.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Anti-contraband decree: to end the 'detestable abuso del contrabando,' authorizes all the King's vassals to seize contraband, rewarding them in the King's name with the full value of what they intercept. Two copies sent to publish.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: DUPLICATE (of C16/C33): Goycoechea named Gov. of Baja Cal.; election of Habilitado Gen. Savage: 'otra copia en pp 12-13 de este tomo.' `dup: internal`

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Loreto supply crisis (no ships): the San Blas Apostadero has only the frigate Princesa & brig Activo, both assigned to Alta California — so Loreto (needing maize & cigars) can't be supplied until their October return, unless its two goletas are used.

author: Vicente Mora (Protomedicato)
summary: Medical dictamen on California's climate & diseases (from José Benítez's tour of Monterey & missions): climate judged *healthy*, no endemic disease; illness comes from cold/exposure/bad water/poor diet and 'carnal disorders,' contact with the infected, and natives' reliance on empirical 'national doctors' over rational medicine; syphilis (gálico) frequent & severe. Proposes an infirmary for the gentiles — esp. vs. syphilis, which 'without aid ravages the populations.'

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Sends the Protomedicato report to surgeon José Mª Benítez, ordering him to visit the San Diego & Channel missions to treat common diseases and remit annual health reports.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Printed anti-contraband decree (the decree summarized in C54; abstract at tomo p.60).

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Arrillaga should obtain his title of Gov. Político of Alta Cal. via an agent/apoderado before the Council of the Indies.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Printed contraband expediente — a case of English goods smuggled in México against Nicolás Mª Zaragoza & accomplices, circulated to all governors incl. Calif. (Argüello registered it at SF, 24 Nov 1805).

author: Rl Tribunal de Cuentas
summary: Goycoechea's salary: draws the Habilitado Gen. pay (held interim), not the SB Comandante's; paid in México where he resides; struck from presidio ajustes.

author: Rl Tribunal de Cuentas
summary: No extraordinary Rl Hacienda expenses without express Viceroy order, even if urgent; disallows certain unauthorized expenses by the Monterey habilitado (roof repairs).

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Forwards the Tribunal's dictamen disapproving the Monterey habilitado's 1802 unauthorized roof/warehouse repairs.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Forwards the Tribunal dictamen on Goycoechea's salary; orders it omitted from the Sta Bárbara presidio budget.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Authorizes the Gov. + Artillery Comandante to grant marriage licenses to artillery soldiers.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: On the company increase: escorts for already-founded missions (incl. Sta Inés) were counted, so the 10 extra men the Gov. requested (6 May) aren't included.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Goycoechea confirmed Gov. Político y Militar of Baja Cal. (Arrillaga = propietario of Alta + interim of Baja); to be recognized once he posts bond & presents his title. [Savage wrote 'Alta' but flags it should be 'Baja.']

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Ships reaching Monterey (22 & 27 June): the sloop Guadalupe (from San Blas) and a brig carrying Presbyter Dn Alejandro Jordán 'with his machine and workers for pearl-diving.'

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Supply funds: $63,206 6r 8½g delivered to the interim Habilitado Gen. to cover the four Alta California presidios + the artillery piquete — supply the Memorias, pay San Blas, honor other presidios' libranzas (per the 1799 Reglamento).

author: Iturrigaray
people: Iturrigaray; P. Solá; Goycoechea; J.Mª de Arista
summary: Habilitación Gen.: Pablo Solá (Capt., Toluca Provincial Regiment) to replace Goycoechea after posting bond; José Mª de Arista to substitute in Solá's absence.

author: Viceroy Iturrigaray
summary: RESERVADO — on the pretensions of the United States. Relays the Sec. of War's 5 Jun order quoting Pedro Cevallos: the U.S., via envoy 'Dn Jayme Monroe' (James Monroe), has made pretensions; arguments against 'los ambiciosos designios de aquel Gobierno' produced no result; the King orders precautions for 'la seguridad de sus dominios, con respecto á las intenciones hostiles que pueden tener los referidos Estados Unidos.'

author: Cosme González Carvajal
places: Sta Catalina; Sta Bárbara channel; Cabo San Lucas
summary: The American frigate 'William Shaler' (Guillermo Sholar) careened at Santa Catalina Island, then to the Sta Bárbara channel, and sailed 3 June toward Cabo San Lucas. [The Shaler/*Lelia Byrd* American-smuggling era.]

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Hemp cultivation: San José vecinos now trained, Condestable Ignacio Sánchez moves to la Porciúncula (LA) to teach; Viceroy urges the industry's development.

author: Iturrigaray
people: Bárbara Gandiaga; Lázaro Rosales; Alejandro de la Cruz; Padre Surroca; Padre M. López[?]; J. Lorís del Cristo y Conde
summary: Death sentences — the Bárbara Gandiaga case. Confirms the auditor de guerra's sentence condemning three Indians — Bárbara Gandiaga (instigator, in revenge for being dismissed from the padre's house), Lázaro Rosales & Alejandro de la Cruz — to death for the premeditated murder of their minister Padre Fr. Eudaldo Surroca[?] (smothered asleep) at Mission Santo Tomás (Baja Cal.); hanging (or shooting if no executioner), then head & right hand spiked in the mission's most public place. A new deposition was ordered from Gandiaga (2nd day in the chapel) re a second murder — Padre Fr. Miguel López[?] + a gentile Indian — in which she is also implicated.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Acknowledges Arrillaga left Loreto (19 Aug) for Monterey to take up his post as Gov. of Alta Cal., leaving instructions for the newly-named presidio commander.

author: José Raymundo Carrillo
summary: His will — leaves goods to wife & children (Savage: 'no interés'). [Carrillo–Sal–Argüello family network. NB: making a will ≠ death — Carrillo was still alive Jan 1809 (registers C110); earlier 'death 1806' inference retracted.]

author: Macario de Castro
summary: Mass slaughter of horses: by the Gov.'s order (overpopulation ruining the pastures), ~7,500 head of caballada sent to the matadero from the main San José pastures, to relieve the useful animals.

author: Castro
summary: Need to kill the mares (yeguada): they ravage pastures & watering holes, harming oxen & cattle — 'la yeguada se renueva con largueza' at the useful herd's expense.

author: Castro
summary: The tithe (diezmo) horse herd: part sold as ordered, the rest (~1,000 head, with the pueblo's) sent to slaughter.

author: Ignacio Vallejo
summary: On the mare slaughter — will gather the vecinos; 6 soldiers with a stake in the yeguada remain. [Ignacio Vallejo = father of M.G. Vallejo.]

author: I. Vallejo
summary: The Sta Clara mission padres lent the potrero/estero to corral & kill the mares; awaits word whether the tithe herd should also be destroyed.

author: José Mª Ruiz
summary: On the San Miguel mission escort — will comply. (minor)

author: Javier Aguilar
places: Santa Ana; Real de San Antonio
summary: Silver mining: 1,508 marcos of silver extracted in 1805 by the 22 miners of the Real de San Antonio (Baja Cal.), plus the mercury (azogue, 25 arrobas) each holds.

author: Viceroy
recipient: Min. of the San Blas Apostadero
people: José de Gálvez (1769 Arancel); M. Velázquez de León
summary: On commerce with the Californias: trade goods carried back from Calif. on the brig Activo & frigate Concepción by their commanders, chaplains, officers — and the missions — without paying the established fletes (per Gálvez's 1769 Arancel) is ruled an abuse. Orders a bando at San Blas / Tepic / the Calif. presidios: every ship registered outbound & inbound, only moderate departmental freight, no sailors smuggling. (Legalized copy Méx 16 Feb 1806, Velázquez de León.)

author: Ramón de Saavedra (Comd. of San Blas)
people: Ramón de Saavedra; Iturrigaray; Arrillaga; B. Vivero y Escaño
places: San Blas; Monterey; Pta Concepción; San Diego; Pto San Quintín
themes: defense; ships; america; britain; trade; foreign-relations; construction
summary: Anti-smuggling patrol strategy: how to stop foreigners trading with the coastal natives. Settles the Arrillaga vs. Benito Vivero y Escaño dispute over where to station the patrol frigate. Dictamen: use the copper-bottomed frigate *Princesa* (faster), based at Monterey, cruising to Punta de la Concepción (34°32') and on to San Diego, checking all anchorages for English/American smugglers; build a small battery at Punta López de Haro (entrance to Pto San Quintín) to block foreign ships. (Legalized Méx 10 Mar 1806.)

author: Junta de Guerra y Marina, San Blas (pres. Ramón de Saavedra)
people: Saavedra; S. Menéndez; M. Murga; B. Otalora; F. Taraga; Arrillaga; B. Vivero
places: San Blas; Monterey; Cabo San Lucas
themes: defense; ships; america; britain; trade; foreign-relations
summary: Naval council on the best means to drive foreign ships off the California coasts & where to station the patrol (resolving the Arrillaga–Vivero dispute). Officers (pilot Salvador Menéndez, Alférez Manuel Murga, Braulio Otalora, Francisco Taraga[?]): the frigate to leave San Blas in Jan/Feb for Monterey, anchor ~1 month, then cruise the coast checking every cove down to Cabo San Lucas for English/American smugglers; also notes foreigners whaling off the coast. Signed by all 5 officers.

author: Viceroy Iturrigaray
summary: Decree assigning a ship to drive foreigners off the California coasts — implementing Saavedra's recommendation & the San Blas Junta de Guerra (C86–C87). The anti-smuggling patrol approved; order sent to the San Blas naval commander.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Acknowledges receipt of the military review documents (extractos de revista) for the presidial troops + artillery piquete for the past year.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: The Russian frigate *Juno* (Rezanov) at San Francisco. Acknowledges the Gov.'s 19 Apr report of the arrival at SF of the Russian frigate *Juno*, Comd. Dn Resanoff [Rezanov], seeking provisions & repair; asks what was learned of the ship's purpose 'y de los pasaportes con que navega.' [The 1806 Rezanov supply mission from Sitka — Rezanov / Concepción Argüello.]

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Royal circular: a corporal who killed a soldier (México) → exile + 5 yrs presidio; King forbids sergeants/corporals to inflict arbitrary punishments on soldiers.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Acknowledges the general state report (estado general) of Alta & Baja Cal. to end of 1805.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Arrival of the supply frigate Concepción at SF (19 Aug), Comd. Tte de Navío Ramón de Moraga[?], with the memorias for presidios & missions.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Fortification policy: will send a qualified engineer officer to assess the presidios' existing defenses & decide what else to fortify; meanwhile only the most essential, economical works (officer/troop housing).

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Two 'foreign Indians' (indios extranjeros) captured on the southern coast, sent to San Blas on the Princesa (Comd. Saavedra).

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Troop reallocation: San Diego company decreased, Loreto increased — the San Miguel mission escort (1 cabo + 5 soldiers) now from Loreto, not San Diego.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Habilitado Gen.: Capt. Manuel Rodríguez embarked on the Princesa for San Blas → México to serve as Habilitado Gen. of the Cal. presidios.

author: Iturrigaray
people: Fr. R. Gutiérrez; Fr. Pedro Cuevas
summary: Two missionaries retiring (ill, with licenses), embarked on the Concepción for San Blas: Fray Romualdo Gutiérrez & Fr. Pedro Cuevas (the padre wounded in the 1805 Cuevas attack).

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Soldier José Vicente Arroyo's petition for invalid status — beyond the Gov.'s power; elevated to the King.

author: undated/unsigned borrador
people: Rezanov; Khvostov; Davydov; Langsdorff; Illyin; Andreev
summary: Crew roster of the Russian frigate *Juno* (arrived SF 8 Apr 1806): Rezanov (Chamberlain/Chevalier), Capt. Khvostov (Chvostoff), Lt. Davydov (Davidoff), Doctor/Naturalist Langsdorff, pilots Illyin & Andreev, 43 crew — 49 total. (Written in French.)

author: Pablo de la Olsa[?]
summary: Requests a cadete post in the presidial companies, as promised.

author: (unsigned)
summary: Instructions (13 articles) for the San José Militia Company: corporals' attendance at drill; the cabo not diverted to other service without order; militiamen still subject to their fathers/masters & the Alcalde; the Sgt. Comisionado administers justice; militia not mixed into the invalids'/regulars' affairs.

author: Arrillaga
places: San Diego; the 4 presidios; Sandwich Islands
themes: admin; defense; indigenous-military; missions; personnel; trade
summary: 'Preceptos Generales' — Arrillaga's general instructions after his first year as governor. Each commander responsible for the pueblo entrusted to him: peace/harmony, a disciplined company, Indians 'dóciles y subordinados' (prevent fugitives), due regard for invalids/retirees/vecinos; escort sizes 3–6 soldiers; pacify Indians by few visits → 'conquista ó reducción'; peace & harmony with the missionaries (extinguish the 'notorias etiquetas'). Ends with a foreign-trade prohibition — notes maize was sold at the ensenada of San José del Cabo (bought in Alta Cal.) and mares taken to the Sandwich Islands from this coast; 'muchas personas caracterizadas' involved. Registered at the presidios 1807.

author: Arrillaga
people: Arrillaga; Borica; de la Guerra; J. Argüello
summary: Measures to cut off commerce with American ships. An American two-masted ship anchored near Mission San Luis [Obispo] on pretext of wood/water; Arrillaga: the pretext hides commercial (or 'más siniestros') intent — 'if our side did not deal with them, they would abandon our coasts.' Orders guards (sgt/cabo + 4 soldiers) at landing points, day & night, deny all provisions/water/wood, seize the foreigners if possible (citing Borica's earlier circular of the law of the Indies).

author: Arrillaga
summary: Sends the retirement cédula for cabo Cayetano Espinosa.

author: Arrillaga
people: Arrillaga; Gabriel Moraga; Fr. M. Cipres; Ruiz
summary: A military expedition under Alférez Gabriel Moraga — 25 men (+ chaplain Fr. Marcelino Cipres & interpreters); SF to ready 1 sgt, 1 cabo, 8 soldiers; Moraga holds verbal orders to explore the far side of the river he couldn't cross last time; 5 men drawn from San Luis/San Miguel. [The 1806 Moraga interior/Central Valley expedition.]

author: Arrillaga
summary: Supply the frigate Princesa (Saavedra) the provisions it requests (reintegro).

author: Arrillaga
people: Gabriel Moraga; Fr. Pedro Muñoz; Arrillaga
summary: Moraga expedition assembled: Alférez Gabriel Moraga left Monterey (1 cabo + 14 soldiers) for Mission San Juan Bautista to join Fr. Pedro Muñoz + the SF detachment (1 sgt, 1 cabo, 8 soldiers); 5 men from Sta Bárbara backfill. [The 1806 Moraga–Muñoz Central Valley expedition — Muñoz's diary survives.]

author: Arrillaga
people: Arrillaga; J. Estudillo; de la Guerra
summary: Estudillo becomes interim commander of Monterey (de la Guerra departed).

author: Viceroy Garibay
summary: Garibay announces he has assumed the viceroyalty of New Spain. Published in Calif. Dec 1808–Jan 1809 (registered by Arrillaga, Estudillo, J. Argüello, J.R. Carrillo [SD, 8 Jan 1809]).

author: (Gaceta de México)
summary: Printed proclamation on the deposition of Viceroy Iturrigaray (the Sept 1808 coup). Cf. Gaceta de México T.18, pp 679–80.

author: Viceroy Iturrigaray
summary: Printed proclamation on Napoleon against Fernando VII & the Royal Family, urging Mexicans to unity & fidelity. [The 1808 Bayonne crisis.]

author: Negreiros y Soria (for Garibay)
summary: On payment for baggage (bagajes) of officers/troops on Royal-Service marches.

author: Manuel Rodríguez
summary: Alférez José Lujan's travel SD→Loreto (reached San Vicente 20th).

author: José Lujan
summary: Complaint of injustice against Capt. Manuel Rodríguez — passed over in promotions, 'desterrado'; asks the reasons.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Sgt. José Pico returned (11th) with 5 soldiers, conducting Indians sent from the frontier to Santa Bárbara.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Pursuit of fugitive Indians (cimarrones) of the missions between San Luis Rey & San Gabriel; best time mid-Oct (water/pasture scarce).

author: Rodríguez
summary: Supplied the Princesa with 300 arrobas of flour; + a foreign frigate at San Borja (Baja) (unknown name/nationality) with ~80 two-man canoes (~160 hunters) fishing — a large otter-hunting fleet; the Princesa went to find it but it vanished by end of July (per Tte Ruiz).

author: Rodríguez
summary: Sends the monthly revista extract + other docs.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Estate of the late Tte. Pablo Grijalva — press the widow to declare all of it; suspicion of concealment harming the heiresses.

author: José Ignacio Maitorena
summary: Acknowledges his Alférez commission for Sta Bárbara (from the Viceroy).

author: Rodríguez
places: San Diego; San Luis Rey; San Miguel
summary: Fugitive-Indian expedition: 20 men under Alférez Maitorena + a sgt left SD 20 Jun, ranged 25–30 leagues from the San Luis Rey sierra to San Miguel; returned 14th with only 2 cimarrones. Diary by Maitorena & Sgt. José Pico (not preserved).

author: Rodríguez
summary: Debt: Lucas Olivera owes $65-5½ (1805 poblador loan), fled to SB & enlisted to escape it.

author: José Raymundo Carrillo
summary: Earthquake at Santa Bárbara (21 May 1806, midnight): strong tremor damaged the chapel; requests authorization to draw materials from the mission for repairs. [Confirms Carrillo alive May 1806.]

author: Rodríguez
people: José O'Cain; M. Rodríguez; Juan Marine; Juan Osuna
summary: American whaler 'O'Cain' at San Diego. A frigate from Nantucket (18 guns, 35 crew, Capt. José O'Cain — recognized by artilleryman Juan Marine as the Anglo-American he'd known trading/hunting) anchored at SD (25 Jun), denied trade/provisions for refusing to show its passport; cabo Juan Osuna + 4 soldiers set to watch & prevent contraband. The ship then moved to Todos Santos and seized the cabo + 2 soldiers, holding them prisoner aboard; Rodríguez can't pursue (troops away on the Maitorena expedition).

author: Rodríguez
people: M. Rodríguez; José O'Cain; J.Mª Osuna; R. Carrillo
places: San Diego; Todos Santos; Sta Bárbara
summary: O'Cain warning to Sta Bárbara: the American frigate (Capt. José O'Cain, 35 crew, 18 guns) sailed north from Todos Santos carrying off cabo Juan Mª Osuna, who had gone to deny it provisions & block contraband.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Proposes invalid status for soldier Manuel Bustante (vision loss).

author: Rodríguez
places: San Diego; San Juan Capistrano; Sta Bárbara
summary: Transport of Indian prisoners to Sta Bárbara: cabo José Pico + 5 soldiers w/ 3 frontier Indians + 2 'incorregibles' from San Juan Capistrano + 4 unbound.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Discharge for soldier Miguel King[?] (dislocated shoulder, pre-existing & concealed at enlistment).

author: Pablo Grijalva — his will
people: Pablo Grijalva; I. Martínez; J.A. Peralta
summary: Will of Tte. Pablo Grijalva (Anza-expedition veteran): goods to wife & children; daughters Mª Josefa (m. José Ant. Serbas) & Mª del Carmen (m. Juan Ant. Peralta) already given their portions at marriage. Witnesses incl. Ignacio Martínez, Felipe Romero.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Death of Tte. Pablo Grijalva (July 1806) + estate dispute — refused to declare jewels/money or leave anything to his daughters.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Alférez José Lujan arrived (with wife) en route to Loreto with his new commission.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Convict José Botiller took church sanctuary at San Fernando; extracted under a writ of immunity & moved to SB's jurisdiction.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Sends the month's military docs.

author: Rodríguez
people: José O'Cain; M. Rodríguez; R. Saavedra; J. Osuna
places: San Diego; San Quintín; Isla Santo Tomás; Nootka
themes: america; russia; ships; trade; defense; foreign-relations
summary: O'Cain in full detail. The Anglo-American frigate (Capt. José O'Cain) released cabo Osuna at San Quintín (8th); headed south, perhaps to Isla de Santo Tomás, where another ship hunts otters with Nootka-type kayaks crewed by NW-coast Indians — 2 'de razón' Americans/English among them spoke with O'Cain. The frigate: 36 crew, 16 guns (14×6-pdr, 2×24-pdr), chain/bar/grape shot; crew always lands armed (4 pedreros in the launch); fast, wary of the San Blas ships. Rodríguez can't detain it (out of jurisdiction).

author: Rodríguez
people: M. Rodríguez; A. Guillén; J. Botiller
places: San Diego; Pta de Guijarros; San Clemente; Sta Catalina
summary: Jailbreak — the 'Peacock' prisoners escape. The 4 foreigners taken from the Anglo-American brig 'Peacock' + the convict José Botiller broke out of the San Diego jail (cut the roof beams, rappelled on a leather rope), stole the flat-bottomed barge from Punta de Guijarros, and fled to the embarcadero to reach a 3-masted ship sighted off San Juan Capistrano (heading between San Clemente & Sta Catalina). Cabo Antonio Guillén + 4 sent to recover at least the barge.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Couldn't send 2 Indian prisoners to SB — no troops (all on duty).

author: Rodríguez
summary: Sends revista extracts + estados de fuerza for Mar–Jun.

author: José Mª Ruiz
summary: Sends the company's monthly military docs.

author: Rodríguez
summary: 88 head of cattle missing from the Rl Hacienda rancho (hard to count, mixed with the mission herd); the mission has had great grain scarcity to feed the Indians, so Christians in the rancherías lack food.

author: Rodríguez
people: José O'Cain; M. Rodríguez; J. Osuna; J.M. Ruiz
places: San Diego; Todos Santos; San Quintín
summary: O'Cain/Peacock armed confrontation + threats to the presidio. The seizure of cabo Osuna + 2 soldiers at Todos Santos: 20 men ashore, 15 armed; they put musket muzzles to the corporal's chest & back and dragged the soldiers from their saddles. Released them (6th) with a threat — unless the held pilot (taken via the San Juan Capistrano escolta to the brig Peacock, also O'Cain's) is surrendered, O'Cain will return and destroy the battery, then the presidio. Rodríguez summoned escort troops from San Gabriel/San Juan/San Luis Rey, built a parapet on the battery esplanade vs. grapeshot; Osuna released at San Quintín (9th).

author: Rodríguez
people: M. Rodríguez; Maitorena; Fr. P. González
summary: News: measles did little harm at the presidio but killed many Indians at the nearby missions; the otters embarked on the American frigate 'Alexander' in 1803 were lost; Cadete Martínez & habilitación; Cadete Maitorena to Monterey 3 May.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Mail/post-office (estafeta) accounts for 1805.

author: Sgt. José Mª Pico
summary: Certificate of a finding at a frontier mission (Savage: 'sin importar').

author: Rodríguez
people: Fr. Pedro González; M. Rodríguez; J. Pico
summary: A missionary suspected of contraband. Fr. Pedro González (Mission San Miguel) vanished for hours; soldiers presumed he was 'comiendo con el inglés' at a place called Carmen; a soldier saw him with a foreign-made leather case locked away. Sgt. José Pico searched & found the case holding 4 books of writing paper 'en forma de remesas.'

author: José Lujan
summary: Grievance: the Viceroy has reassigned him; complains of injustice after being 'ultrajado en su honor' by his commander; will vindicate his honor in a consejo de guerra.

author: Rodríguez
summary: The 4 foreign escapees returned to port in the flat barge (got only 10 leagues); convict Botiller fled up the coast, still uncaught.

author: Rodríguez
people: M. Rodríguez; F.Mª Ruiz; Lujan; I. Martínez
summary: Officer reorganization: Rodríguez made Captain of the new SF company; Francisco Mª Ruiz → Tte of San Diego; Alférez Lujan → Loreto; cadete Ignacio Martínez → alférez.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Delivered the Santa Cruzada bulls (bulas) to the habilitado.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Capt. José de Zúñiga must pay the SD company a debt left by the late Ay. Insp. Nicolás Soler.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Maitorena promoted alférez of the Sta Bárbara company.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Goycoechea ceased as Habilitado Gen.; Pablo Solá (Toluca capt.) interim.

author: Ignacio Martínez (habilitado)
summary: Requests instructions on managing the funds in his charge.

author: Martínez
summary: Sends the Memorias dispositions + 1807 situado budget for the SD company.

author: Martínez
summary: Took charge of the piquete habilitación (15 Feb).

author: Rodríguez
summary: Weather/harvest/measles: cold then mild winter; wheat/maize/barley regular; a worm hurt the beans; measles spared the gente de razón but killed Indians at the nearby missions.

author: Martínez
summary: The Soler debt: Capt. Zúñiga owes the SD company $1,062-2 (Soler's debt); the late Soler's wife claimed his salary.

author: Rodríguez
summary: On the American ships — imprisonment of the 4 *Peacock* crewmen apprehended by the San Juan Capistrano escort; asks how to interpret the order to release them to the ship. Names the Peacock's captain 'Olivos Kimwell' (Oliver Kimball) & O'Cain's frigate 'Reisos.'

author: Rodríguez
summary: Another foreign two-masted ship at the Carmen anchorage (near Mission San Miguel, 23 Feb); cabo Pedro Lobo + 5 soldiers sent to block contraband; ship left south without provisioning.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Certifies the company fulfilled the church precept (confession & communion).

author: Ignacio Martínez (habilitado)
summary: Sale of confiscated contraband: 19 pieces of wide cloth + estopilla, bretaña, 6 blue cloth — seized at Punta de Guijarros 21 Mar 1803 — auctioned for $212-5.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Certifies who made the 1803 Punta de Guijarros contraband seizure: Maitorena + soldiers Juan Cazedo, José Valenzuela, Albino Caicedo; Rodríguez led the night round after spotting the foreign frigate.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Release of 3 neophytes (Francisco Esteban, Pío, Francisco Zerpa) of San Miguel who killed a fugitive Indian (Luis) in self-defense — Rodríguez thinks longer jail would set an example.

author: Rodríguez
people: M. Rodríguez; I. Martínez; J.J. Domínguez
summary: Habilitación handover to cadete Ignacio Martínez; ~$1,000 in debts owed by Los Ángeles vecinos (paying in seeds) — largest Juan José Domínguez ($300, pays in lard/cattle).

author: Francisco Mª Ruiz
summary: Retirement of soldier Antonio Sandoval (→ sargento retirado).

author: José de la Guerra
people: de la Guerra; J.J. de la Torre; J.J. Francisco
summary: Recruit accounts: de la Guerra advanced funds to recruits José Joaquín de la Torre ($139-5-6) & Juan José Francisco ($181) brought from México (their funds exhausted).

author: Fr. José Faura
summary: Certifies the cabo, soldiers & people did NOT fulfill the Easter precept this year.

author: José Góngora / Fr. Antonio Peyri
people: Góngora; Sepúlveda; Serrano; Yorba; Fr. A. Peyri
summary: Easter-precept roster, San Luis Rey: cabo José Antonio Góngora, Patricio Ontiveros; soldiers Francisco Sepúlveda, Juan Germán, Cayetano Duarte, Leandro Serrano; inválido Francisco Serrano + women (Ramona Serrano, Mª Antonia Rodríguez, Rafaela Serrano, Mª Vicenta, Presentación Yorba, Mª Silvas, Dominga Serrano). Fr. Peyri certifies compliance.

author: Juan Varela / Fr. José de Miguel
summary: Easter-precept roster, San Gabriel: cabo Juan Varela; soldiers Luis Manríquez, Juan Bermúdez, Juan Feliz, Estevan Pérez, Diego Lisalde (married).

author: Rodríguez
summary: Received the ship signal-plan (27 Jun); the Princesa assigned as coastal mail/patrol with recognition signals.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Pistols (from José Zúñiga via the late Antonio Frasera) belong to the habilitación, not the Rl Hacienda.

author: Rodríguez
summary: The Princesa as coastal mail (Saavedra); 200 arrobas for biscuits, oven built; supply accounts via San Blas.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Sandoval's replacement cédula (cf. C165).

author: Rodríguez
people: M. Rodríguez; J. Osuna; R. Saavedra; O'Cain
summary: Prisoner-exchange resolution: O'Cain had proposed swapping cabo Osuna + 2 soldiers for the 4 detained Americans; now Osuna is freed, no exchange needed. Osuna was imprudent (could have lost his whole party); more caution in future.

author: Rodríguez
places: San Diego; San Juan Capistrano; San Miguel
summary: Indian murderers: 2 Christian Indians (from the frontier) who killed soldiers — the mestizo León (San Juan Capistrano) & Luis (San Miguel, since died) — sent via Sta Bárbara to Monterey, with 2 more (Malaquías, Mauricio).

author: Rodríguez
summary: Death of a Christian Indian prisoner (Cosme, Mission San Diego), held for stoning an India to death; given last rites.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Instructions sought on pistols left by the late Antonio Frasera (their value to the Frasera estate).

author: Pablo de la Olsa
people: P. de la Olsa; M. Rodríguez
summary: Homicide investigation: Indians Gerónimo & Cayetano accuse Pío & Estevan of murdering the Christian Indian Luis (a frontier fugitive); the accused admit killing him long ago, body buried at Santa Rosa near the beach.

author: de la Olsa
summary: Exhumation of the Indian Luis's body (half decomposed); sends the accused Indians.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Self-defense ruling: the 3 Indians (Francisco Esteban, Pío, Francisco Zerpa) killed Luis in self-defense — he attacked with a bow; a stone by Estevan struck his stomach, killing him; body hidden in a cliff (4–5 months). Judged not liable (cf. C163).

author: Rodríguez
summary: Troop distribution per the 16 Feb augmentation plan.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Will send the population censuses (padrones) of the jurisdiction (not preserved).

author: Rodríguez
summary: Shaler schooner: a schooner (the same that came to San Luis Obispo, tied to Shaler) anchored at a river mouth; Sgt. Luis Pico + 5 soldiers sent to block contraband.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Sends a list of men for the artillery militia being raised.

author: Rodríguez
people: M. Rodríguez; M. Fernández; J. Roca
places: Pta de Guijarros; Los Ángeles; San Gabriel
summary: Artillery/militia: cabo Mariano Fernández to command the Punta de Guijarros battery; an artilleryman to drill the Los Ángeles militiamen Sundays; the little cannon (cañoncito) from San Gabriel moved to LA.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Cabo Mariano Fernández took charge of the Punta de Guijarros battery.

author: Rodríguez
people: M. Rodríguez; Kimball; J. Pico; P. Lobo
places: San Diego; San Juan Capistrano; Todos Santos
summary: Origin of the Peacock arrests + ship movements: 4 crewmen of the brig Peacock (Capt. Kimball), who came ashore at San Juan Capistrano on pretext of provisions, arrested by Sgt. Pico; the brig hovered off the presidio (out of cannon range) then coasted south via Todos Santos & the Carmen anchorage to the island by Santo Tomás, vanishing south.

author: Rodríguez
summary: A foreign ship found by Sgt. Pico's troop at a cove ('las Ranas') near the Santa Ana river; sailed south behind the islands.

author: Rodríguez
people: Thomas Kilby; Juan Pedro; M. Rodríguez
places: San Diego; Boston; NW Coast; Canton; Sandwich Islands; San Gabriel
summary: Interrogation of the 4 captured Peacock crewmen. 1st pilot Tomás Kilven (Thomas Kilby?) of Boston; contramaestre/interpreter Juan Pedro (Jean-Pierre) of Bordeaux; sailors Blas Sincam[?] & Blas Sama of Boston. The brig Peacock: 10 R tons, 6×4-pdr + 4 pedreros, 18 crew (14 left). Left Boston 14 Apr 1805 for the NW Coast to trade furs with the natives, thence to Canton; reached the Sandwich Islands 12 Feb (sweet potatoes/dried meat); the San Gabriel coast 19 May. Cargo: 6 bales of batiste, painting supplies, a box of haberdashery. Fed maize/beans/lard since 12 Apr.

author: José Mª Ruiz
summary: On the Luis killing: thanks for the news — Luis 'era hombre malo'; the killers 'no tienen mayor culpa.' (Closes the Luis homicide thread.)

author: Rodríguez
summary: Rl Hacienda cattle handover to new habilitado Ignacio Martínez: 88 head missing (Rodríguez covering from his own pocket) — loss from the year's sterility + Indians killing some for food amid seed/bean scarcity (cf. C140).

author: Rodríguez
summary: Year's upkeep accounts for the fort & flat barge at Punta de Guijarros.

author: José Lujan
people: J. Lujan; R. Carrillo; M. Rodríguez
summary: Long grievance memorial vs. Tenientes Carrillo & Rodríguez. Savage: 'tan lleno de disparates y confusión' — no historical value.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Order to recruit 18 men for the SD company; the LA comisionado to exhort youths (up to age 40) to enlist or face 'medidas vigorosas.'

author: Rodríguez
summary: Received 8 recruits; continue recruiting.

author: Lujan
summary: Demands an escort for the recruitment.

author: Varela
summary: Refuses the escort (will refer to the commander).

author: Lujan
summary: Charges Varela with disobedience (Savage: charges unintelligible).

author: Fr. José de Miguel
summary: Sympathy for Lujan's troubles; a room ready but unfurnishable.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Foreign two-masted ship at the Carmen anchorage (23 Feb, near Mission San Miguel) — cabo Pedro Lobo + 5 posted; unknown name/captain/nationality; suspicious (waiting for the guard to leave to trade).

author: Rodríguez
summary: Tithe (diezmo) accounts for 1805; no livestock tithe (year's sterility) — both years' to be collected.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Certifies the tithe on the Rl Hacienda cattle (205 head, $27.32).

author: Rodríguez
people: J. Pico; Maitorena; Fr. J. Sánchez
places: San Diego; San Luis Rey; San Miguel
summary: Exploration expedition (20 Jun): Sgt. José Pico, Alférez Maitorena, 1 cabo, 17 soldiers — to reconnoiter, apprehend cimarrones, & visit all rancherías 25–30 leagues out (San Luis Rey sierra → San Miguel); with Fr. José Sánchez + 3 Indian interpreters.

author: Rodríguez
summary: Written instructions for the exploration expedition (transcribing the Gov.'s 19 May order): befriend the gentiles so they 'lose their fear,' gather cimarrones, keep a daily diary of routes; strict orders the troop not to abuse the gentiles, take nothing, no contact with their women; 20–25 day expedition.

author: J.R. Carrillo
summary: Invalid cédula for soldier Javier Pico.

author: Carrillo
summary: SB company accounts (alcances/débitos, gratification fund, montepío militar, invalid fund) to end of 1805.

author: Luis Argüello (interim Comd. SF Cavalry)
people: L. Argüello; G. Moraga; L. Peralta; J.A. Sánchez; Gervasio Argüello
summary: Conduct report on SF company officers (per the Comd.-Gen.'s order): Gabriel Moraga (alférez graduado — valor, aplicación, capacidad, married); Sgt. Luis Peralta (valor, >mediana capacidad, married); Sgt. José Antonio Sánchez (mediana capacidad, married); Cadete Gervasio Argüello (full marks, married, holds the SF habilitación).

author: Henry S. Dearborn, Collector
summary: Passport of the ship *Atala* (American) issued by the Collector of Customs at Boston; Savage's translation largely illegible. [A U.S. vessel's papers preserved in the Spanish archive.]

author: Bernabé Cosío
summary: On consolidating the California gunpowder & playing-card monopoly sales (royal estancos) under the Guadalajara tobacco Factoría, administered by the Loreto & Monterey habilitados alongside tobacco; commission rates 8% (powder), 3–5% (cards); per the new *Ordenanza de Naipes*.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: DUPLICATE of C54 — anti-contraband decree (Savage: 'véase p.60').

author: Iturrigaray
summary: The 1807 situado: $82,741-5-4½ regulated for the Alta Cal. presidios + artillery piquete for next year's Memorias. (cf. C70's $63,206 for 1805.)

author: Iturrigaray
people: Iturrigaray; L. Argüello; Rafaela Sal; Caballero
summary: Royal approval of Luis Antonio Argüello's marriage to Rafaela Antonia de Sal (cf. C32) — children get no Monte Pío rights unless he dies in war.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Foreign-ship patrol: acknowledges reports of various foreign ships sighted on the coasts; supply ships useless for pursuit; proposes the brig *San Carlos* for corso against contraband-suspect ships.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Admiralty duties (derechos de almirantazgo) established for Godoy (Príncipe de la Paz, Generalísimo Almirante), collected at all ports of Spain & the Indies (via Min. Miguel Cayetano Soler).

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Royal order: artillery sub-inspectors as subdelegated judges for fines/penalties.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Royal order on deserters to allied foreign countries who return via embassies (4 yrs' added service).

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Royal order resolving an Artillery-vs-Navy jurisdiction dispute at Cartagena.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Royal order (22 Mar) approving the augmentation of presidial company officers/posts + 12 officer commissions (cf. C44/C45).

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Orders the gov. to investigate (discreetly) the commerce an American ship did at Monterey & San Francisco (denounced; not yet confirmed).

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Abolition of the 1805 anti-contraband bounty decrees (C54/C59): since neutral ships are now permitted to trade legally, clandestine trade will cease; revert to the 12 Jul 1802 instruction; only intercepted silver/gold bullion goes to captors.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Anti-foreign-ship patrol approved: the new brig *San Carlos* to be sent annually to Alta Cal. for corso against foreign ships, based at San Francisco (with the memorias frigate). (Implements C213.)

author: Iturrigaray
summary: The Council of Admiralty inaugurated in Madrid (4 Apr; cf. C214 Godoy).

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Royal order on constancy-premium documentation (filiaciones + certifications).

author: Iturrigaray
summary: Royal order: the means subaltern officers & their brides must prove to marry (both together, no fixed amount).

author: Manuel del Castillo Negrete
summary: Acknowledges the Monterey presidio surgeon's report on common diseases of troops & settlers (cf. C57).

author: Sub-tte Luis Roca
summary: Artilleros returning to México: embargo orders for the artillerymen to accompany him back; bring those not needed in Calif., incl. Mariano Fernández (promoted sgt-2º); ~4 individuals + Fernández.

author: Tte Luis Argüello
people: L. Argüello; J. Estudillo; G. Cantúa
summary: Mail-courier delays: addressing José Estudillo's complaint about couriers loitering 8–20 days in the Santa Clara escort; orders couriers held until the relay; soldier Guadalupe Cantúa cited.

author: Fr. Sánchez
summary: Hemp cost: distance to the embarkation point forces producers to pay transport (wagons carry only 8 arrobas of bulky hemp); asks 4 reales more per arroba.

author: Mariano Mercado
summary: Requests to travel by sea with his family (to save costs).

author: José de la Guerra
summary: Complains the habilitado Martínez withheld his chocolate/effects order; asks for relief.

author: Arrillaga
summary: DUPLICATE of C103 — Arrillaga's 'Preceptos Generales' (same as the 22 Dec 1806 San Diego one, pp 109–13).

author: Arrillaga
summary: Order on the timing of the Memorias to San Blas (Alta Cal. in Dec, Baja in Mar).

author: Arrillaga
people: Arrillaga; Marqués de Casa Irujo; Estudillo; L. Argüello
summary: British-expedition fears: relays the Viceroy's note (10 Feb) that the Marqués de Casa Irujo, Spanish ambassador in Washington, warned (2 Jan) of 'recelos sobre los destinos á que tal vez pueden dirigirse las expediciones ultramarinas de la Inglaterra.' Registered by Estudillo, L. Argüello, Carrillo, de la Guerra.

author: Arrillaga
summary: On the value/freight of 40 carbines + 50 pairs of pistols sent to Monterey in 1791 (per the Tiburón expediente).

author: Arrillaga
summary: Retirement cédula for soldier Manuel Montero.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Order 2 cases of gunpowder to SB (via the Princesa) for salutes.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Mixed: inventory of the late soldier Linares's estate; public punishment of a Christian Indian (of Sta Clara) for outrages against gentiles — shackled & put in the stocks for the gentiles to see, 3–4 blows; the inválido Julián Ríos's daughter brought to Monterey; an artillery militiaman imprisoned (Tte de Navío Tamariz to judge the Sáenz/La Torre matter).

author: José Rodríguez
people: J. Rodríguez; F. Machuca; J. Ríos
summary: The Branciforte Machuca affair: Florentino Machuca hid in inválido Julián Ríos's house (behind his daughter Hermenegilda's bed), struck Ríos & his wife, fled on a horse; resisted arrest (wounding a pursuer) before capture; the daughter fled, found 5 leagues off, detained at Branciforte.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Gunpowder accounts: powder for the Calif. presidios issued via the tobacco administrations (per Viceroy 27 Oct); strict oversight.

author: Varela
summary: DUPLICATE of C209 — powder/cards revenue (same as pp 190–5).

author: Arrillaga
summary: Hemp price set at +4 reales/arroba (per C228), paid by company habilitados from this harvest.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Interim retirement cédulas for soldiers Leocadio Cibrián & José Mª Armenta.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Saddle-maker Antonio Hernández traveling to Monterey.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Sends two relaciones from Capt. Carrillo re correspondence.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Artilleros (cabo La Torre, officer Sáenz) to embark to México with Sub-Lt Roca (cf. C226).

author: Arrillaga
summary: Embark all the hemp; get hemp seed from Fr. Domingo Iturrate.

author: Miguel Espinosa
summary: On a debt to his brother Salvador (to pay in effects).

author: Arrillaga
summary: José Argüello (SB) requests lead & powder; send what arrobas possible + lead sheets.

author: Arrillaga
people: Arrillaga; Ignacio Vallejo; Mariano Estrada; Alvarado
summary: Multi-topic: Sgt. Ignacio Vallejo (can't be stationed away); the Alvarado diary (2nd visit); punishment for an escort's negligence; couriers; hemp; lead for SB; Mariano Estrada named in a homicide case at SF (defense to be mounted).

author: Arrillaga
summary: Sgt. Ignacio Vallejo can't go to Monterey (busy with construction, rainy season near); repeats hemp-seed requests (Fr. Iturrate, the Comisionado), to ship via the Condestable José Sánchez at SB.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Lead for SB; settler flour; soldiers Miguel Archuleta & Rochín assigned to the San Miguel escort (Rochín untrustworthy — a wife-scandal); two ships seen at sea.

author: Arrillaga
people: J.V. Arista; P. Solá; M. Rodríguez
summary: Habilitación Gen. handover (Monterey portion): Solá's agent Arista delivered $5,901-1-11½ balance to Capt. Manuel Rodríguez.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Retirement cédulas for Cibrián & Armenta (cf. C242).

author: Arrillaga
summary: Document for soldado distinguido Carlos Carrillo (SD company).

author: Arrillaga
summary: Mariano Estrada to take charge of the Monterey company habilitación (under Estudillo) from year's end.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Cabo de artillería Mariano Mercado to take charge of the San Diego artillery castillo.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Asks the cause of a delayed dispatch.

author: Arrillaga
summary: References an SF lieutenant's oficio; asks for info.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Constancy-premium royal-order copy (cf. C223, p.205).

author: Arrillaga
summary: Re-transmits the 1807 neutral-ship-trade / anti-contraband order (= C220) for publication.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Royal order on the insignia (cintilla) for soldiers with constancy premiums.

author: Arrillaga
summary: English squadron off Panama: news from Cádiz (23 Oct) of an English squadron (1 navío, 2 frigates, smaller craft) with landing troops sighted off Panama; Calif. ports ordered vigilant.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Royal order on re-enlistment time-credit.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Royal order: arrested officers to report to their superior on release.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Re-transmits the almirantazgo order (= C214), registered at all presidios.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Re-transmits the English-expedition news (= C262), registered.

author: Arrillaga
summary: On collecting admiralty duties per the Almirantazgo cédula — a 2¼-real charge on foreign ships (resolving Veracruz queries).

author: Arrillaga
summary: Note of the Cruzada bulls delivered to Monterey presidio (714 of various classes).

author: Arrillaga
summary: Order to remit the presidio accounts for the year.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Announces Iturrigaray deposed, Garibay succeeded (cf. C110/C111).

author: Arrillaga
summary: Punishment of soldier José Norberto Archuleta — 3 yrs confined at SF, drummed out of the Monterey company.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Transfer of invalid soldier Manuel Higuera (Monterey → SF).

author: Arrillaga
summary: Royal order on time-credit for re-enlisted soldiers (cf. C263).

author: Arrillaga
summary: Royal order: Port Captains to attend the Junta de Sanidad; modifies the 1802 Matrículas ordinance.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Royal order on fines for soldiers caught at prohibited gambling by ordinary justice.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Invalid-pension proposals to note each soldier's *salidas, campaigns, reconnaissances* (as for officers).

author: Luis Argüello
summary: The 1808 SF earthquake swarm: since 21 Jun, 18 earthquakes felt; some so strong Argüello's house cracked (one room ruined); the Castillo de San Joaquín barracks threatens ruin — fears for the troops there.

author: Fr. Sánchez
summary: Hemp crops looking good at the SB pueblo & mission (~a vara tall, vigorous).

author: Sánchez
places: Sta Bárbara; Sta Cruz; San Juan Bautista
summary: Hemp inspection (from the Concepción): Sta Cruz mission hemp rotten/useless; San Juan Bautista's lacks processing (no skilled hands at San Blas), flax mixed in.

author: Estudillo
summary: San José harvest shortage: maize/bean crop short — settlers keep only what they need, the rest for the presidio troops; no grain to leave the pueblo until further order.
A printed document detailing events in Spain (the 1808 Napoleonic crisis).

author: Viceroy Garibay & sec. Manuel Merino
summary: Acknowledges the report of the SF earthquakes (cf. C277).

author: Garibay & Merino
summary: Hostile measures vs French ships & subjects: seize any French ship entering a Calif. port (Spain now at war with France) — capture commander/officers/crew, or conceal the war so the ship reaches Acapulco unsuspecting.

author: Garibay & Velázquez de León
summary: Voluntary war donations (for the war vs France): trusted persons to collect contributions, post proclamations, remit to the royal treasuries.

author: Rl Tribunal de Cuentas
summary: On measures for agriculture & commerce in California (flax/hemp payment scheme; growers paid via San Blas habilitaciones / mission Fathers' loans). [ink-blot defect on n243]

author: Garibay
summary: Recognition/acclamation of Fernando VII as King (done in México).

author: Garibay
summary: On the use of a signature stamp (estampilla).

author: Garibay
summary: Peace with England proclaimed (1808) — the alliance flip: Spain & Britain now allied vs Napoleonic France.

author: Iturrigaray
summary: A general indulto (pardon) on the occasion of Fernando VII's acclamation.

author: Velázquez de León (sec.)
people: Velázquez de León; Iturrigaray; Fernando VII
summary: Certified act of the Junta General de Autoridades (México, 9 Aug 1808, presided by Iturrigaray): recognizes Fernando VII's authority, nullifies acts of his 'representatives' (Napoleon's regime), names the Viceroy first representative. [Prelude to the Sept 1808 coup.]

author: Iturrigaray
summary: The 1809 situado: $86,012-5-5 for the four Nueva Cal. presidios + artillery piquete. (cf. $63k 1805 / $82k 1807.)

author: Iturrigaray
summary: On the anchorage duty (derecho de anclaje): commerce ships from Europe/non-favored American ports pay 2 pesos at Veracruz; nothing charged at Acapulco/San Blas/the Californias — Tribunal recommends no change.

author: Viceroy Garibay & Merino
summary: The American frigate 'Dromo' (Capt. Davis): outfitted in Boston for Pacific contraband, found at Guaymas selling goods, heading to Mazatlán. Detailed seizure orders: if it reaches the coast, detain it (unship the rudder), inventory the cargo, arrest captain/master/supercargo/crew, seize navigation papers, take separate depositions to expose its purpose — contra the King's orders & the treaties of peace/limits/navigation with the United States.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Promotion of cabo Miguel Espinosa to sergeant.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Artillery transfers: Mendoza, Villaverde, Espinosa → SF; Guerrero, Peña, Armas → Monterey.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Approves Miguel Espinosa → sgt, José Castillo → cabo (cf. C294).

author: Arrillaga
people: M. Espinosa; J. Alviso; J. Castro
summary: Expedition under Sgt. Miguel Espinosa (cabo + 10 soldiers, 20 days) to reconnoiter a ranchería — with a named Monterey-company roster (Cornelio Rosales, Lucas Altamirano, Isidoro Soto, Juan Arroyo, Hermenegildo Vázquez, Simón Castro, Ventura Amézaga, Juan Alviso, José Castro, M. Boronda).

author: Arrillaga
summary: Indians from the Russian establishments: two men + a woman from the Russian establishments, sent by the SF commander to Monterey — keep the men in the barracks, the india as convenient; pay 1½ reales daily but have them work with the convicts (axe/earth) as Vallejo directs. [First Russian-establishment doc in the systematic read — ties to the Kuskov/Bodega 1808–09 presence.]

author: Arrillaga
summary: War donations: the Calif. presidio companies contributed $1,889-2½ (loyalty to Fernando VII / war vs France); the Viceroy accepted 'estas demostraciones de patriotismo.' (cf. C284.)

author: Arrillaga
summary: The Archbishop of México (Lizana) assumed the viceroyalty (succeeding Garibay), conferred by Fernando VII. [Rapid 1808–09 viceregal turnover.]

author: Arrillaga
summary: Boundary dispute between San José pueblo & mission (Estudillo's talks with the Padres & Comisionado pending).

author: Arrillaga
places: San José; Sta Cruz; Sta Clara
summary: Grain scarcity: maize short for the troops; settlers can't be forced to haul it (oxen needed for planting); the missions have little (Sta Cruz nothing, San José little, Sta Clara doubtful) — supply the troops with flour. (cf. C280.)

author: Arrillaga
summary: Punishment of soldier Pedro García (scandal with Juan Arroyo's wife) — corporal punishment (palos) 'uncommon in the province'; García to be posted to an escort outside the presidio as an example.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Release vecino Mateo Chávez (2 mo. prison); soldier José Antonio Alba in the stocks.

author: Arrillaga
summary: Advice to Alférez Pedro Amador on disciplining his son (gentle vs harsh).

author: Archbishop-Viceroy
summary: The Marqués de Astorga named president of the Junta Suprema Gubernativa (succeeding Floridablanca).

author: Fr.[?] Argüello
summary: Hemp: the *Princesa* couldn't carry all; part deposited; La Purísima mission hemp. (Dup.)

author: Arrillaga
summary: On the law for shipping silver: the Baja missions shipped 73 marcos to San Blas without registry. (Dup.)

author: José Argüello
summary: A libranza for $682-3-2½. (Dup.)

author: José Argüello
summary: Two old India women sent by Fr. Amestoy (SB minister) for safekeeping; whether to send them to San Diego on the *Princesa*. (Dup.)

author: José Argüello
summary: Hemp logistics: 229 tercios (1158 arrobas) deposited at SB mission; 104 tercios (3329 arrobas) already shipped. (Dup.)

author: Condestable José Sánchez
summary: Rejected hemp: 30 tercios from La Purísima useless (rotten, mixed with flax & straw). (Dup; cf. C279.)

author: Soto
summary: LA settlers protest the hemp program: the commissioner (José Sánchez) wants to sow 460+ fanegas of hemp; the vecindario objects (water/land needed for maize/beans; Sánchez partners with non-resident Manuel Verdugo at 24 parajes). (Dup.)

author: Tte Raymundo Carrillo
summary: Hemp value >$20,000 expected this year; the habilitación lacks funds to pay it all; the commissioner wants pay for his work. (Dup.)

author: José Argüello
summary: Easter-precept certification (LA/SB; Fr. Zalvidea). (Dup; cf. C167–169.)

author: José Sánchez
summary: Hemp shipped/deposited (3329 arrobas on the *Princesa*). (Dup of C311.)

author: Fr. José Mª Ruiz
summary: Deathbed will (in artículo mortis) of Capt. Luis Raymundo Carrillo — confirms Carrillo died late 1809. (Dup.)

author: Estudillo
summary: Defense of soldier Gabriel Cota concluded. (Dup.)

author: Estudillo
summary: Soldier José Mª Pérez discharged; Pelteran to the San Juan Bautista escort; the two soldiers' women sent to the villa / Soledad. (Dup.)

author: José Mariano Estrada (certifies)
people: Arrillaga; Fr. Tapis; Fr. Amorós; Fr. Sarría; Quijano; M. Estrada
summary: Arrillaga's oath of office as Governor: at Mission San Carlos (Carmel), 5 pm, kneeling before a crucifix with one hand on the Gospels & the other on his sword-cross, Arrillaga swore fidelity to Fernando VII & to defend the Crown's rights — before padres Tapis, Amorós, Sarría, surgeon Quijano & cadete J.R. Estrada. (Dup.)

author: Argüello
summary: Response to the LA hemp protest (C313): the gov. backs the program; Sánchez may sow 40 fanegas, leaving land/water for other crops. (Dup.)

author: Carrillo
summary: Indian punishment: an Indian found with a slaughtered cow + a horse stolen from the mission (with gentiles), whip-marks discovered; asks how long to imprison. (Dup.)

author: Argüello
summary: News re Archbishop Lizana (viceroy). (Dup.)

author: Argüello
summary: Forwards the LA hemp petition + resolution + documents (Dup of C313/C321).

author: Fr. Argüello
summary: Mission escort sizes (Sta Inés/La Purísima; 9-man detachment → cabo + 5). (Dup.)
Receipt for the habilitación balance. (Dup.)

author: Estudillo
summary: The San José pueblo vs Sta Clara mission boundary dispute (Las Calaveras paraje — mission cattle on land the pueblo claims). (Dup of C301.)

author: Tribunal de Cuentas
summary: Commerce-registry rule: anything embarked outside registry is forfeit (comiso irremisible) — even from missions; the *Carmen* sloop's 73 marcos of silver excess; circulate to all CA ports. (Dup of C308.)

author: Russian-American Company Directorate
people: RAC Directorate; Rezanov; Baranov; Alexander I
summary: RAC trade overture (the 'RAC Proclamation'): the RAC directorate (under Tsar Alexander I) addresses 'our friends and neighbors the noble and valiant Spaniards, inhabitants of the Californias,' recalls Rezanov's 1806 SF visit, proposes to establish commercial relations, and names Alexander Baranov (RAC governor, Kodiak/Sitka) to send a ship with merchandise to trade for grain, tallow, cattle; plans a second ship from St. Petersburg 'once Europe's war allows.' (A Latin copy follows; this copy marked Dup.)

author: José Sánchez
people: J. Sánchez; A. Ávila; A. Yorba; C. López
summary: 1810 hemp shipment relation: by mission (San Gabriel 623 arrobas, San Fernando 304, Sta Inés 520, San Buenaventura 396, San Luis Obispo 81) & by settler (Anastasio Ávila, Antonio Yorba, Claudio López, José Nieto …); total 4,802 arrobas shipped, 3,950 left.

author: Fr. J.Mª Ruiz
summary: Provided aid to the schooner '[del Rey] / la Moscú[?].'

author: Argüello
summary: Otter-fishing canoes: ~20+ fishing canoes sighted heading to the San José esteros (up to 22); SF Christian Indians report many fishing otters; gentiles say a large ship is at Bodega.

author: Argüello
summary: SF buildings deplorable: storms wrecked the *tercio* warehouse + 4 family quarters + old barracks; Castillo de San Joaquín's merlons & esplanade destroyed, artillery barracks in the worst state; no masons/carpenters/smiths or materials; the clothing warehouse broken into & robbed (rotten timbers, thatch roof).

author: Fr. J.Mª Ruiz
summary: Orders to arrest emissaries of Joseph Bonaparte ('José Napoleón') who reached the US to incite the Americas to revolt; remit them to Monterey (per Archbishop-Viceroy Lizana).

author: José Argüello
summary: Hemp proration — each mission's delivery (San Gabriel 600, San Fernando 300, San Buenaventura 400, Sta Inés 300…); total 4,500 arrobas (2000 Rl Hacienda + 1340 vecinos + 1160 SD).

author: José Sánchez
summary: Hemp shipped on the *Princesa*: 504 tercios (2556 arrobas) + 229 from last year = 3,796 arrobas.

author: José Sánchez
summary: 1810 hemp sowing: ~490 fanegas planted (pueblo 100, San Gabriel 100, San Fernando 36, San Buenaventura 52, Sta Bárbara 25, Sta Inés 57, + Río Sta Ana / rancho Nieto / SD ~40).

author: José Argüello
summary: Hemp embarked on the *Princesa* for San Diego (19th): 1618 arrobas + Monterey's 181 = 3,820 arrobas.

author: José Argüello
summary: Hemp contribution table by mission (San Gabriel 623, Sta Inés 520, San Buenaventura 396…) & settler (Ávila, Yorba, López), per contador Benito Díaz Fernández de la Vega. [n289 = BLACK/defective scan — NEEDS RE-READ; n290 = blotted dup of n288]

author: Viceroy Venegas
summary: Smallpox-vaccination reglamento: circulates Fr. Javier de Balmis's regulation (México, 10 Oct 1810) to propagate & perpetuate the vaccine in New Spain; orders compliance.

author: Rl Tribunal de Cuentas
summary: Decree re the estate of cabo de mar José Ant. Archuleta (San Blas).

author: Viceroy Venegas
summary: Death of Habilitado Gen. Capt. Manuel Rodríguez; Ignacio de Ormaechea named interim; recalls the $6,000-bond requirement (R.O. 14 May 1791); the CA officers to elect a propietario.

author: Venegas
summary: Circulates 2 royal orders: a Minister-of-State appointment + news of the revolution of Caracas (1810).

author: Venegas
summary: Otter-fishing: the skins confiscated from the American frigate *Alexander* were rotten (dumped at sea); CA vecinos may continue otter-fishing freely; vigilance vs fraud.

author: Venegas
summary: Royal order: military trials reviewed by auditors within 24 hours.

author: Venegas
summary: Elections of deputies to the Cortes (of Cádiz, convening at Isla de León).

author: Venegas
summary: Proclamation on the political events (peace/obedience) — the 1810 Hidalgo-revolt context. (printed)

author: Venegas
summary: Query re a debt of pilot Juan Carrasco ($226).

author: Rl Audiencia Gobernadora
summary: The Regency named Nicolás Mª de Sierra Secretary of Grace & Justice.

author: Rl Audiencia Gobernadora
summary: Hermida ceased as Minister of Grace & Justice.

author: Rl Audiencia Gobernadora
summary: Authorization to buy artillery — replace the ports' useless cannon (from Philippine/American ships or the Philippine govt; carriages at San Blas).

author: Carlos de Urrutia
summary: Powder & supplies to Spain (Cádiz/Cortes): 3000 quintales powder, 1000 muskets, shoes, hats, etc.

author: Rl Audiencia Gobernadora
summary: War donations for Spain's independence — solicit from California's inhabitants (via Veracruz). (cf. C284, C299.)

author: Rl Audiencia Gobernadora
summary: Bardají y Azara named Minister of War (Spain).

author: Rl Aud. Gobernadora
summary: Min. of War Bardají authorized a half-signature.

author: Rl Aud. Gobernadora
summary: The Regency's residency extended 6 months.

author: Pedro Catani
summary: Query re people sent to Monterey from San Blas in 1803.

author: Catani
summary: Bull accounts 1804–1805.

author: Catani
summary: Bull accounts 1808–1809.

author: Rl Aud. Gobernadora
summary: Regency instructions on half- vs full signature.

author: Catani
summary: Artilleryman José Ant. Archuleta to settle as poblador; accounts.

author: Rl Aud. Gobernadora
summary: Bardají y Azara named 1st Secretary of State (dup of C354).

author: Catani
summary: Bull sales: Habilitado Gen. to receive the Cruzada summaries lacking at some CA presidios.

author: Tesorería Gen.
summary: Soldier Manuel Mendoza (Monterey) retirement at $8/mo — no grievance.

author: Catani
summary: Query re the cost/freight of carbines & pistols sent to CA presidios in 1807.

author: Rl Aud. Gobernadora
summary: Public burning (by the executioner) of a proclamation by Joseph Bonaparte + insulting documents; published San Carlos/Monterey Nov 1810.

author: José Ant. Roda
summary: Declaration on events in Caracas.

author: Archbishop-Viceroy
summary: Almirantazgo duties to continue (Junta Central), product to the treasury. (cf. C214.)

author: Archbishop-Viceroy
summary: Printed proclamation 'against the perfidious deceits of the Bonapartes.'

author: Archbishop-Viceroy
summary: Printed proclamation to Fernando VII's loyal vassals against Napoleon Bonaparte.

author: Archbishop-Viceroy
summary: The hemp commissioner Joaquín Sánchez's retirement (artillery cabo) elevated to the King; he wants to dedicate himself to hemp cultivation.

author: Archbishop-Viceroy
summary: Hemp payment to mission síndicos: 1074 arrobas at 4 pesos (provisional), via contador Benito Díaz de la Vega.

author: Velázquez de León (sec.)
summary: List of 'Joseph Bonaparte's agents in the two Americas' — named suspected Napoleonic commissioners by territory (México, Veracruz, Acapulco/San Blas, Sonora, Guadalajara, Alta & Baja California [Santiago Tarsons], Sta Fe, Lima, Guatemala…); some entering via New Orleans.

author: José Argüello
people: J. Argüello; A. Ávila; Fr. P. Muñoz
summary: LA water dispute: the vecinos of Los Ángeles complain the San Fernando mission padres built a dam at Cahuenga rancho cutting the river the pueblo draws from; padres deny intent to harm; declarations taken (regidor Anastasio Ávila).

author: Viceroy Venegas
summary: Cortes' order (5 Dec 1810) vs 'the invader's cunning' (Napoleon); rogativas ordered. (printed)

author: Fr. J.Mª Ruiz
people: J.Mª Ruiz; Gabriel Moraga; J. Pico
places: San Diego; San Gabriel; Colorado River
summary: Colorado River Indian movement + Moraga's measures: gave Gabriel Moraga troops/munitions (San Gabriel/San Juan Cap./San Luis Rey companies); lookouts watch the 'indios del Colorado'. Moraga's expedition brought in 2 Indians (one a 'capitán de recoletura') + 11 horses + 61 from rancho de los Nietos + 2 cimarrones.

author: Argüello
summary: Otter-canoes withdrew; no ships at Bodega; a small boat found beached at San Pedro (good condition); no corpse found (unlike one earlier at Sta Cruz).

author: José Argüello
summary: SB building repairs (materials scarce; Moraga's prisoners old/sick; troops doing the cutting/hauling).

author: Fr. J.Mª Ruiz
summary: List of Indian prisoners at the presidio.

author: Venegas
summary: Bando prohibiting short weapons.

author: Venegas
summary: Cortes decree: deputies inviolable. (printed)

author: Venegas
summary: Royal order: Fernando VII's acts null while captive.

author: Venegas
summary: On the Habilitación Gen. (the propietario's travel to México).

author: Venegas
summary: Indulto extended for reos de infidencia (treason convicts).

author: Venegas
summary: Declares the army beneméritos de la patria (for restoring order).

author: Venegas
summary: Allocution vs revolutionary guerrillas, promising protection to the peaceful. (printed)

author: Venegas
summary: A denunciation to investigate (gov. as commissioner).

author: Venegas
summary: Bando permitting the manufacture & free use of mescal kingdom-wide.

author: (printed)
summary: The Regency's attributions.

author: Venegas
people: Gabriel Moraga; J. Herrera; F. Soto; A. Briones; V. Zúñiga
summary: Approval of Alférez Gabriel Moraga's military action (22 May 1810) vs the gentiles (17 men): Moraga → Teniente de Caballería; cabos José Herrera & Francisco Soto (wounded) → sergeant; soldiers Antonio Briones & Ventura Zúñiga (wounded) → a month's bonus.

author: Venegas
summary: Ignacio Pezuela named interim Secretary of Grace & Justice (Spain).

author: Venegas
summary: Prohibition of mistreatment of convicts by presidio overseers (cómitres); they're to be punished for excesses.

author: Venegas
summary: Abolition of seignorial privileges (señorío/vasallaje) — the Cortes decree (6 Aug).

author: Venegas
summary: Royal decree: obligations to foreign powers honored even in war. (printed)

author: Venegas
summary: Bando on Spanish prisoners in France. [n322 = dup scan of Sav 315]

author: Venegas
summary: Royal order: governors may grant marriage licenses to Montepío contributors (no dispensing requirements).

author: José Manuel Ruiz
summary: Indictment of the Baja Cal. governor (Goycoechea): tyranny & corruption — contraband with foreign ships, selling foreign goods at his private Loreto stores (profiting off the royal warehouse), forcing troops to buy there, taking the soldiers' rations & cigarettes to resell, selling the troops' munitions to traders, gambling, troops & families in misery, demoralization; only *gachupines* employed; the Padre Presidente indifferent.

author: Fr. J.Mª Ruiz
summary: San Diego fortification on news of the Mexican insurgency ('revolución de la otra banda'); armed the port; a seditious paper found on the beach; the people show 'no malice.'

author: Luis Argüello
summary: Otter-fishers in SF Bay (June–July): cayucos off San Mateo & by the huichunes/carquines (San Pablo/Carquinez); no ship seen on the coast to Bodega; possibly an American ship beached beyond Bodega (likely wrecked).

author: Argüello
summary: Death of Padre Fr. Andrés Quintana (Mission Santa Cruz) — found dead in his bed; the escort cabo examined the body, no signs of violent death. [The Quintana case — later revealed as a neophyte murder.]

author: José Argüello
summary: List of artillery militiamen who served during the Gabrielino Indian uprising (cabo José Bartolomé Tapia). [→ see C403]

author: Goycoechea
summary: Habilitado Gen. election: relays Manuel Rodríguez's death, Ormaechea interim, de la Guerra elected; officers to elect a propietario + 2 substitutes (Fernando de la Toba, Estanislao Salgado…).

author: José Argüello
people: J. Argüello; J.B. Tapia; G. Cota; G. Moraga
summary: The Gabrielino uprising (28 Oct 1810–4 Jan 1811): LA militia/invalids/vecinos served when the Christian Indians of San Gabriel + gentiles tried to attack the mission & pueblo; cabo José Bartolomé Tapia donated $150; full militia roster (Claudio López, the Ávilas, Verdugo, Higuera, Nieto…). Under Sgt. Guillermo Cota until Alférez Moraga stood them down.

author: Calleja
summary: Forwards a printed copy of the bando promulgating the Constitución Política de la Monarquía (the 1812 Cádiz Constitution).

author: Calleja
places: Bodega; Onalaska; Kodiak; Juan de Fuca; Veliky Luki
summary: 'Sobre los rusos en Cal.'
