A foreign nation, apparently Russian, has established at the puerto de la Bodega — a fortification with artillery and ~100 individuals, mostly Russians from the establishments at Onalaska & Kodiak, agents of the Russian [American] Company, with the aim of opening commerce with the provinces; cites the treaty of alliance signed at Veliky Luki, 8(20) July 1812. Establishing in Spanish territory (the northern coast up to the entrance of Juca [Juan de Fuca]) violates the territory & treaties. Orders relayed to the Comandantes Generales of Nueva Galicia & the Provincias Internas (Baja Cal & Sonora) and the Comandante of San Blas.

author: Manuel Ruiz
people: Manuel Ruiz; Alexander I; Fernando VII
summary: On the prohibition of foreign commerce with the Californias: the three oficios written in three languages (1810) by the Russian Emperor Alexander I; in reply, the governments of Alta & Baja Cal would permit no new dominion nor commerce with any power, being forbidden by law — only Fernando VII (now the Real Junta de Regencia) could grant it; 'siempre que S.M. lo permita… obedeceremos sus órdenes como buenos vasallos.'

author: Calleja
summary: British warships in the Pacific: relays Regency order (25th) — the English Ambassador announced Britain will send 1–2 warships to the Pacific to protect its whalers in the war with the U.S.; Spanish ports to give them good reception, attend the officers/crew, help curb desertion of British sailors, but watch their conduct & prevent contact with 'los descontentos.' (Estudillo copy.)

author: Calleja
summary: Neutrality follow-up: re British complaints, relays Royal order of 9 Apr 1813 that authorities not obstruct Anglo-American privateers, and dispose of English prizes in their ports. (Estudillo copy.)

author: Calleja & interim Sec. Ignacio Aguayo
summary: Printed circular: official papers must use the Constitution's terminology — referring to 'la España ultramarina,' not 'la europea.' (4 copies enclosed.)

author: José Argüello
summary: 1812–13 earthquakes: the 8 March shock was the last felt; cracks oozing chapapote (tar) in the Sierra de San Bernardo, at Lomas, and in the Tulares; a coastal volcano gained new vents + another reported behind the Sierra de los Pinos — Argüello infers subterranean tar 'boiling with sulfur' at the quake's center; the presidio chapel just finished (palisade, tile-roofed, 18 × 6 varas); the alférez's & his own houses still need roofing & plastering.

author: Joaquín Sánchez
summary: Earthquakes: people still fleeing the tremors 'que habían tomado asiento en Santa Bárbara'; 'no son más que lamentaciones.' (Part of the 1812 Califonia earthquake sequence.)

author: Argüello
summary: Supply crisis — no resources to sustain troops & families, the Memorias deficient; 'ya el chapote (la langosta) empieza á presentarse' (locust appearing).

author: Sgt. Martínez
summary: Earthquakes left him 'medio convulso'; planning a trip in April to see if the fear subsides — meanwhile 'pienso dormir en tienda de campaña.'

author: Argüello
summary: Earthquakes damaged the presidio more than the mission; Argüello proposes relocating the Santa Bárbara presidio higher up the mission road (higher, firmer ground), saving the cost of demolishing the present works.

author: Argüello
summary: Departure of his lieutenant + particular matters; Padre Calzada marched to Mission San Luis as supernumerary.

author: Argüello
places: Santa Bárbara; Los Berros; La Purísima
summary: Mission relocation (post-quake): on Padre Mariano Payeras's request to move the mission [La Purísima] to the paraje de los Berros — suitable (a more direct commercial/travel road, nearer San Luis) though water-poor; the Padre would keep the *ramblas* a league off; asks the Padre for laborers.

author: Luis Argüello
people: Gabriel Moraga; Kuskov (Coscof); Rezanof; Luis Argüello
summary: 'Visita á los rusos': Teniente Gabriel Moraga visited the Russians north of Bodega, returning 27 Jan without incident; infers their object was trade in provisions on Rezanof's terms; quotes a paper Kuskov ('Coscof') handed Moraga; woodcutters across the port brought in a deserter (fled harsh punishment); Kuskov asked Moraga to return Russian deserters; vivid troop-nakedness report ('bastantes soldados no tienen camisa… ni chupa ni calzones… la desnudez y la miseria me la está experimentando').

author: Argüello
places: SF; Mission San José; Sacramento–SJ Delta
summary: 'Expedición militar — Combate con los indios': 22 Oct, 10 SF-presidio soldiers (mission launch) joined Sgt. Francisco Soto (from Mission San José; 2 soldiers + 100 Indian auxiliaries) to recover San José-mission cimarrones; night river navigation; reached a large, wooded, swampy island ranchería at dawn the 27th. Forewarned, the cimarrones had massed 4 rancherías (~1,000 armed Indians), posted sentinels, sent away women/children, and put up 'porfiada resistencia,' attacking from all sides. ~3-hour fight, driven off 'á vivo fuego'; the swamp (water knee-deep) let them swim to another Tulares island & escape — none captured but 'bien escarmentados.' The Indians had taunted that 'soldiers are worth nothing on foot, only on horseback,' then learned otherwise. Spanish loss: one auxiliary, 'Julio,' killed + one lightly wounded; heavy enemy loss. Soto praises the auxiliaries' obedience & valor. [→ military-history register.]

author: Nicolás Noé
summary: Prize law: how prizes taken from contrabandists are distributed under the Mar del Sur regulation — contraband ship + cargo appraised, sold at public auction, a share to the corsair's *armadores*, the rest to the crew by class; prisoners to the castillo; captains/pilots lodged by the armadores, crews maintained by the King; after declarations, repeat-offender 'pirates' shipped out via Cabo de Hornos / Europe, sometimes via Panamá.

author: Argüello
summary: Per the Governor's order, furnish volunteers to garrison the frigate *Mercurio* (Don Nicolás Noé) from San Pedro to Monterey; the ship to collect 10 cannon left at San Pedro the prior year.

author: Argüello
summary: Aid to the frigate *Flora*: the *gratificación* to be paid if the vecindario must be called up to help.

author: Argüello
places: Santa Bárbara; San Pedro; San Diego; Monterey; Channel Islands; R°. Refugio
summary: 'Buque corsario contra los contrabandistas': Capt. Nicolás Noé will leave the *Flora* at San Pedro and prizes at San Diego with the *Mercurio*, then scour the coast to Monterey; 3 ships reported in the [Channel] islands off rancho Refugio + others off the presidio; lent Noé 2 presidio cannon; recommends moving 'Capitán Jorge' + the most suspect sailors to Monterey lest they league with the island gentiles & the 'Americanos' who keep raiding the islands.

author: Arrillaga
summary: 'Captura de fragata *Mercurio*': Arrillaga's order to investigate Noé's prize — reconnoiter the captured Anglo-American frigate *Mercurio* ('del mando del que llaman Jorge, que tantos años anda contrabandeando por estas costas'), make an exact inventory, take declarations from Capt. Jorge & the crew, establish their purpose on the coast & whether other company ships are about, and report by duplicate.

author: Argüello
summary: Earthquakes — 'Sufrimiento de la guarnición. Condición del presidio': the 21 Dec tremors still continuing; the garrison suffering immense labor in the weather; asks the Government to help rebuild the presidio (or build anew); describes the buildings' bad state; proposes using the indios presos at Monterey + a *gratificación* (~$4,000), the troop employed throughout.

author: Pedro Negrete
summary: Fiscal query: whether the duties the Viceroy imposed conform to those expected for 1815.

author: José de la Guerra y Noriega
people: José de la Guerra y Noriega; John Elliot de Castro; Anastasio Carrillo
places: Santa Bárbara; Refugio; Monterey; Fort Ross
summary: 'Conducción de presos rusos': the Englishman Don Juan Eliot [John Elliot de Castro] and six more foreigners, captured aboard the Russian corvette *Ilmena* in the ensenada del Refugio, are to be conducted to Monterey with all speed by the Governor's order; asks the missions to furnish *bagajes* & aid to cabo Anastasio Carrillo who leads the escort.

author: La Guerra (Habilitado)
people: La Guerra; G.W. Eayrs; J. Argüello
summary: Provisioning costs of the *Mercurio* prisoners: per the Consejo de Presidios order (18 Sep 1813), the Habilitación supported Eayrs & crew to embarkation — the Captain $406, the others $285.4 r.; Eayrs credited $1/day from 2 June (his capture) to 21 [Oct], when he embarked for San Blas; the 4 sailors at 1½ r./day from 30 Sep (arrival at Sta Bárbara) to the 17th, when received aboard the *Tagle*[?].

author: Arrillaga
summary: Fr. José Señán recognized as Vicario Foráneo: Señán presented the título conceded by the Bishop of Sonora; relayed to the presidio commanders so all recognize & obey him. (Missionaries' acknowledgments listed.)

author: Calleja
summary: Legal ruling on the *Mercurio* seizure: the *Flora* could not lawfully seize her as a corsair — exercising the *derecho de corso* requires a declared state of war between two states; the real question is 'el comercio ilícito que el Capt. de la *Mercurio* protegió en costas de Cal.'; the San Blas marine command has no jurisdiction here.

author: Iván Kuskov
people: Iván Kuskov; Gabriel Moraga; Iván Pisteff (transl.)
summary: KUSKOV'S OWN LETTER (English translation by Iván Pisteff). As 'Commercial Councillor' of the Honorable Russian-American Company, under special protection of His Majesty the Emperor of all the Russias: received the Gov.'s April letter via Moraga but couldn't act (no interpreter, can't read Spanish); offers the Gov. a present (a tent his men left at SF); asks the return of the Kodiak men captured at San Francisco (they 'did no wrong,' only saved themselves from the surf) + 2 men & 2 women Kodiaks detained in 1813.

author: Iván Kuskov
people: Iván Kuskov; Slobodchikov; Moraga; Luis Argüello; Iván Pisteff
summary: Letter (English translation, Iván Pisteff transl.): received the French-language letter via Moraga & Gervasio Argüello but had no interpreter; sends the RAC confidential agent Slobodchikov to San Francisco with a boat & 3 bidarkas to settle the balance due, offering goods per enclosed invoice or 2 oxen; again asks return of the Kodiak men — if Arrillaga has truly ordered it, forward them; else 'it is my duty to inform my superior officers and the Home Government'; forwards the tent & enclosed letter to the Monterey commander.

author: Argüello
summary: Aid to HMS *Racoon*: gave Capt. Black 42 arrobas of gunpowder (to be restored if unconsumed at a Spanish port); the *Racoon* left the 19th for Monterey, then 'de allí en derechura á las islas de Sandwich en persecución de los Anglo-Américanos.'

author: Capt. W. Black (HMS *Racoon*)
summary: (English) Reports desertion of 8 men from the Brit. ship *Isaac Todd*; requests their capture & delivery (with other British subjects in the district) to his ship; the *Racoon* short of men from sickness & casualties.

author: Frazer Smith (master, *Isaac Todd*)
summary: Reports desertion of some crew; requests capture & delivery to HMS *Racoon* at SF. (in very bad French.)

author: [R.] Inman, Surgeon (*Isaac Todd*)
summary: Certifies no infectious disease aboard; 3 crew have scurvy.

author: Donald McTavish
summary: About sailors' wages.

author: Capt. [Frazer] Smith (*Isaac Todd*)
summary: Asking friendship, water & provisions; no contagious disease aboard.

author: José de la Cruz
people: José de la Cruz; Pablo Vicente de Solá
summary: Congratulates Solá on his arrival; notes the difficulties of sending succor to the presidios.

author: Col. Solá
people: Solá; Calleja; Ormaechea; Estudillo; de la Guerra; F.M. Ruiz
summary: Per Viceroy Calleja's 26 Jan order, presidios are henceforth to request their *memorias* from Guadalajara (can't be supplied, 'por causa de los sucesos políticos'); Ormaechea unable to relocate — appoint a new Habilitado General. Ackn.: Estudillo (Monterey 10 Oct), de la Guerra (Sta Bárbara 13), Francisco Mª Ruiz (San Diego 17).

author: Gov. Solá
people: Pablo Vicente de Solá; Calleja; José de la Cruz; José Argüello; Estudillo; de la Guerra; Ruiz
summary: 'Toma de posesión de gobierno político y militar': Solá notifies his appointment by Viceroy Calleja (31 Dec 1814) as Governor & Inspector of Cal. troops; swore before Comdt.-Gen. of Nueva Galicia José de la Cruz; arrived Monterey 30 Aug 1815 aboard the frigate *Paz y Religión* (sent by interim Gov. Capt. José Argüello). Recognized by all presidios (de la Guerra had the troops recognize him 2 Oct; Ruiz at San Diego 8 Oct).

author: Estudillo
summary: Have Solá recognized as Gov./Inspector — via Sebastián Rodríguez, José Joaquín de la Torre, Juan Arroyo (San Miguel).

author: Marcos Briones
summary: Remits a list of the vecinos' goods & tools.

author: Estudillo
summary: Have the invalids & settlers recognize Solá — at Branciforte & San José (Luis Peralta).

author: José de la Cruz
people: José de la Cruz; G.W. Eayrs
summary: Per the reports, acted on provisioning Jorge Hayes [Eayrs] & holding the corvette *Mercurio* pending the Govt's ruling on the seizure.

author: Vallejo
people: Vallejo; Zavala; G.W. Eayrs; Noé; Argüello
summary: Legal dictamen on the *Mercurio*: re Eayrs (owner, seized in the SB Channel by Noé); provisioning for him + family + 4 sailors; proposes selling the corvette to defray costs; $7,700 of effects deposited in the San Blas treasury, >$400 with Gov. Argüello; witness Francisco Mira[?].

author: Lic. González de Velasco
summary: Dictamen: provision Eayrs, sell the corvette, and take the declaration of Francisco Mira[?].

author: José de la Cruz
summary: On the provisions Eayrs requested (cross-ref tomo p. 374). [dup-ish]

author: Zavala
summary: Certified copy of the *Mercurio* dictamen (cross-ref tomo p. 374). [dup]

author: José Argüello
summary: Relays a reserved royal order re the apprehension of [Mariscal Núñez?] & those accompanying him, to render them to 'su Rey y á su Patria.' [text partly unclear]

author: Luis Argüello
summary: 'Comercio con los rusos': among the Ross boats arriving from Bodega came a 130-ton schooner that supplied goods to the troops, payable with interest — cloth, tools, iron in exchange for wheat & flour; the schooner returned to Ross; the itemized list sent to Argüello (not enclosed, the Habilitado being absent). [Direct Russo-Californian provision trade.]

author: José de la Guerra
summary: Escort detail: each escort cabo to furnish a soldier from Sta Bárbara to Monterey to convey the Englishmen Benjamin Brighthouse[?] & John Lima[?] sent by government order.

author: Juan Arroyo
summary: Sin interés (routine).

author: Calleja
summary: Re the British schooner from the Columbia and the protest of the agent of the North West Company of Canada ('Compañía del Norte del Canadá'), the establishment claimed in the name of George III [the Astoria/Fort George takeover]; Calleja decreed per the asesor's dictamen (certified copy enclosed).

author: José de la Guerra
summary: Foreign prisoners: little can be drawn from their declarations (they know little Spanish); runaways to be brought in as prisoners; no soldiers available to guard them (and were there, useless — they'd march off).

author: Guillermo de la Cota
summary: An invalid & a militiaman pass with otter-hunting gear.

author: de la Guerra
summary: The 12 cayuques (small fishing boats) arrived well; leave the 25th; missions to lend carts.

author: Luis Argüello
summary: Expedition to recover fugitive Indians frustrated; 20 men would be better, but no expedition succeeds — the Indians always hide once they know it's coming.

author: de la Guerra
summary: Habilitado General to be named at Guadalajara; the $1,000 salary + $500 transport unresolved.

author: Luis Argüello
summary: Acknowledges the Gov.'s arrival news.

author: de la Guerra
summary: Remits the 12 cayuques [bidarkas] taken from the Russians at the Ensenada de San Pedro [del Refugio]; missions to help transport.

author: Fr. José Joaquín de Sarría
summary: Missions to furnish carts for the cayuques.

author: de la Guerra
summary: Aid for the 22 imprisoned individuals of 'patrón Boris' under Sgt. Carlos Antonio Carrillo.

author: de la Guerra
summary: Remits 22 captured individuals + the 'Patrón Ferreof/Boris' [*Ilmena* crew], guarded by vecinos & soldiers (only one with musket & lance); prisoners ride bareback, 'Boris' with a saddle; 2 remained in the pueblo (one wounded, one tending him).

author: de la Guerra
summary: The cayuques can't come overland — to spare the Indians (as at Ross) and because of sea storms.

author: Guillermo Cota
summary: Asked Padre Zalvidea for 50 Indians to transport the cayuques.

author: de la Guerra
summary: Remits an invalid roster + copy of plans.

author: Francisco Mª Ruiz
summary: Remits the San Gabriel cimarrón census (the padrón Arrillaga ordered in 1810; ~30 today); sends a cabo + men every 8 days to patrol the environs.

author: Calleja
summary: Cluster: (May 8) query re the late Arrillaga & the six-presidio Habilitación accounts; (May 9) approved licensing the American brig *Pedlar* — captured at San Luis Obispo by José Cavenecia (captain/owner of the frigate *Tagle*) — with its cargo; (Jun 27) requires Eayrs's answer to Arrillaga's letter on his purpose; orders vigilance over all foreigners' movements.

author: Calleja
summary: (Dec 30) the *Pedlar*'s cargo to Acapulco to await opportunity; (Dec 31) Real Orden to receive & aid the Russian [ship] 'Boris' / its captain [Kuznetsov?] on its 'viaje científico.'

author: Estudillo & Capt. de la Guerra (sumaria)
people: Benjamin Ingraham; Walter Whitmarck; Baranov; Estudillo; de la Guerra
summary: Interrogation of the brig's crew: ① Benjamin Ingraham, pilot, American (New York): arrived San Luis for water/weather, then Sta Bárbara; a year in these seas aboard the brig — which was sold to the RAC ['Ricco'], sailed under American then Russian flag; Capt. Walter Whitmarck[?] (American); the brig belongs to Alexander Baranov, who resides 12 miles [from New Archangel]. [Continues n395+.]

author: Estudillo / de la Guerra
people: Ingraham; Boris Tarasov; John Elliot de Castro; Pedro Camú; Walter Whitmarck; Baranov; Ortega
places: Monterey; San Luis; Refugio; San Pedro
themes: america; russia; otter; smuggling; ships; depositions
summary: Ingraham concl.: Baranov ('Coscoff/Beranoff') styles himself Governor of Sitka; Ingraham knows Benjamin Tarasoff & John Elliot de Castro (interpreter, English, paid $50/mo.); the brig (the *Ilmena*) worked San Luis, rancho de las Bodegas, San Pedro, San Juan, San Simón for otter pelts & provisions (5–6× at the Ranchos). ② 'El Segundo': a sailor under Patrón Boris [Tarasov] on Baranov's merchant ship; 2 yrs aboard the American brig (Capt. Walter Whitmarck); names Pedro Camú (American); Boris Tarasoff = shoemaker/carpenter; Elliot de Castro sold meat & goods at the Spanish establishments; the rancho del Refugio (Sgt. [José F.] Ortega's) used for provisioning.

author: Calleja (Fiscal Sagárzurieta dictamen)
summary: Dictamen prohibiting commerce with English ships re the English schooner *Columbia* at Monterey & the North West Company of Canada agents' protest: the Company holds an establishment on the Columbia at 46° — founded by Anglo-Americans ~8 years ago, then seized by the English Company [Astoria → Fort George], garrisoned, corresponding with Asia; agents sought to land European goods (Solá might allow it for lack of memorias); the schooner left 10 deserters; partner [McTavish] stayed at Monterey with a carpenter to provision the post, waiting at Bodega while writing London; suspicion they aim to seize the territory under pretext of the beaver trade; orders to watch Fort George, prevent commerce/contraband, provision only per hospitality. (Asesor 4 Jul; Calleja signs 8 Jul 1815.)

author: (sumaria)
people: John Elliot de Castro; José Arce; de la Guerra; José F. Ortega; Carlos A. Carrillo; "Anastasio" (pilot); Solá
summary: THE ELLIOT DE CASTRO / *ILMENA* CAPTURE EXPEDIENTE. (Sep 19, San Luis) José Arce → Estudillo: the Russian brig *Ilmena* arrived at San Luis for water (permitted); aboard, pilot/interpreter John Elliot de Castro. (Sta Bárbara, de la Guerra → Solá) Ortega sighted the ship near the Refugio rancho (Sep 23), sent a cabo + 4 soldiers; the Gov. ordered its capture; for lack of men ~18 assembled (soldiers from Santa Inés & La Purísima). (Sep 25) Carlos A. Carrillo & José Ortega to seize them. Concl. (n401, tomo 1388–98): Carrillo with 13 men captured 7 of the party — Elliot, an American, 'un indio ruso' [an Aleut], + others; the captain escaped to sea (the ship couldn't be taken); the pilot Anastasio offered $2,000 to a soldier for Elliot's freedom (refused); captors = 1 cabo + 11 soldiers, given gratification ($12 to the sergeant, $2 each).

author: Ignacio Martínez & F.M. Ruiz
summary: Habilitado General to be named at Guadalajara ($1,000 salary + $500 transport). [dup theme, cf. C442/C461]

author: José Argüello
summary: Re the 11-presidio Habilitación receiving ~$2,000+ in effects; satisfaction given in Sinaloa re Noé. (copy.)

author: Luis Argüello
summary: The Russian ship 'Sarrasoff' [*Suvorov*] arrived the 20th requesting water/wood/provisions (promised); commander Lazareff [Lazarev]; ~500 tons; from St. Petersburg, destined for Lima; encloses a passport copy.

author: RAC Directors (Buldakov, Kramer, Zelensky)
people: Buldakov; Kramer; Zelensky; Alexander I; Rezanov; Baranov; Lazarev; Mollers
summary: Passport/proclamation of the ship *Suvorov* — addressed 'á nuestros buenos amigos y vecinos, los nobles y honrados Españoles habitantes de Cal.' The RAC under Tsar Alexander I invokes the long Russo-Spanish friendship, the 1812 Veliky Luki treaty, the Battle of Vitoria (21 Jun 1813) (Jourdan's baton, 130 cannon taken), Napoleon's catastrophe in Russia, the Spanish-prisoner 'Regimiento Imperial Alejandro,' Wellington crossing the Pyrenees; recounts Rezanov's 1806 SF visit & the bid to open commerce between California and the RAC colonies at Kodiak & Sitka; references the 15 May 1810 RAC proclamation [= C329]; names Baranov at Sitka dispatching a brig of goods. Concl. (n407–408, tomo 1405–10): they now send the *Suvorov* under Capt. [Lazarev] with supercargo Herman Mollers[?] and a cargo, hoping California will trade; signed by the RAC directors under Alexander I. [Fuller companion to C329.]

author: Crespo de Cáceres / Dr. Toribio González
summary: Restoration of the Inquisition: prints the Viceroy's bando + the México Vicegerencia's oficio publishing the re-establishment of the Santo Tribunal de la Fe by Royal Decree of 21 Jul 1814. (printed)

author: José Mª Estudillo
people: John Jennings; Agustín Belhune; José Argüello; Estudillo
themes: britain; ships; trade; astoria; prisoners; russia
summary: FINAL DOC — NWC schooner *Columbia* (2nd visit): Capt. John Jennings, supercargo Agustín Belhune[?]; the same ship that a year before advanced the Habilitado goods for provisions; now reclaiming deserters (7 delivered); Argüello gave the promised provisions, told her not to return without royal permission, freed the English deserters (among the captives 4 Galicians, 2 Portuguese, 2 Maltese); forbade trade between Jennings's ship and 'la gente del Ross,' posted a wharf guard; ordered the reclaimed prisoners delivered on condition they be absolved of any crime committed. — 'FIN DEL TOM. XIX.'
Title page — Provincial State Papers, Tomo XX, 1816–1822 (C-A 13)

author: Heredia y Vergara (Villahermosa, Tabasco)
summary: Encloses printed *acta* of the Ayuntamiento affirming loyalty to the King per the Viceroy's *bando*; to distinguish loyal subjects from 'rebeldes y traidores'

author: Calleja (Viceroy, México)
summary: Supreme Council of War orders captains-general / governors / judges to visit the jails on the eve of the three Pascuas (Christmas, Pentecost, Easter). Printed

author: Luis Argüello (San Francisco)
summary: Offers to watch & repel the *facciosos* (Buenos Aires insurgents) with armed force; reports the presidio's defense & munitions state; will use the San José militia; notes Magallanes & Baja Calif. events; Fort San Joaquín esplanade

author: Calleja (México)
summary: Printed salary-discount tariff (Treasury distress): 15%→20% retention scaled by salary (600 → 10,000+ pesos)

author: Calleja (México)
summary: 'Subvención Temporal de Guerra' — war subvention taken as *créditos*; paid preferentially to troops; employees over $300 pay ⅔

author: Calleja
summary: On equity in paying employees across provinces given unequal revenues

author: Calleja (México)
summary: Approves the permit given to Kuskov (capt. of Russian schooner *Chirikov*) to put in at SF and barter goods for provisions — he carried the Bodega Russian-colony commandant's reply about leaving Spanish dominions; orders no communication with that colony and not to repeat the concession; presidio *memorias* held at Acapulco

author: Calleja (México)
summary: Acknowledges news of the Russian ship *Suvorov* (*Suwarow*) arrival & watering; same ship the RAC directors had loaded in 1813; orders to learn why it came

author: Calleja (México)
summary: Royal cédula: military *asignaciones* in the Indies paid preferentially to creditors/families — names the mother & widow of José García & Pedro García (Inf. Regt. captains)

author: Calleja (México)
summary: Royal order to prevent the extinction of transport mules & carts (*acémilas y carros*): seize only the indispensable number in troop passports

author: Calleja (México)
summary: Royal cédula reforming officer/chief uniforms (detailed hussar dress: sky-blue dolman, gold embroidery, shako)

author: Calleja (México)
summary: Long royal order on military uniform/dress (spans 9 scans): all ranks & cadets must wear rigorous uniform/insignia/arms, penalties, mustache/beard rules by corps; reproduces the foundational royal decrees of 17 Mar 1785 & 31 May 1785 on officer dress (Royal Guards, infantry, cavalry, dragoons, artillery, engineers); ends with the King's call for moderation against costly dress

author: Calleja
summary: Royal order extending the use of mustaches to the Royal Guards of Infantry & the Army (beyond the 20 Feb concession to Carabineros/cavalry/grenadiers)

author: Calleja
summary: Royal order regulating which uniforms certain officers of various rank/arm may wear (Generals, Brigadiers, Coroneles, Real Cuerpo de Guardias)

author: Calleja
summary: Royal order: army horses no longer marked by cutting the right ear (per 4 Feb 1776) but branded with regiment initials & number

author: Calleja
summary: Declares the *avería* duty (6 per mil) on Mar del Sur seaborne goods belongs to the Consulado de Guadalajara; Consulado de México to collect 6 per mil

author: Calleja
summary: Bando granting the faculty to arm *en corso* (privateering) against the insurgents (per royal order 16 Feb); copyist notes the bando is on folios 71–77, uncopiable (bad binding)

author: Calleja
summary: Bando of 2 royal orders (22 Nov 1813 & 18 Jan 1816): opening the Mar del Sur ports of Tonalá & Tapachula duty-free for 10 years for interim commerce

author: Calleja
summary: The brigantine *San Carlos* (San Blas → Acapulco, provisions & powder) will carry the *memorias de efectos* on to California

author: Apoderado
summary: Denies Manuel Quijano's request for Armada rations for his wife & 2 daughters (San Blas → Peninsula transport), tied to a failed *permuta* with the surgeon José Benito

author: Apoderado
summary: Asks the Comandante general of Nueva Galicia to aid California with relief, given the scarcity among its inhabitants (province in penury)

author: Apoderado
summary: Approves hospitality given to the American frigate *Sultan*, which arrived at SF bound for the Russian establishment — watered, took provisions + 2 cattle & vegetables; urges compliance with the *arribadas* laws

author: Apoderado
summary: Approves aiding a Russian deserter from the Russian colony (gave him Armada ration)

author: Apoderado
summary: Superior Junta de Rl Hacienda ends the salary discount (new tariffs); orders gradual reintegration; revives *pensiones de casa*, horse-riding licences, coach & forced-lottery levies

author: Apoderado
summary: Orders the seditious soldiers Ignacio Zúñiga, Ramón Rubio, José Mª López & forzado Tomás Caneda freed but relocated to avoid bad example

author: Apoderado
summary: Royal cédula announcing the marriages of the King & the Infante to the Infantas of Brazil (Madrid 22 Mar 1816); published at Monterey 22 Jun 1817 (Estudillo)

author: Gervasio Argüello (cadet, SF)
summary: Fort Ross reconnaissance: detailed report on the Russian establishment ~7 leagues N of Bodega — 130-vara square timber wall (~3 varas high) topped by a 6-point palisade, 2 octagonal bastions with gun-ports, 6 houses (37 rooms); 25 Russians + commander + ~80 Kodiak (*Codiaque*) Indians (living outside, hunting otter in cayucos); shipbuilding: a 24-vara *bergantín*, war-launches, 47 leather cayucos; Russians make no local-Indian conquests, some local women married to Kodiaks; encloses the Russian commander's *pliego*

author: Otto von Kotzebue (aboard the *Rurik*, SF)
summary: Letter (in French): entered SF to resupply provisions & communicate with the Russian establishment; thanks the governor; requests orders to dispatch a courier quickly. Signed O.V. Kotzebue

author: Conference record: Gov. Solá / Kotzebue / Kuskov
summary: Solá–Kotzebue–Kuskov conference at SF Presidio. Kotzebue (*Rurik*) mediates; Spain demands Kuskov withdraw from Ross (8 leagues N of Bodega, est. ~5 yrs by Baranov); Kuskov admits residence, will vacate only on Baranov's order, will write the Emperor ('íntima alianza' Russia–Spain). Chamisso interpreter; Estudillo & Luis Argüello witnesses. Prior Viceroy/Arrillaga/J. Argüello demands went unanswered (forwarded to St. Petersburg). ⚠ internal date wrinkle (Kuskov arrives '25th,' doc concludes '23 Oct')

author: Kotzebue (aboard *Rurik*)
summary: Thanks & acknowledges receipt of 3 Russian deserters (Juan, Diego, Gregorio) from the RAC Bodega establishment; Cmdt. Luis Argüello sheltered/maintained them (one since Apr, two since Aug) free of charge. Signed in Russian script

author: Luis Argüello (SF)
summary: Reports the *Rurik* sailed suddenly/'violently' from SF; John Elliot de Castro embarked the day before; no receipt obtained for Elliot

author: interrogating officer "de Ortega", Santa Ynés
summary: Bouchard-raid prisoner deposition of Lt. William Taylor (Boston, 21): Bouchard's 2 ships, 235 crew, Monterey burned, 2 bronze guns taken, church spared, motive = refused provisions. = expanded Document 1 below; full transcription added to bouchard-raid-1818. ⚠ date contradiction flagged

author: (Santa Ynés)
summary: Bouchard-raid prisoner deposition of Martín Romero (Paraguay, 18; tailor pressed into service aboard 'La Gentila'). Names 2nd ship 'La Líberty/Libertad' under the American 'Medio Comodoro' (Peter Corney); crew of Moors, English, Portuguese, Americans, Andalusians, Argentines; *Chacabuco* split off for Lima. = expanded Document 2 below

author: El Callao (Peru)
summary: Relación of the Buenos Aires ships off Callao — describes the siege/bombardment 23 Jan–3 Feb 1816 by 5 BA warships. ⚠ NOT Bouchard — this is the 1816 William (Guillermo) Brown Pacific expedition; Savage's header says '1818' but the content is 1816 (verbatim header kept, error noted). pp. 123–124

author: Guayaquil
summary: Relato of the BA warships at Guayaquil (8–19 Feb): they capitulate and hand over prizes & prisoners to ransom their commander Guillermo (William) Brown. pp. 125–126

author: Intendencia de Guadalajara
summary: Intendant reports on Alta Calif. habilitado accounts; fiscal asks to limit the annual subsidy to both Californias; Asesor approves — Viceroy to fix the amount

author: México
summary: Customs instruction for collecting duties on Jamaica→Panama→Acapulco/San Blas & southern N.E. ports; full tariff schedule (alcabala, convoy, war, *avería*, *vales reales* duties). Signed Humara. 2 printed copies

author: Guadalajara
summary: Mexican independence war: Luis Quintanar reports to Brig. Pedro Celestino Negrete the surrender (11 Dec 1816) of the fort Cuitlatlán (alias San Miguel)

author: San Francisco
summary: Luis Argüello → Solá (omnibus): American schooner arrived w/ tools (requests axes); Sgt. Herrera to rejoin wife in N. Galicia; Fr. Ramón Abella proposes selling 200 mission cattle — Argüello urges each mission add 200 (→600) to found a Real Hacienda ranch to supply the presidio weekly; presidio launch destroyed in a storm (needs new one to haul timber); iron & steel scarce

author: San Francisco
summary: Luis Argüello → Solá: reports the escape of 18 imprisoned Indians and the measures taken to recapture them

'Asuntos para el Ayudante Inspector' — admin omnibus: Indian punishments reclaimed; *cimarrones* heading to S. Vicente; Goycoechea's bid to use S. Luis Indians as paid fábrica labor (20 r./indio) denied; Fr. Luis Sales's request to move Mission S. Vicente denied pending Ortega's report; deceased-soldier accounts; inspection-report duties

author: San Francisco
summary: Luis Argüello → Solá: works on the esplanade of Fort San Joaquín — 200 beams cut, 500–600 boards milled, merlons raised, lime/mortar in hand; provisional chapel finished, first mass on the 25th; old chapel razed to foundations to rebuild next summer

author: San Francisco
summary: Luis Argüello → Solá: sends Alf. Gabriel Moraga to inspect the S. José / Sta. Clara / Sta. Cruz detachments (delayed by rains); can't file the presidio troop return because nearly the whole garrison is occupied escorting/guarding prisoners hauling materials for the works

author: San Francisco
summary: Luis Argüello → Gov.: the launch arrived 6 Feb 'muy maltratada de la quilla y fondo' (badly damaged keel & bottom), being repaired; 2 Indians in its charge to be sent on. = the 'xx.46' half of the boat story (with Doc 40 = xx.42); both added to boat-problem

author: San Diego
summary: Inventory of the San Diego Presidio Archive (handover Cmdt. Francisco M.ª Ruiz → José M.ª Estudillo, per Solá's order 29 Sep 1820). Legajo-by-legajo: governors' corresp. (Rivera, Neve, Arrillaga, Borica, J. Argüello, Solá, 1774–1820), *filiaciones*, criminal-process drafts, soldiers' wills, *padrones*, service records, monthly musters from 1781, Borica's reserved files on war-with-the-English prep + San Blas ship-signal plans, the Domínguez–Nieto land dispute, war-matériel inventory. A finding aid to a lost archive

author: México
summary: Viceroy Apodaca free-trade compilation (now COMPLETE, ends n54). A long bundle reproducing earlier royal orders granting duty-free privileges to merchant expeditions Cádiz→San Blas/California via Callao/Acapulco/Guayaquil: Ramón Márquez (Cinco Gremios Mayores, R.O. 1 Oct 1793); José Ignacio de Iturbe (fragata *Neptuno*, Acapulco scale, R.O. Aranjuez 3 Feb 1795); Fernando Sierra (fragata *Rosalía*, Guayaquil return cacao/cascarilla, then again re San Blas/Tepic/Guadalajara *alcabala eventual* abuse — R.O. Madrid 3 Jul 1818 & 8 May 1820, *Salmón*; foreign goods to pay 7% *almojarifazgo* at fixed Veracruz tariffs). Es copia México 21 Aug 1820 (*Humara*); Monterey 10 Apr 1821; circulated for archival copying — Guerra y Noriega (Sta Bárbara 18 Apr 1821), Estudillo (San Diego 22 Apr 1821)

author: Monterey
summary: Comandante 'M.G.' → Cabo Martín Romero: acknowledges receipt of his *justificantes de revista*; asks for the general state of artillery, munitions & other *enseres*, and that he not include himself in the *relación de revista*. (Copia — letterbook register entry)

author: Monterey
summary: Comandante 'M.G.' → Cabo Homobono Márquez: acknowledges *justificantes de revista*; re the departure of the frigate *Reyna*; to remit the general state of artillery & munitions; Aguilar to request a marriage licence by *memorial*

author: Monterey
summary: Comandante 'M.G.' (= Manuel Gómez, Monterey artillery cmdt — see Doc 52) → Cabo Antonio M.ª Ortega: remit general state of artillery & munitions; reports asking the Governor on what day Rosalino Olivia was imprisoned, since his pay runs from that day

author: Monterey
summary: Comandante → Cabo Ortega: with Lent (*cuaresma*) approaching, make lists of the troop and the wives of married soldiers, and obtain certificates from the *R[everendo] P[adre]* for those who fulfil their Easter Church duty; received Zavaleta's memorial

author: Monterey
summary: Circular recognizing Iturbide — Manuel Gómez (Monterey artillery cmdt) circulates to the artillery detachment corporals a chain of orders: Manuel Antonio de Torres Valdivia (Gral del Cuerpo de Artillería, 16 Jan) transcribing Agustín de Iturbide (titled 'Almirante General,' 9 Jan) re Manuel Gual (Coronel del Cuerpo); troops to recognize the new corps chief; remit rosters (service time, uniform, armament) + artillery/arms/munitions states. Copyist note: following circulars are copied without headers, so their subject is unknown

author: Monterey
summary: Comandante 'M.G.' → Cabo Méndez: discipline/economic/religious matters; received memorials & *justificantes*; approve the arrest the Governor imposed; with Lent begun, all soldiers and their wives must fulfil religious duties

author: Monterey
summary: (Torres [?]) → Cabo Méndez: disapproves of soldiers sending memorials directly to the Governor; recommends supervising the plaza laborers Francisco Pacheco & Juan Acosta

author: Monterey
summary: Manuel Gómez → detachment chiefs (circular): on receipt, form the troop and have them recognize Manuel Antonio/M.ª Torres de Valdivia as general chief of the imperial artillery corps and Manuel Gual as second-in-chief; remit artillery/munitions state

author: Monterey
summary: Comandante 'M.G.' → Cabo Méndez: routine service; return Aguilar's memorial (came in anonymous); received *justificantes de revista*

author: Monterey
summary: Comandante 'M.G.' → detachment corporals (circular): henceforth deal for supply with Subteniente Ramírez, as he (Gómez) marches to Mexico; give notice to the Mexican empire

author: Monterey
summary: Marriage-licence request of artillery militiaman Bruno Bernal; granted by Comandante Gómez. ⚠ header 'Enero 9 de 1822' but body references an *oficio* of 23 Sep 1824 — Savage date inconsistency, kept verbatim

author: Monterey
summary: Comandante 'M.G.' → Cabo Antonio M.ª Ortega: acknowledges receipt of the state of artillery & munitions

author: Monterey
summary: Manuel Gómez → Homobono Méndez [Márquez?]: the American frigate 'Jáchem' [*Sachem*?] carries/delivers a 4-pounder cannon (*pieza de a 4*) to that detachment

author: Monterey
summary: Manuel Gómez announces his departure for Mexico to join the imperial corps of that country, substituted by Subteniente Don José Ramírez

author: Monterey
summary: Manuel Gómez → Cabo Antonio M.ª Ortega: sends a list of munitions & *pertrechos* carried by the bergantín 'Juan B[autista]'; notes the workmen (*obreros*) sent from Mexico are now en route; recommends care of the artillery & detachment equipment

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito (Viceroy) → Gov. Alta Calif.: congratulates on the order/*policía* established among the Indians; orders thanks given in the King's name to the missionary fathers — esp. Fr. Mariano Payeras (aiding/succoring the inhabitants) and Fr. Luis Martínez (during the pirate [Bouchard] invasion)

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov.: orders a *sumaria* on the four Buenos Aires insurgent deserters who crossed over (with arms) to the Californians at San Juan Capistrano, Oct/Nov 1818, and that they be watched. Names + filiations: Pedro Saldívar (Buenos Aires, printer, 22), Juan de la Rosa (labrador, 27, served as drummer), Nicolás Echeverría (Santa Fe, labrador, 24), José Pascual (African, servant, 28)

author: México
themes: defense; fiscal; presidio-decay; ships; bouchard; anglo-spanish-war; missions; ⭐
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Solá: instructions on the war matériel, provisions & 200 troops sent to Calif. aboard the fragata 'Reina,' King's brigantine 'San Carlos,' brig 'Cosak'; rebukes Solá for complaining (his 6 Sep letter to the Cmdt. of Provincias Internas de Occidente) that he got only 300 poor sword-blades 'fit only for sickles,' none of the 500 carbines requested, no situado cash, no officers, no artillery. Viceroy retorts: scabbards are abundant locally; carbines unavailable; troops use muskets like dragoons; the artillery officer + men + munitions came overland to Guaymas (arrived Oct); the complaint is unfounded; California is 'rico de perlas, rico de peces' and rich-soiled — settlers & idle troops should apply themselves and aid the sovereign; orders Guadalajara & Occidente to aid with cash and to ready the *San Carlos* to carry 8 missionaries in May; ⭐ 'los 200 hombres de tropa… no los necesita V. ahí, devuélvalos a sus respectivos puestos' (return the 200 reinforcement troops). = expanded Document 16 below

author: México
themes: bouchard; defense; military-history; foreign-attack; ships; monterey; ⭐
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Solá: emergency response to the Bouchard seizure of Monterey. Has learned — via San Blas, from Don Manuel Varela[?], captain of the private brig 'El Aventurero' (reached port the 30th prev. month) — that on 24 Nov [1818] two large ships + seven insurgent gunboat-launches seized the Monterey presidio, Solá withdrawing to a rancho with all his troops. Has urgently ordered the Cmdt. Gen. of Provincias Internas de Occidente to send from Sonora a ship (the 'San Carlos' or another) with troops & munitions to San Antonio / Concepción, and alerted the Cmdt. Gen. of Nueva Galicia; expects Solá to dislodge the *facciosos* with the reinforcements + his troops + armed *paisanage*

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Calif.: war demands — the invasion of the traitor Mina, the gathering of *facciosos* at Galveston, and the Kingdom's rebels — had prevented timely relief; now free of those *atenciones*, he sends aid

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Calif.: war-matériel shipments — orders José de la Cruz to send 4 field-pieces (cal. 4) w/ munitions & powder, the 400 carbines if possible, 3 garrison flags, and the 15–20 cannon (cal. 18/8/6/4) Solá requested; the 300 swords ordered remitted to San Blas via the Artillery inspection; orders the Cmdt. Gen. of Occidente to send funds to repair the fortifications in bad state; artillerymen + an officer + workers leaving immediately to put the artillery service in best state

author: México
themes: bouchard; defense; military-history; personnel; promotions; ships; monterey; ⭐
summary: Conde del Venadito → Solá — the COLONEL-PROMOTION letter (= expanded Document 3 below; its page cite is corrected here from i.192–197). Acknowledges Solá's reports nº 174 (4 Nov) & nº 177 (12 Dec 1818) warning of two insurgent frigates preparing in the Sandwich Islands [Hawaii] to invade, and the attack on Monterey presidio after 18 Nov; approves the defense; thanks all in the King's name; confers on Solá the grade of *Coronel de Milicias Provinciales* (provisional despacho enclosed, published in extraordinary gazette). Recounts ordering the 'San Carlos' (San Blas, ~100 men) and a Sonora ship; Occidente replied (13 Feb) the brig 'Cosak' was readying at Mazatlán with 100 picked men under Capt. Eduardo García + 6–8,000 pesos & munitions; San Blas departure delayed, José de la Cruz ordered to expedite. ⭐ Immediate promotions to Alférez Manuel Gómez & José Estrada, who commanded the battery [Bouchard defense]

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Alta Calif.: on the funds situation — strict orders to the intendant of Guadalajara; the Sonora treasuries can make no *ministración*; no carbines available in Nueva Galicia or Mexico; supposes Solá has by now received the artillery & munitions held at Mazatlán, with artillerymen & workers ready to embark

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Calif.: on the shortage of farming tools in those districts; denies permission for the inhabitants to trade with the Russian colony [Fort Ross]; notes the Russians 'abundan en frutos y semillas que los llevan a vender a los puertos del Reino' (bring fruits & seeds to sell at the ports)

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Alta Calif.: forwards the order pressing the intendant of Guadalajara to send, at first opportunity, all or part of Alta California's *haberes* (back-pay/funds)

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Alta Calif.: the brig 'San Carlos' & frigate 'Reyna' left San Blas (8 Jun) w/ troops, artillery, arms, munitions & park-effects for the peninsula; the brig 'Cosak' left Mazatlán (15 Jul) w/ 100 men; supposes they have reached their destination

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Alta Calif.: transcribes the R.O. on the Russian government's request that the ships of its expedition to both Poles be well received & aided; S.M. acceded. Ships: 'Vostok' & 'Mirny' (Sp. *El Oriente* / *La Pacífica*) under Capt. Bellingshausen; 'Otkrytie' & 'Blagonamerennyi' (Sp. *La Descubierta* / *La Buena Intención*) under Capt. Vasiliev [Savage: 'Walpilelf']. = relates to expanded Document 5

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. A. Cal.: transcribes the order pressing the Guadalajara intendant to relieve Alta California; recommends *ministrar* the greatest amount possible from the relief funds sent

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Alta Calif.: the excesses the Indians committed during the insurgent [Bouchard] frigates' attack — robbing and deserting to the woods — show they lack *policía*; establish it, make them understand their duties, impose moderate corrective punishments. = expanded Document 4

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Solá: encloses duplicate of the promotion despatches for Solá, Gómez & Estrada (in case the first were lost); supposes the troop/matériel ships have arrived; is urging the Guadalajara intendant for money

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Alta Calif.: on the uprising of the 'indios del Colorado' at San Buenaventura; approves the measures taken to punish them; thanks in the King's name; requests memorials if the soldiers who died left poor families, to grant pensions

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Solá: repeats notice that various ships are coming to his aid; recommends care to conserve everything

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Solá: duplicate of the order at folios 250–252 (= Doc 75)

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Alta Calif.: orders the Guadalajara Intendancy to reimburse the presidio of San Francisco the 2,153 pesos 6 it advanced as *socorro de armada* for the maintenance of various Russian and Anglo-American prisoners, 1808–1815

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Calif.: will send invalid tradesmen (blacksmith, carpenter, tanner) when available (none now); the Orizaba religious (missionaries) will go soon (war-delayed); has ordered Guadalajara & Provincias Internas not to delay overseas correspondence to the Californias

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gov. Solá: order to the intendant of Sonora to provide relief to Baja California, while the intendant of Guadalajara is charged with provisioning Alta California

author: Misión de San Antonio
summary: Gov. Solá → the mission fathers (circular): watch for the relief ships the Viceroy is sending — keep lookouts (*vigías*) and a *cayuco* ready to assist; ships sighted San Juan Capistrano→Santa Bárbara go to the Sta Bárbara roadstead, those further north to Monterey or San Francisco; provide outfitting, food & pack-animals; return the circular once read

author: Mazatlán
summary: Pedro del Corral → Gov. Alta Calif.: by order of Gral Cmdt. Alejo García Conde, the brig 'Cosak' sailed carrying 100 men under Capt. Pablo de la Portilla, 10,000 pesos, and a *tercio* of sealed paper from the Real Caja; troops & officers got two months' pay in advance

author: Mazatlán
summary: Estado de fuerza of the Mazatlán-squadron detachment to Alta Calif. aboard the 'Cosak' under Capt. Pablo de la Portilla: 1 capt., 2 lts., 2 alféreces, 2 sgts., 2 drummers, 4 corporals, 32 soldiers = 'Total 100' [⚠ listed ranks sum to 45, not 100 — Savage roster discrepancy]. Armament: 23 muskets, 93 bayonets, 93 cartridge-boxes, 93 [tinetas?] (⚠ only 23 muskets — under-armed). Signed Pedro Tomás del Corral

author: (México/Mazatlán)
summary: [Header on unread page i.306–307] — tail of an expedition-dispatch packet: encloses war-matériel docs (nos. 1–3) and copies of the *instrucciones* given to the expedition commander Teniente de navío Don Gonzalo Gómez de Ulloa and to Don José Bandini, captain of the frigate 'Reyna' (re chartering, docs nos. 4–5)

author: Mazatlán
summary: Eduardo García → Gov. Alta Calif.: establishing a mail-boat (buque-correo) between Mazatlán and the Californias — inserts brigadier Antonio Cordero's order (18 Mar) for rapid/periodic wartime communications; has contracted a launch to travel monthly Loreto↔Mazatlán carrying the correspondence

author: Durango
summary: Alejo García Conde → Gov. Alta Calif.: cannot send the funds Solá requested (will inform the Viceroy); notes the 'Cosak' reached the San Luis roadstead with troops & munitions; laments Solá's scarcity of money & good arms

author: Durango
summary: Alejo García Conde → Gov. Alta Calif.: learned from the Gov. of Baja California that the brig 'Cosak' continued its navigation toward San Luis

author: Punta de San Luis
summary: Ignacio Morales → Gov. Alta Calif.: the 'Cosak' arrived at the port of San Luis at 9 PM; requests 2,000 pesos to pay the ship's crew (*dotación*); asks the town magistrates supply provisions — he has only 15 days' worth

author: Punta de San Luis
summary: Pablo de la Portilla → Gov. Solá: the 'Cosak' anchored after 23 days from Mazatlán with his 100 men; not the contracted port, but advanced season and 'la decadencia de treinta y siete individuos en estado de muerte' (37 men in near-death condition) forced landing here; will march to approach the governor, bringing 10,000 pesos + a *tercio* of sealed paper

author: Durango
summary: Antonio Cordero → Gov. Alta Calif.: the Viceroy ordered him to aid Solá against the Buenos Aires insurgents [Bouchard] who attacked Monterey; not knowing the state of hostilities, asks Solá to report the war situation & needed aid; is already sending the Mazatlán brig with troops & matériel

author: San Diego
summary: Francisco M.ª Ruiz → Gov. Alta Calif.: acknowledges the invasion communications; has gathered the presidio troop, replaced the escorts with invalids as ordered, and has the soldiers doing target practice (*tiro al blanco*) on foot & horseback for proper instruction

author: Santa Bárbara
themes: legal; missions; indigenous; defense; bouchard; santa-barbara
summary: José de la Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Solá (long): defends Santiago Argüello against the San Juan Capistrano fathers' complaint that, during the Bouchard invasion alarm, the mission escort/guard was withdrawn to a defensive point and the mission houses locked, leaving it unguarded; Guerra argues the circumstances (invasion threat, need to concentrate force) justified Argüello, faults the fathers' grievance; Anastasio Carrillo also implicated; encloses the replies

author: San Diego
themes: legal; missions; defense; bouchard; indigenous; san-diego
summary: Santiago Argüello → Gov. Solá (long rebuttal): answers the San Juan Capistrano fathers' charges over goods lost from the mission *bodega* during their flight at the insurgents' [Bouchard] arrival — says the bodega was empty (they'd moved things), that he helped save items & returned what he found strewn on the beach, that the fathers abandoned the mission and fled to the woods and locked the doors, insulted him with foul words; quotes their dramatic complaint; notes Fr. José Barona had earlier said the insurgents' damage 'valía nada'; praises the San Luis fathers' help

author: Misión de la Soledad
summary: Gov. Solá → the commandants of the Monterey & San Francisco presidios: 3 ships sighted on the coast — could be the awaited reinforcement ships, or pirates; stay vigilant and ready

author: Guadalajara
summary: José de la Cruz → Gov. Solá: received the recommendation letter for Antonio Vicente Tinta[?] Salvador; will do what he can for him

author: Guadalajara
summary: José de la Cruz → Gov. Solá: re the dispatch to the Viceroy of the Russian prisoners Juan (John) Elliot de Castro and Boris Tarasov, captured near Santa Bárbara at the Ensenada del Refugio and San Pedro — taken while going about in 12 cayucos with 24 men hunting otter (*nutrias*); sent aboard schooner(s) 'Fly[?]'/'Religión[?].' ([cf. Mathes ed. — Tarasov effects-lists]; Elliot de Castro later embarks on the *Rurik*, Doc 32)

author: Guadalajara
summary: José de la Cruz → Gov. Solá: encloses testimony of the Real Hacienda resolution on the *libranzas* (drafts) drawn by the presidial-company *habilitados* and endorsed by the Governor

author: Guadalajara
themes: america; ships; trade; otter; legal; foreign-relations; ⭐
summary: José de la Cruz → Gov. Alta Calif.: forwards the decree on the capture of the Anglo-American corvette 'Mercurio' by the Spanish frigate 'La Flora.' Not treated as a *corso* prize (the two states at peace), so ordered: a formal investigation of the illicit trade by the *Mercurio*'s captain Mr. George Ayres (Jorge Eyers) on the California coasts, and of why Don Nicolás de Noé (cmdr. of the *Flora*) boarded & seized her — a *comiso* matter under the Governor as Territorial Hacienda judge; per R.O. 25 Dec 1790 & 10 Aug 1802. Embeds Calleja's order (México 24 Apr 1814): Ayres was mistreated — robbery of cargo/effects, violence to his family — so secure his goods, don't bother him or separate him from his family, prevent extortion

author: México
summary: Calleja → Gov. Alta Calif.: closing of the 'Mercurio' *comiso* — care that Ayres not be molested in person, family or property. Copyist note: this is a mis-placed duplicate of the immediately preceding decree (Doc 101), copied by error without a heading

author: México (orig. Madrid)
themes: britain; nootka; foreign-relations; trade; royal-order; bodega; ⭐⭐⭐
summary: Reserved instructions (Conde de Floridablanca) accompanying the NOOTKA CONVENTION of 1790 with England. Orders the Viceroy to ensure British subjects 'no se excedan de su literal contexto'; the convention does not alter the prior Costa de Honduras logwood/mahogany (*palo de tinte / caoba*) arrangement with the British 'colonos'; arts. 3, 4 & 7 do not bar prosecuting contraband / illicit trade by any nation (incl. England) on Spanish coasts per Spanish law & custom. Signed Floridablanca, Madrid 25 Oct 1790; *Es copia* México 15 Apr 1814 (Humara) — recopied as legal backing for the *Mercurio* prize. → expand; Bodega/Nootka 'Open Door'

author: México (orig. Madrid)
themes: foreign-relations; trade; america; britain; royal-order; anglo-spanish-war; ⭐⭐
summary: R.O. of 10 Aug 1802 (Pedro Cevallos). Foreign vessels in the Mar del Sur that 'so color de la pesca de la Ballena hacen un crecido contrabando' along Spanish Pacific coasts: officials to bar foreigners from landing & confiscate per the rules; cites two ships caught smuggling, 'Eliothin' & 'Tigaty'; notes Spain is warning the U.S. govt to proceed against those who land at the colonies; 'este punto convenido entre todas las potencias que tienen colonias.' Signed Cevallos, Madrid 10 Aug 1802; *Es copia* México 15 Apr 1814 (Humara). (cf. the 1803 Cevallos whaler R.O. in ca54-state-papers-sacramento-i-v-survey)

author: Guadalajara
summary: José de la Cruz → Gov. Solá: thanks for congratulations on the Gran Cruz conferred on him; denies the rumor he'll be promoted to Viceroy ('in these times best to have a corner and a little pot to enjoy'); reports Cartagena surrendered to General Morillo and the 1st division of Spanish forces is about to reach Veracruz

author: Mazatlán
summary: Joaquín Avalía[?] → Pedro Santander: gives details about the Buenos Aires insurgent (naval) expedition [in the Pacific — Brown 1816]

author: Tepic
summary: Relación by Don Tomás Fernández del Castillo of the insurgent expeditionaries' misdeeds at Callao and Guayaquil & other events of the B. Aires expedition

author: Mazatlán
summary: José Ramón de Menchaca: relates how, sailing from Cádiz bound for Lima aboard the frigate 'Consecuencia,' the Buenos Aires insurgents captured them and marooned them on a desert island

author: (Pacific)
summary: Relación of the arrival & situation of the Montevideo corsairs — their arrival at Callao & related events

author: San Francisco
summary: Luis Argüello → Gov. Alta Calif.: Alf. Gabriel Moraga setting out with force to attack the apostate Indians at the ranchería/pueblo of 'Malmi' (Sta Cruz mission jurisdiction); sends the 20 Indians requested (delayed by manpower shortage); he & his family in great privation; the San Joaquín esplanade nearing completion, the merlons worked (6 Jun) 'looking very handsome'

author: San Francisco
summary: Luis Argüello → Gov. Solá: ⭐ the surprise on the apostate Christians of Malmi failed — they were no longer there; service-personnel details; if the B. Aires pirates approach they will be repelled/'escarmentados'

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: José de la Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Calif.: clarifies the presidio accounts in the diezmos (tithes) branch — the 1,474 pesos collected in 1807; Salvino/Santiago Pico[?] not yet owing tithes; José & Francisco Ortega assessed

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: José de la Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Solá: measures to prevent a surprise by the B. Aires expedition — the 2 cannon he has are useless, the militia artillerymen inept ('ni siquiera saben el ejercicio del arma'); asks for matériel & munitions; proposes making 500–600 bows and requesting the missions' best archers (*flecheros*), making machetes, and arming the vaqueros and Indians against the pirates

author: Monterey
summary: Gov. Pablo Vicente Solá → the Pueblo Commissioners: orders immediate construction of a cart-road (*camino carretero*) from Monterey to the pueblo of San José with all needed bridges for carriages & cattle; convokes authorities & residents to cooperate; names Sergeant P. [Lico?] commissioner

author: San Francisco
summary: Luis Argüello → Gov. Alta Calif.: sends sealing-wax (*lacre*)

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: José de la Guerra y Noriega: 'varios asuntos de ningún interés' (Savage's note)

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: José de la Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Solá: consults whether to admit into the presidio several Kodiak (*Codiaque*) Indians who had been on the [Channel] islands since the capture of Elliot [de Castro], and who wish to be baptized and remain at the presidio

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Solá: requests the discharge/licence of D. Olivera & Francisco Flores

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra → Gov. Solá: forwards 5 boxes of powder sent from San Diego

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra → Gov. Solá: that the residents of the Rancho de los Nietos stand guard at San Pedro, though that rancho lies in San Diego jurisdiction

author: Santa Bárbara
themes: russia; indigenous; labor; missions; presidio-decay; otter; santa-barbara; ⭐
summary: Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Solá: knows nothing of the theft of otters (*nutrias*); the Kodiak Indians are numerous — good to baptize them and employ them as servants (requests two); proposes Fr. [Antonio] Ripoll establish a cloth factory (*fábrica de paños*) since the soldiers are without clothing

author: Santa Bárbara
themes: america; women; indigenous; trade; missions; santa-barbara; ⭐
summary: The Indian woman captured on the 'Mercurio' with Capt. [George] Ayres and their daughter: María Antonia de la Ascensión Stuard sailed as Ayres's woman 4 years; they had a daughter; both taken aboard when the *Mercurio* was seized; when Ayres was sent on to San Blas, mother & daughter remained in Guerra's house at Santa Bárbara — both baptized, with Guerra as godfather

author: Santa Bárbara
themes: defense; ships; labor; missions; presidio-decay; santa-barbara
summary: Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Solá: a ship's arrival reported at San Luis — sent Sgt. Carrillo with 8 soldiers to investigate its movements; Fr. Ripoll ordered the first piece of cloth (*paño*) delivered to send the governor; asks to keep part for his naked soldiers

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Alta Calif.: the San Diego commandant did not request the militiamen (as regular) — he commissioned them himself; left with only 8 soldiers; forwards the Governor's circular to the missions about vaqueros; ⭐ 6 earthquakes (*temblores*) since the previous day

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Solá: reports the vicious & scandalous conduct of Alférez Joaquín Maytorena and his latest abuses; has arrested him pending the Governor's decision

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Solá: has sent for the 3 cannons from San Buenaventura, the two 2-pdrs from Santa Inés & Purísima — which have no shot (*bala*); the added artillery needs more men, esp. lacking gun-platforms; the local militia should help; scarcity prevented sending matériel to San Diego; ⭐ approves 'bala roja' (red-hot shot) against the pirates 'pero desgraciadamente no hay allí quien sepa aplicarla' (no one knows how to use it)

author: Santa Bárbara
themes: defense; militia; personnel; census; presidio-decay; santa-barbara; ⭐⭐
summary: Estado of men fit to bear arms in the Santa Bárbara jurisdiction (excl. the artillery militia, sent to San Diego), by class & residence: Pueblo de Los Ángeles (4 sgts, 1 cabo, 15 soldiers-invalids, 70 vecinos), missions San Fernando, San Buenaventura, Purísima, ranchos Simí & del Refugio, + the presidio invalids & 17 vecinos. Total 135 persons — but only 32 muskets, 22 swords, 8 pistols, 6 lances for the whole jurisdiction. Signed Guerra y Noriega. = expanded Document 18

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Solá: re the 1,474 pesos of the 1807 tithe (*diezmo*) charged to habilitado Argüello but never entered; argues tithes are due 5 yrs after a business yields products, not 5 yrs after establishment — citing a Felipe de Neve order exempting soldiers (escort, serving, discharged-settlers) and *pobladores* from tithes for their first 5 years of farming/stock-raising

author: Santa Bárbara
themes: fiscal; diezmos; agriculture; wine; economy; santa-barbara
summary: Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Calif.: sends a list of the jurisdiction's ranchos with the wines & brandies (*vinos y aguardientes*) harvested the prior year and tithes paid, + a demonstration of all collected in the jurisdiction through 1815

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Santiago Argüello: account of supplies furnished to the American prisoners of the frigate 'Albatross' from 18 Jan to 16 Mar; total 27 pesos 5 granos

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Solá: has suspended the cutting of timber (*corte de maderas*)

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Solá: encloses the service record (*hoja de servicios*) of Sergeant Guillermo Cota

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Solá: received, via the schooner 'Lidia,' the 5 Indians who killed Father [Andrés] Quintana [the 1812 murder at Mission Santa Cruz], sent to atone for their crime at the presidio

author: Santa Bárbara
themes: america; prisoners; ships; religion; indigenous; santa-barbara; ⭐
summary: Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Solá: the 'Lidia' arrived (15th) with the prisoners & escort; nothing charged to its captain for relief; ⭐ the captain of the 'Albatross,' Guillermo (William) Smith, and 2 more sailors embarked on the *Lidia*; the remaining Black man and Indian to be educated in religion; declaration enclosed

author: San Francisco
summary: Gabriel Moraga → Gov. Solá: encloses his service record (*hoja de servicios*) from 1784

author: San Diego
summary: Juan María Ruiz → Gov. Solá: defense report — taking all measures vs. a surprise; abundant matériel & munitions but lacks cloth & paper to make the castle & musket cartridges; coasts well watched; the castle esplanade rebuilt; will send for the Sta Bárbara militia artillerymen; missions ordered to drive coastal cattle inland if a suspicious ship is sighted

author: San Diego
summary: Arms roster of the San Diego presidio — arms held by invalids, vecinos & the warehouse (*almacén*): 5 soldiers/sgts, scant pistols/muskets; warehouse 94 [muskets?] / 16 [?]. Signed at San Diego 10 Jul 1816

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra y Noriega → Gov. Solá: argues against drawing the *situados* from Guadalajara rather than Mexico City — Guadalajara's goods are inferior to Puebla's, the troops benefit from receiving effects at capital prices; 'más debe temerse el ser socorridos por Guadalajara si fracasa de un año a dos, que el carecer de memorias'

author: México
summary: Apodaca → Gov. Alta Calif.: royal order that henceforth Subinspectors, not Colonels, appoint the sergeants of their corps

author: México
summary: Apodaca → Gov. Solá: encloses the royal order granting a pay increase to the army of New Spain & the Indies

author: México
summary: Calleja → Gov. Calif.: encloses the printed decree/*bando* prohibiting under severe penalties the export of silver & bullion (*plata y pasta*) from New Spain

author: México
summary: Apodaca → Gov. Alta Calif.: printed circular announcing he has taken possession as Viceroy, Governor & Captain-General of New Spain, President of the Audiencia & Superintendent of the Real Hacienda, conferred by Fernando VII

author: México
summary: Calleja → Gov. Alta Calif.: acknowledges the arrival & departure of the English schooner 'Columbia,' which came to gather provisions

author: México
summary: Calleja → Gov. Calif.: *bando* of the R.O. by which the King (on his saint's day) grants liberty to all imprisoned officers & soldiers whose cases do not carry the death penalty, and a month's pay to those condemned to death

author: México
summary: Calleja → Gov. Alta Calif.: encloses the statutes of the Royal American Order of Isabel la Católica (follow in a notebook)

author: México
summary: Calleja → Gov. Alta Calif.: the 2% agency fee granted to presidio *habilitados* is for collecting/distributing funds; since the San Diego captain [Ruiz?] was in México not San Diego, Guerra has the right to the 2% on his salary

author: San Francisco
summary: Luis Argüello → Gov. Solá: received the 3 cannons with their 300 rounds, to be mounted at Fort San Joaquín; the merlons nearing completion, [delayed] by the absence of Subteniente Manuel Gómez

author: San Diego
summary: Francisco M.ª Ruiz → Gov. Solá: encloses a list of the artillery militiamen who served at San Diego, 8 Jul–24 Oct; judging them trained, sent them home to stay ready; will set assembly (drill) days

author: México
summary: Calleja → Gov. Solá: *bando* prohibiting the commerce carried on from Panama with New Spain through the Mar del Sur (Pacific) ports, + the Santa Cruz lateral-coast arrangement & a tariff (copyist: neither bando nor tariff on the following pp.)

author: México
summary: Calleja → Gov. Solá: grants military pension (*Montepío militar*) to Doña Tomasa Lugo, widow of Don Raimundo Carrillo, former captain of the San Diego presidio; legal requirements & mention of her unmarried children

author: México
summary: Calleja → Gov. Alta Calif.: acknowledges the American schooner 'Lidia' arriving at the Ensenada del Refugio for provisions (given free, great need) and its donation to the battery of 2 four-pounder cannons with munitions, and 50 axes

author: Monterey
summary: Testament of José Marcelino Bravo (will dated Jan 1786): leaves 25 head of cattle to Mission Santa Cruz and as many to Mission San Juan; Gov. Solá settles the heirs disputing the division

author: México
summary: Calleja → Gov. Solá: per the anti-Panama-commerce bando, report how many ships arrived by that route, with duties/details to gauge the trade's magnitude, and the names of the ships' agents, so the proper duties can be demanded

author: Monterey
summary: Gov. Solá → the presidio commandants: orders the formation of a register of cattle brands & marks (*fierros y señales*) of California's stock-raisers — a book of brands granted earlier; each jurisdiction to compile & send theirs

author: Tepic
summary: Pedro Negrete → Gov. Solá: laments his tallow-buying venture (*empresa de sebos*) in Alta California — 'maldita sea'; the 10,000 pesos he spent are lost; the California friars refused cloth (*manta*) for tallow, demanding pesos, and Mexican ones

author: San Blas
summary: Pedro Negrete → Gov. Solá: announces arrival at San Blas; a glowing apologia of Francisco M.ª Ruiz — 'un riesgo americano de aquellos muy pocos que se conocen en la América y en el mundo,' honest, popular with his soldiers, who for love of Fernando VII has them dance, drink & cry ¡Viva España! ¡Viva Fernando 7º! ¡Viva nuestro gobernador!

author: San Antonio
summary: Ignacio Tenorio → Gov. Calif.: that he is setting out (*en marcha*)

author: San Diego
summary: José M.ª Ruiz → Gov. Solá: that a prisoner's shackles (*grillos*) be removed and he be moved to a room by the guard while he recovers — he is very ill

author: Vergara (España)
summary: Fray Josef de Solá → Gov. Solá: begs relief; says he is at the hospice of Bidaurreta in the Vicariate (work against his temperament), hopes for the governor's charity. Savage note: this padre is a relative (brother?) of the Governor [Pablo Vicente de Solá, Basque]

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Francisco Ávila → Gov. Solá: asks an order to find a mule he gave Pedro Guzmán to break, which Guzmán improperly sold; if found, retain it until he sends for it

author: Guadalajara
summary: José de la Cruz → Gov. Solá: reports Apodaca took possession of the Viceroyalty 20 Sep; the surrender of the Laguna de Chapala insurgent stronghold after his campaign; now free of those costs, can aid California more effectively

author: México
summary: Alejandro Arana → Gov. Solá: (most of the letter 'carece de interés') postscript — Guadalajara is to supply California since Mexico City cannot; the Superintendency ordered the presidios supplied on Guadalajara's account; if unpaid, 'clame con energía al Virrey'

author: Monterey (afloat)
summary: Silvestre Madariaga → Gov. Solá: the San Blas naval cmdt., given the hemp (*cáñamo*) scarcity in the Arsenal, preferred that cargo over Solá's others — to be moved to San Diego; also requested 20 loads of flour but got only 4 *tercios*, forced to consume his last grain, so will be delayed

author: San Blas
summary: José de Vidaurre → Gov. Alta Calif.: encloses a list of war matériel (*pertrechos*) carried by the brig 'San Carlos' (Capt. Madrazo); the ship to take on at Acapulco the *memorias* the Real Hacienda ministers load

author: San Blas
summary: Relación of war matériel sent to Alta California on the warship 'San Carlos' (cmdr. Tte. de fragata Silvestre Madrazo): 20,000 musket cartridges + 20,000 loose balls (16-adarme), 5,000 country + 5,000 Castilian flints, 20 boxes of powder (1½ quintals each), 100 new English muskets w/ bayonets, 8 eight-pounder cannons w/ naval carriages + 800 balls. Signed Cmdt. of San Blas

author: Monterey
summary: Gov. Solá → the presidio commandants (circular): the Emperor of Russia notified that the ship 'Rurik,' Capt. Kotzebue, would sail on a scientific expedition — aid & attend him if he reaches Spanish dominions (per R.O. 27 Jun 1815); report his arrival at once. Commandants' acknowledgments follow. (= advance order to the Kotzebue visit, cf. Docs 29–30, Document 6)

author: San Francisco
summary: Luis Argüello → Gov. Solá: the San Joaquín esplanade nearly finished — only needs 3,600 nine-inch nails; the corral for the King's cattle finished at 'Las Pulgas,' a house to be built there; the drummers (*tambores*) can advance no further (teacher knows no more)

author: San Francisco
themes: defense; construction; cattle; missions; presidio-decay; SF
summary: Luis Argüello → Gov. Solá: urgent need of iron/nails for the esplanade (~12 arrobas, works halted); the 600 head of the King's cattle are at Mission San Juan Bautista (per Fr. Ramón Abella), need many men to drive them safely; missions of the jurisdiction to supply cattle for the troops' consumption (don't slaughter the ranch's)

author: San Francisco
summary: Luis Argüello → Gov. Solá: the King's cattle now at Las Pulgas — asks whether to ear-mark them as royal (so missions need a distinct mark); needs tar/pitch for the esplanade but 'no hay un solo pino' to extract it; esplanade done ~5–6 June (delayed by the nail shortage); ⭐ a bitter personal grievance — his reputation damaged, his honor 'anda volando de boca en boca'

author: San Francisco
summary: Luis Argüello → Gov. Solá: report of the *Rurik*'s arrival at SF Bay at 7:15 PM, 2 Oct 1816 — Russian ship, 200 tons, 40 crew incl. officers, Cmdt. Kotzebue (the one cited in R.O. 27 Jun 1815); 17-month voyage with calls at Concepción (Chile) and Unalaska, 34 days out, seeking provisions/wood/water; encloses copies of the ship's papers

author: London
summary: Spanish passport/safe-conduct for the *Rurik*, issued by Don Carlos Gutiérrez de los Ríos, Conde de Fernán Núñez, duque de Montellano (Spanish ambassador in London, *signed at London 16 Sep 1815, 'gratis y vale para este viaje'*), at the request of the Russian ambassador to Britain — for the frigate *Rurik*, Capt. Kotzebue, property of the Chancellor of the Russian Empire, Prince 'Romanoff' [Rumyantsev], on a round-the-world scientific voyage; binds all Spanish civil/military authorities in Asia, Africa & both Americas to aid it, invoking the 'estrecha alianza' Spain–Russia. Certified copy by Luis Argüello, SF 2 Oct 1816

author: Madrid
summary: The royal order behind the *Rurik* hospitality (Lardizábal, Madrid 27 Jun 1815): Spanish authorities in both Americas to receive & assist the *Rurik* (Kotzebue) on its round-the-world voyage. Routed Viceroy of Peru → Marqués de la Concordia → Pres. of Chile (*Judas Tadeo Reyes*) → Talcahuano (*Miguel M.ª de Atero*); certified-copied by Luis Argüello at SF, sent to the Viceroy 10 Oct 1816. ⚠ cf. expanded Document 6 — note this R.O. is dated 27 Jun 1815, not 1816

author: Guadalajara
summary: Gervasio Argüello → Gov. Solá: the Californias *expediente* is in the same state as last month, scarcity increasing daily; encloses the printed account of [Francisco Xavier] Mina's capture/imprisonment

(undated, unsigned) recommends the son of the Governor of Baja California, who wishes a military career, under the general's auspices. (Copyist: lacks date/place/address/signature; seems to be from Gov. Solá)

author: San Miguel
summary: Fray Juan Martín → Gov. Calif.: asks to borrow the architecture book 'Vitruvius' ('Vitrubio') to consult for the church construction at the mission

author: Guadalajara
summary: Ramón de Murcia → Gov. Solá: the habilitado Gervasio Argüello (2 months there seeking funds) will credit Solá's account once paid from the Real Cajas; the general [José de la Cruz] returned from Mexico (conferred w/ the Viceroy, received the Gran Cruz de Isabel); ⭐ no news from Spain 'a causa de los muchos piratas que hay en el golfo de México'

author: Lima
summary: José Cadenas → Gov. Solá: recommends the captain of the corvette 'Francia' [= the French corvette, Roquefeuil's *Bordelais*] sailing that day for the Northwest Coast; in June will send a ship of his own with *memorias* for his friends the padres

author: Lima
summary: Duplicate of the previous (Cadenas) with an added paragraph: asks Solá to forward the enclosed *pliego* to Fr. Ripoll; the carrying ship is an expedition projected by several individuals

author: Lima
summary: José Cadenas → Gov. Solá: recommends Don Camille de Roquefeuil, captain of the French corvette [*Bordelais*], who reached Lima seeking provisions and is sailing for the Northwest Coast (the 1816–19 French circumnavigation; visited SF 1817–18)

author: San Diego
summary: Antonio McCallum[?] → Gov. Solá: arrived safely at the presidio, at his orders, will continue to San Blas

author: Refugio
summary: José de Ortega → Gov. Solá: sends some olives in caper sauce (*aceitunas en salsa de alcaparras*)

author: San Juan Bautista
themes: russia; america; indigenous-intel; otter; concealment; missions; ⭐
summary: Fray Felipe Arroyo de la Cuesta → Gov. Solá: coastal-concealment policy — brought the *cayuco* into the mission (using it indoors to dye wool) so foreigners can't learn the coast, esteros, or otter-hunting sites; but reports Russians and an Anglo-American fished those sites, and two Russians were seen at San Pedro on the SF coast; told Lt. Estudillo & Sgt. Pico the same. [cf. Mathes ed. — the concealment-policy letter; mathes-docs-seccion-1]

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guadalupe Briones → Gov. Solá: asks relief from the back-pay (*alcances*) her deceased father left at this habilitación — she is very needy with four children to support

author: (Monterey?)
summary: Gov. Solá → José de la Cruz (long, emotional): congratulates him on the rumored Viceroyalty and the surrender of 'the Island' [Mezcala/Chapala] (pardon to Vargas & Salgado); then pleads that the garrison has not received its accustomed pay in 7 years, officers & families 'en la indigencia,' the troops naked — ⭐ 'he tenido que despojarme de la mayor parte de mi ropa para cubrirles' (Solá has given away most of his own clothing) — while campaigning continuously against the 'Indios Gentiles,' guarding mission detachments & carrying mail; happier the last soldier than the governor; hopes relief after his 5-yr term

author: México
summary: Antonio Abruna[?] → Gov. Solá: viceregal politics; petitioned the new Viceroy to free him from Calleja's investigation; Noriega's promotion to Captain favorably dispatched; urges Solá to seek an immediate grade for himself

author: México
summary: Antonio Abruna[?] → Gov. Solá: on the Kingdom's politics & the war; urges Solá to keep an effective agent in Mexico to push California's many pending matters; warns against being sent useless/demoralized officers who'd harm 'esos infelices… lo mismo que si estuviesen al servicio del gran Turco'

author: Tepic
summary: Joaquín Mondragón → Gov. Solá: relates the latest political/war news in New Spain

author: Tepic
summary: Joaquín Mondragón → Gov. Solá: various news on the Kingdom's political events & figures

author: Santa Bárbara
themes: trade; missions; fiscal; clothing; presidio-decay; santa-barbara
summary: Fermín de Genoa y Aguirre[?] → Gov. Solá: the San Luis fathers have no cause to complain (unanswered letters); relays a supply-merchant's terms for clothing the troops — can't drop below cost+principal per his Lima instructions but makes the sacrifice: ⭐ 'jamás se ha vestido ni se vestirá el soldado Californio a menos costo ni de mejor género, gracias al buen corazón… [de] Bilbao'

author: Monterey
themes: trade; tallow; missions; fiscal; diezmos; economy; ⭐
summary: Fermín de Yenza y Aguirre[?] → Gov.
