Solá: on the new tithe/duty on tallow (*sebo*) & import duties — agrees to pay the two import duties to equip the troops, has served the King in Peru (once advancing 38,000 in value), but the governor's 17% tallow duty would hurt him (fixed prices, tallow-for-goods); argues (Chile practice) the friars/missions, as the tallow producers, should pay — notes 19 missions trade in tallow; offers his entire cargo to clothe the troops

author: México
summary: Humara → Gov. Solá: approved the governor's *propuestas* (appointments) on arrival; will use his influence for California; sends 60 reams of paper; the Viceroy approved Solá's *visita*; a reserved commission needing slow, careful handling ('el asunto es delicado')

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Juan María Lugo → Gov. Solá: on a share of the estate of the late Juan José Domínguez [grantee of Rancho San Pedro, the 1784 first CA land grant], being divided among his grandchildren; since Lugo's wife is a daughter of the deceased, asks she be considered a sharer

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: [Santiago/James Smith] Wilcocks → Gov. Alta Calif.: arrived; delivered blankets (*mantas*) & other goods to the commandant; thanks Solá for the kind treatment at Monterey; will go to San Diego at week's end [2 docs?]

author: Monterey
summary: Wilcocks → Gov. Solá: thanks for the provisions; won't go to Santa Bárbara so soon; sails that night for San Luis Obispo, will explain why there

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Wilcocks → Gov. Solá: regrets Solá's refusal of an overland meeting; has 'new ideas,' wishes to meet at San Gabriel once his schooner is repaired; asks confidential leave to see the Father President; sends 40 planks (*tablones*) to the presidio

author: (San Gabriel/SD)
summary: Solá → Wilcocks: grants the interview & permission to see the Father President; they will meet at San Diego

author: San Luis Obispo
summary: Wilcocks → Gov. Alta Calif.: re carrying maize & beans from Santa Cruz to Monterey (per 'Don Mariano'); sent the schooner; the wool (*lana*) hasn't arrived; resolved to go overland to Monterey to deliver seeds at Santa Cruz & carry the wool

author: (Monterey area)
summary: Wilcocks → Gov. Solá: timber cargo — will start loading next day, sail Sunday to Sta Bárbara, then go overland via San Buenaventura / San Fernando / San Gabriel to meet Solá; asks him to wait

author: San Gabriel
summary: Solá → Wilcocks: thanks him for loading timber for Sta Bárbara, but cannot grant permission to meet overland at San Buenaventura/San Gabriel — he leaves for San Diego tomorrow, and letting a foreigner travel overland is new & against his orders (and his predecessors'); says the franchise he offers is 'hija del afecto'

author: Santa Cruz
summary: Wilcocks → Gov. Solá: won't leave Monterey until 1 Aug (the timber must be cut); so will meet Solá at San Diego instead

author: Monterey
themes: america; ships; otter; trade; missions; baja
summary: Wilcocks → Gov. Solá: voyage narrative — broke rigging on the mainmast, put into San Quintín to repair; watered at San José del Cabo, then Loreto (delivered provisions, which lacked seeds, at Gov. Argüello's request); the delays cost him a voyage to China; carried Fr. Pablo M.ª Zárate & the sick Fr. Antonio Menéndez (per the Father President); ⭐ asks license to buy otters (*nutrias*) 'the most important [venture] of my life'; Cmdt. Estudillo refused him >3 days at Monterey & ordered him out without bread/beans

author: San Antonio
summary: Solá → Wilcocks (borrador): denies license to buy otters (*nutrias*); can get provisions at Santa Cruz; they will meet at Santa Bárbara

author: Guaymas
summary: Santiago Smith Wilcocks → Gov. Solá: his schooner was seized & he is a prisoner at Guaymas; 'el malvado Ramírez' robbed a large part of his cargo to condemn the ship 'by licit means'; ⭐ denounces the slander that he meant 'levantarme con las Californias para integrarlas a los anglo-americanos mis paisanos' (to seize the Californias and annex them to the Anglo-Americans) — believes the lie reached the governor; he & his sailors held like prisoners on 2–4 reales; encloses copies of all his letters to prove there are no secrets. = expanded Document 19

author: Guaymas
summary: Santiago Smith Wilcocks → Gov. Antonio Cordero: announces his arrival at Guaymas (29th) on the *balandra* 'Nuestra Señora del Rosario,' his ship having been seized at Guaymas 30 Oct by Don Francisco Ramírez; wishes to pass to the capital to plead his right

author: Guaymas
summary: Santiago Smith Wilcocks → Gov. Cordero (2nd): Cmdt. Noriega won't let him leave for Tepic/Mexico without Cordero's licence; wants to plead before the Viceroy over the seizure of his schooner; apologizes for his imperfect Castilian (lacks his books/papers)

author: Guaymas
themes: america; russia; bodega; otter; trade; ships; foreign-relations; presidio-decay; ⭐⭐⭐
summary: Wilcocks's 'historia exacta' (autobiographical voyage account, for Cordero/the Viceroy): bought the schooner in China, sailed Sep 1815 to Port Jackson [Sydney], then a Pacific port closed to foreign trade (castle fired on him as he fled by night; he returned 2 shots, did no harm); provisioned from whalers at the Galápagos; via Guaymas & the Sandwich Islands [Hawaii] (repaired & bought otters/*nutrias*); ⭐ left Hawaii 7 Oct & reached 'un establecimiento ruso llamado Bodega' on the 25th [Oct 1816] — but 'los rusos no quisieron comprar nada'; went to Santa Bárbara (Bodega gave nothing); ran the coast carrying maize/beans/tallow & timber to rebuild the Sta Bárbara presidio + Santa Cruz Island timber for a new church; at Monterey sold Gov. Solá $700 of *manta* for the four presidios' troops (SF/Monterey/SB/SD); ⭐⭐ moral self-defense — content the blame is his 'si es culpa de dar de comer a los hambrientos y a vestir a los desnudos soldados del Rey de España,' noting most soldiers couldn't go to Mass for want of clothing; cargo of sea-otter & sea-lion skins, pearls, 23 cases of silk; robbed of 4,000 pesos at sea + more at Guaymas; gave Cmdt. Narbona a parcel of pearls; asks a passport to Mexico, subsistence money, & to bring Don Guillermo Gual. = expanded Document 19

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Juan Antonio Maya → Gov.: supply goods for the presidios of Santa Bárbara, San Gabriel & San Luis; delivered to Guerra; owed 64 pesos; gave Argüello part of San Luis's; leaves San Gabriel's with Guerra (ship overloaded). Friendly postscript signed Juan Antonio Maya

author: Monterey
summary: Juan José & Juan Antonio Maya → Gov. Solá: thank him for his good wishes on the expedition entrusted to them by Don José Carvencia[?]; offer their services

author: Monterey
summary: Juan Antonio Maya → Gov. Solá: he & his brother arrived in the brig 'San Antonio' (property of Don José Carvencia[?]), charged to put it at Solá's disposal

author: San Luis Obispo
summary: Juan Antonio Maya → Gov. Solá: delivered Andrés Villamar, boatswain of the frigate 'Tasadora[?],' to Cabo Carrillo with his *sumaria* (criminal file); will land his baggage at Sta Bárbara; a Black slave (*negro esclavo*) of the boatswain remains aboard, to be carried to Callao if approved

author: Tepic
summary: José de Achurra[?] → Gov. Solá: the merchants of Tepic & Guadalajara refuse to supply California unless they get great dividends; will send goods via José Medina (bound for California before Panama)

author: Guadalajara
summary: Fernando Palacio[?] → Gov. Solá: news of the military operations of Mina and Padre [Servando Teresa de] Mier

author: Tepic
summary: María Antonia Pinto[?] → Gov. Solá: political-military news; her son Francisco is in Baja California [presidio remount duty?]

author: San Francisco
summary: Manuel Gómez → Gov. Solá: complains of the scarcity & bad state of the SF presidio's artillerymen — all disabled & sickly, 'ha habido día que no ha tenido con qué relevar la batería' (some days too few to relieve the battery)

author: San Francisco
summary: Manuel Gómez → Gov. Solá: per orders, has tried to recruit 2–3 men as artillerymen

author: San Francisco
summary: Manuel Gómez → Gov. Solá: on completing the artillery company — could fill it with provincials (art. 128 Ordenanza) but all prefer cavalry (mounted, better pay); the 18 artillerymen are mostly sickly & useless; the 3 ordered sent to Mexico (Joaquín Mendoza, Juan Villarta, José Villaverde) 'tan ancianos y achacosos que no sirven de nada'; proposes the militia help

author: Refugio
summary: M. Frías → Gov. Solá: private matters

author: Refugio
summary: José de Ortega → Gov. Solá: received Fr. Frías's word to watch for the ship; sent vaqueros to bring the boat to the anchorage

author: Refugio
summary: Relación by José de Ortega → Gov. Solá on the boundaries/lands the Mission of Santa Bárbara occupies (vs. his Refugio rancho): 9–10 leagues E–W from the Sierra to the coast; parajes Las Llagas, Dos Pueblos, Tecolote, Las Armas, El Capitán, their springs & ciénegas; grazing history & disputes with the SB fathers (Frs. Estevan, Juan Cortés, Marcos Amestoy); the new San Marcos rancho on the río Calaguasa. Detailed land-tenure record

author: (Santa Bárbara)
summary: Francisco Morales → Gov. Solá: on his orchard; asks permission for his wife to go to San Miguel, which Estudillo denied

author: (Calif.)
summary: José Antonio Spence[?] → Gov. Solá: that Joaquín[?] arrested/seized him and a soldier defended him

author: Monterey
summary: Manuel Boronda → Gov. Solá: his daughter is to marry the distinguished soldier Don Manuel Cota

author: Lima
summary: José Carvencia[?] → Gov. Solá: recommends the expedition he sends on the brig 'San Antonio' and its supercargo [the Maya brothers]

author: Santa Bárbara
themes: france; ships; exploration; missions; trade; presidio-decay
summary: Gov. Pablo de Solá → José Carvencia: ordered the commandants to aid Maya & the French corvette; the brig 'Antonio' at San Diego finishing its cargo; ⭐ 'la Corbeta francesa, después de 9 días de permanencia en San Francisco, salió para el Noroeste' (the Roquefeuil/*Bordelais* called at SF, 9 days, then NW); the frigate *Cazadora* at Monterey; muses the missions (the province's main sellers) should appoint one buying/selling agent; reflects on the extreme scarcity supplying his troops in his 2 yrs 2 mos here

author: Monterey
summary: Manuel Gómez → Gov. Solá: two soldiers married

author: San Pablo[?]
summary: Jacinto Pinto[?] y Sáinz → Gov. Solá: various private matters

author: Loreto
summary: José Darío Argüello (Gov. Baja Calif.) → Solá: the Alta-Calif. mail hasn't arrived; Baja in 'el más deplorable estado de miseria' awaiting the San Blas memorias; recommends Santiago (family growing on a soldier's pay)

author: (Monterey)
summary: Solá → José Argüello: the mail was delayed by heavy rains since January; henceforth will leave on fixed days

author: Loreto
summary: José Argüello → Solá: lost correspondence/memoria — mail rotting between Sta Gertrudis & San Borja; the memorias shipped from Acapulco on the 'Serafín,' feared lost; sending his son Francisco Argüello as cadet to Guadalajara w/ José de la Cruz

author: Loreto
summary: Gov. José Argüello → Solá: asks a list of supplies (will pay by drafts) amid scarcity & war; ⭐ the Baja Indians, knowing the weakness of Spanish forces, grow insolent — 'se burlen de nuestras armas' — spoiling the work of reducing them to subordination

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Solá → Argüello: regrets the loss of the 'Serafín' with the memorias; various expeditions at Alta Calif.; sends recommendation letters from SF for de la Cruz

author: Guadalajara
summary: Benito Antonio Vélez → Gov. Solá: various news on the Kingdom's politics

author: Monterey
summary: Solá → Benito [Antonio] Vélez: private matters

author: Tepic
summary: Pedro Negrete → Gov. Solá: ships coming for tallow (*sebo*) — next month the brig 'Bastany[?]' sails to Calif. for tallow, & others too; it'll touch Sta Bárbara; asks the tallow owed by the settlers of Sta Bárbara & the pueblo of San Pedro be collected for delivery

author: Monterey
summary: Gaspar Giles[?] → Gov. Solá: just arrived from Lima/Callao on the Spanish frigate 'La Hermosa Mexicana' with a stock of goods to exchange for tallow (*sebos*); hopes Solá will aid the venture

author: Guadalajara
summary: María del Refugio Josefa de San Elías (abbess, Capuchin convent of Guadalajara) → Gov. Solá: begs him to protect/aid her brothers Don Ignacio & Don Manuel Solís[?] in Calif., in misery; she & her sister Sor María Juana de Sta Catarina plead

author: San Francisco
summary: [Camille] de Roquefeuil → Gov. Solá (in French): asks permission to take aboard two foreign sailors — his crew weakened by illness & desertion — pledging to admit no one without the governor's authorization. (The *Bordelais* at SF; cf. Doc 224)

author: San José de Gracia (Sonora)
summary: José Patricio Tico → Gov. Solá: hopes to Christianize various gentile Indians

author: San José de Gracia
summary: José Patricio Tico → Gov. Solá: gave 'humble & complete satisfaction' to Fr. Pedro Muñoz as ordered

author: Guadalajara
summary: Gervasio Argüello → Gov. Solá: on the impossibility of obtaining funds for California — petitions the Royal Ministers daily but gets nothing ('mucha penuria y escasez'); encloses copies of his unanswered letters to the Viceroy, de la Cruz & the Intendant; told nothing will move unless agitated with the Viceroy

author: Guadalajara
summary: Gervasio Argüello → Gov. Solá: received & will pay (when funds suffice) the libranza of 6,946 pesos 6 reales drawn by habilitado Mariano Estrada in favor of Don Santiago Smith Wilcocks 'del Comercio de Cantón' — confirms the scale of Wilcocks's Calif. supply (cf. Document 19)

author: Guadalajara
summary: Habilitado-general dossier ('copia de los documentos citados en la pág. 188') — Gervasio Argüello's reports to Viceroy Apodaca & the Real Hacienda ministers: presented the 1815 *revista* extracts + artillery request, asking a year's pay on account; ⭐⭐ states the troops have had no *socorros* for 6 years, 'ellas y sus familias están en una absoluta desnudez'; requests the revista extracts from 1810–1817 be settled (the back-pay never adjusted); relays the Baja governor's memorias request

author: Guadalajara
summary: Gervasio Argüello → Gov. Solá: the Viceroy (per the Hacienda fiscal) orders him to post a 6,000-peso bond to secure his responsibility as habilitado; asks Solá what to do so the troops' *socorros* aren't delayed

author: Guadalajara
summary: Gervasio Argüello → Gov. Solá: gave the message to José de la Cruz, who wished to help but 'no había dinero, ni para atenciones más urgentes'; they demand the bond; asks Solá's instructions

author: Guadalajara
summary: Gervasio Argüello → Gov. Solá: encloses his oficio to the Viceroy; ⭐ the King approved promotions — Luis Argüello Capt. of San Francisco, Gabriel [Moraga] lieutenant, Santiago Argüello Alférez of Sta Bárbara, José Noriega & Ignacio Martínez lieutenants, and himself (Gervasio Argüello) Alférez of San Diego; asks a posting other than SD; a soldier's place for Magín Bernal; notes he (a cadet, the ex-gov.'s son) hasn't been paid in 6 years

author: Guadalajara
summary: Gervasio Argüello → Gov. Solá: the Viceroy ordered Calif. be relieved, not done 'por las muchas atenciones'; pressed the Ministers on the 6th, told the Californias *expediente* was 'en actividad en manos del fiscal'

author: Guadalajara
summary: Gervasio Argüello → Gov. Solá: will write from Tepic/San Blas; asks Solá to send by draft the value of his *alcances* (back-pay)

author: Guadalajara
summary: Gervasio Argüello → Gov. Solá: arrived Guadalajara on the 22nd (ill on the road); the Intendant told him to deal with the royal officials; no communication with Mexico yet

author: San Blas
summary: Juan Salvador Aspiroz → Solá: warns that the Tepic merchants all hate Solá ('le aborrecen… indistintamente'), supposing he directs Cmdt. Mondragón; he imposed individual contributions; it's 'saliendo muy mal'

author: San Gabriel
summary: Juan Salvador Aspiroz → Solá: asks Solá to order the commandants to accept drafts on the Real Caja of Guadalajara in favor of Gervasio Argüello

author: Monterey
summary: Solá → Juan S. Aspiroz (borrador): willing to serve him & the owners of the frigate 'Cazadora,' but given the scarcity can't take drafts on Guadalajara; suggests applying part of the royal duties to the drafts

author: Monterey
summary: Juan Salvador Aspiroz → Solá: the frigate 'Cazadora' anchored (from Panama); sent the boatswain ashore in irons for repeated insubordination; given the danger to the expedition, will ask the cmdt. to send him under registry to Callao on the brig *San Antonio*

author: Guadalajara
summary: Joaquín de Angulo Nozagaray[?] → Solá: political news; thanks for Solá's gift of cod & salmon

author: Guadalajara
summary: Eugenio Antonio de Queravata[?] → Gov. Solá: various political news

author: México
summary: On the testament of Coronel [ex-Gov.] José Joaquín de Arrillaga (d. 1814): the fiscal *oidor*, at the request of the defenders Manuel de la Luna & Vicente Gámez, decrees that the executor Alférez José Mariano Estrada comply with clauses 2–5, 7, 8 & 13 of the will — '2 años 6 meses que [Arrillaga] falleció y aún no les da cumplimiento'

author: Tepic
summary: Pedro Negrete → Solá: the 'Paz y Religión' (Maestre José Medina) brings goods to Calif.; collect the tallow owed at Sta Bárbara & San Pedro (Noriega to collect; debtors to deposit *sebo* on the beach for loading); ⭐ 'a causa de la revolución de Chile vendrían muchos buques a California en demanda de sebo'

author: Panamá
summary: Juan Salvador Aspiroz → Pedro Negrete: going to California with an expedition & staying a while, asks Negrete to send some rebozos, kimonos[?] etc. (Asian/Pacific goods)

author: Mondragón (España)
summary: Brígida Solá → Don Pablo Vicente Solá (his niece, Basque Country): family news — her aunt the nun sent word; they went to Madrid; her sister Joaquina married the *guardia* Joaquín (brother of 'Pepe'); a 'Canido[?]' who lived w/ Solá in Madrid gave news; hears he likes the guitar; begs him to send 'alguna cosita' — she's the only unmarried one, poor; her mother has died

date_text: 1817-02-29 [⚠ impossible — 1817 not a leap year; Savage error]
author: Mondragón (España)
summary: Brígida Solá → Pablo V. Solá (2nd letter, much repeated): again begs 'alguna cosita,' fears ending an 'old maid' like her poor aunts; her mother died a month ago; signs Brígida Solá y Ceizaga[?]

author: Monterey
summary: María Luisa & Fernando → Ignacio de Acedo[?]: that Don Eduardo[?] & Don Antonio Fernández lodged in their house, arrived on the Mexican frigate with the expedition

author: Loreto
summary: Certificate by José Darío Argüello (Gov. Baja Calif.): Santiago Smith Wilcocks, capt./owner of the schooner 'Caminante,' appeared at Loreto 3 Apr w/ a passport from the Gov. of Alta Calif. (25 Feb), and provisioned the starving Loreto presidio — 126 fanegas maize, 20 of beans (+ other goods), for which he was paid. Certified copy by José M.ª Estudillo, Monterey 12 Jun 1817

author: Loreto
summary: José Darío Argüello → Solá: the Guadalajara cajas barely suffice ('vamos pasando esta triste vida llena de congojas'); on the seizure of Wilcocks's schooner 'Caminante' — nothing resolved, but captor Ramírez extracted goods so all his property is embargoed; Ramírez likely tangled it at the Provincias Internas command; Argüello has told the Viceroy of Wilcocks's good services in both Californias

author: Monterey (aboard the *Chirikoff*)
summary: Lt. Yakov Podushkin (RAC) → Gov. Solá (English translation): requests the arrest & return of a Kodiak Aleut sailor, Peter Leonoff, of the schooner 'Chirikoff,' who deserted on 30 March from the port of 'Little Bodega' — to be forwarded to Novo-Arkhangelsk or Ross. = expanded Document 20

author: Novo-Arkhangelsk
summary: RAC diplomatic protest — Alexander Baranov (Gov.-Gen. of the Russian American Possessions, Collegiate Counsellor, Knight of St. Anne 2nd Cl.) → Gov. Solá: protests 'insults to the Russian flag and the capture and keeping in cruel bondage of Russians and other people subject to our august Monarch… in the waters and coasts of California,' invoking the treaty of 1812 (arts. 3 & 4); has petitioned St. Petersburg / the Emperor; sends Lt. Yakov Podushkin aboard the *Chirikoff* as a *parlamentario*; closes 'The Holy Protector of California preserve you.' Translated by Ivan Petroff (i.e. for the Bancroft project, not 1817). = expanded Document 20

Savage's note: 'Páginas dedicadas a varios documentos en idioma ruso, cuya traducción se hará luego que concurra el Sr Pet[roff]' — flags untranslated Russian-language documents in the volume awaiting Ivan Petroff. + a forward index of two 1820 R.O.s found later (oath to the 1812 Cádiz Constitution, Madrid 15 Apr 1820, tomo p.1140; re-election of the 1814 ayuntamiento officials, 17 May 1820, tomo p.1204)

author: aboard the *Bordelais* (San Francisco)
summary: Camille de Roquefeuil → Gov. Solá (French): transmits a letter from M. Carvencia of Lima; forced to put in at San Francisco; asks Solá's protection for his ship; bases his confidence on the friendship between the two nations and 'the proofs the French gave of their sentiments toward the Spanish in the recent events in Chile'; Luis Argüello to forward two cases of French products as a gift. Signed 'De Roquefeuil, lieut. des vaisseaux du Roi'

author: San Francisco
summary: Manuel Gómez → Gov. Solá: on whether he may leave his post (Subinspector consulted; Viceroy's ruling); defends the cartridges he's charged with — not badly made, but of *bayeta* (baize), the proper material, made on his order

author: (Monterey)
summary: Gov. Solá → Luis Argüello: re buying from the RAC frigate 'Kutusoff' (cmdr. Hagemeister) — it has many goods; deal to buy ~$25,000[?] on draft against the *habilitación general*, goods for the troops & families of the four presidios at fair prices; ⭐ but the Kodiaks the frigate wants to take back are only four (some at Sta Bárbara, others at San Gabriel), some now Christian, too distant to arrive before it sails

author: (Monterey)
summary: Gov. Solá → Cmdt. Hagemeister: very well disposed toward him & his Emperor; cannot deliver the Kodiaks before his departure (ill/far off) — will return them at first opportunity to Baranov; authorizes Lt. Argüello to buy $25,000 of goods for the four presidios on draft against the Guadalajara habilitado-general

author: (Monterey)
summary: Solá → Luis Argüello: repeats the order to buy goods [from the *Kutusoff*], draw drafts, & notify him at once

author: (Monterey)
summary: Solá → Luis Argüello: reprimands his 'poca actividad y celo' — it pains him to remind a man of talent so often of the exactitude & vigilance required when a foreign ship comes to that port [SF]; handle the sale & purchase shrewdly

author: Monterey
summary: Solá → Luis Argüello: demands reports on the foreign ships maneuvering at SF (whether sailed or still there) and the proceeds of goods sold/produce extracted; displeased Argüello hasn't reported; notes (via Fr. Marcelino Marquínez) that Argüello dispatched an expedition — wants its motive/result

author: Monterey
summary: Inventory of the effects of Don Boris Tarasov & the 'Russian Indians' [Aleuts], taken at the Ensenada de San Pedro and returned by the governor's order to Lt. Jacobo (Yakov) Podushkin: a sack of rice, 10½ v. purple ribbon, 6 small bags of otter [pelts], 9 sea-lion skins, 12 gut-shirts (*camisas de tripa* = Aleut kamleikas), fishing instruments, lead, 9 badly-damaged muskets, Boris's notes, tools; note that tobacco/*bayetón*/a field-tent were consumed feeding the returning Aleuts; signed Monterey 5 May 1817 '(aquí hay varias palabras en idioma ruso).' [= the Mathes 'Tarasov effects-lists'; cf. Doc 99, Document 20]

author: Monterey
summary: Seizure inventory of Tarasov's & the Aleuts' effects — from Sgt. Carlos Carrillo, captured at the Ensenada de San Pedro from 'the diver called Boris, with twelve cayucos': 3 sacks rice, 2 tobacco, *bayetón*, purple China ribbon, blue cloth; ⭐ 2 small bags + 40 otter pelts (*pieles de Nutria*), 5 sea-lion skins, 12 gut-shirts (kamleikas), fishing gear, lead, 9 badly-damaged muskets (one barrel burst, most locks useless), powder, a field-tent, Boris's notebook, hammer/tongs/augers, an empty bottle. (capture-side list; cf. Doc 273; Mathes 'Tarasov effects-lists')
Savage's note: ff. 888–895 are a duplicate of the judicial disposition on Coronel Arrillaga's testamentaría (already at folio 198 = catalog Doc 255) — no new content

author: México
summary: Apodaca → Gov. Solá: the British frigate 'Buena Esperanza,' from Bengal with cargo for the Russian establishments (NW coast & Sandwich Islands), put in at Mazatlán (water/provisions, mainmast repair, sick crew); orders Capt. Silvestre Ramsay[?] to leave at once once repaired (fair prices), not to touch any other Spanish port on pain of confiscation; point-commanders to enforce *comiso*

author: Arispe
summary: Antonio Cordero → Gov. Solá: the brig 'Cosak' at Guaymas — tried to water, threatened with sinking for lacking a port-captain; its Capt. Clark[?] jailed; the brig then set sail, raised the Anglo-American flag, fired 3 cannon-shots & fled (with abuse/improperios); orders it seized wherever it arrives, anyone landing arrested, goods confiscated

author: Arispe
summary: Antonio Cordero → Gov. Solá: the intendancy (31 Dec) condemned Wilcocks's schooner 'Caminante,' its cargo & all belonging to Santiago Smith [Wilcocks] & 'Sirca[?]' as forfeit (*decomiso*); orders Solá to embargo anything of theirs in his ports (closes the Wilcocks legal case; cf. Document 19)

author: Guadalajara
summary: Gervasio Argüello → Gov. Solá: posted the bond the *Hacedor* demanded (guarantor Matías Vergara, on condition the money be kept in his house); still gets no *socorro* for Calif. (penury of the Real Cajas)

author: Guadalajara
summary: Gervasio Argüello → Solá: transcribes the Viceroy's order on the bond obligation — if he can't post it, the Governor should name another habilitado

author: Guadalajara
summary: Gervasio Argüello → Solá: cannot receive *socorros* until the bond obstacle is cleared; resents being blamed for the delays; still seeking a hard-to-find guarantor; sends the *libranza*

author: Guadalajara
summary: Gervasio Argüello → Solá: inserts a royal order to the Mexico treasury to send the Real Cajas the revista extracts 1810–1814 of the Alta Calif. troops so their back-pay can be paid

author: San Antonio
summary: Carlos Antonio Carrillo → Gov. Solá: operations vs. the rebels [Bouchard] — arrived with 20 armed vecinos (2 muskets, 14 lances, 3 sabres, pistols); the enemy descended on Mission San Luis [Obispo] to attack it; he plans to go to its aid

author: San Francisco
summary: Luis Argüello → Gov. Solá: sends the 4 Christian Indians of Mission San Francisco — Teocoy[?], Félix, Nájara, Crescencio — culprits in the murder of Tobías & Norato; a revenge killing (the victims had slain Crescencio's brother); the two were surprised sleeping 4 leagues from San José — one beaten to death, one hanged, bound, stones at the neck, thrown in a deep pool; divers recovered the body

author: San Isidro
summary: José de la Guerra → Gov. Solá: the enemy [Bouchard] sailed from the Ensenada del Refugio toward [Sta Bárbara] after burning & robbing the Ortega/Refugio rancho; ship-sighting reports; can't get Monterey's help; 'no tiene artillería útil ni gente; hará la defensa que pueda'; recalled the Monterey relief; if the rebels don't quit the coast he'll attack them where they land

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra → Solá: amid the meat shortage for the troops & families, takes the tithe cattle (from the Father President) on the company's account; asks Purísima's site 'Las Cañadas' (only during the calamitous times, until the presidio gets 2 yrs of memorias); already there with 672 head tended by 3 soldiers

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra → Solá: on an expedition to recover fugitive mission Indians (*cimarrones*) — needs >25 days, best in Sept/Oct or harvest (Jun/Jul); the fugitives shelter on an islet 'Trapui[?]' ~70 leagues NE, surrounded by great lakes (*lagunas*), unreachable without boats — the refuge of most of the region's *cimarrones*; recommends night marches, 30 men from there + San Diego, trusted Indian scouts ahead

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra → Solá: on withdrawing the invalids & service auxiliaries from escort duty; asks whether the vaqueros & Indian auxiliaries should also retire; mission Indians to help with the night guard at the rancho to avoid a surprise

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra → Solá: received the soldier José Antonio Romero, sentenced to 6 months public works; will enforce the sentence

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra → Solá: sends a reinforcement — Lt. Ignacio Martínez setting out at 8 AM with whatever people he can gather (Indians, non-capital prisoners offered the King's pardon if they fight with honor); ⭐ many unarmed (only fine cavalry muskets here; can be fitted with pikes/lances, 'la mejor arma' for them); 'yo quedo sin ninguna… el Señor no tiene atadas las manos; venceremos con su auxilio'

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra → Solá: given the critical circumstances, divested himself of the *habilitación*, handing it provisionally to Alférez Maytorena (to focus on defense)

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Guerra → Solá: the two rebel [Bouchard] frigates left the presidio anchorage at dawn, vanishing from sight; sent reinforcement to Los Ángeles and 24 soldiers under Carrillo to San Diego

author: Rancho de la Real Hacienda
summary: Gov. Solá → the Fathers from San Luis to San Diego (circular): the rebels [Bouchard] hold the Monterey establishment/beach without daring to approach Mission San Carlos; yesterday set fire to the Castillo & Presidio [of Monterey]; keep lookouts, relay their course. ⭐ Followed by the missions' acknowledgment-relay chain with timestamps — Purísima (Fr. Mariano Payeras), Sta Inés (Fr. Francisco Javier Vara), San Buenaventura (José Ruiz), San Fernando (Fr. Marcos A. de Vitoria), San Gabriel (Fr. Joaquín Nuez), San Juan Capistrano (Fr. Gerónimo Boscana), San Luis Rey (Fr. Jaime Escudé) — the coastal warning network in action, 28–30 Nov 1818

author: Monterey
summary: Solá → the commandants of Sta Bárbara & San Diego: the season being advanced (less risk the rebel ships will strike), withdraw the invalids & vecinos to their planting/homes, cover the points with the regular garrisons; keep vigilance; missions to post vaqueros. (Dated before the raid actually hit Monterey ~20–24 Nov)

author: Loreto
summary: José Darío Argüello → Gov. Solá: forwards the representation of the American corvette 'Mercurio' (the subject sought not in his jurisdiction); sends the *diligencias* for Solá to comply with the superior order (late follow-up on the 1813–14 *Mercurio* prize, Docs 101–102)

author: San Francisco
summary: Luis Argüello → Gov. Solá: complains Solá doesn't write; Gabriel Moraga has marched from the presidio; four cannons placed at the most advantageous points, their esplanades being built

author: Monterey
summary: José M.ª Estudillo → Gov. Solá: on arms-making & fortification + the rebel [Bouchard] destruction at Monterey — repairing the muskets; approves making machetes to arm the company + sword-scabbards/belts; has mounted the 3 cannons & the esplanade, about to make grapeshot bags (*sacos de metralla*); on the Sta Cruz events sent the Alférez, Sergeant & others; ⭐ 'da compasión ver la madera y la teja' — and a wrecked house (only the oven-rooms left, orchard fence smashed, peach trees stripped)

date_text: 1819-08-17 [⚠ '1818' in some refs; content matches the 1819 *Cosak* voyage]
author: Monterey
summary: José M.ª Estudillo → Gov. Solá: the scarcity of funds at the Guadalajara cajas; the purchase Pablo de la Portilla brought (arrived at the San Luis ensenada) won't reach San Diego under a month (≥120 leagues, bad waterless country); the brig *Cosak* if it stays will have abundant fish for the crew

author: Monterey
summary: José M.ª Estudillo → Gov. Solá: Buitrón arrived with 'la presa' (the captives) — 37 women & children held at El Carmelo (+ some orphans given to live there); the captives kept from the presidio so the Indian men, seeing their wives & families, won't flee; he is building the furnaces for red-hot shot (*hornillos para la bala roja*)

author: Monterey
summary: José M.ª Estudillo → Gov. Solá: on the state of the batteries — of the cannons the [rebels] burst, found 3 that, mounted at once, could serve as a parapet; has mounted them; requested the 3 cannons SF left

author: Monterey
summary: Estudillo → Solá: on the events of 22 Nov [the Bouchard attack on Monterey]; doubts the efficacy of the Viceroy's measures; beginning the timber-cut; needs workers 'para levantar las ruinas'

author: Monterey
summary: Estudillo → Solá: copied the four royal orders of 4 Apr 1818, 12 Jun, etc.

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: José de la Guerra → Solá: Colorado-Indian attack at Mission San Buenaventura — 20 Colorado Indians came, repelled by the guard (Cabo Fuentes); during Mass the rebels seized muskets, killing a corporal & a servant; 12 soldiers + a sergeant w/ a cannon sent in pursuit. [→ mil-history register; cf. Doc 78, Document 11]

author: (in the field)
summary: Gabriel Moraga → Gov. Solá: expedition against the Amajava (Mojave) — set out with 50 men + 10 vecinos eastward; reached Mongarayait[?]/Agallaga (last pasture, 27 horses lost), carried water in vessels, went inland; a gentile Indian killed one of his boys (to rob his horse & blanket). [= expanded Document 11]

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Gabriel Moraga → Gov. Solá: forwards reports from the San Gabriel & San Fernando escorts on the Amajaba (Mojave) Indians' intentions; warns to live with great caution

author: San Gabriel
summary: Hilario Machado → the commandants up to Sta Bárbara: warning of the Mojave (Amajaba) approach — the Indian corporal of Sumibabi (18 lgs E) reported 20 gentile Indians of San Sebastián relating that an escaped captive among the Amajabas faithfully warned the Mojaves had set out toward these missions 'a pelear y a hacer el daño posible'. [= expanded Document 11; indigenous-intel]

author: San Blas
themes: defense; ships; navigation; foreign-relations; bouchard; military-history
summary: Instructions for the relief-expedition ships 'San Carlos' (war-brig) & 'Reina de los Ángeles' (armed merchant frigate) — to relieve Alta Calif. (attacked Nov 1818 by 2 rebel frigates) w/ 100 troops + artillery; ⭐ detailed sailing orders (route via the Socorro Islands, approach San Francisco to avoid falling leeward of Monterey, avoid any encounter with [rebel] ships, anchor at Monterey unless taken); ⭐ the King's order to keep 'toda armonía con el pabellón anglo-americano y el de la Gran Bretaña' — the only maritime enemy being the insurgent colonies; intercept any ship aiding the rebels; before returning, urge the missions to ship hemp (*cáñamo*) for the San Blas Arsenal. Cmdr. Gonzalo Gómez de Ulloa, Capt. José Bandini (cf. Doc 87)

author: Monterey
themes: bouchard; defense; military-history; missions; scorched-earth; women; ranchos; intelligence; ⭐⭐
summary: Gov. Solá → the presidio commandants — the foundational Bouchard-defense circular + full mobilization/evacuation *ordenanza* (signed Monterey 8 Oct 1818). Warning (via Guerra, from an American ship): 2 pirate frigates (34 guns / 18–20 guns, 350 men) left the Sandwich Islands, bound for this coast in 4–6 days. Plan: secure all church silver/gold to named inland depots (SF→San José; Monterey→Soledad; San Luis→Sta Margarita; Purísima→San Antonio rancho; SD→San Antonio de Pala…); cache provisions; evacuate women & children; drive cattle & horse-herds inland; muster discharged men & invalids (Luis Argüello at SF; Ortega & Mariano Castro ranchos); a flying squad w/ 300-cartridge reserve; disperse 2/3 of the powder inland; ⭐ spike the cannons if abandoned; double mounted-Indian lookouts; ⭐⭐ a statewide mounted-courier relay route (SF→San Mateo→San José→Castro's rancho→Monterey→Soledad→San Antonio→San Miguel→San Luis→Purísima→Sta Inés→Sta Bárbara→San Buenaventura…, relay-padres Oliva/Cabot/Ripoll/Sánchez); free the prisoners for service; Guerra named 2nd-in-command of arms / accidental governor if Solá falls. = expanded Document 21

author: Madrid
summary: Royal order (copy) that army generals & chiefs abstain from levying war-contributions on their own

author: San Francisco
summary: Luis Argüello → Gov. Solá: encloses a list of prisoners awaiting sentence

author: Misión de San Carlos
summary: Gov. Solá → the presidio commandants: a ship was sighted (now vanished) — stay vigilant; warn the women & children to be ready to leave the pueblo

author: Monterey
summary: Manuel Gómez → Gov. Solá: on siting the maestranza (armory) & powder-magazine for the matériel that Subteniente Ramírez brings; all ready before the workmen (*obreros*) arrive — each at 2 pesos/day, $50/month on arrival; the Departmental junta made Gómez responsible for the works/workshop/tools & accounts

author: Monterey
summary: Manuel Gómez → Gov. Solá: encloses a memorial of Andrés Martínez

author: Monterey
summary: Manuel Gómez → Gov. Solá: the best site for the armory & magazine is Monterey or Mission Santa Clara — Monterey has abundant timber in the Carmelo sierra (6 lgs) & is under the governor's watch; Sta Clara has oaks; SF's timber (Contra Costa) is hard/costly to extract; Sta Bárbara lacks timber; SD's is remote; decide before the *maestranza* arrives

author: San Francisco
summary: Manuel Gómez → Solá: imprisonment of Tomás Patrón[?] & his wife on suspicion of adultery (the wife with the husband's consent)

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: the misfortune to the artilleryman 'Brincel[?]' in the salute on the Virgin of Guadalupe's day (12 Dec); requests a skilled surgeon

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: death of artilleryman Joaquín Mendoza; grave condition of Sebastián Armas[?]

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: death of 'Brincel[?]' (the salute-accident victim)

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: 9 casualties (*bajas*) in the artillery company

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: minor service matters

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: re quarterly supply accounts to the Indian [militia artillery]; his habilitado can't keep the monthly list

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: sent Carlos Castro to Sta Clara; the cause of the salute accident — the cannon lacked proper fittings (*gualdras*) and was loaded loose/in bulk; not to load so in future

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: the poor respect of some Indian militiamen; the nakedness & misery of the artillerymen & all the troop — 'carecen hasta de calzones' (lack even trousers); the company's losses & scarcity

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: received the *guía* for the rest of the artillery matériel from Mexico (via Ramírez)

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: will cut timber with 4 Indian axemen

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: approves drilling the militia artillerymen

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: timber-cutting at San Francisquito (marked oaks); the Indians ran out of food, Fr. Sosa[?] said the King had ample maize/beans at the SF presidio, so the Indians left

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: asks Solá to explain arts. 3,4,5,7,16 of the Artillery Regulation

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: bitterly complains of the nakedness & misery of his own family and himself

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: re the 3 cannons of Santa Clara (stored in a corner of the cantor's school)

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → José M.ª Estudillo: noted the imprisonment of artilleryman José A. Frías

author: San Francisco
summary: Manuel Gómez → Gov. Solá — the 1820 Fort San Joaquín collapse report (= expanded Document 7): only 8 cannons, 2 unserviceable (12-pdr's mounting & pitted bore; 8-pdr's ball jams for lack of a *rasador*); gunner's tools broken; 2 cannons at the landing-point unusable for lack of platform planking, emplaced 'in sand'; not a single veteran artilleryman (the rest too ailing to drill); had to destroy cartridge-boxes to sew sacking; little grapeshot, 'only round shot'; a ship sighted near the port at 6 PM as he wrote

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Cmdt. Luis Argüello: encloses the Gov.'s letter to the fathers to provide iron, copper, etc. for artillery tools

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: Antonio Alentura[?] arrived, confined to the detachment for bad conduct; will watch him

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: bring the cannons from the Sta Clara mission; re the timber cut at San Francisquito

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: on the state & service of the artillery — the other presidios get all from the missions, not SF; SF has no forge (*fragua*); San Diego & Monterey have red-hot-shot furnaces, SF still doesn't

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: encloses the memorial of the mother of militia artilleryman Francisco Pacheco

author: San Francisco
summary: Gómez → Solá: reports the crime of artilleryman Salvador Altamirano; asks whether to form a *sumaria*

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: asks that Vicente Herrera, Basilio Quevedo & Luis Herrera go to SF to work in the *maestranza*

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: the *sumaria* against artilleryman Altamirano for theft — no witness proved it, only suspicion from his antecedents; asks Solá to assign a penalty (redoing it would be fruitless); ⭐ detail: they 'had to write with gunpowder' (*escribir con pólvora* — ink shortage)

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: musket/shotgun barrels from Estrada can be repaired, but without armory workmen it will take longer

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: the maestranza budget — sends the workmen's cost estimate; of the $1,476 7½ rls Subteniente José Ramírez brings, $730-14 rls remains from the prior balance to deduct

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: on moving some private papers and his family from SF to Monterey

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: Sebastián Armas marching to SF; 8 retired Indians there

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: the artillery funds nearly exhausted; gave the Sta Clara & San José missions iron

author: Monterey
summary: José Ramírez → Solá: Sgt. Ojeda lost the *pliego* ordering rations for the 4 *maestranza* workmen

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: the artillery-repair workmen from Mexico should work at each presidio progressively

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: money shortage halting the *maestranza* works; Subteniente José Ramírez disbursed $50 of his own pocket to keep work going; asks Solá to remedy it

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: with the $500 sent, the shortages eased & the Sta Bárbara artillery repair will proceed

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: will send the account of funds Ramírez drew in Mexico + a soldier-list

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: economic matters of the military service

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: forwards the retirement cédula of invalid artilleryman Juan Martínez

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: encloses various artillery reports

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: encloses the packing-invoice of the effects from the brig 'San Carlos'

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: complains of the poor obedience of some subordinates

author: Monterey
summary: Gómez → Solá: encloses the *maestranza* budget the workmen formed; info on salaries

author: Monterey
summary: Inventory of the archive of the Monterey Comandancia (by José Estrada): legajos 'Superior Gobierno' 1774–1800 & 1801–1809; *filiaciones*/revistas/service records; 16 old *filiaciones*; 4 vols. printed Leyes de Indias; 2 vols. Ordenanza de Marina; 2 vols. *actas acordadas*; 8 parchment vols.; 6 governor's circulars of the year. A finding-aid to the lost Monterey archive (cf. the San Diego inventory, Doc 46)

author: Monterey
summary: Gov. Solá → the Monterey & SF commandants: re Sonora–Californias communication (Baja Gov. Argüello's superior order, via Arispe & Guaymas); sets the monthly mail — Alta Calif. from San Diego on the 1st, from Monterey the 24th–25th

author: Monterey
summary: Solá → the SF & Monterey commandants: re the Russian expedition to the Poles — the Russian govt asks Spain to protect/aid the polar-discovery expedition (re-application of the 1819 R.O., cf. Doc 74; relevant to the Vasiliev corvettes at SF 1820, Document 10)

author: Monterey
summary: Solá → SF & Monterey commandants: the Viceroy warns four insurgent frigates left Chile to harass the New Spain coasts; stay vigilant per the precautions — esp. the 8 Oct 1818 circular (= Doc 308/Document 21)

author: Monterey
summary: Solá → Monterey & SF commandants: form companies of 'realistas fieles' (loyal royalists) — send a list of all civilians 15+ fit for arms (incl. useful invalids) w/ their qualities for choosing officers

author: Monterey
summary: Solá → commandants: communicates the change the Austrian government made in its naval flag (for ship identification)

author: México
summary: Royal concession to the Cádiz expeditions for the Californias (printed) — duplicate of Doc 47 (tomo pp.48–54)

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gob Solá: printed circular on the oath & admission of the 1812 Cádiz Constitution (the 1820 Riego liberal restoration reaching California)

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Gabriel Moraga → Gob Solá: encloses the memorials of soldiers Cota & Guzmán requesting discharge

author: San Francisco
summary: Luis Argüello → Gob Solá: ⭐ arrival of the Russian war-corvettes 'Otkrytie' & 'Blagonamerennyi' at SF (anchored 22nd, 3 PM); will receive them cordially/hospitably. = expanded Document 10 (the Vasiliev expedition; cf. Docs 74, 359)

author: Rada de San Pedro
summary: Customs record — Don Ignacio Mancisidor's imported goods (Cuzco cloth, *bafetas*, alpaca-goods, etc. from Peru) to the presidio & missions, total $2,284-4, duties 6¼% = $140-7, paid to administrator Joaquín de la Torre

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Gabriel Moraga → Gob Solá: on the Amajaba (Mojave) & Christian-fugitive (*cimarrones*) threat — prefers the expedition in May (horses fat, better weather); doesn't think trail-lookouts necessary; doesn't fear they'll attack the missions. [cf. Document 11]

author: Monterey
summary: José M.ª Estudillo → Solá: on the sale of aguardiente — Oliva arrived w/ brandy; proposes a regulated public *cantina* (a trustworthy seller) under rules: ⭐ never sell to Indians; drink on-site only (unless medicinal, w/ a chit); per-person limits; no credit, no pawning soldiers' clothing/mounts/saddles; hours 6 AM–7 PM; no sale during Mass; the tavern within the presidio to keep order; seller takes 1 real per peso, $6/barrel

author: Monterey
summary: José M.ª Estudillo → Solá: history of religious ministry at the Monterey presidio since 1796 — which Franciscans came each Lent to confess the cavalry & infantry & missionize in the chapel (the governor's family attending): Frs. Antonio Jayme, [Lasuén], Francisco Pujol, Baltasar Carnicer, José Viñals, Florencio Ibáñez, Ismael Martín, Juan Amorós; this year Fr. [Vicente F.] Sarría (Father President) less obliging; asks Solá to intervene so the troops can fulfil their Easter duty

author: Santa Clara
summary: Luis Argüello → Solá: for the company of 'realistas fieles,' having rejected José Castillo for sergeant, proposes Joaquín Higuera (sgt.) & Juan Alviso (cabo)

author: Santa Clara
summary: Luis Argüello → Solá: the distinguished soldier Mauricio Gamboa serving as a prisoner

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Pablo de la Portilla → Solá: Lt. Maytorena[?] requested money; only $2,000 remain; suggests spending it on sergeants'/officers' salaries & footwear for the troop

author: San Diego
summary: José M.ª Estudillo → Solá: Santiago Argüello went to review the escorts & their armament and to settle accounts with the missions for supplies to the habilitación

author: San Diego
summary: Francisco M.ª Ruiz → Solá: on cotton cultivation — the harvest at the nearby mission succeeded; cotton is cold-sensitive (fails year 1, better after); Fr. Tapis tried it at San Gabriel in 1808 & failed for the cold; recommends planting it at the pueblo of Los Ángeles

author: San Diego
summary: Francisco M.ª Ruiz → Solá: received notice of the insurgent-ship threat; will comply with the precautions

author: Monterey
summary: José Estrada → Solá: trivial matters

author: México
summary: Conde del Venadito → Gob Solá: sharp reply to Solá's nº 228 (21 Oct) complaining of the 'wasted' expense of the *San Carlos* & *Reina de los Ángeles* (100 men) — defends the relief expedition's utility (it also took out country produce), asks de la Cruz for explanation

author: México
summary: Venadito → Solá: re Don Sebastián de Arrieta — may move from Peru to settle in California (with a $300 retirement salary, paid by the Guadalajara cajas); his indemnification for insurgent-caused losses denied

author: México
summary: Venadito → Solá: the King grants Don José García[?] of Cádiz to send a free-trade expedition of national produce/goods to the California ports, San Blas, Guaymas & Acapulco, w/ all the franchises of the R.O.s of 1793–1819 (cf. Doc 47). [header names a 'fragata americana'[?

author: México
summary: Venadito → Solá: re the need to administer Confirmation — the apostolic faculty has expired; the King is petitioning Pius VII for a new Bull

author: Santa Bárbara
summary: Pablo de la Portilla: certificate for Sgt. Roberto Pardo[?], denied the Alférez grade granted him accidentally

author: San Juan Bautista
summary: Solá → Monterey & SF commandants: given the govt's scarcities, any national ship arriving w/ goods owes the 6¼% royal duties (known since 1817)

author: México
summary: Royal order: the 1814 ayuntamiento officials (alcaldes, regidores, procuradores, síndicos) may be re-elected [under the restored 1812 Constitution]

author: San Diego
summary: José M.ª Estudillo → Solá: measures against the [Colorado] Indians & Amajabas (Mojaves) who — per the captain of the Cocomaricopas — intended to attack the missions

author: San Diego
summary: Estudillo → Solá: various adultery/misconduct cases of minor persons

author: San Diego
summary: José M.ª Estudillo → Solá — Colorado-River raiding (= expanded Document 11): fugitive Christian *cimarrones* of San Diego & San Miguel + gentiles kill Real Hacienda cattle/horses as far as the Llano de Santa Ana, trade them on the Colorado; attack the mission ranchos, killing shepherds & carrying off the women; account of measures & skirmishes

author: San Diego
themes: indigenous-military; defense; missions; colorado; mojave; cocomaricopa; exploration; ⭐⭐
summary: Estudillo → Solá — defense vs. the Colorado-River 'indios bárbaros' + tribal geography: urges walling Missions San Fernando & San Gabriel; the Colorado tribes (Cocomaricopas, Papagos, Talchidumas…) united on both banks against the Yumas, Apaches & Amajabas/Mojaves; a 7-day itinerary San Gabriel → the Colorado (San Antonio, San José, San Sebastián, Jesús María, Paso de la Alegría, the Mojaves)

author: San Gabriel
themes: indigenous; exploration; colorado; cocomaricopa; intelligence; trade; ⭐⭐
summary: Declaration of the Captain of the Cocomaricopas (= expanded Document 12): his ranchería east of the Colorado (~130 men; the Chiduma & Cocomaricopa nation; he is the head captain); 6 days to the first presidio, Tucson; *gente de razón* come there to trade; he went for cotton & beads; can carry letters to Tucson in 15–20 days. Taken by Santiago Argüello

author: San Vicente
summary: José Manuel Ruiz → Solá: encloses the Gov. of Baja California's letter

author: Loreto
summary: José Darío Argüello → the Lt. Cmdt. of Frontiers: resigns the political & military command of Baja California (his infirmities), transferring it to Capt. Fernando de la Toba

author: San Diego
summary: Estudillo → Solá: relates the lie of a fugitive Indian who falsely claimed the Amajabas (Mojaves) had attacked his ranchería, and the measures taken

author: San Gabriel
summary: Francisco M.ª Ruiz → Solá: via a female Indian captive of the Amajabas (Mojaves), learned they were planning to attack Missions San Gabriel & San Fernando

author: San Diego
summary: Estudillo → Solá: on the diezmo & the Real Hacienda ranch (add 400–500 cattle); Zavaleta's memorial; ⭐ the mission-secularization question — argues the law transferring missions 10 yrs after founding shouldn't apply to New California (its neophytes 'de diferente especie')

author: San Diego
summary: Francisco M.ª Ruiz → Solá: the frigate 'Reina de los Ángeles' sailed for San Blas (carrying a demented officer & family); the militia artilleryman Berdugo deserted from the Castillo & embarked secretly

author: San Diego
summary: Estudillo → Solá: an appointment matter; ⭐ María Eustaquia de la Cruz found 'desflorada'; the case caused by Antonio Alvitre[?] (held prisoner)

author: San Diego
themes: defense; foreign-relations; legal; personnel; otter; san-diego
summary: Pablo de la Portilla → Solá: complains at length of his imprisonment/confinement — punished, he says, for reporting the foreign frigate that sounded San Diego harbor & entered the bay, faulting Francisco M.ª Ruiz for not preventing it; wants a passport to vindicate himself before Alejo García Conde; is carried confined to Sta Bárbara on the *Reina*; mentions an American schooner buying otters at Sta Bárbara

author: Loreto
summary: José Darío Argüello → Solá: confirms he resigned for ill health, transferred command to de la Toba; the Frontier Comandancia under José Manuel Ruiz, who could aid San Diego in danger

author: Monterey
summary: Royal decree of 13 May 1814 (the Cortes) establishing in each provincial capital a 'Hospicio de inutilizados en el Servicio militar' (home for disabled veterans, native or foreign); 34 articles (duplicate)

author: Monterey
summary: Solá → SB & SD commandants: a ship sighted at San Luis Obispo fled on seeing the frigate 'Reina de los Ángeles'; padres to keep horses ready for the postal service

author: Monterey
summary: Solá → commandants: the Viceroy reports the English frigate 'Aspiroz' supplied at San Blas (Valparaíso goods), the 'Piferin[?]' expected; watch them — any tolerance is the commandants' strict responsibility

author: Monterey
summary: Solá → SB & SD commandants: a provisional $3,000 contribution from the missions (prorated), pending the Viceroy's ruling on duties for the brig 'San Francisco Javier' cargo; each mission to list receipts/shipments

author: Monterey
summary: Solá → Monterey & SF commandants: the 6¼% duty on goods from New Spain/Spanish America; padres to present original invoices; honest port registers; the Father Prefect agreed the missions would build shelters for the customs employees near the landings

author: Monterey
summary: Solá → SB & SD commandants: since [José] Bandini can't pay the duties in cash, he may pay in merchandise at fair prices

author: St. Petersburg
summary: The Directors of the Russian-American Company (Cramer & H.G. Severin) → Gov. Solá (= expanded Document 8): thank him for receiving RAC officers at the California ports; praise the mutual grain-for-Russian-goods trade; invoke the close ties between the two governments; announce a new RAC frigate ('Borodino[?]' under Lt. Zacarías Ponafidin[?]) bound for the Russian colonies that may touch Alta California — ask aid & good reception

author: Monterey
summary: Solá → the RAC Directors (= expanded Document 9): acknowledges their 22 Sep 1819 letter (forwarded via the brig 'Buldakov[?]' by the Governor of Sitka); assures them he has shown all the Russian Emperor's subjects — war-ship officers & private traders alike — his particular goodwill (as Russian officers incl. the current Gov. of Sitka can attest); their nation's people have been & will be especially attended

author: San Vicente
summary: José Manuel Ruiz → Solá (= expanded Document 15): the insurgents' depredations — attack on Mission San José del Cabo & the presidio of Loreto (Baja Calif.), the population including the governor fleeing both in panic

author: Madrid
summary: Royal decree on pay increases for the *Ultramar* veteran troops (the full multi-article text cross-ref'd at Doc 140/p.501) — cites the reglamento of 7 Oct 1802 & Viceroy Calleja; pay scales by rank (captains, *granaderos* w/ double bread ration, etc.). Signed El Marqués de Campo Sagrado, Madrid 31 Mar 1816; *Es copia* México 9 Oct 1816 (Patricio Humana). Followed by 'Fin del tomo XX' — the volume ends here

author: Arrillaga
summary: Mission Santa Catalina Mártir foundation in BC frontier. 8 leguas from Sierra SPM; 5 rancherías ~500 souls; only direction left = east toward Río Colorado per Dominican friars

author: Arrillaga
summary: Santa Catalina cont'd: troop garrison 1 cabo + 6 soldiers insufficient; 5 plazas for 400 leguas of Peninsula; ships exposed to Isla del Tiburón hostile tribes; timber shortage suspends courier voyages

author: Arrillaga
summary: Santa Catalina logistics: ornaments from Loreto/Mulegé; settlers transported on goleta *Valdés*; Tiburón attacks on shipping route
Estevan Martínez commands Fragata *Princesa* from Guaymas with situados — the Nootka seizure commander now reduced to supply duty

author: Arrillaga
summary: Mason recruited from Culiacán for SJC stone church — none in all CA or Los Álamos; 5-month journey. Skilled-labor famine

author: Arrillaga
summary: 2–3 English ships at Cape region. Sent his only 7 soldiers. Requests 100 muskets — 'el calibre no importa'

author: Arrillaga
summary: English worsening: 4 soldiers + 1 cabo + 6 vecinos at one mission. 'No hay armas'; 'faltan los elementos para combatir.' Retreat families to interior

author: Arrillaga
summary: 3 frigates anchored at Loreto surgidero — the capital itself. Left behind captured balandra. Intel: ships with 22–24 cannons, 60–116 crew; 'no dejan buque sin darle caza.' *Revolución* under 'Juan Pivoré' coming

author: Bernardo Juárez Infantzón report
summary: Ransom of balandra seized May 21: sugar, chocolate, 1 church bell. Losses ~$8,000. English held from 10th to 16th

author: Arrillaga
summary: KEY DISPATCH: 'la situación de la Península es muy triste, rodeada de buques ingleses, cuyos número no bajan de 4, y sin ningunos elementos de defensa.' Total southern garrison: 8 soldiers, 1 cabo, 1 sargento. Proposes 50-man militia at 4 reales/day

author: Arrillaga
summary: Bergantín *Activo* arrives; Capt. Salvador Hidalgo suspends Sonora mission — unsafe. Spots frigate *Revolución* to north. English capturing vessels, bringing to Puerto Escondido. Hidalgo forms battery with 15 men

author: Arrillaga
summary: English fleet of 4 frigates departs. 4 English apprehended at Cabo SL — commander ordered 3 hanged. Spanish brig Acapulco→San Blas captured

author: Viceroy
summary: Viceroy orders: no refuge for English ships; prevent disembarkation. Hidalgo supplies 20 muskets + 15 from *Sutil*. Militia of 50 approved, armed with 26 muskets. By Oct 18: 67 men including 4 cabos, 3 sargentos, under Sgto. Javier Aguilar

author: Arrillaga
summary: Requests discarded ship flags — Peninsula has none. Courier ship *Enrique* departed Dec 1, never returned — search failed

author: Arrillaga
summary: 'Haber declarado España la Guerra a la Nación Rusa' — received Dec 27, 1799. War of Second Coalition. Militia reduced to 50

author: Arrillaga
summary: 3 English frigates at Ensenada de Palmas. Goleta *Californio* held in port. Scorched-earth order: 'quemar antes que entregarla'

author: Arrillaga
summary: English whale/fish at will. Proposes lanchas cañoneras (gunboats) — exploit sheltered coves. English captain fled Loreto fearing gunboats. Admits only works vs. fishing vessels, not warships

author: Arrillaga
summary: Gov. Borica departs CA Jan 16 on *Concepción* with family, Grájera, Dr. Espi. Arrillaga assumes full governorship. English retired; private navigation resumes

author: Arrillaga
summary: Fort SJ flagstaff broken by hurricane Jan 8 — 'terrible huracan de Sur y Norte,' roofs blown off. Physical vulnerability of key northern fort

author: Arrillaga
summary: Fort SJ hurricane damage — additional details on repairs needed

author: Goycoechea
summary: Goycoechea owed $14,357 for 8 years — SB company pay arrears

author: Arrillaga
summary: War declaration published — 'declaración de guerra á la Rusia [Francia?].' Confirmed as FRANCE at n117–118

author: Arrillaga
summary: 100 muskets arrived from corsair crisis request — distributed to southern militia, mixed calibers

author: Arrillaga
summary: Militia reduced from 67 to standing ~50 as Viceroy approved

author: Arrillaga
summary: No vessels available for coastal defense — goleta *Californio* in poor condition, sole craft

author: Arrillaga
summary: Missionary retention crisis — friars requesting to leave; Arrillaga argues retention; 5-month journey for replacements

author: Arrillaga
summary: Artisan failure — Guadalajara recruits failed to produce results; buildings unfinished; some want to leave

author: Arrillaga
summary: Memorias invoices — routine supply ship paperwork; complaints about delays and quality

author: Arrillaga
summary: Poblador complaints — Branciforte and other settlers 'insolent, rebellious, immoral'

author: Arrillaga
summary: Criminal: woman at southern mission killed her own daughter — referred to military jurisdiction

author: Arrillaga
summary: Criminal: José Barbarón of Branciforte attempted to murder retired Cmdte Hermenegildo Sal

author: Arrillaga
summary: Criminal: Ortega involved in stabbing incident

author: Arrillaga
summary: Branciforte settlers 'scandal to troops, Indians, and neighbors'; 'abhor the place'; Barbarón assassination attempt; another settler tried to kill Borica multiple times

author: Argüello (SF)
summary: Argüello begging for supply ships to enter SF directly — troops suffering from delays; 4 years of direct service with *Princesa*/*Concepción* worked better

author: SD Cmdte
summary: American brig 'Betsy' re-entered Ensenada de Todos Santos despite orders. Arrillaga: warn, repeat, then 'use your right' (open fire)

author: Arrillaga
summary: Fort construction/damage reports — ongoing deterioration

author: Arrillaga
summary: Fort SJ + Yerba Buena battery: $3,500 disputed — Junta Superior de R.a Hacienda questioning costs; habilitados Carrillo and Argüello ordered to account

author: Arrillaga
summary: Arms inventory order — all presidio commandants to report on weapons of veteran troops and militias

author: Arrillaga
summary: Monterey buildings in ruin — church + other buildings; '10 years of work by troops, Indians, and vecinos useless' due to haste/fragility; must rebuild from scratch. Also: 64 arrobas gunpowder missing from Monterey battery

author: Arrillaga
summary: Military inventory cover letter — arms of 5 presidios: 161 muskets, 80 pistols, 177 swords, 223 lances

author: Arrillaga
summary: Routine fiscal: habilitado accounts, memorias shipments

author: Arrillaga
summary: José Raimundo Carrillo — promotion/transfer noted; regular rotation of presidial officers

author: Arrillaga
summary: Sargento Pedro Amador retirement from Monterey company — long-serving NCO since 1770s–80s

author: Arrillaga
summary: Luis Antonio de Argüello proposed as cadete — son of SF Cmdte José Darío Argüello; future last Spanish governor of Alta CA

author: Arrillaga
summary: San Diego storehouse fire — almacén destroyed; investigation ordered, inventory of losses

author: Arrillaga
summary: Mission San Juan Bautista earthquake — damage to church and structures, repairs required

author: Arrillaga
summary: Foundling children distributed among CA families — recurring practice to populate province

author: Arrillaga
summary: Gov. Romeu died April 9, 1793 at Monterey. Arrillaga assumed accidental command from Loreto. Papers placed 'bajo dos llaves.' Junta de Oficiales April 11

author: Monterey Cmdte
summary: Gunpowder quality crisis — powder in reserve is bad quality at Monterey, SB, AND SF simultaneously. 3 of 4 Alta CA presidios with defective gunpowder

author: Argüello
summary: Argüello requests 8 quintales iron for Monterey presidio needs

author: Arrillaga
summary: 14 Dominican friars arrived to replace 13 departing. $14,641 shipped on *Aranzazú* for SD and SB habilitados

author: Arrillaga
summary: Artisans from Guadalajara: Santiago Ruiz (mason, 14r/d), Pedro Gonzales (ironworker, 14r/d), Cayetano López (carpenter, 11r/d), Doroteo + Pedro Ruiz (stonecutters, 7r/d + $30 travel)

author: Arrillaga
summary: French warships denied shore access — aid provided but crews NOT allowed ashore. Even allies kept at arm's length

author: Arrillaga
summary: Tobacco crisis — no shipments in 2 years; only 8 boxes cigars + 1 puro per presidio. Revenue loss to Crown

author: Arrillaga
summary: Miguel Sangrador (barber) contracted at $1,000/yr; Joaquín Merlos at $1/day

author: Arrillaga
summary: Artisans delivered to Monterey July 9 on Fragata *Concepción*. Prisoner José Antonio Ocampo arrived from San Blas

author: Arrillaga
summary: Paquebot *San Carlos* arrives Aug 10, 1st Piloto José Tobar, with supplies, mission goods, 12 Dominican friars

author: Arrillaga
summary: Fragata *Santa Gertrudis* from Nootka anchored at Monterey under Capitán de Navío Juan Francisco de la Cuadra — the officer who later found Monterey defenseless (see #3 thesis-critical)

author: Arrillaga
summary: Fragata *Concepción* from Nootka at Monterey under Pto. de Navío José de Eliza, with memorias for presidios/missions

author: Arrillaga
summary: José de Zúñiga promoted to Captain of Tucson — will not leave current post until replacement arrives

author: Arrillaga
summary: Goleta *Santa Gertrudis* wrecked — carrying memorias of missions + 6 padres; storm night of March 11; struck Isla de Santa Ildefonso; crew/passengers saved, ship and all cargo lost

author: Arrillaga
summary: Habilitado Gral. Manuel Carcaba ordered to pay troops for presidio construction; buildings unfinished. Church of Loreto repairs not executed. King approves new Factoría de Presidios, names Carcaba Habilitado General

author: Arrillaga
summary: Military inventory (estado) of Compañía de Loreto goods, crew of Mar y Arsenal, for 1793

author: Arrillaga
summary: Mission Santo Tomás de Aquino — site dispute between Fages and Dominican Presidente; marsh disease: 'Desde el principio de agosto solo tengo enfermos y la mujer buena.' Recommends relocation to higher ground

author: Arrillaga
summary: THE SITUADOS CRISIS: Troops unpaid 1783–86. 'Encontró la tropa sin más uniformes... muchas familias sin ir a la Iglesia por desnudez.' Service 'odioso.' No wine 1783, nothing 1784. Only $35,000 memoria obtained since. Even seeds arrive too late

author: Arrillaga
summary: Alejandro Jordan colony proposal — foreigner proposes colony in Alta CA to supply San Blas. Arrillaga opposes: ship captains can carry CA goods without formal colony

author: Arrillaga
summary: Arms shortages: Monterey short escopetas + 11 pairs pistols; SF short 6 + 17. Monterey fire 1789: cannon wadding started fire during salute for *San Carlos* — guns destroyed own buildings

author: Arrillaga
summary: 6 dispatches: $4,500 for naturalist José Longinos + Jaime Senseve; departure for SD; Loreto command to Capt. José Francisco de Ortega; Padre Tomás Peña case; Goleta *Sutil* arrived Monterey Sept 22

author: Arrillaga
summary: Sutil, Mexicana, Saturnina, Activo at Monterey from Nootka — Galiano, Valdez, de la Cuadra returning from explorations
Monterey fire at midnight — commandant's house, roof of bedroom and parlor burned

author: Arrillaga
summary: OTTER LICENSE DISPUTE: Arrillaga asserts authority over otter diving licenses on CA coasts vs. Intendente de Sonora. Cites 1782 Croix decree. Shows otter trade already generating turf wars

author: Arrillaga
summary: Troop strength table — 5 presidios. SF lowest in every column (2/100/40/2). Totals: 24/1,400/440/14

author: Arrillaga
summary: Vancouver's ships at Monterey: *Daedalus*, *Discovery*, *Chatham* anchored Nov 20–25, sailed Dec 28/Jan 1. Also at SF Nov 13–14, departed for Nootka Nov 24

author: Arrillaga
summary: English ship at Bodega with cannons on land + another at SF entrance. Commandant suspicious. Temporary fortification? — same year as 'tierra incognita' admission

author: Arrillaga
summary: 'Presidios de Alta Cal. están indefensos, cuya circunstancia ha sido notada por los mismos ingleses.' Cannot resist invasion. 8 English prisoners at Monterey; 3 want to convert

author: Argüello
summary: Interrogation of Vancouver's 5 deserters: 3 Portuguese artillerymen (Catholic, denied mass), 2 Irish (underpaid). All deserted 'of their own free will and because they liked the country very much.' Hid among rocks with biscuits until departure cannon

author: Arrillaga
summary: Padre Mariano Rubí of Mission Soledad ill; PP. Fr. Magín Catalá + José Espi sent as replacements. 8 quintales iron received at Monterey

author: Arrillaga
summary: Governor admits barely governing — command is 'accidental'; only ordered compliance with predecessors' instructions; exhorts commandants to 'maintain good harmony with the gentile Indians who surround them'

author: Arrillaga
summary: English ships at SF + Monterey — 2 English ships sighted; one anchored at Monterey requesting water/firewood. English sloop 'Princesa' entered Monterey

author: Arrillaga
summary: ENGLISH SHIP POLICY AT SF: With 4–6 men: hospitality (wood, meat) but 'no dejarán desembarcar el Comandante' — don't let commander disembark; excuse: 'no tienen permiso del Superior.' Concealment in miniature

author: Arrillaga
summary: 6 docs: Loreto ajustes; armero + carpintero shortage at presidios; service cédulas de premio question; proposes Antonio Domingo Enríquez as artisan instructor at 12r/d; needs batanero + weaver; tobacco accounts

author: Arrillaga
summary: Gov. Romeu death confirmed — died April 9 at Monterey; Arrillaga took command

author: Salvador Fidalgo
summary: Fragata *Princesa* at SF from Nootka — Fidalgo offers assistance

author: Arrillaga
summary: 1793 DEFENSE ASSESSMENT: Orders to fortify SF/Monterey/SD + occupy Bodega; only *Princesa* arrived. SF: 6 cannons ALL INSERVICEABLE. Monterey: 8+2 good. SB: 2+1. SD: 2 dismounted, no operator. Total: 55 men. Guard 'imaginaria.' 'Troops must come from outside'

author: Arrillaga
summary: French warships denied shore access — two French ships of Royal Armada; aid but crews NOT allowed ashore

author: Arrillaga
summary: Soldiers deliberately lose pistols: 'Pistolas no se solicitan' — useless, troops despise them; lance is dominant weapon; soldier 'se alegra de que al paso de algún arroyo se le cayese.' Lances = primary weapon

author: Arrillaga
summary: Matute refuses Bodega — 'no verificaría aquel año el establecimiento de Bodega'; plans to winter at SF
Bergantín *Activo* + Goleta *Sutil* at SF — *Sutil* reportedly from Sacramento River area

author: Arrillaga
summary: Plans to match Vancouver: Since Bodega failed, transport wood from Monterey/SD to fortify SF — Vancouver's ships plan to winter there. *Aranzazú* brought cannons; condestable + carpenter began fort construction

author: Arrillaga
summary: Bodega land expedition: Overland route impossible — 'grande pedrero y tierra incognita.' Borrowed launches, crossed 34 horses through mouth of port. Goycoechee with 10 soldiers + 1 sergeant — the Bodega Bay expedition

author: Arrillaga
summary: SF PRESIDIO: 'abierto y sin defensa ninguna.' Cmdte's house + 6 others, tule/straw roofs renewed annually. Chapel + warehouses 'threatening ruin, wouldn't last 2 years.' Guard post 'serves no purpose.' Total cost since founding: $1,400. Monterey: $2,000. SB: $1,200. SD: $1,200. SD now in ruin

author: Arrillaga
summary: 1793 WAR = FRANCE confirmed. R.a Cédula 30 March: 'declaración de guerra contra Francia.' Published in CA. Inhabitants exhorted to contribute to war costs. Settles n35 ambiguity

author: Arrillaga
summary: Vancouver's 2nd CA visit: Discovery + Chatham at SF Oct 18/20; Chatham to Monterey Oct 29. Arrillaga ordered bare minimum supplies. Vancouver returned blacksmith Santos Ulloa

author: Arrillaga
summary: Vancouver intel: Will explore from 55°N northward. May establish first settlement at Hawaiian Islands — 'cacique principal cedido al Rey' (chief ceded to King of England). CA livestock to Botany Bay all died

author: Arrillaga
summary: Colonial criminal law — indigenous defendant: Gentile 'Atti' killed neophyte Pablo. No jurisprudence but military law. Accused 'without society, without more clothing than a skin.' Interpreters held 'with violence.' Sergeant doesn't know courtroom procedure

author: Arrillaga
summary: Vancouver assessment: Ships don't need to trade — engaged in exploration; warned all commandants. Packet forwarded via Vancouver confirmed at Sandwich Islands

author: Arrillaga
summary: Neophyte fugitive passage: Progress through 'gentle treatment' — 'un Capitán Gentil viniese a entregar sin solicitarlo algunos cautivos fugitivos.' Gentile chief voluntarily surrendered fugitives without being asked
$740 from Peninsula for French war — entire CA war contribution ~$1,134 total with Marina's $394
French revolutionary prints banned — prohibiting jewelry/clothing/prints with inscriptions of 'la depravada libertad de Francia'

author: Arrillaga
summary: Fragata *Concepción* wrecked — 2nd shipwreck; some cargo salvaged from Isla de Tichilinguee
Disabled artisans expecting to starve — requesting retirement; 'exposed to dying of hunger, since they don't expect any salary'

author: Arrillaga
summary: Missions Santiago + Guadalupe suppressed — useless BC missions ordered closed and aggregated

author: Arrillaga
summary: SF has NO armorer — begs for one from San Blas; SD's armorer can't transfer because supply ships never go there

author: Arrillaga
summary: Fort SJ construction: Esplanada and trinchera advanced. No ship can enter/leave without passing under cannon fire. Adobe baluarte. 8 cannons in battery, 6 more available. Condestable reports completion before end of 1794

author: Vancouver
summary: Vancouver's 'gift': Offered London bill for prisoner expenses; when refused, said costs were 'un regalo que le hacía a nombre de la Nación Española' — turned debt into diplomacy
Otter skin export order — circulated on liberty of extracting otter pelts shipped via Acapulco
Goleta *Valdez* from Manila — ship arriving at Monterey, linking CA to trans-Pacific trade
Borica takes command — assumes governorship; Arrillaga departs for Loreto (n148)
Arrillaga departs for Loreto after Borica assumes command

author: Arrillaga
summary: Iron/steel inventory: SF received no iron since 1788. SB received none at all. Monterey has only 4 arrobas from 178 received

author: Arrillaga
summary: ARMS INVENTORY TABLE: SD: 34 good escopetas; SB: 36; Monterey: 46; SF: 20 (lowest in every category). All 13 SF pistols useless

author: Arrillaga
summary: Arms summary totals: 136 good escopetas (+25 middling) = 161 total; 57 pistols (all essentially useless); 177 espadas; 223 lanzas. SD only presidio with regular armorer. Loreto excluded (complete)

author: Arrillaga
summary: Notification of Gov. Romeu's death to Comandante General chain. June entries routine
Copy of accounts; deserter Pedraza reports from SF and Monterey commandants
Royal decrees: sentencias, beatificación, disability cédulas
Retirement despachos; cemeteries cédula; *San Carlos* arrival; order on commanders wearing swords at all times

author: Arrillaga
