author: Tte. José Francisco de Ortega, Comte. of San Diego
themes: indigenous-resistance; missions; san-diego; san-juan-capistrano; martyrdom; 1775-revolt; anza
summary: Primary account of the 4–5 Nov 1775 San Diego Mission revolt & killing of Fr. Luis Jayme. *Expediente de investigación* on the burning of Mission San Diego & death of a missionary. Narrative: 2 Oct, Frs. Luis Jayme & Vicente Fuster let their Indians go fishing; the brothers Francisco & Carlos (Carlos = mission 'capitán') robbed old gentile women of fish at rancho de la Chiapa (2 lgs.); the women complained; thieves fled night of the 5th; 5 principal SD-ranchería Indians seized. Night of 5 Nov: gentiles of the Sierra + various Christians attacked at midnight, burned the church, the Fathers' houses & the guard, and killed Fr. Luis Jayme (found at dawn near the arroyo, stripped, riddled with arrows, head & face smashed). Casualties: carpenter Maestro [José] Urselino mortally wounded (2 arrows, died the 10th); mason Manuel Arroyo killed (body burned); mason Felipe Romero wounded but killed an Indian & held out with the guard; 4 escort soldiers gravely wounded (cabo Rocha[?]; Alejo Antonio Gonzáles, Juan Álvarez, Joaquín Armenta); soldiers Ignacio Vallejo[?], Anastasio Camacho, Juan de Ortega singled out for valor. Christian rancherías in the attack: San Luis, Matamó[?], Tamacha[?], Meté, Yana, Abacaly[?] & newly reduced San Miguel; gentile rancherías Melojó[?], Itai, Pocol, El Corral + ~6 more from the San Luis cañada. Defense: earthwork thrown up around the missionaries' houses; sergeant + 10 soldiers raided ranchería Janat[?] on the 24th seeking ringleader Rafael (not found) but seized a principal cabecilla, 12 women & 3 children + recovered church effects; Christian Indian Joaquín & capitán Mariano Yguatín[?] (rancho de Aguacal[?]) examined — rancherías planned a dawn attack on the presidio within 20 days, 'kill the Padres & soldiers but not the women,' allies from across the Colorado River ('tierras libres' boast). Ortega (27 Nov) asked Anza for 20 men. Certified copy by Capt. Fernando de Rivera y Moncada; original sent to the Viceroy of México. Orig. pp.~1–34 [cite at n8 partly illeg.; Doc 2 begins at orig. p.35].

author: Habilitado Hermenegildo Sal
themes: rosters; san-francisco; demographics; genealogy; missions; pay; rations
summary: San Francisco presidial-company muster, pay & rations, end of 1777 — *Personal y Haberes de la Compª Presidial… Sirvientes del Presidio y de las misiones SF y Sta Clara, Misioneros, Pobladores.* Company: Tte. José [Joaquín] Moraga, Sargto Pablo Grijalva, 5 cabos (Domingo Alviso, Valerio Mesa, Pablo Pinto, Gabriel Peralta; Ramón Reforgera[?]/Bojorques almacenero), 33 soldados (Amézquita, Pacheco, Linares, Soto, Valencia, Bojorques, Joaquín de Castro, Felipe Tapia, Juan Fco. Bernal, Santiago Pico, Cordero, Galindo, Silvas, Peralta, Sebastián López, Vicente Félix…) = 1 Tte, 1 Sargto, 5 cabos, 33 soldados. Plus pobladores, sirvientes of the presidio & of Missions SF & Sta Clara, 4 misioneros (Frs. Palóu, Cambón, Murguía, de la Peña). Pay: Tte $700, Sargto $450, cabo $400, soldado $365; pobladores/sirvientes $120/yr + rations. Rations (no meat issued — meat was hunted): flour, maíz, frijol, lenteja, garbanzo, arroz, manteca, panocha, chile. Total company wages for the year $14,795; owed to 28 men $7,223 + 8 soldiers $549; the 5 cabos posted out (Alviso→Presidio, Mesa→Pueblo San José, Pinto→Presidio, Peralta→Sta Clara, Bojorques→Mission SF). Early SF founding-generation roster — genealogically rich. Orig. pp.35–200.

author: Guarda-Almacén Hermenegildo Sal
summary: SF warehouse cash account (caudales recibidos y distribuidos), 1777: géneros/víveres/efectos received $44,208.5.3; distributed to the presidio & others incl. mermas $32,919.4.3¼; existencia on hand $11,289.1.0¾; shortfall charged to his account $91.7.6. Orig. pp.201–204.

author: Guarda-Almacén Hermenegildo Sal (balance w/ Tte. Moraga)
summary: SF Real Almacén inventory, end of 1777 — total existence of géneros/efectos/víveres $11,178.3.6. Provisions & prices (maíz 67½ fan. @ 2¼ r., frijol 101, garbanzo 14, arroz 90 arr., lenteja 11½, harina flor 13 tercios, manteca, chile, panocha, galleta, carne). War matériel: 232½ lbs powder, ~357+6 cannonballs, 348 lbs munición, 110 lbs lead, 3,634 musket-flints (piedras de escopeta), 2 cannon with carriages, 97 cannonballs, rammer/worm/ladle, mecha; plus almacén/presidio tools (romana, balanzas, branding-iron, hoces, azadones, hachas, rejas, reatas…). Orig. pp.205–209.

author: Comte. Tte. José Francisco de Ortega
summary: Criminal causa — death of an India at San Gabriel. Ortega (24 Sep 1779) ordered Sargto Mariano Carrillo, with 2nd alcalde José Mª Verdugo & Salvador Caroaga[?], to San Gabriel to take testimony on the death of the india Gregoria, found beaten/whipped to death two days before; soldado Ignacio Alvarado was accused (by Ignacio María) of killing her, and cabo José Ignacio Olivera jailed him. Witnesses (4–6 Oct): cabo Olivera, alcalde Juan Diego Verdugo, alférez-indio Nicolás, indio Matías, indio Ignacio María (accuser), Avelia[?] (the accused's mother). Nothing appeared against the accused — the accuser had borne false witness; Ortega ordered the false accuser jailed & given 35 lashes over 3 days, with a warning to be far slower to accuse, 'y mucho más en asuntos criminales.' Orig. pp.211–221.

author: Comte. Tte. José Francisco de Ortega
summary: Criminal causa — aiding a deserter's escape. Declarations vs. soldado Manuel Antonio de Robles, accused of letting the San Francisco deserter Felipe Ochoa (a prisoner, who had taken church asylum) escape. Cabo Vicente Briones commanded the San Luis escort; Sargto Mariano Carrillo acted as escribano (26–27 Mar). Witnesses: Guillermo Carrillo (cabo de plaza, who handed Ochoa over at San Luis), Ignacio de Castro, José Mª Verdugo, José Antonio Peña, & Robles. No collusion by Robles found in Ochoa's flight. Orig. pp.224–233.

author: Tte. José Moraga
summary: Criminal causa — attempted poisoning of a husband. Declarations (escribano soldado José Antonio Sánchez), causa vs. Juana Pinto, accused of trying to poison her husband, soldado Mariano Cordero. Orig. pp.234–245.

author: Tte. José Francisco de Ortega
summary: Investigation into a planned flight of Christian Indians 'con fines sediciosos' (10 months *before* the Nov-1775 revolt — early sign of unrest). '(Vide Provincial State Papers.)' Copy. Orig. pp.246–252.

Cost $8,853.2.7; muleteer Juan Manuel Barros carried 670 arrobas 21 lbs from Guadalajara (freight $587), per the Sonora-expedition treasurer Juan José de Echeasta[?]; Ruiz's receipt to deliver at La Paz. author: (Bodega y Cuadra / *Sonora* voyage)
summary: Manuscript chart: 'Plano del Puerto de la Bodega descubierto con la Goleta *Sonora* el día 3 de Octubre [1775].' The discovery map of Bodega Bay from the 1775 *Sonora* voyage. Shows Punta de Tunguía[?], El Farallón del Padre Sierra, Punta de las Arenas, Punta del Cordón, 'Campo verde,' 'Mar ó Río.' Notes: Punta del Cordón at lat. 38°18'; the bay's mouth shallow between Cordón & Arenas points but with a channel; 'entre la punta de Tunguía y punta del Cordón es la entrada del puerto, de más [de] 25 brazas de agua.' Labeled 'Provincial State Papers, Benicia, 1770–18[?]'. ⭐ Foundational Bodega-discovery cartography. Orig. pag. 465.

author: Capt. Fernando de Rivera y Moncada
summary: Interrogation of imprisoned SD-revolt leaders. Declarations (31 May–18 Jun) from various imprisoned Indians 'acusados como principales en el asalto' of the night of 5 Nov 1775 — burning the buildings, killing P. Jayme & other whites, wounding the cabo & 3 escort soldiers. ⭐ Motive (verbatim sense): the rebels 'no tenían queja contra los soldados, ni contra los Padres sino la de que bautizaban, y aquellos querían acabar con Padres y soldados para vivir como antes.' A striking anti-baptism / anti-mission testimony. Orig. pp.327–343.

author: Guarda-Almacén Rafael de Pedro y Gil
summary: San Diego presidio garrison roster + missionaries, March 1775 (8 months before the revolt). Ministers: Fr. Vicente Fuster (SD), Fr. Antonio Paterna (San Gabriel); cirujano José Dávila (left for Monterey Oct 1774). Comte. Tte. Ortega; guarda-almacén Rafael de Pedro y Gil; Sargto Mariano Carrillo; cabos Guillermo Carrillo, Mariano de la Luz Verdugo, Juan José Robles (San Gabriel escort), José Manuel de León (in mission SD); 31 soldados named (incl. Juan Esteban Rocha, Alférez Antonio Gonzáles, Francisco Mª Ruiz, José Raymundo Carrillo, Mariano Cordero, José Mª Ortega, Anastasio Camacho…) + inválidos (incl. Ignacio Vallejo, Ignacio Alvarado); carpintero, 2 herreros (Felipe Romero, José Manuel Arroyo — both later revolt casualties), arrieros. Pay (Tte $700, guarda-almacén $1000, sargto $450, cabo $400, soldado $365). Notes men transferred to Monterey early 1775 + Monterey-company men provisionally at SD in 1774 with date ranges; soldiers Fco. Cordero, Ignacio Olivera, Julián Murillo, Matías I. Soto resident in Baja Cal. Founding-generation SD garrison roster — genealogically central. Orig. pp.~344–424.

author: Guarda-Almacén Rafael de Pedro y Gil
summary: SD warehouse outflow of víveres/efectos 22 Jul 1774→date: México value $2,186.7.3¼ = $6,571.7.10 at San Diego. Orig. pp.425–429.

author: Pedro y Gil
summary: SD warehouse inflow 1774: México value $2,332.0.2½ = $6,899.5.11 at SD; + 10 fan. frijol, 295 maíz, 44 arr. carne, 16 chile, 11 garbanzo, lenteja. Orig. pp.430–431.

author: Manuel Ramón de Goya
summary: Memoria/factura of 69 piezas of effects for Loreto (Baja Calif.) via conductor Antonio José Barros to San Blas, comisario José Faustino Ruiz — México value $14,883, Loreto value $23,767 (~100% markup); freight 558 arrobas @ 11 r. = $763. Cert. by comisario Francisco Álvarez Osorio (Loreto, 1779); carried by the *Príncipe* (capt. Juan Agustín de Echeverría, anchored Loreto 13 Jul 1779). Orig. pp.433–448.

author: Fernando de Rivera y Moncada
themes: economy; pearls; real-quinto; loreto; baja-california; mission-indians
summary: Loreto Real Almacén inventory — incl. the Real Quinto on Baja California pearls. Inventory/avalúo of effects under comisario Álvarez Osorio = $24,968; adds powder, cartridges, and pearls (perlas de toda mar) from the diving (buceo) seasons, owed as the Real Quinto: 2¾ 'libras largas' from the 1775 buceo handed to Gov. Felipe de Neve; 3 adarmes (1776, Misión Santiago de las Coras, 'en el Sur'); 10½ adarmes (1777, Mulegé); 7½ (1778, dived 'en balsa' by the Santiago Indians). Unpriced effects valued by the Tte. Gobernador → total $25,715. ⭐ Rare pearl-fishery / royal-tribute source. Orig. pp.449–465.

author: Joaquín Cañete[?], Tte. de la Compª de Cueras & Tte. de Gobierno interino
summary: Authenticates the inventory/avalúo of the King's effects in the Loreto almacén (comisario Álvarez Osorio), end of 1779 — total $34,325. Orig. pp.466–486.

author: Joaquín Cañete, Tte. de Gobierno interino
summary: Testimonio of an *expediente* to settle the Loreto Real Almacén accounts (over-stocks, mermas, faltas) flagged by comisario Álvarez Osorio; sent via Capt. Rivera y Moncada to the Viceroy. Orig. pp.487–524.

author: Guarda-Almacén Rafael de Pedro y Gil
summary: SD warehouse inflow/value of effects received 1777 (incl. México & San Blas facturas, supplements for Monterey & SF almacenes, deliveries via Mission San Gabriel / the *Príncipe*) — total $43,253. Orig. pp.527–533.

author: Rafael de Pedro y Gil
summary: SD presidio + dependent-missions pay-settlement roster (ajuste de sueldos y raciones), 1777 — every man with balance owed/in-favor: Tte. Ortega, guarda-almacén Pedro y Gil, Sargto Mariano Carrillo, cabos, 48 soldados rasos, carpintero, herrero (Felipe Romero), arrieros & mission sirvientes; notes 7 soldiers transferred to Monterey 26 Nov 1777 by order of Gov. Neve, men supplied at SD for the Loreto troop, and ⭐ extraordinary 1777 provisions for the troops Anza brought (Tte.-Col. Juan Bautista de Anza) + Indians who drove cattle to Monterey + presidio-construction workers. Totals: 1 Tte, 1 guarda-almacén, 1 sargto, 6 cabos, 48 soldados. Genealogically central (parallels Doc 29). Orig. pp.534–689.

author: Pedro y Gil
summary: SD warehouse outflow of effects/víveres 1777 incl. mermas/averías — total $37,635.5.2. Orig. pp.690–696.

author: Pedro y Gil
summary: SD Real Almacén inventory: 18 fan. maíz, 12 escopetas poblanas, 17 espadas, 16 hats, pita, wax, metal syringe, fine steelyard, 2,500 flints, needles, 11 cargas cristales de La Paz, etc. Witnessed by Fr. Vicente Fuster & Fco. Antonio Mourelle (Maurelle), capt./pilot-commander of the *Príncipe* (the Bodega/*Sonora*-voyage pilot). Orig. pp.697–698.

author: Pedro y Gil

author: Factor Manuel Ramón de Goya
summary: Factura for SD presidio: 56 tercios + 44 cajones of clothing/effects via arriero Antonio José Barros → comisario Faustino Ruiz (San Blas) → SD. México cost $10,235; Calif. value at 150% markup $25,589; total cost (w/ double-stage freight per Viceroy's order) $11,809. Cert. copy by Pedro y Gil. Orig. pp.700–710.

author: Fr. Antonio Cruzado, Ministro
summary: Brief San Gabriel mission item, 1776–77. Orig. pp.716–717.

author: Tte. Ortega
summary: Notice admitting arriero Tadeo Rivera, bound to cart royal effects on the ship's arrival, carry víveres for the escorts, and tend the hato y reata. Orig. p.719.

author: Tte. Ortega
summary: Troop hardship. The troops got only ~1½ almud maíz + ¾ almud menestra, so most of the week they lived on 'pura tortilla'; Ortega authorizes ½ lb powder + 2 rounds per man to hunt the abundant ducks & geese (presidio + San Juan Capistrano & San Gabriel escorts). Vivid daily-life/subsistence detail. Orig. pp.720–721.

author: José Fco. Ortega
summary: SD Real Almacén existence of víveres/efectos — value $2,957; + smith-made pieces, forge & carpentry tools. Orig. pp.733–735.

author: Comte. Tte. José Fco. Ortega
themes: indigenous-resistance; missions; san-diego; execution; justice; 1775-revolt; pamó
summary: The Pamó conspiracy & firing-squad execution of 4 Indian captains. On 16 Mar, Benito, an Indian capitán, warned (via Fr. Lasuén) that the Indians of Pamó — esp. their capitán Aarán[?] — were gathering arms to kill Christians; 3 neighbouring captains + others 'del otro lado de la sierra' were in the plot, which had killed a fugitive from San Juan Capistrano. Aarán defied Ortega, daring the soldiers to come 'para cogerlos á mano y matarlos á macanazos.' Sargto Mariano Carrillo (+ cabo, 8 soldiers, 2 guides) surprised Pamó at 3am on 31 Mar; the captains were seized & bound; 2 Indians killed, a few burned in a house refusing to surrender; the rest submitted & were flogged. >1,500 arrows, 80+ bows & many war-clubs taken & burned. The 4 captains — Achil, Aalcuirin[?], Aarán, Taguaqui[?] — examined 4 Apr, convicted of conspiring to kill the Christians despite a royal pardon, 'por haber juntado gente… para pelear con los de la Soledad después de la destrucción de la Misión' [1775 SD revolt]. ⚠️ Ortega's sentence (verbatim): 'los condeno á que el día 11 á las 9 de la mañana mueran con la violencia de dos tiros de escopeta, presente la tropa armada en guerra, y todas las rancherías cristianas… para que les sirva de estímulo á obrar bien.' And to the friars (re baptism): 'si se reducen á las aguas del Santo Bautismo, mueran el Sábado 11… por la mañana, y si no, también.' Lasuén began instructing them; the condemned disposed to be baptized. (Savage notes a discrepancy in the certified copy.) Orig. pp.737–764, 767–769.

author: Tte. Ortega
summary: Report of the execution. Ortega reports having shot the 4 Pamó ringleaders, explaining the 'poderosas razones' for not delaying the punishment, and awaits the Governor's resolution; the complicit rancherías sued for peace/pardon and were told the only way was to settle and live in peace. Orig. pp.765–767.

author: (unsigned)
summary: Livestock driven from Baja missions to Alta California, per Visitor-General José de Gálvez's reglamento & viceregal order: stock from Misiones San José de Comondú, Guadalupe, Purísima, Sta Gertrudis, San Borja, San Fernando, San Vicente… for Monterey — 290 head total (mares $4, horses/colts $9, pack/saddle mules $20, cargo-machos $19, cows, bulls, goats, sheep…); plus 11½ mares, 1 stud, burros the King held at Loreto; total value of the missions' contributions $1,238. Foundational stock-transfer to Alta Calif. Orig. pp.804–805.

author: Real Tribunal y Audiencia de Cuentas

Neve (rec'd 14 Oct 1778) ordered Tte. de Gobernador Rivera y Moncada at Loreto to make comisario Álvarez Osorio (who held Fages's books) formalize the SD & Monterey troop/artisan ajustes. Orig. pp.843–850.

author: Comte. José [Joaquín] Moraga

Ramón & Pedro Bojorques, Ignacio Linares, Justo Altamirano, Antonio Aceves, Agustín Valenzuela, Ignacio de Soto, José Valencia, Juan Bernal, Manuel Arellano, Felipe Ochoa, Nicolás Berreyesa, Gabriel Moraga (here a young soldado); each docked $13.50 for the alcances fund. Orig. p.870.

author: José Pérez Fernández
summary: Sent a force to join Sargto Pedro Amador to seize, at the Rosario site, a Christian Indian & a gentile stirring up tumults among the Indians near Santa Cruz. Orig. p.871.

author: Tte. José Joaquín Moraga
summary: Desertion + robbery toward the Colorado. Night of 13 Mar, soldado José Mariano Lepis[?] deserted with 3 escort arrieros + a servant of Sargto Grijalva, having robbed the King's cargo & some troop valuables and taken 25 mounts; Moraga pursued (with 'Chumbras'[?] auxiliaries) and caught them 18 Mar, 4 leagues from the Colorado River, on the road to Sonora; Indians killed 3 [animals], 2 deserters lost. Orig. pp.924–925.

author: Rivera y Moncada

author: Rivera y Moncada
summary: Sentenced the young men (mozos) who, with Lepis, robbed at San Gabriel to 1 year forced labor at San Francisco, on ration & without pay. Orig. pp.930–933.

author: José Fco. Ortega
summary: SD presidial-company personnel (vs. end-1777): Aceves, Julián Ríos & José Dolores Domínguez → Monterey; Patrón & Pedro Molina discharged; José Ramírez, Matías de la Vega, Juan José Vallejo deserted; new: Nicolás Beltrán, José Miguel Flores, Tomás M. Camacho, Salvador Cañizo[?]. Company now 1 Tte, 1 Sargto, 5 cabos, 48 soldados. Orig. pp.952–1054.

author: Habilitado Felipe de Goycoechea

author: Gov. Neve (copia sin firma)

author: Gov. Neve / Habilitado Goycoechea

Vázquez, José Mª Velasco, José Esteban Romero, M. I. Lugo, Ignacio Rodríguez, Juan Javier Mejía, José Lorenzo Salazar, Ignacio Cota, José Patiño, Felipe Gonzáles, José Polanco, Julián Guerrero, José Samaniego, Rosalino Fernández, Juan Ignacio Martínez, Agustín Sota[?], Vitorino Félix, Guillermo Soto, Eugenio Valdés, Justo Hernández, José Soto, Juan Antonio Monreal, Hildefonso Domínguez… Summary $2,658; net charge $1,530. author: José Fco. de Ortega / Gov. Neve
summary: Remuda/equipment account — value of a soldier's armament & mount $41.5.2, a new cuera, etc. Orig. p.7.

author: Ortega = Alférez José [Darío] Argüello
summary: Value of effects delivered to Alférez Argüello (pertaining to Capt. Rivera y Moncada) ~$89. Orig. p.~8.

Enlistment dates: Félix Villavicencio (30 May 1780), Antonio Mesa (11 Jun), José Lara (24 Jun), José Vanegas (11 Aug), Pablo Rodríguez (13 Aug), Manuel Camero (17 Aug), Antonio Navarro (21 Aug), José Moreno (Sep), Basilio Rosas (7 Nov), Alejandro Rosas, Antonio Rodríguez, Luis Quintero (5 Feb 1781). author: copy by Bonilla

author: (unsigned)

Castro, Antonio Parra, Manuel Díaz, José Vanegas, Asensio Mariné); pobladores de los presidios de Sonora; oficiales (Capt. Fernando de Rivera y Moncada, Ramón Lasso, José de Zúñiga, Diego Gonzáles, José Moraga); presidios Monterey/SF/SD/Loreto; the LA pobladores (Pablo Rodríguez, José Lara, José Moreno, Antonio Mesa, Villavicencio, Navarro, Vanegas, Basilio & Alejandro Rosas, Luis Quintero, Manuel Camero) + a 2nd settler group (Juan de Dios Murrieta, Andrés Bojorques, Javier Romero, Guadalupe Álvarez, José Domingo Mesa, José Chamorro, José Acuña, Valentín Montaño, Vicente Frías…). Totals: 54 horses, remount mules/machos, 72/196/158 distributed; importe $8,115. Genealogically central. Orig. pp.59–63.

Parra, Nicolás Gonzáles, Gerardo Peña, de León, Estrada, the Bernals, Diego M. Ruiz, José Raimundo Carrillo, Mariano Cordero (total $544) + 1777 charges of the late guarda-almacén Juan Soler ($108). author: copy by Mariano Carrillo
summary: Monterey presidio's charges for recruits, armament & caballería $2,578. Orig. p.108.

author: (to the Governor)
summary: Aftermath of the July 1781 Yuma (Colorado River) Revolt — Cayetano Limón's expedition. Fragmentary, hard hand: Alférez Cayetano Limón, on an *expediente*, 'encontró la Concepción en ruinas, puesta la plaza de gente muerta á palos y quemada' (found [Misión Purísima] Concepción in ruins, the place full of people clubbed to death & burned); was attacked by the Indians; mentions 'su hijo el soldado Cayetano Limón.' ⚠️ *I'm inferring* this concerns the July 1781 Yuma Revolt (destruction of the Colorado missions & killing of Rivera y Moncada + the colonists) and Limón's relief/ransom expedition — to verify against the fuller text. Orig. pp.115–116.

author: Hermenegildo Sal / Diego Gonzáles

Diego Gonzáles, Alférez Mariano Carrillo, Sargto Mariano Verdugo, cabos (Vicente Briones, José Raymundo Carrillo, Rafael Villavicencio, José Domínguez) + ~45 soldados (José Mª Góngora, Marcelo Bravo, Soberanes, Juan Mª Ruiz, Bonifacio Estrada, Severino Lugo, Felipe García, Luis Peralta, Casimiro Varela, Sebastián López, Marcos Briones, Joaquín Beltrán, Norberto Mejía, José Miguel Espinosa, Leonardo & Juan Mª Verdugo, Dionisio Bernal…); transferred to SD & Santa Bárbara (Sargto Antonio Cota + 11); licenciados (Contreras, Patrón); difuntos (Sargto Juan José Robles, cabo Alejo Duarte, Fco. Peña, José Mª Guerrero, Joaquín López, Victoriano Cervantes, J. I. Espinosa); dependents (cirujano José Dávila, carpintero Manuel Rodríguez, herrero Gregorio Segura, Fernando Camacho) + former sirvientes. Crédito $12,467, débito $1,305. Genealogically central for the Monterey founding company. Orig. pp.117–120.

author: (unsigned)
summary: Appoints Alférez José Velázquez & sargento Gabriel de Arce to recover horses & gear (reagua/aparejos) left on the Baja Cal. frontier. Orig. pp.121–122.

author: (unsigned)
summary: Instruction to Moraga on conduct in the presidio during the commander's absence — incl. protocol on approaching/boarding an enemy ship and on receiving the memorias. Orig. pp.125–127.

author: (unsigned)

author: to Joaquín Cañete (unsigned)
summary: Internal Reglamento/carta instructiva for the presidios — 16 numbered articles (1º–16º) on presidial accounting procedures: applying the new Reglamento, charge/credit of the companies' situado, the gratification fund ($1,245 in 1779), Arsenal/Marina dependencies, distribution of effects to the troop, reintegration to the Habilitado & Real Plata ($226), víveres for mission cattle, the still-pending liquidation of the troop/arriero/vaquero accounts Rivera y Moncada formed at SD & Monterey (charge vs. Real Plata $25,017, tying back to the Fages accounts), the inventory/handover of the Departamento de Marina stores (art. 12º–15º: the *balandra y lanchas*, jarcia, remos, anclas, delivered per the Alférez José de Velázquez), carpentry/blacksmith/masonry tools, and the rule that the Habilitado's handover be made within '4 á 5 meses.' Closes (art. 16º) noting the great distance prevents prompt delivery. Long administrative text. Orig. pp.129–141.

author: Felipe de Neve
summary: On the manner of conducting the pobladores to the frontier. Notes a company-strength snapshot: as of 1 Nov 1781 the company had 51 soldados, 2 cabos, 2 sargentos, 1 teniente & 1 alférez; another lacked one soldier. '(Este pequeño documto se halla en el reverso del artº.)' Orig. pp.142–146.

author: Felipe de Neve
summary: Same matter, ordering the semillas (seeds): total value prorated among those who depart, subject in sale to the resulting price. Orig. pp.148–149.

author: José Francisco Ortega

Orig. p.273.

author: José Francisco Ortega (2 docs)

Amézquita, Ramón Bojorques, Mariano Cordero, Isidro Roberto[?], Ignacio Linares, Ant. Acevedo, Ignacio Soto, José Manuel Valencia, Pedro Bojorques, José Ant. Sánchez, Manuel Arellano, Joaquín de Castro, Felipe Tapia, Juan Fco. Bernal, Mauricio Vázquez, Agustín Valenzuela, Ign. Reguera[?], Nicolás Galindo, José Miguel Silvas, Juan José Peralta, José Ant. Gonzáles, Miguel Pacheco, Manuel Figueroa[?], Salvador Espinosa, Felipe Ochoa, Ignacio Castro); difunto José Ant. Soto; licenciados Ign. Gutiérrez, Alejo Miranda[?]; pobladores (Manuel Gonzáles, Manuel Amézquita, Tiburcio Vázquez, Ign. Archuleta, Ant. Romero, Bernardo Rosales, Sebastián Alviso, Francisco Ávila, Claudio Álvarez, Feliciano Carballo[?]). Companion to the 1777 SF muster (Doc 2) — genealogically central. Orig. pp.568–570.

summary: Monterey presidial company roster, 31 Dec 1782. Tte. Diego Gonzáles; Habilitado Hermenegildo Sal; 5 cabos (Vicente Briones, José Raymundo Carrillo, José Domínguez, Antonio Yorba, Rafael Villavicencio); 37 soldados (Marcelino Bravo, José Soberanes, Juan Mª Ruiz, Fco. Reyes, Juan de la Cruz, Bonifacio Estrada, Diego Ruiz, Severino Lugo, Felipe García, Bernardo Heredia, Manuel Higuera, Justo Sáez[?], Manuel Antuña, Javier Beltrán, Antonio Buelna, Manuel Butrón, Fco. Cayuelas, Domingo Aros, Eugenio Rosalío, Manuel Vargas, Toribio Guzmán, Julián Ríos, Ignacio Cantúa, José Ant. Rodríguez, Casimiro Varela, Sebastián López, Juan Mª Pinto, José Lojo[?], Marcos Villela, Manuel Briones, Antonio Montaño, Joaquín Beltrán, Fco. Villagómez, Antonio Alegre, Fco. Sánchez, Leonardo Verdugo, Juan Mª Verdugo); total 44 hombres (1 Tte, 1 Oficial Habilitado, 5 cabos, 37 soldados). Orig. pp.29–30.

author: Gob. Felipe de Neve
summary: On tithes (diezmos). Per the Peninsula Reglamento, settlers must pay tithes on the crops & livestock (frutos y esquilmos) they produce, plus the rescate of suertes de tierra. As the diezmos are assigned to the mitra (bishopric) of Sonora, all collected now & henceforth is to be reduced to remate (auction), composing with the stock-raisers so they pay the value of the tithed head in pesos or seed. Orig. pp.31–32.

author: José Francisco Ortega
summary: Account of effects handed over by Sargto Mariano Verdugo to the Habilitado of the Santa Bárbara presidio (Tte./Habilitado Ortega), with intervention of the Comandante/Inspector [Argüello?]. Orig. pp.45–46.

author: Alférez Mariano Carrillo, Habilitado
summary: Account of effects sent to Tte. José Fco. Ortega, Habilitado at Santa Bárbara. Orig. pp.47–48.

author: José Francisco Ortega
summary: Founding muster of the company to garrison the Presidio & Missions of the Santa Bárbara Channel. Tte. José Fco. Ortega; Alférez José Argüello; Sargtos Pablo Antonio Cota & Ignacio Olivera; Habilitado Hermenegildo Sal; cabos Alejandro de Sotomayor, José Mª de Ortega; ~54 soldados (Luis Lugo, Alejandro Ruiz, José Gonzáles, Martín Reyes, Anastasio Mª Félix, Fco. Salvador de Lugo, Joaquín Higuera, Fco. de Paula García, José Miguel Flores, José Lobo, Ignacio Lugo, José Manuel Valenzuela, José Estevan Romero, Fco. Javier Mejía, Juan Andrés Montiel, Loreto Salazar, Ildefonso Domínguez, Fco. Calvo, José Carmen Arana, Victorino Félix, Agustín Leiba, José Velarde, José Ontiveros, José Prudencio Aranguré[?], Fco. Mª Ruiz, Juan Matías Olivas, Julián Guerrero, Felipe Gonzáles, Justo Hernández, José Parra, Fco. Ontiveros, José Mª Polanco, Fco. Juárez, Eugenio Valdés, José Ant. Cortés, Guillermo Soto, José Mª Samaniego, Fructuoso Ruiz, Joaquín Rodríguez, Juan Ign. Valencia, Ign. Rodríguez, Rosalino Fernández, Vicente Quijada, Juan Ign. Martínez, Eugenio Ruiz, José Villa, Ign. Mª Ortega…). Totals: 1 Tte, 1 Alférez, 3 sargentos, 2 cabos, 54 soldados = 61. Note: 'En 1º de Julio de 1782 estaban los mismos' minus Fco. Ontiveros, Fco. Juárez, José Ant. Cortés & José Prudencio Aranguré. Orig. pp.60–61 & 70–71.

author: Joaquín Cañete, Tte.
summary: Distribution of the Loreto presidial company: on the frontier 2 sargtos, 2 cabos, 22 soldados = 25; at Santa Ana 5; at Loreto 13. Plus livestock (3 horses, 116 good mules, 21 medium). Orig. p.91.

author: Diego Gonzáles
summary: Monterey company summary, 1 Dec 1782: Tte. Gonzáles, Alférez Mariano Carrillo, cirujano José Dávila, Sargto Mariano Verdugo, 5 cabos, 46 soldados, 1 carpenter, 2 smiths; total 58 hombres. Orig. pp.97–98.

author: Hermenegildo Sal
summary: Sal's habilitación accounts, 1 Jan 1782 – 12 Sep 1787 (as Tte./Alférez Habilitado of the Monterey presidial company): received effects valued $76,472.5; disbursed leaving $44,450.4 in the company's favor; net $654 in his favor. Savage's note (verbatim): 'Sal had been suspended & arrested as a defaulter & proved triumphantly that he was innocent of the charge maliciously preferred against him by the Ayudante Inspector [Nicolás] Soler.' Diligencias presented 11 Aug 1788. (Material later than the '1782' tomo label — volume not strictly chronological.) Orig. pp.109–140, 141–167.

author: Joaquín Cañete
summary: Loreto presidial company annual pay scale, 1781: Tte. $550, Alférez $400, 2 sargtos $625, 2 cabos $450[?], 39 soldados $8,882.4, [troop allowances] $190; total $11,022.4. Orig. pp.130–133.

author: Caballero Teodoro de Croix, Comandante General de Provincias Internas
summary: Record of the death of Capt. Fernando de Rivera y Moncada in the Yuma (Colorado River) Revolt. Verbatim: 'El Capitán Dn Fernando de Rivera y Moncada murió el día 18 de Julio á manos de los Yumas en el cual se verificó su alzamiento.' A note from a cuenta-certificate signed by Gob. Felipe de Neve at San Gabriel 3 Jan 1782 states Rivera died at the hands of the Yumas on the río Colorado, per the report of Alférez Cayetano Limón of the Presidio de Buenavista, who on 21 Aug passed that ground & gathered scattered corpses; de Croix's datum at the foot of the document corrects Neve's date of the death. ⚠️ Savage heads it '1782' but the Yuma Revolt & Rivera's death are firmly 17–18 July 1781; transcribed verbatim. Ties to Doc 97 (Limón) & the destruction of the Colorado missions. Orig. pp.185–186.

author: Teodoro de Croix
summary: War donativo from the Californias. Donations collected in the peninsula of the Californias for the urgencies of the war total $4,215.4.3; 12 dependents of San Blas contributed $116. (Bourbon war-subsidy, Anglo-Spanish War era.) Orig. pp.254–256.

author: José Mª Estrada, Habilitado
summary: Loreto fiscal account: Real Quinto on pearl-diving canoes (one owed since 1778, paid $200), salt duty (25 arrobas, $147.50), minor receipts (remate de buceos, paper) $26.75; total $374.25. Orig. pp.281–282.

author: José Mª Estrada, Habilitado
summary: Existence of effects in the Almacenes del Rey at Loreto, value $9,873. Orig. pp.285–290.

author: Estrada, Habilitado
summary: Income from a canoe employed in pearl-diving (buceo) at $100; a passage charged to the King; total $112.4[?]. Orig. p.303.

author: Felipe de Neve
summary: Livestock bought for the Peninsula by Capt. Rivera y Moncada. Per a note signed by Rivera y Moncada: bought at Real de los Álamos for the Peninsula & received at Isla de Lobos 200 mules, 100 horses; died at sea 32 mules, 8 horses; received at Loreto 165 mules, 79 horses; distributed to presidio troops; died en route/on the frontier 3 mules, 16 horses; remitted to Nueva California 90 mules, 57 horses. (The remount herd of Rivera's fatal 1781 expedition.) Orig. pp.313–314.

author: Estrada, Habilitado
summary: Existence of effects in the Almacenes del Rey, value $8,316. Orig. pp.323–328, 329–331.

author: Fr. Manuel Pérez & Fr. Antonio Lazcano[?]
summary: Value of seeds supplied by the mission to the escoltas to end of 1780, $122. Orig. p.335.

author: Tte. José Fco. Ortega
summary: Cost of transporting the settlers & their families from the Real de los Álamos to the playa de Santa Bárbara, from the bahía de San Luis to the Presidio de San Diego, and thence to Mission San Gabriel — bagajes $1,147.7. (The 1781 Neve–Rivera colonist convoy that founded LA & Santa Bárbara.) Orig. pp.346–347.

author: José de Zúñiga, Habilitado
summary: San Diego presidial company: 5 cabos, 51 soldados; each contributed $2.50 to the fondo común de alcances. Orig. pp.379, 382, 393.

author: José de Zúñiga, Habilitado
summary: Armament & mount of each SD cabo & soldado (soldado de cuera kit): 1 escopeta, 1 par de pistolas, 1 espada, 1 lanza, 1 cuera, 1 adarga, 1 silla, 1 cojinillo, 1 freno, 1 cinturón, 1 funda, 1 cartuchera, etc.; company livestock 88 horses, 144 mules. Orig. pp.387–389.

author: (regulation)
summary: Detailed uniform, armament & mount regulation for the Baja California presidial troop & officers. *Troop:* chupa corta de tripe/paño with red cuff & collar, gilt button, blue tripe breeches, matching cape, cartuchera, cuera, bandolera embroidered with the presidio's name, corbatín, black hat, shoes, buckskin boots. *Divisas (rank lace):* sargento = galón of 14 threads on the cuffs; cabo = half-galón. *Armamento:* espada ancha, lanza, escopeta, pistolas, adarga. *Montura:* silla vaquera with mochila, coraza, anqueras, cojinillos, wooden stirrups, freno, espuelas, bozal. *Officers (dress):* blue casaca & breeches, chupín, red collar double-laced (5-thread galón), 3-cornered hat with gold lace; *(campaign):* short chupa, blue cape & breeches, round white hat. Orig. pp.141–145.

author: Tte. Felipe de Goycoechea, Habilitado
summary: Goycoechea succeeds Tte. José Fco. Ortega as Habilitado of Santa Bárbara. (Material later than the '1782' tomo label.) Orig. p.424.

author: Felipe de Goycoechea
summary: Los Ángeles pobladores pay account, 1793: sueldos $1,141; gratificación de la tropa $337.4; haber de los pobladores $528.4. Orig. p.426.

author: Esteban José Martínez
summary: The royal expedition frigates *Princesa* & *Favorita*, under Dn Esteban José Martínez, were at Santa Bárbara, 4 Aug 1782. (Maritime exploration; Martínez later commands the 1789 Nootka occupation.) Orig. p.428.

author: Hermenegildo Sal, Habilitado
summary: Monterey Real Almacén inventory + ordnance, end 1782: géneros/efectos/víveres $3,988 (unsellable to troops $1,076); war matériel: 447 pairs of pistols[?], lance-fittings, 394 common balls, 42 arr. powder (incl. cartridge), 150 musket/fusil ball cartridges, lead, 7,466 gunflints (piedras de chispa). Orig. pp.474–479.

author: José Fco. Ortega, Habilitado
summary: Santa Bárbara company account 1782: Cargo $27,957; Data $28,094; saldo $137 in Ortega's favor; warehouse existence of géneros/efectos/víveres $6,672. Orig. pp.575–576.

author: José Fco. Ortega, Habilitado
summary: Livestock belonging to the Real Hacienda at SB: 19 head cattle, 9 sheep/goats (lana y pelo), 18 mares & young. Orig. p.543.

author: José de Zúñiga, Habilitado
summary: Deductions taken from each soldier's pay: 8 maravedíes per peso for the fondo de inválidos + 2½% agency fee on the Habilitado's liquidation. Orig. p.544.

author: José de Zúñiga, Habilitado
summary: San Diego presidial company roster, 31 Dec 1782. Sargento vacant; Alférez José Velázquez; cabos (Juan Esteban Rocha, Ignacio Rafael Alvarado, Francisco Acevedo, José Mª Verdugo, Juan Mª Olivera); 44 soldados; herrero, carpintero, arriero; difuntos Sargto Guillermo Carrillo, Juan José Robles + soldados Marcos Cañedo, Tomás Mª Camacho, Rafael Márquez, Joaquín Guerrero, Nicolás Beltrán, Juan Ángel Amarillas, [?] Peña; licenciado José Mª López. Orig. pp.548–549.

author: José Fco. Ortega, Tte. Habilitado
themes: rosters; santa-barbara; coastal-defense; ordnance; san-buenaventura; los-angeles
summary: Santa Bárbara presidial company distribution & defense, 1 Dec 1782. Postings: presidio guard 1 sargto/1 cabo/6 sold = 8; caballada 1+6 = 7; Mission San Buenaventura 1 sargto + 14 = 15; pueblo de Los Ángeles 2; 'francos para el servicio' officers + sargtos. Total 56 hombres (1 Tte, 1 Alférez, 2 sargtos, 2 cabos, 50 sold); short 1 sargto & 4 soldados. Caballada 89 horses, 144 mules; arms reserve 13 pairs pistols (short 2 escopetas, 36 espadas, 26 cueras for full company). Presidio guarded by 2 cannon (4-pounders) with 140 ball cartridges; munitions reserve 5,000 musket cartridges, 18 arr. powder, 127 arr. lead, 10 arr. balls. Orig. p.561.

author: Fr. José Señán
summary: Acknowledges $75 from Gov. Pedro Fages for masses (sufragios) for the deceased soldiers of the company — Sargto López & José Mª Guerrero. Orig. p.562.

author: Hermenegildo Sal, Habilitado
summary: Monterey war-munitions on hand: 394 loose balls, 41 arr. powder, 1,500 cartridges, 5 arr. ball, 31 arr. lead, 7,466 gunflints. Orig. p.567.

author: Hermenegildo Sal, Habilitado
summary: Monterey company roster (continuation): 5 cabos, 46 soldados; 'se presume que continuaban los mismos oficiales y sargentos' as in 1781. Orig. pp.510–511.

author: Hermenegildo Sal, Habilitado
summary: Deaths in the Monterey presidial company (per the cuenta visada by Tte. Diego Gonzáles): Alférez Mariano Carrillo; Guarda-Almacén Juan Soler; Sargto Juan José Robles; cabo Alejo Antonio Duarte; soldados Fco. Peña, José Mª Guerrero, Joaquín López, Antonio Espinosa, Joaquín Espinosa, Pablo Victoriano Cervantes; carpintero José Urselino. Orig. p.512.

author: Tte. José Moraga, Comandante
summary: Settlers of Pueblo San José de Guadalupe whose pay & ration ceased 1 Nov 1782: Ignacio Archuleta, Antonio Romero, José Bernardo Rosales, Sebastián Alviso, Francisco Ávila, Claudio Álvarez. (San José founding pobladores.) Orig. pp.573–574.

author: Gob. interino José Joaquín de Arrillaga
summary: Account of what the San José pobladores earned in 1782 — some had pay+ration part-year then ration only; two had ration only all year; one ration only to 3 Nov. Total cost $415. Orig. p.601.

author: Hermenegildo Sal, Habilitado
summary: Detailed livestock census of the Monterey presidio herds, 1782. *Horses/mules:* 274 mares/colts existing end-1781 + 52 colts + 32 fillies + 1 mule = 357; distributed to troops, comisos, deaths (98, $221); 261 remaining. *Cattle:* 938 existing end-1781 + 373 calves (182 male/191 female) = 1,311; distributed/sold (vacas, toros, novillos), matanza (10 arr. manteca); 1,129 remaining. *Sheep (lana):* 336, all sold/dead, none left. *Goats (pelo):* 115, all sold. *Pigs (cerda):* 18 → 12 left. Resumen: caballar 261, vacuno 1,129, cerda 12. Sale proceeds $1,099.7; less herders' pay & costs $120.6; net to Real Hacienda $979.1. Orig. pp.630–636.

author: (Comandante; signer [?])
summary: Cost of the SF presidial company in 1782: Tte. José Moraga $550; Alférez Ramón Lasso $400; 1 sargento $262.4; 4 cabos @ $225 = $900; 26 soldados @ $217½ = $5,655; gratificación de 10$ anuales por plaza sencilla $260; total $8,027.4. Orig. p.641.

author: Diego Gonzáles, Comandante
summary: Donativo gracioso (war gift to the King) by the Monterey company for war expenses = $833. Orig. pp.644–647.

author: Fr. Junípero Serra
themes: missions; demographics; indigenous; donativo; serra; monterey
summary: Padrón of neophyte Indians at Mission San Carlos, by Serra. Adult males (>18) by ranchería: San Carlos 23 married/1 widower; San José 14; Santa Teresa 21; San Miguel 15 (1 widower, 1 single); San Francisco 18; total 96. Plus long-time apostates 'de la misión y de la religión cristiana' whose recovery had required troops (4 @ $50). The 96 neophytes each contributed to the donativo gracioso for the war = $96; settler Ignacio Vallejo gave $10. 659 Indians baptized since the mission's founding; 243 dead; 109 married/widowed, the rest women & children. Orig. pp.648–653.

author: Fr. [Pieras?]/[friars [?]]
summary: Padrón of neophytes at Mission San Luis Obispo. Officers named: Miguel Robles (alcalde), Joaquín Murillo (alcalde), Tomás Llavería[?] (regidor); 104 more married; 1 widower; 2 single; total 107 adult males, each contributing $1 to the war donativo = $107. 528 baptized since founding; 89 dead; rest women & children. Orig. pp.658–661.

author: Frs. Miguel Pieras & Buenaventura Sitjar
summary: Padrón of neophytes at Mission San Antonio de Padua. Bartolomé José Lugo (alcalde, married), Felipe Juan Ella[?] (regidor); 105 more; 14 widowers; 1 single; total 122 adult males, each $1 to the war donativo. 778 baptized since founding; 241 dead; remaining 415 are women & children. Orig. pp.654–657.

author: Diego Gonzáles, Comandante
summary: Monterey company summary: 1 Tte, 1 Alférez, 1 cirujano, 1 sargento, 5 cabos, 46 soldados, 1 carpintero, 2 herreros = 57; of these 1 sargto & 8 soldados on the expedition to the río Colorado (Yuma Revolt). Orig. pp.668–669.

author: Fr. Junípero Serra
summary: Serra certifies the burial in the presidio chapel of Alférez Dn Mariano Carrillo, Habilitado of the company, native of Loreto, who died on the afternoon of the 27th [Jan 1782]. (Confirms Carrillo's death date; cf. Doc 157.) Orig. p.710.

author: José Joaquín de Arrillaga
summary: Loreto presidial company & employees, pay 1784–1789 (per ajustes de cuentas): Capt. José Joaquín de Arrillaga $1,500; Tte. José Fco. Ortega $550; Alférez José Mª Estrada $400; 1 sargento $262.4; 3 cabos @ $225 = $675; 39 soldados $8,482.4; gratificación $390; invalids (Capt. José Cañete $500, Sargto José Gabriel Arce $120, soldados Diego Ochoa & Luis Góngora[?]); maritime crews — 1 carpintero de ribera $132, 1 herrero $120, patrón Diego Pérez $168, guardián $84, 6 marineros @ $72 = $576, arráez $84 + 6 marineros $444; 5 marineros added 22 Mar 1788, serving to 11 Feb 1789. Orig. pp.719–725.

author: Antonio Bonilla, Sec. of the Comandancia Gen. de Provincias Internas (resolved by the Comandante General)
summary: Officer uniform, armament & mount regulation. *Grand uniform:* blue casaca (royal cloth), red cuffs/lapel/collar with 2 gold galones (3-thread), matching breeches, laced chupa, gilt buttons, 3-cornered laced hat — worn only inside the presidio on feast days, royal birthdays & accessions, and outside when on commission where the Governor, Inspector or any jefe is. *Small uniform:* short blue tripe/paño chupa with the same divisas + charretera. *Arms:* same calibers/sizes as the troops but finer quality. *Mount:* silla brida or 'mestiza,' or vaquera. (Companion to the troop reg, Doc 172.) Orig. pp.3–7.

author: Caballero Teodoro de Croix, Comandante General
themes: military-doctrine; caballada; apache; frontier-defense; de-croix; provincias-internas
summary: Memorandum on the presidial horse-herd situados (caballadas) & frontier cavalry doctrine. Argues the heavy company situado (346 horses, 67 mules) is unsustainable & self-defeating: constant field service ruins the soldier's kit & indebts him, leaves no time for drill, the penal law, religion, or to see wife & children — 'un hombre hambriento que lleva una vida miserable y abandonada.' The Apaches 'buscan siempre apoderarse de caballos y mulas'; once robbed, the troops are left 'á pie' and 'cesan sus operaciones por muchos meses.' Remedy: reduce each soldier to 2 horses + 1 mule kept stable-fed (caballerizas). Includes a frontier-wide allotment table: 18 regular presidios 2,284 horses/1,152 mules; 4 Nueva Vizcaya flying cos. 1,184/592; Sonora 92/46; dragoon piquetes; Sta Fe (NM) 224/112; San Antonio de Béxar 188/94; Bahía del Espíritu Santo 120/60; 30 Rosales auxiliaries 60/30 — total 4,332 horses, 2,266 mules = 6,598. Company effective strength: 71 plazas → 61 effective (guardia de caballada 20, escolta de prisiones/caudales 15, correos/enfermos/presos 8 → 21 left). Copia legalizada by Cap. Bonilla. Orig. pp.9–44 (vide pp.12–14).

author: Teodoro de Croix, Comandante General
summary: Remount (remonta) regulation for the presidios. Remounts bought with the inspector's approval when captains judge necessary; officers, sargentos & cabos must receive their remount horses by lot (sorteo), not by choice, so the soldier isn't left the worst; light-troop soldier allotted 3 horses + 1 mule (later orders: 2 + 1); dragoon piquetes 1 horse + 1 mule per man; volunteer-company soldier 1 mule. Copia legalizada by Bonilla. Orig. pp.45–47.

author: Antonio Bonilla
themes: uniform; armament; soldado-de-cuera; military-material-culture; discipline; bonilla
summary: Troop uniform, armament & mount regulation (companion to Doc 169). Troop dress per the Reglamento: short blue chupa & breeches, red cuff/lapel/collar, gilt button, gamuza chupa, black short-brimmed hat looped with a presilla on the left side 'para manejar el fusil con desembarazo,' red wool cap, blue cape (sarape/manga permitted); cuera-troops vs. light-troops distinguished by the white hat; silla vaquera, espuelas vaqueras. Expressly forbids the large iron stirrups (estriberas grandes de hierro) under penalty: officers suspension of office, sargentos loss of seniority, soldiers 4 years' presidio at ration without pay 'con un grillete al pie' (shackled). Orig. pp.49–52.

author: Teodoro de Croix
themes: military-doctrine; indigenous-warfare; apache; frontier-defense; de-croix; firearms; foreign-threat
summary: On the disadvantages of the large caballada situado & the rising military parity of the frontier Indians. Forwards his memo on the drawbacks of horse-herd situados (vide pp.3–9) for opinion; argues a California soldier needs fewer than 7 horses + 1 mule given his lighter fatigues vs. Sonora. Striking verbatim passage: when the enemy Indians were first encountered they 'ignoraban el uso de nuestros caballos y armas' and 2 soldiers could rout hundreds — but now 'todos los indios bárbaros que nos hacen la guerra saben ya el uso del arma de fuego, y algunas naciones la poseen con más acierto y seguridad que nuestras tropas mal disciplinadas, habiendo adelantado en el de sus flechas, lanzas y macanas'; they attack in equal numbers, dispute actions, rove the royal roads at will — 'se acabó el tiempo en que un pequeño número de nuestros soldados se hacía respetar de multitud de indios.' After 40 years of war the frontier costs the King >$804,000/year. Closes: ceasing horse/mule breeding in California & the frontier provinces would deter foreigners from penetrating these dominions (they would lack transport across the intervening deserts). Orig. pp.57–65.

author: Teodoro de Croix
summary: Reglamento on the management of funds: collection & transport of the situados, and the accounting (ajuste de cuentas) of officers & troops. Orig. pp.66–68.

author: Teodoro de Croix
summary: Officer appointments for the Californias presidios. Made alféreces: Sargto Mariano Carrillo, Manuel García Ruiz, Ramón Lasso de la Vega; made tenientes: Alonso Velázquez[?] & Diego Gonzáles — all to serve in the Californias. 5 officers now provided of the increase Gov. Neve proposed; they replace the comisarios, whose posts are abolished. Orig. pp.147–150.

author: José Mª Estrada, Habilitado
summary: Warehouse handover at Loreto: géneros/efectos/víveres in the Real Almacén end-1780, delivered by Comisario José Álvarez to Alférez Estrada (intervention of Cmdt. Sargto Cañete), value $12,860. Orig. pp.152–165.

author: Antonio Bonilla (signed by the Viceroy)
summary: Reglamento on criminal prisoners & justice in the presidios. For capital crimes by officers/soldiers, the governor/commander of the presidio forms the sumaria & advances the autos to sentence, remitting to the Comandancia General. Jurisdiction over the Spanish, mulato & mestizo settlers near the presidios is assigned to the captains (so they serve in war functions & to avoid jurisdictional discord) — no separate justice need be named. Detailed church-asylum (refugio) procedure: for a soldier in church asylum the captain forms the process, publishes edicts over nine days posted at the guardhouse; if a non-capital case, with caución juratoria the refugee is removed & 'corregido con caridad'; capital cases tried & remitted to the Capitanía General. Orig. pp.259–261.

author: Gob. José Joaquín de Arrillaga
summary: Santa Bárbara company pay for 1781 (per an ajuste formed 20 Oct 1793) leveled at $13,902. Orig. pp.340–342.

author: Pedro Fages
summary: Order to open a new road from San Diego. Alférez José Velázquez or Sargto Mariano Carrillo to take a party & open a road from San Diego via a ranchería, leaving the camino real, through the cañada to the head of the arroyo del Encino (a paraje Velázquez had discovered ~4 lgs. from the old route) and the cañada de Todos Santos, rejoining the camino real near the 'sepultura encerrada' — saving 10–12 leagues. Orig. pp.360–361.

author: Pedro Fages
summary: Orders the road to the Río Colorado discovered by the shortcut Fr. Fermín Lasuén indicates; 'que nunca se permita á los indios cristianos tener bailes en su ranchería' (Christian Indians never permitted to hold dances); escort guards to watch & report when Indians (gentile or Christian) rob or kill cattle. Orig. pp.361–363.

author: Pedro Fages
summary: Fages laments the deaths of Sargto Guillermo Carrillo [Cota?] & soldier José Manuel Cañedo. Orig. p.365.

author: José Ramón Lasso de la Vega
summary: Brief item: Lasso de la Vega, habilitado at San Francisco. Orig. p.402.

author: Manuel Ramón de Goya, Factor
summary: Sacred vessels & jewels (alhajas y vasos sagrados) — incl. a bell & an image of St. Francis — sent to Gov. Neve for the chapel of the Santa Bárbara presidio, value $573. Orig. p.410.

author: José de Ávila, Factor
summary: Invoice of géneros/efectos/víveres shipped to the San Francisco habilitado, 1788; incl. libranzas paid to the Síndico of the Colegio de San Fernando $620; total $6,933.4½. Orig. pp.412–418.

author: [Francisco] Trillo y Bermúdez
summary: Víveres & efectos shipped on the frigate *la Favorita* (alias 'dueña de los Remedios') & packet *San Carlos el Filipino*, consigned to the presidios' habilitado Tte. Diego Gonzáles at Monterey, value $8,928. Orig. pp.432–433.

author: José de Zúñiga
summary: San Diego presidial company distribution & defense, 1 Dec 1782. 1 Tte, 1 Alférez, 1 sargto, 5 cabos, 45 soldados = 52 hombres (short 1 cabo & 8). Caballada 120 mules, 88 horses; arms reserve 35 pairs pistols, 8 espadas, 8 lance-heads; munitions (cannon ball cartridges of 2 & 4 lb; 96 bronze balls; 15 arr. powder, 20 arr. balls); 2 mounted field cannon (cañones de campaña); flint reserve. Orig. pp.444–453.

author: Gob. José Joaquín de Arrillaga
summary: Detailed ajuste of the Santa Bárbara company for 1782: Tte. Ortega $550; Alférez Argüello $400; 2 sargtos $525; 2 cabos $450; 49 soldados $10,657; gratificación $540; adjustments $521.2; gross $13,623.6; less invalids' deduction (8 mrs/peso) $400.5½ & agency, net $13,195.7; rebajas (2 facturas de géneros $1,200.58, agencias $1,995.2, prior balance owed the company $2,353.74); $4,349.17 owed. Orig. pp.454–455.

author: José Moraga
themes: rosters; san-francisco; coastal-defense; ordnance; missions; santa-clara; san-jose
summary: San Francisco presidial company distribution & defense, 1 Dec 1782. Tte. Moraga; Alférez José Ramón Lasso de la Vega; Sargto Juan Pablo Grijalva; 4 cabos; 26 soldados. Postings: caballada 7; presidio guard 1 cabo + 4 sold; 5 at each of Missions San Francisco & Santa Clara; pueblo de San José 2; detached/sick/prisoners 8; free for service 2 officers + 5 sold. Caballada 73 horses, 58 mules; recua 13 mules; arms (33 pairs pistols, 7 good escopetas, 3 adargas, 11 cueras, 8 espadas, 32 lance-heads); 2 mounted cannon + 2 remounted; 90 arr. powder, 1,496 ball cartridges, 502 gunflints, 81 balls of 3½ lb & 16 of 6 lb for the cannon. 'Tropa bien vestida y bastimentada.' Orig. pp.456–459.

author: José Moraga, Tte. y Habilitado
summary: Existence of géneros/efectos/víveres in the SF company warehouses, value $8,583. Orig. pp.485–489.

author: Gob. José Joaquín de Arrillaga
summary: Ajuste of the Monterey company for 1782: Tte. Diego Gonzáles $550; 1 sargto $262.4; 5 cabos $1,125; 45 soldados $9,787.4; gratificación $460; $12,185; abonos to Alférez Mariano Carrillo $27.4½, Hermenegildo Sal $133.5, cirujano José Dávila $161.4, & various soldiers ($560.14); $12,745; less 1 soldier's unserved days $63; + 1 carpenter & 2 smiths $540; $13,222; the company ended owing $2,857. Orig. pp.494–495.

author: Diego Gonzáles
summary: Monterey presidial company distribution & defense, 1782. 1 Tte, 1 Alférez, 1 cirujano, 1 sargto, 5 cabos, 46 soldados, 1 carpintero, 2 herreros = 57. Postings: caballada 3; ganado 4; 2 cabos + 14 soldados detached at missions; 1 sargto + 9 soldados on the Colorado expedition (Yuma); pueblo San José 2; presidio Santa Bárbara 2; free for service the officers + 2 cabos + 10 sold. Caballada 95 horses, 113 mules; recua 27; arms reserve 44 pairs pistols, 30 lance-heads; 'armamento… en mediano estado de defensa'; troop well clothed & provisioned. Orig. pp.537–563.

author: José Fco. Ortega
summary: Santa Bárbara company roster: Tte. Ortega; Alférez José Argüello; Sargtos Pablo Antonio Cota & José Olivera; cabos Alejandro de Soto[mayor] & José Mª Ortega; 50 soldados; total 56 hombres. Orig. pp.573–574.

author: Capitán Nicolás Soler, Ayudante Inspector
summary: Brief item: Capt. Nicolás Soler, Ayudante Inspector of the California presidios. (The inspector who later accused Sal — cf. Doc 131.) Orig. p.619.

author: José Moraga
summary: SF presidial company, 1 Dec 1783: 1 Tte, 1 Alférez, 1 sargto, 4 cabos, 26 soldados = 33 hombres; arms/munitions 'poco más ó menos lo mismo' as the prior year. Orig. pp.705–727.

author: Gob. José Joaquín de Arrillaga
summary: Loreto presidial company, 1 Dec 1783: Capitán, Tte, Alférez, 2 sargtos, 3 cabos, 39 soldados = 47 hombres; 1 Tte/1 sargto/2 cabos/26 soldados detached on the frontier; 1 sargto + 4 at Santa Ana. Caballada 135 mules, 14 horses; arms reserve; scarcity of provisions, warehouse empty of effects. (Monthly reports by Capt. Joaquín Cañete show little change.) Orig. pp.728–740.

author: Mariano G. Vallejo
summary: Mexican-period muster: the San Francisco presidial company at Sonoma, 1836. Tte. M. G. Vallejo; Alférez 1º Dámaso A. Rodríguez; Sargtos Prado, Mesa & Antonio Pina; 1 clarín, 2 cabos, 17 soldados = 24 hombres. (Late addition to this 1780–3 tomo; the SF company had relocated to Sonoma.) Orig. pp.805–806.

author: Mariano G. Vallejo
summary: Mexican-period muster: the San Francisco presidial company, 1833. Alférez 1º Mariano G. Vallejo; 2º Dámaso A. Rodríguez; Sargtos Pablo Pacheco & Cayetano Juárez; 4 cabos, 2 clarines, 31 soldados = 41 hombres. Monthly pay: Alférez 1º $47.10, 2º $39.25, sargto $29.12½, cabo $24.25, clarín $14.56, soldado $19.37½, gratificación $11.62½; total $914.25. Rodríguez joined 1 June; strength grew from 36. Orig. pp.807–827.

author: Manuel Ramón de Goya
summary: Invoice of géneros & cajones of effects shipped to the Loreto habilitado: first cost $12,226.7 + freight $284.6 = $12,537.5. Orig. pp.842–845.

author: Gob. Arrillaga
summary: Ajuste of the Monterey company for 1783: Tte, Alférez, 1 sargto, 5 cabos, 46 soldados + gratificación $460 = $12,802.4; carpenter $180, smith $180, soldier Fernando Chamorro (part-year) $164.1; $13,326.5. Orig. pp.849–850.

author: Gob. Arrillaga
summary: Ajuste of the Monterey company for 1784: 1 Tte, 1 Alférez, 1 sargto, 4 cabos, 45 soldados + gratificación $460 = $12,802.4; carpenter & smith $360; soldier adjustments $50; $12,770. Orig. pp.851–852.

author: José Fco. de Ortega
summary: Santa Bárbara presidio distribution: 56 hombres — caballada 8, San Buenaventura 13, Los Ángeles 2, San Gabriel 4, free for service 22. Orig. p.1.

author: José Fco. de Ortega
summary: SB company arms & livestock, Mar 1783: 84 horses, 162 mules; 14 pairs pistols, 140 cannon cartridges (4-pdr), 6,700 musket cartridges, 18 arr. powder, 14 arr. balls, 119 arr. lead, 2,860 gunflints. Mentions 2 pedreros (swivel guns) firing at the target (consuming lead/ball). Orig. p.7.

author: Nicolás Soler
summary: Orders branding-irons (fierros) made for the companies' horses & mules, allotted per presidio: Loreto 5, San Diego 2, Monterey 3, San Francisco 4, Santa Bárbara 5. Orig. p.13.

author: Ortega
summary: SB fondo de retención, 2nd half of 1783: in $1,112.4; out $87.4. Orig. p.55.

author: José de Zúñiga
summary: SD fondo de común de alcances, 1782: in $765; out $207. Orig. pp.63–64.

author: Cristóbal Carralán[?] (José López)
summary: On the Monte Pío Militar (military pension fund): implementing art. 11, ch. 2 of the Reglamento — in which cases the liquid funds apply to the Monte and which dependents (alimentados) are admitted. Orig. pp.70–73.

author: [Gálvez?]
summary: Arms shipment to the California presidios (San Diego, San Francisco, Monterey): 240 swords, 240 lance-heads (mocharras), 257 pairs of pistols. Orig. p.110.

author: José Moraga
summary: SF presidial company roster by name, 1 Apr 1783. Tte. José Moraga; Subtte. Ramón Lasso; Sargto Juan Pablo Grijalva; 4 cabos (Valerio de Mesa, Gabriel Peralta, Joaquín Álvarez, Pablo Pinto); 26 soldados (Ramón Bojorques, Juan Ant. Amézquita, Ignacio Linares, Isidro Altamirano, Mariano Cordero, Anastasio Vázquez, Ant. Acevedo, Felipe Tapia, Agustín Valenzuela, Ignacio de Soto, Pedro Bojorques, José Valencia, Juan Bernal, Joaquín de Castro, Manuel Arellano, Nicolás Galindo, Ignacio Higuera, Felipe Ochoa, Juan José Peralta, Ignacio Castro, José Gonzáles, Miguel Pacheco, Manuel Higuera, Salvador Espinosa, Alejo Miranda, Nicolás Berreyesa); total 33 hombres. Orig. pp.114–115.

author: José Moraga
summary: SF company arms & livestock, Mar 1783: 79 horses, 76 mules (recua 17 mules); 33 pairs pistols, 4 espadas, 20 lance-heads, 2 mounted cannon, 16 escopetas (5 fair, 11 bad), 32 espadas (1 good, 31 medium), 32 lance-heads. Orig. pp.116–117.

author: Felipe de Neve
summary: Orders Cañete to hand over command of the [Loreto] company to Felipe de Goycoechea (named teniente by the Comandante General), on Cañete's promotion to Captain. Orig. p.129.

author: José Moraga
summary: Certifies the death of cabo Pablo Pinto (of illness), 30 Nov. Orig. p.205.

author: Felipe de Goycoechea
summary: Account of the fondo de retención of the habilitaduría handed over by Ortega (habilitado 1781 – Oct 1784). Orig. p.205.

author: José Fco. de Ortega
summary: By order of Col. Fages, Ortega delivers to Hermenegildo Sal Real Hacienda livestock at Los Ángeles: 12 mares @ $4, 1 horse $8, 1 colt & 3 goats $6.4, 1 ram $2.4, 8 cows @ $5, 4 oxen @ $6, 2 steers @ $5. Orig. pp.330–331.

author: José Fco. de Ortega
summary: General summary of the SB presidio account, 1st half 1783: Cargo $15,269; Data $15,269; descubierto $2.7½. Orig. pp.243–250.

author: Felipe de Neve
summary: Orders an accounting credit (~$32 + $84.4) for the benefit of Doña Joaquina Espinosa[?], drawn from the sale of 2 colts @ $12.4. Orig. p.253.

author: Hermenegildo Sal
summary: Balance against the San Francisco habilitado for the year: ~$1,062 charged, ~$856 in his favor. Orig. pp.293–295.

author: José Moraga
summary: SF Real Hacienda cattle account, 1782 + 1783 births: 447 head; 44 sold/lost/dead (value $155). Orig. pp.302–303.

author: José Moraga
summary: Value of SF presidio effects belonging to the Real Hacienda this year, $411.4. Orig. pp.338–340.

author: Caballero de Croix & Matías Gálvez
summary: Retirement (retiro) granted to Joaquín Cañete with $500 pension (commuted in the case of a 'Capitán vivo'). Orig. pp.383–384.

author: José Mª Estrada
summary: Existences of the Loreto presidio to date, $4,750. Orig. pp.402–406.

author: José de Zúñiga
summary: Existences of the San Diego presidio per inventory, $2,879. Orig. pp.495–497.

author: José Moraga
summary: Existences in the SF presidio warehouse, effects &c, $2,773. Orig. pp.508–511.

author: Felipe de Neve
summary: Orders Cañete to hand over command of the presidio company to Alférez José Estrada. Orig. pp.530–531.

author: Felipe de Neve
summary: Transcribes the Comandante General's appointment of Manuel de la Merced[?] as Captain of the Loreto presidio; to pass review from 1 Aug. Orig. pp.534–535.

author: José Mª Estrada
summary: Loreto review: 46 men, of whom 33 absent. Orig. pp.1–9.

author: José Moraga
summary: SF ordnance inventory, Apr 1784: 2 cannon (4-pdr); 140 ball cartridges + 40 grapeshot (metralla); 81 bronze balls; 18 arr. 22 lb powder; 1,526 musket cartridges; 16 escopetas (some powder held at Monterey). Orig. pp.17–18.

author: Hermenegildo Sal
summary: Monterey company account, 1st half 1784: Crédito $12,261; Débito $157. Orig. pp.45–48.

author: Hermenegildo Sal
summary: Summary of the Monterey accounts, 1st half 1784: cargo $10,030; data $10,240; $210 in the Habilitado's favor. Orig. pp.54–60.

author: Hermenegildo Sal
summary: Monterey accounts, 2nd half 1784: cargo $25,593; data $25,545; $152 in the Habilitado's favor. Orig. pp.62–67.

author: José Moraga
summary: The SF presidio supplies rations to several named individuals (José Sánchez, Juan Espinosa, Silverio Altamirano[?], Marcelo Pinto, Mariano Castro) and to 9 Indians of the Sierra de San José. Orig. pp.156–158.

author: Felipe de Goycoechea
summary: Summary of the SB habilitaduría general account, 1784: cargo $26,513; data $26,495; $17 against. Orig. pp.231–240.

author: Felipe de Neve
summary: Neve has ordered tithes (diezmos) levied on the settlers of San José from the day they complete 5 years. (Tithes thread, cf. Doc 125.) Orig. p.248.

author: José Moraga
summary: SF company strength: 34 plazas. Orig. p.267.

author: José de Zúñiga
summary: SD habilitaduría existences per inventory, $2,920. Orig. pp.273–274.

author: José de Zúñiga
summary: SD company strength: 44 plazas. Orig. pp.275–276.

author: cabo Mariano Cordero
summary: On the death of his father-in-law, cabo Pablo Pinto. Orig. pp.322–323.

author: Nicolás Soler
summary: Re Francisca Rosales, widow of cabo Pinto, & their children (Juan María, Marcelo, María). Orig. p.324.

author: Fr. Francisco Palóu
summary: Palóu's receipt for $18.4 alms for a habit-shroud (mortaja de hábito) & a sung funeral mass (misa cantada de cuerpo presente). Orig. p.328.

author: José Mª Estrada
summary: Loreto habilitado's account of effects: cargo $7,034; data $4,858; $2,176 in favor of the Real Hacienda. Orig. pp.422–427.

author: José Mª Estrada
summary: 169 bulls distributed to 25 members of the company, value $431 (incl. 100 sold to private parties @ $3.7). Orig. p.437.

author: José Mª Estrada
summary: Existences of the Loreto presidio, $4,026. Orig. pp.446–450.

author: Nicolás Soler
summary: Inventory of the SF habilitaduría taken on the death of José Moraga (founding SF commander): cargo $12,071; data $12,106; José Ramón Lasso de la Vega took charge of the existences. Orig. pp.1–10.

author: José Moraga
summary: SF review, 1 Jun 1785: 33 hombres. Orig. pp.105–106.

author: Diego Gonzáles
summary: Monterey company 50 hombres; 36 cannot write (illiterate). Orig. p.120.

author: Ramón Lasso de la Vega
summary: SF review, 1786: 30 hombres; 23 cannot read (illiterate). Orig. p.185.

author: José Antonio Rengel
summary: Exempts soldiers attached to Pueblo San José de Guadalupe from tithes for their first 5 years (as the Reglamento allows the settlers); but company members holding cattle & mares pay tithes as pobladores. Orig. pp.204–205.

author: José de Zúñiga
summary: Certifies that Alférez José Velázquez died 2 Nov & was buried in the church of Mission San Gabriel (per its ministers Fr. Antonio Cruzado & Fr. [Miguel] Sánchez). (Velázquez = the road/route surveyor, cf. Docs 179, 186.) Orig. p.322.

author: Felipe de Goycoechea
summary: SB company review: 60 hombres. Orig. pp.298–299.

author: Fr. Francisco Palóu
summary: Palóu certifies the death of Dn José [Joaquín] Moraga (founding commander of the SF presidio), married to María del Pilar de León y Barceló — died the night of 13 July 1785, buried the 15th in the church of the Mission. Orig. p.347.

author: Hermenegildo Sal
summary: Monterey company review: 54 hombres. Orig. pp.585–586.

author: José Mª Estrada
summary: Loreto company 46 plazas + goleta crew 10, lancha crew 8. Orig. pp.399–402.

author: Ramón Lasso de la Vega
summary: SF presidio cattle: 719 head in 1785 (578 in 1784). Orig. pp.463–464.

author: Ramón Lasso de la Vega
summary: SF presidio general account: cargos $16,936; data $17,014. Orig. pp.480–484.

author: José Moraga
summary: SF company strength: 33 plazas. Orig. pp.506–507.

author: José Mª Estrada
summary: Loreto habilitación account: cargo $7,284; data $6,547. Orig. pp.516–526.

author: José Joaquín de Arrillaga
summary: Re Francisco Méndez & Juan N. Uraga[?], sentenced by the Governor to labor without pay (charged to the Real Hacienda). Orig. p.542.

author: Gob. Joaquín de Arrillaga
summary: Loreto company review, 1786. Capt. Arrillaga (absent in the Governorship); Tte. José Fco. de Ortega; Alférez José Mª Estrada; Sargtos Fco. Aguiar, Juan Botiller; 3 cabos, 39 soldados = 47 hombres + marine dept. (carpenter, smith; goleta crew 10, lancha crew 7). Dotación unchanged since 1 Jan. Orig. pp.24, 41–43, 284–288, 332–334, 476–489, 509–512, 526–551 (scattered).

author: Hermenegildo Sal, Habilitado
summary: Monterey presidial company, 1786: Tte. Diego Gonzáles; Alférez Hermenegildo Sal; Sargto Mariano Vázquez; 5 cabos, 46 soldados; smith & carpenter = 56 hombres. Orig. pp.25–92, 593 (scattered).

author: José de Zúñiga, Habilitado
summary: San Diego presidial company, 1786: Zúñiga Tte. y Comandante; Alférez vacant; Sargto Ignacio Alvarado; 5 cabos, 44 soldados, 3 licenciados; smith & carpenter = 57 hombres. Orig. pp.144–592 (scattered).

author: José de Zúñiga
summary: San Diego company, 1788: Tte. Zúñiga; Alférez Pablo Grijalva; Sargto Ignacio Alvarado; 5 cabos, 45 soldados, 1 invalid; same as April. Orig. pp.106–120.

author: José de Zúñiga
summary: San Diego general accounts, 1782–1786. Invoices received from México (1782 $7,064.1; 1783 $5,815.4; 1784 $675.4½; 1785 $8,832.5; 1786 $11,168.4½ = $39,636.6) + effects from San Blas (1782–86 = $17,388.3); débito $86,409; data $80,620; existence of effects end-1786 $6,200; $411 in Zúñiga's favor. Orig. pp.123–131.

author: José de Zúñiga
summary: San Diego war donativo of 1781: the presidial company $574.4 + Mission San Gabriel $441 = $1,015.4. Orig. p.124.

author: José de Zúñiga
summary: Value of supplies furnished by the missions to the SD company: San Diego $1,681, San Juan [Capistrano] $597, San Gabriel $2,042, San Luis $173 = $3,653. Orig. p.126.

author: José de Zúñiga
summary: San Diego special royal revenues: the tobacco monopoly (renta de tabacos) — 1785 $1,494.3½ + 1786 $1,505.3½ = $2,999.7; the mail (renta de Correos) 1783–86 net $207.6.9; carpentry/forge work for the Real Hacienda & private parties $230.7. Orig. pp.127–128.

author: José de Zúñiga
summary: SD presidial company, 1785: Tte. Zúñiga; Sargto Ignacio Alvarado; 5 cabos, 44 soldados; smith & carpenter = 53 hombres. Deaths this year: soldiers Francisco Pérez[?], Joaquín Guerrero & licenciado Mariano López. Orig. pp.151–152.

author: José de Ávila
summary: Invoice of 106 tercios/cajones of géneros/efectos/víveres shipped to the Loreto presidio: first cost $29,346 + freight $880 + cordage $893.2 = $30,239.2. Orig. p.185.

author: Ramón Lasso de la Vega, Habilitado
summary: SF presidial company, 1786: 1 Alférez (Lasso de la Vega); Sargto Juan Pablo Grijalva; 4 cabos, 26 soldados, 2 invalids = 34 hombres. Orig. pp.173–620 (scattered).

author: Felipe de Goycoechea, Habilitado
summary: Santa Bárbara company, 1786: 1 Tte, 1 Alférez, 3 sargtos, 2 cabos; soldiers grew from 45 (March) to 53 (Dec), total ~60 hombres; 1 sargto + 14 soldados detached at San Buenaventura, others at missions/caballada; free for service the 2 officers, 1 sargto, 17 soldados. (Cabos named: Raymundo Carrillo &c.) Orig. pp.213–331 (scattered).

author: Fr. Fermín Francisco de Lasuén
summary: Brief item: Lasuén as minister (signing at Santa Clara). Orig. p.249.

author: Frs. Diego Noboa & Tomás de la Peña
summary: Brief item: the Santa Clara ministers. Orig. p.249.

author: Sargto Mariano de la Luz Verdugo
themes: prosopography; military-history; rivera-moncada; 1775-revolt; yuma-revolt; monterey; genealogy
summary: Service record (hoja de servicio) of Sargento Mariano de la Luz Verdugo, age 42. Enlisted as a soldier 15 Dec 1766; cabo 1 Dec 1774; sargento 5 May 1781; served at Loreto (7+ yrs), San Diego (7 yrs), Monterey. Campaigns: the 1769 party under Capt. Fernando de Rivera y Moncada that intercepted gentiles at the paraje de la Ciénega (8 killed); the suppression of the 1775 San Diego revolt (sierra sweeps to seize the ringleaders 'de la sublevación de cristianos y gentiles que destruyeron la misión de San Diego en 1775'); and the 1782 Río Colorado expedition under Cmdt.-Inspector Felipe de Neve (post-Yuma campaign). Orig. p.335.

author: Gob. Arrillaga
summary: Company ajuste for 1785 (headed 'Compañía de San Francisco' but the named officers — Tte. Diego Gonzáles, Alférez Hermenegildo Sal — are Monterey's [⚠ Savage label likely slipped]): 1 sargto, 5 cabos, 44 soldados, carpenter & smith, gratificación $460; sueldos $12,975.6; total $13,435.6. Orig. pp.346–347.

author: José de Zúñiga
summary: List of SD livestock-breeders (criadores) with their start years (Tte. Zúñiga 1782, Sargto Ignacio Alvarado 1781, cabos Luis Mª Verdugo/Juan Mª Olivera/Macario Castro, soldados from 1781…; vaqueros Ignacio Cota, Juan José Domínguez) + herd totals: cattle 570, sheep 167, goats 261, horses 207, mules 60. Orig. pp.354–355.

author: Luis Gutiérrez, Contador Gen. del Ejército
summary: Accounts of the Loreto Departamento de Marina (goleta/lancha crews), 1781–1785: yearly haberes (1781 $2,227 … 1785 $1,920) = $10,400.6.10¾ over five years, less $201.5.6 invalids' pay. Orig. pp.587–591.

author: Felipe de Goycoechea
summary: 24 sailors of the San Blas Departamento de Marina served as laborers (sirvientes) at Missions San Buenaventura & Santa Bárbara and the SB presidio this year; the habilitado supplied them effects worth $1,403.5.2; 6 returned to San Blas in October. Orig. p.392.

author: José Mª Estrada
summary: Loreto company account 1786: effects end-1785 $1,820; víveres from San Blas $6,326.6.6; ropa from México $31,827.1.6; tobacco-monopoly extraction $2,128.7.7; various Real Hacienda $2,128.4.7; troop charges (invalids/montepío) $572.0.7; total $44,837.6.1; given to troops $24,670, sailors $2,281, other $720; effects end-1786 $17,165.0.4. Orig. p.394.

author: José Mª Estrada
summary: Royal revenues collected in this part of Baja California: alcabalas (sales tax) $532; wild bulls (toros orejanos) $940; salt & saltpetre $279.6; quintos $120.1; arms reintegration $103.1; sea freights $73.1 = $1,915.3. Orig. p.396.

author: José de Ávila
summary: Invoice of 29 tercios/cajones of géneros/efectos/víveres to the SB habilitado Goycoechea: first cost $8,338 + freight $222 = $8,560. Orig. pp.422–426.

author: Felipe de Goycoechea
summary: SB supply invoice — víveres/efectos received on the frigate *Favorita* (Capt. José Tobar) from San Blas, total $2,474, with itemized prices (maíz $4½/fanega, manteca $7½/arroba, arroz, panocha, azúcar $13/arroba, Mazatlán ankle-boots 3½ rls/pair, Aguacate saddles $13…; incl. 15% markup). Orig. p.424.

author: Hermenegildo Sal
themes: lapérouse; foreign-contact; monterey; livestock; exploration; open-door
summary: Livestock delivered to the Lapérouse expedition at Monterey, 1786. In the Monterey Real Hacienda livestock account (caballar 35, burral 15, vacuno 1,882 [+377 born], etc.; total 2,566 head $1,622), among the cattle sold this year: 27 steers @ $5, 2 calved cows @ $6, 1 heifer @ $3, 'entregadas á las fragatas del Rey de Francia *Boussole* y *Astrolabe*' (Savage notes the original wrote 'Brújula y Astrolavio'). The only known C-A documentary trace of the French *Lapérouse* expedition's Sept 1786 call at Monterey — the first foreign (non-Spanish) expedition to visit Alta California. Orig. pp.440–445.

author: Ramón Lasso de la Vega
summary: SF tobacco monopoly (renta de tabacos) account 1786: stock end-1785 (29,300 cigarette packs, 3,740 cigar papers, snuff) $2,042; invoice received $1,891; distribution 23,873 packs + 3,873 papers; 10% sellers' commission. Orig. pp.581–585.

author: Pedro Fages
summary: Records that the lancha *Nuestra Señora del Rosario* was built at San Blas for Mission Todos Santos, cost $3,902. Orig. p.33.

author: (unsigned; Savage notes "documento francés")

Then to supply the presidio with smaller craft: keep a goleta + a small 74-ft-keel goleta + an existing lancha; a new 71-ft-keel goleta is being built for víveres, crewed at 12–15 plazas. Vallejo, Comandante General de Cal.' make this almost certainly the enlistment of Ignacio Vicente Ferrer Vallejo, father of Mariano Guadalupe Vallejo. Orig. p.128.

Orig. pp.248–255.

author: José Soberanes

author: Felipe de Goycoechea, Comandante
summary: 1790 interior-Chumash ambush of a Santa Bárbara patrol. Sargto Olivera + 8 men sent (24 Aug) after a deserter Indian to the ranchería de Tinoqui, also to prospect for metal veins (vetas de metales); joined by 9 Indians of Napalayagua (enemies of Tinoqui). After detaching men to gather ore, the camp (real) was attacked from the rear by 60+ Indians; the sergeant fought back wounding several but could not reach camp, found it in ruins; soldiers Gabriel Espinosa & Ignacio Carlón were killed (Carlón slain asleep; Espinosa made a vigorous defense), weapons (espadas, mangas, escopetas) seized. The survivors regrouped near San Buenaventura & returned to SB 1 Oct. Goycoechea requests mission reinforcements to punish the hostile Indians. Orig. pp.435–437.

Pistols (7 pairs since 1781, rest since 1779) with the defect that the safeties are internal — they fire inadvertently; 'la falta de armero causa graves perjuicios.' Espadas of good temper (no reserve); lance-heads bad. Munitions: 53 arr. powder (= 780 ball cartridges of both calibers), 10 arr. lead, 20 arr. 23 lb balls, 1,400 gunflints. Orig. pp.627–628.

author: Manuel Merino (asesor's opinion)
summary: Opinion on the New California poblador livestock-grant policy & the tithe/cattle-disposal dispute. Each settler was given (as ventajas or 'por poblador') 2 mares, 2 cows with a calf, 2 ewes & 2 goats, to graze together & breed for 2 years (no killing or selling), then to build to 15 mares + a stallion, 15 cows + a bull, 12 ewes + a ram, 10 nanny-goats + a billy. Debates whether settlers retain the old liberty to dispose of their stock (the tithe-holders would limit it); concludes only male animals may be culled as needed, the Government guarding against abuses. A key document on the founding ranching economy & livestock distribution to settlers. Orig. pp.235–240.

themes: foreign-threat; anglo-spanish-war; english-navy; manila-galleon; gulf-of-california; san-diego; loreto; baja-california; open-door; 1799
summary: Investigation into the 1799 capture of the Spanish sloop *Mercedes* by a British squadron in the Gulf of California (Anglo-Spanish War). *See expanded Entry A below.* The balandra *Mercedes* (Bernardo Suárez), San Blas→Guaymas with country produce + passengers (José Gonzáles, Cristóbal Salas, 9 Guainamota Indians for the Islas Marías carey/turtle-shell fishery), found English traces at the Marías (cut wood, salt taken, a barraca, papers in English ink, English gunflints), then boarded & seized by an English frigate (29–31 May). The 'Presidente de la escuadra' claimed a fleet of frigates detached from Cabo San Lucas hunting the ship that left San Blas 20 May and awaiting the Manila galleon ('la nao de China'); they had a fortified sick-camp with cannon on San Juanito island; refused a $1,000 ransom, taking instead cattle/onions/hens + sugar, chocolate, panocha, a bell, etc. transferred from the prize. Named ships/captains: frigate 'Tester' [Vesta?] 18 guns + 14 carronades, Cmdt. Clark[?] (squadron chief); frigate 'Net-siut' 24/16 guns, Capt. Moss[?]/Moore; frigate 'Viñas' [Venus?] 18 guns; 'Pájaro' [Bird] 14 guns, Capt. Powell[?] (who had flown American colors at Banderas valley). They claimed 22 prizes off Peru (incl. a 12-gun brig at Paita) & to have robbed the warehouses of Sonsonate. Bombshell close: they did not fear San Blas even with 60 cannon, might raid it on their December retreat, and planned to winter at San Diego — 'instruidos por los planos de [Clower?] de Nutka' and carrying men who two years prior [c.1797] in two frigates near San Diego had, one moonlit night, entered in their boats, sounded the whole harbour, and knew its approaches.' Sloop recovered 16 June at the ensenada de Cerralvo by Sargto Javier Aguilar; depositions taken (escribano José Mariano Urrutia); copia legalizada by Arrillaga. Orig. pp.481–504.

Orig. pp.707–708.

author: José Argüello

summary: Weather & health report, 1798: harsh climate; regular winter rains, mild frost, hail; middling harvests; 'terrible y continuado viento' + perpetual fog; winter began with frosts & gales damaging building roofs. ⚠️ Notes 'en las misiones se advierten varias enfermedades de gálico [syphilis], especie de tisis [consumption], y otras no conocidas en sus neófitos, de cuya resulta fallecen bastantes de ellos' — epidemic disease killing many mission Indians. Orig. p.709.

author: Gob. interino Arrillaga
themes: foreign-threat; bodega; coastal-defense; british; arrillaga; presidio-decay; open-door
summary: State & needed reinforcement of the California presidios — response to the Viceroy's order to fortify against British ships & to occupy Bodega Bay. Acknowledges the Viceroy's order (24 Nov 1792) to put the new establishments 'en el posible mejor estado de defensa, por si algo de los buques ingleses que hoy cruzan esta costa arribasen… para que no observen nuestra debilidad,' and a second order resolving to occupy the puerto de la Bodega and fortify San Francisco, Monterey & San Diego. Reports the artillery state (SF 2 cannon, both unserviceable; Monterey 8 + 3 pedreros good; SB 2 + 1 pedrero; SD 3, dismounted/no gunners) and that the guard is 'imaginaria.' Proposes garrisons: SD 75, Monterey 75, SB 63, SF 51; a small San Blas schooner at Monterey & SF Oct–Apr; 'y si se ocupa la Bodega se necesitará una fuerza por eso.' Orig. pp.72–78.

author: Gov. Borica
summary: troops content with available meat, scarce at the Rancho del Rey from frequent gentile thefts; ⭐⭐ on the Indians: by the Laws of the Indies neophytes should leave mission tutelage at 10 years, but those of New California 'at this rate will not leave even in 10 centuries — why? God knows, and men know something of it.'

author: Gov. Borica

author: Gov. Borica
summary: received the inquiry into the causes Indians had for fleeing the missions ('abandoning the Catholic religion') to their own lands

author: Gov. Borica
