summary: Companion case to #20: vs. Eladio, Christian Indian of Mission SF, for the atrocious killing of neophytes Germán, Esteban & Fidel (3 May). Other diligencias not copied

author: fiscal Bernardo Navarrete, escribano Santiago Gómez
themes: conspiracy; revolt-1835; foreigners; americans; figueroa; apalategui; torres; borderlands
summary: Causa de conspiración = the 1835 Apalátegui–Torres revolt against Gov. Figueroa (first 2 folios missing). Deposition of American Mr. Job Day via interpreter John B.R. Cooper; confession of Don Miguel Hidalgo detailing meetings w/ Antonio Apalátegui & Francisco Torres, letters smuggled by brigantine to Fernando Escobosa in Guaymas (Sonora), Englishman Lewis Burton, Martin Cooper, Benito Díaz; plotters 'counted on the Monterey troops, minus their officers.' Savage: 'evidently a copy.' ⚠ Date jumps to 1835

author: fiscal Tte. José Joaquín Maitorena (begun by Tte. José M.ª Estudillo); defender Tte. Marcos Faboyat
themes: criminal; theft; indigenous; mission; punishment; santa-barbara
summary: Burglary/theft from the presidio warehouse (night 23 Oct). Accused: Indians Policarpo (Mission S. Vicente, Baja Cal.) & Francisco (Mission SF, Alta Cal.). Notable fiscal dictamen on the futility of punishing Indian theft now that the new legislation has abolished flogging — 'un vicio casi connatural en la mayor parte de los Indios'

author: issued by Cmdt. Gen. Echeandía; signed Santiago Argüello
summary: Hoja de servicios of Capt. José María Estudillo (entered service 23 Jul 1796; total 44 yrs 4 mo; served Loreto/Monterey/San Diego). Campaigns: against Indians at Monterey Bay (killed/captured/wounded), the Tulares, Sierra Nevada, and a road-finding expedition to Sonora via the Colorado. Swore to Independence 9 Oct 1822. Died 8 Apr 1830

author: Cmdt. Francisco María Ruiz
summary: San Diego presidial company strength return (April): 71 men (1 capt. + 1 tte. vacant, 1 alf., 2 sgts, 7 cpls, 1 cadet, 59 soldiers) + 4 artillery + 19 invalids; 437 horses; 25 fusiles. Troop & families 'medianamente vestida,' armament 'continuamente comprometido.' Independence sworn at SD 20 Apr 1822

author: Tte. Juan María Ibarra
summary: Averiguación clearing José Joaquín Estudillo: American Charles (Carlos) Laing claimed goods missing from an embargoed *chalupa* that Estudillo (commissioned for the embargo) had chartered for salt transport. Investigation found Laing relied on sailor Winslow Lewis, who lied; Estudillo cleared

author: Tte. [Santiago] Argüello
themes: foreigners; americans; otter; trade; customs; embargo; san-diego
summary: 'Sobre entrega de mercancías detenidas': the 3 trunks of American Charles (Carlos) Laing detained ~6 months at the SD custom-house. Recaps the 1826 contract Laing & Henry D. Bolt made for sea-otter/seal hunting (pesca de lobos marinos), provisioning the ship, payable in effects at Canton/Manila prices; the duties dispute (Domingo Carrillo held no duties owed since payment was in ship's provisions; Laing disagreed); admin. Juan Bandini & Tte. Ibarra inventory the trunks; per the law of 4 Sept 1823 declared confiscated by the Comandancia Gen. court; referred to the Comisario of Sonora

author: [Luis] Argüello
recipient: José de la Guerra y Noriega (borrador)
summary: Desertion of 5 soldiers: approves they be pursued with 1 sargento, 1 cabo & 7 soldados

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: From 30 Jan the 7%-of-duties assignment to the Habilitados is to be understood. ⭐ On whaling ships (buques balleneros): must conform to the new Arancel; once confirmed truly whalers only putting in for provisions, they pay nothing

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: Accedes that the duties of the Puerto de San Pedro be collected by the Habilitación de San Diego

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: Confers faculties to remedy the Los Ángeles vecinos' complaints about the bad government of that Ayuntamiento

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: Order that ALL the Russians in the jurisdiction be transferred to Monterey with the greatest possible brevity, 'dándoles los medios de transporte' (providing them the means of transport)

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: Asks for the pormenor (details) of the action fought at the Laguna de Buenavista on the 9th [Apr] — the 1824 Chumash Revolt pursuit into the Tulares

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: Named Capt. Pablo de la Portilla *comandante y caudillo* of the whole division going on campaign [the 1824 Chumash Revolt expedition]

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: Comply with the Diputación provincial on duty-collection; + the Supremo Poder Ejecutivo named Brigadier Juan José Miñón Comandante Gen. of both Californias, Manuel Ruiz his 2nd

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: 8-day term for the Indians to return to their Missions, else treated as 'contumaces, revoltosos y apóstatas,' troops sent to subdue them [Chumash Revolt ultimatum]

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: Given the unrest at Missions Sta Bárbara, Sta Inés & La Purísima, Alf. José Joaquín Maitorena kept at his presidio, sent to the consejos de guerra (courts-martial) to be held [Chumash Revolt trials]

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: 8 Englishmen who deserted/stayed from the English whaling frigate *Royal George* have arrived at the Presidio. (Note: in Solá's private letters he addresses de la Guerra as 'Mi estimado Padrino.')

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: Faculties for Maitorena & Sgt. Anastasio Carrillo to enlist 25 volunteer recruits among the Presidio & Los Ángeles vecinos for the Monterey & SF companies; failing volunteers, from the vagos útiles + bachelors by lot

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: Encloses the Ministry's reply on Capt. Portilla's expedition to the Tulares for the reduction of the rebel neophytes of La Purísima & Sta Inés [Chumash Revolt]

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: Informed of the bad conduct of the officers of the brigantine *Aquiles*, who have declared themselves true pirates

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: Returns the criminal case vs. Tomás, neophyte of San Miguel (Baja Cal.) for procedural defects (order of declarations/confession; immunity declaration belongs to the Bishop, not the Padre Presidente)

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: The San Diego presidio ranch cattle are finished (lack of provisions + deaths); the cattle-tithes (diezmos) of the jurisdiction's ranchos & pueblos pass to the Nacional de San Diego

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: 'Son tres documentos': forms lists of the tithe-debtors; remit the padrones de población needed for those to be sent to the Presidente de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos; Comdt.-Gen. Echeandía on arrival will settle the Pueblo's political/economic government

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: The captain of the frigate 'Asia' brings funds to buy hemp (cáñamo) for the Departamento de San Blas; the Missions to send samples

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: The indios Benito & Bernabé of Sta Inés — sentenced to 10 years' prison + perpetual exile for the *sublevación y asesinatos* of early 1824 [the Chumash Revolt] — have escaped; orders their recapture

author: José Joaquín de la Torre (cadete/juez)
summary: Diligencias vs. cabo 2° José de Jesús Vallejo for injuring/abusing the indio Nicolás Dávila (7 Aug); escribano sold. Miguel Ávila; sentence not copied

author: José Joaquín Maitorena (fiscal Tte. Narciso Fábregat)
summary: Sumaria vs. soldado de Mazatlán José Carrizosa for leaving the cuartel 11pm–reveille; sentenced 3 yrs grillete on public works

author: fiscal cadete Joaquín Estudillo
summary: Criminal vs. Leonardo Félix, Serapio Escamilla, José Vásquez & artillery-militiaman Santiago Vásquez, accused of desertion with aggravating circumstances; escribano cabo Paulino Viteri; defender Tte. José Mª Estudillo

author: fiscal Tte. Antonio Sánchez
summary: Sumario vs. soldado de San Blas Antonio Montes for robbing clothing from soldier Manuel Margarito; fiscal urges barca plana (galley) 2 yrs

author: fiscal Joaquín Estudillo
summary: Sumaria vs. Gerardo, neophyte of Santa Clara, for killing Florencia, neophyte of the same mission (10 Feb); ruled accidental not premeditated → 1 yr presidio + grillete

author: fiscal José Palomares (sgt. ret.)
summary: Sumaria vs. artillery-militiaman Julián Lara for stealing a horse from retired sgt. Javier Alvarado; fiscal asks release, prison suffered enough

author: José Antonio de la Guerra y Carrillo (fiscal Narciso Fabregat)
summary: Sumaria vs. artillery-militiaman Rosalino Olivera for selling a stolen horse to a Sta Bárbara neophyte; one witness + confession → de la Guerra urges a moderate penalty

author: Juan Ballesteros, Anastasio Ávila et al.
summary: Petition that Francisco Morales remain as Secretario (a capable, honest man)

author: Gutiérrez
summary: Reply: each signer of the prior petition must submit his request separately

author: Francisco Morales
summary: Resigns as Ayuntamiento Secretario (ill; the $15/mo salary insufficient)

author: Morales
summary: Not having received the decree, asks the motives / whether his citizen rights are disregarded

author: Francisco Morales (Secretario)
summary: 'Observaciones al Ayuntamiento': governance reforms (judges' reunion, fixed court days, study the constitution)

author: Desiderio Ibarra (2° Regidor)
summary: Certifies Morales arrived 1818 to establish a school of 'letras menores,' run 2½ years, then elected Secretario

author: fiscal Subtte. José Ramírez
summary: Averiguación between soldiers Anastasio M.[?] & Lázaro Soto (artillery militiaman) over the sale of a horse & a cow (paid in blue bayeta + $5); no sentence

author: board: Luis A. Argüello, Pablo de la Portilla (fiscal José Sánchez/Domingo Carrillo)
summary: Causa criminal vs. San Blas infantry soldier Eduardo Garnica for killing fellow soldier Esteban Barreras (23 Jun 1823); escribano Joaquín Piña; condemned to 6 yrs Presidio

author: fiscal Antonio Buelna
summary: Causa criminal vs. José Higuera for carnal act with his stepdaughter Mª Francisca Romero; ruled consensual, Higuera perjured; punishment left to the Superioridad

author: fiscal José Joaquín de la Torre
summary: Diligencias vs. José Chacuales, neophyte, for mistreating his wife Dionisia; referred to the Jefe Superior Político

author: fiscal Domingo Carrillo
summary: Causa vs. Serbul[?], mission vecino, on suspicion of killing Eleuterio; found little culpable but, on suspicion, fiscal asks 6 months presidio

author: fiscal Alf. José Joaquín Maitorena
summary: Proceso for desertion vs. Mazatlán soldiers Tomás Herrera, Tiburcio Valenciana, Clemente Ríos, Juan José Navarro, Manuel Osuna (first 3 scaling the wall) + Juan José Nieblas & Juan José Bañuelas (abandoning the San Buenaventura escort guard). Sentences incl. running the gauntlet ('seis carreras de baquetas por 200 hombres') + grillete/presidio; mitigated to 6 yrs (SF) / 4 yrs (Monterey) / 4 mo (Sta Bárbara)

author: Luis A. Argüello (fiscal Capt. de la Guerra)
summary: Diligencias on the robbery by the crew of the American schooner *Águila* of the brig *Las dos hermanas* (formerly the *Cossack*) at the Santa Bárbara anchorage (pretext: the brig 'poorly manned'); the *Águila* seized, inventoried, sold — net $2,980 to the Government (receipt of Anastasio Carrillo, 3 Nov 1823)

author: fiscal José Joaquín Maitorena
summary: Sumaria vs. invalid Ignacio Valenzuela for mistreating his wife María Felipa Fernández (their daughter testifies he's a drunk who tried to kill her mother with sword & pistol); fiscal favors separation

author: Santiago Argüello (Habilitado)
summary: Net product of *propios y arbitrios* duties collected in the year: $145

author: José Mª Iglesias de Orduña
summary: Circular to pursue María de la Luz Rojas for poisoning Capt. Luis Romero (17 Oct 1827); presidio commanders note receipt

author: escribano José Joaquín de la Torre
summary: Remits to defender Tte. José Mª Estudillo the diligencias vs. 4 soldiers for robbing the troop's food warehouse + Subtte. José Ramírez; defender asks release (the dark damp cell enough). — 'Fin del tomo' (Tomo LIV ends n119).

author: Ignacio Vallejo (sgt., commissioned by Gov. Luis A. Argüello)
summary: Commissioned to investigate possible *sublevación* in the San Luis, San Miguel, San Antonio & Soledad missions during the 1824 Chumash Revolt; Fr. Luis Martínez & the escort cabo assure him no sign of disloyalty — the San Luis Indians were solicited by the 'Purísimeños' [the Purísima rebels] but refused. Embeds Vallejo's address to the neophytes (praises their steadfastness, threatens 'providencias con fuego y sangre' if they revolt) + his confiscation of all Indian weapons and order that no Indian keep arms at home

author: José Estrada (Habilitado)
summary: Monterey presidial company haber vencido (2nd sem 1820–Dec 1821): 1 tte, 1 alf, 1 cirujano, 2 sgts, 10 cabos, 1 cadete, 73 soldados, 2 sangradores; +inválidos 2 sgts/8 cabos/20 sold. Total $34,307

author: José Estrada
summary: Monterey company: 3 officers, 2 sgts, 11 cabos, 1 cadete, 74 soldados, 1 sangrador + invalids. Total $23,149

author: José Estrada
summary: Monterey company: tte, alf, cirujano, 2 sgts, 11 cabos, 1 cadete, 76 soldados, 1 sangrador, 1 carpintero; +inv 2 sgts/7 cabos/17 sold. Total $22,564

author: José Estrada
summary: Monterey company: 1 tte, 1 alf, 1 cirujano, 2 sgts, 13 cabos, 1 cadete = Mariano G. Vallejo, 64 soldados; +inv. Total $20,687

author: José Estrada
summary: Monterey company $17,809: ttes José Mª Estudillo, José Estrada; alf Rodrigo del Pliego; cadete Mariano G. Vallejo; sgts Ignacio Vallejo & Dolores Pico; 10 cabos, 55 soldados; 27 invalids

author: José Estrada
summary: Monterey company $16,317: tte José Estrada; alf Rodrigo del Pliego; cadete Mariano G. Vallejo; 2 sgts, 10 cabos, 37 soldados (invalids/discounts omitted)

author: José Estrada
summary: Monterey company: 1 tte, 1 alf, 1 cadete, 3 sgts, 9 cabos, 70 soldados. Total $16,624

author: Francisco Ávila
summary: Account to Gov. Solá of the grape tithes (diezmos de uva): total 1,300 cuartillos; his share 650, the tithe fund 650 (to comisionado Sgt. Anastasio Carrillo)

author: Gov. Solá
summary: *Reconocimiento de doncella* after Sta Bárbara soldier Luis Palomares publicly claimed carnal relations with Clementina Arellanes; midwife Bernardina Álvarez examined her and declared under oath (before the Gov., Capt. de la Guerra, Tte Estudillo, Paulino Viteri) she was a virgin

author: Fr. José Mª de Zalvidea & Fr. Joaquín Pascual Nuez
summary: Ministers' note (tied to the grape-tithe account, p.47)

author: Anastasio Carrillo (sgt.)
summary: Comisionado of the pueblo Feb–Feb this year; the prior 10 years Sgt. Guillermo Cota was comisionado

author: José Estrada (Habilitado)
summary: Import/export duties paid by ships, 1821: Russian brig 'Cutusoff' [Kutúzov] $2323 + $1842; 'Inferior' (San Martín) $905; 'Sn Francisco' $410; 'Europa' $142; 'Hermanos' $1910; 'Reina de los Ángeles' (J.L. Bandini) $2281. Total $9,813

author: José Estrada
themes: trade; customs; russia; grain; khlebnikov; monterey
summary: Customs duties paid by the Russian brig 'Buldacoff,' supercargo Carlos Khlebnikov, for grain & goods extracted: Monterey $1074.5 + Santa Cruz Bay $1248.3 = $2,323 [direct Russian grain-purchase record]

author: Gov. Solá
summary: Give de baja in the Sta Bárbara company Domingo Carrillo, named Cadete of San Diego, from 1 April

author: Capt. José de la Guerra (Comandante)
summary: Santa Bárbara presidial company roster: Capt. de la Guerra; Tte Gabriel Moraga; Alf. José Joaquín Maitorena; sgts Carlos & Anastasio Carrillo, Dámaso Rodríguez; 6 cabos; cadete José Antonio de la Guerra; 56 soldados = 69 total + 31 invalids

author: Matías Guerrero
summary: Maestranza de artillería accounts: expenses to date $3,165.2

author: José Estrada (Habilitado)
themes: trade; customs; russia; britain; americans; ships; monterey
summary: Customs duties by foreign ships, 1822: Eng. 'Col. Young' $2968+$926; Russian 'Volga' $347; Eng. 'Snipe' $854; 'Lady Blackwood' $110; Amer. 'Sachem' $456; 'Panther' $395; Eng. 'John Begg' $177; 'Cleopatra' $178; 'Sir Francis Baring' $45; Amer. 'Eagle' $72; Russian 'Buldacoff' $1451. Total $9,089

author: [Alexander] Rotchev (cmdr. of the *Baikal* / Fort Ross)
summary: The RAC brig *Baikal* left Sitka with goods for Ross but bad weather forced it into SF without unloading at Ross the cargo 'que éste necesita con grande urgencia'; asks leave for a Ross launch to load the goods at SF; Rotchev styles himself Lieutenant of the Russian Imperial Navy & commander of the *Baikal* — [= part of the Mathes 1839–40 Russian-trade set → confirms this volume = Benicia Military 55]

author: Francisco Guerrero (SF frontier customs)
summary: On the Russian frigate 'Elena' [Helena]: certifies no effects found aboard; measured in the port captain's absence

author: Francisco Guerrero
themes: russia; fort-ross; customs; tonnage; ships; san-francisco
summary: Tonnage duty on the Russian frigate 'Elena': 309½ tons @ 12 r. = $464.4, less 25% = $348.4; less $96 the port captain charged to the *Baikal* (Rotchev) → net $252

author: Francisco Guerrero
summary: Arrival of the Russian frigate 'Nicolás' [Nikolai] from Sitka carrying Gov. of Russian America Ivan Kupreanov, 6 officers, 61 men (war-grade crew/armament; the ship itself not a warship). Kupreanov comes to discuss the last Anglo-American ship at Bodega + settle matters with the Governor of Ross, then on to Valparaíso; tonnage-charge query; told to go to Monterey, he awaited Gen. Mariano G. Vallejo or his reply

author: Guerrero
summary: American brig (Capt. Thomas Duncan, supercargo Pedrorena) from Oahu; put in for water; tonnage $243

author: Guerrero
summary: Tonnage-duty dispute over the Russian 'Nicolás': the captain refuses, alleging the ship is on government business (Russian-colonies vs. Mexico differences over Cal., to be settled at St. Petersburg); Pedro Kostromitinov furnished a certificate that she owes no tonnage

author: Guerrero
summary: Acks the 22 May order to permit NO commerce to the ship consigned to the Governor of the Ross & Bodega establishment; will comply

author: Guerrero
summary: The schooner Clementina arrived from Monterey with passengers including Sr. [John] Sutter

author: Guerrero
themes: britain; hbc; foreigners; mofras; ships; san-francisco
summary: Arrival of the HBC frigate *Cowlitz* (bound Sandwich Is. w/ salmon & timber); carries the HBC Governor of Columbia + the general govt of the [HBC] American establishments + Mr. Rae's family; granted 48 hrs then liable for tonnage; passengers Duflot de Mofras & Mr. Hale transferred to the *Bolívar*

author: Prado Mesa (mil. cmdt./port captain)
summary: Measured the RAC brig *Constantine* at 220½ tons

author: William Brotchie (capt., bark *Cowlitz*)
themes: britain; hbc; foreigners; mofras; simpson; ships
summary: *Cowlitz* passenger list (23 crew, 312 tons): Sir George Simpson (HBC gov.), John McLoughlin, John & Dr. Rowand (→ Oahu); Mr. Hale & Monsieur de Mofras (→ Monterey); Mrs. Rae, McGillivray, George Simpson, La Forte, Narcisa Bastía, María McKay (servants → SF)

author: Sgt. Luis Peralta (comisionado)
summary: Handover of the San José pueblo archive & stores (arms, munitions, grillos/grilletes/cepos, chapel ornaments & sacred vessels) to alcalde Juan Alviso[?]

author: José Joaquín Maitorena (Habilitado)
summary: Sta Bárbara company account: mission supplies $1480; effects paid by ships 'Sachem,' 'Tomasa,' brig 'Nilo,' goleta 'Sta Magdalena/Victoria,' whaler 'Don'; cargo $3324 / data $8524; company owed $5199

author: José Mª Estudillo (Habilitado)
summary: SD company (last 5 mo $6728): Capt. Francisco Mª Ruiz, Tte José Mª Estudillo, Alf. Gervasio Argüello (absent Guadalajara), 5 sgts, 3 cabos, cadete Domingo Carrillo, 54 soldados; +invalids (Tte Juan Marine, 3 sgts, 11 sold.)

author: Domingo Carrillo (Comisario Subalterno/Habilitado)
summary: (brief — Carrillo's habilitación)

author: Fernando de la Toba (Alf./Comandante)
summary: Loreto piquete of the Mazatlán squadron: 5 dragoons (was 8 until 3 passed to Alta Cal. w/ the Comdt-Gen. [Echeandía] 4 Oct); revistas before Comisario Luis de Cuevas & Comdt. José Mª Padrés (Tte de Ingenieros); Aug/Sep revistas signed by Romualdo Pacheco

author: Fernando de la Toba
summary: Loreto piquete 1826: 5 dragoons, 8 horses (1 Mulegé, 1 Borja, rest Loreto); revistas before Luis de Cuevas / José Mª Padrés

author: Domingo Carrillo
summary: Asks whether he holds any commission re the quantities/supplies to the officers & Comdt-Gen. at this port

author: Domingo Carrillo (Comisario Subalterno/Habilitado)
summary: SD customs: Juan Mancisidor paid $406 (effects on the frigate 'Sachem'); tonnage duty on the Russian brig 'Vaycal' [*Baikal*] $510; 65 confiscated otter skins auctioned $991. Total $2207

author: Ignacio Martínez (Comandante)
summary: SF company roster: Capt. Luis A. Argüello (acting Gov./Comdt-Gen. at SD), Tte Martínez, Alf. Santiago Argüello (at SD), sgt Luis Peralta + José Sánchez (alf. grade), Francisco Soto (cabo, sgt grade), 5 cabos, cadete Raymundo Estrada, 48 soldados (11 plazas short); +9 invalids

author: José Joaquín Maitorena
summary: Sta Bárbara company: Capt. José de la Guerra, Tte vacant, Alf. Maitorena, sgts Anastasio Carrillo & Dámaso Rodríguez, 6 cabos, cadete Francisco Tapia, 51 soldados = 62 total + 26 invalids; month's pay $1420

author: Ignacio Martínez (Comandante)
summary: SF company: Capt. Luis A. Argüello (absent Monterey), Tte Martínez, Alf. Santiago Argüello (absent SD), José Sánchez (sgt/alf grade), Francisco Soto (sgt), 7 cabos, cadete Raymundo Estrada, 57 soldados = 69 total; revistas before Comisario José Raymundo Estrada

author: José Estrada
themes: military; service-record; estrada; bouchard; chumash-revolt; asia
summary: Hoja de servicios: age 44, b. Loreto, noble, married; entered service 1 Oct 1797, total 28 yr 3 mo. Campaigns: the Nov 1818 Bouchard defense of Monterey; promoted Tte, then led 'la reducción de los rebeldes de la Misión de San Luis y la Purísima' [1824 Chumash Revolt] as jefe; Santa Catarina frontier (Río Colorado); 1822 independence oath; 1825 commissioned to negotiate the capitulation of the Spanish warships *Asia* & *Constante* to the Mexican republic

author: Santiago Argüello (Habilitado)
summary: SD company credits/debits: total owed by Capt. Ruiz/sgts/cabos/soldiers $46,182; less Estudillo $3206 + cadete D. Carrillo $205; net $42,771 owed to the troops [pay arrears]

author: Subtte Mariano González
summary: Artillery detachment roster: Subtte González; sgts José Aguilar, José Medrano; cabos Patricio Ríos, Bernardo Quirós, Mariano Segura, F. Bermúdez, Octaviano Gutiérrez, Casimiro Piña; 36 soldados + Lázaro Piña; distributed across the 4 Alta Cal. presidios (cert. José Mª Herrera, Comisario of both Californias); budget ~$530/mo

author: fiscal José Joaquín Maitorena
summary: The 1824 Chumash Revolt at La Purísima: investigation of what the rebel Indians robbed, took & burned from the mission escort (cabo + soldiers + their families) when they rose on 24 Feb 1824; the escort lost everything; the rebels held soldiers & families captive in the 'nueva ranchería de sublevados'; the alcaldes' claim that all was returned is false. Goal: make the Mission compensate the troops

author: José Ramírez (Juez Comisionado)
summary: Robbery of William (Guillermo) Hartnell's warehouse (10 Aug 1825); thieves Tomás del Castillo & Domingo Estrada (not alone); cadete Mariano G. Vallejo served as escribano; cabo Anastasio Cortés arrested

author: José Manuel Ruiz (Comandante)
summary: Loreto marine department roster: 1 carpintero, 1 herrero, 1 guardián, 1 marinero, 2 grumetes, 1 paje = 7 men

author: José Estrada (Habilitado)
summary: Monterey company: 1 tte, 1 alf, 2 sgts, 10 cabos, 1 cadete, 57 soldados

author: José Estrada (Comandante)
summary: Monterey company: Tte Estrada, Alf. Rodrigo del Pliego, cirujano vacant, 2 sgts, 10 cabos, 1 cadete, 57 soldados = 72 total

author: Guillermo Cota
summary: Artillery militia + invalids: SD milicianos 11; Sta Bárbara invalids 13; SD invalids 8 = 32

author: Antonio Valle (Comandante)
summary: San Blas veteran infantry company: Tte Valle, Alf. Francisco de Haro, 2 sgts, 1 tambor, 4 cabos, 31 soldados = 41 total; distributed Monterey & SF

author: Pablo de la Portilla
summary: Mazatlán cavalry squadron: Capt. Portilla; Ttes Narciso Fabregat & Juan Mª Ibarra; Alf. Ignacio Delgado; 5 sgts, 2 tambores, 6 cabos, 73 soldados = 90 total

author: José Joaquín Buelna (encargado de Justicia)
summary: (brief — Branciforte justice)

author: Flaminio Agazini[?]
summary: List of convicts transported to the California presidios on the brig *Morelos* ('tienen condena'): Joaquín Solís [future leader of the 1829 revolt], José Mª Gallegos, Antonio Ávila, Fernando Cortés, Teodoro González, Francisco Badillo, José Mª Sánchez, Nicolás Alcoxer, Andrés León, Santiago Cárdenas, Pablo Bárcena, Ignacio García, Luis Beltrán, Inés Pilame, Eulogio Guzmán, Ignacio Velasco, Pedro de Salas

author: Marqués de Vivanco (Jefe de E.M.)
summary: On Tte José Mariano Estrada's requested retirement: refer his hoja, keep him in the veterano class

author: Francisco Mª Ruiz (Comandante)
summary: Cadete Domingo Carrillo's exploration of the Laguna de Buenavista (10 soldiers, on foot/swimming/rafts): mapped the lake's unknown interior + recovered firearms & edged weapons, some animals & neophytes [the 1824 Chumash Revolt fugitives' refuge]

author: Marqués de Vivanco (Jefe de E.M.)
summary: War-Ministry bundle: officers in higher posts draw only their class pay (Apr 20); Francisco Cota service record (Jul 1); a cabo's retirement cédula (Jun 9); denial of José Mª Ramírez's request to revalidate his 1823 Tte/Capt. grade (Mar 29); Gen. José Joaquín Herrera named Director of the Cuerpo Nacional de Artillería

author: José Mª Narváez (Comdt. de Marina)
summary: Acks a copy of the capitulation of the Navío *Asia* made at Monterey

author: José Ramírez
summary: Artillery militia company account: cabos + 7 artilleros; movements — transferred to cavalry (SF 11, Monterey 2, SD 4 = 17), 11 dead at various points, 2 exiled to México

author: José Estrada (Tte)
summary: Monterey company: Tte Estudillo (commissioned) & Estrada, cirujano Manuel Toca, sgts Ignacio Vallejo & José Dolores Pico, 10 cabos, cadete Mariano G. Vallejo, 59 soldados = 75 total + 26 invalids; year-end active ~70

author: José Ramírez
summary: Artillery troop arrears (veteran + militia) garrisoning Alta Cal. 1 Jan 1822 – 31 Dec 1825: $50,148

author: Santiago Argüello (Habilitado)
summary: SD habilitación debts to company members & vecinos who loaned cash: $32,743

author: Antonio Suñol (Administrador de la Estafeta)
summary: Net San José post-office product remitted to Monterey: $9

author: Domingo Carrillo
summary: Fiscal crisis: Comisario Herrera's cash is exhausted — only enough for the Monterey & Sta Bárbara companies, nothing for the territory's artillery piquete; orders the artillery fed the cavalry's ration, the increase sought from the San Diego missions

author: Ignacio Martínez
summary: SF company's armament mostly unusable from age & lack of an armero; the troop makes frequent salidas & needs serviceable arms; asks a share of the arms brought from México on the brig *Morelos*

author: Ignacio Martínez (Comandante)
summary: SF celebrates the capitulation of the Castillo de San Juan de Ulúa [the last Spanish foothold in Mexico]: flag raised w/ 5-gun salute, troops parade, the President's letter + the Capitulación read aloud, three vivas + artillery fire

author: Ignacio Martínez
summary: Re the San Blas infantry piquete: recommends disbanding it (the presidial company has ample force; the infantry set a bad example)

author: Ignacio Martínez (Comandante)
summary: Arrival of a Hawaiian-flagged brig-schooner (Capt. Guillermo Dare/Dana?), 9 crew + 40 Sandwich-Islanders hunting sea-otter/seal; put in for meat/salt/wood/water; mixed English/Hawaiian flag (the Hawaiian king's 'cuchillas celestes')

author: Capt. de la Guerra
summary: Per the Comdt-Gen.'s provisional rule, foreign ships may touch only at Sta Cruz, San Luis Obispo, Cosa[?], Refugio & San Juan Capistrano, solely to trade the missions' goods

author: Capt. Luis A. Argüello
summary: SF celebration of the San Juan de Ulúa capitulation + independence: Padre Ramón Abella declined to take part; reduced to artillery salvos (13 shots at sunrise); asked $400 for the troops' refreshments, no prompt reply

author: José Mª Estudillo
summary: Jurisdictional dispute over the arms: as plaza commander, Estudillo demands the muskets, carbines, swords & cartridges in the casemate be placed at his disposition (citing the Ordenanzas) for land defense vs. the surrounding gentiles, the artillery retaining only the guns for sea defense

author: Joaquín Piña et al.
summary: Artillery troops' back-pay grievance: owed *alcances*; promised (when the brig *Morelos* came) 2/3 in effects + 1/3 cash — unfulfilled; paid only Aug–Dec last year, nothing of the arrears, since Jan 'con la ración nada más' (reiterated 7 Jun, no resolution)

author: Director of Artillery
summary: Requests a topographic plan of California's artillery/fortified points + an account of their utility or uselessness ('estos antemurales de la defensa de la República')

author: José Figueroa (Comdt-Gen. of Sonora)
summary: On Colorado River communication routes (the Romualdo Pacheco / Tte Col. José Romero road expeditions + the Yuma route via San Diego); the road needs the Indians gratified to win them over; Arizpe→la Concepción [Colorado] ~200 leagues, →San Diego ~80

author: José Figueroa
summary: On the Yaqui rebellion in Sonora: the 'imprevista sublevación de los Yaquis' forced him back by forced marches; he confronted & subdued the rebels (who returned much of what they'd robbed), stationed a detachment, is pursuing the cabecillas; fears a new rising; Apaches also raiding

author: Echeandía
summary: Re the infantry desertion counter-order (cf. Doc 136): the SF cmdt. sent an express after Sgt. Pedro del Castillo to recall the troop; Echeandía's counter-order; ordered Monterey to send the detached infantry back to SF

author: Echeandía
summary: The pueblo of San José Guadalupe belongs to the SF jurisdiction

author: José Figueroa
summary: Vicente Gómez's overland mission (Cal.→Sonora) arrived destitute & dismounted, threatened with death by the Yumas; to return the same road or embark at Guaymas

author: José Figueroa
summary: Forwards an *instancia* of a San Juan Capistrano mission Indian; recommends him, criticizing 'todo el rigor de esos religiosos' [the friars' harshness]

author: Echeandía
summary: The villa de Branciforte belongs to the SF jurisdiction

author: Echeandía
summary: 1826 congressional elections: secondary electors to vote for diputados on the 24th; but SF hasn't held its primary/municipal juntas, so its elector should wait for Echeandía's notice

author: Echeandía
summary: Branciforte electors: its vecinos belong to SF but are too few to alter the municipal-elector count

author: Echeandía
summary: Distributes 2 copies of the Acta Constitutiva + 1 of the Constitución de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos (1824) to circulate via the escort cabos & mission padres

author: Echeandía
summary: Early emancipation: forwards an Indian's petition to be exempted from mission status (neófia) — orders that if the man is 'capaz de sostenerse con su mujer é hijos fuera de la neófia,' he be given the document accrediting his *desfiliación*

author: Secretarios del Congreso del Estado
summary: Notice that the Congress opened its 2nd ordinary sessions, with 'las más sinceras protestas de Unión'

author: Manuel Jimeno Casarín (Comisario Subalterno interino)
summary: (brief note)

author: Santiago Argüello (Admin. de Correos)
summary: SD post-office account 1824: product $78.4, net $68.2

author: José Estrada (Administrador)
summary: Monterey post-office net products 1820–24 (1820 $28.6.6 … 1824 $73.4 = $256.4.3)

author: [Luis] Argüello
summary: Reprimands the alcalde for prosecuting Blas Obrero (who provoked Gregoria, wife of José Tomás Patrón, into 'palabras soeces'); orders her arrest & a sumaria; alcalde Ignacio Pacheco commissioned regidor Bartolo Bojórques

author: alcalde Ignacio Pacheco (comisión to sec. Matías Guerrero)
place: Pueblo de San José / Sta Cruz
themes: calumny; missions; sexual; santa-cruz; branciforte; indigenous
summary: Calumny case against a missionary: José Mª Pérez (Branciforte) accused of calumniating Padre Fr. Luis Taboada (Mission Santa Cruz); testimony (son-in-law Gervasio Soto, Margarita Rodríguez, Bruno Casal) circles a scandalous rumor — whether the Padre said 'the Indians fornicate with the village women' or that the village 'alcahuetea' Indian women for the friars' service. The Padre, confronted, said he had no complaint; the accused held in prison, case passed to the alcalde

author: Gob. [Luis] Argüello
summary: Vallejo ordered to investigate the wounding of vecino José Antonio Castro at Mission San Juan Bautista (9 Aug — a sable cut to the left arm by Buenaventura Zúñiga 'Quinte' over a gambling quarrel: a won horse + a disputed card-deck payment); witnesses Ignacio Vallejo, Felipe García; cirujano Manuel Toca (wound not mortal); confession before alcalde Ignacio Pacheco — Zúñiga pleads self-defense

author: tribunal: José de la Guerra, José Estrada, Subtte Francisco de Haro (under Gov. Luis A. Argüello)
themes: chumash-revolt; indigenous; missions; la-purisima; santa-ines; death-penalty; military
summary: THE 1824 CHUMASH REVOLT TRIAL & SENTENCES: depositions of captured rebels of Missions La Purísima & Santa Inés, then the *fallo* — 7 La Purísima neophytes (Baltasar, Pacífico, Esteban, Cines, Antonino, Felipe, José Andrés) sentenced to death (último suplicio) as accomplices in the murder of José Dolores Sepúlveda + 3 companions (night of 21 Feb 1824); Mariano, Pacomio, Benito & Bernabé to 10 yrs presidio + exile as 'agentes principales de la revolución' (cf. Doc 46); 7 more to 8 yrs; 5 acquitted. (Antonino, in the chapel before execution, named Martiniano & Camilo as further accomplices.)

author: cadete Domingo Carrillo (Juez), Mariano G. Vallejo (escribano)
themes: criminal; theft; otter; drunkenness; monterey; vallejo
summary: Criminal case vs. miliciano Gracia Lario[?] for stealing an otter skin while drunk; fiscal: 2 yrs public works w/ grillete (drunkenness no excuse; 'satisfacción á los extrangeros presentes')

author: fiscal Ignacio Martínez
themes: criminal; homicide; femicide; sexual; indigenous; mission-san-jose
summary: Femicide at Mission San José: neophyte José Antonio killed the Christian India Valencia (~20, of ranchería Suchun) on 20 Feb 1824 — bludgeoned with stones & knifed (neck & head) after she refused his sexual advances; took refuge in the church; cabo Salvio Pacheco removed him under caución juratoria; case to defender Joaquín de la Torre

author: Ignacio Martínez (Administrador)
summary: SF liquor-tax (propios y arbitrios) on aguardiente since 1821: 1821 $84, 1822 $37.50, 1823 $78, 1824 $59 = $178.50; tax $4/barrel to Apr 1823, then $8/barrel

author: Miguel González
summary: Monterey fort decay: the 24-pounder cannon are being eaten by rust, the casemate & castillo threaten ruin, the munitions are at risk, and the artillery barracks is 'muy bien un sótano, el peor del Presidio'

author: Domingo Carrillo
summary: SD company credits/debits: credits $5,514, debits $333

author: José Antonio Carrillo (fiscal: alcalde Vicente Sánchez of LA)
summary: Sedition case vs. Buenaventura, a San Luis Rey neophyte, for outbursts against the government — 'no había Gobierno Mexicano… no había Nación… los había de matar á todos [los de razón]… el General era un Inglés'; corroborated; no sentence

author: Rodrigo del Pliego (Ayudante de la Plaza)
summary: Sumaria vs. infantry soldier Raimundo González for losing the watchword, getting drunk & abandoning the guard; elevated to proceso; no sentence

author: alcalde Vicente Sánchez (fiscal); escribano José Palomares
summary: Case vs. primer Regidor José Mª Aguilar for saying 'breaking the 6th commandment was no sin'; nothing particular found

author: Argüello
summary: Rancho dispute: Mariano Castro (rancho de las Ánimas) vs. Ignacio Ortega (rancho de San Ysidro) — Ortega's vecinos accused of stealing & killing Castro's cattle

author: Subtte Juan José Rocha (fiscal)
summary: Sumaria vs. soldier Andrés Verdugo for abandoning his caballada-guard post; fiscal: release

author: alcalde Vicente Sánchez (fiscal)
summary: Sumaria vs. Santiago Linares (suspected of robbing Juan Ballesteros's house); 2 months prison + restitution

author: alcalde Vicente Sánchez (fiscal)
summary: Sumaria vs. Juan Mª Duarte — a serial night-prowler who broke into houses to attempt to rape women (Ana Pinto & others); 2 years presidio

author: Domingo Carrillo
summary: SD treasury corte de caja (Nov 1825–): cargo $14,115, data $10,654, balance $3,461

author: José Estrada
summary: 74 men under Estrada went on the La Purísima expedition [the 1824 Chumash Revolt suppression]; paid $2,695 by order of 31 Jul 1824

author: José Joaquín Maitorena
summary: Sta Bárbara habilitación demostración (May 1–): cargo $3773, data $12,910, company owed $9,137

author: Maitorena (Habilitado)
summary: Sta Bárbara habilitación (Jan 1–): cargo $4705, data $11,312, company owed $6,611

author: Maitorena
summary: Sta Bárbara company earned (Jan 1–): $6,025. — 'Fin del tomo nº 58' (856 pp.).

author: José Joaquín Maitorena (Habilitado)
summary: Sta Bárbara company account (detailed = Doc 176): mission receipts (La Purísima 267, Sta Inés 477, Sta Bárbara 166, S. Buenaventura 279, San Fernando Rey 391 = 1580); ship duties — goleta *Spray* 73, fragata *Sachem* 414, brig *Eliza* 1079, *Pizarro* 508 = 2073; cargo $3773 / data $12,910; owed $9137

author: Maitorena
summary: Post account: only 'gastos de oficina justos y necesarios' in the data

author: Maitorena
summary: Will pay the troops 3 days' double pay for 'la plausible noticia de la rendición del castillo de San Juan de Ulúa'

author: Maitorena
summary: Will comply with the 22 Sept order: no sales of any effects to the [English] frigate — only purchases from her, with cash, for this country's goods

author: alcalde Vicente Sánchez (fiscal)
summary: Causa vs. neophyte Antonio Nicolás (San Diego mission) — horse/cattle theft + chronic desertion; Echeandía: 1 year public works

author: Echeandía (fiscal Vicente Sánchez)
summary: Causa vs. Bernardino (neophyte of Sta Catalina, Baja Cal) for robbing Rosalía Ochoa's clothing; restitution by domestic service to her, else 6 mo public works

author: fiscal Santiago Argüello; escribano Pío Pico
themes: criminal; homicide; military; san-diego; pio-pico; zamorano
summary: Homicide: cavalry soldier Víctor Linares killed vecino Juan Germán (night of 28 Apr); defender Tte Agustín Zamorano argued self-defense (Linares on sentry, Germán attacked him armed). Court-martial (Rocha, Valle, Ibarra, Portilla, Romualdo Pacheco + Echeandía) unanimously ACQUITTED him, honor & promotions intact, returned to service (14 Jul)

author: alcalde Vicente Sánchez
summary: Causa vs. Eugenio Merillo for stealing & butchering a head of cattle from Antonio Machado; penalty commuted to $16 (¼ to propios)

author: fiscal Vicente Sánchez
summary: Sumaria vs. Rosa Garibay for drunkenness (repeat offender, once escaped prison); case suspended

author: fiscal Vicente Sánchez
summary: Sumaria vs. Juan José Serrano for disobeying the order to stand the anti-contraband guard at San Pedro beach; Echeandía: release

author: fiscal Vicente Villa
summary: Causa vs. the convict Juan Mª Duarte (cf. Doc 173) — scandals + grabbing a dagger against his guard; Echeandía: released, time served

author: Echeandía (fiscal Vicente Villa)
summary: Case vs. neophyte Macario Rafael (San Gabriel) for stabbing another Indian (Oct 1826); released, not found guilty

author: Miguel González
summary: The alcalde & Indians of Mission San Antonio accuse mayordomo Norberto of extorting 'gratificaciones caprichosas' + threatening to calumniate them to the friars (who over-credit him); seek his dismissal. Pliego found nothing against him

author: José Mª Estudillo (Comandante)
summary: Monterey garrison strength: Artillery 20 (1 tambor, 4 cabos, 15 sold.); Infantry 19; Cavalry 71 (2 sgts, 10 cabos, cadete, cirujano, 55 sold.)

author: Fernando de la Toba (Comandante)
summary: Loreto presidial company: 2 officers (de la Toba present, Mata at SD), 4 cabos, 1 cadete, 14 dragoons = 21 (most absent at missions); revistas before Luis de Cuevas / José Mª Padrés

author: Sgt. del Castillo (Comandante)
summary: SF San Blas infantry detachment: Sgt. 2° Castillo, 2 cabos, 12 soldados = 15

author: Rodrigo del Pliego (Comandante de la plaza)
summary: San Blas veteran infantry company: 3 sgts, 1 tambor, 5 cabos, 31 soldados = 39; revista before Comisario José Mª Herrera

author: alcalde Gerónimo (sec. Simón Cota)
summary: Mission election: the Soledad mission Indians, after Mass, gathered 11 electors (per the Comandante's order) & chose Juan de Dios to send to Monterey as their representative; signed with crosses ('no sabemos escribir')

author: Fr. Pedro Cabot (alcalde Damián Zambrano)
summary: Mission election credential: Mission San Antonio's ayuntamiento (alcalde Zambrano, regidores Porfiriano Fernández & Casiano de la Cruz) names Eugenio [Nactore?] Elector de Partido to help choose the Elector de Provincia & Diputados under the federal Constitution

author: Rodrigo del Pliego
summary: San Blas infantry company (Alta Cal.): 3 sgts, 1 tambor, 3 cabos, 31 soldados; owed 5 months' pay $2676

author: fiscal José Joaquín Maitorena
summary: Causa vs. neophyte Valerio et al. (theft at Mission Santa Bárbara; Valerio implicated in the gentiles' killing of accomplice Hilario) — Valerio sentenced 10 years at Loreto + perpetual exile from the Province

author: José Castro (Alcalde Constitucional)
summary: (brief note)

author: Fr. Felipe Arroyo de la Cuesta
summary: Re thefts from Mission San Juan Bautista by Rafael Romero (~$2000); the friar won't intervene in a capital sentence but warns Romero is 'muy malo' & shouldn't be freed

author: José de la Guerra (Comandante)
summary: Sta Bárbara company roster: Capt. de la Guerra, Tte vacant, Alf. Maitorena, 3 sgts (1 vacant), 55 soldados = 65 total + 26 invalids; annual pay $19,214; note: Hartnell delivered $8000 to Sta Bárbara for 1825 arrears

author: Domingo Carrillo
summary: Re the goleta *Rover* — a disposition to ship ¾ of the effects to SD was not carried out, contravening the Comandante General's order

author: José Mª Estudillo
summary: Can't stop the *Rover*'s departure, having asked Capt. [John] Cooper for a note of her goods & prices

summary: SF anchorage fees from 9 ships ($10 each = $90): goleta *Soberana Guelman*, brig *Rama de Oliva*, whalers *Cyrus*, *Mª Teresa*, *Franklin*, *Peruviana*, *Paragon*, *Carlos*, *Tritón*

author: Ignacio Martínez

author: Martínez
summary: Duties on the American goleta *Washington* (Capt. Thompson, 52 tons, cargo of chile; 'según dice el Gral. M.G. Vallejo') = $230

author: Martínez

author: Martínez
summary: Duties on the American frigate *Sachem* = $226

summary: SF infantry piquete supply (Jan–Oct): $1019

author: Martínez
summary: SF cavalry pay (5 mo): cargo $4148, data $6423, owed $2275

summary: Account to the commander of the Russian Imperial corvette *Descubierta* (a Russian naval visit, 1821): total $268

author: Santiago Argüello
summary: Duties on Fernando Deppe[?] for goods from Loreto & Guadalajara: $21.2.5. — 'Fin del tomo' (604 pp.).

author: regidor Vicente Villa (fiscal); escribano Juan Bautista Alvarado
summary: Destitution case vs. LA secretary Francisco Morales (incompetence, lost archive papers/secrets)

author: Ignacio Martínez
summary: Case vs. Christian Indians Apolonio, Sebastián & Hilario — cimarrones, cattle-killers & horse-thieves ('ya son viejos en el oficio de matar y robar ganado')

author: Echeandía
summary: Desertion case: soldier Lucio Merillo + convict Antonio García fled sentry duty; Merillo released, García held at SD

author: alcalde José [Fabricio] Castro (fiscal)

summary: Inquest on the body of Rafael Arrobla[?]; nothing determined

author: fiscal Rodrigo del Pliego
summary: Sumaria vs. company members Antonio Riguera, José Curibel & cabo José Mª Villavicencio (inducement crime); unfinished

author: fiscal R. del Pliego
summary: Criminal vs. Felipe Figueroa for straying off-route (en route to La Purísima) to the widow Petra Sandes's house; no sentence

author: fiscal R. del Pliego

summary: SF tobacco-revenue account 1827: cargo $726.4, net product $690.1

summary: Artillery company salary scale: Capitán $71-4-2; sgt 2° $18-5-1; tambor $15-6-1; 6 cabos $15-6; 26 artilleros $14-4-6 each; monthly total incl. premios $625

author: Echeandía
summary: Re the artillery command: the Territory ('considered ultramarine') has only one artillery officer, so the plaza's Ayudante should instruct any process against the artillery troop. (borrador)

author: fiscal Romualdo Pacheco
summary: Proceso vs. cabo Bernardo Curiel for slapping his sergeant (after the sergeant's insults & threats with a stick); punish per Ordenanza

author: fiscal Agustín V. Zamorano
themes: fiscal; corruption; forgery; herrera; zamorano; vallejo; brooklyn; gale
summary: Investigation of Comisario [José Mª] Herrera for misappropriation of public funds + forgery of signatures. Witnesses José de la Guerra, Ignacio Maitorena, Juan Bandini, Romualdo Pacheco, Mariano G. Vallejo, Rodrigo del Pliego. Herrera allegedly mismanaged ~$2,000 + México funds, didn't pay duties on goods sold to an American brig (~$600 lost). The forgery scheme (by Luis Bringas) faked the signature of Capt. William Gale (American frigate *Brooklyn*) on a $2,000 libranza (a Guaymas draft); Gale testified it was false ('he signs *Gale* not *Galle*'). Escribiente Romualdo Pacheco

author: Jacinto Lorenzana
summary: Sta Bárbara propios y arbitrios 1827–28: 1827 $64, total w/ 1828 $468-7-5. — 'Fin del tomo 61' (881 pp.).

summary: Monterey company pay (Feb): Tte $43, Alf $32, 2 sgts $42, 10 cabos $182, 57 soldados $1020; + corte de caja

author: Santiago Argüello (Habilitado)

author: Ignacio Martínez (juez fiscal); later fiscal José Mª Padrés; comte Manuel Victoria
themes: criminal; homicide; rape; forensic-medicine; wrongful-execution; richardson; cibrian; san-francisco
summary: The longest expediente in the volume (~LXVI pp. 90–390, dragging from Aug 1828 into 1831). Causa criminal for the murder of two small children — María Antonia (~5 yrs) and Blas de Jesús (~1 yr), children of soldier Ignacio Olivas — and the estupro (rape) of the girl, on the denuncia of soldier Ignacio Ribas; accused: fellow SF-company soldier Francisco Rubis (25, native of Los Ángeles). Post-mortem autopsy 16 Aug 1828 by the English settler-physician Antonio [William A.] Richardson ('de nación inglés'): both children strangled with a cord, the girl raped. Evidence: parents at a fandango at Sra Bibiana's; a surviving small child blamed 'un coyote muy bravo'; Rubis (who had quarreled with the mother 4 days before) tried that night to swap a clean shirt and passed off a bloodstained *sarape* (borrowed from Juan García, returned via young Rafael Pacheco) — central evidence; Rubis claimed the blood was sheep's (de borrego). Procedural wrangling over jurisdiction, fiscal turnover (Mariano G. Vallejo among fiscals), and the Monterey↔SF distance. Fiscal Padrés (27 May 1831) recommended sparing Rubis the death penalty given time served; ⭐⭐⭐ but Rubis was EXECUTED by garrote, maintaining his innocence — and per an inserted eyewitness reminiscence of Ignacio Cibrián (b. 18 Oct 1816, SF; he was ~14 at the execution), ~2 years later an Indian arrested for robbery/murder, sentenced to death, confessed that *he* had committed the crime and that 'Rubis murió inocente' — a documented wrongful execution. (A Bancroft-collector's English note vouches: 'Cibrián is a man of family… very honorable… living in the rancho of Salsi Puedes, Contra Costa County, near Mount Diablo.') An early CA forensic examination + a wrongful-execution case

author: Echeandía; fiscal
summary: Cluster of minor sumarias: militiaman José Romero (imprisoned from 5 Mar 1828 over an incest/amancebamiento matter w/ his stepdaughter María Josefa/Rafaela — the women said voluntarily) freed by Echeandía 4 Jun 1829 for time served + ordered to amend; and Eugenio's scandalous amancebamiento with the gentile Petra Paula (wife of a wounded man) → 2 months public works + paying the victim's cure; process passed to the Jefe Político at San Diego 26 Sept

author: Echeandía; fiscal Capt. Pablo de la Portilla
themes: military; mutiny; back-pay; soldier-poverty; presidio-decay; san-diego → [[presidio-garrison-artillery-register]]
summary: Causa vs San Diego soldiers (Ramón Figueroa, Dámaso Alipaz, Trinidad, Martín Uriarte, Julián Blanco; 4 ringleaders punished: Blas Olivas, Miguel Alvarado, Ignacio Alipaz, Julián Blanco) for collectively petitioning their commander, Tte Cnl Santiago Argüello, for their back pay (alcances) — a near-mutiny; Argüello allegedly retorted that mission Indians would serve 'con más sumisión y obediencia' and the soldiers weren't needed; the soldiers pleaded they 'no podían hacer el servicio por hallarse desnudos y descalzos' (naked & barefoot, ashamed to appear); punishment: the 4 ringleaders dispersed to the Monterey / S. Francisco / Sta Bárbara companies. Savage's note: 'La cuerda siempre se rompe por lo más delgado'

author: Sgt Dámaso Rodríguez (fiscal); Echeandía
themes: indigenous; religion; witchcraft; curanderismo; ethnobotany; missions; mission-control; santa-ines
summary: Sumaria vs mission Indians (Anastasio & Apolonio, neófitos of Sta Inés; José of San Buenaventura; Mariano) for witchcraft/curanderismo: they staged a healing 'baile al diablo' over sick Indians of the ranchería for 2 nights, collecting beads/seeds/rosaries as payment, claiming the Devil would heal the dancers or all would die; named ~30 medicinal herbs; the curandero Anastasio threatened mockers with a 'máquina de yerbas' (poison bag) that could kill; the Padre Ministro had them whipped & jailed. Echeandía (23 Apr 1829): after 11 months' prison frees Apolonio, but Anastasio & José to receive 25 lashes each, administered publicly before the congregated Indians after Mass at Sta Inés, 'por haberse supuesto hechiceros' — indigenous healing/shamanism criminalized as *hechicería* under mission authority

author: Echeandía
summary: 8-month furlough granted to soldier Trinidad García (S. Blas Veteran Inf.) + 4 other soldiers of the same company — explicitly because the Monterey habilitación cannot pay them, so they may 'con su trabajo subsistir' (work to survive) during the furlough [presidio-decay / unpaid troops]

author: Juan José Rocha (comte)
summary: S. Blas Veteran Inf.: sarg 2° Pedro del Castillo, 2 drummers, 4 cabos, 31 soldados; haberes 1 Jan–15 Nov $6,593, of which only $3,026 received — the State still owes them $3,567

author: Raimundo Estrada (admin. de tabacos)
summary: SF tobacco-monopoly (estanco) accounts: Jan–Mar 1828 (804 lb received, net product $100.5) and the 1827 net product $690.3 — remitted to the Monterey administración

author: José Aguila (sarg.)
summary: Monterey artillery piquete: Capt. Miguel González, sarg 2° Aguila, 1 drummer, 6 cabos, 34 artilleros; revista before M.G. Vallejo; detachments at S. Francisco (sarg. José Mª Medrano, cabo Lázaro Piña + 7) and Sta Bárbara (1 cabo + 4)

author: Mariano G. Vallejo (Habilitado)
summary: Monterey presidial company: Tte comte José Estrada, 2 sgts, 10 cabos, 55 sold.; haberes $1,293; notes Vallejo, formerly a cadet of this company, was dado de baja 27 Dec 1827 on promotion to alférez of the San Francisco company

author: Mariano G. Vallejo
summary: Supplies to the infantry for all of 1828: $3,395

author: Romualdo Pacheco (juez fiscal)
summary: Desertion case vs soldiers Leonardo Félix & Francisco Becerra + paisanos Santiago Vásquez & Serapio Escamilla (arrested in the Monterey guardia principal); alcalde Salvio Pacheco (S. José Guadalupe), escribano José Castro. Sentences to the Veracruz Armada: Félix (the 'seductor') 12 yrs, Becerra 10, Vásquez 10, Escamilla 8. ⭐ Fiscal blames desertion not on malice but on 'las circunstancias de la tierra… su escasez no permite se oculten sus personas' (frontier poverty)

author: Romualdo Pacheco (juez fiscal)
summary: Causa vs Ventura Villavicencio for the violent killing of José Arroyo at Mission San Juan Bautista; Villavicencio took church asylum (asilo en sagrado, San Carlos); comte José Estrada, escribano Sebastián Rodríguez; sentenced to 8 yrs in the Veracruz Armada ships

author: Comte Luis Antonio Argüello; fiscal Cadete Raimundo Estrada
summary: Causa de estupro vs artillero Ignacio Higuera for attempting to rape the ~5-yr-old girl Francisca Valencia (14 Sept 1828); Argüello's cover-note calls it 'el tercer acontecimiento en pocos días en este presidio' (the third such case in days at SF) — 'exigen el remedio… los excesos de la maldad humana'; graphic child testimony (he dragged her to a second kitchen, gagged her with a roof-tile shard); inspection by a 'mujer perita' found blood on her shift; witnesses Antonino Buelna, Concepción Valenzuela, Mariano Uriarte. Higuera (18, native of SF) confessed; defense by Alférez Francisco de Haro (25 Oct 1828) argued the only proof is the confused child's statement, pleading 'la miseria del hombre'; fiscal Estrada left it to the consejo de guerra; remitted via Argüello to Echeandía — no final outcome recorded (ends n415). [a cluster of child sexual assaults at the SF presidio, 1828]

author: Domingo Carrillo (comisario subalterno)
summary: SD municipal aguardiente/wine revenue (fondo municipal): 17 barrels aguardiente ($170) + 2 wine ($10) = $180 cargo; after expenses, existencia $99.6 handed to José Antonio Estudillo

author: Seth Rogers (Am. trader)
summary: Seth Rogers owes the municipal ½-real-per-head cattle-export duty on 432 head, imposed by the Honorable Diputación = $27.2 [foreign trader; livestock-export duty]

author: Fray Antonio Menéndez
summary: Receipts: he was the encargado de la enseñanza pública (public-school teacher) at San Diego, paid $15/month by the municipality

author: Juan Bautista Alvarado (Secretario de la Diputación)
summary: 11 receipts: as secretary of the Diputación he was paid $25/month (Sept–Dec) by José Antonio Estudillo from the fondos municipales [the future governor Alvarado, early career]

author: José Aguila (sarg.)
summary: Monterey artillery piquete account: 1 capt, sarg 2°, 1 cabo grad., 1 drummer, 6 cabos, 36 artilleros; month's pay/premios/gastos $780

author: Ignacio Martínez
themes: indian-raiding; banditry; pomponio; military-history; san-francisco; novato → [[bouchard-raid-1818]]
summary: Hoja de servicios documenting the capture of the famous fugitive-outlaw Pomponio: 3 campaigns into the Tulares vs cimarrón Christian/gentile Indians (killed a cabecilla); then ~13 leagues north of SF he captured the cimarrón Christian José/Pomponio at the cañada de Novato after a firefight (with only a cabo + 2 soldiers, one comrade wounded) — Pomponio guilty of 'innumerables robos, homicidios (este de un soldado)'; Pomponio was fusilado (shot by firing squad) at Monterey by government order; 29+ yrs total service (entered 1799)

author: José Ramírez
summary: Tropa at La Purísima (post-1824 Chumash Revolt): 3 veteran artillerymen + 9 milicianos + 2 English auxiliaries ('Ingleses auxiliares Juan y Santiago') = 14 [foreigners aiding the garrison]

author: Agustín V. Zamorano (Tte, ingenieros)
summary: Reference table of net officer/troop salaries: Comand.e Gen. Inspector $3,763/yr; Capitán $1,413; Teniente $753; Alf. 1° $565; … Soldado $232/yr; Gratificación $500

author: Esteban López (juez fiscal, also LA regidor)
summary: Amancebamiento case vs Isidoro Guillén & Francisca Valdés (wife of Domingo Romero); fiscal declines to sentence (Romero is the accuser); Echeandía: Guillén 3 months public works, and warns husband Romero he'll be punished as accomplice if he tolerates his wife's company with Guillén; defender Vicente Sánchez, escribano José Palomares

author: José Sánchez (sarg. grad., fiscal)
summary: Insubordination case vs artillery sarg. José Mª Medrano — toward Capt. Luis Antonio Argüello + for beating soldier Ignacio Soto while Soto was in the stocks; fiscal: the blows 'bien merecidos' given the soldier's insolence; Echeandía frees Medrano for time served; escribano José Berreyesa

author: Juan Mª Ibarra (tte, fiscal)
summary: Sumaria vs Mazatlán soldier Gerónimo Bastidas for wounding convict Pilar Becerra (hand nearly severed); fiscal cites the ordenanza for wounding 'con ventaja y alevosía'; Bastidas paid the cure (Becerra well by 7 Feb); escribano Juan Feliz

author: Vicente Sánchez (fiscal)
summary: Mutual-wounding case: Luciano Valdés (schoolmaster) & Juan Antonio Duarte (reputed vagrant); Echeandía: Valdés pays curandero/jailer + a $5 fine (to pueblo funds), and Duarte, as a vago, sentenced to presidial military service; escribano José Palomares

author: Echeandía; fiscal Alf. José Mª Ramírez
summary: Accusation vs cavalry soldier Antonio Elizalde for stealing 2 head of cattle from the Nation's rancho (13 May 1828); after 10 months' prison freed, charged the cattle's value; escribano José Antonio Estudillo

author: Rodrigo del Pliego
themes: missions; altimira; clergy; foreigners; san-francisco-solano; sonoma; san-buenaventura
summary: Investigation into the clandestine flight to Spain of Padre Fr. José Altimira (controversial founder of Mission San Francisco Solano / Sonoma, 1823) aboard an English-captained ship, Jan 1828: depositions of escort cabo Juan Lugo and mayordomos Crisóstomo Lugo & Vicente Pico on the cargas of provisions, wine, soap, vinegar, chocolate — and possibly mission silver/money moved aboard via Sta Bárbara; Padre Francisco Suñer left holding only the keys to the money/clothing/bodega. Includes Altimira's own farewell letter to 'Mi Sr Jorge [George] Coleman, inglés de nación y recién reconciliado por mí,' written aboard a bergantín [ship name uncertain in MS] 23 Jan 1828, lamenting abandoning 'esos pobres indios mis amados neófitos' and announcing his 'larguísima caminada… por mar á España, mi patria'; letter closes 'Tuyo = Fr. José Altimira' (ends n430, LXVII.215–227)

author: Echeandía
summary: After inspection of a flatboat (barca plana) beached over a year at the parage de los Adoves, orders its serviceable timber (bottom panels, doors) salvaged — its rotten sides useless for sea — and used to build a small landing wharf (muelle de desembarque)

author: Franc° de la Toba
summary: Queja vs Subtte José Mª Mata (habilitado) who beat & expelled his wife Ana Gertrudis Argüello on a false rumor (raised by Mª Cisneros, alleging she had relations with Fr. Tomás Mansilla); it emerges Mata himself planted the rumor as a pretext to leave his wife and live with his lover (Cisneros)

author: Ignacio Martínez
summary: Resumen de alcances — total back pay owed the SF company + inválidos by year: 1821 $10,847; 1822 $9,591; 1823 $9,275; 1824 $5,008; 1825 $7,172; 1826 $6,804; 1828 $9,738 — grand total $58,436 owed (1827 excluded, belongs to predecessor) [presidio-decay / arrears]

author: Octaviano Gutiérrez
summary: Artillery/ordnance inventory of the Sta Bárbara Parque y Batería: bronze guns 2 good / 1 bad / 1 useless; iron guns 1 good / 2 useless; battle carriages 2 (2 bad, 2 useless); marine carriages 1 (4 useless) — most matériel decayed

author: Echeandía; fiscal Romualdo Pacheco
summary: Desertion case vs 4 Sta Bárbara soldiers (cabo José García, Tomás García, Rafael López, José Mª Ayala) who abandoned the San Luis Obispo mission detachment under Alf. Rodrigo del Pliego; escribano José Mª Arana; the 3 (already doing guard) freed to resume service, but cabo Gabriel García, as the ringleader (cabecilla), condemned to 2 yrs presidio (public works) at San Francisco

author: José Fernández del Campo
summary: Monterey garrison strength: Artillery 28 (1 alf, 1 sgt, 1 drummer, 7 cabos, 18 sold.); Infantry 24; Cavalry 76 (2 alf, 2 sgts, 10 cabos, 1 clarín, 61 sold.)

author: José Mª Medrano (sarg.)
summary: SD artillery piquete: sgt + 11 artilleros = 12 men

author: José Estrada (Habilitado)
summary: Summary of the libranzas & recibos of all the corps garrisoning the province, charged to Estrada 1 Jul 1821 – Dec 1827: total $52,894 [provincial military fiscal aggregate]

author: José Fernández del Campo
summary: [brief] principal artillery commander, rank alférez

author: Echeandía; fiscal Cadete Raimundo Estrada
summary: Causa vs SF-company soldier José Soto for wounding Ignacio Pacheco 2° 'con ventaja y alevosía'; sentenced to 2 yrs presidio at Santa Bárbara; escribano cabo José Berreyesa

author: Ignacio Martínez (juez fiscal)
themes: criminal; homicide; sexuality; indigenous; colonial-discourse; missions; santa-clara; san-francisco
summary: Causa vs Serapio, neófito of Santa Clara, for the rape-murder of the boy Miguel (~11): Serapio confessed he forced the boy (who had refused), then stabbed and killed him to keep him silent (26 Dec 1827); while a fugitive he killed 10 Sta Clara mission cattle, took 9 horses, and took church asylum at Sta Clara; fiscal recommends expatriation from the province. ⚠ Includes a colonial-discourse marginal note: 'Noticia de la sodomía entre los indios por el rumbo del Norte es algo común; lo mismo el estupro, y al matar las criaturas. Hay varios ejemplares públicos…' (a Spanish official generalizing about 'the northern Indians' — fraught source for histories of sexuality/colonial discourse)

author: Echeandía; fiscal Raimundo Estrada
themes: criminal; indigenous; chumash-revolt; missions; santa-ines; san-diego
summary: Causa vs Víctor, alcalde of Mission Santa Inés, for communications with the [1824 Chumash Revolt] rebels ('los sublevados'); sentenced to 1 yr public works at Monterey [a judicial coda to the 1824 Chumash Revolt] → indian-raiding / military-history

author: Anastasio Carrillo (sarg., Comisionado of LA)
summary: On an exhorto from Mexico: declarations re a wounding — victims Antonio & Jacinto Reyes (LA vecinos) and Antonio Mª Nieto (SD miliciano), wounded by Vicente Sánchez; Jacinto Reyes left crippled in the right wrist & index finger [⚠ dated 1832 — out of sequence]

author: José Berreyesa (alf. grad., fiscal)
summary: Causa over an accidental shooting: soldiers out hunting deserters near rancho San Ignacio; while remounting, Gabriel Albiso's shotgun snagged in the holster and discharged, wounding his comrade José Soto; fiscal (Sánchez) recommends acquittal — a casualty with no quarrel; passed to comte Ignacio Martínez; no final result

author: Galindo & José Rafael Martínez
summary: A convict, Tomás de Aquino Servín, sentenced in Mexico to 8 yrs presidio in California for the homicide of José Reyes Alaniz, placed at the disposition of the Ministerio de Gracia y Justicia for transport [convict transportation to Cal.]

author: Echeandía; fiscal Ignacio Martínez
summary: Causa vs SF-company soldier Leonardo Félix for desertion + stealing the arms from Alf. José Sánchez's rancho (24 Feb 1829) — and harboring a plan to kill his father-in-law; proof weak; defended by Capt. Luis Antonio Argüello; Félix freed & discharged from service for time served [likely the same Félix as Doc 357]

author: Echeandía
summary: Causa vs Salvador Higuera & Atencio Valencia for robbery (a bottle of aguardiente) at the rancho of Higuera's mother; imprisoned since January, freed

Ordnance/munitions inventory: rammers/sponges/handspikes 72 good / 17 bad / 16 useless; round shot 203 good; canister cartridges 238 good / 67 bad / 32 useless; bagged charges 128; musket cartridges 2,698 (+70 bad); 12 arrobas powder; 350 useless gunflints; 3 useless muskets — matériel partly decayed

author: Rodrigo del Pliego (fiscal); defender Tte Antonio del Valle
themes: criminal; parricide; indigenous; missions; san-buenaventura; santa-barbara
summary: Parricide: criminal vs José Gabino, neófito of San Buenaventura, who struck and killed his father Pascual; fiscal asks hanging, despite the 25 lashes the Padre Ministro already ordered for an earlier slap; defender pleads indulgence

author: José Mª Carrillo (alcalde/fiscal)
themes: criminal; theft; indigenous; missions; san-gabriel; los-angeles
summary: Sumaria vs Cesario & Salvador, neófitos of San Gabriel, for robbing Antonio Ignacio Ávila (cloth, garments, knives) at the rancho de San Pedro (Manuel Gutiérrez's house); fiscal: after restitution apply the penalty the Jefe Político sets; escribano José Palomares

author: Juan Mª Ibarra (tte, fiscal)
summary: Sumaria vs neófito Bartolomé for stealing garments from Vicente Sánchez (which were then stolen from him when he got drunk); fiscal asks 6 months public works with a grillete; escribano Ignacio del Valle

author: Raimundo Estrada (juez fiscal); defender (tte de caballería)
themes: criminal; homicide; indigenous; resistance; foreigners; beaver; san-jose; san-francisco
summary: Causa vs José Lucino (19), neófito of San José, for killing the foreigner 'Rin' — an American, the blacksmith of San José pueblo, who had gone out hunting beaver/otter (castores): Lucino lassoed him with his reata, dragged and killed him, stripped him, with accomplices Victorino & José Colosanes. Lucino's stated motive: he greeted the American 'amar á Dios,' and the American replied 'Indianos mal,' enraging him. The fiscal demands Lucino be shot (pasado por las armas) AND that 10 Indians from each mission be imprisoned as a warning ('escarmiento')

author: Dámaso Rodríguez (sarg., fiscal); defender Anastasio Carrillo
themes: criminal; homicide; self-defense; indigenous; san-fernando; santa-barbara
summary: Causa vs Ignacio Valenzuela for killing Cayetano (neófito of San Fernando): after Cayetano insulted & slapped him at 'el Tapo,' Valenzuela took a lance and, defending himself, fatally wounded him; fiscal asks 3 yrs arms service; defender Carrillo pleads self-defense → liberty (eloquent closing)

author: Santiago Argüello (fiscal); Echeandía
themes: trade; contraband; hawaii; foreigners; santa-catalina; san-pedro; san-diego
summary: Actuaciones vs the captain & master of the Hawaiian (Sandwich Islands) brig-schooner 'Karimoko' for touching at Santa Catalina Island, forming an establishment there, and smuggling goods into the rada de San Pedro (April 1828); Capt. 'Juan Sola' deposed via interpreter — cargo from Hawaii (sugar, rice, tea, honey, tobacco, blankets); he had left 5 English sailors + 3 Hawaiian 'canacas' (kanakas) + ~480 hides on Sta Catalina; Echeandía ordered Argüello to inventory the Catalina establishment; fine $300 for the illegal San Pedro trade + embargo, then released

author: Romualdo Pacheco (fiscal)
summary: Proceso vs Trinidad Rosas for killing cattle of Mission San Juan Bautista; fiscal: 3 yrs presidiario at Loreto (Baja Cal.); escribano cabo Petronilo Ríos; José Estrada remitted the case to the Comandante de ambas Californias 7 May

author: José Castro (defensor)
summary: Conclusion of the Trinidad Rosas cattle-killing case (cf. Doc 398): Padre Felipe Arroyo de la Cuesta (San Juan Bautista) sent the complaint to the S. José Guadalupe alcalde; fiscal Juan Salvio Pacheco; defender pleads indulgence (Rosas not proven the killer)

author: Echeandía
themes: trade; contraband; foreigners; bradshaw; franklin; santa-catalina; san-diego
summary: Order to investigate the contraband of the American frigate 'Franklin' (Capt. John Bradshaw) at Santa Catalina Island / Baja California — supercargo G. Simpson; involves Bradshaw's other vessel the *Lascar* and a brig's boat; the famous 1828 Bradshaw/*Franklin* smuggling affair (the *Franklin* later defied San Diego and ran out under fire); the averiguaciones are a copy of the originals; escribano Francisco de Araujo

author: Romualdo Pacheco (fiscal)
summary: Sumaria vs prisoner Serapio Escamilla for stabbing Guillermo Camilo Garibay (minor wound); no sentence recorded; escribano José Castro

author: Juan Mª Ibarra
summary: Notified Carlos Sainz that his goods incurred decomiso (confiscation); asks the Administrador for the duties owed [foreign-trader contraband]

author: Francisco de Haro (subtte, fiscal)
summary: Insubordination case vs cabo José Antonio Soto (refused his posting citing unproven illness); fiscal: 1 month calabozo + suspension from the squadron to deter the bad example; escribano José Fernández

author: Juan Mª Ibarra (fiscal)
summary: Robbery case vs soldiers León Rosas & José Cañedo for robbing their cabo Hilario García (money, 6 pairs shoes, cigars, silk paper, hides — from the munitions room); only León Rosas found guilty; fiscal cites the ordenanza's hanging penalty for theft within the barracks

author: Anastasio Carrillo, Administrador de Correos
summary: Brief note — Carrillo as SB mail administrator through the year. Orig. pp.2–96.

author: Juan Mª Ibarra, Cmdt. int. — Escuadrón de Mazatlán
summary: Review of the Mazatlán cavalry squadron at San Diego, Dec 1829. Capt. Pablo de la Portilla \"en campaña\" (the Solís-revolt campaign); Ttes Ibarra & Narciso Fabregat; 4 alféreces, 3 cabos, 39 soldados = 50 hombres; much of the unit \"en campaña\" (Portilla, Sargtos Dámaso Tetary[?] & Matías Ramos, 2 cabos, 32 soldados) while Fabregat & Alf. Roberto Pardo[?] were at Santa Bárbara. Company costs ~$1,694; SD subaltern commission monthly pay Jan–Jun 1829 ($687→$781). Orig. pp.2–96.

author: Alférez José Fernández del Campo, Cmdt. int. de Artillería
summary: Review of the Artillery Company (5ª Compañía de la 1ª Brigada), Oct 1829. Tte. Vicente Pico[?]; Sargto 2º José Navarro & Andrés Cervantes; 1 tambor, 8 cabos, 46 artilleros = 57 hombres (was 64). Detachments across Monterey, San Diego, San Francisco & Sta Bárbara; a sargto marching to San Diego (Solís campaign). Orig. pp.97–132.

author: Santiago Argüello, Cmdt. int. Gen. de B. Cal.
summary: Per Cmdt.-Gen. Echeandía's order (1 Jun), Baja California's command made known (order of the 10th) under the Cmdt.-Gen. of B. Cal. [Ahumada?]; B. Cal. left politically subject to this Jefatura of Alta California. Orig. pp.131–134.

author: Santiago Argüello
summary: \"Parte espiritual\": Don José Antonio Estudillo, in charge of the municipal funds, to pay $15/mo to Fr. Antonio Menéndez for the spiritual administration of this población. Orig. pp.137–138.

author: Santiago Argüello, Cmdt. int.
summary: San Diego presidial company review, Aug 1829. Capt. José Mª Estudillo; Tte. Argüello; Alférez 1º Ignacio Argüello (absent in México as Habilitado General); 1º agregado Domingo Carrillo; 6 cabos, 51 soldados (short 13) = 58 + officers; active pay $1,425, invalids $227 (incl. Capt. Fco. Mª Ruiz, Tte Juan Mainer[?], 3 sargtos, 9 soldados). SD garrison on the 15th: artillery 19, cavalry 68, Mazatlán active militia 9. Orig. pp.156–164.

author: Santiago Argüello, Juez Fiscal
summary: Criminal causa vs. a soldier: sumaria against soldier Francisco Verdugo for losing a dispatch (pliego) he was carrying for the Comandante General (20 Sep 1829); convicted & confessed (lost it through carelessness); the fiscal asks 25 lashes (palos) in his quarters + one month's plantón at the Castillo de San Diego. (A note suggests he was freed after the prison term.) Orig. pp.165–180.

author: José Joaquín Maitorena
summary: Santa Bárbara presidial company review 1829: Capt. (in México, paid there); Tte. Maitorena; Alférez Domingo Carrillo (absent at San Diego); Alférez agregado Rodrigo del Pliego[?]; 3 sargtos, 6 cabos, 1 cadete, 58 soldados; invalids 4 sargtos/1 cabo/19 soldados; company + invalid pay this month $1,789. Orig. pp.259–279.

author: Mariano G. Vallejo, Habilitado
summary: Monterey presidial company review, March 1829 — M. G. Vallejo as habilitado: 1 Tte, 1 Alférez, 2 sargtos, 10 cabos, 50 plazas sencillas; gratificación $41.5; total pay this month $1,448. Orig. p.257.

author: Subteniente de Ingenieros Romualdo Pacheco (hoja de servicios)
themes: prosopography; pacheco; colorado-river; san-francisco; 1828-revolt; monterey; military-history
summary: Service record of Romualdo Pacheco (military engineer; father of the future Gov. Romualdo Pacheco): cadet 1 yr 6 mo, total 13 yrs incl. campaign credits. Two Colorado-River expeditions (1825 Nov & 1826 May): the first w/ only 4 soldiers — reconnoitered the river, found a favorable road, made peace with hostile tribes; the second w/ 25 dragoons (his troop nearly abandoned him before \"la inmensa muchedumbre de indios\"); built a bridge. April 1828 reconnaissance of the whole port of San Francisco & its rivers (chose a fort site by Supreme-Govt order). 18 Oct 1828: as adjutant of Monterey, helped suppress \"la sublevación que hicieron los soldados de la Compañía de Caballería de Monterey\" (\"el sostén de la plaza vulnerada por la tropa\") — the 1828 Monterey cavalry revolt, precursor to the 1829 Solís revolt. Then took the Santa Bárbara command (31 Dec 1828) under Cmdt.-Gen. Echeandía. Orig. pp.290–292.

author: Romualdo Pacheco, Comandante
summary: SB garrison strength, Nov 1829: cavalry 3 sargtos/6 cabos/55 soldados + officers; artillery 1 cabo/7 soldados; short 1 infantry Tte & 1 cavalry alférez. SB company+invalid cost for October $1,581. Orig. pp.293–309.

author: Juan José Rocha, Cmdt. int. — San Blas infantry company
summary: Review of the permanent San Blas infantry company at Monterey: Tte. Antonio del Valle (at SB); Sargto-graduado/subteniente Rocha; Sargto 2º Pedro del Castillo; 1 cabo + 19 soldados = 23 present (dwindled from 38 at year-start). Pay/costs to date $3,978. Orig. pp.323–357.

author: Manuel Jimeno Casarín, Comisario Subalterno int.
summary: Jimeno Casarín as interim subaltern commissary since January (recurring item). Orig. pp.323–357.

author: Ministro
summary: Charles Laing contraband case referred back: the detention of a boat loaded with effects found at the puerto de San Quintín, captained by the Anglo-American Mr. Charles Laing, to be remitted to the local alcalde constitucional (1st-instance jurisdiction) pending the district judge who should reside at Guaymas. Orig. p.430.

author: Echeandía
themes: contraband; foreign-contact; san-quintin; baja-california; echeandia; kanakas; hawaiians; rambler
summary: The Charles Laing contraband investigation. Laing, jailed & accused of smuggling; Echeandía (12 Feb) had Tte. Juan Mª Ibarra depose the foreigners — 2 Americans + 2 Kanakas (Hawaiians) who landed at San Quintín (Baja Cal.). Their ship was the frigate Rambler (out of Nantucket); Laing (a U.S. native & navigator) had come from Oahu on the brig Alabama to settle in Baja or Alta Cal. by permission, was put off into the launch after a falling-out at Isla Redonda, survived on sea-birds & fish, traded the soldiers' game for silver; effects landed at the ensenada de Todos Santos; sailor Winslow Lewis, Kanakas Espen/Carena[?]. Echeandía's decree (26 Feb 1829): return Laing's víveres/clothes/papers, hold the powder/balls/arms in deposit, the rest in the Aduana; the launch to the port commander; copy to the Tribunal de circuito for sentence. Orig. pp.431–461.

author: Fco. Mª Ruiz, Comandante
summary: SD presidial company 1825: Capt. Ruiz; Tte. José Mª Estudillo; Alférez Ignacio Argüello; 1 cabo, cadete Domingo Carrillo, 54 soldados = 57; invalids 15. Orig. pp.478–482.

author: Pablo de la Portilla, Comandante — Escuadrón de Mazatlán
summary: Mazatlán squadron 1825: Capt. Portilla; Ttes Narciso Fabregat & Juan Mª Ibarra; Alférez Ignacio Delgado; 5 sargtos, 2 tambores, 5 cabos, 61 soldados = 77 hombres. Orig. pp.479–497.

author: Santiago Argüello (correos), Domingo Carrillo (comisario subalterno)
summary: SD administrative notes 1825 (mail administration; subaltern commissary). Orig. pp.481–497.

author: Juan Mª Ibarra, Cmdt. int. — Escuadrón de Mazatlán
summary: Mazatlán squadron 1826: 1 Capt., 2 Ttes, 1 Alférez, 5 sargtos, 2 tambores, 5 cabos, 53 soldados = 68 hombres (down from 90 in Jan). Reviewed before Domingo Carrillo, SD subaltern commissary. Orig. pp.499–575.

author: Subtte. de Artillería José Fernández del Campo
themes: solis-revolt; conspiracy; monterey; echeandia; vallejo; hijos-de-la-tierra; military-history; 1829
summary: The Solís-revolt conspiracy sumaria (June–Aug 1829). Fernández del Campo reports (18–21 Jun) that cabo Mariano Vaquero[?], artilleryman Pablo/Pedro Guerrero[?] (Savage's copy writes \"Pablo\" on the first leaf, \"Pedro\" on the next) & cavalry-soldier Juan Lisalde[?] revealed a conspiracy \"que debía ser dirigida por el presidiario Sargto Solís\" against the territorial chief (Echeandía) and all the officers, to put the offices \"en manos de hijos de la tierra.\" The plan (a junta): seize the artillery cuartel, casa-mata, castillo & other key points of Monterey, assault set for the night of the 22nd. Alférez M. G. Vallejo, asked to continue the sumaria (8 Jul), recused himself — because he was commanding the very company accused of the plot, and as habilitado; cadet Raymundo Estrada brought from SF (11 Aug) to continue, but Fernández del Campo declined to re-depose before him, and \"aquí queda truncado el asunto\" (the investigation breaks off — Solís launched the actual revolt that November). Escribano artilleryman Petronilo Ríos; Sargto 2º Andrés Cervantes named. The documentary core of the 1829 Solís revolt. Orig. pp.578–598.

author: Pedro del Castillo, fiscal
summary: Criminal causa — knife-wounding. Cabo Mariano Higuera, imprisoned, accused of stabbing the civilian Anastasio Albiso (3 Sep 1829, at/near Mission San Juan Bautista). Vivid verbatim narrative: a quarrel over a sarape by Higuera's horse escalated to a knife fight — Higuera \"metió el puñal en el hueso de entre el hombro y el pescuezo.\" Depositions before Alcalde José Tiburcio Castro, Regidor Feliciano Soberanes; the wound treated by the surgeon Jorge [George] Allen (foreigner) who reports Albiso out of danger by the 19th; witnesses Paulino Serra, Nicolás Albiso, Manuel Boronda. Fiscal: crime proved, deserves grave punishment. Orig. pp.601–626.

author: Echeandía
summary: Criminal causa — soldier sedition. Artillery soldier Manuel Rojas charged with having \"vociferado palabras indecorosas contra los oficiales\"; after Alférez José Sánchez took depositions, Echeandía ordered the sumaria passed to artillery Cmdt. Alférez Fernández del Campo to be judged per ordinance. (Result not found.) An early-1829 sign of the troop unrest that fed the Solís revolt. Orig. pp.629–652.

author: Juan José Rocha, fiscal
summary: Causa vs. cavalry recruit Francisco Soto — insubordination + complicity in the 8 Oct 1828 Monterey revolt (\"delito que la ley castiga con pena de muerte\"). The fiscal recommends not death but transfer to another presidio for correction, given Soto's prison time (since Nov 1828), being a recruit, and the daily-ration scarcities — \"lo mismo que lo están los demás individuos que se sublevaron.\" (Result not found.) Confirms the 1828 Monterey rising was a serious sublevación. Orig. pp.658–679.

author: Fco. Morales, Notario
summary: Bigamy/polygamy causa against Alférez José Mª Ramírez (cavalry). Testimony of documents in his ongoing case (cross-refs Tomo LXX, Dep. St. Pap. Benicia — Military, pp.523–541). Orig. pp.680–698.

author: Juan Mª Ibarra, Juez fiscal
