author: José Figueroa
summary: Reglamento (8 articles) on the missions' temporal administration / livestock distribution: crops to complete each mission's seed; cattle loaned to the national ranchos, to be repaid & integrated back to the missions within 6 years; rules on goods receipt (receipts via the subcomisaría), escoltas, etc. A secularization-era mission-management regulation (Figueroa, prelude to his 1834 secularization). Orig. pp.971–972.

author: Mariano Frías
summary: SF artillery detachment Dec 1832: 5 hombres. Orig. pp.1003–1016.

author: José Estrada, Habilitado
summary: Customs (renta de aduanas): a Russian ship from Sitka imported goods worth $903 at Monterey; total duties $1,107 (5% arancel at 40%, 1% per the latest law, + Sinaloa duties). Orig. p.1020.

author: Juan Malarín, 2º Tte. de la Armada Nacional y Cap. del puerto
summary: Brief note. Orig. p.1021.

summary: Salary certification for navy 1er Tte. Pedro Narváez — $2,062 owed ($129 6r/mo). dated 1840 (a later doc interleaved). Orig. pp.12–13.

summary: Brief. Orig. p.14.

author: Pedro del Castillo

author: Castillo

author: Castillo

summary: Arrest of the schooner Loriot (supercargo A. B. Thompson) at SF: the ship, cargo & supercargo arrested 26 Oct 1833 by the Comandante General's order, placed under M. G. Vallejo's responsibility (sargto García + 4 soldiers aboard), cargo unloaded under inventory. Orig. pp.36–39.

author: Castillo

author: Pedro del Castillo, Sub-comisario Subalterno
summary: Brief note (of 2 Nov). Orig. p.49.

author: José Mª Medrano
summary: Monterey permanent artillery Dec 1833: 2 sargtos, 1 tambor, 8 cabos, 32 soldados = 43 hombres (39 employed). Orig. pp.162–164.

summary: Monterey permanent artillery company 1833: 42 hombres; haberes $7,918. Orig. pp.134–168.

author: Pablo de la Portilla
summary: Escuadrón activo de Mazatlán 1833: Cap. Portilla; Ttes. Ibarra & Narciso Fabregat; 3 sargtos, 2 cabos, 31 soldados = 37 hombres; distributed at presidios/missions; haberes $10,128. Orig. pp.169–231.

author: Juan Mª Ibarra
summary: SB military force Dec 1833: cuera cavalry 37, Mazatlán squadron 5, artillery 6. Orig. pp.259–275.

author: Gen. José Figueroa (fiscal Nicolás Gutiérrez)
themes: indigenous-resistance; uprising; san-diego; independence; tecate; molina; tesochi; figueroa; gómez; war-council; mistreatment; 1833
summary: Sentence in the San Diego Indian-uprising conspiracy. A planned general uprising of the SD-area Indians (reported by Tomás Olivera from Rancho Tecate): to gather the convened Indians, assault the San Diego presidio on the 20th, \"acabar con los blancos p[ar]a ser independientes, y gobernarse ellos mismos,\" the same intended for the missions up to San Gabriel (emissaries carried the convocatoria). Accused: José Antonio Molina (emissary), Tomás Tesochi, Ramón, Andrés, Gregorio, Francisco Roman, Domingo. Investigated by Argüello, continued by fiscal Nicolás Gutiérrez under Figueroa/Gómez → consejo de guerra (pres. Capt. Juan Antonio Muñoz; vocales Zamorano, González, Navarrete, Peña; Alf. Francisco Pacheco). Sentence: Molina & Tesochi → 2 yrs public works; Roman → 1 yr; Gregorio & another → 1 yr; Domingo → freed — penalties attenuated for \"la gran ignorancia de los acusados\" & their not executing the plot; the Asesor acknowledged \"la probabilidad de los malos tratamientos de q[u]e se quejan, y alegan por motivos de su asonada\" (the likely mistreatment cited as the uprising's motive). Figueroa confirmed 22 Nov 1833. A significant indigenous-resistance case with an explicit independence / self-government aim and the authorities conceding Indian grievances. Orig. pp.314–528.

author: Agustín V. Zamorano
summary: Monterey presidial company Nov 1833: Cap. Zamorano; Tte. Gervasio Argüello (absent); Alf. José Sánchez & Ignacio del Valle; 35 hombres. Orig. pp.551–552.

author: Mariano G. Vallejo
summary: SF presidial company July 1833: Alféreces Vallejo & Dámaso Antonio Rodríguez; 2 sargtos, 2 clarines, 4 cabos, 30 soldados = 40 hombres. Orig. pp.534–536.

summary: Mazatlán company detachment at SB: 19 hombres; haberes ~$320/mo. Orig. pp.537–558.

author: Bernardo Navarrete
summary: Mazatlán active-company detachment at Monterey. Orig. p.543.

author: Bernardo Navarrete
summary: Mazatlán squadron detachment Oct 1834: 8 hombres. Orig. pp.559–560.

author: Juan Salazar
summary: SD presidial company May 1833: Cap. Santiago Argüello; Tte. Rodrigo del Pliego (absent in LA); Alf. Salazar; sargto José Antonio Pico; 34 present. Orig. pp.562–564.

author: Santiago Argüello
summary: SD garrison July 1833: cuera cavalry 38, permanent artillery 6, Loreto cavalry 9, Mazatlán militia 3. Orig. p.575.

author: Gen. Figueroa
summary: Protects Mission San Rafael's timber: the SF commander to leave the mission the free use of its woods, \"prohibiendo p[ar]a el común q[u]e sin permiso de este Gob.no nadie podrá cortarla.\" Orig. p.634.

author: Juan Salazar
summary: SD garrison Dec 1833: cuera cavalry + artillery + Mazatlán militia incl. officers = 58 hombres; same in Sept/Oct/Nov. Orig. pp.641–4.

author: González (junta)
summary: Election of 9 electores by the mission vecindario for 1834 — the junta named secretaries Antonino Buelna & José Fernández; elected \"los Ciudadanos\": Raymundo (50 votes), Eduardo (48), Hilario (45), Severo (44), Norberto (39), Gerónimo (34), Máximo (29), Sebastián Aparicio (26), Pascual (26). Single-name electors ⇒ almost certainly neophytes voting as citizens — a striking secularization-era municipal-election record at a mission. Orig. p.645.

author: José Antonio Ávila (presidiario)
themes: solís-revolt; convicts; ávila; pacheco; malarín; estrada; 1833
summary: Memorial: exiled to California since 1824 \"por malos informes… en el 1er día de la independencia de México\"; during Solís's 1829–30 revolt the Cmdt. de Armas Alf. Francisco Pacheco gave him command of 30 men to pursue Solís, and he captured Solís & his followers and delivered them. Certified by Alf. Francisco Pérez Pacheco (10 Feb 1830), 3er Tte. de Marina Juan Malarín (28 Feb 1830) & Tte. retirado José Estrada (26 Mar 1830). Orig. pp.675, 680–2.

author: José Antonio Ávila / Capt. Zamorano (informe)
themes: civil-war-1832; convicts; ávila; vicente-sánchez; zamorano; victoria; ibarra; santa-inés; 1833
summary: The fullest account of the 1832 civil war. Ávila's 2nd petition: he headed a band of presidiarios \"para restablecer la autoridad legítima contra los que se levantaron en San Diego y destruyeron al… Cap. Político D. Manuel Victoria,\" asking return of the arms confiscated in 1832 + a passport to return to Mexico by land. Zamorano's informe: the arms were seized not for the anti-Victoria action but later, \"por haberse puesto á la cabeza de una partida de presidiarios que alarmó de acuerdo con D. Vicente Sánchez, vecino… de los Ángeles, para destituirme\"; narrates the pursuit (Alf. Anastasio Carrillo, 25 men), the split with Sánchez ~8 leagues past Mission San Buenaventura (Sánchez meant to attack San Diego, counting on Tte. Ibarra's division), the presidiarios abandoning Ávila \"por verse engañados\" and surrendering at Mission Santa Inés. Figueroa returned Ávila's pistol but denied the passport \"hasta que finalice el tiempo por que fue destinado á este Territorio.\" Orig. pp.675–9.

author: José Rafael González (Administrador de la Aduana marítima y terrestre)
summary: Customs-revenue reports: alcabalas + import duties (cattle, aguardiente, soap, hides; national goods to Sonora/Santa Catarina) ~$1000; a 2nd report on aguardiente/wine revenue $215 (42 barrels aguardiente, 2 wine \"del país\"). Orig. pp.3, 24.

author: Gov. Figueroa + Asesor Rafael Gómez (dictamen 25 Apr)
themes: jurisdiction; military-vs-civil; santa-barbara; reyes; noriega; gómez; figueroa; 1833
summary: The Reyes vs. Capt. Noriega jurisdiction causa (SB): a complaint by Antonio Reyes against Capt. José de la Guerra y Noriega raises whether a military commander may judge civilians where there is no ayuntamiento. Gómez's dictamen — plus a candid private letter — holds that where there are no constitutional alcaldes the military commander exercises 1st-instance justice, but declares Santa Bárbara's \"Alcalde Constitucional\" illegally established (SB lacked the population/elements; the Diputación gave him only Juez Conciliador standing), and frets that saying so \"abriría heridas políticas que aún no están cicatrizadas\" (he has been called \"enemigo del territorio\" for such opinions). A vivid source on the contested legitimacy of civil vs. military authority in 1833 California. + an out-of-order 1821 SB muster (de la Guerra's company, 69 men) and a July 1833 Monterey muster. Orig. pp.33–42.

author: Capt. Zamorano
summary: Muster analysis: only 35 men total present in Cal.; the company's bulk at Santa Bárbara with Figueroa, only 10 at Monterey (Alf. Valle, clarín, 2 cabos, 6 soldados); haberes $743. + SJ Capistrano muster of the Monterey presidial company. [gap-sampled] Orig. pp.89–101.

author: Alcalde José Antonio Carrillo
themes: desertion; incest; concubinage; san-gabriel; olivas; luisa-pérez; women; gómez; 1833
summary: The Domingo Olivas causa (desertion + first-cousin concubinage — NOT a straightforward homicide; corrected in the completeness pass). Soldier Domingo Olivas of the San Gabriel escort deserted and had by force taken Luisa Pérez — his first cousin, with whom he had lived amancebado for years (3 living daughters); her brother José Pérez complained. After 7+ months' jail the LA alcalde absolved him (to rejoin his company). A separate/entangled dictamen (n405) states Olivas \"infirió á Luisa Pérez la muerte del 5 de Feb\" and refers that matter to the Superior Consejo de la Guerra — but the two leaves don't fully reconcile (the abduction complaint treats her as alive) and Savage's record is incomplete (\"Facultado no aparece\"). Do not assert a homicide as established — flagged for a careful re-read. Orig. pp.135–63.

author: Gov. Figueroa / Sec. Zamorano
themes: penal-colony; convicts; presidiarios; padilla; figueroa; zamorano; 1833; 1834; 1835
summary: The penal-colony convict register (→ Expanded Entry G): a compiled dossier of convict-release petitions \"por [las cuales] aparece que este territorio era colonia penal de México\" — ~25+ named presidiarios with sentence dates & 1833–35 liberty decrees (Moreno, Roso, Trinidad Cruz, Rangel, Barreto, Vásquez, M. Sánchez, J.A. López, Badillo, Olvera, Quiñones, Vargas, Sandoval, Arcena, Gallardo, Mata, Domingo Hernández, Francisco Padilla — the one denied release & recommended for Veracruz hard labor — et al.), plus the standing order that a convict leaving his assigned place is \"irremisiblemente pasado por las armas.\" A systematic prosopographical source on California-as-penal-colony. Orig. pp.183–306.

author: Agustín V. Zamorano
summary: The Solís-revolt defector register — \"Razón tomada á los presentados que abandonaron la facción de Solís\": ~30 named participants with role, arms, and how/when each joined Solís & surrendered to Pacheco (mostly forced in at San José Guadalupe/Santa Clara, surrendered 14–18 Jan 1830) — incl. José Cordero, Mariano Frías, José Ma. Flores, José Miguel Alvarado, Manuel Peña, Inocencio Romero, Leonardo Virgen, Gabriel & Joaquín García, José Ma. Cota, Francisco Ma. Soto, Juan Corona, Antonio Hernández, Fulgencio Higuera, Norberto García, Francisco Becerra, and Cabo Lázaro Piña (\"clavó el cañón\" — spiked the gun). The rank-and-file complement to the officer/leader Solís dossier — fulfills Doc 64's roster request. Orig. pp.271–306.

author: José Ma. Medrano
summary: 5a Cía de la Brigada de Artillería: sargto 2º Medrano + Andrés Cervantes, 1 tambor, 8 cabos, 32 soldados = 43; reviewed by Ayudante retirado José Estrada. Orig. pp.451–5.

author: Nicolás Gutiérrez
summary: Piquete del 2º Batallón permanente — pay list ($4,598 for 14 Jan–date); the piquet's movements (SB, SD, SJ Capistrano, Monterey).

author: Gen. Figueroa
recipient: Cmdt. de la Escolta de San Gabriel
summary: Orders re the New Mexico horse-thieves (cross-ref. oficio to the LA alcalde, p.301). Orig. p.i.177.

author: José Antonio Carrillo (Alcalde Constl.)
summary: \"Robos y otros excesos de aventureros de N. Méx.\" — a denuncia that New Mexico vecinos were driving many horses & mules toward Sonora without the required registro; 5 LA citizens pursued them 20 leagues, seized their partida (~200 bestias, incl. ~100 of a mission padre's); the thieves imprisoned. Orig. pp.301, 485.

author: Alcalde Carrillo
themes: new-mexico; horse-theft; tulares; san-luis-obispo; neophytes; carrillo; 1833
summary: Recovery of stolen stock: the party captured some NM thieves and recovered many animals in the valle de los Tulares near Arroyo Grande — of these 75 mules, 30 mares, 11 horses belong to Mission San Luis Obispo (may reclaim them); two SLO neophytes — Lucas & Coronado — appear as accomplices, whom Carrillo asked the Gefe Político to hand over for prosecution. Orig. pp.486–7.

author: Gen. Figueroa
summary: Seeks extradition of the criminal Juan de Jesús Villapando directly from the authorities of New Mexico (without the Gefatura Superior). Orig. pp.498–500.

author: Gen. Figueroa
summary: Orders the alcalde to name armed vecinos to join Alf. Dámaso Rodríguez's expedition (max secrecy) against the NM thieves. Orig. pp.501–2.

author: Gen. Figueroa
themes: new-mexico; horse-theft; tulares; expedition; rodríguez; figueroa; 1833
summary: Formal INSTRUCTIONS for a military expedition into the Tulares to \"perseguir y destruir la reunión de individuos del territorio de Nuevo México denunciados de ladrones de ganado caballar y mular\": 15 cavalry from the Santa Bárbara presidio to march to Los Ángeles, join the alcalde & armed vecinos, well-armed & munitioned. A rare organized 1833 punitive expedition vs. Old-Spanish-Trail / Chaguanoso horse-raiders in the San Joaquín Valley. Orig. p.334+.

author: Asesor Gómez (dictamen 13 Apr) / Figueroa (28 Jun 1833)
summary: Legal dictamen on the Comandante General acting as Juez de 1ª/2ª instancia (art. 7 of the 15 Sep 1833 decree); notes Zamorano's visita general of 25 May 1833. [gap-sampled]

author: Anastasio Carrillo (Comisario)
summary: Compañía provincial inválidos: sargto Guillermo Cota (in LA), 1 cabo, 16 soldados = 18. Orig. pp.2–5.

author: Juan Antonio Muñoz
summary: Artillería permanente: capitán interino Muñoz, 2 sargtos 2º, 1 tambor + 1 pito, 8 cabos, 27 artilleros = 40. Orig. p.8.

author: Muñoz / de la Portilla / Domingo Carrillo
place: Monterey / San Luis Rey / Santa Bárbara
summary: 1834 pay musters: Muñoz artillería (May, $612); de la Portilla — Escuadrón activo de Mazatlán at San Luis Rey ($844, \"diseminado en varias misiones y presidios\"); Domingo Carrillo SB provincial company ($982). Orig. pp.95, 103–10, 117–8.

author: [seniority comparison]
summary: Notes promotion inequities: Santiago Argüello (1st post 15 Oct 1805) & Zamorano (1 May 1821, ~16 yrs later) both made Captain the same day, 23 May 1831; Ignacio Argüello (2 Apr 1804) & Ramírez del Pliego (21 Dec 1824, ~19 yrs later) both made Lieutenant the same day, 23 May 1831 — \"estas diferencias en los favores de la fortuna.\" Orig. p.25.

author: José Ma. Mendoza (Comisario Gral. de Sonora)
summary: Reclamación against Gen. Figueroa for $3,300 he left owing the fondo de temporalidades in Sinaloa, to be recovered from the Alta-Cal. sub-treasury. Orig. pp.2, 6–7.

author: José Rafael González (Sub-Comisario)
summary: Salary-arrears statement: as of 30 Jun 1834, total owed this year to presidial companies + military/civil salaried = $52,835; total accrued $134,072; received $80,657; arrears mostly from 1833, some 1832, a few back to 1817–1827. Notes the office lacks archives/books/documents to compile a complete account. Includes a companion roster (n460–n466) naming the whole unattached/retired officer corps + civil employees owed salary — Figueroa, Nicolás Gutiérrez, Juan Antonio Muñoz, Ignacio Martínez, Francisco Ma. Ruiz, Mariano Estrada, José de la Guerra y Noriega, Antonio del Valle, José Ma. Ramírez, Juan José Rocha, Bernardo Navarrete, José Peña, et al. A stark presidio-decay / fiscal-collapse datapoint + 1834 officer prosopography. Orig. pp.15, 329–30.

author: Ignacio Martínez (Cmdt. accidental)
summary: SF artillery detachment to the capital: 1 cabo + 6 soldados = 7; haberes $111. [bound out of chronological order] Orig. pp.332–3.

author: Anastasio Carrillo
summary: SB presidial company haberes/gratificaciones (active co. $989 + inválidos $146, etc.). Orig. pp.334–356.

author: Manuel Jimeno Casarín (Alcalde Constl. de Monterey)
summary: Entry begins (alcalde correspondence). Orig. p.399.

author: hojas de servicio
summary: Volume ends with service records: Ignacio del Valle (cadet 23 Jan 1828, Alférez 2º 23 May 1831, \"no tuvo campañas ni acciones de guerra\"; p.425); Gabriel de la Torre (soldado 1 Apr 1827, cabo 1 May 1833, sargto 1 Jan 1834; p.474); Dámaso A. Rodríguez (pay budget, 1 Jun 1834). Orig. pp.425, 474.

author: Juan Antonio Muñoz (Cmdt. Artillería)
summary: Province-wide artillery census — bronze & iron cannon by caliber at all four presidios: Monterey (bronze 8s/6/4 + iron 8/6×5/4/3), San Francisco (bronze 18 ×5/8/2½ + iron 24 ×3/12/4/2½), Santa Bárbara (bronze 6/4×2/3/2 + iron 4×2/3/2), San Diego (bronze 6 ×7 + pedreros + iron 8×2/6×2), each with carriages/munitions \"en suficiente cantidad.\" A rare 1833 total-province ordnance snapshot for the [[presidio-garrison-artillery-register]]. Orig. pp.1–19 (LXXVI).

author: Anastasio Carrillo (Sub-Comisario)
summary: SB sub-treasury account (cargo/data): revenue $6,710 incl. mission suministros to the presidio (La Purísima $578, Sta Inés $164, SB $150, San Buenaventura $984, San Fernando $454 = $2,270), tithes, sealed paper, the company rancho, a tonnage duty on the frigate Guipuzcoana; expenditure $6,725. Shows the missions still bankrolling the presidio in 1833 on the eve of secularization. Orig. pp.131–3.

author: José Rafael González (Sub-Comisario)
summary: Monterey presidio monthly ration/supply requirement: troops by unit (artillery 23, 2º Batallón 35, cavalry 30, Mazatlán 5, plus presidiarios, prisoners, \"neófitos amanuenses\" 8) + foodstuffs (maíz 91½ fanegas, frijol, manteca, carne, cecina, jabón, zapatos, velas) — with a note there was not one blanket left in the storehouse (200 needed). A vivid presidio-poverty/logistics datapoint. Orig. p.166.

author: Fray Juan Cabot
themes: new-mexico; horse-theft; san-miguel; cabot; liquor-trade; neophytes; 1833
summary: The mission's complaint against the New Mexico traders: since the \"Nuevo Mexicanos\" arrived (trading lonas/canvas, buying horses) the mission lost many horses & 108 mules believed driven to New Mexico; details thefts at Rancho Asunción &c.; the traders sell aguardiente/wine to get the neophytes drunk (illicit — the Indians' goods being communal), causing \"muchos trabajos á los misioneros\" (one got the horse-wrangler drunk → thrown, 2 broken ribs). Ringleader Pando captured then jumped bail. The mission-side complement to alcalde Carrillo's reports (Docs 228–229). Orig. pp.470–81.

author: Cmdt. SF / Asesor Rafael Gómez
summary: Causa against Francisco Sánchez (vagrancy + fomenting insubordination among the SF troops, Jan 1834; fiscal Dámaso Rodríguez; the accused recused him). Records Asesor Rafael Gómez's resignation mid-1834 — \"que no se le remitan más sumarios, procesos y expedientes, por no ser ya Asesor ni ejercer la abogacía\" — the end of the moderating asesor's tenure across this volume's criminal docket. Orig. pp.510–11.

author: Fiscal Francisco Araujo
summary: Church-robbery causa: neophytes Verano & Cesáreo robbed the SLO mission church (~20 Jan 1833) — in via the carpentry shop → forge → up the bell tower → down the choir → sacristy, taking 18 valuable ornaments (chasubles, capes, surplices, altar cloths) + carpentry/forge tools; both confessed; ornaments returned under bond by Fray Luis Gil; Cabo Isidro Ibarra of the escort reported it. Orig. pp.543–5.

author: Capt. José Snook (receipt)
themes: maritime; catalina; guy-stone; snook; san-blas; foreign-crew; 1833
summary: Maritime assault: Isidro Núñez, cabin boy of the Mexican brig Catalina, \"hirió alevosamente\" the pilot Guy Stone aboard ship; on Asesor Gómez's advice (28 Feb 1833) the accused was remitted with a sealed record to the naval command at San Blas, embarked 1 May on the same vessel, Capt. Snook receipting custody. Foreign-crewed Mexican shipping + violent-crime-at-sea. Orig. pp.567–8.

author: Juan Antonio Muñoz / Manuel de Alba
summary: Munitions inventory (651 lbs powder, 13,525 musket cartridges, 4,720 gunflints, iron & steel) + artillery pay for the 4 presidios; and Manuel de Alba, \"Cirujano de las fuerzas del territorio\" (the territory's military surgeon — a rare medical-officer reference). Orig. pp.288–320.

author: Romualdo Pacheco (Ayudante de plaza)
themes: privateering; griego-corsair; james-smith; emilia; spanish-war; pacheco; 1828
summary: Deposition re the Spanish corsair Griego (bound out of order): the American James Smith, cook of the schooner Emilia of the Sandwich Islands, had crewed the Spanish privateer Griego which in 1827 captured patriot (Mexican & South American) ships — a sugar brig sent to Manila, a schooner off Acapulco, a sloop off Mazatlán. Smith deposed he was forced aboard at Cádiz, June 1827; taken via interpreter by order of Cmdt. José Estrada. A post-independence privateering datapoint. Orig. pp.1+ (LXXVII, misbound).
Title page: 'Provincial Records Vol. I, 1775–1790 / Eldridge 20'; C-A 22 stamp

author: Neve
summary: 'Índice de cartas dirigidas por el Gobernador Neve al Sup.o Gobierno, por el Paquebot *Concepción*', pp.1–16 (a register index header)

author: Neve
summary: Announces arrival (4th of month) & installation as governor; predecessor Felipe de Barri embarks next day on *Concepción* for San Blas; has received public archives & certain provisions

author: Neve
summary: Reviewed armament of this presidio & others; arms scarce & useless; needs to buy arms, mules & horses for service; requests cloth/clothing & certain vessels, rigging (jarcia) & supplies

author: Neve
summary: Reports delivery of 89 mules for the new establishments

author: Neve
summary: Delivered to P. Benito Cambón the cannons held at Velicatá belonging to the missionaries of the new Monterey establishments; positions taken re mission cattle & caballada

author: Neve
summary: Re Comisaría accounts: complied w/ Art. 5 of Instrucción re accounts of Comisario Francisco Álvarez y Osorio, who asks extension to end of Dec for the settlement

author: Neve
summary: Published the *bando* in respective places on cattle-branding (herraje de ganados)

author: Neve
summary: Published superior order / Art. 18 of Instrucción: no vessel may sail from any port of this dept. without licence; *causa* formed vs. the arráez Juan Caspio[?] for contravening it

author: Neve
summary: Instructed by superior order of 25 May last (to predecessor Barri) on the district margin assigned the Franciscans for peopling missions; will cooperate

author: Neve
summary: Order of 18 Nov last to supply provisions/troops/aid to the new Misión de Ntra Sra del Rosario (founded at Viñaraco[?], P. Francisco Galisteo[?]); 190 gentiles reduced, 200 fanegas maize raised

author: Neve
summary: Acknowledges Real Cédula of 5 Aug 1773 on stipends/sínodos of Religiosos Doctrineros; will comply

author: Neve
summary: Personal letter to a friend: arrived here 4 March after 50 days' navigation

author: Neve
summary: San José de Guadalupe founding narrative (29 Nov 1777) — 14 settlers / 66 souls; see Entry 3. Salutation 'Muy Sr mío'

author: Neve
summary: Formal letter to Viceroy ('Exmo Señor') on land distribution & founding of Pueblo San José (same event, formal version; 'con calidad de reintegro')

author: (register index)
summary: Index for 1790–93 of the Viceroy's *oficios*, compiled by Gov. Diego de Borica — *front-matter index, body elsewhere*

author: (register index)
summary: List of numbers & dates of Neve→Viceroy correspondence — *front-matter index*

author: Neve
summary: RO (10 Nov 1776, recd 2 Aug) to form *padrones* of all settlements; notes province = 4 presidios, 23 missions + Real de Santa Ana, 650 leguas Cabo San Lucas→San Francisco; censuses by presidio officers & parish priests

author: Neve
summary: Acks RO of 14 Jul 1776 to bar the two English corvettes of Capt. Cook from this peninsula's ports; has warned the port commanders — see Entry 4

author: Neve
summary: Received order of 8 Mar w/ arts. 9 & 10 of the Rl Instrucciones for the govt & Comandancia Gral; will observe

author: Neve
summary: Acks Art. 20 of the Instrucciones; communicated to all presidio commanders of his jurisdiction

author: Neve
summary: Replies to order of 8 Mar w/ Art. 4 of the Instrucción Real; understands himself subdelegated the faculties of that article re govt of the province (cont. → n15, ends 31–32)

author: Neve
themes: defense; personnel; missions; indigenous-military; indigenous-demo; construction; fiscal
summary: 'Informe sobre el estado de la Península' — full state-of-province report: garrisons of all 4 presidios, mission escoltas, physical condition, 15 Baja missions listed, livestock shortage, 1775 SD revolt & 1776 San Luis Obispo burning. See Entry 5

author: Neve
summary: Congratulates someone on appointment as Secretary of the Comandancia General

author: Neve
summary: Reports to Com. Gral the founding of the pueblo of San José, enclosing the *padrón de vecinos* + general estados/noticias of the new establishments

author: Gov. [Fages]
summary: Military-discipline circular (under Savage's note 'Documentos truncos y sin dirección que á continuación copio según se encuentran'): *despilfarro* & filth in quarters, arms/clothing uncared-for, arms carried unloaded on guard & marches 'no debe disimularse'; commanders not to use troops/King's servants privately; avoid *familiaridades* eroding subordination; P.D. *forasteros*, bachelors & husbands absent from wives to sleep in the cuartel

author: Gov.
summary: Protocol for receiving the Gobernador–Comandante–Inspector: guard drawn up in line, commander in full uniform, officers' salute, reciprocal gun-salutes at the governor's expense, troop turned out by the Cabo whenever he passes the guardhouse

author: Gov. Fages
summary: Bundle of Rl Órdenes communicated: exclude corporals/sergeants from the deserter-*abono*; European *soldados cumplidos* must return to Spain unless they re-enlist; alcabala exemption (5 yrs, then 5 at half); the *Dieta* on *vínculos*; thanks for *estampilla* signature to the Minister; bando on the *manda* of Gregorio López

author: Gov.
summary: Receipt of the SF review; order that Diego González leave San Francisco

author: Gov.
summary: Order for the 1 May departure of Ignacio Ortega, Francisco ('Curro') Joaquín Higuera, and Juan López

author: Gov.
summary: Circular orders: all officers to have *cueras* (leather armor); no selling aguardiente; no cash to the troops while the ships are in port

author: Gov. Fages
summary: Inspection of accounts: the conduct of Alférez Ramón Lazo (habilitado) varies from his reports; ~200-peso discrepancy pending recheck; the *cotejo* (audit) was made by Hermenegildo Sal on the gov.'s order; balance to run from early 1786 per the Com. Gral's resolutions

author: Gov.
summary: Circular to conserve certain articles for the sick

author: Gov.
summary: On return of accounts for registry, &c.

author: Gov.
summary: Habilitados to take charge of Rl Hacienda product-entries (meat, wine, tobacco, mail/franking, mission & settler seed supplied to presidios) and justify each with accounts; connects company & general habilitación accounts

author: Gov. Fages
summary: On the arrest of 'dicho Ayudante' [identity ambiguous]: now free, but ordered for no pretext to pass before Hermenegildo Sal's house — enter/exit the presidio by the small gate behind the church; must answer in writing for the cause of arrest; finish the review and transfer promptly to Santa Bárbara — see Entry 6

author: Gov.
summary: Method for examining habilitados' accounts: verify the legitimacy of all *partidas* by justifying documents

author: Gov.
summary: Review the Sta Bárbara company (musters of 1785 & 86), then go on to Loreto; report the 4 sickest/most useless soldiers & what is owed them, to discharge; state how much powder is needed for the *repuesto*

author: Gov.
summary: On *permutas* (transfers) of four individuals of the troop

author: Gov. Fages
summary: Arrest & embargo of Alférez Hermenegildo Sal: the Ayudante's review (by gov.'s order) finds Sal descubierto en 2,922 pesos owed to the SF presidio company; orders his arrest, embargo & public auction of all his goods, and to form the *sumaria* — see Entry 6

author: Gov. Pedro Fages (Monterey)
summary: Circular + Bando on the acopio de Pieles de Nutria (sea-otter pelts): per the plan of Commissioner Vicente Vasadre [y Vega] approved by the Real Audiencia, only the Missions (through the padres) and the Indians may collect/trade pelts; all other inhabitants of the Peninsula absolutely prohibited; pelts found in anyone's hands confiscated (decomiso); commanders to publish & enforce — see Entry 7

author: (unstated)
summary: Delivery of the testament of Mariano Carrillo to satisfy creditors; account for all incl. the reales

author: (unstated)
summary: Savage: 'Documento trunco, de ninguna importancia'

author: Gov.
summary: Acks duplicate *Extractos de Revista* & company strength-states for the month

author: Gov.
summary: Receipt of the *arancel* (price-list) of goods held in the presidios

author: Neve
summary: Re cattle to be drawn from Baja missions Sta Gertrudis, Guadalupe, Sn Ignacio, Sn Borja for the new Alta Cal. establishments; encloses a state of the herds available

author: Neve
summary: History of Misión Sta María de los Ángeles (Baja): founded by the Jesuits ~2 yrs before their expulsion; the Franciscans, at the time of Portolá's 1769 Monterey expedition, moved it (with its rancherías) to San Fernando de Velicatá, leaving Sta María depopulated; sited 4 leguas from the pueblo of San Luis

author: Neve
summary: Acks the oficio of 20 Dec last conferring the government of the Peninsula on him *en propiedad*

author: Neve
summary: To deal directly with the San Blas Factor Ramón Manuel de Goya for the supplies this Peninsula needs

author: Neve
summary: José Velázquez to continue as Alférez of this presidio's company at 450 pesos/yr

author: Neve
summary: On separation of the mission *escoltas* & troop increase in this presidio's company; has issued instructions for the necessary recruitment

author: Neve
summary: On whether to permit or abolish the use & *asiento* of mezcal in the Californias; acks the Fiscal's *pedimento* & 9 questions to answer re utility/harm to the Rl Hacienda. *(Savage: doc 'tachado'/crossed out)*

author: Neve
summary: State & progress of the frontier missions (per Alférez José Velázquez's reports of 10/18/20 Mar): Santo Domingo only 2 gentiles baptized — P. García won't baptize 33 women who came wanting to be Christians 'hasta que estén civilizadas é instruidas' (6 fan. wheat, 233 cattle); Ntra Sra del Rosario (Viñadaco) 557 baptized; San Fernando [Velicatá] 1,466 Christian souls in 11 rancherías. *(Savage: doc 'trunco y tachado')*

author: Neve
summary: Forwards the *relación* on missions referenced in the prior oficio

author: Neve
summary: Announces the schooner *Sonora* reached this *rada* on the 5th with provisions, rigging (jarcia), supplies, &c.

author: (unstated)
summary: General adjustment of the company's accrued salaries (per dispatch of 31 Oct), via this presidio's Comisaría

author: Neve
summary: On approval of the instruction of 24 Aug last for the arreglo of this Comisaría's accounts

author: (unstated)
summary: Reports to the Viceroy the licit/supplied drinks used in the Peninsula; answers the 9 questions of the Fiscal's *pedimento* of 9 May 1775 (mezcal follow-up to Doc 52)

author: (unstated)
summary: Sent the Viceroy a copy of the inventory formulary for the missions he drew up, also given to P. Vicente de Mora, per Sup. Orden of 19 Feb

author: Neve
summary: The sloop *Pilar* anchored here the 7th with 3 recruits + 27 sets of arms, saddles & other articles to augment the service force

author: Neve
summary: Acks order of 26 Mar to recruit all possible men here to augment the Monterey & San Diego garrisons; instructions issued

author: Neve
summary: Requests permission to establish a *siembra* and rancho near Viñadaco

author: Neve
summary: Character of the Colorado River Indians & lands, per Fr. Francisco Garcés (who accompanied Lt. Juan Bautista de Anza to the Río Colorado): docility & desire of the riverine nations for padres/Spaniards; lands good for all crops & esp. cattle; flood-prone but with *mesas* fit to settle; area demarcated for the padres — will further 'esta conquista' — see Entry 8

author: Neve
summary: Conchas y perlas: acks the Viceroy's gift of shells; will strive to obtain the pearl he wants, to be sent with an exact relation of how it is extracted and of the pearl-fishery's progress

author: Neve
summary: Forwards a *memoria* of the provisions & effects needed for this presidio

author: (unstated)
summary: Via the launch *Sinaltérea[?]* (for Sta Cruz de Mayo): reports the arrival of the *Concepción* on 4 Aug with cargo from San Blas and the arms sent by Commissioner Manuel Ramón de Goya [name as written; cf. 'Ramón Manuel de Goya' Doc 49]

author: (unstated)
summary: The *Concepción* brought 26 troops, forwarded via the *Pilar* to the port of San Luis and thence overland to San Diego; uniformed & fully armed; *filiaciones* sent to Cmdt. Fernando de Rivera; total cost $1,751.25

author: (unstated)
summary: Acks order of 8 Jun; instructed the Teniente of Santa Ana not to impede the extraction of silver via the port of Mazatlán

author: (unstated)
summary: Acks order of 24 Apr: the President & religiosos urged to fulfill the Visitador's determination to settle the Indians in pueblos

author: (unstated)
summary: Acks circular announcing the Secretaría de Indias conferred on José de Gálvez

author: Neve
summary: Re his march to Monterey: sent the Viceroy 5 letters on the order to move and arrangements to drive the cattle out of these [Baja] missions

author: Neve
summary: Cattle-slaughter for troops/sailors (per Sup. Decreto 14 Jul 1774): no vaqueros/running-horses; no meat supplied for >1 yr; ordered a matanza of 50 bulls at the Real de Sta Ana (Nov) — tasajo cost 10 reales/arroba vs 8 from the missions; recommends placing a southern mission's *Temporalidades* under the governor for meat supply *(vide p.137)*

author: Neve
summary: The launch *Santa Lucía* (sent from San Blas in '1870' [*sic*, = 1770] or 71, chartered to Don Alonso Soldevia[?] 'á quien mató su tripulación' — killed by his crew), found at Saratapá[?] & brought to this *rada*; hull sound but lacking rigging & sails; to be returned to service

author: Neve
summary: Price & weight of a fanega of salt per Gálvez's instruction of 12 Apr 1776: 6 arrobas 6 lbs net; $3.50 at the Real de Sta Ana, $3.00 at La Paz, $2.50 for the missions; orders the fixed prices observed (ending the loose 2-arroba practice)

author: (unstated)
summary: Sent the Viceroy a *memoria* of goods & effects to supply the warehouse

author: (unstated)
summary: Forwarded 14 mission inventories delivered by Fray Vicente de Mora

author: Neve
summary: Forwards to the Rl Tribunal y Audiencia de Cuentas this Comisaría's accounts for the last 6 months of 1775 & 76, delivered by Comisario Francisco Álvarez Osorio

author: Neve
summary: Forwards Rivera y Moncada's report (dated 25 Jan, from San Diego presidio) of the 5 Nov 1775 San Diego mission revolt: Christians & gentiles rose after 1 a.m., attacked & reduced the mission to ashes, killing P. Luis [Jayme] ('cuyo apellido no se expresa'), a blacksmith & a carpenter; Anza rushed to San Gabriel; various Indians taken prisoner; dispatched via the *Pilar* to San Blas — see Entry 9

author: Neve
summary: Alférez Don Antonio Crespo returns to his corps for treatment, transported to San Blas on the *Pilar*

author: Neve
summary: The nave de Filipinas (Manila galleon) arrived 24 Dec on the coast at San José del Cabo; that mission & Santiago supplied it cattle & provisions, paid in goods (per the enclosed list); asks whether the value received should enter the Comisaría

author: (unstated)
summary: Formulary for the regulation of the Loreto missions, agreed with P. Vicente de Mora (per Viceroy's order of 19 Feb 1776), communicated to the missions; original sent to the Viceroy

author: Neve
summary: Instructions given to Capt. Fernando de Rivera y Moncada for command of the Loreto presidio (departs the 3rd); held the 6 frontier-escort soldiers in these establishments to aid San Diego

author: Neve
summary: Judicial aftermath of the SD revolt: Lt. José de Ortega's inquiry (4 Mar) proves a second *convocación* of 21 rancherías by Manuel, Christian captain of the Soledad ranchería (1 legua from Mission SD), + 3 accomplices — arrested & confessed; prisoners told they deserved death but pardoned in the King's name (prison deemed sufficient), warned reoffense = death; troops & prisoners acclaimed the King, two cannon fired ('tienen mucho miedo los Indios'); released to the padre to be admonished, fed & sent home — see Entry 9

author: Neve
summary: Arrival of the frigate *Santiago* under Lt. de Navío Ignacio Arteaga (anchored SF 12 May, delivered grain, left 27th, anchored at Monterey 28th) & the packet *Príncipe* at San Diego 9 May, with provisions/goods

author: Neve
summary: The *Concepción* has made three mule transports; recommends buying 100 more

author: Neve
summary: Reports the affair with the gentiles of El Encino and those near Mission Santa Clara

author: Neve
summary: Acks the bandos prohibiting speaking, disputing or writing about the extinción de la Compañía [de Jesús — Jesuit suppression]

author: Neve
summary: Acks to Melchor de Peramás the bandos of 19 & 23 Oct on the prórroga of the extracción de moneda (coin export)

author: Neve
summary: Encloses the warehouse-keepers' *memorias* of articles needed for subsistence in 1778

author: Neve
summary: Per Sup. Orden of 23 Oct: the Nave de Filipinas (Manila galleon) is to call ANNUALLY at Monterey or San Francisco and be supplied víveres; has taken all measures, with observations on the cattle on hand to provision the ship

author: Neve
summary: Cattle-branding: communicated to Antonio Otto[?] the 1-yr extension granted for branding his herd at the Real de Sta Ana; stipend (sínodos) payment ordered for Isidro Harzabal[?], cura of the Real de Sta Ana

author: Neve
summary: The causa of Lorenzo Macapa, neophyte Indian of Mission Viñadaco (Rosario): per Sup. Orden of 20 Nov 1776, sent a copy to Alférez José Velázquez to conduct the ordered *diligencias*

author: Neve
summary: Acks the appointment of Brigadier Caballero [Teodoro] de Croix as Comandante General of the Provincias Internas (this province + Sonora, Sinaloa, Nueva Vizcaya, Nuevo México, Coahuila, Texas); henceforth to deal with him directly on govt matters, though provisions/effects still to be requested from the Viceroy's government

author: Neve
summary: Report on Missions, Presidios & other matters (answering instruction chapters): on arrival the missions San Juan Capistrano, Santa Clara & 'Sta Ynes'[?] [*sic* — Sta Inés not founded until 1804; likely misreading/Savage error] were founded; Santa Clara 10 leguas from SF presidio; livestock to missions (1 horse SD; 2 mules SJ Capistrano; 2 San Gabriel; 1 Carmelo; 4 mules + 2 horses Santa Clara; 3 horses + 1 mule SF); no land distribution except one volunteer soldier titled a plot at Carmelo by Rivera; *siembras* unfeasible near presidios for lack of land/water; SD troop-increase (from the reduced SJ Capistrano escort, now 19 plazas) to be suspended as the local gentilidad is unquiet; escolta strength 12 SD / 11 SJ Capistrano / 9 San Gabriel; SF received the *lienzo del Santo*, bell & ornaments for the presidio chapel; ⭐ Neve observed the conduct of the soldier Hermenegildo Sal, named SF guarda-almacén by Rivera, but couldn't judge his fidelity (he'd only handled ration distribution)

author: Neve
themes: defense; missions; indigenous-military; indigenous-demo; construction; exploration
summary: 'Sobre la situación de las Rancherías de Gentiles' — the Santa Barbara Channel establishment plan: Canal runs ~24–26 leguas, 8,000–10,000 gentiles; proposes central Fuerte + 3rd Mission 'Santa Bárbara' plus Missions San Buenaventura & Purísima Concepción; total 1 Tte, 3 sgts, 2 cabos, 62 soldados; SF fort garrison set at 1 Tte, 1 Alférez, 2 sgts, 4 cabos, 34 soldados; detachments from Monterey/Santiago/SF companies; best fort site near Mescaltitán; harbor recon (ref. Tte. de Fragata Diego Choquet[?], anchored near the 3 Channel towns in 1776) — see Entry 10

author: Neve
summary: Acks circular (Sept 76): the King approved the restoration of the Junta de Justicia for contraband cases & contentious matters of the Renta de Tabacos

author: Neve
summary: Reserved order (of 23 Oct prev.): the King has notice that two corvettes under the English Capt. Cook left London 'á hacer nuevos descubrimientos,' presumed (per earlier news of 23 Mar) to head for 'el mar del Sur y costas septentrionales de Cal.a' — the substantive warning behind Doc 19 (cf. Entry 4)

author: Neve
summary: Two brief items Savage marks '(Sin importancia)'

author: Neve
summary: Recommends raising the salary of José Joaquín Moraga, Lt. of the SF presidio, and the Lt. of San Diego, from 700 to 1,000 pesos with double rations/yr

author: Neve
summary: 'Borradores de Comunicaciones' (drafts) — Savage's date-list cluster: (Jun 4, 2 docs) remove Francisco Álvarez Osorio as Comisario of Loreto for non-compliance, name José María Estrada Guarda Almacén ($818/yr); an ill official resigns after 7 yrs at the Colegios de Zacatecas admin, asks transfer to Seville to rejoin family separated since 1764; + (Jun 6, 3 docs; Jun 8, 1; Sept 19, 2) and 1788 (Apr 15, 2; Apr 13, 1) — most trivial/listed only

author: Neve
summary: Presidio construction: Monterey — 2 *lienzos* of 120 varas raised, 3 corner *baluartes* at 3 varas (cannon emplacements), walls of stone; San Diego & San Francisco to be adobe on stone foundations

author: Neve
summary: Acks that on 25 Dec the King conferred the Comandancia General of this Peninsula & other provinces [Croix's appointment; cf. Doc 93]

author: (unstated)
summary: Suministros para Presidios — *vestuarios* & *monturas* spec: Monterey black-taffeta cravats, blue-cloth *chupas* w/ crimson collar & brass buttons, blue breeches, hats, *tahalíes* (one embroidered for the sergeant), 21-round cartridge-boxes in red Moscovia leather, 40 vaquera saddle-frames + cuirasses/fittings; San Diego 68 cravats + 35 saddle-frames; San Francisco 36 cravats + 20 saddle-frames — see Entry 11

author: (unstated)
summary: Armas y Municiones per Reglamento: Monterey (45 men) 45 ea swords/muskets/pistol-pairs/lances, 135 lb powder (@3 lb/man/yr), 6,480 balls + reserve 360 lb (@8 lb)/17,280; San Diego 44 ea, 204 lb, 7,792 (+ reserve 69 ea/544 lb/26,112; 25 plazas already armed); San Francisco 16 ea exc. 36 pistol-pairs, 105 lb, 5,040 (+ reserve 36 ea/280 lb/13,440; 20 armed); NOTE: no armeros in these presidios — 2 needed for repairs — see Entry 11

author: (unstated)
summary: Estado de Compañías de Presidios (muster + equipment): Monterey — Sgt José Robles, 5 cabos, 69 sol; 42 swords/54 lances/50 muskets/10 pistol-pairs/54 cueras/52 adargas/53 saddles/90 horses/124 mules. San Diego — Tte José Francisco Ortega, Sgt Mariano Carrillo, 5 cabos, 45 sol; 44/48/49/11/47/49/52/50/107. San Francisco — Tte José Moraga, Sgt Pablo Grijalva, 4 cabos, 28 sol; 15/22/30/1/26/19/30/67/48. Loreto — no memoria, 'en buen estado.' Each presidio has 2 four-pounder cannon — see Entry 11

author: (unstated)
summary: Estado de Armamento pedido (1778): Monterey 45 men need 45 ea + reserve; SD 69 men need 44 ea + 69 reserve; SF 36 men need 16 ea (exc. 36 pistol-pairs) + 36 reserve

author: Neve
summary: On remitting arms to the presidios

author: Neve
summary: Thanks the King for the grade of Colonel (vide p.164)

author: Neve
summary: [2 docs] A *pliego* returned, opened by mistake; will form new mission inventories

author: Neve
summary: The packet *San Carlos* reached San Diego on 16 May with provisions for that presidio

author: Neve
summary: Forwarding 'su renuncia de Gobernador' — a resignation as Governor [whose is unstated; possibly Rivera y Moncada as Lt.-Gov. — *unconfirmed*]

author: Neve
summary: Cost & delivery of livestock from Álamos: 200 mules + 100 horses bought, only 163 mules & 75 horses arrived (rest lost/died); cost rose $22→$34/mule, $8→$20/horse w/ expenses; 79 mules + 10 horses to these presidios (+45 lately to SD, mounting 25 recruits); troops charged 150% recargo = $70/mule, $51/horse 'insoportable' — recommends charging only $25/mule, $12/horse

author: Neve
summary: *Varios asuntos*: Santiago reached Monterey 31 Jul; order forbidding the governor to change residence; the presidio's few troops delay dispatches to Antigua Cal. (7-soldier parties to SD; >500 leguas); re Loreto presidio

author: Neve
summary: [list cluster] Cattle for the Nao de Filipinas & accounts of Comisario Juan Soler; ⭐ *estupro* (rape) of 2 Indian girls (p.141); instrucción y cuentas; Santiago's arrival at SF (p.142); accounts (pp.143–145)

author: Neve
summary: [cluster, Aug 5–8] Comisario Álvarez Osorio to render accounts or pay $200 fine; general troop-salary *ajuste*; RO of 21 Mar 1775 changing the Reglamento + on building & good treatment of friendly/neighboring nations; marginal notes/index on communications; bando approving the Cuerpo y Tribunal de Minería

author: Neve
summary: [cluster] Bando reducing gold duties to 3% (quinto in America) / 2% (entering Spain); bando ordering surgeons to treat the wounded immediately before reporting to justice; the *San Carlos* reached SD (Aug 10) with provisions

author: Neve
summary: Monterey presidio wall & bastions FINISHED 3 July 1778 — 537 varas circumference, 4 varas high, 1½ thick, 'de piedra el todo de su fábrica'; 3 adobe houses (7×8 v.) built, guard & barracks under construction (34 v. front, double adobe); SF fence rising; SD gathering foundation stones

author: Neve
summary: Pueblo de San José: 26½ fanegas maize sown, most lost (no water until 12 Jul); Lt. José Moraga commissioned to settle the pobladores; bad land-distribution near Mission Santa Clara — reordered; ⭐ the mission *Ministros*, esp. P. Junípero Serra, oppose the civil pueblos (Serra's plan that the missions supply the presidios), which Neve rejects as failing 'el poblar estas tierras con vasallos útiles' — see Entry 12

author: Neve
summary: Lacking arms, asked the commander of the *Santiago* for 6 musket-and-sabre sets; asks that the requested arms be remitted

author: Neve
summary: [cluster] Request for arms (×2); to replace the Monterey guarda-almacén Juan Soler; thanks for the favorable report 'sobre su renuncia' [whose resignation unstated — *unconfirmed*; cf. Doc 111], announcing the presidio-wall construction (already extracted, Doc 117)

author: Neve
summary: [cluster] Remission of accounts; order to the Loreto teniente; *diligencias* for Juan Soler's guarda-almacén accounts; ⭐ difficulties with the *Santiago*'s Teniente over delivery of *pliegos* (pp.169–72); memorias for presidio bastimento shipments; thanks

author: Neve
summary: [cluster] Re Lt. José Moraga's *despacho*; governor's receipts; arrivals of the *San Carlos* (SD) & *Santiago* (SF); recommends Lt. Moraga's *solicitud*

author: Neve
summary: [cluster] Instructions to the Loreto captain; index of letters to the Com. Gral; ⭐ encloses padrones (censuses) of the province's settlements + general news (Apr 15); triplicate service records for 1777; ⭐ annual Nao de Filipinas call (28 Oct at San José del Cabo) — the Loreto mission padre gave 3 California Indian boys to the ship's officers; order on soldiers' wills

author: Neve
summary: On 13 Aug [1777] a party returning from San Diego was attacked by gentiles — the corporal killed & 2 soldiers wounded (= the Antonio Briones killing; cf. Doc 127)

author: Neve
summary: (Original) — already extracted (vide Sav. p.95)

author: Neve
summary: [5 docs] ⭐⭐ The King has NOT accepted 'his' resignation of the government — asks that the *memoriales* sent to that end not be forwarded [subject still unstated — cf. Docs 111/120; outcome now known: refused]; Montepío *ingresos* order; ⭐ bando prohibiting the purchase from the troops of service articles (anti-corruption); copies re the resignation

author: Neve
summary: [2 docs] ⭐ Arrest of the Indian captain of the rancherías of the Arroyo de San Juan Bautista for the attack on the SD-returning party that killed Cabo Antonio Briones & wounded 2 soldiers; + desertion of soldiers from SD & SF 'llevándose las bestias y armas'

author: Neve
summary: On the Reglamento governing this Peninsula (genesis: RO 21 May 1775 to revise the defective Reglamento) + full comparative establishment/budget for all 4 presidios ('Conforme al Reglamento' vs 'Actual pié'), mission escoltas, the Loreto Departamento de Marina (balandra *Pilar*, launches *Sta Lucía* & *San Juan Nepomuceno*; ~$16,393), mission *sínodos*, the Resumen General (4 presidios $41,793 vs $55,385; GRAND TOTAL $62,520 vs $76,105 as-is), the 150%/100% recargo critique, a per-soldier cost ($439.7.1¾) + ration schedules, and a discursive Informe (frontier service; 'solo 3 que casaron con Indias naturales' count as settlers); ends 'propone varias otras modificaciones al reglamento… pp.194–225' — the budget core of the Reglamento de 1779 — see Entry 13

author: Neve
summary: Forwards the accounts of the SD guarda-almacén Rafael de Pedro y Gil (1777) with a shortfall of $5,617.7t.7¼g

author: Neve
summary: [4 docs] víveres/armas; officer appointments; presidio horses & mules; ⭐ proceso against 3 soldiers for the *estupro* (rape) of 2 Indian girls

author: Neve
summary: Forwards the proceso against soldier Diego Ruiz for drawing his sword against Cabo Raymundo Carrillo

author: Neve
summary: Reports the gentilidad quiet; the death & arrest of deserters

author: Neve
summary: [5 docs] ⭐ Recommends Hermenegildo Sal as Guarda Almacén of the SF presidio; that Juan Soler's resignation as Monterey guarda-almacén be accepted; cattle purchase for the Nave de Filipinas; arrest/proceso of deserters; Juan Soler's accounts

author: Neve
summary: *Causa* against soldier Juan Miguel Camacho (Loreto presidio) for incest with his aunt María Guadalupe

author: Neve
summary: Election of Indian *alcaldes* & *regidores* in the missions — San Carlos de Monterey & San Diego 2 each; San Antonio, San Luis & San Gabriel 1 each — 'según el n.o de familias de Indios naturales que cada una tiene'

author: Neve
summary: On the missionaries' rations

author: Neve
summary: Remits accounts

author: Neve
summary: *Ajuste* of livestock prices

author: Neve
summary: [cluster] Handover of the Loreto Comisaría from Álvarez Osorio to José M.ª Estrada (Mar 27); accounts of Rafael de Pedro y Gil & Juan Soler; 'remitió Reglamento'; sueldos; surplus goods in warehouses; recommends employing certain persons; ⭐ requests an Inspector de Presidios to serve as Ayudante to the Governor (Apr 3); deserter procesos

author: Neve
summary: Approval of the founding of the Santa Barbara Channel Presidio & 3 Missions and the two pueblos (on the Guadalupe & Porciúncula rivers) — confirms the 1777 Channel plan (Entry 10); 2 religiosos per Channel mission; only 1 cajón of ornaments exists (for San Buenaventura) — detailed church-inventory (cáliz, copón, vinajeras, Santos Óleos vessels, plata para bautizar, 5 casullas of the 5 colors, 2 campanas…); the 3 Channel missions to be paid $3,000 in Mexico to the Síndico of the Colegio de San Fernando; San Luis & San Gabriel can supply 1,000 fanegas maize

author: Neve
summary: *Sobre ganados* — livestock census: at end of Dec 1778, 156 mares + 507 head cattle + 124 at SF = 631 head of royal *ganado mayor* (excl. this year's calves); the presidio's *yeguada* suffices, with the 13 San José settlers supplied; bring 140 brood mares (80 tamed) + donkeys to stock the new pueblo & each presidio; ⭐ requests 40 settler-farmers (some who can make plows) + a mason, each bringing 2 mules & 1 horse

author: Neve
summary: *Desertores, robo de bestias y daños por las aguas*: 5 frontier soldiers deserted with 22 beasts from the Sto Domingo mission/escolta; the Jan–Feb 1779 rains knocked down the SF presidio's new adobe walls (forcing a rebuild in stone) & collapsed most of the warehouse palisade & casamata; Mission Santa Clara & the San José pueblo flooded (the dam carried off, wheat drowned); at SD & Monterey no house free of leaks

author: Neve
summary: [cluster] Lt. Moraga's account adjustment; ⭐ gratification for soldiers working on presidio construction; deserters; Juan Soler's bonds (fianzas); the *causa* of Ávila & others

author: Neve
summary: *Inventarios de Misiones* — the missionaries refuse to give the governor mission inventories: Serra (1775) excused himself saying he sent them direct, and later told Neve in person he had a *precepto de obediencia* from his Guardián 'de no dar informe ni noticia alguna de las Misiones al Gobernador'; Neve reads in this the friars' 'espíritu… de mantener las Misiones y sus naturales con total inhibición del conocimiento' of government — see Entry 14

author: Neve
summary: That José M.ª Góngora[?] is not promoted

author: Neve
summary: On the Comandante General's *visita* (inspection)

author: Neve
summary: Forwards the Nuevo Reglamento de Gobierno

author: Neve
summary: *Misiones de la Antigua California*: the plan to settle the Baja Indians in pueblos (lots, lands, cattle-roundups so they maintain themselves) — entrusted to Rivera y Moncada & Pres. Vicente de Mora, who 'con errada inteligencia' moved families to San José del Cabo & Todos Santos; ethnography of Santiago (only 70 souls), SJ del Cabo, Todos Santos, Comondú, Mulegé — fed from royal cattle, working fields only a few days then dispersing to the hills (vandijas, roots, wild fruit), at the mission only on feast days; 'de esta manera no pueden civilizarse y ser útiles al Estado'

author: Neve
summary: 3 docs Savage marks 'sin interés'

author: Neve
summary: Requesting copies of the Reglamento

author: Neve
summary: [4 docs] Rl Cédula on agents/solicitadores for Indies business; ⭐ Cédula prohibiting the book 'Año 2440' [Mercier's *L'An 2440*]; bando to collect old coin; bando naming an Apartador Gral de Oro y Plata

author: Neve
summary: [4 docs] ⭐ José M.ª Estrada named Comisario of Loreto; duplicate despatches; officer salaries; *Santiago* arrival with provisions

author: Neve
summary: [4 docs] employees' bonds; ⭐ embarkation of Fernando de Rivera y Moncada; arms request; livestock transport

author: Neve
summary: [2 docs] ⭐ Death of soldier José Antonio Espinosa[?] killed by Cabo Alejo Antonio Duarte; confirmation of Indians

author: Neve
summary: *Expedición al Norte* of the frigates *Favorita* & *Princesa* (Lt. Ignacio Arteaga cmdg.): reached 61°4′, could not make 70° as wished, retreated to 55° (long. W of San Blas); the Russians place the passage at 43–44°; explored Puerto de Bucareli (discovered at 55° on the prior expedition by Juan de la Bodega y Quadra), an archipelago 'lleno de buenos puertos'; brought back 3 boys & 2 girls + native artifacts; >7 months' navigation, crews scurvy-stricken — see Entry 15

author: Neve
summary: [4 docs] víveres for Loreto; ⭐ recommends the retirement of Capt. Fernando de Rivera y Moncada; suspension of confirmations; same re *moneda*

author: Neve
summary: Announces the entry of the frigate *San José* into the Carmelo *ensenada*

author: Neve
summary: That the first alférez fill the vacant Teniente post of the Loreto presidio

author: (from Arizpe, sin dirección)
summary: Order [from Croix's HQ at Arizpe] to set Loreto Orduño at liberty — *(1782 insertion; date/place jump)*

author: Neve
summary: Relación: to put the 4 California presidios' troops in order, 350 mules & 150 horses are needed

author: Neve
summary: To augment the presidios' forces: 2 tenientes, 4 alférez, 3 sargentos, 2 cabos, 54 soldados

author: Neve
summary: Armament needs for Monterey, SD, SF & the Canal de Sta Bárbara: 59 swords, 59 lance-heads, 299 muskets, 59 pistol-pairs, 1,528 lb powder, 10,185 balls, 2 four-pounder cannon, 740 cartridges, 6,000 gunflints; SF has 1,659 lb surplus powder

author: (Rosario)
summary: Duplicate relación of cattle taken from the old [Baja] missions — *[cf. Prov. State Papers Benicia Military I, pp.804–5; Savage cuadernos pp.44–5]*

author: Neve (certif.)
summary: Relación/certification of 152 head of *Diezmo* cattle bought at Loreto (Pres. Vicente de Mora) by Capt. Rivera, to be moved to Monterey to supply the Nao de Filipinas — $562

author: Neve
summary: Certification re the cattle the Baja missions could supply (per 9 May 1776) for the new Monterey establishments — $1,209 per the arancel

author: Neve
summary: [cluster, truncated] A duplicate/truncated comm (cf. pp.52–53); a 1776-01-07 Loreto trunc. (horses & maize); a trunc. announcing the *Concepción*'s arrival with oxen[?]; ⭐ to a friend — sending the Viceroy a box of books, two of them surviving 'del tiempo de los Regulares Expulsos' (the expelled Jesuits), plus shells

author: Neve
summary: *Ganados*: cattle taken from the old missions for the new establishments ($1,209); routed via Mission Viñadaco to rest; 80 mules + 36 horses from Loreto to the frontier to mount 25 recruits for the SD garrison

author: Neve
summary: Sends triplicate service records

author: Neve
summary: *Armamento y caballerías*: arrived Monterey the 3rd; en route inspected the SD garrison & mission escorts — ⭐ uniforms/arms/saddles 'en deplorable estado,' no uniformity, arms of unequal calibre; the 3 companies (147 plazas, excl. officers) have only 338 beasts (many old/useless); needs 200 mules + 300 horses

author: Neve
summary: *Fundación de Misiones*: on arrival, Mission San Diego already rebuilt & San Juan Capistrano's founding concluded (both *terrado*-roofed after the 1775 burning); the Presidio of San Francisco & Mission Dolores occupied 27 Jul [1776] (mission ½ legua from the fort by the Laguna de los Dolores); Mission Santa Clara sited 4 Jan, 15 leguas SE on the Guadalupe river; SF presidio still 'barracas é chozas' w/ no defense but the adobe commander's house & warehouse (nearly collapsed) — to be walled per the Reglamento

author: Neve
summary: [2 docs] SD food shortage relieved with grain from Missions San Gabriel & San Luis; ⭐ requests a troop & escolta increase to better contain the Indians

author: Neve
summary: 13 Indians imprisoned at SD presidio as principal instigators of the SD mission revolt, + 2 gentiles at Monterey complicit in the burning of Mission San Luis [Obispo]; asks the punishment they should suffer be determined (cf. Entry 9)

author: Neve
summary: Mail via the Real de los Álamos + a launch to Sta Cruz de Mayo, receivable/answerable monthly

author: Neve
summary: Arrival of the *Concepción* with provisions

author: Neve
summary: Acks the order for the Sonora purchase of 200 mules & 100 horses & its transport

author: Neve
summary: Memoria of the armament this presidio's company needs (Savage: *tachado*)

author: Neve
summary: Ship news (barco at SD, not yet Monterey); receipt of 89+25 mules & cattle to SD (via P. Vicente de Mora); ⭐ Indian attacks — Lt. Ortega: the mail-soldiers from Viñadaco attacked by gentiles Santa Ysabel→El Mégano[?], pursued 30 leguas (order: no mail with fewer than 5 men); the SD *recua* & escort attacked by gentiles in the Sta Bárbara Channel (cost lives); ⭐ Ortega's intel that 'mucha gente, bestias y ganado' have come up the Río Colorado building houses (the Anza expedition)

author: Neve
summary: Rl Cédula: the Vicaría & Curato of the Real de Sta Ana conferred by José de Gálvez on Presbítero Isidro Ibarzábal ($365/yr) + the cura's payment petition [= the 'Harzabal' of Doc 91]

author: Neve
summary: Asks that salary & rations be assigned to Alférez José Velázquez (not included in the Reglamento)

author: Neve
summary: Comisario Álvarez Osorio's general account of troop pay & rations from 1 Jan 1776; rations at $4/mo paid in goods with 100% recargo; marina & maestranza total $4,088/yr

author: Neve
summary: Re the 200 Sonora mules/100 horses — only those applied to the troop to be discounted, at $20/mule, $12/horse (the troop not to bear the losses)

author: Neve
summary: *Misiones &c.*: reconnaissance (May–June) of the 5 Baja missions + the Real de Sta Ana — too poor to maintain their natives (except Todos Santos); failure to settle the Indians in pueblos per Gálvez's 1769 decree; no operarios; recommends a Padre Presidente 'separado del espíritu de facción, comercio y tráfico'; the Indians cannot be made 'vasallos útiles' — *closely parallels the 1779 version, Doc 148*

author: Neve
summary: *Ramos de Rl Hacienda* — the peninsula's royal-revenue branches: tobacco, azogue (mercury), powder, naipes, gold/silver quintos & duties, pearl quintos, salt *estanco*, bulas, papel sellado, *forasteros* tax; the pearl-quinto yields only ~$200/yr, this year from just 2 canoes diving the far coast

author: Neve
summary: Per Art. 21 of his instruction — the Rl Hacienda accounts of the Comisaría of Sta Ana del Sur; no silver production credited; the account to be formed by the dept.'s first comisario, Manuel Espinosa de los Monteros

author: Neve
summary: [Sta Ana mines/Hacienda, under Lic. Joaquín Velázquez [de León: comisario Bernardo Moreno y Castro received 386 marcos of silver from Espinosa; with additions, 1,408 marcos total remitted to Guadalajara (16 Oct, via the paquebot *Concepción*) to Factor de Rentas José de Trigo; pearl-quinto history (Manuel de Ossio, comisionado 1745)

author: Neve
summary: *Avalúo* of the Hacienda & Minas at the Real de Sta Ana = $1,193, abandoned 3 years & 'aterradas' (collapsed/flooded)

author: Neve
summary: Reports the 1-month imprisonment of Francisco Juan Carpio for setting sail without the governor's licence [= the arráez 'Juan Caspio[?]' of Doc 9]

author: Neve
summary: $666 of Mission Santiago de las Coras held by Guadalajara merchant Antonio de Mena (per P. Antonio Salas)

author: Neve
summary: The 34-plaza Loreto company is insufficient to garrison its posts & escorts; asks an increase to 44 plazas

author: Neve
summary: Dispatch of a pliego to Rivera y Moncada, cmdr. of the new establishments

author: Neve
summary: A site reconnoitered 1½ days from Viñadaco, named Santo Domingo, for a new mission (water, lands, gentilidad 'muy aceptable')

author: Neve
summary: 2 vessels built for the missions (7- & 9-vara keels; the missions have 5)

author: Neve
summary: 528 baptized at the Velicatá & Viñadaco missions

author: Neve
summary: Remission of arms to Loreto & San José del Cabo

author: Neve
summary: (Savage: 'Extractada, vide p.85')

author: Neve
summary: *Causa* against soldiers Francisco Ávila, Sebastián Alvitre & Mateo Soto for the *estupro* (rape) of two young girls [names the 'estupro de 2 inditas' / 'Causa de Ávila' of Docs 114/130/143]

author: Neve
summary: On the decline of California cattle; fines for killing cows that are not barren (*macheras*) or old

author: Neve
summary: (Savage: 'Extractada, vide p.154')

author: Neve
summary: Imprisonment of the Christian Indian Lorenzo Macapa, who at Viñadaco killed his pregnant wife by kicking her belly [= the Macapa causa of Doc 92]

author: Neve
summary: Remits the 12 original Jesuit-era account-books of this Comisaría + a legajo (Nos. 1–46, 359 fojas); no copyists to duplicate

author: Neve
summary: Arsenal effects received; the balandra *Pilar* being careened

author: Neve
summary: Lt. Ortega reports (31 Oct) the founding of Mission San Juan Capistrano between SD & San Gabriel (23 leguas from the presidio, 19 from SG); Frays Fermín Francisco de Lasuén & Gregorio Amurrio assigned by Serra; cattle received

author: Neve
summary: Founding of Mission Santo Domingo (Baja): on 30 Aug 1775 PP. Manuel García & Miguel Hidalgo arrived with cattle, with Alférez José Velázquez & the escort; establishment begun

author: Neve
summary: Damages suffered by the *Concepción* transporting mules from the Isla de Lobos

author: (register indices)
summary: Index fragments copied by Savage: 'en tiempo de Borica 1794–1800' (381–82); 'Legajo de Papeles Antiguos' 1773–80 (382–88); 'Índice á correspondencias de Vireyes á Gobernadores' 1776–96 (389–403); a Borica index of viceroy→governor correspondence 1776–88 & 1790–93 (404–7)

author: Neve
summary: [2 docs] Remits the accounts of Juan Soler (Monterey guarda-almacén) & ⭐ Hermenegildo Sal (SF guarda-almacén)

author: Neve
summary: 2 letters 'de ningún interés'

author: Neve
summary: [2 docs] *Santiago* arrival with víveres; requesting clothing & effects for the presidios

author: Neve
summary: [2 docs] On guarda-almacén surpluses

author: Neve
summary: Return of the frigates *Princesa* & *Favorita* to SF (14–15 Sept) from the discovery expedition [cf. Entry 15]

author: Neve
summary: Lt. Joaquín Cañete (Loreto) reports a shortage of víveres

author: Neve
summary: The royal-birth *indulto* applied to Francisco Serrano, deserter of the Regimiento de Dragones de España

author: Fages
summary: [cluster] Silver/mercury pricing for the Loreto habilitado; Antonio Miranda dropped as settler → enters as soldier; Missions San José del Cabo & Santiago de las Coras exempt from a $3,500 payment; instructions to Alférez José M.ª Estrada (Comisionado of the Visita del Sur); ⭐ to curb troop drunkenness, habilitados not to pay mission claims vs. soldiers/sailors unless in credit — under which the missions may sell wine & aguardiente

author: Fages
summary: [5 docs] Tobacco provision; calafate José de la Cruz → soldier; Patrón Diego Pérez retirement refused; extra pay to launch-patrón Salvador González; Sgt Espinosa accounts; ⭐ harvest-notes for the pueblos of San José de Guadalupe & La Reina de los Ángeles

author: Fages
summary: [4 docs] ⭐ the launch *Sta Lucía* sailed for the Río Yaqui carrying José Joaquín de Arrillaga; padrón of Mission San José del Cabo; soldier Hermenegildo Flores deserted 12 Jan (pursuit blocked by 10 days' rain)

author: Fages
summary: *Diezmos*: tithe question at Pueblo San José — do the settlers' 5-yr exemption cover the agregado soldiers? Named: Cabo Valerio Mesa, Felipe Tapia, Manuel Higuera, Severino Lugo

author: Fages
summary: Pueblo de la Reina de los Ángeles guard: per the Reglamento the SD company posts 4 soldiers (first 2 yrs); soldier Vicente Feliz entrusted with command of the pueblo 'por su inteligencia'; escort brothers Roque & Antonio de Cota (raised 200 fan. maize last year, old w/ large families); San José escort: Felipe Tapia, Manuel Higuera, Severino Lugo

author: Fages
summary: Carpenters Manuel Rodríguez & Francisco Murillo and blacksmith Gregorio Segura request their retirement

author: Fages
summary: [2 docs] Tithes on soldiers' cattle; royal-cattle account — 30 colts collected (10 Monterey, 10 SD, 8 SF)

author: Fages
summary: Account of the San José settlers' cattle

author: Fages
summary: [4 docs acks]

author: Fages
summary: [2 docs] RO thanking the vassals for the war *donativo*; order that no inhabitant pass to Spain without royal approval

author: Fages
summary: Order for presidio inventories

author: Fages
summary: 'Relación de los empleados políticos' — 1784 officer roster of the Californias (names/posts/seniority): Gov. Lt.Col. Pedro Fages (Monterey, 1y4m); Capt. José Arrillaga (Loreto); Tte José Ortega (Frontera de Loreto, 1.º); Tte Diego González & Tte José Zúñiga (San Diego); Tte José Moraga (SF, 1.º); Tte Felipe Goycoechea (Sta Bárbara); Ayte Mayor Antonio Marrón[?] (Dep. del Sur); notes Felipe de Neve is now Comandante General of the Provincias Internas — see Entry 16

author: Fages
summary: [4 docs] Archive of papers; prevent P. José Naranjo from leaving; SD habilitación paid $118.4 for 1,896 packs of cigarettes; royal-cattle *matanza* for presidios (hides & tallow for the Rl Hacienda)

author: Fages
summary: Instructed to preserve the *yeguada* (mare-herd)

author: Fages
summary: [4 docs] Royal cédulas: a $500/yr life-annuity (*rentas vitalicias*) fund in Mexico; ⭐ the marriage cédulas — a child marrying without paternal consent cannot inherit from the mother; sons over 25 must seek paternal counsel or the Juez Judicial (Real Pragmática de 23 Mar 1776); the mining district under the Rl Caja del Rosario

author: Fages
summary: [4 docs] ⭐ The King assigns Fages $5,000 as Comandante Inspector de Presidios + the full governor's salary; cédula (13 Nov 1778) to examine the Dominican padres present under Pres. Vicente de Mora; the Loreto cmdr armed the lancha *Sto Domingo* (víveres shortage); the pearl-diving (buceo de perlas) lease

author: Fages
summary: [4 docs] ⭐ All quiet, abundant water, pueblo/mission crops in good state; ⭐ P. Junípero Serra paid the $15 he owed Mission San Carlos; orchard & garden planted at the old huerta; San José tithes $428.4.1

author: Fages
summary: [2 docs] Cabo Pinto (SF mason) died — soldier Juan Álvarez (SD) to take the office; cattle-matanza provisions

author: Fages
summary: *Franquicias de pliegos*: postal franking for officials (per Viceroy Mayorga's 1780 instruction); Penas de Cámara / Gastos de Justicia fund; + ⭐ a bando de buen gobierno: fines rather than costly imprisonment for drunkenness, gambling & brawls

author: Fages
summary: Order to Ayte Insp. Nicolás Soler to license soldier Leonardo Verdugo

author: Fages
summary: Bando extending the collection of old coin

author: Fages
summary: [3 docs] Promotion of soldier Manuel Vargas; tithes to be handled via Intendant Pedro Corbalán & paid in coin; a Monterey man sentenced to shackled hard labor on presidio works for wounding another; ⭐ warning to soldier José González & blacksmith José Segura over prohibited gambling

author: Fages
summary: Goycoechea reports the Channel gentiles at first refused the work (paid in sacked wheat) but now respond well — Chumash labor on the Santa Barbara presidio works

author: Fages
summary: Remits a *relación de plata beneficiada* by Antonio de Osio

author: Fages
summary: The San José pueblo dam carried off by the river — to rebuild it of *cal y canto*; San José's settlers more negligent than those of Los Ángeles; ⭐ Mission Santa Clara's adobe church completed (timbered roof); ⭐ death of Fr. José Antonio Murguía (Santa Clara's principal minister) on 11 May 1784

author: Fages
summary: Header: 'remitiendo las comunicaciones que á continuación siguen' (intro to the following enclosures)

author: Fages
summary: Re Moraga's report: the SF presidio has 512 head of cattle; pasture sustains no more than ~800; proposes a July–Aug *matanza*; Sgt Pablo Grijalva reports 60 head harmed; herds to be kept from gentile raids; excess removed per the Reglamento

author: (Loreto, enclosed)
summary: Loreto Rl Hacienda revenue account to end-Dec 1783: alcabalas $586, diezmos $215, pearl-diving $252, oregano bulls $312, salt, Todos Santos $2,081 — total $4,186 — signed by José Joaquín de Arrillaga & habilitado José M.ª Estrada

author: José Fran.co de Ortega
summary: Report on the Pueblo de la Reina de los Ángeles: 38 fan. wheat sown (33 común, 2 particular), 12 maize, 8 frijol to come; irrigation from 2 leguas of good salt-free land; the gentiles helped plow/sow but now refuse (busy harvesting wild seeds); buildings strong under encargado Vicente Felix; ⭐ the town plaza & streets laid out per the Comandante General's order, royal houses to be built, guard of 9 vecinos + 8 soldiers — see Entry 17

author: Fages
summary: *Pasaportes*: discharge of SF soldiers Joaquín Castro & Atanasio Vásquez[?], who seek passports for San Blas

author: Fages
summary: Remits a *causa* against a San Diego soldier for the theft of a pistol

author: Fages
summary: Reply to the report that someone traveled in a litter on Indians' shoulders with much baggage

author: Fages
summary: [cluster] Mission inventories; mules & oxen received; *San Carlos* paquebot at SF; monthly correspondence/all quiet/arms reviewed

author: Fages
summary: Francisco Arbisu[?] & Cristóbal Tapia (boys of San José), having caused the death of a gentile Indian — being youths who acted 'sin malicia,' to get 25 lashes before the deceased's ranchería & his relatives; mission inventories remitted

author: Fages
summary: [4 docs] Invoice/baggage; ⭐ the Indian Luis López, for many depredations, sentenced to shackled labor at the Sta Bárbara presidio; *Favorita* at SB with víveres; wheat harvest 100 fan. (Indians stealing much)

author: Fages
summary: Notice of the approval of the founding of Mission Santa Bárbara at 'el Montecito'

author: P. [Junípero] Serra
summary: [2 docs] Instructed that the Channel mission is to be founded — will give all provisions; remits mission documents *(one of Serra's last letters; he died 28 Aug 1784)*

author: Fages
summary: On the handover of an orchard (huerta)

author: Fages
summary: Recalls Croix's order of 12 July 1782 for Fages to take the interim government of the Californias (Neve named interim Inspector Gral of the Provincias Internas forces) — confirms Fages's accession

author: Fages
summary: [6 docs] ⭐⭐ Received copies of the definitive peace treaty between Spain & England (1783); San José & LA pueblo grain to be paid in goods not cash; royal *indulto* (royal birth); Reglamento copies; libranza; *Ntra Sra de Aranzazú* at Loreto

author: Fages
summary: [6 docs] Instancia of José Antonio Sánchez; oficios; ⭐⭐ death of P. Junípero Serra, 28 Aug 1784 — Fr. Francisco Palóu acting President; inventories remitted by Pres. Fr. Miguel Hidalgo

author: Fages
summary: Sta Bárbara construction (60 pine beams from SF) & matanza (15 reses → 22 manteca; herd reduced to 800–900, 60 to SB, 40 to SD); ⭐ a 'volcán ó pozo' (tar-seep/hot spring) found near Mescaltitán — old per the Indians, deep, thin sulfurous smoke, water boiling at high tide, stones hot

author: Fages
summary: [6 docs] ⭐⭐⭐ Foreigner-exclusion order (foreigners not tolerated, esp. in the ports — arrest the unlicensed, penalize & deport to Spain; 'actualmente no existe ninguno'); ⭐⭐⭐ warning that a foreign power is sending disguised commissioners to incite the inhabitants to revolt — arrest any found/arriving; deserter Hermenegildo Flores killed by gentiles; salt stockpiling for San Blas ships (Arrillaga to load salt at the Salina del Carmen) — see Entry 18

author: Fages
summary: Several persons, incl. a padre, boarded the Nao de Filipinas and took out 4 bales of clothing; purchase of goods from ships being prohibited, asks González to account for it

author: Fages
summary: 'Varios asuntos sin importancia'

author: Fages
summary: Arrival of the frigates

author: Fages
summary: Pres. Fr. Miguel Hidalgo (Loreto) had P. Nicolás Muñoz embarked for San Blas, 'disponiéndolo dementemente' (treating him as insane)

author: Fages
summary: One of the discharged men arrived on the *Princesa* stayed 'con el fin de poner una escuela de niños' at a presidio; warns against letting anyone off the ships remain without his knowledge ('pueden introducirse algunos inútiles')

author: Fages
summary: *Cosechas y ganado*: Los Ángeles harvest 1,800 fanegas, all houses + guard + Casa de Ayuntamiento finished, church building (all adobe); F. Sinova[?] & Manuel Butrón resettled (Butrón → San José); ⭐⭐ first (interim) rancho grants — Juan José Domínguez (ex-SD soldier; 4 mare-herds + ~200 cattle, below the San Gabriel river → Rancho San Pedro), Manuel Nieto, & the children of the widow Ignacia Carrillo (Arroyo Hondo), with conditions not to harm gentiles or mission/pueblo herds; mission+pueblo grain ~20,000 fanegas (no more San Blas seed) — see Entry 20

author: Fages
summary: [2 docs] ⭐ Branding tally (Monterey 53 colts/59 fillies/215 heifer-calves/248 bull-calves; SF 80/61); goods sold by the Nao de Filipinas; SB has so much wheat that none is to be sown this year

author: Fages
summary: [2 docs] Cattle-brand (*fierro*) registration free for pobladores, fee for non-pobladores (how much to charge?); causa vs José Castañeda for attacking his corporal

author: Fages
summary: [3 docs] ⭐ Insubordination & arrest of Teniente Diego González; the presidio's & SF's cattle now ~1,900 head, hard to guard (send some to SD); announces the death of 'el Pres.te Juan[?] [surname illeg.]' at San Diego *[uncertain — possibly a re-notice of Serra's death; flag]*; all well

author: Fages
summary: Remits a proceso of deserters

author: Fages
summary: Proposes an expedition to discover communication between this Province [Alta California] and New Mexico, and how to carry it out

author: Fages
summary: Remits a state of the Los Ángeles cattle

author: Fages
summary: San José pueblo self-government: elected its own alcaldes/regidores since 1783 but 'corruptelas' & laxity appeared; Cabo José Domínguez detached as Comisionado to oversee; though the Reglamento let him appoint justices for the first 3 yrs he had let them elect (2 yrs); now appoints Manuel González alcalde; the 9-man electorate 'más propios para ser mandados que para mandar' — see Entry 19

author: Fages
summary: Nothing new in the presidios (Feb 2); going to visit San Diego (Feb 20)

author: Fages (San Diego)
summary: [5 docs] Acks the addressee's interim command of the Provincias Internas; ⭐⭐ Fages's personal reconnaissance from Mission San Vicente toward the Río Colorado (18 men + Alférez José Velázquez) — surveying terrains/distances/nations to the river-mouth & the route to SD; old-coin extension; Pedro Garrido y Durán now Secretary of the Comandancia Gral; the curate Luis Padilla's mail-canoe (from Pto. Las Cruces) feared shipwrecked — see Entry 21

author: Fages (San Gabriel)
summary: [2 docs] Remits a padrón; ⭐ registered a new shorter road from Mission San Vicente (via rancherías 'La Punta' & 'La Grulla'), saving 10–12 leguas & avoiding the coast & the 'Indios playanos' (insolent/treacherous) — see Entry 21

author: Fages
summary: *Fundación de Misiones* (Dominican Baja→SD frontier): the 3rd (San Vicente) founded 1780; Pres. Fr. Miguel Hidalgo preparing the 4th; best site 'El Encino,' midway San Diego–San Vicente

author: Fages
summary: [3 docs] Frontier escorts (Loreto co.; Real de Sta Ana 1 sgt+6; El Encino) — recommends +11 men & 1 sgt to the SD company; ⭐ recommends establishing cattle at SD (from Monterey) so families get free milk (as at Monterey/SF), to feed the troops & frontier soldiers (who live mostly on maize & wheat) & provision the ships wintering at SD

author: Fages
summary: Ships winter at SD; arrival at Loreto of the navío 'San Francisco de Paula alias el Hércules'; the Loreto royal houses needed repair

author: Fages (Monterey)
summary: [4 docs] Arrived at the capital; remits the reconnaissance diary; ⭐ remits Pres. Fr. Fermín [F.] de Lasuén's petition in favor of imprisoned Indians (San Diego); ⭐ no Mass at San Francisco for >2 years though its 2 ministers are there

author: Fages
summary: *Sobre Misión*: recommends the Río de Santa Rosa site for founding Mission La Purísima Concepción in the Channel (good, well-watered lands)

author: Fages
summary: To deal with the Intendant Pedro Corbalán re tithes [*dup. of Doc 234*]; remits a libranza

author: Fages (SF)
