summary: *Bando*: no petition to any court without franking it first; no private letter/packet carried without presenting it to the estafeta (or, lacking one, the corporal/soldier carrying the mailbag)

author: Neve
recipient: Antonio Joaquín Fernández de la Vega
summary: (trunc.) On establishing postal *estafetillas* — correspondence already carried by Indians, presidio officers overburdened; will route mail via Los Álamos/Guaymas (10×/yr vs San Blas 1×); about to move to found the Sta Bárbara Channel presidio + 3 missions *(Savage: 'ya extractada')*

author: (Neve, Monterey)
summary: State table of the presidios' *fuerza actual* & armament distribution (Loreto/Monterey/SD/SF/Sta Bárbara; espadas, alabardas, escopetas, fusiles, pistolas) with a Nota on Monterey/SF *bajas* — see Entry 22

author: Neve (San Gabriel)
recipient: Comte de Presidio / Nicolás Soler / Com. Gral
summary: [cluster] Military *rutinas*; salaries of the Tte del Pueblo del Paso[?] & the Ayudante Inspector; a review at San Diego

author: Neve
summary: $1,516 of the $3,902 cost collected — the lancha *Ntra Sra del Rosario* (built at San Blas) for Mission Todos Santos

author: Neve
summary: [4 docs] Remits inventory; sale of the deceased Juan Soler's goods; Loreto habilitado José M.ª Estrada's Arsenal de Marina account ($1,630); guarda-almacén bonds; requests effects

author: Neve (San Gabriel / Sta Bárbara)
summary: [cluster/list] Aug 10 cattle accounts (3 docs); Aug 12 *Princesa* & *Favorita* arrival; Apr 25 (→Nicolás Soler) ordered to hold a review; ⭐ Jan 11 Hermenegildo Sal named to armaments/stores; May 21/Jun presidio lists; Jun 8 (→Tribunal) returning a despacho; a *pólvora-y-balas* relación; a trunc. doc; an índice of presidio-accounts documents

author: Neve
summary: 'Tocante á nuevo Reglamento'

author: (San Gabriel)
summary: 'Relación del Armamento existente… en los Presidios de Calif' — arms-inventory table (Monterey/SF/Sta Bárbara/SD/Loreto: espadas, alabardas, escopetas, fusiles, pistolas; totals ≈263/52/376/138/239/51/434/185[?]) — see Entry 22

author: Fages
summary: *Expedición al río Colorado*: the Purísima [Colorado mission] destroyed (1781 Yuma-revolt aftermath); the Yumas withdrawn, other gentiles peaceful

author: Fages
summary: Per superior order, Fages is now recognized as Comandante Inspector of the companies

author: Fages
summary: Handover of the Comisaría (Oct 13); contabilidad & revista (Nov 6)

author: (Monterey)
summary: Livestock list charged to Tte Francisco José Ortega: 34 cows @$5, 11 bulls @$4, 16 mares @$4, 1 stud horse $12

author: Fages
summary: Neve having died, asks him to recommend the matter to the new Comandante General Don José Rengel, 'que ha sabido gobernará interinamente'

author: Fages
summary: [2 docs, Nov 7 & 14] On reviews (revistas)

author: Fages
summary: [2 docs] Sta Bárbara review concluded; to comply with the superior order on delivering the cargo

author: Fages
summary: *'Animosidad'* — re accountability & 'la pasión' in forming inflated charges (echoes the 1787 Lazo–Sal feud, Entry 6)

author: (Monterey)
summary: *Orden gral*: in spring, train the troop & hold target practice

author: Fages
summary: [cluster] Jan 4 contabilidad (10 docs); Feb 15 cattle memorias (3); Mar 10 salaries; Apr 3 contabilidad

author: Fages
summary: *Sobre Misiones / abastecer Presidios*: the missions 'apenas tienen para sus neófitos' — the better means is to foster Colonization; surplus grain could be sold via San Blas; good farmland exists [iteration of the missions-can't-supply theme, cf. Entries 12/14]

author: Fages
summary: Economic-administrative reflections on the habilitado officers

author: Fages
summary: [Apr 25 on administration; Jun 20 contabilidad]

author: Arrillaga (Loreto)
summary: [cluster, Jun–Oct] ⭐ Arrillaga (acting gov.) → Goycoechea — inválido cédulas; gold remission; monthly mail; goods/force states; ⭐ 'Plan del Presidio con 32 indios'; matanza; ajustes; Cordero[?] added to Sta Bárbara; land distribution; Francisco García to San Diego; a soldier sentenced 8 yrs sent with a shackle (rationed, unpaid); cédulas; Oct 24 frigate arrival; Oct 29 copy of an oficio

author: Arrillaga
summary: [Dec 3] permuta de soldados; *conducción* to Sta Bárbara; ⭐ causa criminal de Rocha

author: Arrillaga (San Diego)
summary: [Mar 23–Sep 30] Arrival of the Real barco at Sta Bárbara; recibo de documentos (5 docs); Mar 31; Aug 11; Sep 30 the Capt. of Chaaraui[?] departs with families

author: Arrillaga (San Francisco)
summary: 'Instrucciones sobre la expedición á Bodega': the force = 10 soldiers + 1 sergeant; go 'con precaución' and harm no gentiles; if they go with Matute (who should be at Bodega); reconnoiter all the surroundings of the fort — see Entry 23

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Recibo de comunicaciones (6 docs)

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: If Vancouver's English ships arrive pretexting *víveres ó aguada*, give them none — 'pues ya les ofreció y no aceptaron' — see Entry 23

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Sale of the hospital[?]; ⭐ approves the punishment imposed on Návarez[?] & de la Cuesta; return of a soldier; sending of colts

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: [Jan–Jul cluster] memorias; ajustes/permutas; ⭐ 'only 4 vacas chichiguas (milk-cows) per soldier'; accounts; Tapia to SD; ⭐ fuga de gentiles (3 docs); Easter compliance; herradores; ⭐ May 24 marriage licence; ⭐ death & testament of Ignacio Olivera; Tribunal; Jul 5 Olivera's goods; Jul 16 tithe accounts 1791–92

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Relación de Armas destinadas por Arrillaga al Presidio de Sta Bárbara: 52 escopetas @11 p, 53 espadas @28 r, 114 lanzas @14 r, + freight → total ≈ $886.11.4

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Olivera testament; revista & strength-states (3 docs); ⭐ imprisonment of an Indian (2 docs); José Santos Ulloa kept as servant; distribution of effects

author: Arrillaga (San Diego)
summary: [May–Jul cluster] Viceroy requests iron/steel; powder; ⭐ Eugenio Olachea mismanaged the Hacienda (his sons defrauded silver + 11 arrobas mercury); Arsenal works/excursion south/artillery report; ⭐ approves Ortega's reply to the Guadalajara Factor on the powder & playing-card monopoly; ⭐ wreck of the schooner *Santa Gertrudis* (iron salvaged); Bernal named habilitado; death of Castro

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Settle Sgt López's shortfall; López promotions; 'in spring a founding will be made in the South'

author: Arrillaga (SF)
summary: Relocation of Dominican Mission Santo Tomás de Aquino (founded 1791; site unhealthy/mosquito-ridden, the gentiles would never live there) to a higher *cañada* — per the Junta Superior de Rl Hacienda; cites the 1788 move of Mission San Miguel (founded 1787 at El Encino); per Viceroy Revillagigedo

author: Arrillaga (Monterey/SF)
summary: [Sep–Oct cluster] Despatch inventories/remissions; ⭐ Viceroy orders Missions Loreto, San Javier & Mulegé to reimburse the salt they received; tithe accords; ball-burning; timber; Bernal not to give Pedro Obet[?] $1,100 in goods; inventories; ⭐ arrival of the pearl-divers' canoe of Padres Meza & Valdés

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: [Jan–May cluster] New-Year greeting; Rosa's embarkation; Cisneros lawsuit; ball accounts; blacksmith Saavedra; schooner repair; ⭐ announces Borica's appointment [as governor] & preparations to receive him; donativo reduction; Bernal's exit (2 docs); Ortega–new-teniente agreement; royal-launch inspection

author: Arrillaga (SF)
summary: [May–Aug cluster] bajas of Zúñiga & Borica[?]; Arsenal construction (4 docs); ⭐ Ortega hands over [the SD command] to Rosa[?] (Jul 5); observations on the accounts (Aug 5) — 'Fin del Tomo I' (tomo p.654)

author: (Neve)
summary: 'Documento trunco' — the missions-disorder / Fiscal inquiry [dup. of the Vol. I leftover]

author: Neve (Monterey)
recipient: Fran.co Trillo y Bermúdez / Com. Gral
summary: Re a *cobro*; ⭐ N.º 220 — cost of conducting arms via Fernando Rivera (surplus of 100 sets received at Álamos)

author: Neve (Misión del Rosario)
summary: Deserter Izquierdo's account; religious ceremonies + Channel memorias + new Reglamento [dup. of Vol. I 1780]

author: Neve (Monterey)
summary: *Matanza* at the Real de Sta Ana del Sur (tasajo/manteca/sebo figures); *arancel de precios* per Gálvez

author: Neve (Loreto/Monterey)
summary: Sonora goleta & balandra *Pilar*; departure of 17 colonist families from Loreto (12 May 1781) under Alférez Ramón Lasso for the bahía de San Luis [Los Angeles-founding party]; José Zúñiga follows with the rest; Sgt Juan José Robles + 12 soldiers to the Colorado to meet Capt. Fernando Rivera [the Yuma-massacre expedition] — see Entry 24

author: Fages (Monterey)
summary: Salt; an officer's commission & political conduct [dup. of Vol. I Fages]

author: Fages
summary: No foreign ship to arrive without licence, much less enter the interior; alcabala +35%; aid to artisans; apprehend the *indio* Tharquin[?] [foreign-ship genre; dup. draft — cf. Entry 18]

author: Arrillaga
summary: Collect papers from León Parrilla's house; ⭐ Jun 30 Relación de armas to Monterey (41 escopetas, 40 espadas, 52 lanzas → total $775.4.3¾); ⭐ Aug 4 11 Indians sent from San Luis as 'sediciosos,' take their declarations

author: Arrillaga
summary: The six deserters from Vancouver — rations/accounts; gratification list — LAST LEAF of Vol. II

author: (Neve, trunc.)
summary: 'Documento trunco': the denunciation by Fr. Antonio de la Concepción of disorders & mistreatment of neophytes in the Alta California missions; contradictory declarations → Fiscal wants corroboration; continues into the report on Comandante-vs-missionary friction, the soldiers' 'comercio con las Indias,' and the disputes over punishing Indians [dup-theme → Vol I Docs 118/144]

author: Fages
summary: 'Borrador de la correspondencia con el Presidio de Sta Bárbara desde 1.º Enero 1784' — fierro/acero via Miranda; arancel approval; Plan del Presidio; padrón; pasaporte Tte Mesa; licencia de matrimonio á Vicente Quijada; madera/fragua to Felipe Moreno; 53 novillos; ⭐ Sep 18 Reglamento impreso + Tratado de Paz + 60 vigas + 75 @ carne [dup → Vol I Docs 213–252]

author: Fages
summary: 'Sobre Gentiles, fugas, envío de ganados, llamamiento y permuta de soldados': ⭐ free entry/exit of gentile Indians not to be tolerated; fugas (San Gabriel; 8–12 lashes); cattle distribution [dup-theme → Vol I 1784–85]

author: Fages
summary: 'Sobre cosechas, llamamiento, ropa, toma de mando, deserciones y embarco de bestias': San Buenaventura married escorts; ⭐ China-cloth (ropa de China) distribution permit (prohibited by ordinance); cabo Ortega to take over La Purísima; vigilance orders [dup → Vol I 1788]

author: Neve (Monterey)
summary: [numbered borradores] ⭐⭐ N.º 223 (22 Mar 1781) 'Marcha de familias — Expedición al Canal': soldier & settler families to move via Loreto to the port of San Luis; Capt. Rivera y Moncada crosses via the Colorado; the troop/settler *memorias* to garrison the Channel establishments; San Gabriel sends 46 carneros + 114 sheep/goats w/ breeding stock for the settlers; + N.º 220 (cost of arms conducted by Rivera, Álamos surplus); a *cobro* to Trillo y Bermúdez — complements Entry 24

author: Neve (Monterey)
summary: [N.º 171–176] ⭐ N.º 171 deserter Francisco Serrano (Dragones de México) pardon; N.º 173 Cañete to collect tithes at the Real de Sta Ana, Sgt Francisco Aguiar; N.º 172 triplicate oficios re mail lost from the Havana/Coruña packets; funeral-rights RO; apostates/deserters [dup-theme → Vol I 1780]

author: Neve (Monterey)
summary: [N.º 177–180] ⭐ N.º 177 salary increase for Tte Joaquín Cañete (Loreto, to equal San Diego); N.º 178 Cañete's *solicitud* for a royal título + Monte Pío pension for wife/children; livestock/agriculture notes (180 vacuno, 104 caballar…)

author: Neve (Monterey)
summary: [N.º 182–184] ⭐ N.º 184 commissary Álvarez Osorio accounts (Real Fiscal); N.º 182 San Blas vessels not to carry passengers without the governor's licence (passengers to his disposal) [= the original very-first gap-sample at n240]

author: Neve (Monterey)
summary: [N.º 179, 183] ⭐ N.º 179 indulto of deserters José Beltrán, Antonio Alegre, Francisco M.ª Ruiz, Francisco Villagómez + servants José Guerrero & Mariano Tapis, restored to their plazas (crime: desertion); N.º 183 on frequent troop desertions; guarda-almacén supervision

author: Neve (Monterey)
summary: Serra refuses to exhibit his patent to administer Confirmation ('S.R. se excusa á exhibirla') [complements Entry 14]; Carlos Gallego ajuste (27 p to Moraga); transport of a deserter

author: Neve (Misión Rosario)
summary: Situado/víveres delay; ⭐ founding of Mission San Vicente Ferrer (Baja, at the Sta Rosalía site, 1780); mines under Cañete's jurisdiction; religious-ceremonies/Channel memorias [dup → Vol I 1780]

author: Neve (San Diego)
summary: Camacho (Loreto soldier) incest causa w/ his aunt María Guadalupe [= Vol I Doc 134 draft]; *Santiago* arrival; Monterey/SF/SD guarda-almacén accounts; ⭐⭐ RO re (British) Admiral Hughes communicated to SD, to be executed at Monterey & SF (foreign-naval alert); deserters Joaquín Diez Díaz & Rafael Valenzuela to Loreto; tobacco bando

author: Neve (Monterey)
summary: Mission debts ($3,500 embargo; P. Nicolás Muñoz; Álvarez Osorio report); *Santiago* arrival (Esteban Martínez letter) [dup → Vol I]

author: Neve (Monterey)
summary: New Reglamento provisional; ⭐ 'Recluta de soldados y pobladores' — Capt. Fernando Rivera to recruit 45 soldiers + 7 settlers via Guadalajara; the Colorado expedition; Rivera resolves to retreat without touching Guadalajara, marching from Rosario mid-Aug [complements Entry 24 / V2-5]

author: Neve (sin lugar)
summary: Cavalry / *situado de caballada* resolutions per the Reglamento (utility vs. impossibility on the frontiers); *Reglamento de precios* (arancel) [dup → Vol I Docs 285/319]

author: Neve (Monterey)
summary: Death of Juan Soler + SF guarda-almacén inventory; two *índices* of Álvarez Osorio's Loreto inventory & Alférez Mariano Carrillo's charge; ⭐ arms-inventory tables (San Diego, San Francisco) [draft of the 1782 arms state, Entry 22 / Docs 280·286]

author: Neve (San Gabriel)
summary: Capt. Manuel de la Azuela[?] to Loreto to take his company; ⭐ 'Marcha para el Río Colorado' — provisions for the detachment leaving 21 Aug; Neve to follow 25/26, arriving 15 Sept [Neve's 1782 Colorado march] — see Entry 25

author: Neve (San Gabriel)
summary: RO re the obispo de Sonora; ⭐ Missions San Juan Capistrano, San Francisco & Santa Clara declared exempt from paying for the rations ministered; RO on penalties for 'desertores de América'; a mission transfer

author: Neve
summary: 'Expedición contra los Yumas' — detailed account of the 1782 punitive campaign: down to the Colorado with 60 men, based at the ruined pueblo of La Concepción where Capt. of Dragoons José [Antonio] Romeu held with 108 men; 50 soldiers + 12 settlers; Jalchedunes & Cocomaricopas allied; an alférez + 5 soldiers + 12 Indians scout; Romeu with 40 men + 12 Indians routs the Yuma cavalry (great mortality; 4 soldiers wounded); baggage & armament recovered; the 'rebel Yuma nation subjugated,' communication freed — see Entry 25

author: Neve
summary: Junta de Guerra y Rl Hacienda orders for the Yuma campaign (Fages to march from Yuma territory with the rescued families, convoking friendly nations to attack the Yumas); plan for the Yumas/allies to scout a site to re-establish a presidio on the Colorado ensuring free communication; 'Suspensión de guerra' (war suspended to mid-Sept; Colorado unfordable till April); Romeu & Fages retire to their destinations; the Yuma 'Capitán grande' — see Entry 25

author: Neve (San Gabriel/Sta Bárbara)
summary: Gentiles friendlier after Fages's march (Cajón del Príncipe, Sta Catarina); ⭐ Channel presidio + missions to be garrisoned, Serra to demarcate, site near San Buenaventura w/ water, walling begun [Channel founding 1782, dup-theme → Entry 10]

author: Neve (Sta Bárbara)
summary: Remisión de documentos; ⭐ establish *estafetillas* (postal stations); lancha replacement; deserter; ⭐ 'Trámite de Apelaciones' — appeals from Real-Patronato causes to the Com. Gral, the rest to the Audiencia de Guadalajara

author: Neve
summary: Bando vs. merchants selling/trafficking mercury (azogue); Real de Sta Ana mineros; gold paid in silver; donativo; Cañete suspends alcabala (per P. Miguel Hidalgo's representation re mission goods sold on the Costa Opuesta); Diezmos of the Dep. del Sur auctioned 3 yrs

author: Neve (San Gabriel)
summary: 'Antecedentes de una mortandad' — the causes of the 1781 Yuma Revolt: at the expedition's passage through Jalchedun lands the captain took gentile/Christian women; Rivera failed to gift the Yuma chief Palma; the expedition's mules destroyed the mesquite the Yumas live on part of the year → 'apostasía y rebelión'; a Yuma (Limón[?]) killed Capt. Bastón[?]; Palma's brother; the Jalchedunes — see Entry 25

author: Neve (San Gabriel)
summary: Envío de documentos; maize/grain prices & harvests (LA wheat); 'establezca estafetillas desde 1782'; insignificante items [dup]

author: Neve (San Gabriel)
summary: Bando on free sale of azogue (mercury) to mine-owners; ⭐ Monterey + Missions San Carlos/San Antonio/San Luis donate $1,310 for the Yuma expedition (missionaries contributing against soldiers' wages); SF Co. & Missions SF/Sta Clara likewise; Hermenegildo Sal *indultado*; provisions for the expedition

author: Neve
summary: Yuma-massacre narrative (augments Entry 25): scouts to the Colorado's north bank; gentiles told Velázquez that Capt. Carrizosa[?] was killed; Salvador Palma & 3 others gathered their people; at 2 a.m. fell on the Padre[s] & Fernando Rivera; soldiers scattered & seized; the missions attacked the same hour; 2 armed horsemen crossed, burned houses, killed Indians; interpreter Javier; alférez Limón; Palma fled to the sierra ~2 months

author: Neve (San Gabriel)
summary: 'Relación del Armamento que existía en Dic 1780 en Californias' — arms table (Monterey/SF/SD/Loreto: espadas/alabardas/escopetas/fusiles/pistolas; totals ≈289/80/364/138/195/66/580/91[?]) + Nota: of 195 escopetas only 39 are new (rest 12-adarme calibre); swords so brittle they break & can't be repaired (Catalonia-factory tools lacking) [Dec-1780 arms state; cf. Entry 22]

author: Neve (San Gabriel)
summary: Informes; thanks for the *parto de la princesa*; ⭐ prevention re Serra continuing Confirmations (cf. E14/V2-10); moneda-antigua indulto; Reglamento de sueldos; ⭐ 'Revista á Pobladores / Retiro de soldados' [the Pobladores review; Rivera recruiting]

author: Neve (San Gabriel)
summary: 3 Indians (Diego, Carlos, Rafael, Luis) sent to the SD fábricas to avoid subversion; ⭐ robbery at the Monterey almacén by soldier Juan Antonio Labra; sentences/salary/deserter; RO re mail lost from Spain (1780); memorias; a proceso; 1780 military-prisoners-of-causes [dup]

author: Neve
summary: Commissary accounts (lost originals; Rivera's recruit-receipts; Los Álamos); ⭐ forming the Santa Bárbara company (1 cabo + 7 soldiers drawn from each of the Monterey & SD companies); 40 palisade houses for troop families (rainy season); ⭐ José Zúñiga & the recruits/settlers/families arrived, camped a league off [LA/Channel colonists; complements Entry 24]

author: Fages (Monterey)
summary: Docs/embarcación; ⭐ asks surgeon & munitions; 3 sailors to build the Sta Bárbara presidio walls; ⭐ Fages entered the Cal. government 10 Sept 1782; tabacos/fierro/acero/tejas; 88 war-supply cases (double requested); escopetas urgent ('no armorers'); ⭐ labor shortage — asks San Blas send 'algún indio próximo sin vicios, y si dable gente de campo'; ganado mayor [dup → Vol I 1784]

author: Fages (Monterey)
summary: 'Representación contra los Frailes' (the missions' system 'diametralmente opuesto' to the Reglamento's aims; Gálvez's 1769 establishment); inventories to Loreto; ⭐ frigate *San José* — 3 men/company requested; armed boat (6 soldiers) vs. contraband, Fages 'ronda de noche' [dup → Vol I]

author: Fages (Monterey)
summary: Reflections on the Gabrieleño Indians' plan to kill padres & soldiers (cf. the 1778 SD-frontier case; 'cruel lashes'); the few Indians of San José & SF; ⭐ Bishop [Antonio de los] Reyes's visit; 'esto está tachado' [dup → Vol I]

author: Fages (Monterey)
summary: [cluster, mostly 'sin interés'/cartas particulares] to Juan Gutiérrez Coral, Estevan Solá[?], Juan Gasiot, Cristóbal Corbalán, Nicolás Soler, P. Palóu, Fr. José García; ⭐ Dec 1784 branding tally (Monterey 112 colts/463 calves; SF 156; ≈619 total) [= Vol I Doc 262]; 1785 representación al Com. Gral

author: Fages (SD/Monterey)
summary: Asks the Inventarios y Padrones of the missions; ⭐ the new San Vicente→Colorado road reconnaissance (Encino, San Vicente, the Colorado mouth, Cucapás/Cajuenes/Jalchedunes, presidio site) [dup → Entry 21]

author: Fages (Monterey)
summary: [cluster, 'notas de docs sin interés'/cartas] Trillo y Bermúdez; the Comisario; ⭐ Com.te de Marina Francisco Maurelle (Bodega's pilot); Fr. Luyando; Pedro & Cristóbal Corbalán; Juan Ortiz de Rosas; Pedro Mata; Pedro Fuñón; P. Barbastro; Fr. Matías 'sobre una India'; N. Soler re diezmos; expressive/friendship letters (Juan Gasiot, Prudencio Ruiz Equino, José Lozada, Manuel Elorduy, Juan Portusat *sobre armamento*, Pedro Abellanar, José Larriva) [trivial/dup]

author: Fages (Monterey)
summary: The 'missions-can't-supply-presidios' report + a 'Nueva California' description (4 presidios, 9 missions, 2 pueblos; climate & terrain by latitude; the colonization argument) [= Vol I Docs 297–298 draft]

author: Fages (Monterey)
summary: Informe on the 5 presidios' troop duties [dup]; testamentos/inventarios; ⭐ the San José comisionado *instrucción* (numbered: fulfill the Reglamento; 2 yuntas + 2 arados per married poblador; watch no one sell to foreigners; arms ready; oversee barbechos/siembras) [= Entry 19 draft]

author: Fages (Monterey)
summary: 'Quietud de Indios/Gentiles en la Península'; ⭐ death of José [Joaquín] Moraga, SF commander (Habilitación to Ramón Lasso, arms to Diego González); ⭐ Ignacio Vallejo's water-source/rancho near San José; ⭐ Sebastián Alvitre (the 1775 rape-case man) sentenced to a *grillete*, sells his goods, transferred with his cattle to Los Ángeles

author: Fages (Monterey)
summary: Missions to supply San Blas surplus grain; índices of pliegos 'sin interés'; ⭐ Fr. Miguel Hidalgo's departure to Guadalajara; ⭐ missionary opposition/abuses re presidio administration; a friar embarking without licence [dup-theme]

author: Fages (Monterey)
summary: Sebastián Alvitre fled with the SD presidiario Antonio Yépez (pursued to the Sierra Nevada); soldier Juan José Bernal discharged to the Manila galleon (short in habilitación); the Gabrieleño Indians conspiracy [dup]

author: Fages (Sta Bárbara/Monterey)
summary: Correo/franking (the Fernandinos refuse); ⭐ Sgt Ignacio Olivera punished 2 San Gabriel rebels without authority; ⭐ Alvitre, imprisoned & deemed 'perjudicialísimo,' sent 'á la otra banda' (deported); Nao de Filipinas

author: Fages (Monterey)
summary: Cattle-increase to soldiers; ⭐⭐ approves the proceeding against a foreign[?] ('Ruso[?]') ship-captain for carrying off 2 Christian Indian women [1786 — ⚠️ verify reading; possibly a La-Pérouse-era foreign vessel]; ⭐ orders soldiers not punished with cueras while on sentinel; a mission (Sta Cruz/Clara[?]) relocated to a less flood-prone site; repair church/warehouses/quarters; ⭐ Sgt José Pérez Fernández named Alférez of SF

author: Arrillaga (SD/Sta Bárbara)
summary: 'Que se informe sobre los dos barcos ingleses'; orders re the English balandra & fragata (Vancouver's *Discovery* & *Chatham*) at port & Monterey — handle 'con prudencia… buen modo,' but force if needed; only the ship's commander may land letters; disembarking prohibited per standing orders [augments Entry 23 — Arrillaga/Vancouver English-ships policy 1793]

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Artisans destined for the establishment of the port of Bodega; fragata *Aranzazú* brings powder; 30 breaking-colts; punish recruit Francisco González; remit unusable cannon & pedreros to San Blas; ⭐ aprehensión de Charquín (cf. C-A 55 Doc 212); ⭐⭐ Lt. de Fragata Juan Matute decides to retire from Bodega with his ship, troops & artisans to San Blas; ⭐ fragata 'La Descubierta'; ⭐ the British frigate's arrival [augments Entry 23]

author: Arrillaga (SF/Monterey)
summary: Arms-relación to SF (20 escopetas @11p=286; 8 espadas @28r; freight; total ≈$343) [= Vol I Docs 308/323]; soldier Joaquín Bernal imprisoned (1 mo. calabozo + grillete); admin

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Aid to prisoners; prohibited gambling; ⭐ Indian Agustín's sentence → private service till the new gov. decides; ⭐ Vallejo to tend Fages's huerta (assigned with it to the widow); 2 *tercios de tocas* to the gov.'s widow; José Castillo

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Herradero; ⭐ artisans (Leocadio Martínez, Manuel Dorotea[?]); presidiario José Ocampo; ⭐ tanner Miguel Sangrador to be employed; ⭐ Tte Diego González retires (inválido) to the Real del Rosario, Sonora; descuentos to artisans

author: Arrillaga (Monterey)
summary: Cantero Santiago Ruiz + 2 officials to finish the church; artisan-instruction; ⭐ hospitality to foreign ships 'con la cautela debida (pues fácilmente pueden aparentar)'; ⭐⭐⭐ reimbursement for the supplies furnished to the ships under Juan [Bodega] de la Cuadra and two under Vancouver — see Entry 23

author: Arrillaga
summary: Permitted two London merchant ships water & wood; ⭐⭐⭐ the 5 English deserters not to be handed to any British ship but Vancouver's — and not even to him without paying their costs; ⭐ weaver-master Antonio Domingo Enríquez to teach the natives; ⭐⭐ an English sloop en route to Nootka — no contact/commerce, water & wood freely but víveres/meat limited 'como una demostración de política' — see Entry 23

author: Arrillaga (Sta Bárbara/Monterey/SF)
summary: León Parrilla provisional Tte of the SF company (arms & habilitación); ⭐ weaver-instruction contract (Antonio Domingo Enríquez, 12 r/day, 4 yrs, to teach the Naturales); Pablo Grijalva's illness; Sgt Manuel Vargas inválido cédula (Revillagigedo); ⭐ the Indian Sósimo Berrón[?] killed a woman & dashed a little daughter — apprehended

author: Arrillaga
summary: Collect papers from León Parrilla's house; ⭐ arms-relación to Monterey (41 escopetas/40 espadas/52 lanzas → ≈$775); ⭐ 11 'seditious' Indians from San Luis, take their declarations; ⭐ the six Vancouver deserters (rations) — LAST LEAVES of Vol. II [= the gap-sample rows 323–324, now read systematically]

summary: Real Cédula granting Comte. Gral. Provincias Internas Ugarte y Loyola & successors the Subdelegación de Correos, Postas y Estafetas, except Sonora & Sinaloa

author: Goycoechea (La Purísima)
summary: Remitting documents

author: Goycoechea (La Purísima/Sta Bárbara)
themes: trade; contraband; missions; indigenous-labor; fiscal; environment
summary: Sworn relation of *mermas* (losses) of San Blas goods, $640; will not permit introduction of foreign goods except those under the Habilitado Gral's facturas, confiscating any others (Jul 9); received 614 ewes (100 wethers) from Tte. Antonio Grajera, wool at San Gabriel 20 rs/arroba (Jul 28); must economize on the presidio church works, dismiss the 10 Indian workers (Jul 21)

author: Goycoechea (Sta Bárbara)
summary: Re Ramo de Bulas, will follow Viceroy's order of 11 Feb; (Oct 28) ordered to remit every 6 months a list of youths attending the *escuelas de primeras letras*

author: José Joaquín de Arrillaga (Loreto, Comte.)
summary: Roster of Loreto Compañía Presidial: Capt. Arrillaga; Tte. José Franco Ortega; Alférez José Mª Estrada; 1 sgt, 3 cpls, 39 soldiers; Inválidos (retired Capt. Joaquín Cañete, 1 sgt, 4 sol.); Marina/maestranza (1 ship's carpenter, 1 smith); goleta crew (patrón, guardián, 8 sailors); 2nd lancha crew (arráez + 6 sailors); excludes Sgt. José Gabriel Arce & Sol. Fco. Javier Ochoa (since 11 Dec 83), Sol. Macario Castro (to San Diego, order of Sub-Insp. N. Soler 22 Mar 1784)

author: Caballero de Croix (Arispe)
summary: Copy of asesor's *dictamen* + decree resolving doubts of Oficiales Reales of Caja de Álamos (moved to Rosario) on R.O. 10 Apr 1777, re leasing the pearl-diving (*buceo de perlas*) on the Tepoca coast & the Peninsula; requests pearl-yield report (royal fifth) for last 2 quinquennia to end of 1782

author: Asesor Galindo Navarro
summary: Long verbatim *dictamen* on the pearl-fishery leases (*buceo de perlas*) of the Tepoca coast & the Californias — see expanded entry. Three questions: (1) whether to auction the lease; (2) whether divers owe a *licencia* right beyond the royal fifth; (3) into which caja it's paid. Cites *Recopilación* lib. 4 tít. 25 leyes 29–30 (free pearl-fishing for vassals & *naturales* with permit, paying the fifth), R.O. 10 Apr 1777, Junta de México 1 Apr 1776, José de Gálvez's *instrucción* at La Paz 14 Apr 1769 (arts. 6, 14), the $13,000/yr offer of militia capt. Manuel García Morales, Fiscal of the Audiencia de México (8 Aug 1780), and a 1755 Panama-fishery cédula precedent

author: Goycoechea (La Purísima)
summary: Remits receipts/docs on Fran. Vejena[?]'s reunion of 49 *tercios* of the Memoria de México & 5 *cajones* of cigars sent to the Fiel of San Carlos pueblo

author: Fr. Bernardo, Obispo de Sonora
summary: Names an *apoderado* in Culiacán to pursue his suit against [Rubirosa?] Ignacio Argüello for payment of $500 (Savage: pp.73–75 undated/unsigned, presumed part of same)

author: Gob. Pablo Vicente de Solá
themes: foreign-relations; trade; britain; defense; indigenous-military; fiscal
summary: On swearing the Constitución Política de las Cortes Españolas (1820 Trienio re-swearing); Comte. de Frontera José Manuel Ruiz fears Indian attack, no means to defend, will withdraw to San Diego; English ships arriving 'con objeto de pescar' (to fish) will leave some revenue to the Real Hacienda for the duties they incur; 3 San Blas ships + 1 from Callao at México with goods but 'sin un centavo de dinero'; everyone from the last soldier up is unpaid; waters scarce, everything costly

author: Solá (Sta Bárbara)
summary: On his resignation as governor: a year begging the Viceroy to accept it, in vain; could not list his grievances 'en un tomo entero'; asks influence so his *renuncia* be approved and he be given a *Intendencia de Provincia* or named Ministro of a Caja Real; published the bando on the oath to the Constitution; wishes the Obispo de Sonora would visit (confirmations suspended >24 yrs)

author: Solá (Sta Bárbara)
summary: Juan Malarín, captain of the brig *Señoriano[?]*, arrived at that port without incident; received the gifts sent

author: Solá
summary: Entered his 61st year; many causes of discontent; made his resignation; $4,000 salary insufficient; begs his *renuncia* admitted and one of the posts he solicited

author: Solá (Sta Cruz)
recipient: Comte. de Los Ángeles Anastasio Carrillo
summary: Received advices on new boundaries for dividing pueblo lands & the properties of Manuel Gutiérrez and Sgt. Cristóbal Domínguez

author: Solá (Sta Inés)
recipient: Comte. de Marina de San Blas
summary: Returns, in the brig *San Javier*, 16 soldiers of the Compañía Fija de San Blas for insubordination

author: Solá
summary: Received the goods sent to San Blas for the 4 presidios; had it been now, instead of the $36,000 due the 4 presidios he'd have sent 10–12 in kind, given the great shortage

author: Solá
recipient: Gob. B. Cal. José Darío Argüello
summary: The Fathers know the mules he requested are not for his troop but to sell at a good price in Cabo San Lucas (per a trustworthy person, told to Sayas[?]); no mules sent unless he sends payment

author: Solá (Sta Inés)
summary: Having published the Constitución Española in the Province

author: Solá
summary: Sending a letter for his nephew Lt.-Col. Gabriel de Solá; asks a *guía de Madrid* for 1821

author: Solá
summary: Opened a cause against soldier Hermenegildo Vásquez of the Monterey cavalry co. (crime unstated)

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: Open a cause against Domingo Peralta, son of Sgt. Luis Peralta (commissioned at San José), for illicit cohabitation with Petra Vásquez, daughter of the *inválido* Tiburcio Vásquez

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: An expedition to be made against the Chasta[?] Indians with 100 men of the Regimiento de Zamora, to forestall their incursion; 'en el Pto de San Francisco hay 4 cabezas Rusas de Guerra' making a scientific expedition, who will not leave until next month for lack of *víveres*

author: Solá
summary: He'd be better off with the Conde; however he has resigned; repeated pleas to the Viceroy on his *apatía y abandono* toward so useful a Province

author: Solá
summary: Received Real Orden of 16 Jan with due punctuality

author: Solá (Mont.)
recipient: Pascual de Liñán, Sub-Inspector Gral. del Reyno
summary: On a proposal to fill the vacant captaincy of the San Diego presidial company

author: Solá
summary: Repeated representations to the Viceroy to re-establish the *correos* traffic in those Provinces by the same route & footing as before

author: Solá
summary: Cannot supply the Province's troops, lacking means even for his own; situation very bad — no commerce in the Peninsula; only non-Indians paying *diezmo* yield anything; has begged the Viceroy for *gente industriosa*; urges moving the Apostadero de San Blas to the Province, rich in shipbuilding timber & mines left 'vírgenes' for want of skilled hands — 'en 54 años que está en poder de España, no se han disfrutado ninguna de sus riquezas'

author: Solá (Mont.)
recipient: his nephew José Gabriel de Solá
summary: He went to México city to cure grave wounds from the *asalto de San Gregorio[?]*; sick men must be let go to seek cures; Sgt. Antonio Mª Ojeda is recommended, went mad (*se volvió loco*), maintains his family from his own pocket

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: The grape *diezmo* yielded 4,300 quintales of aguardiente, half to be drawn for its making per contract with the Govt.; renew same contract next year

author: Solá
recipient: Sgt. Luis Peralta, Comte. de San José
summary: School there has no teacher; notify Saturnino Buelna he'd prefer he serve it at his soldier's pay (San Francisco co.); warn Juan Álvarez that if he keeps running card-games in Solá[?]'s houses (w/ Feliciano Soberanes, Quintín Ortega) even in the company barracks, he'll be exiled on the first ship

author: Solá (Mont.)
recipient: Cabo de Escolta of San Juan Bautista Manuel Butrón
summary: Received his last campaign diary (forwarded to Viceroy); has proof Butrón is himself one of the card-players — if he won't reform, despite his age/post/family, he'll be punished severely

author: Solá
summary: Enforce attendance at Mass — heads of family, sons, *agregados*; Mariano Castro's wife (nearby rancho) never appears; bring a list of who is missing

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: Remit a note of goods he & his *patrón* Antonio Cot[?] sold in the missions & to private parties (to collect duties); the chief author of the $400 theft of collected duties was Fermín Lavastida, soldier of the San Blas infantry co., now gravely ill (a leg cut off) — if he dies, his houses will be sold to repay the theft

author: Solá
summary: On a $500 debt to the estate of José Joaquín de Arrillaga (d. 25 Jul 1814) owed by Presbítero José Ignacio Argüello

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: The only ship carrying the mail to the *otra banda* is in deplorable state — beg the Viceroy for another; the $30,000 for the 4 presidios barely began to pay them, so he has imposed duties on goods entering & leaving the Peninsula; same scarcity expected for B. Cal.

author: Solá
summary: The Arrillaga testamentaría *autos* (radicated in the Juzgado Gral. de Bienes de Difuntos under Oidor Isidro Sáinz, Audiencia Nacional); Col. José Ignacio Camacho[?], Habd.º Gral., has not reintegrated Arrillaga's salaries (Jul 1811–Jul 1814), pending since Jan 1819 with no resolution

author: Solá
summary: Receipt of documents

author: Solá
recipient: nephew Lt.-Col. José Gabriel de Solá
summary: In México he has fully recovered; scarcely a trace remains of the dangerous wounds received at *Logroño[?]*

author: Solá
summary: Will make Artillery Alférez José Ramírez pay for goods he sold from the *fardo* he offered to carry from México, delivering them in the Peninsula

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: On Subteniente Francisco de Haro's request for permission to marry Hermiliana[?] Sánchez, daughter of Alférez graduado José Antonio Sánchez

author: Solá
summary: Received the provisional *despacho* of Capt. of the San Diego cavalry co. (issued 1 Jul) for Francisco Mª Ruiz, Tte. of that company

author: Solá
recipient: Comisionado del Pueblo de San José
summary: Punish Tiburcio Vásquez, Joaquín Buelna & Hernández's son for stealing horses & cattle; jail Faustino Vásquez 2 months for the same; replace Antonino[?] Buelna in running the school while ill, naming his brother Joaquín with pay + gratification

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: Asks whether two Indians from Mission San Miguel (Baja Cal.), married, are at Mission San Gabriel — sought by P. José Martínez, its minister

author: Solá
summary: On goods for the presidio troops; send the same blue cloth as before; all commanders to remit ready-made *chupas* & *calzones* per his order; to avoid this, have them and the sergeants' made in their respective *divisas*

author: Solá
summary: Inquire whether another has been named to replace him in the Peninsula govt (the climate disagrees with him), or to help get a replacement named soon

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: Has repeatedly told the Viceroy of the mail delays since the Arispe establishment, and that there is only one unserviceable boat to carry mail *de una banda a otra* — only one voyage made by the one built at Mazatlán

author: Solá
recipient: Comte. de Marina de San Blas
summary: Remits accounts of the 16 soldiers sent on the brig *Ulises[?]* (Capt. Vicente Ferrage[?]), of the 100 sent to the Province as *auxilio* by order of Viceroy Conde del Venadito

author: Solá
summary: Has not wearied of begging the *relevo* to spend his last days among *'gente de otra especie'* than that of *'este rincón del mundo'*

author: Solá
summary: Family letter; rarely hears from his *mayorazgo* brother Casimiro; reports their brother Fr. Faustino died 18 Oct at the Colegio de San Fernando after ~30 yrs there

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: San Diego command filled by Tte. Francisco Mª Ruiz, so cannot grant his request for that captaincy; ⭐ force note: light-cavalry troop of 60 men (sgt down), half mounted for the 5 missions (each baptizing ~100 Indians, 2 San Fernando friars on *estípendio*); since the war of independence began the pay is unpaid

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: The Chasta[?] Indians defeated, many killed, by the troops of Capt. Antonio Narbona (cf. Doc 32 — the Zamora-regiment expedition); the B. Cal. *vecindario* named Alférez Foro[?] to petition (Durango / Junta Provincial) on that region's *estado decadente* and propose remedies

author: Solá
summary: Will do all he can so her recommended Fr. Jaime Escuder is granted retirement license

author: Solá
summary: Having attended to his recommended Ignacio Acedo

author: Solá
summary: Receipt of *gacetas*

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: On his resignation: recommends Juan Malarino (capt. of brig *El Señoriano*); reports the King made him Caballero de las Órdenes de Carlos III e Isabel la Católica; begs influence for the *relevo* — an Intendencia or Caja-Real ministry, or failing that the *sueldo de inválidos* for his 25 yrs' service

author: Solá
summary: Received copy of R.O. June 1820 approving his governorship-appointment by Viceroy Calleja, Conde de Calderón; cannot continue; notes Tte. de Navío Gonzalo Gómez de Ulloa solicits the Peninsula govt — begs influence so it be granted

author: Solá
summary: Influence so his resignation be admitted & one of the solicited posts granted

author: Solá
recipient: Domingo Mª de Pozo y Chinchurreta
summary: Same; can no longer remain in the Peninsula — he is 60, the climate & hardships very harmful to his health

author: Solá
summary: Same as the previous

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: Remits a *libranza* of $10,000 against the Intendente de México for his back salary, charging its collection

author: Solá
summary: The Peninsula's climate is good & healthy but for being under-populated; the militia footing would be insufferable had he not promoted so many resources

author: Solá
recipient: Encargado de Justicia de Santa Gertrudis
summary: Sends his appointment; scandals in the rancho go unpunished; Cartagena[?] de la Cruz & Patricia Rodríguez as scandal-makers to be shaved with a razor & marched bare-headed into the church, then 6 months' penance near the San Gabriel presbytery

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: Named, on his recommendation, the Encargado de Justicia of Rancho Santa Gertrudis

author: Solá
recipient: Juan Cruz Ruiz de Cabañas, Diputado
summary: The frigate *Reyna* arrived bringing his recommended Juan Ruiz, son of José Domingo Ruiz Altamirano of the Audiencia de Guadalajara; will help him

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: Two frigates anchored at Monterey: the Spanish merchantman *Reyna de los Ángeles* and the Russian warship *Kutúzov* (*'Cutussoff'*), from Callao, which brought Intendente Sebastián de Arrieta (to stay some time in the Peninsula)

author: Solá
summary: On the $10,000 *libranza* for his back pay

author: Solá
summary: On the $500 debt to the Arrillaga estate by Presbítero Ignacio Argüello, son of Capt. José Darío Argüello

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: Forwards the Viceroy's letters; has news of the latest turns of the *guerra de los insurgentes*

author: Solá
summary: Has interceded for his son Juan as recommended

author: Solá
summary: Has written to Comtes. Grales. de Provincias Internas Alejo García Conde & Brig. Antonio Cordero begging news of the *guerra de insurrección*; will relay any

author: Solá
summary: Pay the $806 of duties owed — sorely needed, the troops lack pay

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: Remission of goods

author: Solá
summary: Royalist reaction to Mexican independence: received his *papeletas e impresos*; scorns the *'destinos'* being printed since the independence; praises *'la inmortal, incomparable nación Española'* with great resources to make itself respected; Gervasio [Argüello] 'no ha querido jurar la detestada independencia' and may return to his family on the means given for his good conduct

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: Jura de la Independencia en la Península: on a *correo violentísimo*, has sworn independence in a junta of captains & mission prelates; received the Decreto of the Soberana Junta / Suprema Regencia del Imperio on electing a Diputado

author: Solá
summary: Lord Cochrane's privateers (Chilean navy) at Cabo San Lucas, Feb 1822: several of his vessels (incl. brig *El Alción*, with another bound for San Blas) sacked Missions San José & Todos Santos, and a brig went to Loreto taking the church valuables & Gov. Argüello's baggage; discusses establishing communication via the *pueblo* & Río Colorado

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: Remits notes to the Regencia del Imperio; wants Loreto–Guaymas communication by sea opened, given the pirates

author: Solá
summary: San José pueblo discipline: jail the mother of militiaman Santiago Vázquez 15 days in the *monjerío* for insulting Sgt. Hernández; warn Encarnación Altamirano, [Perea] Galarza[?] & Ignacio Pacheco's wife (2 months' jail) for throwing filth in the *acequias* & cursing authorities; bachelors/widowers without houses to sleep at the guard; a third of the year's tallow from the *correría* to fund building a jail & casa real

author: Solá (Mont.)
recipient: Joaquín Astiasarán, Diputado Consular de Tepic
summary: Remitting a *libranza*

author: Solá
summary: Preparing his march to the Capital, having been elected Diputado for the Peninsula by unanimity; rejoices that he too has now 'adherido á la causa de independencia' (reversal from Doc 83)

author: Solá
summary: Appoints schoolmaster: lacking able men, the young Fermín de Labastida (San Blas auxiliary soldier, discharged after losing a leg — cf. Docs 43, the $400-theft figure) to serve the vacant school, the *vecinos* to pay him a monthly/yearly gratification

author: Solá
summary: Remission of documents

author: Solá
summary: Named all the electors Diputado *propietario*, and Argüello *supplente* for his 2 years; explains the cause of the mail's detention across the Peninsula

author: Solá
summary: The Hartnell–McCulloch tallow contract: the *vecinos* beg to defer this year's *unto* tax (failed harvests); he grants one year only, calling them vice-ridden; reports 'los comerciantes ingleses Arnell y Macala' (Hartnell & McCulloch) contracted with the Prefecto de Misiones to take tallow (*sebo*) for 3 years at $1, soap at $2/arroba, *manteca* at $4, etc.; he got them to offer the same prices to the *vecinos* — a real/arroba of sales to benefit the pueblos & fund troop pay

author: Solá
summary: Militia discipline: judges must ensure militiamen meet civil & moral duties, but try them criminally per their *fuero* / the *Ordenanza de Milicias Provinciales*

author: Solá
summary: Harsh pueblo-discipline code: a *vecino* scandalizing during Mass gets 25 lashes before the pueblo + a month's confinement; community-labor absences punished (14×4 days' arrest + paying a substitute's wage); on the *matanza* of wild cattle & deer since the 1818 liberties yielding only ~4,500–5,000 arrobas

author: Solá
summary: The war-brig *San Carlos* arrived at Monterey 8 Aug bringing imperial commissioner Canónigo [Agustín] Fernández [de San Vicente], and the American frigate *Phantom* with some mail

author: Solá (Mont.)
recipient: José Argüello, Manuel Ruiz & José de la Pola
summary: Requires them to answer his letters & explain why Alta–Baja Cal. communication has been interrupted

author: Solá
summary: Receipt of the Regencia's dispatches

author: Solá
summary: Receipt of his credential as Comisionado por la Regencia; thanks for the praise; will join old Narváez at Monterey & travel together to San Blas

author: Solá
summary: On the bid & sale (*remate*) of the schooner *Águila*

author: José Estrada, Tte. Habilitado (Mont.)
summary: Ration supplied to convict Serafín Archuleta for a year at 1½ rs/day — $69

author: José Estrada (Mont.)
summary: Munitions inventory, Monterey presidio: 80 arrobas cannon powder, 12 @ fine, 290 cannon cartridges, 84 @ lead, 4,600 ball cartridges

author: José Estrada w/ J. Mª Estudillo (Mont.)
summary: Inventory of Real Hacienda *enseres* in the Habilitación: arms, shearing gear, pottery & ribbon-making tools — total $595

author: José Estrada w/ José Mª Estudillo (Mont.)
summary: Artillery/ordnance inventory, Monterey presidio — see expanded entry: 7 bronze 8-pdrs mounted, 1 six-pdr, 2 three-pdrs; 1,340 iron 8-lb balls (+6/4/2-lb & 340 bronze), 443 grapeshot bags, palanquetas, *cotense* cartridges, etc.

author: José Argüello (Mont.)
summary: Remission of accounts

author: Diego de Borica (Mont.)
summary: Forwards Viceroy's note of 27 Aug on R.C. 8 Nov 1795: military of all classes to be covered by the Real Pragmática of 23 Mar 1796

author: José Argüello (Mont.)
summary: Appointment of Alférez of San Francisco for Sgt. José Pérez Fernández

author: José Argüello
summary: On distribution of the *situado*

author: Hermenegildo Sal, Guarda Almacén (San Francisco)
summary: San Francisco presidio founding-year pay roster (1776) — *resumen general* of what each soldier is owed; ~46 names (de Anza colonists) — see expanded entry. Totals: suministrado $6,084, *haber* $19,720, *alcanzan* $14,256, *debiendo* $403; categories: sirvientes del Presidio / de la Misión / de Santa Clara

author: Hermenegildo Sal (San Francisco)
summary: Munitions consumed by the troop, SF presidio 1776: powder 57½ lb, 33 musket cartridges, 16 cannon cartridges, 100 musket balls, 22½ lb raw lead

author: Hermenegildo de Sal (San Francisco)
summary: SF 1776 treasury summary: goods/provisions received $14,627; delivered $14,634; value of stock in the Real Almacén $3,531

author: José Joaquín de Arrillaga (Loreto)
summary: Forwards Viceroy's note authorizing the sea-mail (*correo de mar*) to run under Arrillaga's direction, its allotment folded into the presidio's

author: Viceroy Revillagigedo (México)
summary: Take the *correo de mar* under his direction

author: Andrés de Mendívil y Amirola (México)
summary: Notice the King named him Administrador Gral. de Correos y Postas & of the Renta de Estafetas of New Spain & the Philippines

author: Mendívil y Amirola
summary: Receipt of his note announcing he took command of the Peninsula

author: Mendívil y Amirola
summary: Order Felipe de Goycoechea (postmaster, Sta Bárbara presidio) to send the 2,970 reales the *estafeta* yielded 1783–88, held in his keeping

author: Mendívil Amirola (México)
summary: Has not received the post-office proceeds Goycoechea says he remitted

author: Laureano Feixa de Senande (México)
summary: The Comte. Gral. de Provincias Internas holds the title of Subdelegado de la Renta de Correos; all postal matters go to him

author: Mendívil y Amirola (México)
summary: Having inquired into the whereabouts of a letter

author: Arrillaga (Loreto)
summary: Remission of documents

author: Manuel Antonio Flores (Viceroy, México)
summary: Establishing a maritime *correo* from San Blas to the Peninsula for official & private mail; has asked the dept. comandante Fran.º Hijosa for reports; asks Arrillaga which Peninsula port is best & the proper means of quick transport (cert. copy by Arrillaga, i.291) — cf. the similar Flores→Fages *correo* letter near [n320](https://archive.org/details/bancarchca_81_17/page/n320) (poss. duplicate in a later tomo)

author: Fr. Fermín Lasuén (Mission San Carlos)
recipient: Coronel & Comte. Insp. Diego de Borica
summary: Observations on the difficulties of founding the Villa de Branciforte (per Viceroy Branciforte's order, via P. Pres. Pedro Callejas) next to Mission Santa Cruz — Lasuén thinks founding a *villa* so near the mission all but 'imposible,' yet will obey; lists mission livestock issued to settlers (13 tame horses, 21 mares + 2 studs, 13 ox-yokes, 160 sheep, 232 ewes, 56 young bulls…); on Santa Cruz's poverty, ruined church & dwellings, grain shortage *(corrected — earlier mis-attributed to 'Borica→Viceroy'; read mid-document)*

author: Hermenegildo Sal
summary: Employed 12 more men on the *abasto* so as not to overstock; for lack of *víveres* asks to defer; on stone [readings uncertain — `[?]`]

author: Raymundo Carrillo (w/ J. Mª Estudillo), certifying Viceroy Branciforte
summary: Fortification of San Francisco & Monterey — see expanded entry. Branciforte acknowledges engineer Alberto de Córdoba's 3 plans (SF presidio; Monterey battery; Santa Cruz); the Batería de San Joaquín, 'única defensa' of SF port, is poorly laid out & defective — orders it strengthened; Tte. Col. Pedro de Alberni; two 24-pdr cannons at the port mouth; arms/provisions sent via the prepared frigates & paquebot *San Carlos*; received in Cal. 15 Mar 1797

author: José Joaquín de Arrillaga (Loreto)
summary: Returns the *oficios* on the Intendente de Monterey's *pliego* of the 15th (sent 3 May); on a habilitado's accounts & an Alférez's funds (5 Jun 1794)

author: Gob. Borica (Mont.)
summary: Transcribes Viceroy Branciforte's 5 Oct oficio (inserting a 30 Sep decree per the *auditor de guerra*): Monterey missionaries must obey Borica's orders; & the 7 Apr order that escort soldiers not sleep away from their escoltas

author: Jacobo Ugarte y Loyola (Arispe)
summary: Approves giving the Padre of Mission San José del Cabo one of the 3 canoes built on the Yaqui, on the proposed terms

author: Arrillaga (Loreto)
summary: *Informe* on the maritime *correo* San Blas–Peninsula: best landfall Cerralvo, else the Ensenada by Mission San José del Cabo; the Real de Santa Ana detachment (4 soldiers + 1 sgt); distances (Santa Ana→Ensenada 100 leguas; 30 leguas S); Gulf winds Sept–Apr Nortes/Noroestes make the S→N voyage slow; recommends an 8-day *correo*

author: Hermenegildo Sal (San Francisco)
summary: 'Buques ingleses en Bodega' — see Entry 9. An Indian 'de las Islas' before the presidio reports two ships in the Puerto de la Bodega; the foreigners' speech not understood; sailors wore clothes 'con nuestras vueltas,' believed to say they were English; ashore 4 days & still there, taken water, firewood, hunted many deer, asked the Indians for cattle, showing them otters beforehand

author: Hermenegildo Sal (San Francisco)
summary: Index of the communications he has sent

author: Hermenegildo Sal
summary: Forwards correspondence; missions & presidios lack the *situado* because the ship carrying it — under [Francisco de] Eliza — did not enter the port: Eliza said he had no order to enter and lacked crew, boat, anchors & cables

author: Hermenegildo Sal (San Francisco)
summary: Had only 1 pair of *grillos* & 1 *grillete*; asked Tte. Eliza for 2 pairs *grillos* & 4 *grilletes*, destined for Missions Santa Clara & Santa Cruz, to punish when needed

author: Hermenegildo Sal
summary: Forwards soldier Justo Altamirano's petition to marry

author: Hermenegildo Sal
summary: Reports his arrival at the presidio, returning from Monterey with the *memorias*

author: Hermenegildo Sal (San Francisco)
summary: THE TARGET — 'Haber hecho fuego contra un buque español por equivocación.' The SF port-mouth guard sighted the ship at 8 a.m., fired a *cañonazo* as a signal; when it didn't respond, manned the *cantil blanco* battery (its 8 cannon) with munitions, meaning to sink it (*echarlo á pique*) if it stayed silent; at 11 a.m. it was at the harbor mouth — fired 8 *cañonazos* at it, from 11 a.m. until 5 p.m., when it signalled; pilot Juan Carrasco then reported it was the Goleta *Saturnina*, out of El Pilar 11 June, carrying dispatches for the commander of the Expedición de Límites, [Bodega y] Quadra (at Nootka), driven into port for lack of water & provisions — see Entry 10

author: Hermenegildo Sal (San Francisco)
summary: Depredations by Indians of the Arroyo del Pájaro: soldier Pablo Acebes, escorting a servant + an Indian of Sta Cruz with *Memoria* cargo, was halted, the *naturales* unloaded the cargo & threatened them with arms; Acebes mounted & fled; the Mont.→Sta Cruz road is unsafe (only passable Jun–Aug); few troops, most occupied conveying *memorias* & serving the frigates *Concepción* & *Gertrudis*

author: Manuel Lujano (Soledad)
summary: Remission of accounts

author: Hermenegildo Sal (San Francisco)
summary: Supplied provisions to the frigate of H.M. *Santa Gertrudis*, capt. Alonso de Torres y Guerra, arrived at Monterey from Nootka low on victuals, crew of 240 (not counting the cámara)

author: Hermenegildo Sal
summary: Note to the *Santa Gertrudis* (from Nootka): if Captain Vancouver arrives at San Francisco, have him reported; a mounted sentinel posted in view of the port to give notice of his arrival

author: Hermenegildo Sal (San Francisco)
summary: Receipt of the *inválido* cédula for soldier Ignacio Linares

author: Hermenegildo Sal (San Francisco)
summary: THE CANNON-BURST — see Entry 11. During the fiesta of the patron saint (Llagas de San Francisco), 'reventó un cañón, sin causar daño, y se hizo diez pedazos' (a cannon burst, harming no one, blown into ten pieces), some fragments reaching the church, 145 *varas* away; fired by soldier Alejandro Arriola

author: Gob. Arrillaga (Loreto)
summary: Remission of documents

author: José Mª Estudillo (Mont.)
summary: Monterey presidio company muster (1817): Tte. Estudillo, Alf. José Estrada, surgeon Manuel Lujano, 2 sgts, 10 cpls, 69 soldiers, *sangrador*, carpenter, 2 smiths, 5 artillerymen 'en la batería,' 29 *inválidos*

author: [Gov.?]
summary: Governance/conduct instruction: an officer 'no es capaz por sí solo de desempeñar… el mando interino por la muerte del anterior Gob[ernador]'; that an officer should be guided 'por los impulsos de su propio honor que no por el temor de la autoridad'; above all to keep peace among troops, vecinos, Indians, Christians & gentiles [partial read — confirm sender/date; spans the skipped n75]

author: Hermenegildo Sal (San Francisco)
summary: Second *Saturnina* notice (corroborates Doc 135): 'llegó Goleta *Saturnina* el 10, con pliegos para el Comte de la Expedición de Límites, le facilitó auxilios y el mismo día se dió á la vela' (arrived the 10th with dispatches for the Límites-Expedition commander; aided, sailed the same day)

author: José Mª Estudillo (Mont.)
summary: Militia detachment, 2nd Artillery Corps (Monterey): cpls. José de Jesús Vallejo, Quintín Ortega; 15 artillerymen; total 17, all 'en la batería'

author: Carcaba (México)
summary: On the *vestuario* (uniforms) of Alberni's company; cost note for garments, war-chests & arms per Viceroy's order of 26 Jun; swords/sabers/war-chests to be issued every 5 *vestuarios* (~20 months)

author: Carcaba (México)
summary: Account of the net cost of *vestuario* & arms for the Compañía de Voluntarios de Cataluña detached in Nueva California (itemized: 62 casacas for sgts/soldiers, *calzones*, *chalecos*, *escarapelas*, swords, sabers, war-chests…); total ~$1,307 / *libranza* $1,313

author: Juan M. Ibarra, Comte. Militar (Sta Bárbara)
summary: Santa Bárbara *estado de fuerza* (1835): Artillery (1 cpl, 4 sol. effective; 1 in the battery; 1 sick; 1 on leave); Cavalry (2 alféreces, 3 sgts, 2 cpls, 27 sol. = 34; guard, escort at S. Buenaventura, 3 prisoners at Mont., etc.); Escuadrón de Mazatlán (1 lt., 5 sol.)

author: José Mª Estudillo (Loreto)
summary: *Relación de cargo y data* of munitions for the Loreto company: stock end-1782 (cartridges, powder arrobas, lead… ~2,500), distributions per 6 *libramientos* of Joaquín Cañete, remainder in reserve

author: Ramón Lasso de la Vega (San Francisco)
summary: SF presidio company roster (1787): Tte. vacant; Alf. Lasso de la Vega; 1 sgt, 4 cpls, 25 soldiers (31 total); 2 *inválidos*

author: Francisco Mª Ruiz, Habilitado (San Diego)
summary: Cargo/data account of Real-Hacienda cattle at San Diego: 1,403 head (vs. 1,020 prior year; +383)

author: José Águila, Sgt. 2º (Mont.)
summary: National Artillery Corps roster (Monterey, 1828): Capt. Miguel Gonzáles (suspended); 2 sgts, 1 drummer, 6 cpls, 36 gunners (45 total)

author: Sgt. Macario de Castro (Monterey)
themes: britain; america; ships; defense; exploration; indigenous-demo; indigenous-intel
summary: 'Diario de novedades' — his inspection tour of the escorts under SF/Monterey jurisdiction, ordered by Hermenegildo Sal, kept from 22 Dec 1796 (but folding in 1796 events) — see Entry 12. Highlights: a neophyte woman from Nootka; Borica's Order of Santiago investiture; H.M.S. *Providence* under Broughton, 4–18 June 1796 (11-gun salute); a Boston/American frigate (Capt. 'Juan Gare[?]', 26 persons, 6 guns) at Carmel/Monterey Oct–Nov 1796, with English sailors (+ a woman) put ashore at pistol-point & rounded up (10 set to presidio work); 18 ships sighted off San Diego, Oct 1797 (alarm relayed up the coast); Indians reporting 'cañones en la mar' near San Antonio; detailed arms/munitions inspections (Dec 1796 & Jan 1797); a 1798 sentinel wounding the guard corporal

author: Manuel Antonio Rodríguez, Habilitado Gral. (México)
summary: Account for the Monterey presidio: cost of iron goods (*hierros*) sent on the *situado* $29,439; presidio's *haber* $34,075; balance owed $10,364[?]

author: Gob. Borica (Mont.)
summary: Certificate for Fr. Francisco Miguel Sánchez (Franciscan of San Fernando) attesting his missionary service at Soledad & Santa Clara since June 1792 — confessing, preaching, administering sacraments to neophytes & the San José pueblo *vecinos*, catechizing frontier *infieles* 'con mucha moderación y religiosidad'

author: Fermín Fco. de Lasuén (Sta Bárbara)
summary: Urges retiring an insane padre (for the Peninsula's & México's sake); wants him put aboard the frigate as soon as he arrives, fearing 'exterioridades ruinosas y tal vez desgraciadas en tierra'; P. José Miguel de la Concepción to accompany him to Monterey

author: Fr. [Hilario?] Pedro Buena Puente[?] (San Diego)
summary: Asks leave to retire to his Colegio de San Fernando, suffering grave ailments

author: Rafael de Pedro y Gil, Guarda Almacén (San Diego)
summary: *Factura* sent from México by factor Manuel Ramón de Goya for San Diego: value at México $13,038; at San Diego $29,743

author: Rafael de Pedro y Gil (San Diego)
summary: Discrepancies found in Goya's *memoria* of 30 Oct 1777; $1,060 in favor of the Real Hacienda

author: Fr. Francisco Miguel Sánchez (Mission San Carlos)
summary: Requests the good-conduct certificate (the petition that prompts Doc 156); notes he was assigned to New California by the Viceroy 28 Sept 1791

author: Fr. Vicente Fuster (San Juan Capistrano)
summary: Requests a carpenter — there is none at the mission

author: Fr. Fermín Fco. de Lasuén (San Luis Obispo)
summary: On deaths of gentiles; the 'imprudent resistance' to the Berg. *Amador* expedition; the *memorias* of the 4 latest foundations; the new San Miguel ('con hambre de tener otra'); gentiles too numerous

author: José Joaquín de Arrillaga (Loreto)
summary: *Estracto Gral.* of the 12 monthly musters of the Marine Department (1802): maestranza (1 carpenter, 1 caulker, 1 smith); crew (patrón, 2 arráez, guardián, 23 sailors) — total 27

author: Hermenegildo Sal (San Francisco)
summary: 'Buques ingleses en Bodega' (2nd report; see Entry 13) — two English ships anchored at Bodega 15–17 Jan 1793; cannon-signals heard ('5 cañonazos,' answered with one); fog hid them near the harbor mouth; Vancouver sailed from Monterey the 13th, and Sal does not doubt it was Vancouver who fired the shots to call those ships — presumed bound for Bodega ever since he was at San Francisco; will watch & take reports from the *indios isleros*

author: Hermenegildo Sal
summary: Remits the Viceroy's *bando* of 21 Aug 1792, with Real Cédula of 4 Feb on the admission & renunciation of títulos de Castilla & who pays *lanzas y medias anatas*

author: José Estrada, Habilitado, w/ José Mª Estudillo (Mont.)
summary: Inventory of all effects, provisions & goods at the Monterey habilitación: total $24,742; + cargo/data of the San José pueblo common fund (owed $1,655 to the deposit) and private debts to the habilitación ($733)

author: José Argüello (San Francisco)
summary: Climate & livestock report: much cold, high winds & fog; mild rains, light frosts; pastures sparse but harvests regular in the district's 3 missions incl. newly-founded San José; a disease among the SF neophytes, of which several died

author: Felipe de Goycoechea, Comte. (Sta Bárbara)
summary: Climate/health *relación* for 1797: many frosts, great winds Jan–May, thick summer fogs; deaths from *tabardillo* (typhus) & pleurisy, many in the presidio from consumption & ulcers; abundant births & good wheat, but rats, mice & moles destroyed the seed, even on irrigated land

author: José Joaquín de Arrillaga (Loreto)
summary: State of the Peninsula, first 6 months 1797: drought — water so scarce stock can barely subsist; wheat 'promises something' on the frontier; the *gálico* (syphilis) & regional disease did no great harm this half-year

author: Antonio Grajera, Comte. (San Diego)
summary: State of the jurisdiction, 1st semester: benign climate, regular waters, late snows; good wheat/maize; births sparse near the presidio & Mission San Miguel but copious at San Juan Capistrano & San Gabriel; no epidemics; natives quiet — 'se goza de felicidad'

author: Fr. Vicente Fuster & Fr. Juan Norberto de Santiago (San Juan Capistrano)
summary: On weaving works: 180 varas of cloth for the church (yellow/green/blue/purple *bayetas*, jaspered white warp); 80 blankets on the broad loom; 150 *pastoras* for Sta Bárbara; the weaver's wife teaches Indian girls to spin; carpet dyed with *palo campeche, brasil, zacatlaxcal* & a *grana* (cochineal) stone (short of lemons, used vinegar); colors poor — the Indians get better ones; needs a *batán* & press; instruction given by 'Enrique'

author: [Diocesan] Gob. de Sonora (Real del Rosario)
summary: Grants Lasuén & his friars faculties to administer sacraments to troops/individuals of the *fuero militar* (castrense jurisdiction), per the Viceroy's order for punctual attendance to soldiers & the Patriarch-Vicar-Gral's edict (4 Aug 1786); cites the Pontifical Breves of Clement XIII & Pius VI

author: Fr. Lasuén (San Luis Obispo)
summary: Notes the King named Miguel José de Azanza (Secretary of War) Viceroy of México, replacing Branciforte

author: José Argüello (Mont.)
summary: Note on increasing the *Memoria* now that 7 artisans have arrived, to be clothed; (Jul 6) remits a *libranza*

author: Juan B. Matute (Mont.)
summary: To receive aboard Fr. Manuel Fernández, licensed to retire to San Fernando

author: Lasuén (San Carlos)
summary: Certifies retirement licenses for friars Pascual Martín de Arenaza, Fco. Miguel Sánchez, Diego García, José Mª Fernández (decade served / ill; the last by grave accident, per Royal-Navy surgeon Luis Faba)

author: Dr. José Mª Hernández (San Francisco)
summary: The wreck of the supply ship *San Carlos* at San Francisco: rebuts a complaint that the mission fathers failed to aid it the night it was lost — P. Martín was alone (the other ill at Santa Clara); he sent every available Indian, who worked 3 days & nights up to the waist in water, then 50 more for the salvage maneuvers, and provided the survivors with food

author: José Mª Estrada, Alférez Comte. (Loreto)
summary: Loreto presidial company roster (1788): Capt. Arrillaga, Tte. Ortega, Alf. Estrada, 1 sgt, 3 cpls, 38 soldiers, 1 caulker (46 total); *inválidos* (retired Capt. Cañete, Sgt. José Gabriel Arce, 2 sol.); Marina (goleta crew: patrón, guardián, 7 sailors)

author: José Argüello (Mont.)
summary: On the criminal cause of soldier José Vicente Gonzáles

author: Hermenegildo Sal (San Francisco)
summary: 'El arroyo del Pájaro impide pasar al correo': the monthly mail left for Monterey on the 1st but returned to Santa Clara on the 5th, unable to cross the Arroyo del Pájaro or find the ford (few know it) — happens repeatedly

author: José Argüello (Mont.)
summary: Remission of accounts

author: Felipe de Neve (Arispe)
summary: On a mission debt to the Real Hacienda: the $3,500 owed by Missions San José del Cabo & Santiago de los Coras have been paid since 1774; the *autos* are suspended

author: Viceroy Iturrigaray (México)
summary: Noted that on 28 July the frigate *Concepción* (the Manila galleon) anchored at the Santa Bárbara presidio, from Manila

author: José Joaquín de Arrillaga (Loreto)
summary: Foreign-ship exclusion order: forwards the Viceroy's note (17 Aug) on the entries the American frigate *Alexander* made into Peninsula ports; reiterates the Reales Órdenes prohibiting foreign vessels; orders the commanders to 'de ningún modo franqueen auxilios á buques extranjeros, á menos que no se vean obligados á discreción'

author: Fiscal Borbón / Viceroy Iturrigaray (México)
summary: Dictamen on the San Francisco presidio works of 1801: the warehouse, guard & cavalry-quarter roofs needed repair; the hurricanes of the 2nd & 17th [of the month] 1801 destroyed both newly-built and old roofs; Borica's repair order, the Habilitado's accounts, the dispute over costs

author: Manuel Carcaba (México)
summary: Provisions sent from San Blas that year on the frigate *Concepción* & brig *Activo* for the 4 presidios, Catalan Volunteers & Royal Artillery = $6,765

author: Manuel Carcaba (México)
summary: Artisan-survival certificates (1797); Tte. José Argüello replaces Alf. Raymundo Carrillo managing the SF presidio's interests; *factura* sent to Loreto ($41,449; situado $43,947; balance for San Blas goods $26,9[?]8)

author: Alf. José de la Guerra y Noriega, Comte. Inte. (Mont.)
summary: Monterey company muster (1804): Tte. Raymundo Carrillo, Alf. de la Guerra, surgeon José Benites, 1 sgt, 5 cpls, cadet Fernando de la Toba, 50 soldiers (60 total); artillery (sgt, cpl, 4); 24 *inválidos*

author: José de la Guerra & José Joaquín Maitorena (w/ Ignacio Martínez, Sta Bárbara)
summary: General cargo/data account de la Guerra managed Jan 1817–30 Sep, handing the Sta Bárbara habilitación to Maitorena: Cargo $91,911, Data $90,774, $1,137 against the habilitado

author: Manuel Carcaba (México)
summary: *Memoria* for Monterey 1798: prior-year goods debt $8,191; 12 *libranzas* of Habdo. José Pérez Fernández 1796 $8,499; total Memoria $24,110; presidio owes $1,674 (off the 1798 situado)

author: Manuel Carcaba (México)
summary: *Memorias* for San Diego ($17,564; +$1,057 in favor), the Artillery packet ($5,871; $2,155 unpaid San Blas goods), and San Francisco ($8,992; +$910)

author: Caballero de Croix (Arispe)
summary: On the Río Colorado (Yuma) catastrophe & orders to pursue its authors — quotes his 26 Aug prevención (awaiting confirmation of 'la catástrofe' before grave measures; leave presidio garrisons, assemble the rest of the troop against any rebel-Indian incursion & to support the punitive operations); convened a Junta de Guerra y Real Hacienda; passed orders to Gob. Militar Jacobo Ugarte y Loyola & Tte. Col. Pedro Fages; the expedition commander to circle the Río Colorado, reach the ruined establishments, then hand the command to Neve [cf. yuma-revolt-1781]

author: Antonio Bonilla (Arispe), certifying the Junta de Guerra y Rl. Hacienda (Croix presiding)
summary: Certified resolution on the punitive expedition against the rebel Yumas — see Entry 14. Junta of Croix, Intendant Pedro Corbalán & auditor Pedro Galindo Navarro; reviews the Anza/Garcés expeditions & the founding of the Yuma establishments (Comte. Santiago de Islas, P. Juan Díaz), the Yumas' voluntary submission then apostasy/rebellion (per Capts. Juan Manuel de Bonilla of Tucson & Pedro de Tueros of Sta Gertrudis del Altar, reports 6/18/31 Aug); legal basis (*Recopilación* ley 9 tít 4 lib 3); fears rebellion spreading to nations by San Gabriel/San Diego/Sta Bárbara Channel & the land route being cut; resolves to send a 'cuerpo respetable de tropas'; terms — surrender all prisoners & stolen stock/arms, hand over the ringleaders for exemplary capital punishment, else mercy this once; 3 reales daily rations for the auxiliary Pima/Gileño/Pápago Indians; route via the Pimería Alta (Tueros); communicated to Neve & to Rivera y Moncada at Mission San Gabriel; signed Croix, Corbalán, Galindo Navarro, Bonilla [cf. yuma-revolt-1781]

author: José de Zúñiga, Comte. (San Diego)
summary: San Diego presidio company roster (1792): Tte. Zúñiga, Alf. José Velásquez, 1 sgt, 5 cpls, 45 soldiers (53 total); 1 smith, 1 carpenter

author: Jacobo Ugarte y Loyola (Chihuahua)
summary: 'La correspondencia de los Religiosos la pagará el Síndico' — the Peninsula missionaries' official mail postage to be paid/franked, charged to the Síndico's account

author: [Fiscal] de Real Hacienda (México)
themes: foreign-relations; america; ships; defense; legal; trade
summary: Dictamen on the seizure of the American frigate *Cromo[?]* — repeats the Viceroy's 19 Feb order: if the *Cromo* reaches their districts, secure its cargo in deposit, make a formal inventory, hold the ship in the bay, arrest the captain/master/supercargo & crew, seize the navigation papers, take their depositions promptly & separately so they cannot collude — to establish the voyage's true provenance & objects and whether they violated the King's orders & the treaties of Peace, Limits & Navigation with the United States or meant to run contraband; remit all to the Viceroy with a report. A detailed foreign-ship interdiction protocol

author: Gob. Arrillaga (Loreto)
summary: Copy of a commission from México: a solemn mass to be sung in each parish head-church (*cabeceras de los curatos*) for 'la Paz celebrada por España y Rusia' (the 1801–02 peace between Spain & Russia)

author: Habd.º José Estrada & José Mª Estudillo (Mont.)
summary: Monterey Compañía Presidial Fondo de Retención account: fund at end-1807 $987, outflows 1808 $380, balance $2,607

author: José Estrada (Mont.)
summary: Retention Fund accounts: 1810 (balance $3,587) and 1811 (retentions, outflows $750, balance $2,975)

author: Jacobo Ugarte y Loyola (Chihuahua)
summary: Acknowledges with satisfaction Fages' 28 Jan report on the *socorro* & progress [Lasuén/missions]

author: Ugarte y Loyola (Chihuahua)
summary: Acknowledges Fages' reports (17 Jun, Jul, 9 Aug) on the tranquility & pacification of the gentile Indians of California; on the Santa Bárbara visit & cattle to supply the San Diego→SF frontier escorts; the *reos* of the Pueblo de la Reina de los Ángeles in their goods & crops

author: Guillermo Carrillo[?] (San Diego)
recipient: Comte. Fernando de Rivera y Moncada
summary: Explains the death of soldier Garci [Brito] Murillo at the paraje 'San Felipe' — Carrillo's pistol discharged accidentally, killing Murillo instantly; the case joined to the *sumaria*

author: Alf. José Velásquez (San Diego)
summary: *Diligencias* on Murillo's death: ruled accidental homicide, sentencing the killer to a month's confinement in barracks, doing service on foot until further order

author: Tte. José Fco. de Ortega (San Diego)
summary: On a tumult of Indians at 'Pala' & resistance near the new Mission San Juan Capistrano: gentiles tried (arms in hand) to ambush soldiers (Ant. Peña; Dolores Domínguez & Juan Acevedo) driving cattle from San Gabriel to the mission, meaning to rob it — averted by the San Gabriel interpreter's parley; orders Sgt. Mariano Carrillo + 4 soldiers to the mission to investigate the gentiles' intent, overawe them, and inventory deserters' effects found there

author: Sgt. Mariano Carrillo (Mission San Juan Capistrano)
summary: Report (per Ortega's 16 Nov order): reached the new mission the 17th; confirms via Cabo Orlas Beltrán the 'Trabuco' incident — armed gentiles tried to stop the cattle & cargo for the mission (the P. Presidente [Serra] & 3 soldiers escorting); the gentile-captains of nearby rancherías, wishing to be Christian, calmed via the San Gabriel interpreter; on the deserters (incl. Manuel Robles) & their effects; leaves soldier Armenta there pending the lieutenant's decision

author: Hermenegildo Sal (San Francisco)
summary: Reports the *correo*'s departure for Monterey; no novelty at SF & its missions

author: Hermenegildo Sal (San Francisco)
themes: missions; ships; defense; britain; fiscal; indigenous-intel
summary: Bundle incl.: *factura* of San Blas provisions (via comisario Fran.º Hijosa) for the SF troop, carried on the frigate *Aránzazu*; ⭐ founding of Mission Santa Cruz (1791) — aided P. Pres. Lasuén (at Sta Clara) to scout the Santa Cruz site; 5 sailors assigned as the new mission's servants; Tte. José Darío Argüello sent 12 men + a sergeant, well armed, to join a Monterey party at San Juan Bautista; (Nº8) ⭐ a foreign-ship signal — ~10 Sept, Lasuén at the San Felipe ranchería (≈6 leguas from SF mission) heard 6–7 cannon-shots near midnight, 'como entre cañones,' seeming a ship off the port to leeward; asks the SF presidio's help to verify the new Santa Cruz *reducción* before the rains close the roads

author: Fr. Fermín Lasuén (Santa Clara)
summary: Letter on the founding of Mission Santa Cruz — received Gov. Romeu's copy of the Viceroy's order on the two new missions; awaits Sal's arrival to carry it out; coordinating the road being worked, supply transport & custody, the friars destined for the founding, and a *casita* the Indians are to build at the site; warm thanks for Sal's help

author: José Estrada, Habd.º (Mont.)
summary: Monterey Retention Fund accounts: 1809 (balance ~$3,775) and 1813 (balance $2,657)

author: José Argüello, Habd.º (Mont.)
summary: Remits (duplicate) the muster & *estado de fuerza* of the company for the month

author: José Estrada (Mont.)
summary: Account of Real-Hacienda gunpowder sold this year; the missions' share = $63

author: Hermenegildo Sal, Habd.º (San Francisco)
summary: Bundle — (Nº1) an artisan/smith-armorer arrived; (Nº2) admin re Ramón Lasso & the Sta Bárbara company; (Nº3) ⭐⭐ the CHARQUÍN uprising: a Christian Indian, Charquín, scarcely 8 days at Mission San Francisco, fled, took up arms against the mission Christians, grew 'temible entre la indiada'; per Sgt. Miguel Pacheco (Sta Cruz escort) he holds up to ~20 Mission SF Christians — women & children — captive in the sierra, letting none leave; P. Baldomero López (SF minister) alarmed; (Nº4) P. Danti (Dolores) on the same — see Pending for military-history-project

author: José Mª Estudillo, Comte. Inte. (San Diego)
summary: San Diego company muster (1826): Capt. Vicente del Corral (in México), Tte. Fco. Mª Ruiz, Alf. Gervasio Argüello (habilitado), 2 sgts, 7 cpls, 59 soldiers, 2 artisans, 7 artillery 'en batalla,' 20 *inválidos* — total 100 men

author: Gob. Borica (Mont.)
themes: royal-order; trade; defense; missions; fiscal; construction; france; indigenous-labor; legal
summary: Bundle transcribing Viceroy Branciforte's orders. (Nº1–2) R. Cédula of 1 Apr: arms carried to Cádiz not in the ship's register liable to *comiso* + customs-certificate rule; (Nº3) collect chupas, cannon & 50 @ powder from the San Blas ships for Monterey; (Nº4) missions must furnish horses for extraordinary troop service; (Nº5–9) Monterey habilitación accounting (Alf. José Pérez Fernández); (Nº6) supply missions cloth & farm tools at cost; (Nº10) $2,122 from the *Cuenta Mayor de muertos* for the Monterey fort works (Habd. Manuel de Carcaba); (Nº11) bando re the French Nation (1796 war); (Nº12–13) the Compañía de Voluntarios de Cataluña + a 15-man artillery *piquete* to California (armed by the Veracruz treasury; pay via the California Síndico from 1796); (Nº14–15) advances to artisans Manuel Esteban Ruiz (mason/stonemason) & tailor Joaquín Botello ($1,000); ⭐ (Nº16) Borica's FOUR-ARTISAN PROGRAM to teach trades to the *naturales*: tailor Joaquín Botello, potter Mariano Tapia, ribbon-maker Manuel Muñoz, weaver Mariano Mendoza ($100 each; certified by Alberni); (Nº17) $300 ($75 ea.) for their *regalos*; (Nº18) carpenter Leocadio Martínez wounded the Monterey smith Pedro Gonzáles — merited 10 yrs presidio but took church asylum, sent to San José; (Nº19–21) accounting/liquidation & glosa orders for the Tribunal de Cuentas

author: José de la Guerra y Noriega, Habd.º (Sta Bárbara)

author: [Diocesan] Gob. de Sonora (Hda. de Pamoca[?])

author: Antonio Grajera (San Diego)

author: [official] (Guadalajara)

author: Viceroy Branciforte (México)
summary: Bando of the Royal order lifting the prohibition on sugarcane (*caña*) cultivation & manufacture in the realm, with the *reglamento* for the new branch of the treasury

author: José Estrada (Mont.)

author: José Mª Estudillo (Mont.)
summary: Certifies (with the Princesa's captain, Tte. de Navío Ramón de Aragui[?]) that four barrels of muscatel aguardiente are spoiled — no longer potable, harmful to health

author: José Estrada (Mont.)

author: Comte. José Argüello (San Francisco)

Luis Peralta; Sgt. José Antonio Sánchez; Cabo [Tiburcio] Murillo (holds the habilitación, manages it 'con legalidad')

author: Viceroy Branciforte (México)
summary: Bundle: re San Blas personnel/effects staying in the Province; ⭐ on establishing a mission at 'Santa Catarina Mártir' (over Missions San Miguel & Sta Rosalía, Baja); $1,500–600 from the Monterey presidio fund for the church works; $2,226 to Habd. Carcaba for the Santa Bárbara presidio works; suministración/inventory

author: José de la Guerra y Noriega, Comte. (Mont.)
