author: José García (San Blas)

author: José Argüello (Mont.)

author: José Pérez Fernández (San Francisco)

author: José Argüello (Mont.)

author: Hermenegildo Sal (Mont.)

author: José Pérez Fernández (San Francisco)

Domingo Iturrate, Fr. José García

summary: Instruct Fr. Junípero Serra to STOP administering the Sacrament of Confirmation until further order (the Serra confirmation-faculties dispute)

author: Caballero de Croix (Arispe)
summary: Indian policy: manage the gentile rancherías so they don't learn to make war like the Apache provinces; warns they not become habituated to riding horses, lest they later use them & their arms to attack

author: José de la Guerra y Noriega, Habd.º (Sta Bárbara)
summary: Sta Bárbara muster + gratification fund (1817): Tte. de la Guerra, Alf. Joaquín Maitorena, sgts Guillermo Cota, Carlos Carrillo, Juan Ortega, 6 cpls, cadet Santiago Argüello, 63 soldiers (75 total); 28 *inválidos*; *haber* $18,872

author: De la Guerra (Sta Bárbara)
summary: Retention Fund: in $4,937, out $887, balance $4,050

author: Tte. José Fco. de Ortega (San Diego)
summary: Long *sumaria* (Sgt. Fco. de Aguiar) on the disorders in the San Juan Capistrano & San Gabriel escorts — soldiers going at night to the rancherías to solicit/force gentile women ('forzar á las mujeres de los gentiles'); Fran.º Villagómez twice violated 'el 6º mandamiento' with the gentile captain's wife (she came to wash clothes at the *playa*; question whether by force or will) — put in irons; Mathías de la Vega, José/Fran.º Peña, Julián Acevedo, 'Gloria,' Soberanes named; ⭐ key motive: these soldiers 'les han quitado las pieles de venado á los gentiles' (stripped the gentiles of their deer-skins), 'cuyas maldades pueden producir muy malas consecuencias de perturbar la paz.' Formal witness declarations (soldiers Acevedo, 'Gloria,' Juan Álvarez et al. — going to the rancherías, an *india* hurt) closing at San Juan Capistrano (21 Jun 1777, Guillermo Carrillo; cert. copy Ortega, San Diego Jul 1777) & the San Gabriel escort (1 Oct 1777). ⭐ Embeds a punitive skirmish at the ranchería *Alacua Chomi* (20 Jun 1777): a cabo + 5 soldiers attacked; gentiles loosed arrows (wounding Mathías López's & Acevedo's horses); soldiers fired, 3–4 Indians killed, 2 captured (one given 25 lashes); the gentiles had vowed to behead the 'cabo guapo' & threatened mission Christians — over soldiers stripping them of deer-skins & forcing their women. The documented cause behind the SJC indigenous resistance (Docs 204–205, 231). Long *sumaria*, n257–n278, abstract pp.360–396

author: José Mª Estudillo (Mont.)
summary: Monterey artillery detachment: Tte. Estudillo, cabos José de Jesús Vallejo & Quintín Ortega, 15 gunners (18 total)

author: Antonio Bonilla (Arispe)
summary: On Confirmations — the Bull of Clement XIV (10 Jul 1774) giving P. Junípero Serra the faculty to confirm; Borica/Neve denied the escort, so it's suspended pending the Bull's presentation (companion to Doc 313)

author: Caballero de Croix (Arispe)
summary: Río Colorado water for the Mission San José de Guadalupe; deserters José Ignacio Pérez, Fco. Sotelo, Hermenegildo Flores apprehended in Sonora (Rivera y Moncada to bring soldiers' families to the peninsula) — duplicate of earlier Croix–Neve material

author: Arrillaga (Loreto)
summary: Pérez Fernández's alférez nomination for SF; + SF company roster (1816): Capt. Rodríguez, Tte. Luis Argüello, Alf. Gabriel Moraga, 2 sgts, 8 cpls, cadet, 58 soldiers (72 total); *inválidos* incl. Pedro Amador

author: José Estrada (Mont.)
summary: Description of the Monterey presidio buildings: the *cuadro* of the guard, the cabos' quarters, an infirmary, the sergeant's house; chapel & 3 quarters (one with 2 cellars + kitchen), powder-store; the forge; the Casa de Gobierno (Tte. Olejano Cota), 2 warehouses; a breach 15 varas long, 10 wide, in the presidio wall

author: Gov. Simón Yanovsky (Russian America, New Archangel)
summary: English-translation letter proposing Russo-Spanish commerce & joint fur-hunting — see Entry 16. Hagemeister left ill; the RAC brig *Ilmena* is bound for 'Port Ross' with St-Petersburg papers & goods; sends agent Khlebnikov ('Clebnicoff') to arrange 'a commercial intercourse'; offers goods for grain; recalls Hagemeister's petition to the Russian ambassador at Madrid for leave to hunt furs on the California coast 'in company with His Catholic Majesty's subjects, on equal shares'; gift of a Russian glass

author: Gov. Solá (Mont.)
summary: Reply to the RAC overture (Entry 16): supplied seeds via Khlebnikov & supercargo; thanks for the gifts (a mirror & glass), sends a small one back; ⭐ on the otter-hunting request — he CANNOT grant it on his own authority, only the Viceroy of the Reyno can, so he will petition; until then he lacks the faculty to concede it

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: Asks for a detailed report on the accounts the late José Joaquín de Arrillaga held in the habilitación

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: Coastal-security order (per Viceroy, 17 Aug): rigorous inspection of all vessels & papers in the ports — two mercantile expeditions sailed from Lima 'para encender de nuevo la discordia'; scrutinize papers 'especialmente los que procedan de Italia'

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: No Indian jailed for theft/*maleficio* to be freed without his order; monthly list of names, crimes & dates of imprisonment

author: Solá (Mont.)
recipient: vecinos Mariano Castro, Ignacio Ortega & San José pueblo
summary: Repair the roads SF→San Juan [Bautista] for *carreta*/*recua* transport (Pájaro river crossing); Sgt. José Dolores Pico commissioned; San Ysidro *vecinos* to help

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: 'Sobre buques piratas' — the Bouchard-raid defense mobilization: per Capt. José de la Guerra's letter, two frigates (34 & 18 guns, 230 crew) sailed from the Sandwich Islands for California, known via an American ship to be pirates; orders — hide gold/silver & church vessels/vestments in interior missions; ready families to evacuate women/children/elderly; drive off livestock; Capt. Luis Argüello (SF) to mount the men; form a flying party under an officer; 500 cartridges to Monterey [cf. bouchard-raid-1818]

author: Solá (Mont.)
summary: Bouchard aftermath: relays the Viceroy's pleasure (note of 22 Mar) at the report of the 15 Oct 1818 attack by the insurgent Buenos Aires frigates, praising the valor, honesty & fidelity of the troops & *vecinos*; + a population-census order (heads of family, children by sex/age, excluding mission Indians) [cf. bouchard-raid-1818]

author: Juan Antonio Riaño (Guanajuato)
summary: The Viceroy enclosed the *filiación* of 17 individuals destined for the new Peninsula settlement

author: (unsigned)
summary: List of vagrants & minor offenders destined to settle California — named, with age, *calidad* & trade (companion to Doc 285): Cornelio Rocha (23, español), Juan José de la Luz (27, mulato), José Mª Chavira (30, master tanner), Mateo de la Luz Chávez (29, hatter), José Cornelio Valderrama (25, tailor), José Dionisio Cabrera (39, master *labrador*), etc.

author: Fr. Cayetano Pallés (Loreto)
summary: Reports P. Vicente Fernández died 4 Jun at the Rosario mission (Baja Cal.)

author: Hermenegildo Sal (Mont.)
summary: Persecution of the Ausaymes Indians: they renewed threats (having killed some gentiles), in the sierra de la Natividad; the gov. sent a sergeant + 9 soldiers to seize the *capitanejo*; the sgt. returned with the capitanejo & 13 Ausaymes Indians (a captured Christian Indian) [cf. register #55 area]

author: Hermenegildo Sal (San Francisco)
summary: 'Sobre establecimiento de la Bodega' — forwards Tte. de Fragata Juan Matute (Comte. del Pto. de la Bodega)'s report that a Spanish establishment at Puerto de la Bodega is feasible; Sal to receive the goods, war-supplies, 2 military men & 5 artisans brought on the frigate *Aránzazu* (housing them at the SF presidio; Salvador Méndez). The 1793 Spanish Bodega-occupation attempt — core of the project_bodega_open_door / Bodega-failure thesis

author: Viceroy Marquina (México)
summary: Coastal vessels must carry passports stating their names, tonnage, captain & crew

author: Viceroy Iturrigaray (México)
summary: To drive the American ships doing clandestine commerce off the coasts, has ordered the San Blas comandante to send a warship to cruise from San Francisco to Bahía de San Quintín

author: (King & Viceroy)
summary: Doc recording the King's & Viceroy's thanks to individuals of New California for contributing to the costs of the late war

author: Viceroy Branciforte (México)
summary: On the English Capt. Brown[?]'s stay in port & his reconnaissance of the San Antonio river (made as he embarked to leave); approves Borica's measures; orders the recon kept hidden; to deter foreign ships at Peninsula ports, will station the frigate *Concepción* (or another San Blas vessel) [cf. C-A 54 'Capt. Brown / English at Bodega']

author: Branciforte (México)
summary: War donation of $3,821 raised among officers, troops, *inválidos* & *vecinos*

author: Branciforte (México)
summary: On the commissions of the English frigate captain Guillermo Broughton (HMS *Providence*, cf. Entry 12); the books of Rafael Bachiller (alcalde/juez de crimen) & Manuel de Hargreves[?] to be remitted via San Blas

author: José García (San Blas)
summary: Could not obtain the hemp seed (*cáñamo*) requested

author: José García (San Blas)
summary: Provisions worth $1,088 sent on the frigate *Concepción* for the SF & Monterey presidios

author: José García (San Blas)
summary: Armament shipped on the *Concepción* (under royal-navy pilot Esteban José Martínez, the Nootka-crisis officer) for Monterey: 20 cases of muskets, 2 of cartridge-boxes, 4 of gun-flints, 4 with musket-covers

author: José Antonio Sánchez (Mission Santa Cruz)
summary: 'Sin interés'

author: Viceroy Azanza (México)
summary: Received the 5 *habilitado-gral.* nominations for Capt. Antonio Columna

author: Azanza (México)
summary: On reintegro of supplies the presidio gave the San Blas ships in 1793–95; the San Blas archive in disarray, payment-notes lacking

author: Viceroy Azanza (México)
summary: Encloses the inventory of the goods of Capt. Antonio Grajera (d. aboard the frigate *Princesa*); form the *testamentaría* expediente

author: Azanza (México)
summary: Noted a large ship sighted 28 Dec heading north

author: Azanza (México)
summary: Noted the vessels sighted from 23 Sept to 24 Oct

author: Viceroy Branciforte (México)
summary: Encloses 9 *inválido* cédulas for Monterey-company cpls & soldiers

author: Branciforte (México)
summary: On arming & fitting out two war frigates to guard the province (carrying the *memorias*); sends on the *Concepción* 200 short 16-cal. muskets, 200 covers & cartridge-boxes, 16,000 gun-flints; re the new Villa de Branciforte

author: Branciforte (México)
summary: R.O. (via Pedro Varela, 23 Aug) that Marine officers & dependents at various posts execute orders punctually & represent through channels

author: Branciforte
summary: Returns Ildefonso Bernal's petition — he must route it through the Comte. Gral. de Provincias Internas

author: Ildefonso Bernal, Alférez (Mission San Vicente)
summary: Petition to transfer his service to Sonora; Arrillaga & Borica endorse

author: Borica (Mont.)
summary: Forwards Bernal's transfer petition

author: Viceroy Marquina (México)
summary: On selling at auction the clothing of the late San Diego comte. Antonio Grajera, proceeds held pending the inventory

author: Azanza (México)
summary: Noted [Arrillaga's?] move to San Diego to embark for the capital

author: Azanza
summary: Noted 3 ships sighted at the boca grande of Loreto, then vanished

author: Azanza
summary: On Alférez José Lujan (San Diego co.) — to continue earning merit

author: Azanza
summary: Reminder re the R.O. of 23 Jan on the natives of the Isla de Menorca (Minorcan settlers[?]) — no reply yet received

author: Azanza
summary: Noted the events in the Peninsula from the English ships sighted

author: Azanza
summary: The frigate *Concepción* anchored at San Diego

author: Manuel Carcaba (México)
summary: Alféreces José Pérez & Raymundo Carrillo made habilitados of the Monterey & SF presidios; Tte. José Argüello ceased

author: Antonio Grajera (San Diego)
summary: Difficulty forming the cargo/data account — proposes Carrillo go to Sta Bárbara with the documents to draft it (w/ Ortega & Goycoechea)

author: Norsagaray / José Orozco Santos (Guadalajara)
summary: *Bando* (Viceroy, Orizaba 18 Apr) removing 2 cigarettes from each pack (Nº11) — owing to the present war, paper scarcity & treasury needs

author: Viceroy Marquina (México)
summary: Resolves the boundary dispute between San José pueblo & Mission Santa Clara: the Río Guadalupe to be the dividing line; the coastal land left to the pueblo for wood-cutting; fixed *mojoneras*

author: Marquina
summary: Encloses the interim *inválido* cédula for cabo José Mª Salgado

author: Marquina (México)
summary: The anglo-American brig *Betsy* at San Diego (1800): arrived seeking aid (given at fair price); also called at San Blas; Capt. Salvador Hidalgo (frigate, Puerto Escondido) left a captain, supercargo & sailors ashore and seized their passports & nautical charts for examination; approves the aid & the precautions against clandestine commerce

author: Branciforte (México)
summary: The pailebot *San Carlos* (Tte. de Fragata Ramón Saavedra) sailed from that port for San Diego

author: Marquina (México)
summary: Surgeon Juan de Dios Morelos (replacing Pablo Soler on the *Concepción*) not to be entered on the muster until he presents his approval titles

author: Viceroy
summary: Friar movements: P. Pres. of the New California missions' petition & leave for P. Isidoro Barcenilla to return to San Fernando; P. Cayetano Pallás embarked on the *Activo*; P. Melchor Pons embarked on the *correo* for Santo Domingo

author: Marquina (México)
summary: Petition of Fr. Miguel Abad (minister of Santo Domingo) to retire, being ill — cannot be granted now for the stated reasons

author: Viceroy Manuel Antonio Flores (México)
summary: Establishing a San Blas → opposite-coast *correo* (informants Hinojosa & Arrillaga) — DUPLICATE of Doc 121

author: Manuel Carcaba (México)
summary: On the *ajustamiento* of the company musters (1793–95) — sends copies for verification

author: Carcaba (México)
summary: On Borica's consultation that the Los Ángeles & San José *vecinos* be sent an annual *memoria* (account) of goods

author: Carcaba (México)
summary: Report (per Fiscal Hernández de Alba) on Borica's request that the King annually send a *memoria* of goods to the pueblos — a LONG economic *dictamen* running n323→n345 (tomo pp.~50–62): critiques the mission economy (Indians clothed/fed but 'no se conoce industria ni comercio'); argues for California fisheries — salmon 'de los 35 grados al Norte,' salt-cod/*bacalao*, anchoa, *salmones salpresos* — as a branch of industry & commerce; the 6,000-peso *memoria*, administrators vs. *habilitados*, the cost of San-Blas shipping. ⭐ (economic-history interest)

author: Manuel Carcaba (México)
summary: On a *reparo* in the remittance of flour (~50 arrobas[?]) for the Compañía de Voluntarios de Cataluña

author: Carcaba (México)
summary: Accounting bundle: accounts of the 4 New-California presidios passed to the Real Tribunal y Audiencia de Cuentas for *glosa*; the *reparos* in the 1793–95 *ajustamientos* of Argüello & Sal (2 docs); abono of single plazas, *inválido*-discount for surgeon & bleeder (not for the Monterey/San Diego carpenters)

author: Manuel Carcaba (México)
summary: On the remission of *víveres* & effects in the frigates *Concepción* y [*Princesa*?]

author: Viceroy Azanza (México)
summary: Transport & sea-costs of D. Pablo García de la Osa (a *soldado distinguido* aboard the frigate *Concepción*) charged to the *fondo común*; reimburse habilitado-gral. Carcaba

author: Viceroy Marquina (México)
summary: Remits a copy of the R.O. of 24 Jan 1800 on how prisoners are to be treated

author: Viceroy
summary: R.O. (Sevilla 20 Jun 1801, cap. 54) ordering punctual compliance with the prize-*ordenanza* addition of 21 May 1799 to the 12 Oct 1796 ordinance — only for prizes taken while that addition was in force

author: Viceroy Marquina (México)
summary: Juan Antonio Hernández passes to New California as a *poblador* with his family

author: Viceroy Iturrigaray (México)
summary: Noted the arrival of the private Balandra *Ntra. Sra. de Guadalupe*

author: Pedro de Nava, Comte. Gral. (Chihuahua)
summary: Remits two legajos (Nº1 & 2) of papers the late D. Felipe de Neve brought, comprising various orders

author: Comte. Gral. Pedro de Nava (Chihuahua)
summary: Real cédula prorogating by 4 yrs the alms-account for the beatification of D. Juan de Palafox; a *bando* permitting Indians to travel to Spain for a limited time

author: Conde de Revilla Gigedo (México)
summary: Received the *cuentas de consumo* of [the presidios]

author: Viceroy
summary: The Viceroy orders 2 pagas de ordenanza advanced to Ttes. Antonio Gragera & León Parrilla for their journey to their posts

author: Jacobo Ugarte y Loyola (Guadalajara)
summary: Received the certification of the artisans in the province; orders their families continue to be supported

author: Viceroy Iturrigaray (México)
summary: R.O. on a U.S. ship called the *Eagle*, ~100 tons, 10–12 cannon, being fitted out at New York to sail — intelligence relayed via the S.M. consul at New York; foreign/American-ship warning (cf. *Cromo*, *Betsy*, *Alexander*, foreign-exclusion seam)

author: [roster]
summary: A roster of *pobladores*/*reos* — named, with *casta* (mestizo/español/indio/mulato), age, marital status, trade (panadero, sastre, labrador, obrajero, sombrerero…) & origin (León, Dolores, Guadalajara, Querétaro, Santiago…)

author: José García (San Blas)
summary: Provisions worth 2,764 p. 7 r. 19 mrs. shipped on the frigate *Princesa*

author: Viceroy
summary: Thanks for the *enhorabuena* on establishing Mission San Pedro Mártir; noted [Arrillaga?] has taken possession of the political & military government

author: José Mª Estrada, Alférez (Loreto)
summary: Muster (*fuerza*) of the Loreto presidial company: Capt. José Joaquín de Arrillaga, Tte. José Fran. de Ortega, Alf. Estrada, Sgt. Fran. de Aguiar, cabos López/Berdusco/Botiller, 39 soldados = 46 hombres; plus the Departamento de Marina (herrero, patrón, guardián, 8 marineros… ~20)

author: [diario de reconocimiento]
summary: A land-reconnaissance diary: arroyos, water-sources, *lagunas que fertilizan*, terrain fit for cattle-raising & sowing, the *escolta de Santa Cruz*, bad road 'por los muchos zanjones' — surveying land near Santa Cruz

author: [muster]
summary: A presidial muster: Cirujano Manuel Quijano, Sgt. Ignacio Vallejo, José Dolores Pico, 10 cabos, 70 soldados = 85; sangrador José Castillo; 1 carpenter, 2 smiths, 5 artilleros

author: José Joaquín de Arrillaga (Loreto)
summary: The Viceroy (3 Apr) accepted Manuel Torres's resignation as surgeon of Monterey & named José Benítez in his place (550 p. salary)

author: Felipe de Goycoechea, Tte. (Santa Bárbara)
summary: On preventing the waste of grain

author: Viceroy Marquina (México)
summary: 1800 war-scare ship-sighting cluster: noted the Goleta *Ntra. Sra. del Carmen* anchored; 'desde que se avistaron los buques enemigos no á ocurrido novedad' (Oct 30); 3 interim *inválido* cédulas for 3 Santa-Bárbara-company soldiers; 'quedó enterado de los buques avistados en la parte del Sur' (Nov 24) — the Anglo-Spanish-war coastal alarm
